PT P
PN JP2021143642-A
TI Carbon dioxide separation apparatus of internal combustion engine of motor vehicle, has honey-comb rotor that is provided with adsorption area which adsorbs carbon dioxide and separates carbon dioxide from exhaust gas of engine.
AU MAKI M
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2021B0302H
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide separation apparatus (3) has a honeycomb rotor (32) which is provided with an adsorption area (32A) which adsorbs a carbon dioxide and separates from the exhaust gas of an internal combustion engine (1). A desorption area (32B) is provided to the rotor to detach the adsorbed carbon dioxide while supporting a carbon-dioxide adsorbent. A synchronous mechanism (33) synchronizes the rotation of the honeycomb rotor with the rotation of the engine.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation apparatus for internal combustion engine of motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide separation apparatus of the internal combustion engine is compact and is excellent in energy saving property.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide separation apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Internal combustion engine (1)
   Carbon dioxide separation apparatus (3)
   Honeycomb rotor (32)
   Adsorption area (32A)
   Desorption area (32B)
   Synchronous mechanism (33)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q17 (Vehicle construction, fittings, propulsion arrangements (B60J-K, B60R, B60V-W)); 
MC E11-Q02A; E31-N05C; H06-C04; J01-E02D; Q17-E09; Q51-J02B
IP F01N-003/24; B01D-053/92; F01N-003/08

PT P
PN US2019301795-A1; US10641549-B2
TI Regenerating ultra-lean solvent for deep carbon dioxide removal of treated feed gas comprises using ultra-lean physical solvent in absorber to remove carbon dioxide from the treated feed gas and removing in nitrogen rejection unit nitrogen.
AU MAK J
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
   FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 201983529X
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating an ultra-lean solvent for deep carbon dioxide removal of a treated feed gas comprises: using an ultra-lean physical solvent in an absorber to remove carbon dioxide from the treated feed gas to thereby form a carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent and a carbon dioxide-depleted feed gas; removing in a nitrogen rejection unit nitrogen from the carbon dioxide-depleted feed gas to thereby produce a nitrogen waste stream and a pipeline gas; and regenerating the ultra-lean physical solvent from the carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent in a process that includes flashing the carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent and stripping the flashed carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent using the nitrogen waste stream as a stripping gas.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating an ultra-lean solvent for deep carbon dioxide removal of a treated feed gas (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of flashing comprises at least 1000 psig reducing a pressure of the carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent. The step of regenerating is performed without heating the carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent. The step of regenerating is performed without heating the flashed carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent. The method further comprises: increasing a pressure of the nitrogen waste stream before using the nitrogen waste stream as a stripping gas, where the nitrogen waste stream is increased to a pressure of 5-50 psig; removing carbon dioxide from a feed gas to produce the treated feed gas and increasing a pressure of the treated feed gas prior to removing CARBON DIOXIDE from the treated feed gas. The step of removing carbon dioxide from a feed gas comprises contacting the feed gas with an amine solvent, where the step of removing carbon dioxide from a feed gas produces a carbon dioxide loaded amine solvent and the method further comprises regenerating the carbon dioxide loaded amine solvent, where the feed gas is a hydrocarbonaceous feed gas and the feed gas has a nitrogen concentration of at least 10 mol%. The feed gas has a carbon dioxide concentration of at least 2 mol%. Preferred Components: The ultra-lean physical solvent is a non-aqueous physical solvent, where no dehydration of the carbon dioxide-depleted feed gas is required. The treated feed gas has a pressure of 1000-1500 psig. The flashed carbon dioxide-loaded physical solvent has a pressure of less than or equal to 100 psig. The carbon dioxide-depleted feed gas has less than or equal to 3000 ppmv carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide-depleted feed gas has less than or equal to 1000 ppmv carbon dioxide. The treated feed gas has less than or equal to 2 mol% carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC E10-J02D; E11-Q01A; E31-H03; E31-N05C; H06-A02; H06-X01; H06-X03; Q75-F

PT P
PN CN110294478-A; CN210193419-U
TI Dry ice machine with pre-crystallization tank has crystallization cylinder for crystallizing liquid carbon dioxide and liquid carbon dioxide storage tank in which pre-crystallization tank is connected to gaseous carbon dioxide storage tank.
AU CHEN S
   YUAN Y
   ZHANG Y
AE DALIAN ELITE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201985289M
AB    NOVELTY - A dry ice machine with a pre-crystallization tank (3) comprises crystallization cylinder (4) for crystallization of liquid carbon dioxide by hydraulically pushing a piston (51) and for supplying dry ice to crystallization cylinder; and liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (11) connected to pre-crystallization tank to supply liquid carbon dioxide. Pre-crystallization tank is connected to crystallization cylinder by a pipeline and to a gaseous carbon dioxide storage tank (12). Pre-crystallization tank is also provided with a gaseous carbon dioxide outlet for providing a pressure regulating valve.
   USE - A dry ice machine with a pre-crystallization tank.
   ADVANTAGE - Liquid carbon dioxide can be output to the crystallization cylinder at a stable pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the dry ice machine.
   Pre-crystallization tank (3)
   Crystallization cylinder (4)
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (11)
   Gaseous carbon dioxide storage tank (12)
   Piston (51)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).)
MC E31-N05C; J07-B01

PN CN207913002-U
TI Carbon dioxide absorber comprises carbon dioxide            absorbent, where carbon dioxide absorbent contains            tubular monomer group, semi-spherical monomer group and            semi-cylindrical single group, and tubular monomer            group contains tubular monomer.
AU YU B

PT P
PN WO2021103330-A1; CN112852004-A; CN112852004-B
TI Thermally expandable microsphere coated with carbon dioxide comprises a core-shell structure having polyacrylate as a shell and triethanolamine for absorbing carbon dioxide as a core.
AU CHEN H
   TANG H
   WU C
AE ZHEJIANG HAIHONG HOLDING GROUP CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG HAIHONG HOLDING GROUP CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202159830X
AB    NOVELTY - Thermally expandable microsphere coated with carbon dioxide comprises a core-shell structure having polyacrylate as a shell and triethanolamine for absorbing carbon dioxide as a core.
   USE - Thermally expandable microsphere coated with carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and safe; and prepares thermally expandable microsphere which has good expansion performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the thermally expandable microsphere coated with carbon dioxide comprising (a) mixing beta -hydroxyethyl methacrylate, glycidyl methacrylate, carbon dioxide absorbent, crosslinking agent and water to obtain an aqueous phase, where the carbon dioxide absorbent is triethanolamine, and the crosslinking agent is 1,4-butanediol dimethylacrylate and/or 1,3-butanediol dimethylacrylate, (b) mixing paraffin oil and Span 60 (RTM: Sorbitan monostearate) emulsifier to obtain an oil phase, (c) evacuating a reactor and filling the reactor with carbon dioxide, adding the aqueous phase into the reactor, and maintaining the pressure of carbon dioxide at 1-6 atm, so that the triethanolamine may fully absorb carbon dioxide, and (d) adding the oil phase into the reactor, maintaining the pressure of carbon dioxide at 1-6 atm, fully stirring, and adding an aqueous solution of ammonium persulfate or potassium persulfate at 0 degrees C to react to obtain the thermally expandable microspheres coated with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of beta -hydroxyethyl methacrylate, glycidyl methacrylate, carbon dioxide absorbent, crosslinking agent, water, paraffin oil, Span 60 (RTM: Sorbitan monostearate) emulsifier and ammonium persulfate or potassium persulfate is 100:5-15:30-60:0.5-2.5:4-8:400-600:3-7:0.2-1.5. Preferred Method: The step (d) comprises adding the obtained oil phase into the reactor, maintaining the carbon dioxide pressure at 1-6 atm, stirring the solution evenly at a speed of 100-400 revolutions per minute, preferably 200-400 revolutions per minute, adding an aqueous solution of ammonium persulfate or potassium persulfate to react at 0 degrees C for 4-10 hours, preferably 6-10 hours and naturally warming to room temperature to stop the reaction to obtain the product. The method further comprises post-processing after stopping the reaction including centrifugally collecting the product, using petroleum ether and methanol to wash the product, drying at room temperature to obtain the microsphere. The aqueous solution of ammonium persulfate or potassium persulfate is the aqueous solution formed by dissolving 0.2-1.5 pts. wt., preferably 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate or potassium persulfate in 1-5 pts. wt. water.
   EXAMPLE - 10 g beta -Hydroxyethyl methacrylate, 0.5 g glycidyl methacrylate, 5 g triethanolamine, 0.05 g 1,3-butanediol dimethylacrylate, and 0.6 g water were mixed uniformly to form aqueous phase. 50 g Paraffin oil, 0.5 g Span 60 (RTM: Sorbitan monostearate) emulsifier were mixed uniformly and oil phase was obtained. A reactor was evacuated, filled with carbon dioxide, aqueous phase was added into the reactor and the carbon dioxide pressure was maintained at 1 atm so that triethanolamine fully absorbs carbon dioxide. The oil phase was added into the reactor, the carbon dioxide pressure was maintained at 1 atm, fully stirred at a speed of 200 revolutions per minute. Aqueous solution of 0.1 g ammonium persulfate dissolved in 0.3 g water was added in a drop wise manner into the reactor at 0 degrees C, continued to react at 0 degrees C for 6 hours and thermally expandable microspheres coated with carbon dioxide were obtained.

PN WO2021200348-A1; JP2022511964-X
TI Carbon dioxide-absorbing fiber used as carbon            dioxide absorbing material in living environment and            used for removal of carbon dioxide in space suit or            spacecraft, comprises polyvinyl alcohol and amine-based            polymer.
AU KABAYAMA H
   WASHITAKE Y
AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 2021B5188J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-absorbing fiber comprises                polyvinyl alcohol and amine-based polymer, where                the nitrogen element concentration on the fiber                surface is 1.40 times or more the nitrogen element                concentration of the entire fiber.
   USE - The carbon dioxide-absorbing fiber is used as                a carbon dioxide absorbing material in living                environment and used for various applications                including removal of carbon dioxide in a space suit                or a spacecraft, and in thermal power plants,                ironworks, chemical factories, foods,                pharmaceuticals, cosmetics and batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-absorbing fiber exhibits                excellent carbon dioxide absorbing property even at                the temperature, humidity and the amount of carbon                dioxide in the living environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the carbon                dioxide-absorbing fiber involving (i) obtaining                spinning stock solution comprising polyvinyl                alcohol, an amine-based polymer and solvent,                extruding the spinning stock solution into dry air,                (ii) removing solvent to form undrawn fibers and                (iii) stretching the undrawn fiber 2.5 times or                more; and
   (2) a fiber aggregate containing the carbon                dioxide-absorbing fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The nitrogen element                  concentration on the fiber surface is 50 mol% or                  more. The content of the amine-based polymer is                  5-50 %mass with respect to the mass of the carbon                  dioxide-absorbing fiber. The average molecular                  weight of amine-based polymer is 500-50000.                  Preferred Components: The amine-based polymer                  comprises polyvinylamine, polyallylamine,                  polydiallylamine, polyalkyleneamine,                  polyethyleneimine or polydiallyl quaternary                  ammonium salts, preferably amine-based polymer                  having a branched structure. The fiber assembly                  is a non-woven fabric. Preferred Conditions: In                  step (i), the stretching temperature is 150-250                  degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - A water-containing chip of polyvinyl alcohol                (PVA) was prepared and put into an extruder to                dissolve PVA. The dissolved PVA aqueous solution                and Epomin SP-200 (RTM: Polyethyleneimine) were                mixed in a mass ratio of PVA and polyethyleneimine                of 60/40. The undiluted solution was extruded in                air at 100 degrees C from a nozzle having 70 holes,                dried and then wound to obtain undrawn fibers. The                obtained unstretched fiber was stretched in a hot                air furnace at 160 degrees C at a stretching ratio                of 3 times and a stretching time of 30 seconds to                obtain a carbon dioxide absorbing fiber (fineness                of 35 dtex).

GA 2022B32481
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises performing cleaning and                pre-processing to beta titanium alloy, placing the                beta titanium alloy after cleaning and                pre-processing in the device for oxygen-carbon                co-osmosis the surface of titanium alloy, using a                vacuum device to evacuate to a pressure, heating to                the solution temperature of titanium alloy under                vacuum condition and passing carbon dioxide with a                pressure for a heat preservation, cooling to an                aging temperature of titanium alloy, introducing                carbon dioxide with pressure for cooling or furnace                cooling to room temperature to obtain a beta                titanium alloy with a hardened layer on the                surface.

PT P
PN ES2909953-A1
TI Obtaining organic carbonate comprises reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide by catalyzing using hydrophilic ionic liquid in presence of water as reaction medium, and then recovering the obtained organic carbonate by separating.
AU NAVARRO TEJEDOR P
   HERNANDEZ MUNOZ E
   VELA GALLEGO S
   MOYA ALAMO C
   SANTIAGO LORENZO R
   HOSPITAL BENITO D
   LEMUS TORRES J
   PALOMAR HERRERO J
AE UNIV AUTONOMA MADRID (UMAD-C)
GA 2022809607
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining at least one organic carbonate comprises reacting carbon dioxide and at least one epoxide by catalyzing using at least one hydrophilic ionic liquid in presence of water as reaction medium, and then recovering the organic carbonate obtained after the reaction by separating between two aqueous phases.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining at least one organic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is optionally carried out in a closed cycle using water as an extracting solvent; allows valorization of carbon dioxide and the easy recovery of the catalyst as an improving additive in the reaction stage; are summarized in a reaction with high conversions under mild temperature conditions, efficient recovery of the product in an easily separable aqueous stream and a practically total recovery of the catalyst in the water; shows high range of valid operating temperatures; and utilizes water for assisting the reaction and separation process which allow drastically improve the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of water in a synthesis of organic carbonates catalyzed by ionic liquids, comprising a stage of separation between two aqueous phases for the recovery of the carbonates and of the ionic liquids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the process: the reaction to obtain the at least one carbonate, the molar ratio of water and the at least one hydrophilic ionic liquid is 1000:1-1:10, preferably 50:1-10:1; the added water is an aqueous stream; the added water is in a water:reaction medium volume ratio of 5:1 and 0:1, preferably 3:1 and 1:3; the molar ratio between carbon dioxide and the at least one epoxide is 10:1-1:10, preferably 2:1-1:1; and the molar ratio between the at least one epoxide and the at least one hydrophilic ionic liquid is 1000:1-2:1, preferably 100:1-50:1. Preferred Process: The process comprises: adding water after obtaining the at least one carbonate and prior to the separation step; and (b1) optionally, adding water to the reaction medium to form a first aqueous phase comprising the hydrophilic ionic liquid and a second aqueous phase comprising the at least one organic carbonate, and (b2) separating the aqueous phase comprising the hydrophilic ionic liquid from the aqueous phase comprising the at least one organic carbonate. The process comprises (a1) mixing carbon dioxide, at least one epoxide and at least one hydrophilic ionic liquid-water pair, and (a2) providing thermal energy for the insertion reaction of carbon dioxide in the at least one epoxide. In the process: steps (b1) and (b2) are carried out simultaneously; and the hydrophilic ionic liquid is recovered after the separation step between two aqueous phases and is fed into the reaction. The process is carried out in absence of an alcohol in a closed cycle. In the use: the synthesis of carbonates is a continuous process; and the water is an aqueous stream.
   EXAMPLE - Carbon dioxide insertion reaction in an epoxide ternary mixture with molar ratio of of propylene oxide:water and propylene oxide:(bmim)(Gly) of 10:1 and 199:1, respectively, and were added at 5 plus minus 1 degrees C in a reactor which was hermetically sealed. Subsequently, carbon dioxide was added to the reactor continuously to ensure that the epoxide was in the liquid phase and to keep the pressure constant. The tests were carried out at 120 degrees C under pressure of 20 bar. Various mixing points were prepared and stirred at controlled temperature. Shaking was performed in an orbital shaker (Julabo Shake Temp, model SW-22) at 25 plus minus 1 degrees C at a stirring speed of 100 rpm, optimized the shaking time with increasing times until equilibrium was reached. Phase separation was performed in a bath at 25 plus minus 1 degrees C. The reaction product and the two phases of the liquid-liquid extraction were analyzed by NMR. The analysis was performed using deuterated water as solvent in a Bruker model Varian Unity 500 NMR spectrometer.

Preferred Components: The acid comprises: organic                  acids including carboxylic acids comprising 1-8                  carbon atoms, preferably propanoic acid, butanoic                  acid, acetic acid, lactic acid, citric acid,                  oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, fumaric                  acid, glutaric acid, tartaric acid, adipic acid,                  maleic 5 acid, glycolic acid, salicylic acid                  and/or malic acid; or mineral acids including                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid or                  phosphoric acid, preferably hydrochloric acid or                  phosphoric acid. The weight ratio of the quantity                  of organic or mineral acid to the quantity of                  dioxide-generating compound is &#8805; 0.5,                  preferably &#8805; 2. The chemical oxidizing                  agent comprises (i) peroxygenated salts,                  preferably persulfates, perborates, peracids or                  its precursors, percarbonates of alkali metals or                  alkaline-earth metals e.g. sodium carbonate                  peroxide, (ii) alkali metal bromates or                  ferricyanides, (iii) solid hydrogen                  peroxide-generating chemical oxidizing agents                  e.g. urea peroxide and polymer complexes that can                  release hydrogen peroxide, preferably                  polyvinylpyrrolidone/hydrogen peroxide complexes                  and/or (iv) oxidases that produce hydrogen                  peroxide in the presence of a substrate. The                  oxidation dyeing agent comprises oxidation bases                  or couplers. The oxidation bases comprise                  para-phenylenediamines, bis(phenyl)alkylene                  diamines, para-aminophenols, ortho-aminophenols,                  heterocyclic bases or their salts of organic or                  inorganic acid, or their optical or geometric                  isomers, tautomers and/or solvates e.g. hydrates,                  and the couplers comprise meta-aminophenols,                  meta-phenylenediamines and meta-diphenols,                  naphthols, mono- or poly-hydroxylated naphthalene                  derivatives, and heterocyclic coupler or their                  salts of organic or inorganic acid, their optical                  or geometric isomers, tautomers and/or solvates                  eg hydrates, preferably paraphenylene diamines,                  para-aminophenols or heterocyclic bases, the                  couplers comprise meta-phenylenediamines,                  meta-diphenols or meta-aminophenols their salts                  of organic or inorganic acid, their optical or                  geometric isomers, tautomers and/or solvates eg                  hydrates. The oxidation dye precursor comprises                  para-phenylenediamine, meta-aminophenol,                  meta-aminophenol, and meta-diphenol or resorcinol                  and/or aminophenols or their salts of organic or                  inorganic acid, and solvates e.g. hydrates.                  Preferred Method: The composition is mixed with                  water in a weight ratio of the quantity of powder                  composition to the quantity of water is 2:1-1:20,                  preferably 1:5-1:10. The obtained mixture has a                  pH of 7-12, preferably 7.5-11.
   EXAMPLE - A typical composition comprised of: 30 wt.%                sodium percarbonate; 20 wt.% ammonium chloride; 10                wt.% cellulose gum; 5 wt.% sodium lauryl sulfate; 2                wt.% dimethicone; 9.5 wt.% sodium bicarbonate; 21                wt.% citric acid; 1 wt.% para phenylenediamine; 1.3                wt.% para amino ortho cresol; 0.2 wt.% para                aminophenol; and 8 wt.% water.

TI METHOD FOR REMOVING LIGNIN-CARBOHYDRATE COMPLEX FROM BLACK LIQUOR IN SULFATE CELLULOSE PRODUCTION (3 VARIANTS).
AU FEDOROVA O V
   AKIM E L
   TARAZANOV A A
AE UNIV ST PETERSBURG STATE IND TECHNOLOGIE (UYSP-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1085C
AB    NOVELTY - Group of inventions relates to the field of cellulose production and can be used for the removal of a lignin-carbohydrate complex from black liquor in the production of sulfate cellulose. The proposed methods for removing the lignin-carbohydrate complex from the black liquor in the production of sulfate cellulose include processing the liquor with a temperature of up to 95C and a concentration of 7-40% on dry matter with a liquid saturated with carbon dioxide at a pressure of 3.0-7.0 atm, separation of lignin from the liquid phase with flotation foam, and the black liquor is treated with a liquid saturated with carbon dioxide at temperatures of 20-40C to a pH of 7-8 and containing additionally as a precipitator highly dispersed particles of the solid phase of mineral origin, natural or re-precipitated calcium carbonate, or particles of organic origin, wood flour, while decanting the liquid phase from the solid, filtering the formed flakes of the lignin-carbohydrate complex to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Cellulose industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Simplifying the technology of separating the lignin-carbohydrate complex from black liquor by reducing the time of treatment with carbon dioxide, eliminating flotation while improving filtration due to a controlled phase separation process, with the production of well-filtered sediment.

TI Preparing metal-free catalyst in synthesis of cyclic carbonate, involves using dicarboxylic acid derivatives and triazine nitrogen-containing heterocyclic organic compounds as raw materials, reacting in presence of solvent.
AU SHAN S
   YUE Z
   ZHI Y
   HU T
   JIANG L
   SU H
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021838846
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-free catalyst involves using dicarboxylic acid derivatives and triazine nitrogen-containing heterocyclic organic compounds as raw materials, reacting in the presence of a solvent at 65-150 degrees C for 48-72 hours, cooling to room temperature and filtering. The reaction product is washed with a solvent and dried to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing metal-free catalyst used in the synthesis of cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of converting carbon dioxide and epoxide into cyclic carbonate without catalyst and solvent and suitable for industrial production and market popularization and application, has obvious catalytic effec and high selectivity, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using metal-free catalyst, which involves placing the metal-free catalyst and epoxide into the autoclave, passing carbon dioxide gas to replace the air in the autoclave, passing carbon dioxide gas again to make the pressure in the autoclave reach 0.1-3.0 megaPascal and closing the gas inlet and outlet valves, reacting at 70-120 degrees C under stirring conditions for 2-30 hours to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dicarboxylic acid derivatives are 3,4-furandicarboxylic acid, 2,4-furandicarboxylic acid, 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid, 2,3,4,5-furantetracarboxylic acid, imidazole-4, 5-dicarboxylic acid, 2,5-thiophene dicarboxylic acid, 2,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid, 3,4-pyridinedicarboxylic acid, 2,4-pyridinedicarboxylic acid, 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid, 2,3-pyridinedicarboxylic acid and 3,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid. The triazine nitrogen-containing heterocyclic organic compound is one of melamine, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)triazine, and 2,6-trihydrazino-1,3,5-triazine. The solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylacetamide, ethylene glycol, methanol, dimethylformamide, 1,4-dioxane, and acetic acid. The epoxide is propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, and ethylene oxide. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of dicarboxylic acid derivatives to triazine nitrogen-containing heterocyclic organic compounds is 3:2-03:1. The molar ratio of the metal-free catalyst to the epoxide is 1:250-1:1000.

   USE - An electrochemical filter press reactor for electrochemical apparatus for converting carbon dioxide to formate ion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical filter press reactor provides high concentrations of HCOO-, and high energy efficiency in terms of Faradaic Efficiency (EF) towards the production of HCOO- while obtaining low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for electrochemical apparatus for converting CO2 to HCOO- comprising the filter press reactor, controlled evaporation mixer (CEM), water mass flow controller, and CO2 mass flow controller, where CEM is configured to feed a gaseous stream of CO2 directly to the cathodic compartment of the filter press, and electrochemical apparatus is configured to carry out a continuous electrochemical conversion process from CO2 to HCOO- and with a single passage of the reactants through the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Cathode comprises 3 layers, i.e. carbonaceous support, microporous layer, and catalytic layer. Microporous layer comprises carbon powder, preferably carbon powder and binding agent, where carbon powder are black powder pigments carbon, and binding agent is a compound comprising fluoropolymer chains. Microporous layer comprises carbon powder load of 1-4 mg/cm2, preferably 2 mg/cm2, preferably, the carbon powder are black carbon powder pigments. Catalytic layer comprises nanoparticles of bismuth, tin, lead, and/or indium, preferably nanoparticles are arranged on a carbon support. Catalytic layer comprises bismuth nanoparticles, preferably nanoparticles are arranged on a carbon support. Bismuth nanoparticles are arranged on a carbon support and a binder. Catalytic layer comprises a load of bismuth nanoparticles arranged on a carbon support of 0.25-3 mg/cm2, preferably 0.75-1.5 mg/cm2. Feed to the anode compartment comprises a basic solution, preferably potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, or mixtures, in a concentration of 0.1-3 M, preferably greater than or equal to 1 M. Preferred Processes: Microporous layer is obtained by direct deposition of an ink comprising carbon powder, binding agent, and organic solvent. Humidified CO2 gas stream is fed by the CEM directly into the cathode compartment. CEM is further configured to control the temperature of the humidified gaseous stream of CO2. CO2 is not fed to the filter press reactor as a mixture comprising CO2 and a liquid electrolyte. Humidified CO2 gas stream is fed into the filter press reactor under controlled temperature conditions. Water mass flow controller and CO2 mass flow controller are configured to regulate the flow rate of the water and CO2 feed, respectively. Preferred Conditions: Water supply flow varies between flow rates of 120-0.1 g/hour, preferably 20-0.5 g/hour. CO2 flow varies between 40-2000 ml/minute, preferably 100-200 ml/minute. Temperature of the humidified gas stream at the outlet of the controllers is the same as the temperature at the inlet of the filter press reactor, preferably 20-25 degrees C. Preferred Device: Apparatus has a relative humidity probe, located between the filter press reactor and the CEM, which is prepared to control the relative humidity of the humidified CO2 gaseous stream, before being fed to the filter press reactor. Temperature is controlled by a temperature probe. Apparatus has gas-liquid phase flow separator, preferably located at the outlet of the filter press reactor.

PN CN111943248-A
TI Synthesizing barium carbonate by dissolving barium chloride in water, adding barium chloride solution, adding carbonate to salt water, adding barium carbonate mixed solution to reactor, reacting solution with carbon dioxide, and separating.
AU XIONG Z
   XIONG G
AE HENGYANG JIJIAN JUXIN CHEM CO LTD (HENG-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8853M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing barium carbonate at room temperature, involves (a) dissolving barium chloride in water stirring, and setting the stirring speed to 200-250 rpm for 3-5 minutes to obtain a barium chloride solution, (b) adding the barium chloride solution to light salt water, adding carbonate to the light salt water, stirring using a stirring device, and setting the stirring speed to 30-50 rpm for 30-40 minutes to obtain barium carbonate mixed solution, (c) adding the barium carbonate mixed solution to a reactor, vacuuming, setting an internal pressure to 5-6 MPa, setting the speed of the reactor to 180-200 rpm, and discharging the barium carbonate mixed solution, and (d) continuously filling the barium carbonate mixed solution with carbon dioxide, reacting with carbon dioxide to obtain barium carbonate and salt water, and separating the barium carbonate and salt water by a filter mechanism to obtain barium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing barium carbonate at room temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, adopts easy to obtain raw materials, and is economical and environmentally-friendly, and prepares the product with high purity and excellent chemical stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of barium chloride is 0.25 mol/l. The concentration of the dilute slat water is 0.9%. Preferred Device: The reactor in step (c) is a medium-pressure reactor. Preferred Component: The obtained barium carbonate is in solid form.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water used in the step (a) is pure water.

AE SHENZHEN IBOX TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020895580
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous spray ink includes 10-25 wt.% carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, 5-10 wt.% fluorine-containing polyether polyol, 1-5 wt.% hydroxyl acid-containing, 5-10 wt.% isocyanate, 0.05-0.5 wt.% catalyst, 1-5 wt.% neutralizer, 1-5 wt.% chain extender, and the balance is water.
   USE - Aqueous spray ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous spray ink has good adhesion and is not easy to block the mouth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the water-based spray ink, which involves:
   (A) adding the carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, the fluorine-containing polyether polyol and the hydroxy acid into a reaction kettle at one time, heating the reaction kettle, and then vacuuming water;
   (B) adding portion of the catalyst after cooling down, slowly drop the isocyanate into the reaction kettle under the protection of protective gas, controlling the reaction temperature of the material and reacting for a certain time;
   (C) adding the remaining catalyst and continue to keep warm;
   (D) adding the neutralizer to lower the temperature;
   (E) adding dewatering and the chain extender, control the material temperature and stirring the reaction; and
   (F) adding the remaining dewatered water to the reactor and stirring.

The ink also includes an appropriate amount of additives.

PN CN111252796-A; CN111252796-B
TI Preparing crystal type controllable high-pure rare earth carbonate comprises adding rare earth solution and carbonate solution to reaction crystallizer, stirring, passing aging crystallizer, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering, and drying.
AU ZHANG S
   ZHANG X
   WANG J
   WANG D
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2020536814
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing crystal type controllable high-pure rare earth carbonate comprises (a) adding rare earth solution and carbonate solution to reaction crystallizer according to the volume flow ratio of 1:1-1.05, then at the same time starting the stirrer I in the reaction crystallizer for mixing uniformly the feed liquid I in the reaction crystallizer to obtain slurry I through reaction crystallization, and (b) passing the slurry I to the aging crystallizer, starting the stirrer II in the aging crystallizer for uniformly mixing the material liquid II in the aging crystallizer, introducing carbon dioxide into the aging crystallizer, maintaining the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the aging crystallizer at 120-1000 kPa to obtain slurry II through enhanced aging, filtering the slurry II to obtain solid phase I, washing the solid phase I and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing crystal type controllable high-pure rare earth carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The rare earth carbonate: has good crystal form, good shape, size controllability, has easy filtering, high purity and high value-added products and has convenient industrial implementation. The method: is suitable for large-scale industrial promotion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rare earth solution is 0.1-2 mol/l rare earth chloride solution. The ratio of the concentration of carbonate solution to concentration of the rare earth solution is 1.45-1.55. The carbonate solution comprises pure sodium carbonate, or pure sodium bicarbonate, or a mixture of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate. Preferred Method: The reaction crystallizer is operated at 40-180 degrees C. The residence time of the feed liquid I in reaction crystallizer is 0.5-4 hours. The aging crystallizer is operated at 40-160 degrees C. The residence time of the feed liquid II in the aging crystallizer is 1-6 hours. The step (b) comprises setting a circulation fan in the aging crystallizer, where the exhaust capacity of the circulating fan is 5-100 m3/m3 feed liquid II.

The particulate metal source comprises one or more of metallic iron, metallic zinc, alkaline salt of the one or more metals, solution of the one or more metals having a pH of at least 3.5, hydroxide of the one or more metals, and silicate of one or more metal. The aqueous source is water. The source of carbonate or bicarbonate ion comprise gas. The one or more transition and/or post-transition metal is chosen from manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, cadmium, and lead, preferably iron and zinc. Preferred Composition: The water is added to particulate metal source at liquid to solid ratio of 0.5 or less. The gas comprises at least 15 vol.%, preferably at least 30 vol.% carbon dioxide. The carbonate bonded article comprises at least 1 wt.%, preferably 20 wt.% carbonate of one or more transition and/or post-transition metal, 0.5 wt.%, preferably at least 20 wt.% siderite and/or smithsonite, and at least 4 wt.%, preferably at least 20 wt.% siderite. The carbonate bonded article is free of metal oxide or comprises 10 wt.% or less, preferably 2 wt.% or less metal oxide.

TI Carbon dioxide core displacement test device, has storage cylinder whose two sides are fixedly connected with automatic telescopic rod that is fixedly connected with holding ring, and material holding ring connected with high pressure hose.
AU CE S
   SUN L
   PAN Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 201903203B
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide core displacement test device, comprising a base, the top of the base are fixedly connected with a first support column from left to right, the second support column and the third support column, the top end of the first supporting column is fixedly connected with a top plate, the top end of the second supporting column is fixedly connected with a supporting plate; the surface of the supporting plate through the fixing cylinder, this utility model relates to the technical field of petroleum exploitation simulating. the carbon dioxide core displacement test device, material holding ring-containing core, the material holding ring by first automatic telescopic rod, matched with the annular concave groove and convex ring, the rock core is fixed in a sealed environment, through the opened sealing door; it can quickly replace the rock core, solving the problem that the current clamping is beneficial for beginner to operate, meanwhile it brings trouble to change of the core cannot be fast unfolding experiment, delay time of the operator, and it affects the work efficiency.

PT P
PN CN109011923-A
TI Waste gas processing method, involves decomposing waste gas molecule, installing high-energy UV light tube in photon cracking machine, releasing certain UV wavelength, bombarding oxygen molecule, and combining oxygen ions.
AU ZHOU S
   HAN J
AE ZHOU S (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 2018A54062
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves removing waste gas by using a remover. Dust and water vapor are absorbed by using a water curtain. The water vapor is condensation collected in the waste gas. Exhaust gas is entered into an electronic cracking machine. Molecules are bombarded in the waste gas by using energetic electrons. Waste gas molecule is decomposed into low-molecular compounds, water and carbon dioxide. Peculiar smell is removed. A high-energy UV light tube is installed in a photon cracking machine. Certain UV wavelength is released. Oxygen molecule is bombarded into oxygen ions. The oxygen ions are combined with oxygen molecule to generate ozone.
   USE - Waste gas processing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables decomposing exhaust gas molecule into low-molecular compounds, water and carbon dioxide, and removing peculiar smell of waste gas inlet. The method enables decomposing the waste gas with toxic or odorous in photon cracking machine to make completely decomposed non-toxic and harmless and odorless carbon dioxide and water and elementary substance material. The method enables improving safety performance, environmental protection, efficiency, excellent quality and economy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a waste gas processing method.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q01B; E31-D03; E35-K02; J01-E02B; J01-E02D; J01-G06; N03-B01A; N07-L01C

PN CN108775923-A; CN208848057-U
TI Self-protecting function featured grain bin            gas-water detecting unit, has grain pile for extracting            gas sample to realize counting pest trapping density            and shoot concentration of image and detecting grain            and carbon dioxide concentration.
AU LIN R
   WU C
   XIAO X
   CHEN Y
   YANG C
AE CHENGDU BEST TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018899880
AB    NOVELTY - The unit has a detecting unit main body                provided with a control circuit board, a recovery                device, a gas circuit control device, a detection                element, an electromagnetic valve control circuit                and a pump control circuit. A grain pile extract a                gas sample to realize counting pest trapping                density and shoot phosphine concentration of pest                image and detecting grain, carbon dioxide                concentration, oxygen concentration and grain                moisture content. The control circuit board is                provided with a central processor and a power                supply management module. A RS485 line terminal is                connected with an intelligent storage measuring                terminal through a communication module.
   USE - Self-protecting function featured grain bin                gas-water detecting unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The unit controls detection process, prevents                phosphine gas damaged by an oxygen sensor, a carbon                dioxide sensor and a temperature and humidity                sensor so as to meet need of intelligent                storage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                self-protecting function featured grain bin                gas-water detecting method.

PT P
PN US2018289880-A1; US10245365-B2
TI Method for replenishing urease in sorbent cartridge for use in sorbent dialysis, involves detecting amount of urea converted by urease inside sorbent cartridge to ammonia and carbon dioxide.
AU GERBER M T
   HOBOT C M
AE MEDTRONIC INC (MEDT-C)
GA 2018782963
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting an amount of urea converted by urease inside a sorbent cartridge to ammonia and carbon dioxide. The urease is supplied to sorbent cartridge if the amount of urea converted by urease inside the sorbent cartridge to ammonia and carbon dioxide is insufficient. The sorbent cartridge is adapted to receive adjustable amount of urease, such that the urease introducer is any one of slideable tray (26) and door. The detection process is performed by units selected from the group consisting of optical sensor, chemical sensor, blood urea nitrogen assay, and combinations.
   USE - Method for replenishing urease in sorbent cartridge for use in sorbent dialysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The urease can be provided while the system is operating or off-line. The desired amounts of urease are added in a simple and convenient manner in adjustable amounts. The compartment can be physically separated or marked off without a physical barrier. The method is capable of replenishing urease that can be stripped out of the sorbent cartridge during maintenance or dialysis session.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a sorbent cartridge provided with a urease introducer in the configuration of urease tray.
   Urease injection port (24)
   Tray (26)
   Outlet connector (30)
   Inlet connector (31)
   Injection site (32)

PT P
PN CN108195097-A
TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump assembly structure, has compressor mounted with oil cooler that is connected with hot water outlet, where oil cooler is provided with cooling inlet that is connected with water pump through pipeline.
AU LIU R
   ZHANG B
AE ANHUI HUANQIUXING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018521001
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a water pump (1) connected with an electromagnetic valve (5). An end of the electromagnetic valve is fixed with a valve. The valve is connected with a pipeline that is provided with a T-shaped temperature sensor. Another end of the electromagnetic valve is fixed with an end of a cooler (2). The pipeline is fixed between the electromagnetic valve and the cooler. An oil cooler (9) is fixed with an outlet (902) that is connected with a compressor (8). The compressor is mounted with the oil cooler that is connected with a hot water outlet (13). The oil cooler is provided with a cooling inlet (903) that is connected with a water pump through the pipeline.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump assembly structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure reduces energy consumption rate, realizes heat water recycling process and pressuring value knowing process and improves safety performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump assembly structure.
   Water pump (1)
   Cooler (2)
   Electromagnetic valve (5)
   Compressor (8)
   Oil cooler (9)
   Hot water outlet (13)
   Outlet (902)
   Cooling inlet (903)

PT P
PN CN108180470-A; CN108180470-B
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide coal circulating fluidized bed boiler, has low temperature ultra-heater fixed with heat exchanger, and high temperature ultra-heater connected with low temperature re-heater and high temperature re- heater.
AU GENG C
   ZHONG W
   SHAO Y
   CHEN X
   ZHAN J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201850318Q
AB    NOVELTY - The boiler has a hearth connected with a cooling wall and a middle-temperature over- heater. A low temperature ultra-heater is fixed with an external heat exchanger. A high temperature re-heater is connected with the cooling wall and the middle-temperature over-heater. A tail flue pipe is provided with a low temperature re-heater, a lower coal saver and an air preheater. A high temperature ultra-heater is connected with the middle-temperature over-heater, the low temperature re-heater and the high temperature re- heater. A middle temperature ultra-heater outlet and a high temperature ultra-heater inlet are formed on a spray de-ultra-heater.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide coal circulating fluidized bed boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The boiler has safe and reliable operation, high heat efficiency, compact design, rapid response speed of a load and strong adaptability, and effectively controls cooling wall temperature, and reduces coal resources, and improves energy utilization rate, and ensures safety of energy sources.

PN IN202021006932-A
TI Sequestering dissolved carbon dioxide from water for producing high-purity/ultra-pure water, involves neutralizing dissolved carbon dioxide by reacting calcium carbonate with dissolved carbon dioxide to form soluble calcium bicarbonate.
AU ITAGI K
   DSOUZA C
   HASE P
AE ION EXCHANGE INDIA LTD (IONE-Non-standard)
GA 2021A1640P
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestering dissolved carbon dioxide from water involves passing a water feed stream to be purified through a system to obtain water having preset resistivity. The dissolved carbon dioxide is neutralized by the system by reacting calcium carbonate with dissolved carbon dioxide to form soluble calcium bicarbonate, reacting sodium hydroxide with dissolved carbon dioxide to form soluble sodium carbonate, reacting calcium oxide with dissolved carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate, and reacting calcium hydroxide with dissolved carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate.
   USE - Sequestering dissolved carbon dioxide from water for producing high-purity/ultra-pure water (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for sequestering system, which comprises 30 ml sodium hydroxide, 100 ml limestone, 20 ml calcium hydroxide, 100 ml magnesium oxide, 100 ml ion exchange resin and 50 ml marble chips.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The ion exchange resin is a strong base anion exchange resin containing quaternary ammonium group.

PN JP2018202416-A; JP6687075-B2
TI Separating carbon dioxide by reacting gaseous mixture containing carbon dioxide and methane with zeolite film complex which has zeolite film on ceramic support body, allowing carbon dioxide to permeate, and separating carbon dioxide.
AU FUJITA N
   TAKEWAKI T
   OSHIMA K
   SUGITA M
   MIYAGI H
   HAYASHI M
AE MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
GA 201900329Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is separated by reacting gaseous mixture containing carbon dioxide and methane with zeolite film complex which has zeolite film on a ceramic support body, allowing carbon dioxide to permeate, and separating carbon dioxide from gaseous mixture. Zeolite which comprises zeolite film is a chabazite (CHA)-type aluminosilicate, where zeolite film complex is treated with carbon dioxide before separation is carried out.
   USE - Separation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The zeolite film complex has excellent chemical resistance, heat-resistant stability, oxidation resistance, and pressure resistance, and excellent separation of gaseous mixture with high separation factor, high wet heat stability, and high gaseous permeation amount. Moreover, gas is highly efficiently separable at a small-scale installation, making the method energy saving and low cost by using the zeolite film complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Permeance when zeolite film complex is permeated with carbon dioxide gas at 50 degrees C and differential pressure of 0.098 MPa is 1x 10-9-1x 10-7 mol/(m2.s.Pa). Separation factor when zeolite film complex permeate mixed gas of carbon dioxide and methane at 50 degrees C and differential pressure of 0.098 MPa is greater than or equal to 2. Zeolite film has thickness of is less than or equal to 20 mu m. Preferred Conditions: When treating with carbon dioxide, the permeate side of the zeolite film complex is brought to atmospheric pressure. Differential pressure of the gas at a supply side and the gas at a permeate side is 0.02-20 MPa.

AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)

GA 2022267190
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering oil from a reservoir involves                providing a first foam comprising carbon dioxide                (CO2) to the reservoir; providing a second foam to                the reservoir, where the second foam is produced by                alternately injecting into the reservoir: a                gas-mixture comprising CO2 and nitrogen (N2) or CO2                and methane (CH4) or CO2 and N2 and CH4; and a                solution comprising brine and a surfactant; and                recovering oil from the reservoir.
   USE - The methods are useful for recovering oil from                reservoir; and for flooding a reservoir with CO2                foam (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method results in a greater apparent                viscosity of the CO2 as compared to a method that                does not include the step of providing the second                foam to the reservoir. It increases the total sweep                efficiency of the reservoir as compared to a method                that does not include the step of providing the                second foam to the reservoir. The method provides                increased oil recovery as compared to a method that                does not include the step of providing the second                foam to the reservoir. Because some CO2 floods                occur at the higher reservoir pressures, this shows                that diluting the injected CO2 with other gases,                such as N2 or CH4, at reservoir conditions can                increase foam strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for flooding                a reservoir with CO2 foam, which involves providing                a foam to the reservoir, where the foam is produced                by alternately injecting into the reservoir: a                gas-mixture comprising CO2 and N2 or CO2 and CH4 or                CO2 and N2 and CH4; and a solution comprising brine                and a surfactant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Providing the second foam                  to the reservoir is repeated until the recovery                  of the oil from the reservoir reaches an economic                  limit. It                  is formed by alternately injecting a solution                  comprising CO2 and a solution comprising brine                  and a surfactant. The alternate injection of the                  solution comprising CO2 and the solution                  comprising brine and a surfactant is repeated                  until the recovery of the oil from the reservoir                  reaches an economic limit; or the alternate                  injection of the solution comprising CO2 and the                  solution comprising brine and a surfactant is                  performed one time or more than one time. Solution comprising N2 and                  solution comprising brine and a surfactant are                  injected simultaneously. Alternate injections of                  the gas-mixture and the solution are performed                  one time or more than one time. The solution                  comprising N2 and a solution comprising brine and                  a surfactant are injected simultaneously. Preferred                  Components: The gas-mixture comprises CO2 and N2,                  or CO2 and CH4. The gas-mixture or the solution                  is provided as a slug. The solution comprising                  CO2 and the solution comprising brine and a                  surfactant are each provided as slugs. Preferred                  Composition: The gas-mixture comprises 1-99 mol.%                  N2, and 1-99 mol.% CH4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  selected from a nonionic surfactant, an anionic                  surfactant, a zwitterionic surfactant, and                  combinations. Preferred Composition: The                  surfactant comprises 1-15% of the solution.

PN CN113858721-A
TI Antifouling radiating composite board comprises modified straw, polypropylene microporous film, carbon dioxide, copper ion solution, dopamine and isocyanate glue.
AU LIN Z
AE NANJING HEYURUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202210460E
AB    NOVELTY - Antifouling radiating composite board comprises 60-80 pts. wt. modified straw, 4-8 pts. wt. polypropylene microporous film, 30-60 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 30-50 pts. wt. copper ion solution, 20-30 pts. wt. dopamine and 30-100 pts. wt. isocyanate glue. The preparation raw material of the modified straw comprises pre-processing straw, emulsifying agent OP-10, benzoyl peroxide, glycidyl methacrylate and polyethyleneimine.
   USE - Used as antifouling radiating composite board.
   ADVANTAGE - The board has excellent heat-dissipating property and good oil-fouling resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the composite board, comprising (i) mixing the pre-treated straw with water, adding emulsifying agent, glycidyl methacrylate and initiator, stirring and reacting, filtering, drying to obtain pre-modified straw, mixing the polyethylene imine with N, N-dimethylformamide, and adding pre-modified straw, stirring and reacting, filtering, and drying to obtain modified straw, (ii) mixing the modified straw with copper ion solution, allowing to stand, filtering to obtain the mixed modified straw blank, mixing the mixed modified straw blank with organic solvent, and introducing carbon dioxide, and filtering to obtain the mixed modified straw, (iii) mixing the mixed modified straw with isocyanate glue to obtain the core layer, paving the core layer between two polypropylene micro-porous films, and performing hot pressing to obtain the composite plate blank, and (iv) mixing the composite plate blank with the buffer solution, and adding dopamine, mixing and soaking, filtering, drying to obtain the modified composite plate blank, fumigating the modified composite plate blank by acid liquid, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper ion solution is a copper nitrate solution with a mass fraction of 10% or a copper chloride solution with a mass fraction of 10%.Preferred Method: The pre-treated straw is prepared by boiling cotton straws by alkali, peeling and crushing. The preparation method of composite board mainly comprises (i) mixing the pre-treated straw and water according to mass ratio of 1:8-1: 12 in the beaker, and adding emulsifying agent OP-10 of 0.08-0.12 times of pre-treated straw mass into the beaker, pre-processing straw mass 6-8 times of methacrylic acid ethylene oxidic ester and pre-processing straw mass 0.12-0.18 times of benzoyl peroxide, the temperature is 60-70degrees Celsius, the rotating speed is 350-600 revolutions/minute under the condition of stirring and reacting for 1-2 hours, filtering to obtain the pre-modified straw blank, drying the pre-modified straw blank at 60-70degreesC for 2-5 hours to obtain the pre-modified straw, mixing the polyethylene imine and N, N-dimethyl formamide according to mass ratio of 1:10-1:15 in the reaction kettle, and adding pre-modified straw with 2-5 times of polyethylene imine mass into the reaction kettle, stirring and reacting for 7-8 hours at 70degrees Celsius, filtering to obtain the modified straw blank, drying the modified straw blank at 50degrees Celsius to constant weight to obtain the modified straw, (ii) mixing the modified straw with the mass fraction of 10% of copper nitrate solution according to mass ratio of 1:12-1:15, allowing to stand for 3-6 hours at room temperature, filtering to obtain the mixed modified straw blank, mixing the mixed modified straw blank with organic solvent according to mass ratio of 1:8-1: 12 in the flask, and introducing carbon dioxide into the flask at a rate of 30-80 ml/minutes, continuously introducing for 20-60 minutes, and filtering to obtain the mixed modified straw, (iii) mixing the mixed modified straw with isocyanate glue according to mass ratio of 1.2:1-2.0:1 to obtain the core layer, the core layer is paved between two polypropylene microporous film, and hot pressing to obtain the composite plate blank, and (iv) mixing the composite plate blank and the buffer solution according to mass ratio of 1:8-1:15, and adding the composite plate blank mass 0.2-0.3 times of dopamine, mixing and soaking for 3-4 hours under the condition of temperature of 30-40degreesC, filtering to obtain the filter cake, drying the filter cake at 60degrees Celsius to constant weight to obtain the modified composite plate blank, fumigating the modified composite plate blank with sulfuric acid with concentration of 0.1 mol/l for 2-5 hours, and drying to constant weight under the condition of the temperature of 100-120degreesC.In the step (i), the preparation method of the pre-processing straw is mixing the cotton straw and sodium hydroxide solution with mass fraction of 5% according to the mass ratio of 1:50-1: 80, stirring and reacting for 2-3 hours at 80degreesC, filtering, washing until the washing liquid is neutral, obtaining pre-processed straw blank, peeling the pre-processed straw blank, and crushing to obtain pre-processed straw. In the step (ii), the preparation method of the organic solvent is to mix 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:2 to obtain the organic solvent. In the step (iii), the hot-pressing condition is to be 5 MPa prior to pressure, and temperature is hot-pressed 10 minutes at 185degreesC, and then at pressure of 3 MPa, and temperature is hot-pressed 1 minute at 185degreesC.In the step (iv), the preparation method of the buffer solution is to mix tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane and water in a mass ratio of 1:5-1:10, and adjusting the pH to 8.5 with hydrochloric acid with a concentration of 0.1 mol/l to obtain a buffer solution.

   LIEN E
AE SEARAS AS (SEAR-Non-standard)
   GIGANTE SALMON AS (GIGA-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0949A
AB    NOVELTY - Tank system for flow through and recycling                solutions for breeding of marine species, comprises                two water reservoirs; a water reservoir (12) which                has a water velocity of flow arranged for breeding                of marine species, and a water reservoir (10) which                has a similar or lower water velocity of flow than                the water velocity of flow in water reservoir,                adapted for depositing/sedimentation of particles                of a predetermined size.
   USE - The tank system is useful for flow through and                recycling solutions for breeding of marine species                (claimed) such as fish; and for sedimentation of                particles, such as feces, in water.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively replaces carbon dioxide                from carbon dioxide rich fluid with air or oxygen,                so that the habitat of the fish is improved. The                system reduces the need to pump fluid through the                tanks, and the displacement of fluid from the first                tank to the second tank, or between different units                of the same tank, takes place at the same time as                the exchange of carbon dioxide with oxygen in the                fluid. The sedimentation unit or sedimentation tank                provides for sedimentation and removal of some                larger particles, in the area of size/density from                1.5 to 1.2 mm, such that fluid purified of                particles may be returned to the breeding tank or                breeding section of the tank.

   YANG W
   GONG W
   HOU N
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)

GA 202209749V
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises an ethane oxidative dehydrogenation (ODHE) reactor, an ODHE acid-water separating tower, a carbon dioxide absorbing tower, an ODHE alkali washing tower and an ODHE dryer, which are connected in sequence along the main material flow direction. The ODHE reactor is further connected to an ethane product outlet of a methanol to olefin (MTO) system. The ODHE dryer is further connected back to the MTO system and is separated with the MTO product gas.
   USE - The device is useful for increasing the production of ethylene coupled with methanol to olefin process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: is economical; has simple ODHE system process flow; provides a new path for increasing the production; and utilizes the obtained crude product gas in the separation device of the existing MTO system to realize the separation and purification of ethylene in the ODHE crude product gas.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant comprises air, oxygen-enriched and/or pure oxygen. The dilution gas comprises nitrogen, water vapor and/or carbon dioxide. The absorbent comprises water and/or alkaline aqueous solution. The auxiliary deoxidizing gas comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and/or methane. The absorption liquid comprises alcohol amine aqueous solution, potassium carbonate aqueous solution, sulfolane, propylene carbonate, polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether or methanol solution.

PT P
PN CN113428854-A; CN113428854-B
TI Preparing wood-based porous carbon material useful            for carbon dioxide adsorption comprises e.g. taking            Eucalyptus wood powder into three-mouth bottle, adding            delignification solvent, heating, stirring, cooling,            filtering, washing, adding activator to delignified            wood powder and drying.
AU LI Z
   WU M
   LI X
AE UNIV NORTHEAST FORESTRY (UNDB-C)
GA 2021B4204U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing wood-based porous carbon material                comprises (i) taking a specific amount of                Eucalyptus wood powder into a three-mouth bottle,                adding delignification solvent, heating at                90-120&#8451;, stirring at constant temperature                for 4-8 hours, and cooling the mixture at room                temperature, (ii) adding acetone solvent to the                mixture obtained in the step (i), stirring for 4-8                hours, filtering the acetone solvent, washing with                distilled water and vacuumizing drying at                50-70&#8451; for 24-36 hours to obtain                delignified wood powder, (iii) adding activator to                the delignified wood powder obtained in the step                (ii), uniformly mixing, adding distilled water,                uniformly stirring, and drying at 50-70&#8451;                for 24-36 hours to obtain activated wood powder,                and (iv) heating the activated wood powder obtained                in the step (iii) in a tube furnace at                500-900&#8451; under gas protection for 1-3                hours, cooling and washing with hydrochloric acid                solution and distilled water and drying at                50-70&#8451; for 24-36 hours.
   USE - The method is useful preparing wood-based                porous carbon material, which is useful for carbon                dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple steps and low device                requirements. The material: reaches the carbon                dioxide adsorption performance about 97% at                25&#8451; and 1 atm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                wood-based porous carbon material prepared by above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  delignification solvent is prepared by taking                  choline chloride and lactic acid in a molar ratio                  of 1:1-15 under anhydrous condition, dissolving                  choline chloride in a lactic acid solvent and                  heating at 70&#8451; for 1-3 hours. In the                  step (iv), the heating speed of the tubular                  furnace is 5-15&#8451;/minute.Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of Eucalyptus wood powder to                  delignification solvent is 1:1-15. In the step                  (ii), the volume ratio of the mixture to the                  acetone solvent is 1:3-5. In the step (iii), the                  activator comprises potassium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, zinc chloride, or phosphoric acid. The                  mass ratio of delignified wood powder and                  activator potassium hydroxide is 1-10:1. The                  material-to-liquid ratio of delignified wood                  powder and distilled water is 1:2-5 g/ml. In the                  step (iv), the protective gas comprises nitrogen                  or argon. The concentration of the hydrochloric                  acid solution is 0.1 M.

PT P
PN CN113209999-A; CN113209999-B
TI Catalyst used for methane/carbon dioxide dry gas reformation, uses oxide coated with three or less surface ultrathin boron nitride layers as carrier, and metal nickel as active component, where oxide is silica, alumina, magnesium aluminate, magnesia, or zirconia.
AU FU Q
   BAI Y
   BAO X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202194087P
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst uses an oxide coated with 3 or less surface ultrathin boron nitride layers as carrier, and metal nickel as active component. The oxide is silica, alumina, magnesium aluminate, magnesia, or zirconia.
   USE - Catalyst is used for methane/carbon dioxide dry gas reformation to prepare syngas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst comprises hexagonal boron nitride ultrathin coating layer, which enhances the heat conducting performance of oxide carrier and improves the heat transmission efficiency around the nickel particles. Carbon dioxide and methane interact with the defect-rich hexagonal boron nitride layer. The catalyst has excellent carbon deposition resistance, and can be prepared by a simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the catalyst, which involves dissolving a boron-containing precursor in water or alcohol solvent, mixing with the oxide carrier, immersing, drying at 60-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain a carrier precursor modified with boron-containing compound, processing the carrier precursor at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours in atmosphere containing ammonia or nitrogen to obtain oxide coated with ultrathin boron nitride, dissolving a nickel precursor in an aqueous solution, immersing the oxide in the solution, drying for 12-24 hours at 80-120 degrees C to obtain a catalyst precursor, and calcining the precursor for 1-3 hours in a reducing atmosphere to obtain a nickel/oxide-hexagonal boron nitride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The number of ultrathin boron nitride layer is preferably 1-2. The oxide is silica, alumina, or magnesium aluminate. The carrier comprises 0.5-20 %mass, preferably 3-15 %mass nickel. The boron-containing precursor is boric acid, and/or a mixture of boric acid and urea. The oxide contains 0.01-0.5 %mass boron. The concentration of ammonia or nitrogen is 5-100%. The precursor is nitrate, sulfate, chloride and/or acetate of nickel. The reducing atmosphere contains hydrogen or ammonia with a concentration of 5-100%. Preferred Property: The size of nickel nanoparticles is between 2 nm and 5 nm, preferably between 2 nm and 3 nm. Preferred Condition: Calcination is carried out at 500-800 degrees C by heating at 0.5-10 degrees C/minute.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN JP2021075406-A
TI Manufacture of lithium orthosilicate used for forming carbon dioxide absorbent material, involves mixing lithium metasilicate and lithium compound, obtaining reaction precursor containing lithium metasilicate, and baking reaction precursor.
AU OKUBO K
AE NIPPON CHEM IND CO LTD (NIPC-C)
GA 202153599C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of lithium orthosilicate (II) involves mixing lithium metasilicate and lithium compound (I), obtaining reaction precursor containing lithium metasilicate and lithium compound (I), and baking the reaction precursor.
   USE - Manufacture of lithium orthosilicate used for manufacturing absorbent material for absorbing carbon dioxide (claimed) emitted from industry. Uses include but are not limited to chemical processing plant, energy plant, motor vehicle, and exhaust gas from thermal power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy manufacture of lithium orthosilicate having excellent reactivity with lithium compound (I), using X-ray diffraction method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of lithium orthosilicate of formula: Li2SiO4 (II) involves mixing lithium metasilicate and lithium compound of formula: Li2SiO3 (I), obtaining reaction precursor containing lithium metasilicate and lithium compound (I), and baking the reaction precursor. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of lithium metasilicate, which involves adding a liquid (liquid b) in which the lithium compound (2) is dissolved in an aqueous solvent to a liquid (liquid a) in which silicate is dissolved in an aqueous solvent to obtain a reaction material liquid, and heating the reaction material and obtaining metal-lithium silicate, in which in the reaction liquid after adding the whole amount of the (liquid b), the molar ratio of lithium oxide/silica is 1.1 or more, when the lithium element is converted to lithium oxide and silicon element is converted to silica; and
   (2) manufacture of carbon dioxide gas absorbent material, which involves contacting the reaction precursor with a base material, obtaining reaction precursor covering base material, and baking the reaction precursor covering base material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mixing of the lithium metasilicate and lithium compound (I) is performed by a dry-type process. The manufacture of absorbent material involves contacting the material mixed solution, lithium compound (c3), and lithium silicate comprising 0.32 mol or less lithium oxide/silica to obtain material mixture covering base material, heating lithium silicate and lithium compound (c3) to obtain reaction precursor covering base material, and baking material. Preferred Composition: The reaction precursor comprises 0.9-1.1 mol lithium oxide/silica. The liquid (a) comprises 10-40 %mass silicate. The molar ratio of lithium oxide/silica is preferably of 0.32 or less. Preferred Components: The lithium compound (I) is lithium hydroxide and lithium carbonate, preferably lithium hydroxide. The silicate is sodium silicate and/or lithium silicate. The base material is porous alumina. Preferred Conditions: The reaction is performed at 70-95 degrees C, and maintaining for 70-95 degrees C for 0.15 hours or more.

GA 2021588606
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a valve (1), tee, a first elbow (3), a second elbow (4) and a cylinder (5). A three-way pipe (2) that provides a first air inlet, a first air outlet, and a second air outlet. The valve that is connected to the first air inlet. One end of the first elbow that is connected to the first air outlet. The other end of the first elbow is connected to the cylinder. One end of the second elbow is connected to the second air outlet. The other end of the second elbow is connected to the cylinder. The first elbow and the second elbow are arranged on opposite sides of the cylinder.
   USE - Connection structure of single valve and cylinder of supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure balances the thrust of the two sides of the air cylinder from the pipeline, thus ensuring the stability of the cylinder. The two connecting portion adopt horizontal tangential air inlet mode, so that the air flow field is uniform, thus effectively improving the efficiency of the machine set. The three-way pipe is connected with the cylinder through the flange, so that the cylinder is lifted and repaired when mounting and overhauling. The structure is simple to operate, and improves the efficiency of installation and maintenance.
