   FANG X
   YE H
   YANG H
AE GUANGDONG SHENGLU TELECOM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021483107
AB    NOVELTY - Luneburg lens antenna dielectric material                comprises polymer microporous foamed coils with a                cell diameter of 5-50 mu m. The polymer is at least                one of polypropylene, polyethylene, polyvinyl                chloride, polystyrene, polyurethane, and                polycarbonate.
   USE - Luneburg lens antenna dielectric material for                Luneburg lens antenna (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Luneburg lens antenna dielectric material                uses polymer coils as raw materials, and can                accurately control the cell size and distribution                density of the microcellular foamed polymer by                adjusting the pressure of the autoclave, the                temperature of the heating pool/heating time, and                Luneburg lenses with different diameters can be                produced by setting the thickness and number of                turns of the coil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing Luneburg lens antenna dielectric                material, which involves infiltrating polymer coil                with plant fiber material, and then supercritical                carbon dioxide is injected under pressure to                infiltrate and diffuse to obtain a supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid-polymer substrate, taking out                the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid-polymer                substrate to form a bubble core, heating and                growing into cells, cooling and solidifying to                obtain polymer microporous foamed coils, which are                rolled and cut to obtain the Luneburg lens antenna                dielectric material, where polymer is at least one                of polypropylene, polyethylene, polyvinyl chloride,                polystyrene, polyurethane or polycarbonate, the                heating temperature is 30 degrees C-160 degrees C,                and the heating time is 5-20s, cooling is to cool                the temperature to 30 degrees C-160 degrees C, and                the cooling time is 5-10s.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The relative                  dielectric constant of the polymer microporous                  foamed coil material decreases gradually along                  the radial direction, from 2.0 in the center of                  the coil material to 1.0 in the outermost layer                  of the coil material. The plant fiber material is                  one of bamboo fiber, sisal fiber, and jute fiber.                  The pressure of the pressure infiltration is 7-8                  MPa, and the time of the pressure infiltration is                  10-50 min. The heating temperature is 70 degrees                  C-160 degrees C, and the heating time is 5-15s.                  The cooling is to cool the temperature to 60                  degrees C-140 degrees C, and the cooling time is                  7-10s. The cell diameter of the polymer                  microporous foamed coil is 10-50 mu m. The                  gradient of the dielectric constant of the                  adjacent layer from the inside to the outside is                  0.2.

PN MY180665-A
TI Making vegetable oil products comprises extracting            vegetable oil and desired constituents, removing            unwanted constituents and impurities, and manipulating            relative solubilities of constituents and impurities in            supercritical fluid.
AU MAY C Y
   NGAN I M A
   BASIRON T S D Y
   WEI P C
AE MALAYSIAN PALM OIL BOARD (MPOB-C)
GA 2022949660
AB    NOVELTY - Producing vegetable oil products comprising at                least one constituent e.g. monoacylglycerols,                diacylglycerols, triacylglycerols, carotenes,                phytosterols, squalene and free fatty acids,                comprises: selectively extracting, at least one                vegetable oil, and desired constituents without                using an adsorbent, removing unwanted constituents                and impurities by manipulating relative                solubilities of the constituents and impurities in                supercritical carbon dioxide, and manipulating the                relative solubilities of the constituents and                impurities in the supercritical fluid by adjusting                a temperature, a pressure, a ratio of the                supercritical carbon dioxide to feed material, and                a flow rate of the supercritical carbon dioxide,                where the selective extraction of monoacylglycerols                is carried out by using a temperature of 30-80                degrees C, and a pressure of 8-20 MPa (80-200                bar).
   USE - The process is useful for producing vegetable                oil products.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is simple, efficient and free                from hazardous solvents and chemical processes; and                provides vegetable oil products without using an                adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                vegetable oil product from vegetable oil sources                comprising (i) less than or equal to 10 wt.%                diacylglycerols, (ii) greater than or equal to 5                wt.% partial acylglycerols (monoacylglycerols and                diacylglycerols), (iii) less than or equal to 0.1                wt.% carotenes, (iv) less than or equal to 1 wt.%                carotenes, (v) less than or equal to 0.35 wt.%                tocols, (vi) less than or equal to 0.2 wt.%                phytosterols, (vii) less than or equal to 0.25 wt.%                squalene and (viii) 80-100 wt.% free fatty                acids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The selective extraction                  of: diacylglycerols was carried out by using a                  temperature of 30-80 degrees C, and a pressure of                  18-30 MPa (180-300 bar); triacylglycerols was                  carried out by using a temperature of 30-80                  degrees C, and a pressure of 10-40 MPa (100-400                  bar); carotenes was carried out by using a                  temperature of 30-80 degrees C, and a pressure of                  25-40 MPa (250-400 bar); phytosterols was carried                  out by using a temperature of 30-80 degrees C,                  and a pressure of 8-22 MPa (80-220 bar); squalene                  was carried out by using a temperature of 30-80                  degrees C, and a pressure of 8-30 MPa (80-300                  bar); and free fatty acids was carried out by                  using a temperature of 30-80 degrees C, and a                  pressure of 8-30 MPa (80-300 bar). Preferred                  Components: The vegetable oil sources comprise                  palm oil sources or palm kernel oil sources,                  preferably palm oil sources comprising crude palm                  oil, crude palm olein, crude palm stearin, RBD                  (refined, bleached and deodorized) palm oil, RBD                  palm olein, RBD palm stearin or palm oil                  by-products (palm fatty acids distillates).
   EXAMPLE - 30 g Refined, bleached and deodorized palm                olein was loaded into extraction vessel placed in                the column oven. The experiment uses isocratic                condition of 60 degrees C and 24 MPa (240 bar). The                extracts were collected at the outlet of the back                pressure regulator. The extract after 6 hours of                extraction was high in diacylglycerols up to 11%                enriched with other constituents.

PN CN110655961-A; CN210765205-U; WO2021083234-A1
TI Uniform temperature process system for carbon dioxide methanation synthetic natural gas, has steam drum which forms circulating water path through shell side of riser and descender and shell-and-tube type temperature equalization reactor.
AU WANG X
   HE Z
   GAO S
   XIAO T
   XU S
   CHENG A
AE CHINA HUANENG GROUP CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG GROUP CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 202007552L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a second heater (E202) that is connected to an inlet of a tube side of a tubular uniform temperature reactor (R2). The tube-side outlet of the tube-type uniform temperature reactor is connected to the inlet of a fourth heat exchanger (E104) whose outlet is connected to the inlet of a second gas-liquid separator (V104). The gas phase outlet of the second gas-liquid separator is connected with a SNG discharge pipe. The cooling medium inlet of the fourth heat exchanger is connected with a desalinated water inlet pipe. The cooling medium outlet of the fourth heat exchanger is connected to a drum (V101) through a make-up pipe. The steam drum forms a circulating water path through the shell side of the riser and descender and the shell-and-tube type temperature equalization reactor.
   USE - Uniform temperature process system for carbon dioxide methanation synthetic natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The design of the technological system is reasonable, which reduces the number of equipment in the system, and thus, reduces investment, reducing operating difficulties and high production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing synthetic natural gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a overall schematic view of the carbon dioxide methanation-synthesized natural gas process system.
   Fourth heat exchanger (E104)
   Second heater (E202)
   Tubular uniform temperature reactor (R2)
   Drum (V101)
   Second gas-liquid separator (V104)
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN110559684-A; CN110559684-B
TI Two-stage separation device for extracting oil ingredient in oily solid waste by using supercritical carbon dioxide, has check valve connected to first-stage separation extraction reactor and second-stage separation extraction reactor.
AU JIA W
   SONG S
   LI C
   WU X
   YANG F
   SONG X
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2019A6754B
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a high pressure gas cylinder (16) for passing through a pressure reducing valve (8) and a gas mass flow controller (4). A first check valve (12) is connected with a tubular supercritical extraction reactor (24), a first-stage separation extraction reactor (30) and a first pressure gage (1). A second pressure gage (9) and a pressure monitor (18) are connected with a system. A distribution pipe is connected with the supercritical extraction reactor. A second check valve (36) is connected to a second-stage cyclonic separation extraction reactor (28) and a first-stage separation extraction reactor to realize continuous extraction and separation of oil-containing solid waste.
   USE - Two-stage separation device for extracting oil ingredient in oily solid waste by using supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can switch between primary and secondary extraction processes according to oil content of solid waste to achieve two-stage oil-based solid waste so as to reduce processing cost, thus implementing mobile-drill processing of oily solid waste in petrochemical industry. The device is easy to use and convenient to operate, and has high automation degree.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a two-stage separation device for extracting oil ingredient in oily solid waste by using supercritical carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN110465019-A; CN110465019-B
TI Self-spraying fire extinguishing ball, has air chamber and spray channel are glued with thin film, where air chamber drives thin film to expand when foaming agent reaches minimum thermal decomposition temperature.
AU XIA H
AE XIA H (XIAH-Individual)
   GU H (GUHH-Individual)
GA 2019990820
AB    NOVELTY - The ball has a spherical body glued with a spherical frame (3) that is a spherical structure. A middle part of the spherical frame is fixed to a sphere (6). The spherical frame is annularly provided with multiple ejecting channel points (10). An opening of the injection channel is provided with a needle (8). A side wall of the injection channel is opened with an air chamber (5). A side of the air chamber is formed with a material cavity (4) that is filled with foaming agent. The air chamber and the spray channel are glued with a thin film (9) that is filled with fire extinguishing dry powder, where the decomposed air chamber drives the thin film to expand when the foaming agent reaches minimum thermal decomposition temperature.
   USE - Self-spraying fire extinguishing ball.
   ADVANTAGE - The ball can support the film through decomposition of carbon dioxide gas by using the characteristic of decomposing foaming agent when the film ruptures and can drive the ball to pop up under reaction force, so that the sodium bicarbonate and carbon dioxide are sprayed out to extinguish the fire. The ball is convenient for storage, and avoids dangerous trigger mechanism such as a lead is required, and the decomposition products such as sprayed carbon dioxide are harmless and environmentally friendly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a self-spraying fire extinguishing ball.
   Spherical frame (3)
   Material cavity (4)
   Air chamber (5)
   Sphere (6)
   Needle (8)
   Thin film (9)
   Ejecting channel points (10)

TI Extracorporeal therapy system, has control unit            operated with dialysis fluid preparation structure and            blood pump, and programmed to prepare gas generation            fluid different than dialysis fluid for extracorporeal            therapy treatment.
AU HOBRO S
   STERNBY J
   LINDEN T
   JANSSON O
   HANSSON P
AE GAMBRO LUNDIA AB (GAMB-C)
   GAMBRO LUNDIA AB (GAMB-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The system (10a) has a dialysis fluid circuit                (30) including dialysis fluid preparation structure                (22, 24, 26, 32, 34, 36, 38, 40, 42, 44, 46, 48) to                prepare dialysis fluid for extracorporeal therapy                treatment. A blood circuit (100) includes a blood                filter (102) for use during the extracorporeal                therapy treatment. A blood pump (112) is operated                to pump blood through the blood circuit and the                blood filter. A control unit (20) is operated with                the dialysis fluid preparation structure and the                blood pump. The control unit is programmed to                prepare gas generation fluid different than the                dialysis fluid for the extracorporeal therapy                treatment, where the gas generation fluid generates                carbon dioxide gas, and the carbon dioxide gas is                used to prime the blood circuit including the blood                filter.
   USE - Extracorporeal therapy system.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an                employing carbon dioxide gas generation and priming                device.

PT P
PN CN109535270-A; CN109535270-B
TI Preparing Anji white tea polysaccharide useful for preparing medicine for treating human cytomegalovirus infection, comprises using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method, and carrying out extraction.
AU MAO G
   ZHANG Z
   WANG S
   WAN X
   XU X
   SU H
   WANG X
   CHEN S
   DAI J
AE ZHEJIANG HOSPITAL (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201931033F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Anji white tea polysaccharide comprises using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method, carrying out extraction at 65-85 degrees C for 80-100 minutes, under pressure 20.0-35.0 MPa, where the cosolvent ethanol concentration is 85-95%.
   USE - The Anji white tea polysaccharide is useful for preparing medicine for treating human cytomegalovirus infection.
   ADVANTAGE - The Anji white tea polysaccharide: has obvious anti-HCMV, exhibits remarkable inhibit HCMV host cells in vitro, human embryonic lung fibroblast HCMV gene IE1/2, expression of UL44, which effectively inhibits HCMV DNA replication, obviously improves cell lesion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the Anji white tea polysaccharide comprising (i) taking Anji white tea, summer and autumn leaves, drying in oven at 70 degrees C, moderately pulverizing, adding absolute ethanol immersion, refluxing extraction at 90 degrees C for 2 hours to degreasing, (ii) suction filtrating, collecting residue, drying in a vacuum drying oven and passing through a 40 mesh sieve, (iii) taking screened Anji white tea powder into the extraction kettle, where the cosolvent flow rate is 0.5ml.min1, carbon dioxide flow rate is 2l.min1, outlet valve temperature is at 70 degrees C, the extraction time is 80-100 minutes, the extraction pressure is 20.0-35.0 MPa, the extraction temperature is at 65-85 degrees C, and the cosolvent ethanol concentration is 85-95%, and (iv) dialyzing and desalting the extract, and adding with absolute ethanol for 3 times, stirring the mixture for overnight, adding absolute ethanol to precipitate and washing twice, and centrifuging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In step (iii), the outlet pressure of the carbon dioxide storage tank is 5-6 MPa, and the temperature is at 25-30 degrees C at room temperature.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

GA 2019079912

One side of second sealed tank (2) is connected to an external water source through a d-segment heat pipe (11). One side of third sealed tank (3) is connected to second sealed tank through a heat-conducting pipe (8). The heat pipe is directly connected to first sealed tank. The first and third sealed tanks are connected to a circulation pump (5). The third sealed tank is connected to a heat transfer tube section.
   USE - High temperature steam preparing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The problem that the heat pump does not produce high temperature and high pressure steam is solved. The heat transfer oil is used as the heat exchange medium and high boiling point medium during high temperature and high pressure liquid state. The annular pipe is provided in closed tank to fully absorb heat, to increase the surface area, and to increase the heat exchange rate when the carbon dioxide is heated to 200 degree Celsius.

PT P
PN WO2019008261-A1; FR3068700-A1; FR3068700-B1; EP3649187-A1; EP3649187-B1
TI Production of component made of polymer material, involves contacting component made of polymer material with organometallic complex, and contacting component with fluid comprising carbon dioxide and transforming complex to metal carbonate.
AU PONCELET O
   GETTO D
AE COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
GA 2019046346
AB    NOVELTY - Production of component made of polymer material and having fireproof properties, involves providing a component made of polymer material and introducing the component into a reaction chamber, contacting the component with an organometallic complex (I) and a fluid (f1) under a pressure and a temperature such that the fluid (f1) is in the supercritical state and incorporating the organometallic complex (I) into the component, and contacting the component with a fluid (f2) comprising carbon dioxide under a pressure and a temperature such that the fluid (f2) is in the supercritical state and transforming the organometallic complex (I) into metal carbonate (II).
   USE - Production of component made of polymer material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple production of component made of polymer material with improved fireproof properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of component made of polymer material and having fireproof properties, involves providing a component made of polymer material and introducing the component into a reaction chamber, contacting the component with an organometallic complex of formula: MmRn (I) and a fluid (f1) under a pressure and a temperature such that the fluid (f1) is in the supercritical state and incorporating the organometallic complex (I) into the component, and contacting the component with a fluid (f2) comprising carbon dioxide under a pressure and a temperature such that the fluid (f2) is in the supercritical state and transforming the organometallic complex (I) into metal carbonate of formula: Mm(CO3)n (II).
   M = metal chosen from aluminum, magnesium, zinc, manganese, cesium and strontium;
   m,n = more than 0;
   R = linear or branched 1-4C alkyls ort SiR';and
   R' = linear or branched 1-4C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fluid (f1) is ethane, propane, ethylene or propylene. The co-solvent is chosen from ether, xylene and limonene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method involves contacting the component with a solvent chosen from water, alcohol and water/alcohol mixtures for converting the metal carbonates to metal hydroxide of formula: Mm(OH)n, and adding an oxidizer trap such as an alkylphosphate or an alkylphosphinate during contacting the component with the fluid (f2) or contacting the component with the solvent. The metal carbonates are in excess with respect to solvent so as to obtain the component comprising metal carbonates and metal hydroxides. The step of contacting the component with the organometallic complex (I) is carried out in the presence of a co-solvent added to the supercritical fluid (f1). The organometallic complex (I) is synthesized by reacting a metal halide of formula: MmXn with an organoalkaline of formula: AR (a) in the reaction chamber or in a secondary chamber connected to the reaction chamber. The thermoplastic polymer is chosen from polyolefins such as polyethylene and polypropylene, and polyamides such as polyamide 11 or polyamide 12.X = halo, preferably Cl, Br or I;andA = Li, Na, or K.Preferred Composition: The polymer is a thermosetting polymer or a thermoplastic polymer.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A08-F; A08-R; A11-A03

AU MORI T
   NISHIDA Y
   SHONO E
   SHONO M
   SEN J
AE HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI SHIP & ENG CO LTD (HITF-C)
GA 201861414K
AB    NOVELTY - A methanation reaction catalyst is a sintered                material of a wet mixture of zirconia and/or a salt                of zirconium, a salt of a stabilizing element, a                salt of nickel and an inorganic oxide. The                stabilizing element is chosen from yttrium,                lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium,                samarium, gadolinium, dysprosium, calcium,                magnesium, manganese, iron and cobalt. The                inorganic oxide is chosen from silica, alumina,                titania and ceria. The molar ratio of nickel to                silicon is 0.2-10, when the inorganic oxide is                silica. The molar ratio of nickel to aluminum is                0.2-7, when inorganic oxide is alumina. The molar                ratio of nickel to titanium is 0.3-10.5, when the                inorganic oxide is titania. The molar ratio of                nickel and cerium is 0.7-25, when the inorganic                oxide is ceria.
   USE - Methanation reaction catalyst for methanation                of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and                hydrogen and producing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic activity,                and improves methane production rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of methane, which involves                contacting the catalyst with a mixed gas containing                carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen                gas, and reacting at 200 degrees C or more;                and
   (2) manufacture of methanation reaction                catalyst, which involves performing wet blending of                zirconium and/or zirconia, salt of the                stabilization element, salt of nickel and inorganic                oxide, and calcining at 400-800 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  zirconium to silicon is 0.1-4.5, when the                  inorganic oxide is silica. The molar ratio of                  zirconium to aluminum is 0.1-4, when inorganic                  oxide is alumina. The molar ratio of zirconium to                  titanium is 0.15-6, when the inorganic oxide is                  titania. The molar ratio of zirconium and cerium                  is 0.3-12, when the inorganic oxide is ceria. The                  content of the inorganic oxide is 5-62 %mass with                  respect to total salt of zirconium and/or                  zirconia, salt of the stabilization element, salt                  of nickel and inorganic oxide. Preferred                  Property: The catalyst has several pores and                  total volume of pores having fine diameter of                  2-12 nm of 0.05 cm3/g or more per unit                  mass.

PN CN108373407-A; CN108373407-B
TI New fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based ethylbenzene used as highly effective cosolvent and multifunctional intermediate for preparing high performance fluoromonomers and functional materials e.g. fluoropolymers.
AU CHEN J
   YANG Z
   LIU Z
   YANG L
   SHEN S
   JIANG J
   HAO Z
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2018624723
AB    NOVELTY - A fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based ethylbenzene (I) is new.
   USE - New fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based ethylbenzene (I) used as highly effective cosolvent and multifunctional intermediate for preparing high performance fluoromonomers and functional materials e.g. fluoropolymers.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based ethylbenzene is prepared using simple method with high yield, has excellent solubility in supercritical carbon dioxide medium, can be miscible with supercritical carbon dioxide under mild conditions, and has less transformation pressure in supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based ethylbenzene of formula (I) is new.n = 1-15.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based ethylbenzene (I). The process involves adding tetrahydrofuran and catalytic amount of iodine to magnesium powder, adding p-bromoethylbenzene in drops, reacting at room temperature for 1-3 hours under inert gas protection to obtain p-ethylphenyl magnesium bromide solution, preparing p-ethylphenyl cuprous solution under an inert gas protection and ice bath conditions, by adding the p-ethylphenyl magnesium bromide solution to a suspension of cuprous chloride in tetrahydrofuran, and reacting at room temperature for 1-2 hours, and preparing the compound (I) using inert gas protection and ice bath conditions, by adding fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based acyl halide of formula: R'fCOX (II), in drops to the p-ethylphenyl cuprous solution, reacting at room temperature for 4-6 hours, hydrolyzing the reaction product, and performing separation and purification.
   X = F or Cl;and
   R'f = group of formula (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the p-bromoethylbenzene, magnesium powder, cuprous chloride, and fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based acyl halide (II), is 1:1.1-1.3:1-1.3:0.8-1.2.

PN CN108338988-A
TI Sodium bicarbonate Ringer injection comprises hydrated tannic acid as first pH adjuster, and carbon dioxide as second pH adjuster.
AU ZHANG D
   ZHANG Z
   ZHAN F
   WANG W
   SHI X
   LIN L
   ZHAO D
   HU S
   WANG L
   WANG J
AE SICHUAN KELUN DRUG RES INST CO LTD (SCKL-C)
GA 201861940L
AB    NOVELTY - Sodium bicarbonate Ringer injection comprises first pH adjuster and second pH adjuster, where the first pH adjuster is hydrated tannic acid, the second pH adjuster is carbon dioxide, the pH of the injection before sterilization is 6.5-6.8, and the concentration of bicarbonate after sterilization is 26.6-29.4 nmol/l.
   USE - Used as sodium bicarbonate Ringer injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases stability of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing Ringer injection, comprising (i) injecting required amount of 8-12% of the total amount of water for injection into the first preparation container at 80-85 degrees C, (ii) turning on stirring, and adding sodium chloride, potassium chloride, magnesium chloride hexahydrate and calcium chloride dehydrate, (iii) completely dissolving above materials, adding hydrated tannic acid and sodium citrate, (iv) before dissolving, adding activated carbon of 0.01-0.05% of the prepared product quality for adsorption, (v) cooling the preparation liquid in the first preparation container until the temperature drops below 40 degrees C, (vi) adding 4-55% of the total required amount of water for injection to the second preparation container at a temperature lower than 40 degrees C, (vii) adding sodium bicarbonate into the second preparation container, and stirring and dissolving, (viii) completely dissolving the sodium bicarbonate material, adding the preparation liquid in the first preparation container into the second preparation container, and uniformly stirring mixture, (ix) adding water for injection below 40 degrees C to the required total amount to the second preparation container, (x) adjusting the pH value of the preparation liquid to between 6.5-6.8 by introducing carbon dioxide gas, and (xi) filtrating, filling, sealing and sterilizing composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the hydrated citric acid is from 0.13-0.15 g/l. The solvent of the sodium bicarbonate Ringer injection is water for injection. The Ringer injection further comprises 5.6-5.9 g/l (preferably 5.84 g/l) sodium chloride, 0.29-0.31 g/l (preferably 0.3 g/l) potassium chloride, 0.19-0.20 g/l (preferably 0.20 g/l) magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.21-0.23 g/l (preferably 0.22 g/l) calcium chloride dihydrate, 2.34-2.36 g/l (preferably 2.35 g/l) sodium bicarbonate, 0.18-0.22 g/l (preferably 0.2 g/l) sodium citrate, and 0.14g/l hydrated citric acid. Preferred Method: The filtering process is 5 level filtering. The first stage of filtration uses 19 titanium rods. The pore size is 30 mu m, the second stage of filtration uses 7 polypropylene filters, the pore size is 0.45 mu m, the third stage of filtration uses 7 polypropylene filters, the pore size is 0.22 mu m, the fourth stage of filtration uses 5 polyethersulfone filters, the pore size is 0.22 mu m, the terminal filter uses 5 polyethersulfone filters, The hole diameter is 0.22 mu m. The interval between the end of step (x) and the end of step (xi) must not exceed 4 hours. The method further comprises sterilization, (xii) carrying out light inspection and set of barrier bags. The barrier bag is filled with a gas having a carbon dioxide concentration of 8-12%. The sterilization is carried out at 115-125 degrees C for 8-40 minutes, F0 value is 8-30.

PN CN107981148-A
TI Selenium-enriched dragon fruit granule is prepared by soaking peeled fresh dragon fruit in salt water and lemon juice, drying and grinding fruit into powder, mixing dragon fruit powder, lemon powder, selenium yeast flake powder with lactose.
AU LI X
   LI G
AE DANYANG SANMU FRUIT CO LTD (DANY-Non-standard)
GA 2018367401
AB    NOVELTY - Selenium-enriched dragon fruit granule is prepared by peeling fresh dragon fruit, soaking peeled fresh dragon fruit in 3% salt water, washing soaked dragon fruit with water, soaking dragon fruit in lemon juice, subjecting dragon fruit to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid treatment system, extracting water in dragon fruit obtain dried dragon fruit, grinding dried dragon fruit using grinder into powder to obtain a dragon fruit powder, mixing dragon fruit powder, lemon powder, selenium yeast flake powder and lactose and dispersing content.
   USE - As selenium-enriched dragon fruit granule.
   ADVANTAGE - The selenium-enriched dragon fruit granule is easy for absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Selenium-enriched dragon fruit granule is prepared by (a) peeling fresh dragon fruit, soaking peeled fresh dragon fruit in 3% salt water, washing soaked dragon fruit with water, soaking dragon fruit in lemon juice, subjecting dragon fruit to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid treatment system, extracting water in dragon fruit using a supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide to obtain dried dragon fruit, grinding dried dragon fruit using grinder into a powder to obtain a dragon fruit powder, (b) juicing a fresh lemon, and concentrating lemon juice at a low temperature to obtain a lemon powder in a solid state and grinding into a powder using grinder to obtain lemon powder and (c) mixing dragon fruit powder, lemon powder, selenium yeast flake powder and lactose and dispersing content by a high-speed disperser to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (a), soaking dragon fruit in salt water for is 3-5 minutes and soaking dragon fruit in lemon juice for 3-5 minutes. The lemon juice further comprises 15 wt.% ginger juice. In the step (c), weighing 60-100 parts dragon fruit powder, 1-3 parts lemon powder, 10-15 parts selenium yeast powder and 15-20 parts lactose. Preferred Component: The lactose is granulated anhydrous lactose.

AE RONGCHENG FEICHUANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 201831548X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing natto capsule involves soaking the soybeans in water, steaming the soybeans, removing portion of the liquid, aseptically inoculating Bacillus subtilis in the steamed soybeans, fermenting the mixture, crushing the fermented mixture into a slurry, adding sodium hydroxide, passing carbon dioxide through the slurry, stirring, filling the slurry into the soft capsule shell to obtain a soft capsule.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing natto capsule which is used for preparing natto healthcare food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of soft capsule which has high quality and excellent aroma and nattokinase activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing natto capsule involves (a) soaking the soybeans in water for 15-20 hours, steaming the soybeans for 30-40 minutes at 0.1-0.15 MPa and then removing portion of the liquid such that the weight ratio of the soybeans and the liquid is 1:(1-1.5), (b) aseptically inoculating Bacillus subtilis in the steamed soybeans at 80-90 degrees C and fermenting the mixture at 37-40 degrees C for 20-24 hours, (c) crushing the fermented mixture into a slurry and adding sodium hydroxide until the pH reaches 9.5-10.5, (d) passing carbon dioxide through the slurry obtained in step (c) in an ultra-high pressure reactor, stirring at 25-30 MPa, holding for 20-40 minutes and then withdrawing the pressure and (e) filling the slurry obtained in step (d) into the soft capsule shell to obtain a soft capsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In step (a), soaking soybeans for 18 hours, steaming the soybeans for 35 minutes at 0.13 MPa and removing a portion of the liquid such that the weight ratio of the soybeans and the liquid is 1:1.25. The step (a) further involves adding 20-50 g/l tyrosine, 5-10 g/l lignosulfonate and 5-10 g/l sodium chloride to the steamed soybeans. In step (b), aseptically inoculating 1-10x 108 colony-forming unit (CFU)/g B.subtilis in the steamed soybeans at 80-90 degrees C and fermenting the mixture at 38 degrees C for 22 hours. In step (c), adding sodium hydroxide until the pH reaches 10. The step (c) further involves introducing nitrogen into the slurry while ultrasonically processing the slurry for 10-30 minutes under vacuum. In step (d), stirring at 27 MPa and holding for 30 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN107746458-A
TI Preparing room temperature curing cyclic carbonate prepolymer used in preparing nonisocyanurate polyurethane coating, involves adding epoxy resin to autoclave, adding catalyst, and maintaining carbon dioxide at pressure.
AU QU J
   WU Z
   DENG K
   CHEN R
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   CARPOLY CHEM GROUP CO LTD (CARP-Non-standard)
GA 201821057L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing room temperature curing cyclic carbonate prepolymer involves adding epoxy resin to the autoclave, adding catalyst, maintaining carbon dioxide at a pressure of 0.6-3.0 MPa to carrying out the reaction, controlling reaction temperature at 80-150 degrees C. The amount of catalyst added is 0.5-2.0% epoxy resin mass, reacted for 4-24 hours. The catalyst is mixtures of tetrabutylammonium bromide, lithium chloride, lithium bromide and tetrabutylammonium chloride, added organic solvent and cyclocarbonate into reactor, stirred, and dissolved, added diamine.
   USE - Method for preparing room temperature curing cyclic carbonate prepolymer used in preparing nonisocyanurate polyurethane coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare room temperature curing cyclic carbonate prepolymer is environment friendly coating with excellent property, which has high hardness, excellent impact resistance and chemical resistance, and replaces traditional polyurethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing room temperature curing cyclic carbonate prepolymer involves adding epoxy resin to the autoclave, adding catalyst, maintaining carbon dioxide at a pressure of 0.6-3.0 MPa to carrying out the reaction, controlling reaction temperature at 80-150 degrees C. The amount of catalyst added is 0.5-2.0% epoxy resin mass, reacted for 4-24 hours. The catalyst is mixtures of tetrabutylammonium bromide, lithium chloride, lithium bromide and tetrabutylammonium chloride, added organic solvent and cyclocarbonate into reactor, stirred, and dissolved, added diamine, stirred, and heated at 80-130 degrees C, refluxed for 2-8 hours, cooled out and saved, added antifoaming agent to removed foam. The amount of diamine accounts is 5-15% mass of the cyclocarbonate prepolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The epoxy resin is autoclave further comprises adding 0-100% dimethylformamide solvent of the epoxy resin mass. Preferred Components: The epoxy resin is E12, E20, E42, E-44 and E-51. The diamine is ethylenediamine, butylenediamine, hexamethylenediamine, octyldiamine, dianthraquinone, isophoronediamines, triethylenetetramines, and tetraethylenepentamines. The organic solvent is 1-ethyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide, methylformamide, 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, and nitrile, and organic solvent is added in an amount of 100-150% by mass of the cyclocarbonate. Preferred Conditions: The prepolymer is a brown liquid with a solid content of 30-60% and viscosity of 20-100 mPas, storage stability is 1 year, curing temperature is room temperature, air humidity is below 70%. Preferred Conditions: The hardness of the coating film is 2-4H, the impact resistance is 50cm, and the adhesion is 0 grade, flexibility through a diameter of 1mm, and gloss greater than 90. The anti-foaming agent is BYK-R605, TEGO Airex 900 and TEGO Airex 962 or mixture of 0.5-1% of the mass of the coating raw material, and polyethyleneimine curing agent has molecular weights of 400, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, and 10000.

TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide gas cooling and recycling device for Brayton cycle power generation system, has outer spiral groove extends to outer edge of sealing plate to form high pressure area on outer side of sealing plate for inhibiting ultra-critical carbon dioxide leakage.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a rotating shaft arranged with an impeller power output shaft (8). A cooling ring structure is provided with an air inlet channel and a recovery channel. The air inlet channel and the recovery channel are respectively connected with a cooling cavity (15) and a working medium circulating gas circuit. Two sealing plates are fixedly arranged on the rotating shaft and arranged corresponding to two ends of the cooling ring sleeve structure. Each sealing plate is provided with a sealing side surface that is connected with the cooling ring structure to form an assembling gap (14). The sealing side surface is provided with an outer spiral groove, where an outer end opening of the outer spiral groove extends to an outer edge of the sealing plate to form a high pressure area on an outer side of the sealing plate for inhibiting ultra-critical carbon dioxide leakage when the sealing plate rotates along with the rotating shaft.
   USE - Ultra-critical carbon dioxide gas cooling and recycling device for a Brayton cycle power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can solve problem of affecting rolling support bearing working due to transmitting steam turbine temperature to a rolling supporting bearing through the rotating shaft, reduce dynamic pressure characteristic of gas by an annular pressure reducing groove, improve system stability, provide convenience to realize axial location of the sealing plate by a clamping shaft sleeve and form the high pressure area at an inner side of the sealing plate when the sealing side surface of the sealing plate is provided with a reverse inner spiral groove so as to effectively inhibit the gas from entering into a working medium circulation gas path, thus inhibiting ultra-critical carbon dioxide leakage.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated beverage comprises 900-988 mg/g                water; 3-60 mg/g ethanol; 0.2-8 mg/g dissolved                carbon dioxide; 0-4 mg/g protein; ethyl acetate in                a concentration of 1 -20 mg per gram of ethanol;                isoamyl acetate in a concentration of 0.1-5 mg per                gram of ethanol; and 3-5C alcohols in a                concentration of 1.5-50 mg per gram of ethanol.    USE - The scrubber water is useful for preparing                beverage (claimed). The carbonated beverage is                useful as beer, cider, or wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated alcoholic beverage having a                very pleasant taste can be produced from the                aqueous liquid that is obtained when the carbon                dioxide stream that is generated during                decarbonation of alcoholic beer is passed through                water (scrubbing).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) producing a carbonated beverage which                involves providing a yeast fermented liquid                comprising greater than or equal to 1.5 vol.%                ethanol and volatile flavor components; subjecting                the yeast fermented liquid to a decarbonation step                in which a gaseous component comprising carbon                dioxide, ethanol and volatile flavor components is                removed from the yeast fermented liquid; contacting                the gaseous component comprising carbon dioxide,                ethanol and volatile flavor components with an                aqueous liquid to transfer at least a portion of                the ethanol and the volatile flavor components from                the gaseous component to the aqueous liquid, thus                producing scrubber water; optionally diluting the                scrubber water; and carbonating the optionally                diluted scrubber water; and
   (2) use of scrubber water obtained by                decarbonation of a yeast fermented beverage for                preparing a beverage containing 0-4 mg/g of starch                hydrolysis components selected from maltose,                maltotriose, maltotetraose and combination,                involving combining the scrubber water with one or                more components selected from carbon dioxide,                sweetener, food acid, fruit juice, fruit juice                concentrate, essential oil and combinations; where                the scrubber water contains: 890-990 mg/g water;                5-100 mg/g ethanol; 0-0.1 mg/g non-volatile                components selected from monosaccharides,                disaccharides, oligosaccharides, polysaccharides,                amino acids, dipeptides, oligopeptides,                polypeptides and combinations; ethyl acetate in a                concentration of 1 -20 mg per gram of ethanol;                isoamyl acetate in a concentration of 0.1-5 mg per                gram of ethanol; and 3-5C alcohols in a                concentration of 1.5-50 mg per gram of ethanol;                where ethyl acetate and isoamyl acetate are present                in a weight ratio that is within 2:1-30:1.

The beverage                  contains 0-4 mg/g of starch hydrolysis components                  selected from maltose, maltotriose, maltotetraose                  and combinations. The carbonated beverage                  preferably contains 0-1 mu g/g hop acids selected                  from alpha -acids, iso- alpha acids and                  combinations; 0-3 mg/g protein; acetaldehyde in a                  concentration of 0.1-5 mg per gram of ethanol;                  dimethyl sulfide in a concentration of 1-20 mu g                  per gram of ethanol; amyl alcohols in a                  concentration of 1-50 mg per gram of ethanol, the                  amyl alcohols being selected from                  3-methylbutan-1-ol, 2-methylbutan-1-ol and                  combinations; and isobutanol in a concentration                  of 0.2-5 mg per gram of ethanol. The                  yeast fermented beverage has a temperature of                  10-80 degrees C when it is subjected to the                  decarbonation step.

PN CN111298753-A; CN212142628-U
TI Device used for producing continuous carbonized synthetic calcium carbonate has first-level venturi injector connected to first-level pipe spiral reactor, primary pipe spiral reactor, secondary venturi ejector and discharge end.
AU YAN P
   WANG Z
   ZHU J
AE HANGZHOU ZHENGHE NANOMETER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU ZHENGHE NANOMETER TECHNOLOGY CO (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202059121L
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a first-level venturi injector, whose output end is connected to the input end of a first-level pipe spiral reactor. The output end of a primary pipe spiral reactor is connected to the input end of a secondary venturi ejector, which is connected to the input end of a secondary pipe spiral reactor. The output end of the secondary pipe spiral reactor is connected to the input end of a tertiary venturi ejector. A discharge end of the three-stage venturi ejector is connected to the feed end of a two-stage spiral reactor.
   USE - The device is useful for producing continuous carbonized synthetic calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: realizes continuous, dataized and automated production; has low power consumption, high kiln gas carbon dioxide absorption rate, multi-group combination, high output, easy cleaning and cleaning and small area occupation; and is economical and convenient to install.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing continuous carbonized synthetic calcium carbonate using device comprising allowing the refined lime milk to enter the first-level venturi ejector at the set flow rate and pressure, allowing the kiln gas carbon dioxide to enter the first-level venturi ejector at the set flow rate and pressure, allowing the lime milk and kiln gas carbon dioxide to enter the first-stage spiral reactor under the action of the first-level venturi ejector, stirring the liquid at the set speed using propeller blades of the first-stage pipeline spiral reactor under the set pressure, fully synthesizing lime milk and kiln gas carbon dioxide, pressurizing the mixed liquid of the carbonization reaction in the first-level pipeline spiral reactor by the first-level pressure pump into the second-level venturi ejector, allowing the supplementary kiln gas carbon dioxide to enter into the secondary pipeline spiral reactor under the effect of the secondary venturi ejector, performing the carbonization reaction at the set pressure, pressurizing the mixed liquid of carbonization reaction in the secondary pipeline spiral reactor into the tertiary venturi ejector through the secondary pressure pump, allowing the supplementary kiln gas carbon dioxide to enter the three-stage pipeline spiral reactor under the action of the three-stage venturi ejector, carrying out the carbonization reaction under the set pressure to obtain the finished slurry and pressurizing and discharging the finished slurry to the designated position by a three-stage pressure pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device is useful for producing continuous carbonized synthetic calcium carbonate.

PT P
PN US2018298409-A1
TI Biological and chemical process for the capture and conversion of e.g. carbon dioxide, into organic compounds, by fixing carbon dioxide into organic compounds via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction using chemoautotrophic microorganisms.
AU REED J S
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 201880723N
AB    NOVELTY - Biological and chemical process for the capture and conversion of carbon dioxide (CO2) and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds, involves introducing CO2 gas, either alone and/or dissolved in a mixture or solution further comprising carbonate ion and/or bicarbonate ion, and/or introducing inorganic carbon contained in a solid phase into an environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotroph cell extracts; and fixing the CO2 and/or inorganic carbon into organic compounds within the environment via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing obligate and/or facultative chemoautotrophic microorganisms. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically and/or are introduced into the environment from source external to the environment.
   USE - The biological and chemical process is useful for the capture and conversion of CO2 and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process can enable the effective capture of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere or from a point source of carbon dioxide emissions for the production of liquid transportation fuel and/or other organic chemical products, which can help address greenhouse gas induced climate change and contribute to the domestic production of renewable liquid transportation fuels without any dependence upon agriculture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biological and chemical process for the capture and conversion of carbon dioxide (CO2) and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds, involves introducing CO2 gas, either alone and/or dissolved in a mixture or solution further comprising carbonate ion and/or bicarbonate ion, and/or introducing inorganic carbon contained in a solid phase into an environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotroph cell extracts; and fixing the CO2 and/or inorganic carbon into organic compounds within the environment via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing obligate and/or facultative chemoautotrophic microorganisms. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically and/or are introduced into the environment from source external to the environment. Biomass and/or biochemicals are produced by the chemosynthetic reaction. The biomass and/or biochemicals are separated from the environment and are processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Processes: Alternatively, capture and conversion of CO2 and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds, involves introducing a carbon source in the form of flue gas comprising CO2 and/or in the form of an aqueous solution comprising inorganic carbon into an environment in a bioreactor that is suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic microorganisms; introducing an electron donor that is separate from the carbon source into the environment in the bioreactor; and fixing the CO2 in the flue gas and/or inorganic carbon in the aqueous solution into the organic compounds within the environment in the bioreactor via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing chemoautotrophic microorganisms and using electron donor and electron acceptor; and where the electron donor and/or the electron acceptor are generated and/or refined from inorganic chemical, where the electron donor is generated separately from the carbon source and externally to the bioreactor using a renewable, alternative, or low CO2 emission power source, where the electron donor is molecular hydrogen that is generated using the power source, through electrolysis of water, where biomass and/or biochemicals are produced by the chemosynthetic reaction, and where the biomass and/or biochemicals are separated from the environment and are processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms. The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a carbon source for a large scale fermentation and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms, where the large scale fermentation and/or the other microbes or organisms produce commercial enzymes, antibiotics, amino acids, vitamins, and bioplastics. The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a nutrient source for the growth of fish. Molecular hydrogen acts as an electron donor. The hydrogen is generated through electrolysis of water and/or thermochemical splitting of water. Electrolysis of water involves proton exchange membranes (PEM); a liquid electrolyte; high-pressure electrolysis; and high temperature electrolysis of steam (HTES). The thermochemical splitting of water involves iron oxide cycle; cerium(IV) oxide-cerium(III) oxide cycle; zinc zinc-oxide cycle; sulfur-iodine cycle; copper-chlorine cycle; calcium-bromine-iron cycle; and hybrid sulfur cycle. Hydrogen is generated through electrolysis of hydrogen sulfide; thermochemical splitting of hydrogen sulfide; and the half-cell reduction of H+ to H2 accompanied by the half-cell oxidization of electron sources comprising ferrous iron (Fe2+) oxidized to ferric iron (Fe+) and/or the oxidation of sulfur compounds, where the oxidized iron or sulfur is recycled to back to a reduced state through additional chemical reactions with minerals comprising of a metal sulfide, hydrogen sulfide, and a hydrocarbon. Hydrogen is generated through an electrochemical or thermochemical process known to produce hydrogen with low- or no-CO2 emissions, involving carbon capture and sequestration enabled methane reforming; carbon capture and sequestration enabled coal gasification; the Kvaerner process; a process that generates a carbon-black product; and carbon capture and sequestration enabled gasification or pyrolysis of biomass. The electron donors and/or electron acceptors are generated or recycled using renewable, alternative, or conventional sources of power that are low in greenhouse gas emissions, and th sources of power comprise of: photovoltaic, solar thermal, wind, hydroelectric, nuclear, geothermal, enhanced geothermal, ocean thermal, ocean wave, and tidal power sources. The electron donor is generate within or recycled to the environment through non- or low-CO2 emitting chemical reactions with hydrocarbons, comprising one or more of: thermochemical reduction of sulfate reaction (TSR); the Muller-Kuhne reaction for the production of hydrogen sulfide or reduced sulfur; and methane reforming-like reactions utilizing metal oxides in place of water, where the metal oxides comprise one or more of: iron oxide, calcium oxide, and magnesium oxide, and where the hydrocarbon is reacted to form solid carbonate with little or no emission of CO2 gas along with hydrogen electron donor product. Fixing step is followed by one or more process steps in which unused nutrients and/or process water left after removal of chemoautotrophic cell mass and/or chemical co-products of chemosynthesis and/or waste products or contaminants of the process stream produced during the fixing step are recycled back into a reactor system in which the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is performed to support further chemosynthesis. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is performed by chemoautotrophic microorganisms that have been improved, optimized or engineered for the fixation of CO2 and/or other forms of inorganic carbon and the production of organic compounds. The electron donor is generated from pollutants or waste products selected from process gas; exhaust gas; enhanced oil recovery vent gas; biogas; acid mine drainage; landfill leachate; landfill gas; geothermal gas; geothermal sludge or brine; metal contaminants; gangue; tailings; sulfides; disulfides; mercaptans selected from one or more of methyl mercaptan, dimethyl mercaptan, and ethyl mercaptan; carbonyl sulfide; carbon disulfide; alkanesulfonates dialkyl sulfides; thiosulfate; thiofurans; thiocyanates; isothiocyanates; thioureas; thiols; thiophenols; thioethers; thiophene; dibenzothiophene; tetrathionate; dithioite; thionate; dialkyl disulfides; sulfones; sulfoxides; sulfolanes; sulfonic acid; dimethyl sulfoniopropionate; sulfonic esters; hydrogen sulfide; sulfate esters; organic sulfur; and sour gases. Delivery of reducing equivalents from the electron donor to the chemoautotrophic microorganisms for the chemosynthetic reaction during the fixing step is kinetically and/or thermodynamically enhanced by introduction of hydrogen storage materials into the environment in the form of a solid support media for microbial growth that facilitates bringing absorbed or adsorbed hydrogen electron donors into close proximity with the chemoautotrophic organisms; introduction of electron mediators comprising one or more of: cytochromes, formate methyl-viologen, NAD+/NADH, neutral red (NR), and quinones to help transfer reducing power from a poorly soluble electron donor comprising H2 gas or electrons in solid state electrode materials into chemoautotrophic culture media in the environment; and introduction of electrode materials in the form of a solid growth support media directly into the environment to facilitate bringing solid state electrons into close proximity with the chemoautotrophic microorganisms. The environment comprises a bioreactor, and the microorganisms are maintained in a culture medium in the bioreactor. The bioreactor is formed at least in portion by a microbial culture apparatus selected from: an airlift reactor; a biological scrubber column; a bubble column; a continuous stirred tank reactor; a counter-current, upflow, expanded-bed reactor; a digestor for a sewage and/or waste water treatment or bioremediation system; one or more filters; a fluidized bed reactor; a gas lift fermenter; an immobilized cell reactor; a membrane biofilm reactor; a mine shaft; a Pachuca tank; a packed-bed reactor; a plug-flow reactor; a static mixer; a tank; a trickle bed reactor; a vat; and/or a vertical shaft bioreactor. The process further involves prior to the fixing step, a step of reacting CO2 with minerals to form a carbonate or bicarbonate product, which is then used in the fixing step; and fixing the CO2 and/or inorganic carbon into the organic compounds via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction within a reactor system, where the electron donor utilized in the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is produced via a non-biological process in the reactor system. The process for capture and conversion of CO2 or inorganic carbon results in a net reduction of gaseous CO2 released to the atmosphere. Preferred Microorganism: The chemoautotrophic microorganisms comprise Acetoanaerobium sp.; Acetobacterium sp.; Acetogenium sp.; Achromobacter sp.; Acidianus sp.; Acinetobacter sp.; Actinomadura sp.; Aeromonas sp.; Alcaligenes sp.; Arcobacter sp.; Aureobacterium sp.; Bacillus sp.; Beggiatoa sp.; Butyribacyerium sp.; Carboxydothermus sp.; Clostridium sp.; Comamonas sp.; Dehalobacter sp.; Dehalococcoides sp.; Dehalosprillum sp.; Desulfobacterium sp.; Desulfomonile sp.; Desulfotomaculum sp.; Desulfovibrio sp.; Desulfurosarcina sp.; Ectothiorhodospira sp.; Enterobacter sp.; Eubacterium sp.; Ferroplasma sp.; Halothibacillus sp.; Hydrogenbacter sp.; Hydrogenomonas sp.; Leptospirillum sp.; Metallosphaera sp.; Methanobacterium sp.; Methanobrevibacter sp.; Methanococcus sp.; Methanosarcina sp.; Micrococcus sp.; Nitrobacter sp.; Nitrosococcus sp.; Nitrosolobus sp.; Nitrosomonas sp.; Nitrosospira sp.; Nitrosovibrio sp.; Nitrospina sp.; Oleomonas sp.; Paracoccus sp.; Peptostreptococcus sp.; Planctomycetes sp.; Pseudomonas sp.; Ralstonia sp.; Rhodobacter sp.; Rhodococcus sp.; Rhodocyclus sp.; Rhodomicrobium sp.; Rhodopseudomonas sp.; Rhodospirillum sp.; Shewanella sp.; Streptomyces sp.; Sulfobacillus sp.; Sulfolobus sp.; Thiobacillus sp.; Thiomicrospira sp.; Thioploca sp.; Thiosphaera sp.; Thiothrix sp.; sulfur-oxidizer; hydrogen-oxidizers; iron-oxidizers; acetogens; methanogens; consortiums of microorganism that include chemoautotrophs; chemoautotrophs native to of hydrothermal vents, geothermal vents, hot springs, cold seeps, underground aquifers, salt lakes, saline formations, mines, acid mine drainage, mine tailings, oil wells, refinery wastewater, coal seams, deep sub-surface, waste water and sewage treatment plants, geothermal power plants, sulfatara fields, and soils; and extremophiles selected from thermophiles, hyperthermophiles, acidophiles, halophiles, and psychrophiles. The chemosynthetic microorganisms are obligate anaerobes. Preferred Components: The animal feed product is a feed for cattle, sheep, chickens, pigs, and/or fish.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte comprises potassium hydroxide. The electron donor comprises ammonia; ammonium; carbon monoxide; dithionite; elemental sulfur; a hydrocarbon; hydrogen; a sulfide; a sulfite; a thionate; a thionite; a transition metal and/or its sulfide; an oxide; a chalcogenide; a halide; a hydroside; an oxyhydroxide; a phosphate; a sulfate; a carbonate; and a conduction or valence band electron in a solid state electrode material. The electron acceptor comprises CO2; oxygen; a nitrite; a nitrate; a transition metal ion; a sulfate; and a valence or conduction band hole in a solid state electrode material.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).);

PT P
PN CN108659187-A; CN108659187-B
TI Polyurethane based on carbon dioxide to regulate            affinity/hydrophobicity comprises prepolymerization            reaction of hydroxyl monomer, functional monomer and            diisocyanate monomer followed by chain extender for            chain extension reaction.
AU DING Y
   YANG G
   WEI H
   YANG S
   SUN X
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 201883628L
AB    NOVELTY - Polyurethane based on carbon dioxide to                regulate affinity/hydrophobicity comprises                prepolymerization reaction of hydroxyl monomer,                functional monomer and diisocyanate monomer                followed by a chain extender for chain extension                reaction, and finally a polyurethane prepolymer                obtained by blocking with a blocking agent. The                polyurethane prepolymer realizes cyclic regulation                of hydrophilicity and hydrophobicity by carbon                dioxide. The polyurethane prepolymer is added to an                aqueous CO2 solution, and mechanically stirred to                complete protonation of the hydrophobic                polyurethane to obtain a polyurethane aqueous                dispersion to achieve a transition from hydrophobic                to hydrophilic. The CO2 in the aqueous polyurethane                dispersion is removed. The deprotonation of the                hydrophilic polyurethane is completed, and the                hydrophobic system of the polyurethane is obtained                to realize the transition of the polyurethane from                hydrophilic to hydrophobic.
   USE - Polyurethane based on carbon dioxide to                regulate affinity/hydrophobicity.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane based on carbon dioxide to                regulate affinity/hydrophobicity realizes                dispersion and separation of the polyurethane in                water, and substantially remove contradiction                between the dispersion of the polyurethane in water                and the water resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The aqueous polyurethane dispersion is                obtained again by adding an aqueous solution of CO2                to the hydrophobic system of the polyurethane, and                the polyurethane is again transformed from                hydrophobic to hydrophilic. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a method for preparing polyurethane                based on carbon dioxide-regulated                affinity/hydrophobicity, which involves:
   (A) adding prepolymerization reaction                diisocyanate monomer, hydroxyl monomer, functional                monomer and catalyst to the reaction flask, and                carrying reaction at a temperature of 60-90 degrees                C for 1.0-5.0 hours to obtain a polyurethane                prepolymer;
   (B) adding chain extension reaction extender                to the polyurethane prepolymer obtained and                continue reaction for 1.0-3.0 hours;
   (C) capping reaction, cooling reaction system                to 60 degrees C, adding blocking agent, carrying                reaction for 1.0 hours to obtain a polyurethane                prepolymer;
   (D) lowering polyurethane prepolymer to room                temperature, and then adding to the CO2 aqueous                solution, and stirring mechanically at a rotation                speed of 500-1000 revolution per minute for 0.5-1.0                hours to complete the hydrophobic polyurethane,                obtaining aqueous polyurethane dispersion,                achieving transition from hydrophobic to                hydrophilic;
   (E) removing CO2 to the aqueous polyurethane                dispersion obtained, blowing an inert gas or airin                to displace the CO2, completing deprotonation of                the hydrophilic polyurethane, obtaining hydrophobic                system to realize the polyurethane by the pro                Water-to-hydrophobic transition; and
   (F) performing dispersion emulsification to                hydrophobic system of the polyurethane, adding                aqueous CO2 solution, mechanically stirring,                achieving transition from hydrophobic to                hydrophilic to obtain a polyurethane aqueous                dispersion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The functional monomer                  is an organic substance containing a CO2                  protonating functional group. The CO2 protonating                  functional group is a tertiary amino group, a                  fluorenyl group or a fluorenyl group. The organic                  substance containing a CO2 proton functional                  group is represented by group of formulae                  (I-II)A=CH2 or CH2O group;B'=selected from O or NH linking                  group;m=selected from an integer between 0 and 10;                  andR5=selected from a hydrogen atom, a methyl                  group or an ethyl group;R9=alkyl chain having 1-4 carbon                  atoms;R10 and R11=independently selected from a                  hydrogen atom, a methyl group or an ethyl                  group;n and m=each Independent integers selected                  from 0-10;Y'=CO2 protonating functional group, which                  is selected from a tertiary amino group, a                  fluorenyl group or a fluorenyl group; the                  tertiary amino group, a fluorenyl group or a                  fluorenyl group represented by formula (III-V);                  andR1, R2, R3 and R4=independently selected                  from alkyl chains having 1-4 carbon atoms.. The hydroxy monomer is a mixture of one or                  more of a polyether polyol having a molecular                  weight of 500-4000 and a polyester polyol. The                  diisocyanate monomer is an aromatic diisocyanate                  or an aliphatic diisocyanate or an alicyclic                  diisocyanate. The polyether polyol is                  polyoxypropylene diol, polyoxyethylene diol,                  polytetrahydrofuran ether diol or                  polyoxypropylene triol; the polyester polyol is                  polyadipate-1,4-butyl Glycol ester diol,                  polyadipate-1,6-hexanediol diol, polyadipate                  neopentyl glycol diol, polycarbonate diol or                  polycaprolactone diol. The diisocyanate monomer                  is a mixture of one or more of                  4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, toluene                  diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate and                  hexamethylene diisocyanate. The mass ratio of the                  hydroxy monomer to the diisocyanate monomer is                  1-3:1, and the mass ratio of the functional                  monomer to the diisocyanate monomer is                  0.06-0.40:1, the mass ratio of catalyst to the                  diisocyanate monomer is 0.001-0.008:1. The mass                  ratio of the chain extender to the diisocyanate                  monomer is from 0.02-0.2:1. The mass of the                  blocking agent is calculated based on the amount                  of the substance of the residual isocyanate                  group. The aqueous solution of CO2 is obtained by                  continuously introducing CO2 gas into deionized                  water..
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

PN JP2018012683-A; JP6987374-B2
TI Foamable skin external preparation comprises acidic substance, carbonic acid gas generating substance which reacted with the acidic substance to generated carbon dioxide gas, and thickener in same agent or more agents.
AU NAGASAKI A
   KAYASHIMA A
   AKIMOTO K
   KITAMURA S
   YAMAZAKI A
   TAKAGAKI K
AE TOYO SHINYAKU KK (TOYS-C)
   TOYO SHINYAKU CO LTD (TOYS-C)
GA 201807367A
AB    NOVELTY - Foamable skin external preparation comprises acidic substance, carbonic acid gas generating substance which reacted with the acidic substance to generated carbon dioxide gas, and a thickener in same agent or more agents, ascorbic acid glucoside, allantoin, estradiol, glycyrrhizinate dipotassium, glycyrrhizinic acid monoammonium, beta -glycyrrhizic acid, stearyl glycyrrhetinate, tranexamic acid, d-camphor, Delta l- alpha -tocopherol acetate, nicotinic acid.
   USE - Foamable skin external preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamable skin external preparation has reduces amount of moisture evaporation, improves skin elastic force|texture which makes the color of skin white of making the moisture content of skin, improves skin condition, excellent usability, safety|security, and stability, and has excellent activation effect|action of skin cells, and pore of skin becomes small and texture is ready.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Foamable skin external preparation comprises acidic substance, carbonic acid gas generating substance which reacted with the acidic substance to generated carbon dioxide gas, and a thickener in same agent or more agents, ascorbic acid glucoside, allantoin, estradiol, glycyrrhizinate dipotassium, glycyrrhizinic acid monoammonium, beta -glycyrrhizic acid, stearyl glycyrrhetinate, tranexamic acid, d-camphor, Delta l- alpha -tocopherol acetate, nicotinic acid, Delta l- alpha -Tocopherol, D-pantothenyl alcohol, pantothenyl ethyl ether, ethinyl estradiol, salicylic acid, biotin, resorcinol, trichlorocarbanilide, triclosan, benzethonium chloride, sulfur, photosensitizer 201, phenol, pyridoxine hydrochloride carbon dioxide gas foaming external skin preparation is selected from sin, zinc oxide, arbutin, phosphate-L-ascorbyl magnesium, ascorbyl dipalmitate, placenta, retinol and retinol palmitate when teffervescent external skin preparation comprises agent which is mixed with the water-containing substance comprises two or more agents, where one agents comprises water.    EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2022060063-A
TI System for carbon-free combined cycle power generation and linked smart farm comprises e.g. hydrogen fuel cell power generation unit for producing electricity and high-temperature steam from hydrogen purified from hydrogen purification unit.
AU CHAE H
   KIM Y
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202265238W
AB    NOVELTY - System (10) comprises ammonia-carbon dioxide compound decomposition unit (100) in which ammonia-carbon dioxide compounds are decomposed under thermal energy supply to discharge carbon dioxide and ammonia; a carbon-dioxide/ammonia separation unit (200) separating the carbon dioxide and ammonia mixture transferred from the ammonia-carbon dioxide compound decomposition unit into carbon dioxide and ammonia; a carbon dioxide supply unit (900) for supplying carbon dioxide discharged from the ammonia-carbon dioxide compound decomposition unit; an ammonia decomposition unit (300) for pyrolyzing ammonia discharged from the ammonia-carbon dioxide compound decomposition unit to produce nitrogen and hydrogen; a hydrogen purification unit (400) for purifying hydrogen produced from the ammonia decomposition unit; and a hydrogen fuel cell power generation unit (500) for producing electricity and high-temperature steam from hydrogen purified from the hydrogen purification unit.
   USE - The system is useful for carbon-free combined cycle power generation and linked smart farm.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has the effect of enabling carbon-free power generation without carbon dioxide emissions by using ammonia instead of fossil fuels as a hydrogen source, and promotes mass production; is easy and economical, and improves the crop yield by supplying carbon dioxide generated in the decomposition process of the ammonia-carbon dioxide compound to a crop cultivation facility as it exists in a solid state under normal temperature and atmospheric pressure conditions, so a transportation means equipped with special storage facilities at high pressure or low temperature; and maximizes energy generation efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   System (10)
   Ammonia-carbon dioxide compound decomposition unit (100)
   Carbon-dioxide/ammonia separation unit (200)
   Ammonia decomposition unit (300)
   Hydrogen purification unit (400)
   Hydrogen fuel cell power generation unit (500)
   Carbon dioxide supply unit (900)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The ammonia-carbon dioxide compound is urea, ammonium carbamate, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate.
