PN CN114345079-A; CN216986975-U
TI Temperature swing pressure swing adsorption device            for flue gas carbon dioxide capture, has solar            collector that uses heat transfer oil to heat carbon            dioxide gas, provides heat for regeneration of carbon            dioxide adsorption subsystem, and stores solar heat            generated during day.
AU LIAN H
   GAO Y
AE CHINA POWER ENG CONSULTING GROUP NORTHWE (CECG-C)
GA 202264004N
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a solar generator (1) that is                used to provide the electrical energy required by                the flue gas pressurization and cooling subsystem,                the carbon dioxide desorption subsystem and the                carbon dioxide liquefaction subsystem. A solar                cooler (2) is used to provide the cooling capacity                required by the carbon dioxide liquefaction                subsystem to condense the gaseous carbon dioxide. A                solar collector (24) uses heat transfer oil to heat                carbon dioxide gas, provides heat for the                regeneration of the carbon dioxide adsorption                subsystem, and stores the solar heat generated                during the day. An organic Rankine cycle generator                (21) is used to replace the solar generator at                night. The stored liquid carbon dioxide is used as                a working medium to drive the flue gas                pressurization and cooling subsystem, the carbon                dioxide adsorption subsystem, and the carbon                dioxide desorption subsystem to work continuously                at night, with the carbon dioxide liquefaction                subsystem.
   USE - Temperature swing pressure swing adsorption                device for flue gas carbon dioxide capture for                solar energy storage and power plant flue gas                purification used in power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes all-weather uninterrupted                operation, has simple operation, large load                adjusting elasticity, reliable, safety-friendly and                high energy utilization efficiency, and fully uses                the cleaning performance and reproducibility of the                renewable energy to capture the carbon dioxide,                which effectively improves the economical                efficiency and environmental friendliness of the                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control                method based on the temperature swing pressure                swing adsorption device for flue gas carbon dioxide                capture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                temperature swing pressure swing adsorption device                for flue gas carbon dioxide capture. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Solar generator (1)
   Solar cooler (2)
   Organic Rankine cycle generator (21)
   First heat-conducting oil storage tank                (22)
   Solar collector (24)
   Eighth control valve (37)
   Third control valve (52)

PT P
PN CN113528215-A
TI Preparing high direct base number calcium            sulfonate useful for lubricating grease, comprises e.g.            adding solvent, base oil, alcohol, calcium oxide, water            and carboxylic acid compound into reaction vessel,            heating, adding calcium oxide and water, carbonizing,            and separating alcohol and water.
AU LI X
   FAN J
   WANG L
   BU W
   ZHANG C
AE XINXIANG RUIFENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (XINX-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2555N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high direct base number (DBN)                calcium sulfonate comprises adding solvent, base                oil, alcohol, calcium oxide, water and carboxylic                acid compound into reaction vessel, heating, and                stirring, dropping sulfonic acid into reaction                vessel, dropping polyisobutylene succinic                anhydride, heating for neutralization reaction,                adding calcium oxide and water into reaction                vessel, heating, continuously introducing carbon                dioxide into reaction vessel for primary                carbonization reaction, maintaining temperature                unchanged, adding calcium oxide and water into                reaction vessel, continuously feeding carbon                dioxide into reaction vessel for secondary                carbonization reaction, heating to remove alcohol,                water and solvent, cooling, adding alcohol, calcium                oxide, water, and solvent into reaction vessel,                cooling, continuously feeding carbon dioxide into                reaction vessel for tertiary carbonization                reactions, removing and separating alcohol and                water, centrifuging upper reaction liquid and                distilling.
   USE - Calcium sulfonate is useful for lubricating                grease.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of continuous production                and low production cost and has wide raw material                source, and simple production technique                operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high DBN calcium sulfonate comprises                adding 250-350 pts. wt. solvent, 150-200 pts. wt.                base oil, 30-70 pts. wt. alcohol, 20-30 pts. wt.                calcium oxide, 3-10 pts. wt. water and 30-60 pts.                wt. carboxylic acid compound into the reaction                vessel, heating to 55-60&#8451;, and stirring                uniformly, dropping 82-155 pts. wt. sulfonic acid                into the reaction vessel, dropping 30-40 pts. wt.                polyisobutylene succinic anhydride, heating to                80-85&#8451; for neutralization reaction,                adding 40-50 pts. wt. calcium oxide and 10-20 pts.                wt. water into the reaction vessel, heating to                80-85&#8451;, continuously introducing carbon                dioxide into the reaction vessel for primary                carbonization reaction, maintaining the temperature                unchanged, adding 40-50 pts. wt. calcium oxide and                10-20 pts. wt. water into the reaction vessel,                continuously feeding carbon dioxide into the                reaction vessel for secondary carbonization                reaction, heating to remove the alcohol, water and                solvent, cooling to 50-55&#8451;, adding 40-80                pts. wt. alcohol, 40-50 pts. wt. calcium oxide,                10-20 pts. wt. water, and 250-350 pts. wt. solvent                into the reaction vessel, cooling to                45-50&#8451;, continuously feeding carbon                dioxide into the reaction vessel for tertiary                carbonization reactions, removing and separating                the alcohol and water, centrifuging upper reaction                liquid and distilling under vacuum to obtain the                calcium sulfonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  calcium oxide and carbon dioxide in the primary                  carbonization reaction and the secondary                  carbonization reaction is 1:0.45-1:0.55 and the                  mass ratio of calcium oxide and carbon dioxide in                  the third carbonization reaction is                  1:0.85-1:0.95. The alcohol is at least one of                  1C-8C alcohols, the base oil is 100N, 100SN,                  150N, 150SN, 500N, 500SN, and/or 150BS, the                  carboxylic acid compound is monobasic and/or                  dibasic carboxylic acids, and the solvent is                  benzene, toluene, xylene, 120 solvent, 200                  solvent, petroleum ether, n-hexane, and/or                  n-heptane. The sulfonic acid includes                  alkylbenzene sulfonic acid and petroleum sulfonic                  acid in a mass ratio of 7:3. The number of carbon                  atoms in the carbon chain of the alkylbenzene                  sulfonic acid is 12C-30C, preferably                  22C-24C.Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises adding 250-350 pts. wt. solvent,                  150-200 pts. wt. base oil, and 30-70 pts. wt.                  alcohol into the reaction vessel under protective                  gas conditions, stirring uniformly for 20                  minutes, adding 20-30 pts. wt. calcium oxide and                  3-10 pts. wt. water into the reaction vessel and                  stirring for 30 minutes, and dropping 30-60 pts.                  wt. carboxylic acid into the reaction vessel,                  dropping 82-155 pts. wt. sulfonic acid to the                  reaction vessel at a uniform speed for 60-90                  minutes, adding 30-40 pts. wt. polyisobutylene                  succinic anhydride, heating to 80-85&#8451;                  for neutralization reaction for 1.5 hours,                  measuring the pH value of the reaction solution                  with a pH meter, and controlling pH value to more                  than 10.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); H07 (Lubricants and lubrication - this excludes self-lubricating surfaces e.g. PTFE coated surfaces and lubrication systems in general. The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).)

PT P
PN CN112920357-A; CN112920357-B
TI Porphyrin-based covalent organic framework material based on metal ion doping used in e.g. adsorbing carbon dioxide, methane and molecular nitrogen, comprises repeating structural unit of (5Z,9Z,14Z)-1,20,22,24-tetrahydro-porphine compound.
AU BEN T
   WANG T
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202165632F
AB    NOVELTY - Porphyrin-based covalent organic framework material based on metal ion doping comprises repeating structural unit i.e. (3-(4-iminomethyl-phenyl)-prop-2-yn-(E)-ylidene)-(4-((1Z,5Z,9Z)-10,15,20-triphenyl-14,15,22,24-tetrahydro-porphin-5-yl)-phenyl)-amine compound (I).
   USE - The porphyrin-based covalent organic framework material is useful in: adsorption of carbon dioxide, methane, molecular nitrogen and molecular hydrogen; and separating mixed gas of molecular nitrogen and carbon dioxide, mixed gas of carbon dioxide and methane and the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and molecular hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porphyrin-based covalent organic framework material: solves the problem of poor carbon dioxide adsorption and separation performance and poor recyclability of the covalent organic framework material in the prior art; has high crystallinity, excellent specific surface area, permanent porosity, excellent thermal stability and chemical stability, excellent gas adsorption and separation function; and can be recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porphyrin-based covalent organic framework material based on metal ion doping comprises repeating structural unit i.e. (3-(4-iminomethyl-phenyl)-prop-2-yn-(E)-ylidene)-(4-((1Z,5Z,9Z)-10,15,20-triphenyl-14,15,22,24-tetrahydro-porphin-5-yl)-phenyl)-amine compound of formula (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing porphyrin-based covalent organic framework material, comprising (i) mixing 5,10,15,20-tetra(tetraaminophenyl)porphyrin, 2.5-bis(4-aminophenyl-1-yl)1,4-xylene, organic solvent and acetic acid aqueous solution under the protection of an inert atmosphere, reacting at 100-140 degrees C for 25-75 hours, cooling to room temperature, washing and vacuum drying to obtain a covalent organic framework material, where the molar ratio of 5,10,15,20-tetra(tetraaminophenyl)porphyrin and 2.5-bis(4-aminophenyl-1-yl)1,4-xylene is 1:2, (ii) uniformly mixing the covalent organic framework material obtained in step (i), metal salt and organic solvent under the protection of an inert atmosphere, reacting at 100-140 degrees C for 8-72 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing and vacuum drying to obtain final product, where the metal ions of the metal salt are iron(III) ion, cobalt(II) ion, nickel(II) ion, copper(II) ion, palladium(II) ion and/or zinc(II) ion; and the mass ratio of the covalent organic framework material and the metal salt is 1:0.03-0.06.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises first grinding 5,10,15,20-tetra(tetraaminophenyl)porphyrin and 2.5-bis(4-aminophenyl-1-yl)1,4-xylene until they are uniformly mixed, then sequentially adding organic solvent and acetic acid aqueous solution under the protection of an inert atmosphere and mixing well. In step (i) and step (ii), the reactions are all carried out under stirring and the stirring speed is 800 revolutions/minute. In the step (i), the reaction temperature is 120 degrees C and the time is 72 hours; in step (ii), the reaction temperature is 80 degrees C and the reaction time is 12-24 hours. In step (i), washing is first soaking in tetrahydrofuran for 12 hours, repeating for three times, then soaking in acetone for 12 hours, repeating for three times; in step (ii), washing comprises of first soaking in deionized water for 12 hours, repeating for three times, then soaking in tetrahydrofuran for 12 hours, repeating for three times, then soaking in organic solvent of step (ii) for 12 hours, repeating for three times and finally soaking in acetone for 12 hours, repeating for three times. In step (i) and step (ii), the drying temperature is 80 degrees C and the drying time is 4-8 hours. Preferred Components: In step (i), the amount of acetic acid is the amount of catalyst. In step (ii), the metal salt is ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, cobalt acetate dihydrate, nickel nitrate hexahydrate, copper sulfate pentahydrate, zinc acetate dihydrate and/or palladium acetate. In step (i), the organic solvent is a mixture of anhydrous n-butanol and o-dichlorobenzene in a volume ratio of 1:3; in step (ii), the organic solvent is dimethylformamide, methanol and/or dichloromethane.

TI Preparation of metal corrosion inhibitor comprises            preparing carbon dioxide liquid phase corrosion            inhibitor for natural gas storage, and preparing carbon            dioxide gas phase corrosion inhibitor for natural gas            storage.

AE PANJIN TIANCHENGYUAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (PANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202119295P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of metal corrosion inhibitor                comprises preparing carbon dioxide (CO2) liquid                phase corrosion inhibitor for natural gas storage,                and preparing carbon dioxide gas phase corrosion                inhibitor for natural gas storage. Preparation of                CO2 liquid phase corrosion inhibitor comprises                subjecting cis-octadec-9-enoic acid and                triethylenetetramine through amidation, cyclization                and dehydration; subjecting intermediate of                imidazoline and benzyl chloride undergo                quaternization, and transforming; and reacting                imidazoline quaternary ammonium salt with ethyl                sulfide nitrogen, and introducing ethyl sulfide                nitrogen polar group. The preparation of CO2 gas                phase corrosion inhibitor comprises subjecting                acetic acid and triethylenetetramine through                amidation and cyclization reaction, and                dehydration; subjecting intermediate of imidazoline                and benzyl chloride undergo quaternization, and                transforming; and reacting imidazoline quaternary                ammonium salt with sodium ethylxanthate.
   USE - The method is used for preparing metal                corrosion inhibitor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of metal corrosion inhibitor                comprises preparing carbon dioxide (CO2) liquid                phase corrosion inhibitor for natural gas storage,                and preparing carbon dioxide gas phase corrosion                inhibitor for natural gas storage. Preparation of                CO2 liquid phase corrosion inhibitor for natural                gas storage comprises subjecting                cis-octadec-9-enoic acid and triethylenetetramine                through amidation, cyclization and dehydration                twice to form five-membered heterocyclic                imidazoline intermediate of formula (I); subjecting                intermediate of imidazoline and benzyl chloride                undergo quaternization, and transforming                oil-soluble quaternary ammonium salt of imidazoline                from oil-soluble to water-soluble imidazoline                quaternary ammonium salt of formula (II); and                reacting imidazoline quaternary ammonium salt with                ethyl sulfide nitrogen, and introducing ethyl                sulfide nitrogen polar group so that imidazoline                quaternary ammonium salt has more active adsorption                sites of CO2 liquid phase corrosion inhibitor for                natural gas storage 2- ethylthioazinylamine                ethylbenzyl-1-heptadecenyl imidazoline quaternary                ammonium salt of formula (III). The preparation of                CO2 gas phase corrosion inhibitor for natural gas                storage comprises subjecting acetic acid and                triethylenetetramine through amidation and                cyclization reaction, and dehydrating twice to form                five-membered heterocyclic imidazoline intermediate                of formula (IV); subjecting intermediate of                imidazoline and benzyl chloride undergo                quaternization, and transforming oil-soluble                quaternary ammonium salt of imidazoline from                oil-soluble to water-soluble imidazoline quaternary                ammonium salt of formula (V); and reacting                imidazoline quaternary ammonium salt with sodium                ethylxanthate to introduce polar group of                ethylxanthogen acid to make imidazoline quaternary                ammonium salt have more active adsorption sites for                CO2 gas phase in natural gas storage corrosion                inhibitor 2-ethylsulfonated amine                ethylbenzyl-1-methylimidazoline quaternary ammonium                salt of formula (VI).
   R=C17H33, cis-octadec-9-enoic acid                CH3(CH2)7CHCH(CH2)7COOH, or triethylenetetramine                NH2(C2H4NH)2C2H4NH;
   R'=C6H5-CH2, or benzyl chloride C6H5-CH2Cl;                and
   Y'=(C2H5)2NCSS.
   R=CH3 or acetic acid; and
   R=C6H5-CH2.

PN CN110045082-A; CN110045082-B
TI Method for measuring and evaluating high-temperature properties of lump coal in smelting reduction ironmaking, involves weighing lump coal sample, and heating lump coal sample treated, followed by calculating average particle diameter.
AU LIU Q
   YANG S
   WANG M
   DONG J
   CAO S
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
   UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 201966402X
AB    NOVELTY - A lump coal high-temperature properties measuring and evaluating method involves weighing a lump coal sample of a preset mass m1, the average particle size of the lump coal sample is d0. The lump coal sample weighed is heated and kept in a 100% nitrogen atmosphere, the lump coal sample treated is heated to 700-850 degrees C in an atmosphere of 20% carbon dioxide + 80% nitrogen, and kept warm, the lump coal sample treated is heated to 1100 degrees C in an atmosphere of 20% carbon dioxide + 80% nitrogen, and the atmosphere is switched to 100% carbon dioxide, and the reaction is completed, the lump coal sample after the reaction is cooled to room temperature under the protection of nitrogen, and the mass m2 of the lump coal sample is obtained, and the reactivity of the lump coal is calculated, followed by measuring the particle size distribution of the lump coal sample after the reaction at different input power W, and calculating the average particle diameter df after the reaction is obtained.
   USE - Method for measuring and evaluating high-temperature properties of lump coal in smelting reduction ironmaking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A lump coal high-temperature properties measuring and evaluating method involves weighing a lump coal sample of a preset mass m1, the average particle size of the lump coal sample is d0. The lump coal sample weighed is heated and kept in a 100% nitrogen atmosphere, the lump coal sample treated is heated to 700-850 degrees C in an atmosphere of 20% carbon dioxide + 80% nitrogen, and kept warm, the lump coal sample treated is heated to 1100 degrees C in an atmosphere of 20% carbon dioxide + 80% nitrogen, and the atmosphere is switched to 100% carbon dioxide, and the reaction is completed, the lump coal sample after the reaction is cooled to room temperature under the protection of nitrogen, and the mass m2 of the lump coal sample is obtained, and the reactivity of the lump coal is calculated, followed by measuring the particle size distribution of the lump coal sample after the reaction at different input power W, and calculating the average particle diameter df after the reaction is obtained, when the input power W is linearly fitted and plotted on the abscissa, and the fracture coefficient of the lump coal sample is calculated according to the fitted slope k.

TI Bottle used in device for measuring respiratory breathing rate of bio-membrane, comprises bottle body, carbon dioxide absorbing tube, and perforated partition plate, where perforated partition plate is disposed inside bottle body.
AU LIU G
   LU Z
AE UNIV JINAN GUANGDONG (UJIN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 201912357Q
AB    NOVELTY - Bottle comprises bottle body (1), carbon dioxide absorbing tube (11), and perforated partition plate (6), where perforated partition plate is disposed inside the bottle body. The perforated partition plate divides the inside of the bottle body into an oxygen mass transfer agitating zone (8) and bio-membrane reaction zone (3), and the oxygen mass transfer agitating zone is provided with a stirring device (7). The bio-membrane reaction zone is used to place moving bed bioreactor filler (2). The carbon dioxide absorption tube is provided with a venting hole (10) and comprises a solution capable of absorbing carbon dioxide to in the bottle body. The perforated partition has first hole and second hole, where the first hole is located at a middle portion and lower portion of the perforated partition is set with an liquid exchange passage (5). The second hole is located at upper portion of the perforated partition (6) and serve as gas exchange passage (12).
   USE - Bottle used in device for measuring respiratory breathing rate of bio-membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The bottle allows to measure the respiratory breathing rate of bio-membrane in a simple and fast manner, and the measurement result is more accurate and effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) device for measuring a respiratory rate of a bio-membrane, comprises respirator for measuring oxygen absorption rate, and the respiration bottle, which is connected with respirator and by respirator internal connectivity; and
   (2) method for measuring respiration rate of a bio-membrane, involves using moving bed bioreactor filler to which the bio-membrane is attached and putting it into the bio-membrane reaction zone, and the immersing the moving bed bioreactor filler in the bio-membrane reaction zone by adding water sample; adding a solution capable of absorbing crbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide absorption tube, sealing the bio-membrane reaction zone and the oxygen mass transfer stirring zone, and connecting the respirator through the pipe to the bottle body, then starting a stirring device located in the oxygen mass transfer stirring zone; and obtaining a rate and total amount of inhalation recorded by the respirator.

PT P
PN CN108796544-A; CN108796544-B
TI Preparing magnesium carbonate by electrochemical mineralization of carbon dioxide.
AU LIU Z
   YANG P
   LI C
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201892680H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium carbonate by electrochemical mineralization of carbon dioxide where the electrochemical carbon dioxide device for preparing magnesium carbonate is cationic membrane electrolytic tank comprises a tank body, an oxygen gas diffusion electrode (cathode), lead telluride lead(II) sulfide/ titanium oxide nanotube arrays titanium anode, Nafion cation exchange membrane, a stirrer and a voltage stabilizing direct current power supply. The cathode is a gas diffusion electrode. The complex electrode is porous titanium plate (300-500) as substrate, through surface dipping and chemical reduction-supported platinum nano particles. The loading amount of platinum is 0.5-2 mg/cm and oxygen carrying gas as sacrificial reagents. The anode is titanium lead telluride, lead(II) sulfide/ titanium oxide nanotube arrays (PbS/TNAs) nano-structure electrode, sulfide ions as sacrificial reagents, low using industrial waste heat at the same time.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing magnesium carbonate by electrochemical mineralization of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing magnesium carbonate by electrochemical mineralization of carbon dioxide where the electrochemical carbon dioxide device for preparing magnesium carbonate is cationic membrane electrolytic tank comprises a tank body, an oxygen gas diffusion electrode (cathode), lead telluride lead(II) sulfide/ titanium oxide nanotube arrays titanium anode, Nafion cation exchange membrane, a stirrer and a voltage stabilizing direct current power supply. The cathode is a gas diffusion electrode. The complex electrode is porous titanium plate (300-500) as substrate, through surface dipping and chemical reduction-supported platinum nano particles. The loading amount of platinum is 0.5-2 mg/cm and oxygen carrying gas as sacrificial reagents, obviously reduce the cathode reaction of over-potential. The anode is titanium lead telluride, lead(II) sulfide/ titanium oxide nanotube arrays (PbS/TNAs) (TNAs overpotential over-potential is a titanium dioxide nanotube array) nano-structure electrode, sulfide ions as sacrificial reagents, low using industrial waste heat (below 200 degrees C) at the same time, obviously reduces the sulfide ion oxide, so as to greatly reduce the anode reaction, during the electrolysis, the anode reaction to make sulfide ion, the cathode reaction to oxygen reduction, a cathode chamber and an anode chamber separated by Nafion cation exchange membrane and avoid OH-ions generated by the cathode chamber of migration to the anode chamber. sulfide oxide by oxygen reduction and coupling significantly reduces the power consumption of the electrochemical process, anode chamber working temperature at 10-70 degrees C, which can effectively utilize the low industrial waste heat (below 200 degrees C), its heat efficiency is 40-50%, comprehensive power consumption 1.2-2.4 kWh, kg-1 magnesium carbonate, Faraday efficiency is 90-99%, and energy efficiency 85-95%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of the gas diffusion electrode used is as follows a porous metal titanium substrate (300-500 mesh) is used, and immersed in 0.001-0.005 mol/l of chloroplatinic acid for 8-12 hours, and mixture was reduced in 0.01 mol/l of hydrazine hydrate for 30-120 minutes, washed repeatedly with deionized water, and dried by cold air. The titanium dioxide nanotube array film prepared by anodizing is used as a carrier and placed at 40-50 degrees C. 0.01 mol/l water-soluble lead salt was immersed for 30 minutes, taken out, washed with deionized water and blown dry, placed in 0.05 mol/l Na2H solution prepared by Sodium borohydride reduction of bismuth powder, in high purity nitrogen Immersion for 30 minutes under protection, washing and drying, taking out, the above process as a cycle, reciprocating cycle for 5 cycles to obtain PbTe/TNAs sample as the working electrode, platinum plate as the counter electrode, saturated calomel electrode as the reference The specific electrode was linear voltammetric scanning with 0.5 mol/L sodium sulfide and 0.5 mol/l sodium sulfite aqueous solution at room temperature. The scanning range was -0.5 to 0.5 V, the scanning rate was 2-10 mV/seconds, and the scanning was repeated 5 times, taking out, washing and drying, the prepared electrode is a titanium-based PbTe-PbS/TNAs electrode.

PN CN108192868-A; CN108192868-B
TI Inducing amplification of immune cells involves inoculating mononuclear cells into bioreactor, culturing in T cell expansion medium and culturing in second and third T cell expansion medium.
AU XU Z
   ZHANG X
AE GUANGZHOU SHAAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201851416D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing amplification of immune cells involves (a) inoculating mononuclear cells into bioreactor, culturing for one day at 37 degrees C, 2-7% carbon dioxide, 8-10% oxygen and aseptic conditions, where the bioreactor is pre-coated with anti-human cluster of differentiation (CD)61 antibody, bioreactor comprises first medium, which is a T cell expansion medium comprising 10% autologous plasma, 1000 IU/ml interleukin (IL)-2 and 0.01 KE/ml sapylin, (b) further culturing the mononuclear cells cultured in step (a) for 14-21 days to obtain immune cells, transferring 1-8 days culture to second medium and culturing at 37 degrees C, 2-7% carbon dioxide, 8-10% oxygen and under sterile conditions, where the second medium is T cell expansion medium containing 10% autologous plasma and 1000 IU/ml IL-2 or T cell expansion medium containing 1000 IU/ml IL-2, and transferring 9-21 days culture to third culture medium and culturing at 37 degrees C, 12-18% oxygen, 2-7% carbon dioxide and aseptic conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing amplification of immune cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for inducing amplification of immune cells involves (a) inoculating mononuclear cells into bioreactor, culturing for one day at 37 degrees C, 2-7% carbon dioxide, 8-10% oxygen and aseptic conditions, where the bioreactor is pre-coated with anti-human cluster of differentiation (CD)61 antibody, bioreactor comprises first medium, which is a T cell expansion medium comprising 10% autologous plasma, 1000 IU/ml interleukin (IL)-2 and 0.01 KE/ml sapylin, (b) further culturing the mononuclear cells cultured in step (a) for 14-21 days to obtain immune cells, transferring 1-8 days culture to second medium and culturing at 37 degrees C, 2-7% carbon dioxide, 8-10% oxygen and under sterile conditions, where the second medium is T cell expansion medium containing 10% autologous plasma and 1000 IU/ml IL-2 or T cell expansion medium containing 1000 IU/ml IL-2, and transferring 9-21 days culture to third culture medium and culturing at 37 degrees C, 12-18% oxygen, 2-7% carbon dioxide and aseptic conditions, where the third culture medium contains 1000 IU/ml IL-2 SuperCulture (RTM: Serum free medium for lymphocytes) L500 medium or contains 1000 IU/ml IL-2 SuperCulture (Serum free medium for lymphocytes) L500 medium and 1 mg/ml white saponin S. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for immune cells obtained by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The mononuclear cells cultured in step (a) are further cultured for 12-14 days to obtain immune cells. In the process of culturing mononuclear cells, on day 14, mononuclear cells cultured for 13 days are recovered and resuspended physiological saline containing 5% human serum albumin, the single nuclear cells are inoculated into bioreactor and cultured for 1 day at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide, 9% oxygen and aseptic conditions, cultured for 1-8 days in second medium at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide, 9% oxygen and aseptic conditions and cultured for 9-13 day in third medium at 37 degrees C, 15% oxygen, 5% carbon dioxide and aseptic conditions. The IL-2 is purchased from Pepro Tech company. The mononuclear cells are isolated from peripheral blood by (a) centrifuging peripheral blood at 2500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15 minutes, (b) collecting upper plasma layer obtained by first centrifugation and centrifuging at 3500 rpm for 15 minutes to obtain supernatant plasma and blood cell sediment, (c) performing inactivation at 56 degrees C for 30-50 minutes and centrifuging at 3500 rpm for 15 minutes, (d) resuspending blood cell pellet in 50 vol.% physiological saline and adding 1/3 times hydroxyethyl starch to the suspension, (e) subjecting suspension obtained in step (d) to static treatment to sediment the red blood cells, (f) centrifuging suspension obtained in step (e) at 1800 rpm for 5 minutes to obtain leukocyte precipitate, (g) resuspending the leukocyte pellet in physiological saline to obtain resuspension, (h) adding resuspension to equal volume of human peripheral blood lymphocyte separation solution and centrifuging the resulting mixture at 2000 rpm for 25 minutes to obtain an intermediate misty layer of white blood cells and (useful for ) washing the intermediate misty layer of white blood cells twice with physiological saline to obtain mononuclear cells, where the human peripheral blood lymphocyte separation liquid comprises dextran and diatrizoate meglumine. In the method for inducing amplification of immune cells, the anti-human CD61 antibody is pre-coated in the bioreactor by administering a physiological saline containing 2.5 mu g/ml anti-human CD16 monoclonal antibody, placed in the bioreactor, bioreactor was incubated overnight at 4 degrees C in the dark and the incubated T cell expansion medium was washed using physiological saline. Preferred Components: The T cell expansion medium is OpTmizer CTS (RTM: T-cell expansion serum free medium).

PN CN107987889-A; CN107987889-B
TI Energy-saving and emission reduction of semi-coke gasification coal boiler involves combustioning of heat with nitrogen-free gas and reducing amount of flue gas in boiler by oxygen in combustion engine compared to amount of hot gas.
AU KONG L
AE KONG L (KONG-Individual)
GA 2018393844
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving and emission reduction of semi-coke gasification coal boiler involves combustioning of heat with nitrogen-free gas and reducing the amount of flue gas in boiler by oxygen in combustion engine compared to amount of hot gas containing 40-79% nitrogen in the air. The composition of hot gas at temperature greater than or equal to 750-950 degrees C is mainly the combustion of carbon and oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen are combusted to generate high-temperature steam, leaving the gas boiler. The temperature of boiler is maintained at 300-600 degrees C and discharged as waste heat.
   USE - Method for energy-saving and emission reduction of semi-coke gasification coal boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to save energy and reduce emission of semi-coke gasification coal boiler which produces tar-free, non-nitrogen gas, sulfur and high hardness blue carbon particles used in fixed-bed gasification furnacesto use oxygen and post-combustion gas carbon dioxide instead of water vapor as gasification agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy-saving and emission reduction of semi-coke gasification coal boiler involves combustioning of heat with nitrogen-free gas and reducing the amount of flue gas in boiler by oxygen in combustion engine compared to amount of hot gas containing 40-79% nitrogen in the air. The composition of hot gas at temperature greater than or equal to 750-950 degrees C is mainly the combustion of carbon and oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen are combusted to generate high-temperature steam, leaving the gas boiler. The temperature of boiler is maintained at 300-600 degrees C and discharged as waste heat. The 20-60% component of the boiler is carbon dioxide, high-temperature steam and oxygen are mixed into gasification agent to enter slag layer of blue carbon gasifier. The gasification layer and the reduction layer constitute a portion of the waste heat gas carbondioxide discharged from the boiler and then into blue carbon gasification furnace. The carbon dioxide in gas reduces the carbon monoxide in the high temperature hot carbon layer in the furnace. The high-temperature steam again neutralizes and releases hydrogen and carbon monoxide, saving 15-30% of coal and oxygen consumption by reducing carbon monoxide, into the boiler oxygen and hydrogen are combusted into high-temperature hot water steam and replaces a portion of the water vapor, and remaining exhaust gas carbondioxide is cooled and purified to obtain higher-purity carbon dioxide product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for energy saving and emission reduction of blue carbon gas fired boiler where blue carbon is nitrogen-free hot gas dry distillation of bituminous coal, nitrogen-free gas-cooled blue carbon equipment, and method for preparing the same. A tar-free, low-sulfur, high-hardness blue carbon particle used as a gasification agent in a fixed-bed gasification furnace in which mixture of oxygen-free and nitrogen-free exhaust gas carbondioxide is exhausted from the boiler and excess water vapor in exhaust gas is matched and mixed. The gasification temperature reaches 1100-1400 degrees C in the oxidation layer, carbon dioxide in nitrogen-free hot exhaust gas introduced by gas boiler is reduced by carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas is neutralized by high-temperature steam and high-temperature carbon, and hydrogen and carbon monoxide are decomposed to make 20-60% gas boiler discharge at 300-600 degrees C, nitrogen-free exhaust gas carbondioxide is reduced to portion of gas, replacing or no longer using external steam. The energy-saving and emission reduction of semi-coke gasification coal boiler in which oxygen and non-nitrogen waste gas are mixed into gasification agent after blue carbon is put into gasification furnace. The temperature is stable at 200-600 degrees C, temperature of the oxide layer in the furnace reaches 1100-1400 degrees C, and the temperature of reducing layer reaches 750-1100 degrees C. The temperature of the retorting layer reaches 500-800 degrees C. The gas from preheating zone to the gas outlet temperature of 300-700 degrees C and gas temperature of 300-700 degrees C are directly send to gas burner through heat-removal and high-efficiency dust removal to form closed circuit for various hot gas cycles. The gasification agent is 300-600 degrees C oxygen is mixed with gas boiler and mixed with 300-600 degrees C nitrogen-free waste heat as gasification agent to enter the gasifier. The gas produced by the gasifier is kept at 300-700 degrees C and then send directly to burner. The closed loop of heat and hot air is circulated, and temperature of various flows is 300-900?C. The non-nitrogen waste heat carbondioxide is reduced to original portion of the gas in the oxidation layer and reduction layer in the furnace. The oxygen used for the gasification of blue carbon and small amount of water vapor are used as starting gasification agents and formed the entire circulation system without nitrogen operation, using nitrogen-free waste heat carbondioxide into the gasifier and high-temperature thermal carbon energy, decomposition, reduction. The energy-saving and emission reduction of semi-coke gasification coal boiler in gas boiler, nitrogen-free gas and oxygen are converted into waste heat and main components are 20-60% carbondioxide, 30-40% water, and less than or equal to 0.5% nitrogen, waste heat gas carbondioxide 20-60% (no nitrogen) is send to the blue carbon furnace to reduce fuel gas through reducing layer during high temperature oxidation reaction of oxygen and carbon, various gas flows from the exhaust port of boiler. The blue carbon gasifier is mixed with oxygen gasification agent, gasification in the gasifier is maintained at 300-700 degrees C for thermal energy vaporization in closed circuit, and reduction process decomposes, restores and decomposes in matching state. The coal is broken through sieve of 30mm, separated out 10mm, 15mm, 20mm, 25mm, 30mm small coal, and remaining coal is mixed with 10-20% water, and 10mm, 15mm, 20mm, 25mm, 30mm cylinder pellets are put into blue carbon furnace to remove tar, sulfur, benzene and phenol from the nitrogen-free hot gas at 550-1050 degrees C, naphthalene becomes a blue carbon, which can be put into medium and large blue carbon gasifiers, blue carbon or coke, blue coal, charcoal, charcoal, tar free, low sulfur, high hardness, resistance to burning, and burning after oxygen combustion hot gas carbon dioxide mixing produces high temperatures in furnace and reacts with blue carbon to produce nitrogen-free gas, with heating value exceeding 2400 kiloCalorie per cubic meter. The nitrogen-free gas and oxygen are used to control temperature at 850-950 degrees C and nitrogen-free waste heat carbondioxide is discharged in a supercritical boiler, temperature is released to 400-750 degrees C directly back into blue carbon gasifier by entering waste hot gas above 400 degrees C into the blue carbon gasifier at full efficiency, and the nitrogen-free waste gas carbondioxide above 400 degrees C increase the reduction of carbon monoxide and hydrogen efficiency in the blue carbon furnace, reducing the carbon dioxide and water vapor in non-nitrogen waste heat above 600 degrees C in the reducing layer at high temperature of oxidation to replace the 15-30% coal. The oxygen enter the blue carbon when the temperature in the steam, water vapor, and hearth reaches 500-900 degrees C, blue carbon produces nitrogen-free gas, and the total thermal efficiency reaches 80%. The blue carbon gasification includes bituminous coal input into double-stage continuous gasification furnace, utilizing bottom of the furnace. The hot carbon to be gasified falls from the upper section and hot charcoal is carbonized in dry distillation state. The temperature of the gas produced in bottom or lower section of the furnace is above 300-600 degrees C and enter the blue carbon gasification system. The blue carbon is a product after dry distillation of bituminous coal, or uses coke, anthracite coal, charcoal, and is produced in gasification furnace. The purified gas after gas purification is the exhaust gas after combustion with oxygen in boiler. The carbon dioxide, water vapor are not return to gasification furnace excess carbon dioxide liquefaction into fine product recycling comprehensive utilization to achieve carbon emission zero. Preferred Conditions: The oxygen purity is greater than or equal to 99% when the nitrogen-free gas is produce and oxygen-enriched air is inefficient to use.

PN CN113648826-A; CN113648826-B
TI Cooperative removal of carbon dioxide and nitric            oxide based on calcium cycle comprises producing            unburned coal or biomass coke, reducing nitric oxide to            nitrogen by carbon monoxide under catalysis of            manganese modified calcium oxide catalyst and absorbing            carbon dioxide by catalyst.
AU ZHAO J
   ZHANG C
   ZHANG W
   LI Y
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2021D6810E
AB    NOVELTY - Cooperative removal of carbon dioxide and                nitric oxide based on calcium cycle comprises using                coal or biomass to calcinate manganese modified                calcium oxide catalyst, and producing unburned coal                or biomass coke, reacting coal char or biomass coke                with oxygen in the flue gas to generate carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide, and consuming the coke                by oxygen and nitric oxide, reducing nitric oxide                to nitrogen by carbon monoxide under the catalysis                of manganese modified calcium oxide catalyst, and                absorbing carbon dioxide by manganese modified                calcium oxide catalyst, oxidizing remaining carbon                monoxide in the flue gas to carbon dioxide by the                air, and absorbing the carbon dioxide by the                manganese modified calcium oxide catalyst.
   USE - Method for cooperatively removing carbon                dioxide and nitric oxide in coal-fired power plant                flue gas based on calcium cycle used in                environmental pollutant control and clean                combustion fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cooperatively removes carbon                dioxide and nitric oxide based on calcium                circulation using carbon monoxide generated by coal                coke or biomass coke oxidation in calcium                circulating carbonation process as denitration                reducing agent, and using manganese modified                calcium-based oxynitrifying agent and carbon                dioxide absorbing agent. The method realizes                efficient cooperative removing of carbon dioxide                and nitric oxide in the calcium circulating carbon                dioxide carbonation cycle. The carbon dioxide                capture efficiency is as high as 88%. The method is                simple and easy for industrial application, and                does not need to add additional carbon dioxide                reducing agent and another carbon dioxide reactor.                Carbon dioxide in the coal-fired power plant flue                gas are efficiently removed at the same time in the                carbonation reactor of the calcium cycle, and the                denitrate efficiency is high as 99% (preferably                99%).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preparation method                  of manganese modified calcium oxide catalyst                  utilizes limestone particles and manganese salt                  solution. The form of manganese in the manganese                  modified calcium oxide catalyst is manganese                  oxide. The manganese salt comprises manganese                  acetate, manganese chloride, manganese sulfate                  and/or manganese nitrate. The biomass comprises                  rice husks, straws, and/or trees.

TI Combined collecting recycling device used for carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide in flue gas, comprises carbon dioxide membrane separation units, nitrogen membrane separation unit and flue gas treatment system set with processing inlet.
AU WU Q
   XU J
   SHI L
AE BEIJING CCUS TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201831210N
AB    NOVELTY - A combined collecting recycling device comprises flue gas treatment system (10), first carbon dioxide membrane separation unit (20), second carbon dioxide membrane separation unit (30) and nitrogen membrane separation unit (80). The flue gas treatment system is provided with processing inlet and processing outlet, and used for processing flue gas to remove liquid impurities in first carbon dioxide membrane separation unit. The air inlet of nitrogen membrane separation unit is connected with first high pressure non-permeating gas outlet.
   USE - Combined collecting recycling device used for carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high enrichment degree of carbon dioxide, recycles nitrogen and avoids resource waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A combined collecting recycling device comprises flue gas treatment system (10), first carbon dioxide membrane separation unit (20), second carbon dioxide membrane separation unit (30) and nitrogen membrane separation unit (80). The flue gas treatment system is provided with processing inlet and processing outlet, and used for processing flue gas to remove liquid impurities in first carbon dioxide membrane separation unit. The first carbon dioxide membrane separation unit is provided with first air inlet, first carbon dioxide enriched gas outlet and first high pressure non-permeating gas outlet. The second carbon dioxide membrane separation unit provided with second gas inlet, second carbon dioxide enriched gas outlet and second high pressure non-permeating gas outlet. The nitrogen membrane separation unit is provided with nitrogen enriched gas outlet. The air inlet of nitrogen membrane separation unit is connected with first high pressure non-permeating gas outlet.

PN EP3733264-A1; WO2020221629-A1; AU2020265999-A1; CA3134718-A1; CN113784777-A; IN202147055113-A; EP3962630-A1; BR112021018964-A2; US2022185666-A1
TI Hydrogen and carbon dioxide production involves            feeding an input gas containing hydrogen, carbon            dioxide and other components or impurities through an            adsorbent material suitable to adsorb carbon dioxide in            pressure vessel.
AU STREB A
   HEFTI M
   GAZZANI M
   MAZOTTI M
   MAZZOTTI M
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2020A70386
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen and carbon dioxide production                involves feeding an input gas containing hydrogen,                carbon dioxide and other components or impurities                through an adsorbent material suitable to adsorb                carbon dioxide in a pressure vessel under a high                pressure, and withdrawing from the pressure vessel                gas stream which is hydrogen-rich product gas                containing less carbon dioxide than the input gas                due to adsorption of carbon dioxide in the                adsorbent material. The pressure is lowered in the                pressure vessel to a target intermediate pressure,                which is lower than the high pressure to cause the                adsorbent material to release another gas stream.                The adsorbent material is purged by passing carbon                dioxide-rich purge stream through the adsorbent                material for obtaining a third gas which is a purge                gas containing impurities desorbed from the                material. The pressure in the pressure vessel is                lowered to a low pressure, which is lower than the                intermediate pressure. The vessel is re-pressurized                to the high pressure.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the hydrogen and carbon                dioxide in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with high purity hydrogen and                high purity carbon dioxide in a single separation                unit, achieved reduction of equipment cost and                complexity compared, high purity carbon dioxide                starting from the bottom and any adsorbed                impurities at the bottom of the vessel are replaced                by carbon dioxide due to the stronger                affinity.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Emulsion for stimulating subterranean formations and used as treatment fluid in wellbore comprises internal phase containing acid, external phase containing supercritical carbon dioxide, and nanoparticles that stabilize the phases.
AU AL-HARBI B G
   AL-OTAIBI F M
   AL-KHALDI M H
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201889969T
AB    NOVELTY - An emulsion (100) comprises internal phase containing acid (104); external phase containing supercritical carbon dioxide (102); and nanoparticles (106) that stabilize the internal phase and external phase.
   USE - An emulsion for stimulating subterranean formations and used as treatment fluid in wellbore.
   ADVANTAGE - Extraction efficiency is increased. Emulsion enables rapid and effective clean up and liquid recovery after wellbore stimulation. Emulsion can transition to gaseous phase and be vented from wellbore as gaseous carbon dioxide. Emulsion is more stable than acid-in-diesel emulsion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) process which involves forming an emulsion for stimulation of formation by mixing nanoparticles and carbon dioxide at a temperature and pressure sufficient to convert the carbon dioxide into supercritical carbon dioxide; and mixing acid with nanoparticles and supercritical carbon dioxide to give a mixture where acid contains hydrochloric acid, citric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, hydrofluoric acid, and/or chelating agent; and
   (2) manufacturing an emulsion (100) which involves mixing carbon dioxide with nanoparticles (106); applying heat to convert the carbon dioxide to supercritical carbon dioxide (102); and mixing acid (104) with supercritical carbon dioxide and nanoparticles at a ratio of acid to supercritical carbon dioxide of 30-70%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the acid-in-supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion.
   Emulsion (100)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide (102)
   Acid (104)
   Nanoparticles (106)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Acid contains hydrochloric acid, citric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, hydrofluoric acid, and/or chelating agent. Ratio of concentration of acid to concentration of supercritical carbon dioxide is 30-70%. Acid contains hydrochloric acid. Nanoparticles contain hydrophobic nanoparticles with concentration of greater than or equal to 0.1 wt.%. Emulsion further contains corrosion inhibitor. Concentration of corrosion inhibitor is 0.25-0.6 vol.%, preferably greater than or equal to 0.25 vol.%. Preferred Process: Method includes blending the mixture to give emulsion, and applying heat to give the temperature sufficient to convert the carbon dioxide into supercritical carbon dioxide. Mixing the acid with nanoparticles and supercritical carbon dioxide includes adding the acid at drop-wise rate. Method includes adding a corrosion inhibitor to acid prior to mixing the acid with nanoparticles and supercritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Parameters: Temperature is greater than or equal to 40 degrees C and pressure is 1100 psi.

PN CN108545819-A
TI Neutralizing printing and dyeing wastewater by using recycled carbon dioxide instead of waste acid involves recovering carbon dioxide from waste gas in industrial park, liquefying and purifying is store in carbon dioxide storage tank.
AU CAO J
   LI C
   QIAN T
   XUE C
   FANG F
   FENG Q
   ZHOU S
AE UNIV HOHAI (UYHO-C)
GA 2018758676
AB    NOVELTY - Neutralizing printing and dyeing wastewater by using recycled carbon dioxide instead of waste acid involves recovering the carbon dioxide from the waste gas in the industrial park, liquefying and purifying is store in a carbon dioxide storage tank to obtain liquid carbon dioxide. The obtained liquid carbon dioxide is vaporized by a vaporizer and then introduced into the tubular mixer by the self-controlled dosing system. The printing and dyeing wastewater is adjusted by the regulating tank and uniformly mixed with carbon dioxide in the tubular mixer. The printing and dyeing wastewater passes through the tube mixer and then enters the mixing tank to further mix the wastewater with carbon dioxide in the tank to obtain desired process.
   USE - Method for neutralizing printing and dyeing wastewater by using recycled carbon dioxide instead of waste acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to neutralize printing and dyeing wastewater by using recycled carbon dioxide instead of waste acid, which has improved biochemical treatment efficiency, and reduces waste water processing cost and promotes recycling of carbon source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide after the vaporizer is depressurized to 400-500 MPa through the pressure reducing valve. The carbon dioxide is introduced into the tubular mixer by continuous introduction, and the ventilation amount is 1-2 L/minute. The outlet pressure when the carbon dioxide is introduced is 2.0-5.0 kg/cm2, and the carbon dioxide micro-bubbles are uniformly mixed with the printing and dyeing wastewater, where the diameter of the carbon dioxide micro-bubbles is less than 50 mu m. The pH online monitoring system is arranged in front of the mixing pool, and the pH value of the wastewater is fed back to the self-controlled feeding system in real time. The self-controlled dosing system controls the electric valve opening degree to realize the automatic adjustment control of the carbon dioxide dosage by comparing real-time pH value of the wastewater and setting the pH value. The self-controlled dosing system has a set pH range of 7.0-9.0.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN KR2020001407-A; KR2078280-B1
TI Preparing alcoholic beverage comprises e.g. contacting gas-liquid for converting low concentration carbon dioxide containing exhaust gas into high concentration carbon dioxide by spraying aqueous solution of alanine metal salt.
AU JUNG S
   BAK K T
   YOUN M
   LEE J
   HYUN P B
   PARK I G
   PARK Y S
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2020048241
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alcoholic beverage comprises (a) contacting gas-liquid for converting low concentration carbon dioxide containing less than or equal to 5000 ppm exhaust gas into high concentration carbon dioxide containing greater than or equal to 20000 ppm exhaust gas by spraying aqueous solution of alanine metal salt in which ammonium ion of alanine is converted into active amino group with carbon dioxide absorbent in scrubber, and (b) regenerating carbon dioxide absorbent by heating the carbon dioxide absorbent absorbing carbon dioxide through gas-liquid contact and reusing the carbon dioxide absorbent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alcoholic beverage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves working environment, reduces indoor carbon dioxide concentration, and controls temperature of indoor air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fermentation process comprising the above steps (a) and (b).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the exhaust gas containing greater than or equal to 20000 ppm high concentration carbon dioxide during preparation of alcoholic beverage, and the exhaust gas includes microorganism. In the (b), the regenerated carbon dioxide absorbent is confirmed by measuring the pH of the carbon dioxide absorbent. The step (a) is carried out by adjusting concentration of alanine to form alanine metal salt in aqueous solution to 0.1-5 M and spraying the carbon dioxide absorbent nozzle and scrubber does not bubble. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent comprises alkali metal hydroxide which activates ammonium ion of alanine to amino ion in aqueous solution. The high concentration carbon dioxide contains greater than or equal to 5000 ppm exhaust gas during fermentation process. The fermenter is microorganism.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN GB2592841-A
TI Extraction of carbon dioxide from biomethane by            digesting carbon dioxide in biomethane with algae and            venting off methane.
AU MORRIS D
AE AUTICHEM LTD (AUTI-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6674L
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of carbon dioxide from biomethane                involves digesting the carbon dioxide in the                biomethane with algae and venting off                methane.
   USE - The process is useful for extraction of carbon                dioxide from biomethane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The addition of carbon dioxide gas to algae                growing in wastewater can provide extra carbon                source and consequentially increase the nutrient                removal efficiency from the wastewater. Improved                carbon capture and biogas treatment process                provides more efficient mixing of liquid and or                gases containing sources of carbon and microalgae.                The improved mixing in the system enables the                microalgae to sequestrate any carbon with increased                productivity. The biomass produced by this system                is suitable for conversion to a range of other                valuable products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a process which involves intimately mixing                a carbon dioxide containing feed, algae and water                allowing the algae to absorb the carbon dioxide and                convert the carbon dioxide into protein and lipids                which are recovered where the pH increases through                the process; and
   (2) an apparatus for carbon capture comprising                means to deliver a carbon dioxide containing feed                to a dissolver and means to deliver algae and a                liquid to the dissolver where the carbon dioxide is                intimately mixed with the algae containing liquid                in the dissolver and containing an absorber vessel                where the algae absorbs the carbon dioxide and                further containing a converter vessel where the                carbon dioxide is converted to protein and                lipids.

GA 202176186Y
AB    NOVELTY - A radioactive pollution supercritical carbon                dioxide chemical decontamination method comprises                putting radioactive pollution-containing component                into the decontamination chamber, starting the                booster pump and pressurizing stored carbon                dioxide, starting decontamination agent carrying                pump to pressurize the decontamination agent,                starting constant temperature heating device,                reacting supercritical carbon dioxide and detergent                on the surface of the radioactive                pollution-containing component, separating                radioactive pollution from the surface of the                component and entering supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid, feeding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                containing radioactive pollution into the                separation chamber container through the valve for                reducing pressure, separating radioactive pollution                from the carbon dioxide, precipitating, entering                into waste collector, filtering carbon dioxide,                entering into air storage tank for the next                decontamination cycle use.

It has good reachability, small                damage, recyclable and reusable decontamination                medium, and little second pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                radioactive pollution supercritical carbon dioxide                chemical decontamination device.

PN CN112813454-A; CN214830706-U
TI Natural gas reforming combined carbon dioxide hydrogen production generating system comprises e.g. carbon dioxide battery system, where anode and cathode in carbon dioxide battery system are connected by lead wire to supply power outwards.
AU YAO H
   ZHANG C
   WANG X
   HOU J
   WU Q
   CHEN H
   MU X
   SONG P
   WANG C
   ZHANG Y
   SUI Y
   WANG R
   HOU H
   WANG S
AE CNOOC GAS & POWER GROUP CO LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 202158519P
AB    NOVELTY - Natural gas reforming combined carbon dioxide hydrogen production generating system comprises a natural gas steam reforming hydrogen production system and a carbon dioxide battery system. The natural gas steam reforming hydrogen production system is connected with the carbon dioxide battery system. The hydrogen and carbon dioxide generated by the natural gas steam reforming hydrogen production system enter into the conductive medium in the anode chamber of the carbon dioxide battery system through the connected pipeline. The anode and cathode in the carbon dioxide battery system are connected by a lead wire to supply power outwards, and hydrogen is generated in the anode chamber in the carbon dioxide battery system.
   USE - Used as natural gas reforming combined carbon dioxide hydrogen production generating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: has the function of purifying hydrogen gas; realizes the separation of hydrogen; and eliminates the need for purification and separation devices (e.g. membrane separation devices, and pressure swing adsorption devices) in the traditional natural gas steam reforming process system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing hydrogen by reforming combined carbon dioxide with natural gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the natural gas reforming combined carbon dioxide hydrogen production generating system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide battery system, and anode is a composite electrode material comprising noble metal/carbon. The cathode is metal sodium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the carbon dioxide battery system, the conductive medium in the anode chamber is the pure seawater or water. The conductive medium in the cathode chamber is non water-based conductive organic solution.

PN WO2022207533-A1
TI Converting waste to chemicals, comprises            performing anaerobic treatment of waste stream selected            from solid, aqueous and gaseous waste streams            originating from microbial fermentation process,            reforming biogas and using carbon monoxide comprised by            syngas generated for synthesis of phosgene.
AU BLOECHER C
   ZABALZA G
   CHEN L
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2022C7583N
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises: (a) performing anaerobic                treatment of at least one waste stream selected                from solid, aqueous and gaseous waste streams                originating from at least one microbial                fermentation process, where the anaerobic treatment                results in biogas formation; (b) reforming the                biogas so that a syngas comprising carbon monoxide                and optionally hydrogen is generated; and (c) using                the carbon monoxide comprised by the syngas                generated in step (b) for the synthesis of                phosgene, where the carbon dioxide is added to the                biogas prior to or during the reforming in step                (b).
   USE - The method is used for converting waste to                chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures conversion to chemicals                like phosgene or isocyanate to generate higher                value out of the waste and to reduce carbon dioxide                emissions by using at least part of the carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The microbial fermentation                  process is a process generating a product                  selected from alcohols, sugar alcohols, organic                  acids, sugars, antibiotics, amines and amino                  acids. At least a part of the carbon dioxide                  originates from the microbial fermentation                  process which produces the waste stream treated                  in step (a). The biogas generated in step (a) is                  purified prior to step (b) in order to at least                  partially remove hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon                  dioxide. The amount of added carbon dioxide is                  selected such that the molar ratio of carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen produced in step (b) is at                  least 10:1. The amount of carbon dioxide added is                  selected to achieve a molar ratio of carbon                  dioxide to the methane comprised by the biogas of                  0.7:1-1.3:1. Any carbon dioxide produced as                  by-product in step (a) and not used in step (b)                  is subjected to electrolysis to generate carbon                  monoxide. A solid waste stream and a liquid waste                  stream originating from the same microbial                  fermentation process are combined prior to the                  anaerobic treatment in step (a). The product of                  the microbial fermentation process is an organic                  amine and the method further comprises (d)                  phosgenating the organic amine using at least a                  part of the phosgene produced in step (c) so that                  an isocyanate is generated. At least 50 mol.%                  methane used in step (b) originates from the                  anaerobic digestion of biomass.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, efficient and environment-friendly method for preparing nitrogen-sulfur co-doped porous carbon ball mixed matrix film. The prepared mixed matrix membrane has high carbon dioxide permeability coefficient and CO2/N2 separation factor. The method uses cheap and easily obtained glucose as carbon source and is rich in sulfur.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves taking glucose and thiourea in water solution to mix uniformly, then at a frequency of 20-50 KiloHertz, 20-90degrees Celsius of ultrasonic for 0.5-12 hours to disperse uniformly, then the mixed solution in the polytetrafluoroethylene lining of the hydrothermal kettle at 100-200degrees Celsius water heat reaction for 8-24 hours, the product with distilled water to wash for 3-5 times, in 60degrees Celsius of vacuum drying box for 24 hours obtaining the nitrogen-sulphur co-doped glucose carbon ball precursor, named as product A, adding the product A and activator to de-ionized water to mix uniformly, where the mass fraction of product A in the de-ionized water is 2-45%, drying for 12 hours in the vacuum oven at 120degrees Celsius, grinding the dried product in the ceramic boat, keeping the temperature for 1-3 hours under the protection of nitrogen at 500-800degrees Celsius in the tubular furnace, using de-ionized water to wash to neutral, drying for 24 hours in a vacuum oven at 60degrees Celsius, after grinding, marking the obtained porous carbon ball co-doped with nitrogen and sulphur as product B. The product B is added into the solvent C needed by the film preparation, ultrasonically processed for 0.5-12 hours under the condition of frequency of 20-50 KiloHertz and temperature of 20-90degrees Celsius to disperse uniformly, naming as solution D, then added the polymer matrix into the solution D, the magnetic stirrer is used at the temperature range of 20-80degrees Celsius, the rotating speed range is 100-2000 rpm, mechanically stirred for 2-24 hours until the polymer is completely dissolved, standing and defoamed for 12-48 hours to obtain the casting solution E, uniformly coated the casting film liquid E on the clean glass plate or polytetrafluoroethylene plate by the film coating machine, controlled the thickness of the wet film to be 300-1500 microm, then placed the coated glass plate or polytetrafluoroethylene plate in a vacuum oven of 25-80degrees Celsius for vacuum drying for 12-48 hours, then dried for 8-48 hours in the vacuum oven at 80-150degrees Celsius, removed the solvent after removing the film from the glass plate or polytetrafluoroethylene plate. The mass fraction of glucose in the water solution is 3-15% and the mass ratio of glucose and thiourea is 1:(0.5-2). The mass ratio of the product A and the activator is 1:(0.5-2). The gas separation performance test adopts constant pressure variable volume method, the effective area of the film permeation is 10-100 cm2, the scavenging is hydrogen, the scavenging flow rate is 10-100 mL/minutes, the feeding gas flow rate is 10-60 mL/minutes, the flow rate of the raw material side and the permeable side is measured by the flowmeter, the content of the penetrating side component is tested by gas chromatography; the test temperature is 20-100degrees Celsius and the pressure difference is 0.1-1.5 megaPascal. Preferred Components: The activating agent is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium oxalate, zinc chloride. The solvent C is N, N-dimethyl acetamide, N, N-dimethyl formamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methyl pyrrolidone, tetrahydrofuran, the type of the polymer matrix is polyether block amide, sulfonated polyether ether ketone, polyimide, cellulose acetate, dimethyl silicone polymer, where the mass ratio of the product B to the polymer matrix is (0.01-0.1):1 and the mass ratio of the polymer matrix to the solvent C is (0.02-0.15):1. The dry film thickness of the prepared mixed substrate film is 20-200 microns.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN113387633-A
TI Toughened polystyrene material comprises polystyrene foam particles, cement, water, fly ash, kaolin, surfactant, hydrophobic agent, carbon dioxide and organically modified graphene oxide.
AU WANG X
AE SHENZHEN XINHEXIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B1978R
AB    NOVELTY - Toughened polystyrene material comprises 10-20 pts. wt. polystyrene foam particles, 200-300 pts. wt. cement, 100-150 pts. wt. water, 15-20 pts. wt. fly ash, 10-20 pts. wt. kaolin, 2-4 pts. wt. surfactant, 3-8 pts. wt. hydrophobic agent, 5-10 pts. wt. carbon dioxide and 3-8 pts. wt. organically modified graphene oxide.
   USE - As toughened polystyrene material.
   ADVANTAGE - The toughened polystyrene material has excellent performance, and improves thermal stability of the polystyrene material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the toughened polystyrene material, which involves adding polystyrene foam particles, cement, fly ash and kaolin to water, stirring and uniformly mixing, adding hydrophobic agent, surfactant and organic modified graphene oxide, stirring and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixture, pouring the mixture to a closed mold, evacuating the air in the mold, introducing carbon dioxide, after 30 minutes, molding by a hydraulic press, and demolding to obtain the toughened polystyrene material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The organically modified graphene oxide is prepared by (i) adding graphene oxide to N,N-dimethylformamide, ultrasonically processing, mechanically stirring, adding ethylenediamine, uniformly mixing, performing stirring and reflux reaction, washing the reaction product, and drying to obtain ethylenediamine modified graphene oxide, (ii) adding ethylenediamine-modified graphene oxide to sodium hydroxide solution, uniformly mixing, adding cyanuric chloride, and performing low-temperature reaction to obtain cyanuric chloride modified graphene oxide, (iii) adding cyanuric chloride-modified graphene oxide to acetone solvent, uniformly mixing, adding 4-hydroxythiophenol and sodium hydroxide, performing water bath reaction under nitrogen atmosphere, washing the reaction product, recrystallizing and drying to obtain p-thiophenol cyanuric chloride modified graphene oxide, (iv) adding p-thiophenol cyanuric chloride modified graphene oxide to tetrahydrofuran solvent, uniformly mixing under nitrogen atmosphere, adding 2-methyl-2-adamantyl methacrylate, and benzoyl dimethyl ether photoinitiator, performing click reaction under 200-300 W UV light irradiation, centrifuging the reaction product, washing, and drying to obtain the organically modified graphene oxide. The water bath reaction in step (iii) involves reacting at 25-40 degrees C for 12 hours, increasing the temperature to 85-95 degrees C, and continuously reacting for 12 hours. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the reflux reaction is carried out at 50-80 degrees C for 6-8 hours. In step (ii), the low-temperature reaction is carried out at -5 to -3 degrees C for 12-16 hours. In step (iv), the click reaction is carried out at 50-70 degrees C for 2-6 hours. Preferred Composition: The volume-to-mass ratio of ethylenediamine and graphene oxide in step (i) is (30-50) ml:(2-4) g. The mass ratio of ethylenediamine modified graphene oxide, cyanuric chloride and sodium hydroxide solution in step (ii) is 10:(12-14):(80-120). The content of the sodium hydroxide solution in step (ii) is 10-15 %mass. The mass ratio of cyanuric chloride modified graphene oxide, 4-hydroxythiophenol and sodium hydroxide in step (iii) is 10:(16-20):(3-5). The mass ratio of 2-methyl-2-adamantyl methacrylate, p-thiophenol cyanuric chloride modified graphene oxide and benzoyl dimethyl ether in step (iv) is 100:(40-60):(0.5-1). Preferred Property: The particle size of the fly ash is 100-200 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfate or sodium dodecyl sulfonate. The hydrophobic agent is modified polymethyl methacrylate.
