TI Nanocluster catalyst useful in gas diffusion electrode for converting carbon dioxide.
AU LEE D I
   SEOCK K M
   SUNG H
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 2020A8756D
AB    NOVELTY - Nanocluster catalyst (I), is claimed.
   USE - The nanocluster catalyst is useful in gas diffusion electrode for converting carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanocluster catalyst has excellent economic efficiency compared to the gold nanocluster catalyst composed of gold atoms, and excellent ability to convert carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Nanocluster catalyst of formula (Au12M32(SR)30) (I), is claimed.
   SR = organic thiol-based ligand containing fluorine group, where R in SR is fluorine group-containing aryl group having 6-30C atoms, fluorine group-containing arylalkane group having 6-40C atoms, fluorine group-containing heteroaryl group having 6-30C atoms, or heteroaryl alkane group having 6-40C atoms; and
   M = Ag or Cu.

PN RU2725912-C1
TI Method to control pressure of transcript of refrigerating unit on carbon dioxide gas.
AU USHAKOV P V
   MIFTAKHOV R M
   MALUNOV V A
   DOLGIKH S D
   SHIGABUTDINOVA A S
AE CRYOGENIC TECH SCI TECH COMPLEX STOCK CO (CRYO-C)
GA 2020702350
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to refrigeration engineering, primarily to methods of adjusting pressure of transcritical cycle of refrigerating plant on carbon dioxide gas for ships with unlimited navigation area, when temperature of cooling water reaches 36C, and ambient temperature of 60C. In operation of the plant and changes in heat load, the pressure of the gas cooler and the evaporator (equilibrium boiling point) is kept constant by means of regulators, suction pressure is controlled with positive feedback by compressor capacity; overheating after evaporator supports expansion device controlled by controller. When the plant is stopped, the expansion device is switched off, the pressure of the evaporator is reduced to 6 bar, the electric valve of the pipeline is connected pneumatically connecting the discharge line to the carbon dioxide gas capacity, after termination of pressure drop in the line and gas cooler, the valve and the compressor are switched off and the adjustment of the evaporator pressure regulator is restored.
   USE - Cooling or freezing equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is method simplification.
5 cl, 1 dwg

AE XIAN YUBIN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201942921L

A cylinder is manufactured with 0.7 cubic meters liquid capacity. An upper name plate is removed. Liquid is conveyed into a connecting sleeve on the liquid storage cylinder. PT P
PN CN109553521-A
TI Extracting palmitic acid from lotus seeds comprises soaking seeds in water, disinfecting, blending, adding water, solid-liquid separating, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, adsorbing upper layer and analyzing by resin and drying.
AU REN X
AE REN X (RENX-Individual)
GA 2019324935
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting palmitic acid from lotus seeds comprises soaking the lotus seeds in clean water and washing, placing the washed lotus seeds in a sterilizer for disinfection, transferring into a blender, adding water, stirring in the mixer, subjecting the stirred mixture to solid-liquid separation using a filter, removing the lotus seed residue to obtain a mixture, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting at an analytical temperature of 20-40 degrees C and analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, extracting after passing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 5-15 l/hour, allowing the extract to stand at room temperature, taking the upper layer, adsorbing the upper layer and analyzing by a resin and drying to obtain palmitic acid extract.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting palmitic acid from lotus seeds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the purity of palmitic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises taking the sterilized lotus seeds into the blender, adding water into the blender for agitation, setting the speed of the mixer to 800-1200 revolutions/minute, turning on the mixer and stirring, analyzing at 20-40 degrees C at a pressure of 5-15 MPa. The extraction is carried out under carbon dioxide flow rate of 5-15 l/hour, preferably 5 l/hour at an analytical temperature of 20 degrees C and analytical pressure of 5 MPa. The washed lotus seeds are placed in the sterilizer for disinfection, preferably disinfection in an ozone sterilizer.

PN CN109453801-A; CN109453801-B
TI Preparing ferric oxide-zirconium oxide-sulfur            oxide molecular sieve complex catalyst useful for            desorbing carbon dioxide rich solution comprises e.g.            dissolving soluble iron salt, performing ultrasonic            treatment and calcining.
AU LIANG Z
   ZHANG X
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 201925440N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ferric oxide-zirconium oxide-sulfur                oxide molecular sieve complex catalyst for                desorbing a carbon dioxide rich solution, comprises                (i) dissolving the soluble iron salt and zirconium                sulfate in a methanol solution to obtain a mixed                solution A, (ii) heating the mixture A at 50                degrees C for 1 hours to obtain a mixture B, (iii)                cooling the mixture B to room temperature, and                adding the molecular sieve powder and stirring for                5 hours to obtain a mixed solution C, (iv)                performing ultrasonic treatment of the mixture C                for more than 0.5 hours to obtain a mixture D, and                (v) drying the mixed solution D and                calcining.
   USE - The complex catalyst is useful for desorbing a                carbon dioxide rich solution (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple. The catalyst is easy to                separate, has good stability, can be                recycled.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass ratio of ferric oxide to zirconium oxide is                  1:10-1:2. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of                  zirconium oxide and molecular sieve is 1:8-1:1.                  In the step (iii), the SAPO series molecular                  sieve is MCM series, SBA series, SAPO series, H,                  HY and/or HUSY-6 molecular sieve. Preferred                  Method: In the step (v), the calcining                  temperature is 500-750 degrees C and the                  sintering time is greater than or equal to 3                  hours. In the step (iv), the ultrasonic treatment                  time is 0.5-2 hours. In the step (ii), the                  heating temperature is 40-80 degrees C. In the                  step (ii), the heat treatment time is 1-4 hours.                  In the step (iii), the stirring time is 5-12                  hours. In the step (v), the drying temperature is                  100-200 degrees C and the drying time is greater                  than or equal to 8 hours.

PT P
PN CN109231265-A
TI Preparing hydrophobic titanium dioxide comprises microwave reacting supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with passivation solution (oleic acid and n-heptane) and titanium alcohol liquid, high temperature pressure relief reaction and drying.
AU ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201910277M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobic titanium dioxide comprises (i) dissolving oleic acid in n-heptane, sealing and stirring until dissolved completely to obtain a passivation solution, (ii) adding n-butyl titanate to absolute ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing for 10-30 minutes to obtain titanium alcohol solution, (iii) placing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in a sealed reactor at a constant temperature and constant pressure, microwave reacting for 4-8 hours, spraying passivation solution and titanium alcohol liquid above the surface of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid level, (iv) subjecting to a high temperature pressure relief reaction for 2-5 hours and purging with nitrogen to obtain a precipitate and (v) adding into absolute ethanol, soaking, taking out and drying under UV light for 2-5 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrophobic titanium dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the high cost of the hydrophobic titanium dioxide treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: in the step (i): the concentration of oleic acid in n-heptane is 2-10 g/l; and the stirring speed is 1000-2000 revolutions/minute. The concentration of n-butyl titanate in absolute ethanol in the step (ii) is 100-200 g/l. The volume ratio of the passivation solution to the titanium alcohol liquid in the step (iii) is 1:3-6, where the passivation solution is sprayed at a rate of 10-30 ml/minute and the droplet size of the spray is 0.2-0.5 mm. The spray rate of the titanium alcohol liquid is 25-40 ml/minute and the droplet size of the spray is 0.05-0.1 mm. The amount of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is 200-250 vol.% of the titanium alcohol liquor. Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises ultrasonically dispersing at an ultrasonic frequency of 20-40 kHz at 50-60 degrees C. The microwave reaction in the step (iii) comprises reacting at 40-60 degrees C at a pressure 4-6 MPa at a microwave power of 500-1000 W. The high temperature pressure relief reaction in the step (iv) comprises reacting at 100-150 degrees C at pressure relief rate of 0.2-0.4 MPa/minutes and purging treatment at 80-100 degrees C, preferably 300-350 degrees C at a gas flow rate of 10-20 ml/minute, preferably 10-15 ml/minute, where the reaction adopts a gradient temperature pressure release reaction at (p) 120-150 degrees C at a rate of 0.1-0.2 MPa/minute for 10-20 minutes, (q) at 200-230 degrees C at a pressure of 0.05-0.1 MPa for 5-10 minutes and (r) at 300-340 degrees C for remaining time. The step (v) comprises drying at UV light intensity of 3-10 W/cm2 at 70-80 degrees C.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.)
MC E11-P; E35-K01
IP C01G-023/053

PN KR2018036192-A; KR1852396-B1
TI Nitrogen-free metal-organic framework, useful as            acidic gas adsorbent for absorbing acidic gas including            carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide and            nitrogen dioxide.
AU HYUNG C Y
   JONG N K
   SANG S H
   TAE S J
   DONG W C
   KANG H C
   HEE T B
   PHANI R
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201830505C
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-free metal-organic framework having                specific surface area of 1900-45000 m2/g, is                claimed.
   USE - The metal-organic framework is useful as                acidic gas adsorbent for absorbing acidic gas                including carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur                dioxide and nitrogen dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic framework can produce under                low temperature and low pressure conditions via                solid phase reaction, and can provide excellent                adsorption properties to carbon dioxide. The method                is simple and economical, does not use solvent,                increases synthesis yield by increasing the space                time yield significantly, and produces                metal-organic framework in mass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing metal-organic framework, comprising (a)                mixing metal salt powder and terephthalic acid                powder to prepare a powder mixture, (b) pulverizing                the powder mixture to produce pulverized                powder-mixture, (c) adding mineralizing agent to                the pulverized powder-mixture, and (d) heating the                pulverized powder-mixture to produce bulk                composite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic                  framework: is MIL-101 (M), where M is Fe, Al, Cr,                  Ti, Sc, Cu or vanadium; and has porous structure,                  and carbon dioxide selectivity to methane of                  greater than or equal to 40 at 25-75 degrees C                  according to formula-1, as given in                  specification. The metal salt includes iron,                  aluminum, chromium, titanium, scandium, copper                  and/or vanadium nitrates, sulfates, phosphates,                  carbonates, acetates, chlorides, halides,                  hydroxides and acetyl acetone salts. The                  metal-organic framework is produced by preparing                  nitrogen-free metal organic framework without                  addition of solvent and hydrofluoric acid.                  Preferred Method: The step (b) is carried out for                  2-12 hours. The step (d) is carried out at                  150-250 degrees C for 5-15 hours. The step (c) is                  carried out by adding 0.1-3 equivalents of                  mineralizing agent to the metal salt. The method                  further comprises (e) washing the bulk composite                  using water and/or organic solvent, and drying at                  70-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mineralizing agent                  includes organic acid, preferably, acetic acid,                  trifluoroacetic acid, trifluoromethane sulfonic                  acid, oxalic acid, tartaric acid, formic acid,                  succinic acid and benzoic acid.
   EXAMPLE - 4 g Chromium(III) nitrate nonahydrate and 2.2                g terephthalic acid were pulverized, mixed for 20                minutes to obtain homogeneous mixture. The mixture                was poured into 100 ml Teflon (RTM:                Polytetrafluoroethylene) lined autoclave, 0.58 ml                acetic acid was added, heated to 220 degrees C for                8 hours, and cooled to ambient temperature to                obtain dark green bulk product. The bulk product                was grinded, washed with hot water to remove                unreacted chromium(III) nitrate and dried in oven                at 80 degrees C in air atmosphere. The grinded                product was refluxed in dimethylformamide                overnight, filtered and washed with                dimethylformamide at the same time. Dried at 100                degrees C in air atmosphere, the product was                refluxed in 0.03M ammonium fluoride solution for 8                hours, filtered, washed with hot water, and dried                at 120 degrees C overnight in air atmosphere to                obtain MIL-101 (Cr) powder with yield of 70%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Carbon-dioxide trans-critical cycle of waste heat power generation system designing method, involves converting integral model of system into multiple single-circuit systems, where system is controlled by each single circuit-system.

AE ELECTRIC POWER RES INST GUANGDONG POWER (CSPG-C)
   ELECTRIC POWER RES INST GUANGDONG POWER (CSPG-C)
GA 2018220945
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a component part model, where the model is provided with an evaporator, and a condenser. The component part model is connected through pre-set input output relation to obtain an integral model of a waste heat power generation system. The integral model of the system is converted into multiple single-circuit systems by establishing a partial least squares (PLS) frame. A controller is designed for each single-circuit system, where the integral model of the waste heat power generation system is controlled by the single circuit-system independently.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide trans-critical cycle of a waste heat power generation system designing method.

PT P
PN CN107540009-A
TI Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate, involves mixing carbide slag, ammonium chloride, hydrogen peroxide and water, filtering to obtain pure calcium chloride solution, adding dispersant, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and carbonizing.
AU DU G
   LIU L
   ZHU H
AE BEIJING YIYIXING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018035351
AB    NOVELTY - The method of preparing nano-calcium carbonate using carbide slag, involves mixing carbide slag, ammonium chloride, hydrogen peroxide and water according to a certain ratio, filtering to obtain pure calcium chloride solution, adding dispersant, introducing carbon dioxide gas, performing carbonation reaction, filtering the product, washing and drying to obtain nano-calcium carbonate product.
   USE - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate using carbide slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the solid waste carbide slag as the raw material, and thus greatly reducing the production cost of preparing the nano calcium carbonate and enabling the industrial solid waste to be reused. The method avoids the need of cleaning and calcining the carbide slag, and uses carbon dioxide gas obtained from the nearby factory flue gas so as to save water and energy, and recycles the leaching solution so that the production cost is greatly reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials used in the preparation of nano-calcium carbonate comprise carbide slag which is industrial waste discharge, industrial grade ammonium chloride, industrial grade hydrogen peroxide, industrial grade dispersant and carbon dioxide with volume fraction of greater than 10%. The carbon dioxide gas for carbonization uses near-factory flue gas. Preferred Processes: The method of preparing nano-calcium carbonate, involves (i) mixing carbide slag, ammonium chloride and hydrogen peroxide with water, and stirring evenly, reacting for 10-30 minutes where the mass ratio of carbide slag and ammonium chloride is 1:0.5-2, the hydrogen peroxide is added in an amount of 1-5% of the mass of carbide slag, and the mass ratio of water and carbide slag is 6-10:1, (ii) filtering the product obtained in step (i) to obtain pure calcium chloride solution, adding dispersant to the filtrate in an amount of 0.5-5% of mass of carbide slag, fully stirring with a stirrer having linear speed of greater than 10 m/second, introducing carbon dioxide gas while stirring, then carrying out carbonation reaction, when the pH of the solution reaches 7.0, then stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide gas and, completing the reaction, (iii) filtering the product obtained in step (ii), washing, drying and dispersing to obtain nano-calcium carbonate The leaching solution is prepared from calcium carbonate, the filtrate is ammonium chloride solution, and the solution is recycled as a leaching agent, so that only a certain amount of ammonium chloride is recycled in the next leaching reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersant is sodium polyacrylate or triethanolamine.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN112871092-A; CN215049772-U
TI Modular device useful for producing methane by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises pre-heater, reactor, quench cooler and drier, which are connected in series and the reactor is tubular fixed bed reactor with cooling medium.
AU FENG Y
   SONG T
   NI H
   LIU M
   YAN Y
   LI X
   XIAO W
AE SHANGHAI AEROSPACE SMART ENERGY TECHNOLO (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202164416K
AB    NOVELTY - Modular device comprises a pre-heater (1), reactor (2), quench cooler (3) and drier (4) which are connected in series in turn, where the reactor is a tubular fixed bed reactor with cooling medium, the shell side of the reactor is provided with a reinforced heat exchange mechanism, the reinforced heat exchange mechanism is at least one of baffle plate, bulk filler, regular filler, circulating cooling medium is filled in the shell side, the top part of the reactor is provided with a raw material gas inlet, the bottom is provided with a reaction product outlet pipe and is connected with the raw material gas inlet and the reaction product outlet and the tube side is filled with methanation catalyst.
   USE - The modular device is useful for producing methane by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modular device: has simple structure and convenient operation, adopts a tubular fixed-bed reactor with a cooling medium, can remove the heat generated by the reaction in time, and can improve the service life of the catalyst and the efficiency of the methanation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing methane by carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation by using the modular device, comprising preheating the mixed raw material gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the pre-heater, the raw material gas after preheating is sent into the reactor, the methanation reaction occurs under the action of methanation catalyst, the generated reaction product is methane and water, sending the reaction product into the quench cooler, removing most of water in the reaction product; sending the reaction product from the quench cooler into the dryer, removing the rest water, obtaining the qualified methane product, where the operation pressure of the pre-heater is 0.1-10 MPa, the outlet temperature is 80-500 degrees C, the reaction pressure of the reactor is 0.1-10 MPa, but not higher than the operation pressure of the preheater, the outlet temperature is 150-500 degrees C, the operation pressure of the quench cooler is 0.1-10 MPa, and not higher than the operation pressure of the reactor and the outlet temperature is -10 to 80 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the modular device for producing methane by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Pre-heater (1)
   Reactor (2)
   Quench cooler (3)
   Drier (4)
   Control system (5)

PN US2020392416-A1
TI Production of hydrocarbon used for producing            renewable hydrocarbon fuel, involves contacting gaseous            mixture containing carbon dioxide, and feeding            synthesis gas product to Fischer-Tropsch reactor to            convert hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
AU MARKER T
   LINCK M B
   WANGEROW J
   ORTIZ-TORAL P
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 2020C7451G
AB    NOVELTY - Production of 4C or more hydrocarbon involves                (a) contacting a gaseous mixture comprising methane                and carbon dioxide with a reforming catalyst to                produce a synthesis gas product in a reforming                stage, and (b) feeding the synthesis gas product to                a Fischer-Tropsch (FT) reactor to convert hydrogen                and carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas product to                hydrocarbons, including 4C or more hydrocarbons,                provided in a FT product without removal of carbon                dioxide from the synthesis gas product.
   USE - Production of 4C or more hydrocarbons e.g.                methane used for producing renewable hydrocarbon                fuel (claimed) in liquid fuels such as gasoline,                jet fuel, and/or diesel fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of hydrocarbons                with excellent product quality and stability, and                reduces the concentration of hydrogen sulfide and                other sulfur-bearing contaminants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) production of renewable hydrocarbon fuel,                which involves hydropyrolyzing a biomass-containing                feedstock or biomass-derived feedstock (67) with a                hydrogen-containing feed gas stream (69) in a                hydropyrolysis section to generate (i) a                hydropyrolysis gaseous mixture comprising methane                and carbon dioxide, and a fully deoxygenated                hydrocarbon liquid, and contacting the                hydropyrolysis gaseous mixture with a reforming                catalyst comprising a noble metal on a solid                support comprising cerium oxide to produce a                synthesis gas product comprising hydrogen and                carbon monoxide; and
   (2) a system, which consists of a reforming                reactor configured to connect, via a system input,                to a source of a gaseous mixture comprising methane                and carbon dioxide, a synthesis gas product cooler                configured to receive the synthesis gas product                from the reforming reactor, a compressor configured                to receive a cooled synthesis gas product from the                synthesis gas product cooler, an FT reactor                configured to receive an FT feed from the                compressor, an optional FT product heater                configured to receive an FT product from the FT                reactor, a finishing reactor configured to receive                a heated FT product from the FT product heater, and                a separator (82) configured to receive a finishing                product from the finishing reactor and further                configured to provide, via a system vapor output                and a system liquid output, vapor and liquid                fractions, respectively, of the finishing                product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of production                of a renewable hydrocarbon fuel from the                hydropyrolysis of biomass.
   Hydrocarbon fluid (61)
   Aqueous liquid (63)
   Biomass-derived feedstock (67)
   Gas stream (69)
   Hydropyrolysis reactor (81)
   Separator (82)
   Hydroconversion reactor (83)
   Hydropyrolysis vapor (87)
   Effluent (89)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The dewaxing catalyst                  comprises 0.1-3 wt.% gallium, deposited on an                  acidic support comprising a zeolitic or                  non-zeolitic molecular sieve having at least 15                  mmol/g of acid sites, measured by temperature                  programmed desorption (TPD) of ammonia. Preferred                  Components: The acidic support comprises ZSM-5                  zeolite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The system is                  transportable, further comprises a condenser                  configured to condense liquid water from the                  cooled synthesis gas product, where the                  compressor is configured for receiving the cooled                  synthesis gas product from the condenser.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 4C or more                  hydrocarbons in the FT product include wax                  fraction comprising 20C or more hydrocarbons. The                  portion of the gaseous mixture comprises a                  hydrocarbon-containing feedstock component chosen                  from hydropyrolysis gaseous mixture, natural gas                  comprising carbon dioxide, biogas obtained from                  bacterial digestion of organic waste, a                  hydrogen-depleted pressure swing adsorption (PSA)                  tail gas, or a gaseous effluent (89) from a                  bacterial fermentation stage. The hydropyrolysis                  section includes (i) catalytic hydropyrolysis of                  the biomass-containing feedstock or                  biomass-derived feedstock in the presence of the                  hydrogen-containing feed gas stream and a                  deoxygenating catalyst to provide a                  hydropyrolysis reactor vapor (81), and (ii)                  catalytic hydroconversion of at least a portion                  of the hydropyrolysis reactor vapor in the                  presence of a hydroconversion catalyst to provide                  the hydropyrolysis gaseous mixture and fully                  deoxygenated hydrocarbon liquid. Preferred                  Process: The production of hydrocarbons method                  further involves (c) converting at least 75% 20C                  or more normal hydrocarbons to normal or branched                  14-19C hydrocarbons, provided in a                  hydroisomerization/hydrocracking product. The                  step (c) is carried out in a finishing reactor                  comprising a dewaxing catalyst including                  hydroisomerization and/or hydrocracking activity                  with respect to normal 20C or more hydrocarbons.                  The step (b) is carried out with an FT feed                  having molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide in the synthesis gas product, produced                  in step (a). The water is condensed from the                  synthesis gas product, produced in step (a). The                  production of renewable hydrocarbon fuel further                  involves recycling at least a portion of (i) the                  synthesis gas product or (ii) an                  hydrogen-enriched portion, to provide at least a                  portion of the hydrogen-containing feed gas                  stream, converting the hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide in the synthesis gas product to                  hydrocarbons, including 4C or more hydrocarbons,                  in a FT product, to increase yield of 4C or more                  hydrocarbons from the biomass-containing                  feedstock or biomass-derived feedstock, relative                  to a baseline yield in the absence of converting                  the hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the synthesis                  gas product to hydrocarbons. Preferred                  Composition: In step (b), the synthesis gas                  product comprises 5-45 mol% carbon dioxide. The                  hydroisomerization/hydrocracking product                  comprises less than 1 wt.% hydrocarbons which are                  solid at room temperature. Preferred Condition:                  The finishing reactor is performed at 232-399                  degrees C.

PT P
PN CN111589311-A; CN111589311-B
TI Preparing metal organic framework molecular sieve membrane by supercritical fluid technology involves using porous carrier modified with a dense metal precursor layer as a support matrix and placing in the microreactor cavity.
AU LIU Y
   LIU L
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202085841W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal organic framework molecular sieve membrane by supercritical fluid technology involves using porous carrier modified with a dense metal precursor layer as a support matrix and placed in the microreactor cavity, placing the solid organic ligand powder in the micro-reactor cavity, and placing the micro-reactor cavity in the supercritical kettle, entering gaseous carbon-dioxide the supercritical reactor under high pressure after the supporting compressor. The conversion of gaseous carbon-dioxde to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is realized by adjusting the pressure and temperature in the reactor, and then the supercritical CO2 fluid diffuses into the microreactor cavity. The solid organic ligand powder in the cavity gradually dissolves and diffuses under the action of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, formed a uniform supercritical CO2 homogeneous reaction system in the cavity that fully dissolves the organic ligand.
   USE - Method for preparing metal organic framework molecular sieve membrane by supercritical fluid technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal organic framework molecular sieve membrane by supercritical fluid technology has good industrial application prospect and excellent gas separation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing metal organic framework molecular sieve membrane by supercritical fluid technology involves using porous carrier modified with a dense metal precursor layer as a support matrix and placed in the microreactor cavity, placing the solid organic ligand powder in the micro-reactor cavity, and placing the micro-reactor cavity in the supercritical kettle, entering gaseous carbondioxide the supercritical reactor under high pressure after the supporting compressor. The conversion of gaseous carbondioxde to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is realized by adjusting the pressure and temperature in the reactor, and then the supercritical CO2 fluid diffuses into the microreactor cavity. The solid organic ligand powder in the cavity gradually dissolves and diffuses under the action of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, formed a uniform supercritical CO2 homogeneous reaction system in the cavity that fully dissolves the organic ligand. The fully dissolved organic ligand molecules in the supercritical CO2 homogeneous reaction system and the metal precursor layer on the surface of the carrier undergo coordination at the interface area, and gradually transform to form a continuous dense MOF molecular sieve membrane layer, released the pressure and lowering the temperature, the remaining organic ligand/supercritical CO2 homogeneous solution in the reactor cavity in the kettle automatically undergoes phase separation, and the solid organic ligand powder stays in the microreactor cavity recovery, gaseous CO2 is released and recovered through the exhaust port of the supercritical kettle, and the MOF molecular sieve membrane is taken out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The structure of the porous carrier is flat, tubular or hollow fiber type. The porous carrier is a porous metal oxide, porous metal or porous non-metal oxide carrier. The metal precursor layer modification process is through physical deposition, chemical deposition, and chemical vapor deposition when the carrier is modified by deposition or atomic layer deposition, the deposition thickness of the metal precursor layer is 10 nanometer-20 micrometer, and the particle size is 10 nm-5 micrometer. The microreactor is a semi-permeable or fully permeable reactor with a pore size For 50-6000 mesh. The temperature range of supercritical CO2 is 31.1-150.0 degrees C. The pressure range is 7.4-100.0 megaPascal. The concentration is 0.0001-10 mole/litre. The growth period of the supercritical state single process is 0.1-96 hours. The MOF molecular sieve membranes prepared are HKUST-1 series, ZIF series, MOF-74 series , MIL series, UiO series or IRMOF series molecular sieve membrane, MOF molecular sieve membrane thickness is 10nm-20 micrometer, and the grain size is 10nm-5 micrometer. The pressure reduction rate of supercritical CO2 is 0.005-50 megaPascal/minutes, and the temperature is lowered. The rate is 0.1-20 degrees C/minutes. Preferred Components: The metal precursor layer is a metal oxide, metal hydroxide or metal salt, and the metal element in the metal precursor layer is zinc, magnesium, zirconium, iron, cobalt, nickel or copper. The organic ligand mentioned is imidazole, carboxylic acid and pyridine ligands.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Decomposition of chlorofluorocarbon for manufacturing hydrogen, involves directly contacting chlorofluorocarbon containing hydrogen and fluorine with calcium oxide to produce hydrogen, and decomposing cracked gas containing carbon monoxide.
AU MAEDA S
   MAE N
   KANAZAWA M
   HOSOKAWA R
   YANAGISAWA K
   KONO T
   YANO Y
AE DAIOH SHINYO CO LTD (DAIO-Non-standard)

   DAIOH SHINYO KK (DAIO-Non-standard)
   SKC CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   KOCHI PREFECTURE (KOCH-Non-standard)
GA 2020767725
AB    NOVELTY - Decomposition of chlorofluorocarbon involves directly contacting chlorofluorocarbon (1) containing hydrogen (10) and fluorine with calcium oxide in a reactor (30) maintained to predetermined temperature (36), to produce hydrogen, and decomposing cracked gas (5) containing carbon monoxide (20) and carbon dioxide (15).
   USE - Decomposition of chlorofluorocarbon for manufacturing hydrogen and manufacture of calcium fluoride as fuel for fuel cell for generating electric power for decomposing chlorofluorocarbon gas (all claimed), and heater. Uses include but are not limited to spray agent, air conditioner, cleaning of electronic component, and freezer-refrigerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and environmentally-friendly, and enables decomposition of chlorofluorocarbon with zero emission, and without producing acidic gas since the superheated steam is not utilized and eliminates the showering of mixed water of calcium hydroxide for neutralizing acidic gas, suppresses the power consumption for maintaining the temperature in the reactor by maintaining temperature at 600 degrees C, without using a superheated steam, provides cracked gas with high yield, and target gas with high purified solid substance. Since the reaction residue becomes a solid substance, the process becomes easy, and the durability of the decomposition apparatus is maintained high. Since the hydrogen content in the cracked gas is large in the decomposition process, it can be recovered as a useful resource and reused.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) chlorofluorocarbon decomposing apparatus, which consists of the reactor in which the calcium oxide is accommodated inside, a heating unit to heat a reactor to predetermined temperature, and a carbon dioxide removal apparatus for supplying cracked gas containing only hydrogen;
   (2) manufacture of hydrogen, which involves generating hydrogen containing a trace amount of carbon dioxide from chlorofluorocarbon by decomposing chlorofluorocarbon containing hydrogen and fluorine;
   (3) manufacture of calcium fluoride, which involves decomposing chlorofluorocarbon gas, and producing calcium fluoride as a solid in the reaction residue after decomposition of chlorofluorocarbon; and
   (4) a fuel cell (70), which consists of the apparatus for decomposing chlorofluorocarbon connected to the fuel cell which isolates negative electrode and positive electrode through an electrolyte membrane and supplies hydrogen as fuel to the negative electrode, and producing electric power by supplying target gas to the negative electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the decomposition apparatus.
   Chlorofluorocarbon containing hydrogen and fluorine (1)
   Gas cylinder (2)
   Cracked gas (5)
   Hydrogen (10)
   Carbon dioxide (15)
   Carbon monoxide (20)
   Calcium oxide (25)
   Reactor (30)
   Predetermined temperature (36)
   Removing carbon dioxide (51)
   Combustion process (53)
   Target gas (55)
   Fuel cell (70)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus further consists of a aqueous shift reaction pipe for supplying steam, a steam condensation tank for supplying the cracked gas (g2), a selective carbon monoxide oxidation apparatus or carbon monoxide adsorption apparatus as carbon removal apparatus.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves accommodating calcium oxide inside the reactor, decomposing chlorofluorocarbon, maintaining cracked gas to predetermined temperature and supplying water to an aqueous shift reaction pipe, performing an aqueous shift reaction, generating hydrogen from carbon monoxide in the cracked gas to form a cracked gas (g2), supplying gas (g2) to a steam condensing tank to condensate and remove the stem to form cracked gas (g3), removing carbon monoxide from the gas (g3) to obtain a target gas (55) containing hydrogen and trace amount of carbon dioxide. The method involves maintaining the temperature inside the reactor at a temperature in which the decomposition ration of chlorofluorocarbon, is 99.9% or more. Preferred Property: Calcium fluoride has purity of 97%. Preferred Conditions: The cracked gas is maintained at 200 degrees C or more. The reactor is maintained at 600 degrees C or more.

TI Carbon steel welding technique by acceptance inspecting tool, marking pipe material, checking size, pipe material cutting, machining groove on incision end face of two pipes to be welded, removing debris, and showing metallic luster.
AU JIANG Y
AE JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 201962086J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon steel welding technique involves selecting tool, acceptance inspecting, marking pipe material, checking size, pipe material cutting, ensuring perpendicularity of end face of slit and axis of pipe, machining groove on incision end face of two pipes to be welded, removing debris, and showing metallic luster; confirming tooling fixtures used by the group, setting the two pipes horizontally, spot welding of welding wire, melting spot welds into weld, avoiding spot welding joint, checking quality of spot welding, and grinding both ends of spot welding joint into gentle slope type; checking external objective factors before welding, manual tungsten-argon arc backing welding, inspecting objective factors, and post-weld inspecting; and material selecting suitable welding current, voltage and carbon dioxide gas flow, welding, controlling width of weld tank, running flux cored wire carbon dioxide gas shielded welding torch, checking objective factors of welding, and post-weld inspecting.
   USE - The method is used for carbon steel welding technique.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has small amount of heat input, high quality welds, no welding head, less welding slag, less smoke, high welding production efficiency and beautiful weld bead, replaces hand arc welding, and reduces temperature around the molten tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon steel welding technique involves:
   (A) selecting tool, acceptance inspecting, marking pipe material, checking size, pipe material cutting, ensuring perpendicularity of end face of slit and axis of pipe, machining groove on incision end face of two pipes to be welded, removing debris within 20 mm from the inner and outer surfaces of groove, and showing metallic luster;
   (B) confirming tooling fixtures used by the group, setting the two pipes horizontally, spot welding of welding wire, melting spot welds into weld, avoiding spot welding joint, checking quality of spot welding, and grinding both ends of spot welding joint into gentle slope type;
   (C) checking external objective factors before welding, manual tungsten-argon arc backing welding, inspecting objective factors of welding during the manual tungsten-argon arc backing welding process, and post-weld inspecting manual tungsten-argon arc backing welding; and
   (D) according to wall thickness of carbon steel pipe, material selecting suitable welding current, voltage and carbon dioxide gas flow, avoiding 12 o'clock position, swinging, welding at speed of 6-15 cm/minute, controlling width of weld tank at range of 5-12 mm, running flux cored wire carbon dioxide gas shielded welding torch to 6 o'clock position, checking objective factors of welding, and post-weld inspecting flux cored wire carbon dioxide gas shielded welding.

AU YANG X
AE NISHA XIAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NISH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1370C
AB    (CN214371298-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide collecting and liquefying recycling device, the device comprises a pre-processing unit, a compression unit, a drying unit and a liquefaction recycling unit; the pre-processing unit comprises a desulfurizing tower and a pre-cooling separator, used for desulfurizing and dehydrating the acid gas from the LNG factory; the compression unit comprises a carbon dioxide compressor, an air cooler and a gas-liquid separator, for pressurizing the pre-treated acid gas; the drying unit comprises a drying tower for deeply drying the compressed acid gas; the liquefying recycling unit mainly comprises a heat exchange device for liquefying CO2. The utility model according to the physical and chemical properties of the components and CO2 of the acid gas discharged by the LNG factory, designing CO2 collecting and liquefying recycling device, collecting the original discharged acid gas to prepare the liquid CO2, so as to effectively recycle the CO2 discharged by the factory; The invention reduces the emission of greenhouse gas and improves the economic benefit of the factory.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide capture liquefying recycling method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a carbon dioxide capture liquefying recycling device.
EA    NOVELTY - The device has a drying tower whose carbon dioxide inlet is connected to an outlet of a last stage gas-liquid separator of a compression unit through a pipeline. A carbon dioxide outlet of the drying tower leads to a liquefaction recovery unit through a pipeline. The liquefaction recovery unit is arranged after a drying unit, and provided with a heat exchange equipment and a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The carbon dioxide inlet of the heat exchange equipment is connected to an outlet of the drying tower through a pipeline. The carbon dioxide outlet of the heat exchange equipment leads to the inlet of the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. A bottom outlet of the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank is provided with a liquid carbon dioxide product pipeline. A reboiler is formed as an electric heater.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture liquefying recycling device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device designs a carbon dioxide capture liquefaction recovery unit according to the composition of the acid gas discharged from the liquefied natural gas plant and the physical and chemical properties of carbon dioxide, and produces the liquid carbon dioxide by capturing the originally vented acid gas, so as to effectively recover the carbon dioxide emitted by the factory, reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve the economic benefits of the factory.

AB    NOVELTY - Continuously controlling a ratio of input                gases provided to a bioreactor of a continuous gas                fermentation process comprises providing a gas                fermentation process, measuring a hydrogen: carbon                monoxide: carbon dioxide molar ratio of the                bioreactor outlet gas stream or the bioreactor                headspace, to provide a measured hydrogen: carbon                monoxide: carbon dioxide molar ratio, inputting the                measured hydrogen: carbon monoxide: carbon dioxide                molar ratio to a controller and comparing the                measured hydrogen: carbon monoxide: carbon dioxide                molar ratio to a predetermined hydrogen: carbon                monoxide: carbon dioxide molar ratio, and adjusting                the flowrate of the first gaseous stream, the                flowrate of the second gaseous stream, or both, in                response to the difference between the measured                hydrogen: carbon monoxide: carbon dioxide molar                ratio and the predetermined hydrogen: carbon                monoxide: carbon dioxide molar ratio to maximize                the concentration of inert components in the                bioreactor outlet gas stream.
   USE - The method and system is useful for:                controlling a ratio of input gases provided to                bioreactor of continuous gas fermentation process                (claimed); and controlling flexible gas                fermentation platforms for improved conversion of                carbon dioxide into products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system: exhibits improved                carbon utilization; maximizes the concentration of                inert components in an outlet gas stream; greatly                reduce the volume of the bioreactor required and,                consequently, the capital cost of the                culture/fermentation equipment; exhibits high                process versatility, allowing different kinds of                reactions to be carried out; is low investment and                operating costs; has no requirement for rare earth                metals; and is convenient modular setting, as                plasma reactors scale up linearly with the plant                output.

TI Carbon dioxide refrigeration system, has air            cooler connected between outlet end of compressor and            inlet end of ejector, and injection loop whose end is            connected with inlet and outlet ends of evaporator and            compressor, respectively.
AU GE F
   ZHAO R
AE ZHEJIANG DUNAN COLD CHAIN SYSTEM CO LTD (DUNN-C)
GA 2022B9033F
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical                field of refrigeration, especially relates to a                carbon dioxide refrigeration system. The carbon                dioxide refrigerating system comprises an injection                loop, one end of the injector loop is connected                with the inlet end of the ejector, the other end is                respectively connected with the outlet end of the                first evaporator and the inlet end of the                compressor. Compared with the existing technology,                the advantages of the utility model are as follows:                not only ensures the throttling effect of the                system, but also can flexibly adjust the                evaporation temperature of the first evaporator,                realizing fast cooling of the first evaporator to                the target temperature area; The setting of the                injector and the injection loop can make the two                temperature areas share one compressor with small                load, so that the system is integrally reduced cost                and improves the flexibility of the system.

TI Method for analyzing carbon dioxide gas storage            reservoir mixed gas sensitive parameter, involves            obtaining influence of different numerical values of            preset parameter on natural gas exploitation under dry            up mining condition.
AU YANG H
   ZHANG X
   LIU J
   CHEN W
   WANG Y
   LI X
AE SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN PETROLEUM ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN PETROLEUM EXPLORATION (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC OILFIELD SERVICE CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2022C00002
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves building a multi-parameter                mechanism model based on a user instruction                according to a pure air-driven gas storage                reservoir seepage mechanism formula. An influence                of different numerical values of a preset parameter                on natural gas exploitation under a dry up mining                condition is determined. A mixed gas sensitive                parameter is determined according to the influence                of the natural gas exploitation. Transverse                permeability, longitudinal permeability and                vertical permeability of a point space are                obtained.
   USE - Method for analyzing carbon dioxide gas                storage reservoir mixed gas sensitive                parameter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method analyzes the parameter sensitivity                of the influence gas mixed gas and gas storage                operation, quickly finds main sensitive parameter                and reduces labor opertion cost.

TI Preparation of zinc sulfide crystal used in            electrochemical reduction reaction of carbon dioxide,            involves stirring zinc nitrate hexahydrate and            thiourea, ultrasonically processing, crystallizing,            separating, drying to obtain nanoparticles, and            washing.
AU ZHANG B
   YU X
   CHEN Y
AE JIANGXI BALIUSAN IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B8981P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zinc sulfide crystal based on                controlling shape of copper-based catalyst                involves: preparing an article weighing platform                with high precision and deionized water; weighing                2.44 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate through the                platform, then weighing 1.27 g thiourea, pouring                zinc nitrate hexahydrate and thiourea into                deionized water, and stirring to obtain a mixed                solution; ultrasonically processing; pouring into a                crystallization kettle; crystallizing the mixed                solution in the kettle at high temperature; cooling                to room temperature; centrifuging and separating;                drying at high temperature to obtain zinc sulfide                nanoparticles; washing with ethanol and deionized                water, and taking out the washed particles.
   USE - Preparation of zinc sulfide crystal used in                electrochemical reduction reaction of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides zinc sulfide crystal,                which has high chemical utilization rate, and                excellent shape controllability, and prevents                influence of carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction reaction of the catalyst.

AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing the normalization of a metabolic                disorder in a subject, comprises administering                supercritical carbon dioxide, defatted N.                sativaground seeds and at least one a                phytoceutical, and a pharmaceutical.
   USE - The method is useful: for enhancing the                normalization of a metabolic disorder in a subject,                where the metabolic disorder comprises at least one                of obesity, metabolic syndrome, diabetes, hepatic                steatosis, hyperlipidemia, leaky gut, irritable                bowel disease, and cardiovascular disease (all                claimed); and in medical foods, dietary supplements                or therapeutics for inflammation-related                disorder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes defatted N. sativaseeds                which decrease mitochondrial membrane potential in                adipocytes implying decreased adenosine                triphosphate synthesis and increased thermogenesis;                modifies adipocyte physiology in a subject; and                enhances adipocyte thermogenesis or decreases                oxidative stress utilizing supercritical fluid                defatted N. sativaseeds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                composition for enhancing the normalization of a                metabolic disorder in a subject, comprising at                least one supercritical carbon dioxide defatted N.                sativaground seeds, a phytoceutical, and a                pharmaceutical.

PT P
PN US2022169593-A1
TI Composite materials used for integration into            direct air carbon dioxide capture and other mixtures of            gases e.g. natural gas and flue gases, comprise            alkyl-aryl amine-rich small molecules amines            impregnated into porous supports.
AU SUJAN A
   SAKWA-NOVAK M A
   ROSU C
   PING E W
   KUMAR D R
   JONES C W
AE GLOBAL THERMOSTAT (GLOB-Non-standard)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
GA 2022763756
AB    NOVELTY - Composite materials comprise alkyl-aryl                amine-rich small molecules (I) impregnated into                porous supports.
   USE - The materials are useful in various                applications, preferably integration into direct                air carbon dioxide capture (DAC) and other mixtures                of gases e.g. natural gas and flue gases or other                carbon dioxide capture technologies and hydrogen                sulfide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The materials utilize: alkyl-aryl amine-rich                small molecules that can fill the support's pores                up to ~90% and displayed good thermal stability;                and alkyl-aryl small molecules contain a high                amount of amine groups and due to the aryl core,                have better thermal stability than regular linear                alkyl amines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composite materials comprise alkyl-aryl                amine-rich small molecules of formula (Ph-XX-YY)                (I) impregnated into porous supports.
   Ph = phenyl moiety;
   XX = number of substitutions on phenyl ring (3                or 6); and
   YY = ethylene diamine or                propane-1,3-diamine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The support is                  mesoporous silica, porous alumina, porous carbon,                  porous polymer or porous metal organic                  frameworks. The silica is Santa Barbara                  amorphous-15. The Ph-XX-YY is                  N1,N1',N1''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyltris(methylene))tris(ethane-1,2-diamine),                  N1,N1',N1''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyltris(methylene))tris(propane-1,3-diamine),                  N1,N1',N1'',N1''',N1'''',N1'''''-(benzene-1,2,3,4,5,6-hexaylhexakis(methylene))hexakis(propane-1,3-diamine),                  NN1,N1',N1'',N1''',N1'''',N1'''''-(benzene-1,2,3,4,5,6-hexaylhexakis(methylene))hexakis(ethane-1,2-diamine),                  N1,N1',N1'',N1'''-(benzene-1,2,4,5-tetrayltetrakis(methylene))tetrakis(ethane-1,2-diamine),                  N1,N1',N1'',N1'''-(benzene-1,2,4,5-tetrayltetrakis(methylene))tetrakis(propane-1,3-diamine),                  (N1,N1',N1''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyltris(methylene))tris(N2-(2-((2-((2-aminoethyl)amino)ethyl)amino)ethyl)ethane-1,2-diamine),                  N1,N1',N1''-(benzene-1,3,5-triyltris(methylene))tris(polyethylenimine),                  N1,N1'-(1,4-phenylenebis(methylene))bis(ethane-1,2-diamine),                  or                  N1,N1'-(1,4-phenylenebis(methylene))bis(propane-1,3-diamine).
   EXAMPLE - Santa Barbara amorphous-15 (SBA-15) was loaded                with 1,2,3,4,5,6-hexakis(bromomethyl)benzene with                propane-1,3-diamine by wet impregnation. Before use                SBA-15 was dried overnight under vacuum (less than                20 mTorr) at 110degrees Celsius to remove trace                water. Then for each composite formulation 200 mg                SBA-15 and 50 ml methanol was mixed into a round                bottom flask and stirred for 2 hours. Separately,                the desired amount of                1,2,3,4,5,6-hexakis(bromomethyl)benzene with                propane-1,3-diamine was dissolved in methanol 10 ml                and stirred for 10-15 minutes. This solution was                added to SBA-15/methanol mixture and stirred                overnight at room temperature. The solvent was                removed under reduced pressure (rotary                evaporation). The resulting powder was dried                overnight under vacuum (less than 20 mTorr) at                50degrees Celsius to give composite powder stored                in glass vials at ambient conditions.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC A12-W11D; E34-C02; E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; E31-P03; E31-N04D; E31-F01B; E10-B01A4; J04-X
IP B01J-020/10; B01J-020/28; B01J-020/32; C07C-209/06

PT P
PN WO2022090671-A1; FR3115711-A1
TI Catalytic system used for converting gas containing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, comprises support comprising cerium and/or zirconium, nickel, and promoter chosen from gadolinium, yttrium, strontium, copper, manganese, cobalt, iron and their mixtures.
AU GALVEZ-PARRUCA M E
   MIKHAIL M
   OGNIER S
   DA COSTA P
   CAVADIAS S
   TATOULIAN M
AE PARIS SCI & LETTRES (PARS-Non-standard)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV SORBONNE (USRB-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM PARIS (ECOL-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES-QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV SORBONNE (USRB-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM PARIS (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 202258869V
AB    NOVELTY - A catalytic system comprises a support comprising cerium and/or zirconium, nickel, and a promoter chosen from gadolinium, yttrium, strontium, copper, manganese, cobalt, iron and their mixtures.
   USE - Catalytic system used for converting gas containing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide for producing hydrocarbon, preferably methane, carbon monoxide, alcohols, and formic acid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic system has improved catalytic performances, selectivity, catalytic stability and conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) conversion of gas containing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, which involves using catalytic system and a cold plasma, preferably a plasma generated by dielectric barrier discharge (DBD); and
   (2) preparation of the catalytic system, which involves bringing the support and/or a precursor into contact with a nickel precursor and a precursor of the promoter, and performing calcination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The promoter comprises 0.1-20 wt.%, preferably 1-10 wt.% promoter, and 3-30 wt.%, preferably 7-17 wt.% nickel. The molar ratio of cerium with respect to zirconium is 90:10-40:60, preferably 80:20-50:50. Preferred Components: The support is a mixed oxide of cerium and zirconium. The nickel, support and the promoter form a homogeneous mixture. Preferred Process: The conversion of gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide is performed in the presence of hydrogen gas, where the molar ratio of the carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas is 1:4.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN114084890-A
TI Crystalline silicon material used in preparing hydrogen peroxide, carbon dioxide and lithium-ion battery, has a three-dimensional structure, comprising spherical, rod-shaped, plate-shaped, disc-shaped or cone-shaped.
AU YANG Z
   DENG X
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2022357413
AB    NOVELTY - Crystalline silicon material, has a three-dimensional structure, comprising spherical, rod-shaped, plate-shaped, disc-shaped or cone-shaped.
   USE - The crystalline silicon material is used in preparing hydrogen peroxide, carbon dioxide and lithium-ion battery (claimed). Can also be used for efficient photo-catalytic synthesis of hydrogen peroxide and photo-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high purity, which can relieve the high demand of the photovoltaic industry to the crystalline silicon, which is good for relieving the energy crisis. The crystalline silicon material has wide application range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the crystalline silicon material comprising: (S1) modifying the active terminal group on the surface of the two-dimensional silicon material to obtain the layered silicon material; and (S2) and reacting the layered silicon material with an oxidizing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The grain diameter of the crystalline silicon material is 1 nm to 20 microm. The pores are distributed in the crystalline silicon material, and the size of the pore is 1-200 nm. The etching solution is ethanol, water and hydrofluoric acid according to the volume ratio of 1:(1-3): 1. The oxidant comprises at least one of dimethyl formamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, hydrogen peroxide, peracetic acid, sodium dichromate, chromic acid, nitric acid and potassium permanganate. The oxidant is further added with diluent.Preferred Method: The preparation method of the two-dimensional silicon material comprises: peeling silicide by liquid phase reaction. The method for modifying the active end group is as follows: dispersing the two-dimensional silicon material to the etching solution to react.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E12-A01; E31-P06A; E31-N05B1; E31-E01; E11-P; J04-E04C; J04-E04A; J04-E01; L03-E01D5; L03-E01B5B; L03-E01B3; L03-A02B; L04-A01A; X16-E; X16-B01F1

TI Preparing red mud-based carbonized brick, involves            uniformly mixing and forming carbonized cementitious            material, red mud, nitrate, water, mineral admixture            and cement and introducing carbon dioxide-containing            gas for carbonization.
AU HE Y
   WU W
   LIU Z
   WANG F
   ZHANG T
   MU G
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X
   CUI D
   MA Y
AE SHANDONG HANBO YUZHOU NEW MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202222076G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing red mud-based carbonized                brick, involves uniformly mixing and forming                carbonized cementitious material, red mud, nitrate,                water, mineral admixture and cement and introducing                carbon dioxide-containing gas for carbonization to                obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing red                mud-based carbonized brick (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The brick has high strength in short period of                time, compressive strength, flexural strength and                excellent durability. The method utilizes carbon                dioxide-containing gas to solve the problem of red                mud treatment, improves comprehensive utilization                level of resources, and reduces production                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing the brick, involving molding                adopting grouting molding with a water-to-solid                ratio of greater than 1 during grouting molding,                and baking the brick blank after grouting molding                until the water content is less than 25% before                carbonization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonized                  cementitious material is gamma-type dicalcium                  silicate, monocalcium silicate, steel slag powder                  and/or tricalcium disilicate. The red mud is                  sintering method red mud, Bayer method red mud                  and/or mixed method red mud, and the water                  content of less than 40%. The nitrate is crushed                  stone, construction crushed material and/or sand,                  and its particle size of 5-20 mm. The mineral                  admixture is silica fume or mineral powder. The                  cement comprises common silicate cement,                  aluminate cement, sulphoaluminate cement, ferric                  aluminate cement, fluoroaluminate cement,                  phosphate cement, and cement with volcanic ash or                  potential hydraulic material and/or active                  materials as main components. The carbon                  dioxide-containing gas comprises industrial                  exhaust gas or carbon dioxide gas, where the                  concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas is greater than 10%.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the                  carbonized cementitious material to the red mud                  is 1:(0.1-1.5). The mass ratio of the carbonized                  cementitious material to the cement is                  1:(0.06-0.6). The brick comprises is 10-25 wt.%                  nitrate and 0-10 wt.% mineral admixture. The                  brick comprises 400-600 pts. wt. carbonized                  cementitious material, 400-600 pts. wt. red mud,                  100-300 pts. wt. petrolatum, 100-2000 pts. wt.                  water, 70-115 pts. wt. mineral admixture and                  50-100 pts. wt. cement.Preferred Method: The step of molding                  involves adopting press forming, where the                  water-to-solid ratio during press forming is                  0.1-0.25. The step of carbonization conditions                  are a carbonization pressure of 0.05-1.5 MPa, a                  relative humidity of 20-98%, a carbonization                  temperature of 25-60degrees Celsius, and a                  carbonization time of 1-72 hours.

PN CN113584501-A
TI Preparation of bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheet material used in electrocatalytic reduction, involves electrodepositing potassium iodide, bismuth nitrate, anhydrous ethanol of benzoquinone to form bismuth oxyiodide nanosheet, and reducing nanosheet using carbon dioxide-saturated potassium bicarbonate.
AU YAO W
   WANG Y
   JIANG W
AE CHINA ACAD SPACE TECHNOLOGY (CAER-C)
GA 2021C8501B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheet material involves adding nitric acid in a potassium iodide solution to adjust the pH to a preset value (A), adding bismuth nitrate pentahydrate and stirring to obtain a mixed solution, mixing the mixed solution with anhydrous ethanol solution containing benzoquinone to obtain a plating solution, electrodepositing the plating solution, washing the obtained electrodeposit with deionized water, drying to obtain a bismuth oxyiodide nanosheet material, using the obtained bismuth oxyiodide nanosheet material as a working electrode, and performing reduction reaction in a three-electrode system using carbon dioxide-saturated potassium bicarbonate solution as an electrolyte.
   USE - Preparation of bismuth oxycarbonate nanosheet material (claimed) used for adsorbing reaction intermediates in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, fast, provides nanosheet material having abundant reaction active sites, and does not use adhesive thereby effectively reducing impedance and avoiding burying of the reactive site.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mol ratio of added bismuth nitrate pentahydrate and potassium iodide solution is 1:5-20. The concentration of the potassium iodide solution is 0.2-0.6 mol/L. The concentration of potassium bicarbonate solution is 0.1-1 mol/L.Preferred Process: The plating solution is electrodeposited using an electrodeposition system comprising a working electrode, a counter electrode and a reference electrode.Preferred Components: The working electrode is made of carbon paper. The counter electrode is made of platinum sheet. The reference electrode is made of saturated silver/silver chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the anhydrous ethanol solution containing benzoquinone, the concentration of benzoquinone is 0.1-0.5 mol/L. The volume ratio of the mixed solution and the anhydrous ethanol solution containing benzoquinone is 2-5:1.

PN WO2021031533-A1; CN112409286-A; CA3148367-A1; IN202217009171-A; EP4001268-A1; EP4001268-A4; US2022267284-A1; CN112409286-B
TI Synthesizing N-substituted            phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone, comprises using            aniline based compound and epoxy chloride propane under            alkaline conditions and carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU YU D
   WANG Q
   BAO L
   HOU D
AE HANGZHOU DIKE TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU DIKE TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU DIKE TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU DIKE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU DIKE TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU DIKE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2021195074
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing N-substituted                phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone, comprises                using aniline based compound (I) and epoxy chloride                propane as raw material under alkaline conditions                and carbon dioxide atmosphere to prepare                N-substituted                phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                N-substituted                phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces N-substituted                phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone in simple,                economical and easy manner, and is particularly                suitable for the industrial production of the                intermediate of antibiotic linezolid and the                intermediate of anti-thrombotic drug                rivaroxaban.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing N-substituted                phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone, comprises                using aniline based compound of formula                (3-R2-4-R1-aniline) (I) and epoxy chloride propane                as raw material under alkaline conditions and                carbon dioxide atmosphere to prepare N-substituted                phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone.
   R1 = morpholine group, morpholin-3-one group,                piperazine or its derivatives group; and
   R2 = halo, H or lower alkyl group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic base is                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, trisodium                  phosphate and/or tripotassium phosphate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The synthesis method is a                  one-pot method. The synthesis method is carried                  out by using (I) and epichlorohydrin as raw                  materials to prepare                  1-chloro-3-(3-R2-4-R1-phenylamino)-2-propanol,                  adding inorganic or organic base and introducing                  carbon dioxide, and cyclizing                  1-chloro-3-(3-R2-4-R1-phenylamino)-2-propanol                  with carbon dioxide to obtain N-substituted                  phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazole alkanone. The                  method comprises using (S)-epichlorohydrin as                  reaction material to obtain (R)-N-substituted                  phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone; using                  (R)-epichlorohydrin as reaction material to                  obtain (S)-N-substituted phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl                  2-oxazolidinone; and using racemic                  epichlorohydrin as reaction raw material to                  obtain racemic N-substituted                  phenyl-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone. The                  1-chloro-3-(3-R2-4-R1-phenylamino)-2-propanol is                  prepared by dissolving 3-R2-4-R1-aniline,                  epichlorohydrin and boron trifluoride ether in                  organic solvent, and reacting at 30-70 degrees C                  for 12-24 hours. The temperature of the                  cyclization reaction is 20-100 degrees C. The                  molar ratio of (I) and inorganic base or organic                  base is 1:1-20. Preferred Components: The molar                  ratio of (I), epoxy chloridepropane and boron                  trifluoride ether is 0.8-5:1:0.05-0.2. The                  organic base is organic amine, sodium methoxide                  and/or sodium ethoxide. The organic amine is                  triethylamine, tetramethylguanidine,                  1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene, pyridine,                  piperidine, quinoline, 4-dimethylaminopyridine                  and/or N-methylmorpholine. (I) is                  3-fluoro-4-morpholinaniline or                  4-(4-aminophenyl)-morpholin-3-one and (S)-epoxy                  chloropropane used as raw material to prepare                  (5R)-3-(3-fluoro-4-(4-morpholinyl)phenyl)-5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxazolidinone                  or                  (R)-4-(4-(5-hydroxymethyl-2-oxooxazolidin-3-yl)-phenyl)-morpholin-3-one.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
