PN US2021017035-A1; WO2021016200-A1; AU2020315891-A1; CA3145314-A1; KR2022035153-A; IN202217008555-A; CN114430697-A; EP3999215-A1; JP2022541560-W; BR112022001026-A2
TI Formation of product e.g. cementitious product,            involves calcining limestone to calcium compound            comprising vaterite, treating calcium compound with            N-containing salt in water, and contacting aqueous            solution with carbon dioxide.
AU WEISS M J
   GILLIAM R
AE ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
   ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
   ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
   ARELAC INC (AREL-Non-standard)
GA 202109946G
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of product involves calcining                limestone in a cement plant to form carbon dioxide                and calcium compound comprising calcium oxide                and/or calcium hydroxide, treating the calcium                compound with N-containing salt in water to produce                an aqueous solution comprising calcium salt and                N-containing salt, and contacting the aqueous                solution with the carbon dioxide under one or more                precipitation conditions to produce a precipitation                material comprising calcium carbonate and a                supernatant aqueous solution. The calcium carbonate                comprises vaterite.
   USE - Formation of product such as cementitious                product such as formed building material, masonry                unit, construction panel, conduit, basin, beam,                column, slab, acoustic barrier and insulation                material (all claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to pre-cast concrete products,                architectural panel walls, building trim, floors,                tunnel segments, catch basins, manholes, roofing                tiles, pavers, etc.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                formation method of the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The N-containing salt                  is N-containing inorganic salt and/or                  N-containing organic salt. The N-containing                  inorganic salt comprises ammonium halide,                  ammonium acetate, ammonium sulfate, ammonium                  sulfite, ammonium nitrate, and/or ammonium                  nitrite. The N-containing salt is ammonium                  chloride. The supernatant aqueous solution                  comprises residual N-containing salt. The                  precipitation material comprises residual                  N-containing salt. The calcium carbonate                  comprises reactive vaterite. The aqueous solution                  further comprises solids comprising carbon,                  silica, iron oxide and/or aluminum oxide.                  Preferred Process: The method further involves                  adding anhydrous ammonia or an aqueous solution                  of ammonia, removing and optionally recovering                  ammonia and/or N-containing salt using one or                  more process of (i) recovering a gas exhaust                  stream comprising ammonia during treating and/or                  contacting process, (ii) recovering the residual                  N-containing salt from the supernatant aqueous                  solution and (iii) removing and optionally                  recovering the residual N-containing salt from                  the precipitation material, and adding water to                  the precipitation material comprising reactive                  vaterite, transforming the vaterite to aragonite,                  in which the aragonite sets and hardens to form                  cement or cementitious product. The method                  involves recovering residual N-containing salt                  from the supernatant aqueous solution using                  recovery process comprising thermal                  decomposition, pH adjustment, reverse osmosis,                  multi-stage flash, multi-effect distillation,                  vapor recompression and/or distillation. The                  method involves recovering residual N-containing                  salt from the precipitation material by heating                  the precipitation material at 150-360 degrees C                  to evaporate the N-containing salt from the                  precipitation material with optional recovery by                  condensation of the N-containing salt. The method                  involves recovering the gas exhaust stream                  comprising ammonia by subjecting the gas exhaust                  stream comprising ammonia to a scrubbing process.                  The scrubbing process involves scrubbing the gas                  exhaust stream comprising ammonia with the carbon                  dioxide from the industrial process and water to                  produce solution of ammonia, or scrubbing the gas                  exhaust stream comprising ammonia with                  hydrochloric acid and water to produce solution                  of ammonium chloride. The method involves                  separating the solids from the aqueous solution                  before the contacting process, and adding the                  solids to the precipitation material as filler.                  The solids are not separated from the aqueous                  solution and the aqueous solution is contacted                  with the carbon dioxide to produce the                  precipitation material comprising solids.                  Preferred Property: The molar ratio of the                  N-containing salt to calcium compound is                  (0.5:1)-(2:1). Preferred Condition: The                  precipitation is carried out at a pH of 7-8.5 of                  the aqueous solution, temperature of the solution                  of 20-80 degrees C for 15-60 minutes, which                  favors the formation of the calcium carbonate or                  formation of reactive vaterite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The N-containing                  organic salt comprises N-containing organic                  compound comprising aliphatic amine, alicyclic                  amine and/or heterocyclic amine.

PN KR2020077122-A; KR2243187-B1
TI Inducing infectious condition of infective            pathogenic nematode used in culture medium of insect            pathogenic nematode for controlling pest, involves            using carbon dioxide or ammonia, and infective            pathogenic nematode is Steinernema carpocapsae.
AU OH H S
   YU Y
   PARK S H
   IMSEONG-GWON
AE OH H S (OHHS-Individual)
GA 202060630N
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing infectious condition of an infective                pathogenic nematode involves using carbon dioxide                or ammonia.
   USE - Method for inducing infectious condition of an                infective pathogenic nematode used in a culture                medium of an insect pathogenic nematode for                controlling pests (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for inducing infectious condition                of an infective pathogenic nematode can increase                the insecticidal power against pests by increasing                the culture yield and the infection rate through                the gas treatment of carbon dioxide or ammonia. In                addition, the insect pathogenic nematode induced by                the infectious state can be produced as a product                as it forms a protective oil film and allows                long-term storage at a low temperature. It can be                used in environment-friendly cultivation areas and                has a high insecticidal effect, which is effective                in reducing pest control costs and control power of                farmers, and can increase farm income by increasing                the quantity and productability of cultivated                crops.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The carrier is a                  sponge. In the pest control method, the plant in                  which the pest to be controlled is a plant                  selected from the group consisting of a fruit and                  vegetable plant, a leafy plant, and a flower                  plant. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide                  or ammonia is treated at a concentration of 80 to                  90%. Preferred Conditions: The injection timing                  of carbon dioxide or ammonia is injected into a                  culture vessel in which the insect pathogenic                  nematodes are cultured for 8 to 10 days. The                  carbon dioxide is exposed to the culture vessel                  in which the insect pathogenic nematodes are                  cultured for 3 to 5 minutes. The ammonia is                  exposed to the culture vessel in which the insect                  pathogenic nematodes are cultured for 1 to 3                  minutes. Preferred Process: The method of                  inducing infection of an insect pathogenic                  nematode involves performing a first step of                  preparing a medium containing yeast extract, soy                  flour, milk powder, olive oil, egg yolk powder,                  carrier and water, carrying out a second step of                  inoculating and culturing the symbiotic strain on                  the medium of the first step, performing a third                  step of inoculating insect pathogenic nematodes                  into the medium of the second step in which the                  symbiotic strain is cultured, carrying out a                  fourth step of culturing the insect pathogenic                  nematode for 8 to 10 days, performing a fifth                  step of injecting carbon dioxide or ammonia into                  the culture vessel in the fourth step, and                  carrying out a sixth step of further incubating                  for 2 to 4 days after injection of carbon dioxide                  or ammonia. The method for controlling pests,                  involves the step in which the culture medium is                  sprayed onto a plant in which the pest to be                  controlled.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Ionic liquid grafted metal-organic framework            composite material preparation used as catalyst in            catalyzing cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and            epoxide, involves adding vinyl carboxymethylimidazole            bromide.
AU XU W

   LIU L
   HU J
   HAN Z
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 202051995C
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic liquid grafted metal-organic framework                composite material preparation involves adding                1-vinyl-3-carboxymethylimidazole bromide,                1-[bis(dimethylamino)methylene]-1H-1,2,3-triazolo[4,5-b]pyridinium                3-oxide hexafluorophosphate and triethylamine are                added into N,N-dimethylformamide with stirring at                room temperature. The metal organic framework                (MIL-101-NH2) is placed in the obtained mixture                with stirring for 22 hours, where the resultant is                washed with N,N-dimethylformamide, filtered, and                dried to obtain the metal-organic framework                composite material.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The metal organic                  framework is prepared by mixing chromium salt,                  2-aminoterephthalic acid, sodium hydroxide and                  water with stirring uniformly for 12 hours at                  150?C to obtain the metal organic framework. The                  chromium salt is chromium nitrate. The ionic                  liquid 1-vinyl-3-carboxymethylimidazole bromide                  is prepared by placing 1-vinylimidazole and                  bromoacetic acid in acetone with stirring at                  60&#176; C for 48 hours, and then washed with                  ether, filtered and dried to obtain the ionic                  liquid 1-vinyl-3-carboxymethylimidazole bromide.                  The reaction is carried out under anhydrous and                  anaerobic conditions. and acetone is first                  degassed before use.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                heat-transfer medium, which comprises the                composition.

PT P
PN CN110498866-A
TI Preparation of desertliving cistanche polysaccharide by cutting desertliving cistanche stem, liquid carbon dioxide supercritical extracting, mixing with D101 macroporous resin, concentrating, dissolving using methanol, and purifying.
AU YANG C
AE JIANGSU ZHINENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A4102W
AB    NOVELTY - A desertliving cistanche polysaccharide is prepared by cutting desertliving cistanche stem as raw material into sections, putting into high-speed organization triturator, keeping for 5-7 minutes at speed of 12000 revolutions/minute (rpm), sieving with 60-mesh sieve, introducing liquid carbon dioxide supercritical extraction at 60-70 degrees C for 2-3 hours at pressure of 5-10 MPa, analyzing to obtain extract, fully and uniformly mixing with D101 macroporous resin at weight ratio of 1:1-3, loading in chromatography column, eluting with 70-80% ethanol at flow speed of 1-2 BV/hour, collecting eluent, concentrating to obtain crude product, dissolving using methanol, purifying to high pressure silica gel column chromatography, gradient eluting with acetonitrile-methanol solvent system, collecting fraction, vacuum concentrating, and crystallizing with supercritical carbon dioxide anti-solvent.
   USE - The method is used for preparing desertliving cistanche polysaccharide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation and high extraction efficiency. The product has high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of acetonitrile-methanol is 1:1-3. Crystallization is done at 30 degrees C at 10-20 MPa.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).)

PT P
PN CN110250313-A
TI Explosion candy comprises greengage extract, Perilla extract, white granulated sugar, and carbon dioxide.
AU MA Y
   FENG F
   LIU J
   YE Q
   TIANXIGELE
   YANG G
AE UNIV XIHUA (UYXH-C)
   JINCHUAN HUINONG PLANTING PROFESSIONAL (JINC-Non-standard)
GA 2019845794
AB    NOVELTY - Explosion candy comprises 20-40 pts. wt. greengage extract, 15-25 pts. wt. Perilla extract, 50-60 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, and 2-5 pts. wt. carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as explosion candy.
   ADVANTAGE - The explosion candy: utilizes the nutrient substances, which is rich in organic acids, minerals, vitamins, biological active substances, and the nutrients rich in plant protein rich in Perilla, and complements into a nutrient-rich taste unique and interesting green plum Perilla explosion candy to increase the availability of green plum and Perilla and enrich the variety of green plum and Perilla products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the candy, comprising (i) mixing the plum fruit extract and the perilla extract, and uniformly obtaining a mixed liquid; (ii) vacuum-concentrating the mixture to obtain a concentrated liquid; (iii) twicely homogenizing the concentrated liquid at 25-45 degrees C under 240-250 MPa; and (iv) using concentrated liquid as the core material, and using the white sand syrup filled with carbon dioxide as the wall material, then cooling and molding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The candy comprises 30 pts. wt. greengage extract, 20 pts. wt. Perilla extract, 50 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, and 5 pts. wt. carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The greengage extract is prepared by placing the plum in extracting tank, using edible ethanol as cosolvent at a high pressure pump pressure 30-35Mpa and pressure of the extraction kettle 20-25 Mpa, 1 pressure 5-7 Mpa separation kettle, separating kettle pressure of 2 is 5-7 Mpa, CO2 flow rate is 17-25 l/hour, and the constant temperature of 35-45 degrees C, and extracting for 1.5-2 hours. The microwave extraction before the plum pulp for enzymolysis, the enzymolysis method is: the greengage after pulping, adjusting pH to 3-4, adding 0.2-0.4% pectinase, and performing 35-45 degrees C lower enzymolysis for 1-3 hours. The preparation of the plum pulp is specifically comprises choosing the fresh and mature plum cleaning, removing the core, placing into 0.01-0.02% sodium sulfite solution, and blanching to more than 85-95 degrees C for 5- 10 minutes, and cooling to room temperature after pulping into plum pulp. The preparation of plum pulp is specifically comprises choosing the fresh and mature plum cleaning, removing kernel, placing into 0.1-0.3% isoascorbic acid, 0.02-0.06 N-Lcysteine, 0.5-1% citric acid mixed solution for 10-15 minutes, and then pulping into plum pulp. The Perilla extract is prepared by adding Perilla to 0.5-1 hour under the condition of 0.4-0.67 Mpa, where the Perilla powder is dried, pulverized by perilla leaves, and sieved through 40 mesh. The white sugar is mashed and filled with carbon dioxide to obtain uniform single high-inflated syrup, and the sweet tissue is brittle after cooling.

PN CN109999915-A; CN109999915-B
TI Porphyrin based metal organic framework material useful for carbon dioxide catalytic cyclization reaction comprises nickel ion+coordinated tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl) porphin as organic ligand and zirconium ion as framework metal ion.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV DALIAN MINZU (UYND-C)
GA 201962866G
AB    NOVELTY - Porphyrin based metal organic framework material comprises nickel ion + coordinated tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl) porphin as organic ligand and zirconium ion as framework metal ion.
   USE - The material is useful for carbon dioxide catalytic cyclization reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material exhibits high catalytic activity and high recyclability on the carbon dioxide cyclization reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the porphyrin based metal organic framework material comprising ultrasonic dispersing of zirconium chloride, triethylamine, acetic acid, nickel ion + coordinated tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl) porphin, water in dimethyl formamide, magnetic stirring at room temperature, transferring the obtained homogeneous solution to a stainless steel hydrothermal reactor, heating at 0-200 degrees C for 1-20 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging and washing many times with ethanol to obtain a flower shaped porphyrin based metal organic framework material PCN222 (nickel).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The morphology of the material is flower shaped with a particle size of 0.05-5 microns and a pore size of 1-5 nm. The material is a nano-catalyst capable of catalyzing the cyclization of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds. The epoxy substrate is an aromatic epoxy compound and an aliphatic epoxy compound. The molar ratio of zirconium chloride, triethylamine, acetic acid, nickel ion+ coordinated tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl) porphin and water is (1-20):(0.5-3):(0.5-3):(1-10):1.

TI Preparation of spherical calcium carbonate involves mixing quicklime with water for digestion, sieving, adding with solvent, refining to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, and performing supergravity carbonization reaction.
AU TONG Z
   LI S
   LI L
   LIAO D
   SU Y
   WANG J
   HE X
   HUANG S
   ZHOU W
   WANG Q
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
   GUANGXI CALCIUM CARBONATE INDUSTRIALIZAT (UYGU-C)
GA 2019500610
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of spherical calcium carbonate involves mixing quicklime with water in proportion for digestion, sieving through sieve to remove impurities, adding with solvent, adjusting and refining to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, adding calcium hydroxide slurry into circulating liquid tank of process device for supergravity carbonization reaction process, adding crystal form controlling agent to control the carbonization temperature, carbon dioxide gas flow rate, and super-gravity level, and allowing the calcium hydroxide slurry to countercurrent contact with the carbon dioxide gas to carry out carbonization reaction, and stop introducing carbon dioxide after the carbonization reaction is completed, centrifuging the slurry after carbonization, drying the obtained precipitate and pulverizing.
   USE - Preparation of spherical calcium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of spherical calcium carbonate with high production efficiency, narrow product particle size distribution, small particle size, uniform morphology and high dispersion effect, under short carbonization time, by industrial process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of quicklime to water is 1:(5-10). The concentration of calcium hydroxide is 70-115 g/L. The addition amount of crystal form controlling agent is 1-5 wt.% of calcium hydroxide slurry.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is ethanol solution having volume fraction of 30-80%. The crystal form controlling agent is amino acid. The amino acid is aspartic acid and glutamic acid. Preferred Conditions: The carbonization temperature is 10-40 degrees C. The carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 0.2-0.6 m3/hour. The rotational speed of rotor in supergravity reactor is 1000-1800 rpm. The centrifugation speed is 3000 rpm and centrifugation time is 3 minutes.

PN CN109384250-A
TI Direct utilizing method of mixed sodium salt comprises adding mixed sodium salt to ammonia water, carrying out gas-liquid reaction by introducing carbon dioxide into ammonia brine and calcining the precipitated crystals.
AU LI L
   MA Y
   LI P
   ZHAO Y
   CHEN W
   WEI G
   ZHANG X
   LI Z
   JIANG T
AE CHAMBROAD CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (CHAM-Non-standard)
GA 201921296S
AB    NOVELTY - Direct utilizing method of mixed sodium salt comprises (a) adding the mixed sodium salt to ammonia water to obtain an ammonia brine, where the mass concentration of the ammonia water is 6-8%, and the mass ratio of sodium sulfate and sodium chloride in the mixed sodium salt is 1:0.5-2, (b) carrying out gas-liquid reaction by introducing carbon dioxide into ammonia brine until the crystallization does not precipitate, and (c) calcining the precipitated crystals to obtain a resourced product.
   USE - The method is useful for directly utilizing the mixed sodium salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares ammonia salt by controlling the ratio of sodium sulfate and sodium chloride, uses carbon dioxide gas to carbonize the mixed sodium salt in the ammonia brine under alkaline conditions and converts the non-utilized mixed sodium salt to high value-added sodium carbonate product for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass concentration of the mixed sodium salt in the ammonia brine is 15-17%. The resourced product is sodium carbonate. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises adding the mixed sodium salt to the ammonia water at 40-45 degrees C. The gas-liquid reaction in the step (b) is carried out at 35-45 degrees C. The step (b) comprises washing the precipitated crystals with saturated sodium bicarbonate solution and then calcining at 260-300 degrees C for 1-2 hours.

TI Processing high quality instant sea cucumber            product, comprises e.g. soaking dried sea cucumber in            pure water, removing internal organ, placing in pure            water, adding rehydrating agent, soaking and processing            with high density carbon dioxide.

   XU C
   SUN H
AE COMTEMPORARY MARINE BIOTECHNILOGY SHENZHEN CO              LTD (COMT-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4681A
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high quality instant sea cucumber                product, comprises (a) pre-processing by placing                the dried sea cucumber in pure water at 30-60                degrees C, soaking for 30-90 minutes, and removing                internal organs, (b) re-hydrating sea cucumber by                placing in pure water, adding 10% rehydrating                agent, soaking at 40-65 degrees C for 30-60                minutes, and placing in an ice water bath, (c)                processing with high density carbon dioxide by                placing the rehydrated sea cucumber in a container                of high-density carbon dioxide treatment device,                raising the temperature to 20-75 degrees C, turning                on the high pressure pump to control the pressure                to 10-60 Mpa, processing for 10-60 minutes, and                releasing pressure for 5-10 minutes, and (d) vacuum                packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for processing high                quality instant sea cucumber product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens rehydration time.

PT P
PN IN201711017261-A
TI Producing biomaterial as biofuel from carbon dioxide concentrating endolithic bacterium Serratia by continuously enriching in presence of sodium bicarbonate as co-carbon source along with glucose and glycerol molasses.
AU THAKUR I S
   KUMAR M
   KHOSLA K
AE UNIV JAWAHARLAL NEHRU (UYJA-Non-standard)
GA 201895071X
AB    NOVELTY - System and method for production of biomaterial as biofuel from carbon dioxide concentrating endolithic bacterium Serratia ISTB104 comprises continuously enriching the bacteria in presence of sodium bicarbonate (50 mM) as co-carbon source along with glucose, glycerol molasses and municipal sewage secondary sludge.
   USE - The method is useful for producing biomaterial as biofuel from carbon dioxide concentrating endolithic bacterium Serratia ISTB104.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces excellent quality biomaterial product having suitable saturated and unsaturated fatty acid to increase fuel efficiency and properties and is suitable for existing engine technology. The method provides an alternative method for production of value added product using sludge as feedstock, utilizes municipal sewage sludge as carbon source, reduces environmental burden and cost of sludge management. The biomaterial extracted from biosolid enhances run time in genset and reduces consumption of conventional diesel. The method reduces anticipated decreasing supply of fossil fuels coupled with environmental safety and encourages use of biomaterial as renewable energy source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method involves optimization of sodium bicarbonate (10-100 mM) as co-metabolites in presence of methylsulfonylmethane (MSM), adjusting pH to 7.5 and stirring at 150 rpm for 48 hours. The characterization of municipal secondary sludge and its lipid content estimated before and after the amendment of bacteria shows that lipid content of municipal sewage secondary sludge increases after amendment of bacteria from 11.92% to 12.42% of biosolid. The municipal secondary sludge after amendment of bacteria and 50 mM sodium bicarbonate results in 4.7% increase in lipid in biosolid and formation of lipids is inside bacterial cells. The method involves using 1-5%, preferably 3% sulfuric acid as catalyst based on the vol.% of methanol, and carrying out reaction at a temperature of 45-65 degrees C, preferably 60 degrees C, biosolid to methanol ratio of 1:10-1:30 (weight/volume), preferably 1:25 (weight/volume) for 6-18 hours, preferably 15 hours to give the best yield of biomaterial. The optimization of conditions for production of fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) comprises sulfuric acid concentration of 3% (based on the weight of FAME), biosolid to methanol ratio of 1:25, temperature at 60 degrees C and duration of 15 hours and the method achieves a transesterification efficiency of 91% and purity of FAME of 97.68%. The carbon range produced by Serratia ISTB104 amended in sludge is 7-25C by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry and forms a potential source of biofuel. The biofuel produced and extracted from Serratia ISTB104 amended in municipal sewage secondary sludge is 1-5%, preferably 5% blended in conventional diesel, tested in genset (2.5 kW) showing the enhanced run time from 11 minutes/100 ml (conventional diesel) to 19 minute/100 ml (5% blended diesel volume/volume) and analyzed by gas analyzer showing 16-18% carbon dioxide, 0.10-0.11% cobalt, 40-47 ppm hydrocarbon and 17-18% oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The biomaterial produced and extracted from Serratia ISTB104 amended in municipal sewage secondary sludge is 1% blended in conventional diesel to balance fuel properties in terms of FAME composition and energy efficiency, K and viscosity 2.13 cSt at 40 degrees C and has total acidity of 0.30 mg potassium hydroxide (KOH/g), flash point of 37 degrees C, specific gravity of 0.8320 at 15 degrees C, cetane number by calculation of 56, cloud point of -10 degrees C, pour point of -18 degrees C and heating value (GCV) of 45796 KJ/kg.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108608093-A
TI Welding duplex stainless steel involves providing two duplex stainless steel plates to be docked, and forming single-sided V at docking position of two duplex stainless steel plates and fixing carbon dioxide welding gun to oscillator.
AU JIANG Z
   LIU B
   MAO W
   XU J
AE GUANGZHOU SHIPYARD INT CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 201880462B
AB    NOVELTY - Welding duplex stainless steel involves providing two duplex stainless steel plates to be docked, and forming a single-sided V at the docking position of the two duplex stainless steel plates, such that the angle of the groove is 55-65 degrees C, gap of the groove is 4-12 mm, and gasket is arranged on the back of the groove, and fixing the carbon dioxide welding gun to the oscillator such that the automatic oscillator performs driving of carbon dioxide welding torch to swing the weld from the front surface of the groove, and forming a bottom layer, filling layer and cover layer in turn in the groove.
   USE - Method for welding duplex stainless steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to weld duplex stainless steel, which reduces the welding seam grinding work during welding and improves the welding efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The welding process parameters for welding the underlayer are welding current 150-160 ampere, welding voltage 22-24 volt, welding speed 100-110 mm/minute. The welding process parameters for welding the filling layer are welding current 160-180 ampere, welding voltage 23-25 volt, welding speed 115-125 mm/minute. The welding process parameters for welding the cover layer are welding current 160-170 ampere, welding voltage 23-25 volt, welding speed 108-127 mm/minute. The carbon dioxide welding gun is oscillated along the width direction of the groove under the driving of the oscillator. The groove has opposite first bevel sides and second bevel sides. The carbon dioxide welding gun is oscillated in sequence between the side of the first groove, middle of the groove, and the side of the second groove. The carbon dioxide welding gun is oscillated in sequence between the second groove side, intermediate position of the groove, and the first groove side. The time for carbon dioxide welding gun to stay on the side of the first groove is 0.4-0.5 seconds. The carbon dioxide welding gun stays in the middle position of the groove at a time of 0.1-0.2 seconds. The height of each of the bead layers is less than 6 mm. The carbon dioxide welding torch has a swing frequency of 70 times/minute in welding the underlayer. The oscillation frequency of carbon dioxide welding torch in welding the filling layer is 40-50 times/minute. The swing frequency of carbon dioxide welding torch in welding the cover layer is 40-50 times/minute. The double-phasestainless steel plate is 9-36 mm thick. The liner is ceramic pad or copper pad. Preferred Process: The method further involves cleaning the welding residue of the gasket and the periphery of the cover layer, and smoothing the surface of the cover layer.

AU LEI W
   WANG J

   LI X
   ZHANG Y
   HOU W
AE UNIV JIANGSU TECHNOLOGY (JIAA-C)
GA 2018628823
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a nickel-based graphene composite material comprises derusting using 3-5% diluted hydrochloric acid for 5-15 minutes, sanding and polishing to obtain a surface roughness of 0.025-0.05 mu m, degreasing using a 1:1 mixture of absolute ethanol and acetone, and water washing a copper substrate to be plated; ultrasonically dispersing graphene oxide in deionized water, mixing with a plating solution, and ultrasonically stirring to obtain a complex plating solution; transferring the complex plating solution to a reaction kettle, adding surfactant, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and controlling carbon dioxide at 8-18 MPa and 40-80 degrees C; and carrying out phase commutation pulse electrodeposition using nickel plate as anode and the plated substrate as cathode.
   USE - Method for preparing of a nickel-based graphene composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has a compact structure, smooth surface. It has improved compactness hardness, wear resistance, and corrosion resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The complex plating solution comprises 0.05-0.3 g/L, preferably 0.15-0.2 g/L graphene oxide, 200-400 g/L, preferably 300-350 g/L nickel sulfamate, 20-40 g/L, preferably 30-35 g/L nickel chloride, 30-50 g/L, preferably 35-40 g/L boric acid, and 0.1-0.3 g/L, preferably 0.15-0.25 g/L sodium dodecyl sulfate. The surfactant comprises 0.1-0.2 mass% polyethylene glycol trimethyl nonyl ether.

The preparation of the quark cheese comprises mixing pre-fermented milk with chymosin, fermenting to pH 4.3-4.7 and draining the whey and obtaining the quark cheese. The fermentation temperature is 30-33degrees Celsius. The pre-fermentation step comprises mixing the pre-treated raw milk with a fermenting agent and pre-fermenting to obtain pre-fermented raw milk, where the fermenting agent comprises lactic acid bacteria. The temperature of the pre-fermentation is 30-33degrees Celsius and the time is 30-60 minutes. The pre-treated process comprises sterilizing, homogenizing, cooling the raw milk to 30-33degrees Celsius to obtain pre-treated raw milk, where the temperature of the sterilization is 72-75degrees Celsius and the time is 15-20 seconds, the homogenizing speed is 14000-16000revolutions/minute and the time is 0.5-1.5 minutes. The whey row comprises hanging strip whey. The temperature of the whey row is 10-14degrees Celsius and the time is 6-8 hours.

TI Carbon dioxide recirculation system, comprises a four-step heat exchanger that liquefies carbon dioxide and stores it in the same device, and a cooling subsystem operated with water and coupled to the exchanger.
AU STASHENKO E
   MARTINEZ MORALES J R
   ARIAS VELANDIA A J
   BELTRAN MORENO S A
   GELVEZ AROCHA O A
   RIVERO GEREDA S A
AE UNIV SANTANDER (UYSA-Non-standard)
GA 201830936E
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recirculation system, comprises: a four-step heat exchanger that liquefies carbon dioxide and stores it in the same device; and a cooling subsystem operated with water and coupled to the exchanger, where carbon dioxide coming from an application under known conditions of temperature and pressure enters the exchanger that is responsible for removing the heat, so that the carbon dioxide at a constant pressure can be liquefied and stored in a liquid state within the same device.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide recirculation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system cab be manufactured in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide recirculation system, comprises: a four-step heat exchanger that liquefies carbon dioxide and stores it in the same device; and a cooling subsystem operated with water and coupled to the exchanger, where carbon dioxide coming from an application under known conditions of temperature and pressure enters the exchanger that is responsible for removing the heat, so that the carbon dioxide at a constant pressure can be liquefied and stored in a liquid state within the same device. The carbon dioxide can be re-pressurized and used again since the system consists of two cleaning filters (in which a first cleaning filter (10) is activated carbon, and a second cleaning filter (11) with functionalized zeolite is located in line where the carbon dioxide passes in gaseous state coming from the application with pressure of 45-55 bar and 20-40 degrees C), and a ball valve (17) that controls the passage of carbon dioxide in a gaseous state to a condenser (12). The carbon dioxide in a gaseous state has contact with the external surface of the tubes at the operating pressure at which the carbon dioxide condenses, through which cooled water circulates inside it at a temperature of 9-11 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the components of carbon dioxide recirculation system.
   First cleaning filter (10)
   Second cleaning filter (11)
   Condenser (12)
   Ball valve (17)
   Carbon dioxide valve (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The integrated device for liquefying and storing the fluid, comprises: a condenser with a carbon dioxide valve (20) through which the liquid carbon dioxide passes for recirculation; a condenser that condenses carbon dioxide and stores the carbon dioxide in the same container; a centrifugal pump for circulating water that passes from a reservoir of chilled water to the condenser; and a condensing unit. The condenser comprises a storage capacity of 25 kg liquid carbon dioxide to ensure proper operation, tubes, tube holder, blind flange, blind flange bolts, base bolts of tube holder, helmet and helmet cup. The liquid carbon dioxide fluid is sucked by positive displacement pumps with a minimum density of 850 kg/m3. The capacitor has a safety valve coupled to prevent the increase of pressure in case the electric flow maintained by the water cooling subsystem is interrupted. The condenser has: a front head type C, helmet type E and rear head type U, where the front head type C has bolts, blind flange and tube holder plate that allows the cleaning of the helmet without having to disassemble the tube bundle and consists of a 10 inch steel tube with a length of 0.2 m welded to a blind flange rating of 300 that makes the function of the tube holder plate; and T-shaped steel plate, nipple and nipple with their respective nuts and having at the opposite end a slip-on rating 150 flange that allows access to the tube bundle. The E-type hull is configured in the condenser with an upper inlet and a lower outlet having a 10 inch steel tube with a length of 1.1 m welded to U-type rear head with 10 inch steel cup in the upper part and a slip-on flange on the lower part. The U type rear head of the condenser has a configuration that allows the four steps through the tubes independently without having to involve the helmet, facilitates the removal of the same for maintenance and consists of the circulation tubes of coolant water bent in a U-shape in such a way that allows the refrigerant to pass through the helmet four times before leaving the condenser. The integrated liquefied device condenser and fluid storage comprise parameters in its condenser including 124 tubes of 17 mm cross-section, longitudinal passage of 14.7 mm, diameter of 0.5 inch tubes, material copper pipes type-m, length of tubes of 1 m, four steps per tube, temperature of 9-11 degrees C, pressure in the pipes of 1-2 bar and mass flow of cooling water of 0.00185 kg/second, carbon dioxide flow with four baffles, separation between baffles of 20 cm, entry temperature of 20-35 degrees C, output temperature of 12-14 degrees C, minimum output density of 850 kg/m3, pressure at the hull of 55 bar, carbon dioxide mass flow of less than 100 g/minute and hull diameter of 10 inch. The condenser has a helmet with a thickness of 3.45-9.27 mm, tubes with a thickness of 0.0396-0.635 mm, plate holder with a thickness of 16.1-54 mm, 16 bolts with diameter of 0.75-1 inch, torque of tightening of 1670 lbf-inch, required thickness of the flange for the helmet of 53.6-54 mm. The cooling subsystem comprises a water reservoir, a pump to move it, an evaporator and a condensing unit that uses R-22 (dichlorodifluoromethane-saturated liquid) as a cooling liquid to cool the water, where the subsystem is responsible for circulating the cooling water through the copper tubes that make up the condenser and storage of carbon dioxide, whose coolant temperature inside the copper tubes must be lower than 12 degrees C in such a way that the surface temperature is less than 12.9 degrees C to obtain subcooled carbon dioxide with a minimum density of 850 kg/m3 and the amount of heat required to extract the carbon dioxide that is calculated to enter at a rate of 100 g/minute at 35 degrees C and under pressure conditions of 55 bar that is 360 Watts equivalent to 1230 BTU/hour or 310 kcal/hour, a coolant temperature of 11 degrees C since the process of condensation of carbon dioxide at 55 bar starts at 18.27 degrees C.

TI Carbon dioxide production and sealing technology involves using industrial gas rich in carbon dioxide, producing liquid and solid carbon dioxide through short process flow, and transporting produced carbon dioxide to appropriate sea area.
AU WEI R
   WEI S
AE WEI S (WEIS-Individual)
GA 202044294W
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide production and sealing technology comprises using industrial gas rich in carbon dioxide, producing liquid and solid carbon dioxide through short process flow, transporting produced carbon dioxide to appropriate sea area, injecting and putting into low-temperature deep sea and permafrost ice layer, utilizing own cooling capacity, condensing surrounding water into ice shell, sealing in ice, and absorbing very slowly by seawater and organisms.
   USE - Carbon dioxide production and sealing technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The separated purge gas is used as refrigerant to form product. The separated residue is mixed with impurities removed by compression, dust accumulated by dust removal, and calcium oxide by-product, and introduced into the desert to fix sand.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide production and sealing technology comprises utilizing fluid rich in carbon dioxide producing gas source, mixing with dry powder calcium oxide, dry powder calcium carbonate and/or other substances that can neutralize acidic substances into reactor including loop reactor, fluidized bed reactor, fluidized bed reactor, fixed bed reactor, cyclone reactor and/or stirred reactor, neutralizing, feeding into dust collector, feeding fluid into fan, compressing, moving into slag remover, removing impurities and slag, cooling and condensing, processing into primary cooling heat exchanger and cryogenic heat exchanger, feeding into separator, performing absorption desorption separator and flash heat separator, moving into steam rectification distillation kettle, thermal rectification distillation tower, oblique distillation tower and/or spiral distillation tower, releasing heat, cooling and condensing, separating, and obtaining purge gas product, non-condensable gas, fine carbon dioxide liquid product, crude carbon dioxide liquid product and separated residue, compressing non-condensable gas, condensing and purifying, and processing purge gas product, compressing non-condensable gas, condensing and purifying, processing purge gas product, crude liquid nitrogen product and crude carbon dioxide liquid product into dry ice products, mixing separated residue is mixed with slag discharged from slag remover, utilizing dust accumulated from dust remover and calcium oxide by-product discharged from reactor to form crude calcium salt product, injecting crude carbon dioxide liquid product into low-temperature deep sea through liquid carbon dioxide sealing system, introducing dry ice products into low-temperature deep sea through dry ice sealing system, stacking and piling crude calcium salt products, burying through the calcium salt sand fixation system, and injecting into mine and desert sand fixation after slurry adjustment.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).)
MC E31-N05C; J01-E; J01-E03; J01-G; J01-G06; J07-A08

PN KR2006474-B1
TI Mediated electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide involves injecting catholyte containing precursor of electron mediator into cathode chamber, reducing precursor by applying current, and reacting carbon dioxide with electron mediator.
AU MOON I
   GOVINDAN M R
AE UNIV IACF SUNCHON NAT (UYIA-Non-standard)
GA 201968201K
AB    NOVELTY - Mediated electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide involves introducing a catholyte solution containing a precursor of an electron mediator into a catholyte reservoir, injecting anolyte solution into a anolyte reservoir, injecting catholyte containing precursor of electron mediator into a cathode chamber, injecting anolyte into anode chamber, reducing precursor of the electron mediator by applying current to the anode chamber, dissolving carbon dioxide in the catholyte solution to react carbon dioxide with electron mediator to remove carbon dioxide to produce formaldehyde.
   USE - Mediated electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and efficient mediated electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide without carrying out catalyst treatment, by preventing catalyst deactivation or fouling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for mediated electrochemical reduction system of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The precursor of the electron mediator is halogenated transition metal compound of formula: (M(power)(x+)(HA)y)(power)(z-), preferably of formula: (Co2+(CN)5)3-, and electron mediator is halogenated transition metal compound of formula: (M(power)((x-1)+)(HA)y)(power)((z+1)-), preferably of formula: (Co1+(CN)8)4-, where M is transition metal, HA is halogen or pseudohalogen material, x, y and z are 2-10, and y-x = z. The anolyte solution comprises an acidic solution. The catholyte solution contains a basic solution. The concentration of carbon dioxide is 1-100 ppm. The system further comprises a carrier gas supply unit for supplying carrier gas for carrying carbon dioxide. Preferred Properties: The acidic solution has a pH of 1 or less. The pH of the basic solution is 13 or more.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN WO2022194559-A1; EP4059905-A1
TI Converting starting material containing calcium            silicon hydroxide phases and calcium aluminum            hydrohydro phases into supplementary cementitious            material and calcium carbonate additive, comprises e.g.            mixing starting material with water.
AU ZAJAC M
   PATO N
   SKOCEK J
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
GA 2022C41455
AB    NOVELTY - Converting a starting material containing at                least 40 wt.% calcium silicon hydroxide phases and                calcium aluminum hydroxide phases into an silicon                dioxide rich supplementary cementitious materials                and a calcium carbonate additive, comprises:                providing the starting material with a D90of                &#8804; 1 mm; mixing the starting material with                water or adjusting the water content to provide a                starting material slurry having a solid:liquid                ratio of 2:1-1:100; passing the starting material                slurry into a gravity separation reactor together                with carbon dioxide; subjecting the starting                material slurry and carbon dioxide to centrifugal                motion inside the reactor; and removing a heavy                slurry of higher density particles, removing a                light slurry of lower density particles comprising                the undissolved silicon dioxide rich remains of the                starting material from a second outlet of the                reactor, and removing liquid from a third outlet of                the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for converting a starting                material containing 40 wt.% calcium silicon                hydroxide phases and calcium aluminum oxide phases                into an silicon oxide rich SCM and calcium                carbonate additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high performance SCM to                minimize the environmental footprint of cement and                concrete industry environmental footprint by                utilization of industrial by-products used as                supplementary cementitious materials (SCM) has                reached global availability limits of these                materials. The method reuses concrete demolition                waste and provides high-performance SCM.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting a starting material containing at                least 40 wt.% calcium silicon hydroxide phases and                calcium aluminum hydroxide phases into an silicon                dioxide rich supplementary cementitious materials                and a calcium carbonate additive, comprises:                providing the starting material with a D90of                &#8804; 1 mm; mixing the starting material with                water or adjusting the water content to provide a                starting material slurry having a solid:liquid                ratio of 2:1-1:100; passing the starting material                slurry into a gravity separation reactor together                with carbon dioxide; subjecting the starting                material slurry and carbon dioxide to centrifugal                motion inside the reactor; and removing a heavy                slurry of higher density particles comprising the                calcium carbonate additive formed by reaction of                carbon dioxide with calcium ions dissolved or                leached from the starting material from a first                outlet of the reactor, removing a light slurry of                lower density particles comprising the undissolved                silicon dioxide rich remains of the starting                material from a second outlet of the reactor, and                removing liquid from a third outlet of the reactor.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for gravity                separation reactor comprising an inlet (11) for a                slurry (a) and an inlet (12) for carbon dioxide (b)                or an inlet for a slurry premixed with carbon                dioxide (c), a reaction chamber (13) adapted to                subject the introduced slurry (a, c) to centrifugal                motion, a first outlet (14) for a heavy slurry (d)                of higher density particles, a second outlet (15)                for a light slurry (e) of lower density particles                and a third outlet for liquid (f).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows the device.
   11Starting material slurry inlet
   12Carbon dioxide inlet
   13Reactor chamber
   14First outlet
   15Second outlet
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The starting material is                  obtained from waste materials and/or by-products,                  preferably from concrete demolition waste,                  material left over from concreting, slag, ash,                  and muds, most preferably from concrete                  demolition waste and/or material left over from                  concreting. The centrifugal motion is provided by                  adjusting the rotational speed to 10000-150000                  rev min-1, preferably 20000-50000 rev min-1. The                  liquid removed from the third outlet of the                  reactor is used to at least partly replace water                  for mixing with the starting material to form the                  starting material slurry. Preferred Components:                  The starting material has a D90of &#8804; 500                  pm, preferably &#8804; 125 pm. The starting                  material comprises at least 50 wt.%, preferably                  at least 70 wt.% calcium silicon hydroxide phases                  and calcium aluminum hydroxide phases. The                  calcium silicon hydroxide phases and calcium                  aluminum hydroxide phases comprises calcium                  silicon hydrates, alite, belite, rankinite,                  wollastonite, hydrogarnet, ettringite, calcium                  aluminum hydrates, or calcium silicon/aluminum                  hydroxides with additional elements. The carbon                  dioxide is at least partly introduced in gaseous                  form, preferably with a concentration of 1-100                  vol.%. The carbon dioxide is at least partly                  introduced in the form of a solution, preferably                  with a concentration of 0.1-20 wt.%. The carbon                  dioxide gas is an exhaust gas, preferably from                  cement manufacturing and/or from a gas fired                  power plant and/or a coal fired power                  plant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The gravity separation                  reactor further comprises: a pre reactor adapted                  to receive starting material slurry (a) and                  carbon dioxide (b) and means for passing a slurry                  premixed with carbon dioxide (c) from the                  pre-reactor to the inlet for slurry; and a fluid                  connection for passing the liquid (f) to a mixing                  means for making the starting material slurry                  (a). The first outlet is configured to remove a                  heavy slurry with a density of 2.50-3.10 g/cm3,                  preferably 2.60-3 g/cm3, and/or the second outlet                  is configured to remove a light slurry with a                  density of 1.5-2.5 g/cm3, preferably 1.7-2.3                  g/cm3, and/or the third outlet is configured to                  remove a liquid with a density of 1-1.2 g/cm3,                  preferably 1-1.1 g/cm3.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)

PN CN114890556-A
TI Integrated device for removing calcium from carbon            dioxide, comprises movable mounting frame, reaction            container, feeding pump, dosing pump, pipeline mixer,            mixing pipeline and control module, where feed pump            mounted on mounting frame, conveys wastewater            containing calcium ions, to pipeline mixer.
AU CHEN Z
   SHANG Y
   ZHOU H
AE SINOCHEM ZHEJIANG MEMBRANE IND DEV CO (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2022A7028C
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated device for removing calcium from                carbon dioxide, comprises a movable mounting frame,                a reaction container, a feeding pump (110), a                dosing pump (220), a pipeline mixer, a mixing                pipeline and a control module (10). The feed pump                mounted on the movable mounting frame, is used for                conveying the wastewater containing calcium ions,                to the pipeline mixer. The dosing pump mounted on                the movable mounting frame, is used for conveying                liquid alkali to the pipeline mixer. The pipeline                mixer arranged on the movable mounting frame, is                used for mixing the waste water and the liquid                alkali. The reaction vessel arranged on the movable                mounting frame, has side wall close to the bottom,                provided with a feed inlet and air inlet. The feed                inlet is communicated with the pipeline mixer                through the mixing pipeline, so that the mixed                liquid of the waste water, and the liquid alkali                enter the reaction container through the feed                inlet.
   USE - Integrated device is used for removing calcium                from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device having compact structure, has                improved efficiency of removing calcium from carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated device for removing calcium from                carbon dioxide, comprises a movable mounting frame,                a reaction container, a feeding pump (110), a                dosing pump (220), a pipeline mixer, a mixing                pipeline and a control module (10). The feed pump                mounted on the movable mounting frame, is used for                conveying the wastewater containing calcium ions,                to the pipeline mixer. The dosing pump mounted on                the movable mounting frame, is used for conveying                liquid alkali to the pipeline mixer. The pipeline                mixer arranged on the movable mounting frame, is                used for mixing the waste water and the liquid                alkali. The reaction vessel arranged on the movable                mounting frame, has side wall close to the bottom,                provided with a feed inlet and air inlet. The feed                inlet is communicated with the pipeline mixer                through the mixing pipeline, so that the mixed                liquid of the waste water, and the liquid alkali                enter the reaction container through the feed                inlet. The carbon dioxide enters the reaction                container through the air inlet. The liquid outlet                is arranged on the side wall of the reaction vessel                close to the top. The supernatant liquid formed                after reaction, is suitable for being discharged                from the liquid outlet. The top of the reaction                vessel, is provided with a gas outlet for                discharging unreacted gas and the side wall of the                reaction vessel close to the bottom, is also                provided with a discharge hole for discharging                sediment generated by the reaction. The side wall                of the reaction container, is also provided with a                detection port. The PH detector enables detecting                the PH of the solution in the reaction container,                through the detection port. The control module                installed on the movable installation frame, is                electrically connected to the feeding pump and is                used for controlling the opening and closing of the                feeding pump. The control module is further                electrically connected with the dosing pump and the                PH detector, to automatically control the opening                and closing of the dosing pump, based on the data                detected by the PH detector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows top view of carbon dioxide                decalcification device.
   10Control module
   110Feeding pump
   220Dosing pump

PT P
PN CN112479848-A
TI Synthesizing coenzyme Q10 by biological fermentation comprises e.g. subjecting coenzyme Q10 bacteria residue to pressure and carrying out cycle extraction by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction separation and initial extraction.
AU CHENG Z
AE SHANDONG TAIHE WATER TREATMENT CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021306674
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing coenzyme Q10 by biological fermentation comprises e.g. (i) subjecting the coenzyme Q10 bacteria residue produced by the microbial fermentation method to a pressure of 42MPa, where the extraction and separation temperature is 40-45 degrees C, controlling flow rate of carbon dioxide to 30-50 liter/h and carrying out the cycle extraction for 1-4 hours by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction separation and initial extraction treatment to obtain a 50-60%crude extract of Coenzyme Q10; (ii) mixing the crude Coenzyme Q10 extract obtained with anhydrous ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:(1-5), heating to 45-50 degrees C and stirring to make it a homogeneous liquid mixed system; (iii) maintaining continuously system temperature to 45-50 degrees C, adding silicon dioxide gel powder with a particle size of 100-200 meshes according to mass ratio of the mixed solution obtained in step (ii) of (1-3):1, and stirring thoroughly to make the material uniformly adsorb on the silicon dioxide gel to form a sample.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing coenzyme Q10 by biological fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can utilize the elution of the mixed system of supercritical carbon dioxide and organic solvent to separate and purify the bio-fermentation synthesis of coenzyme Q10; provides good separation effect and high carbon dioxide utilization rate; utilizes less organic solvent; is safe and environmentally-friendly; produces product with purity of more than 99%; and reuses silicon dioxide gel and solvent which is conducive to the implementation of large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing coenzyme Q10 by biological fermentation comprises (i) subjecting the coenzyme Q10 bacteria residue produced by the microbial fermentation method to a pressure of 42MPa, where the extraction and separation temperature is 40-45 degrees C, controlling the flow rate of carbon dioxide to 30-50 liter/h and carrying out the cycle extraction for 1-4 hours by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction separation and initial extraction treatment to obtain a 50-60%crude extract of Coenzyme Q10; (ii) mixing the crude Coenzyme Q10 extract obtained with anhydrous ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:(1-5), heating to 45-50 degrees C and stirring to make it a homogeneous liquid mixed system; (iii) maintaining continuously the system temperature to 45-50 degrees C, adding silicon dioxide gel powder with a particle size of 100-200 meshes according to the mass ratio of the mixed solution obtained in step (ii) of (1-3):1, and stirring thoroughly to make the material uniformly adsorb on the silicon dioxide gel to form a sample; (iv) spreading the sample prepared in step (iii) flat on the lower end of the extraction kettle, adding blank silicon dioxide gel powder of the same specification in step (iii) into the top of the sample preparation, compacting fully it so that 2-3 cm of space is reserved at the upper end of the extraction kettle, using the supercritical and organic eluents for elution and separation, distilling and drying to obtain yellow Coenzyme Q10 product and recycling the recovered solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the content of coenzyme Q10 in the bacterial residue is 2-3%. In the step (i), the impurity components in the crude extract with a mass percentage of coenzyme Q10 of 50-60% are mainly 5-demethoxy-coenzyme Q10, coenzyme Q9, coenzyme Q11 and reduced coenzyme Q9 and a small amount of menadione 8. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the method comprises injecting supercritical and organic eluents simultaneously into the extraction kettle for chromatographic separation of the silicon dioxide sample composition. The method comprises injecting the organic eluent from the carrier inlet of the supercritical extraction device according to actual needs at a set flow rate. In the step (iv), the circulating flow rate control range of supercritical carbon dioxide is 10-20 liter/h, the flow rate control range of the organic eluent is 0.1-1 liter/h, and the elution separation time is 1-3 hours. The method comprises recycling and reusing the silicon dioxide gel powder after repeated extraction of supercritical carbon dioxide, and recycling and reusing the organic solvent eluent by distillation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The organic eluent is formed by mixing any two or three of absolute ethanol, petroleum ether, ethyl acetate, chloroform, acetone or n-hexane, etc. in any suitable ratio.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F10; B04-L02; B10-A06; B11-A01A; B11-B03; B14-Y; D05-C03A
IP C07C-046/10; C07C-050/28

PN CN112403474-A; CN112403474-B
TI New supported carbon dioxide hydrogenation            catalyst comprising copper-aluminum-magnesium hydrogen            peroxide complex, where carrier comprises            self-assembled and staggered stacking of sheet-like            basic structural units.
AU DU T
   FANG X
   CHEN C
   LI Y
   JIA H
   LI J
   LI Z
   WANG Y
   SONG Y
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202123617H
AB    NOVELTY - A supported carbon dioxide hydrogenation                catalyst comprising copper-aluminum-magnesium                hydrogen peroxide complex (I), is new and ensures                stable structure and performance of the catalyst,                where the carrier comprises self-assembled and                staggered stacking of sheet-like basic structural                units, the surface has sheet-like protrusions, the                outer surface area is abundant, the carrier as a                whole is a regular sphere with uniform size and                high dispersion, with an outer diameter of 10-20 mu                m and a hollow structure, the copper support is                carried on the outer surface of the hollow sphere,                and the average particle size is less than 200                nm.
   USE - As supported carbon dioxide hydrogenation                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The supported carbon dioxide hydrogenation                catalyst has high specific surface area, improved                copper dispersion effect and high raw material                conversion rate. The method ensures high yield,                does not require structure-directing agent and                special requirements for equipment, and is simple                to operate and suitable for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A supported carbon dioxide hydrogenation                catalyst comprising copper-aluminum-magnesium                hydrogen peroxide complex of formula (I):                CunAlxMgyM1-x-yCx/2H2O1.5x+2, is new and ensures                stable structure and performance of the catalyst,                where the carrier comprises self-assembled and                staggered stacking of sheet-like basic structural                units, the surface has sheet-like protrusions, the                outer surface area is abundant, the carrier as a                whole is a regular sphere with uniform size and                high dispersion, with an outer diameter of 10-20 mu                m and a hollow structure, the copper support is                carried on the outer surface of the hollow sphere,                and the average particle size is less than 200                nm.
   Copper = supported substance;
   AlxMgyM1-x-yCx/2H2O1.5x+2 = carrier;
   M = divalent metal cation comprising zinc ion,                nickel ion, ferrous ion, strontium ion, barium ion,                manganese ion or cobalt ion;
   n = 0-x;
   x = 0.25-0.5;and
   y = 0.5x (1-x)-1.25x (1-x).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the supported carbon dioxide                hydrogenation catalyst, which involves (i)                preparing magnesium ion (2+)-metal ion                (2+)-aluminum ion (3+) ternary mixed metal salt                solution according to the metal element molar ratio                of magnesium ion (2+) and metal ion (2+) to                aluminum ion (3+) to be (1.25-0.5):1, (ii) adding                excess urea to the ternary mixed metal salt                solution obtained in step (i) under continuous                stirring conditions, and continuously stirring at                room temperature until completely dissolved to                obtain a clear precursor mixture, (iii)                transferring the precursor mixture obtained in step                (ii) to a homogeneous reactor, and reacting under                high temperature and stirring conditions, (iv)                filtering the post-reaction solution obtained in                step (iii), and completely washing and drying the                filter residue to obtain a self-assembled carrier,                (v) activating, uniformly dispersing the                self-assembled carrier obtained in step (iv) in a                copper ion solution, and dripping a reducing agent                solution under water bath conditions to perform                liquid-phase reduction, and (vi) after the liquid                phase reduction is completed, repeatedly washing                the precipitate, filtering, and drying to obtain                the supported carbon dioxide hydrogenation                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The number of moles                  of urea in step (ii) is 1.1-1.5 times the total                  number of moles of metal cations in the solution.                  Preferred Components: The copper ion solution                  used in step (v) comprises copper nitrate, copper                  sulfate, copper chloride and/or copper acetate.                  The reducing agent solution used in step (v)                  comprises vitamin C solution, sodium citrate                  solution, sodium borohydride solution, oleic                  acid, hydrazine hydrate, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  formaldehyde and hydroxylamine hydrochloride.                  Preferred Method: The step (v) involves calcining                  and activating the carrier at 250-450 degrees C                  for 4-6 hours before use, where the calcination                  atmosphere comprises air, nitrogen, argon and/or                  helium. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the                  ultrasonic treatment is carried out on the mixed                  metal salt solution for 5-30 minutes. In step                  (iii), the homogeneous reaction is carried out at                  100-150 degrees C while accompanied by a stirring                  operation.

AE ZHUHAI GONGTONG LOW CARBON TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2021272310
AB    (CN212594024-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a CO2 split reboiler structure, comprising a rectifying tower; and a reboiler; the rectifying tower bottom liquid outlet is connected with the reboiler shell side bottom liquid inlet pipeline through pipeline; the rectifying tower middle gas phase inlet is connected with the reboiler shell side gas phase outlet through pipeline; the shell side gas phase outlet of the reboiler is connected with the middle gas phase inlet of the rectifying tower. The split reboiler structure of the utility model, the rectifying tower bottom CO2 liquid to obtain the new heat source, again vaporizing and separating non-condensable gas; The utility model can randomly design the structure form according to the actual need, heat exchange effect size, which can improve the re-boiling efficiency and obtain larger processing ability. The utility model reboiler shell inner CO2 liquid level can be accurately controlled by the liquid level meter monitoring and liquid level adjusting valve; the temperature can be adjusted by the thermometer monitoring and temperature adjusting valve, improving the rectifying and purifying effect, so as to obtain the carbon dioxide product with higher precision.

   Rectification tower (1)
   Reboiler (2)
   Product outlet pipeline (5)
   High-temperature condensed gas pipeline (6)
   Low temperature condensed liquid outlet pipeline (7)

   Temperature adjusting valve (11)
EA    NOVELTY - The structure has a rectification tower (1) and a reboiler (2). The liquid outlet at the bottom of the rectification tower is connected to the liquid inlet pipeline at the bottom of the housing side of reboiler through a pipeline. A gas phase inlet in the middle of rectification tower and the gas phase outlet of the housing side of the reboiler are connected by a pipeline. The reboiler is independently set as a complete U-shaped tube kettle reboiler. The tube side takes the condensing medium as the heat source, and the housing side takes the carbon dioxide medium. The tube side inlet of the reboiler is connected with a high-temperature condensed gas pipeline (6).
   USE - Carbon dioxide (CO2) split reboiler structure used in chemical device.
   ADVANTAGE - The split reboiler structure is designed according to the actual structure form, heat exchange effect size which improves the re-boiling efficiency and obtain larger processing capacity. The carbon dioxide liquid level in the reboiling housing is accurately controlled by the liquid level meter monitoring and liquid level adjusting valve. The temperature is adjusted by the thermometer monitoring and temperature adjusting valve, and improves the rectifying and purifying effect to obtain the carbon dioxide product more high precision.
