TI Production of urea involves heating ammonia and carbon dioxide, decomposing ammonium carbamate, and separating mixed gas, absorbing, generating low pressure steam, and producing gas phase and liquid phase.
AU KOJIMA Y
   SASAKI K
   YANAGAWA T
AE TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
GA 202042068K
AB    NOVELTY - Production of urea involves synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide to produce a urea synthesis solution, heating the urea synthesis solution produced in the synthesis step, decomposing ammonium carbamate and separating a mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the urea synthesis liquid, absorbing a mixed gas, generating a low pressure steam using heat generated during this condensation, treating after a urea synthesis liquid in the high-pressure decomposition step is heated at a pressure lower than the high-pressure decomposition step and higher than atmospheric pressure, producing a gas phase and a liquid phase and separating the gas phase to obtain a urea synthesis solution having an increased urea concentration and a low pressure steam condensate, heating the urea synthesis liquid after is treated in the high-pressure decomposition step, and using a portion of the low-pressure steam generated in the condensation step.
   USE - Production of urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of urea has high utility value from the low-pressure-steam condensate. The obtained urea synthesis solution having increased urea concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of urea involves synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide to produce a urea synthesis solution, heating the urea synthesis solution produced in the synthesis step, decomposing ammonium carbamate and separating a mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the urea synthesis liquid, absorbing a mixed gas obtained in the high-pressure decomposition step by an absorption medium and condensed, generating a low pressure steam using heat generated during this condensation, treating after a urea synthesis liquid in the high-pressure decomposition step is heated at a pressure lower than the high-pressure decomposition step and higher than atmospheric pressure, producing a gas phase and a liquid phase and separating the gas phase to obtain a urea synthesis solution having an increased urea concentration and a low pressure steam condensate, heating the urea synthesis liquid after is treated in the high-pressure decomposition step, and using a portion of the low-pressure steam generated in the condensation step. The urea synthesis liquid is treated in the purification step is heated at a pressure lower than the pressure in the purification step and at atmospheric pressure or pressure lower than atmospheric pressure. The low pressure fluid generation process of generating the low-pressure-steam condensate and the low pressure steam by pressure-reducing at least one portion of the low-pressure-steam condensate obtained from the one or both of the purification process and the concentration processes to a pressure higher than atmospheric pressure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for urea production apparatus, which comprises synthesizer to synthesize urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide to produce a urea synthesis solution, high-pressure decomposer to separate a mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the urea synthesis liquid, condenser to absorb at least portion of the mixed gas obtained by the high-pressure decomposer into an absorption medium, refining device comprising heat exchange structure for heating a portion of the low-pressure steam generated in the condenser, and low pressure steam condensate and low pressure fluid generator to generate low pressure steam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process-flow diagram of a schematic structure of urea manufacturing plant.
   Condenser outlet gas line (123)
   Outlet gas (132)
   Mixed gas line (133)
   Absorption medium (173)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The low-low pressure steam condensate and the low-low pressure steam have a pressure of 3 bar or more. The difference between the pressure of low pressure steam condensate and low pressure steam and the pressure of the low pressure steam generated in the condensation step is 2 bar or more. Preferred Process: The method involves pressurizing at least a portion of low-pressure steam condensate used as a steam condensate for generating the low-pressure steam in the condensing step, further involves heating the raw material ammonia supplied to at least one step chosen from synthesis step, the high pressure decomposition step, and the condensation step by using at least a portion of the low pressure steam condensate or low pressure steam as a heating source. In the concentration step, at least a portion of low-pressure steam condensate and low-pressure steam are used as a heating source for heating the urea synthesis liquid which is treated in the purification step. The method further involves producing granular solid urea using air from the urea synthesis liquid treated in the concentration step, heating the air using at least a portion of the low pressure steam condensate and heating the air using at least a portion of the low pressure steam. The synthesis step and the condensation step are performed in a single pressure vessel.

PN ID201900493-S
TI Optimizing soluble molecule from mesenchymal stem cell metabolite used for e.g. regenerating damaged tissue, by transferring mesenchymal stem cells into hypoxic chamber, passing carbon dioxide, incubating and harvesting soluble molecules.
AU PUTRA A
AE PUTRA A (PUTR-Individual)
GA 2019820475
AB    NOVELTY - Method of optimizing soluble molecule from mesenchymal stem cell metabolites, involves: inserting the mesenchymal stem cells from culture into a flask, and observing the cell growth with an inverted microscope; transferring the obtained derivative-4 and mesenchymal stem cell-like substances into a hypoxic room in the form of a chamber; placing oxygenometer in the chamber; passing carbon dioxide gas into the chamber; closing the hypoxic chamber by closing the carbon dioxide gas flow; ensuring no leaks to maintain the oxygen level; incubating the hypoxic chamber with 5% carbon dioxide to obtain optimal soluble molecule with mesenchymal stem cell metabolites and incubating to retrieve soluble molecules; harvesting soluble molecules in biosafety type II cabinets and inserting into a sterile conical tube; centrifuging conical tubes containing soluble molecules; and filtrating with a membrane syringe to obtain a sterile soluble molecule, which can be used as a therapeutic material.
   USE - The soluble molecule is used as a therapeutic material for accelerating the proliferation and regeneration of damaged tissue, and suppressing the inflammatory conditions, degenerative diseases, and autoimmune diseases such as diabetic ulcers with complications and esthetic problems of facial skin (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of optimizing soluble molecule from mesenchymal stem cell metabolites with a 5% hypoxic room technique, involves: inserting the mesenchymal stem cells from isolation and culture into a flask, and observing with an inverted microscope to ensure the cell growth in a good condition; obtaining pasasse-4 (derivative-4) and mesenchymal stem cell-like substance in 80% confluent, and then transferring into a hypoxic room in the form of a chamber with pads filled with sodium chloride liquid; placing oxygenometer in the chamber to measure oxygen levels; passing the carbon dioxide gas into the chamber while observing the oxygenometer panel until it reaches a concentration of oxygen level in the 5% hypoxia chamber and becomes stable; closing the hypoxic chamber which contains the mesenchymal stem cells by closing the carbon dioxide gas flow and closing the entire hole in the hypoxic chamber; ensuring no leaks to maintain the oxygen level in the chamber, which is stable at 5% concentration; incubating the hypoxic chamber in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C to obtain optimal soluble molecule with mesenchymal stem cell metabolites and incubating for 24 hours; removing the chamber from the incubator to retrieve the soluble molecules after 24 hours incubation; harvesting the soluble molecules in biosafety type II cabinets using a micropipette and inserting into a sterile conical tube to maintain sterile conditions; centrifuging the conical tubes contains soluble molecules at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-15 minutes to separate the remaining debris/pellet from soluble molecules; and filtrating with a membrane syringe measuring 0.2 pm to filter other microbes in order to obtain a sterile soluble molecule, which can be used as a therapeutic material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a soluble molecule mesenchymal stem cell metabolites obtained by hypoxic technique mentioned above, comprising higher soluble molecular concentrations than technique without hypoxic or tumor necrosis factor- alpha induction technique.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The soluble molecule hypoxic mesenchymal stem cells content is in the form of molecule growth factors such as vascular endothelial growth factor and platelet-derived growth factor, and antiinflammatory molecules such as interleukin-6, interleukin-8, and transforming growth factor- beta .
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary; Antiinflammatory; Neuroprotective; Dermatological; Immunosuppressive; Antiulcer. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating Agaricus bisporus, comprises e.g.                placing Agaricus bisporus culture medium on the                mushroom bed in mushroom house in amount of 100                kg/m2, controlling the temperature of the medium,                inoculating Agaricus bisporus on the medium                material, controlling temperature of the culture                medium to 26-28 degrees C, controlling relative                humidity of the air in the mushroom room at 85-90%,                controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in the                air in the mushroom room at 4000-5000 ppm, and                maintaining for 12-14 days, controlling room                temperature in the mushroom room at 23-27 degrees                C, relative humidity of the air in the mushroom                room at 95%, controlling concentration of carbon                dioxide in the air in the mushroom room at above                8000 ppm, and maintaining for 2-4 days under this                condition, covering soil, fruiting, and picking                mushroom.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating Agaricus                bisporus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short cultivation period and                high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating Agaricus bisporus, comprises (1)                placing Agaricus bisporus culture medium on the                mushroom bed in mushroom house in amount of 100                kg/m2, and controlling the temperature of the                medium at 25-27 degrees C, (2) inoculating Agaricus                bisporus on the medium material by 1 l/m2, where                80% of the strains are uniformly mixed with the                medium material, and make 20% of the strains                uniformly distributed on the surface of the culture                medium, (3) controlling temperature of the culture                medium to 26-28 degrees C, controlling relative                humidity of the air in the mushroom room at 85-90%,                controlling concentration of carbon dioxide in the                air in the mushroom room at 4000-5000 ppm, and                maintaining for 12-14 days, controlling room                temperature in the mushroom room at 23-27 degrees                C, controlling relative humidity of the air in the                mushroom room is above 95%, controlling                concentration of carbon dioxide in the air in the                mushroom room at above 8000 ppm, and maintaining                for 2-4 days under this condition, (4) covering the                soil material with water and watering, the amount                of water to be watered is 1 l/1m2, controlling the                temperature of the medium material at 24-26 degrees                C, and relative humidity of the air in the mushroom                house at 95-100%, controlling concentration of                carbon dioxide in the air in the mushroom room                above 9000 ppm, and maintaining it for 8-10 days,                (5) watering on the culture medium, the amount of                water to be watered is 0.7-0.8 l/1m2, controlling                room temperature in the mushroom room at 18-20                degrees C, and the relative humidity of the air in                the mushroom room at 85-90%, controlling                concentration of carbon dioxide in the air in the                mushroom room at 1400-1600 ppm, and maintaining it                for 3-4 days, and (6) controlling room temperature                in the mushroom room at 16-18 degrees C, relative                humidity of the air in the mushroom room at 80-85%,                the concentration of carbon dioxide in the air in                the mushroom house at 800-1000 ppm, and collecting                the mushroom under these conditions to obtain an                edible finished Agaricus bisporus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (1), placing                  the substrate on the mushroom bed of the mushroom                  house and placing the Agaricus bisporus culture                  medium at rate of 100 kg/m2 and controlling the                  temperature of the culture medium at 26 degrees                  C. In the step (3), controlling the temperature                  of the control medium at 27 degrees C, the                  relative humidity of the air in the mushroom                  house at 88%, and the concentration of carbon                  dioxide in the air in the mushroom house at 4500                  ppm, and the condition is maintained for 13 days,                  controlling the room temperature in the mushroom                  room at 25 degrees C, controlling relative                  humidity of the air in the mushroom room is above                  95%, controlling concentration of carbon dioxide                  in the air in the mushroom room above 8000 ppm,                  and and maintain this condition for 3 days. In                  the step (4), covering soil and watering on the                  culture medium, the amount of water to be watered                  is 1 l/1m2, and the temperature of the control                  medium material is 25 degrees C, controlling the                  relative humidity of the air in the mushroom                  house at 98%, concentration of carbon dioxide in                  the air in the mushroom house at 9000 ppm, and                  maintained this condition for 9 days. In the step                  (5), the mushroom is ground and watered on the                  culture medium, the amount of water to be watered                  is 0.75 l/1m2, and the room temperature in the                  mushroom room is controlled to be 19 degrees C,                  controlling the relative humidity of the air in                  the mushroom room at 88%, and concentration of                  carbon dioxide in the air in the mushroom room at                  1500 ppm, and was maintained under this condition                  for 4 days. In the step (6), picking mushrooms,                  controlling the room temperature in the mushroom                  house at 17 degrees C, the relative humidity of                  the air in the mushroom house was at 83%, and the                  concentration of carbon dioxide in the air in the                  mushroom room at 900 ppm, under this condition,                  the mushroom was collected to obtain an edible                  finished Agaricus bisporus.

   WANG J

   GAO S
AE HUANENG POWER INT INC (CHHN-C)

GA 201967393E
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid-liquid phase change absorbent comprises 20-60 wt.% co-extracting agent, 10-50 wt.% organic amine, 0-5 wt.% auxiliary agent and the rest is water.
   USE - Liquid-liquid phase change absorbent for carbon dioxide capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid-liquid phase change absorbent can automatically stratify into liquid-liquid two phase after the reactor is loaded with a certain amount of carbon dioxide without additional energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using liquid-liquid phase change absorbent for carbon dioxide capture, which involves making a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to enter the absorption tower, fully counter-contacting with a liquid-liquid phase change absorber for carbon dioxide capture from the top of the absorption tower to form a carbon dioxide rich liquid, redistributing of carbon dioxide in the phase separation clarification tank, where the liquid-liquid two phases which are immiscible are automatically formed, that is, the rich phase layer enriched in carbon dioxide and the poor phase layer which is hardly loaded with carbon dioxide is returned to the solution storage tank, and the rich phase layer enriched in carbon dioxide and the hot lean liquid from the regeneration tower are sufficiently heat exchanged and then enter the regeneration tower for heating and desorption, regenerating into a lean liquid for producing high purity carbon dioxide gas, making the regenerated hot lean liquid to enter the solution storage tank after heat exchange, cooling, mixing with carbon dioxide-free phase-poor layer solution to form a carbon dioxide absorption solution for carbon dioxide capture in the absorption tower to form absorption-phase separation-regeneration-absorption cycle.

The organic amine is one or mixture of primary amines, secondary amines, or cyclicamines. The auxiliary agent is selected from antifoaming agent, antioxidant and corrosion inhibitor. The organic amine is selected from diethanolamine, N-methylethanolamine, ethylenediamine, N-ethylethylenediamine, N-(2-hydroxyethyl)ethylenediamine, 1,4-butanediamine, diisopropanolamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, piperazine, N-methylpiperazine and morpholine. The defoaming agent is organic siloxane or fatty alcohol. The antioxidant is potassium tartrate, sodium tartrate, antimony potassium tartrate or sodium thiosulfate. The corrosion inhibitor is chromate, nitrate or ammonium molybdate. Preferred Conditions: The absorption temperature is 30-50 degrees C, absorption pressure is 0.1-5 MPa and regeneration temperature is 80-160 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN113106138-A
TI Extracting and separating antitumor active proteins from shiitake mushrooms useful in preparation of antitumor or antitumor cell product, involves pretreating shiitake mushrooms, performing compound enzymatic hydrolysis, extracting active protein and separating active protein.
AU XIN F
   LIU Y
   LI Z
   WEN B
   WANG Y
   GU T
AE CAAS FOOD SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (CAAS-Non-standard)
GA 2021818468
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method for extracting and separating antitumor active proteins from shiitake mushrooms involves (a) pretreating by cleaning fruiting portion of shiitake mushrooms, absorbing surface moisture with paper towels, quickly freezing in liquid nitrogen, grinding into powder and passing through a 50-200 mesh sieve, (b) performing compound enzymatic hydrolysis, (c) extracting active protein by adding the above enzymatic hydrolysis solution to a supercritical extraction kettle equipped with a stirring and heating device, sealing the top of the lid, turning on the stirrer, releasing the supercritical portion of water and carbon dioxide with active substances in the extraction kettle to the separation kettle for separation, slowly reducing pressure and temperature, transforming the supercritical fluid into carbon dioxide gas and a water layer containing active substances, collecting water layer and recycling carbon dioxide gas, and (d) separating the active protein.
   USE - The preparation method is useful for extracting and separating antitumor active proteins from shiitake mushrooms which is used in preparation of antitumor or antitumor cell product. The tumor cell is a human lung adenocarcinoma cell (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts an activity tracking separation method to improve the purpose of separating the antitumor active protein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method for extracting and separating antitumor active proteins from shiitake mushrooms involves (a) pretreating by cleaning fruiting portion of shiitake mushrooms, absorbing surface moisture with paper towels, quickly freezing in liquid nitrogen, grinding into powder and passing through a 50-200 mesh sieve, (b) performing compound enzymatic hydrolysis by immersing the mushroom powder in normal saline, adding cellulase, hemicellulase and protease inhibitors and performing compound enzymatic hydrolysis under stirring condition at 4 degrees C for 12-16 hours, (c) extracting active protein by adding the above enzymatic hydrolysis solution to a supercritical extraction kettle equipped with a stirring and heating device, sealing the top of the lid, turning on the stirrer, transporting carbon dioxide gas, by adjusting the drain valve, slowly releasing the supercritical portion of water and carbon dioxide with active substances in the extraction kettle to the separation kettle for separation, slowly reducing the pressure and temperature in the separation kettle, transforming the supercritical fluid into carbon dioxide gas and a water layer containing active substances, collecting water layer and recycling carbon dioxide gas, and (d) separating the active protein by mixing the water layer containing the active substance with an equal volume of 20-100 mM, pH 7-8 tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane hydrochloride (Tris-HCl) buffer, loading the sample on a Source (RTM: Polymeric, strong anion exchanger) Q anion exchange chromatography column, performing gradient elution of the active protein of shiitake mushroom, dividing into different components with respect to the absorbance at UV 280 nm, using A549 cells to detect the antitumor activity of each component, using Superdex (RTM: Column for high resolution gel filtration in small scale preparative purification) 200 gel filtration chromatography to further separate the most active components, with respect to the peak position, dividing into high molecular weight component and low molecular weight component, detecting activity by A549 cells, obtaining protein extract with high antitumor activity, freeze-drying and preserving product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (b), the diameter of the mushroom powder is less than 0.1 mm, the material-liquid mass ratio of the mushroom powder to the physiological saline is 1:10, the amount of cellulase is 30-50 IU/g, the amount of hemicellulase is 100-130 IU/g and the compound type protease inhibitor is Roche protease inhibitor cocktail, and the dosage is 1 g/l. In step (c), the temperature and pressure of the extraction kettle is controlled at 35-40 degrees C and 55-65 MPa, respectively, the stirring speed is 5-15 m/second, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is adjusted to 20-30 l/hour and the delivery time is 1-3 hours. In step (c), a cooling device is added to the separation kettle, the temperature of the separation kettle is slowly reduced to 0 degrees C, the pressure of the separation kettle is slowly reduced to 4-5.5 MPa and the carbon dioxide under low temperature conditions is removed to maintain protein viability. The recycled is removed and fresh is removed in step (c) pass through the heat exchanger, filter, and compressor in turn into the extraction kettle for gas supply. The Source (RTM: Polymeric, strong anion exchanger) Q anion exchange chromatography in step (d) uses a gradient of 10-50 mM, pH 7-8 Tris-HCl buffer containing 0-1 M sodium chloride to elute the active protein, with an elution volume of 10-15 column volumes to separate the active protein. The polysaccharide components in the active protein are all adhered to the Source (RTM: Polymeric, strong anion exchanger) Q anion exchange chromatography, to achieve the effect of removing polysaccharides. In step (d), the buffer used in Superdex (RTM: Column for high resolution gel filtration in small scale preparative purification) 200 gel filtration chromatography is phosphate buffer with pH 7.4.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN114547851-A
TI Detection method of indoor carbon dioxide solubility used for evaluating air quality in room of building, involves identifying and obtaining indoor carbon dioxide release rate, and identifying and calculating indoor natural ventilation quantity.
AU LIANG S
   JI Y
   HU S
   ZHENG J
AE UNIV QINGDAO TECHNOLOGY (UQDT-C)
GA 202275036A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying and obtaining indoor carbon dioxide release rate. The carbon dioxide balance diffusion model is combined based on the carbon dioxide released rate and indoor ventilation quantity. The indoor carbon dioxide solubility is calculated to obtain the indoor solubility rate. An indoor ventilation volume is divided into indoor mechanical ventilation volume and indoor natural ventilation volume. The obtained carbon dioxide is used as the known amount to identify and calculate the indoor natural ventilation volume.
   USE - Detection method of indoor carbon dioxide solubility used for evaluating an air quality in a room of a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully considers a breathing amount and a frequency of indoor personnel, and accurately obtains the indoor carbon dioxide release rate and ventilation quantity parameter under the natural ventilation condition. The change rule of the carbon dioxide solubility can be simulated in the room based on the established carbon dioxide diffusion model, thus reducing consumption of manpower and material resources, improving efficiency, and ensuring accuracy of the detection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an indoor carbon dioxide solubility detection system;
   (2) a non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing a program for detecting indoor carbon dioxide solubility;
   (3) a computer program product for detecting indoor carbon dioxide solubility; and
   (4) a electronic equipment
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the detection method of indoorcarbon dioxide solubility. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

An interior of a flux testing cavity (2) is provided with two layers of a carbon dioxide adsorption filler (6) in parallel spaced from top to bottom, where a flux testing cavity is cylindrical. PN DE102017005680-A1; WO2018228716-A1; CA3065571-A1; CN110770369-A; IN201917049175-A; EP3638827-A1; US2020131647-A1; JP2020524212-W
TI Producing a gas product containing carbon            monoxide, comprises subjecting carbon dioxide to            electrolysis to obtain raw gas containing carbon            monoxide and carbon dioxide, partially or completely            subjecting raw gas to obtain retentate mixture.
AU HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHEL A
   FRANZ A
   LEITMAYR W
   DELHOMME-NEUDECKER C
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2018A1485V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a gas product containing carbon                monoxide, comprises subjecting carbon dioxide to                electrolysis to obtain a raw gas containing carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide, and in which the gas                contained carbon dioxide is partially or completely                returned to the electrolysis, partially or                completely subjecting the raw gas to obtain a                retentate mixture and compared to the raw gas                carbon dioxide enriched permeate to a membrane                separation, and the retentate mixture, obtaining                the carbon dioxide-enriched and carbon                dioxide-depleted gas product compared with the                retentate mixture, and partially or completely                subjecting to adsorption opposite to the retentate                mixture of carbon monoxide depleted and carbon                dioxide-enriched residual mixture.
   USE - The method producing a gas product containing                at least carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method partially or completely recycles                raw gas and improves the yield of a gas                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant for producing a gas product containing at                least carbon monoxide, comprising an electrolysis                unit adapted to subject at least carbon dioxide to                an electrolysis containing raw gas containing at                least carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and means                arranged are the carbon dioxide contained in the                raw gas partially or completely due to the                electrolysis, and means adapted to partially or                completely subject the raw gas to a membrane                separation to obtain a retentate mixture and a                permeate mixture enriched in carbon dioxide with                respect to the raw gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The permeate mixture                  and/or the residual mixture are recycled                  partially or completely in the form of one or                  more recycle streams to the electrolysis. The                  electrolysis is carried out at a pressure level                  which corresponds to a pressure level at which                  the raw gas is supplied to the membrane                  separation, where the recycle stream or streams                  are compressed to the pressure level of the                  electrolysis using compressors. The electrolysis                  is carried out at a pressure level lower than a                  pressure level at which the raw gas is supplied                  to the membrane separation, where the raw gas is                  compressed to the pressure level of the membrane                  separation using compressors. The raw gas                  contains hydrogen, and in which the membrane                  separation is carried out such that the retentate                  mixture is depleted of hydrogen compared with the                  raw gas and the permeate mixture is opposite to                  the raw gas enriched in hydrogen. At least a                  portion of the hydrogen contained in the permeate                  mixture is discharged from the process. At least                  part or all of the recycle streams are subjected                  to hydrogen removal, in the form of catalytic                  and/or non-catalytic oxidation, and a remainder                  remaining after hydrogen removal is returned                  partially or completely to the electrolysis. A                  first portion of the permeate mixture and/or of                  the residual mixture in the form of or the                  recycle streams combined with the raw gas and the                  membrane separation is subjected, and in which a                  second portion of permeate and/or the remainder                  of the mixture with a fresh feed combined and                  returned to the electrolysis. The membrane                  separation comprises at least two membrane                  separation steps, where the permeate mixture                  comprises in each case in the two membrane                  separation steps formed permeate. The permeate                  mixture and the residual mixture are each formed                  at a pressure level of 1-10 bar. Carbon monoxide                  or a carbon monoxide-rich gas mixture is formed                  as the gas product, where the gas product 90-100%                  carbon monoxide. The synthesis gas is formed as                  the gas product, where the gas product in total                  90-100% carbon monoxide and hydrogen and where a                  ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the gas                  product is 1-4 and/or the gas product is a                  stoichiometric number from 0.8-2.1. The                  electrolysis is carried out in the form of a                  high-temperature electrolysis using one or more                  solid oxide electrolysis cells and/or a                  low-temperature co-electrolysis on a liquid                  electrolyte.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107686724-A; CN107686724-B
TI Very low water containing carbon dioxide            fracturing fluid useful for e.g. improving tight gas            and shale gas reservoir, comprises e.g. hydrophobic            group-containing anionic polymer, gaseous carbon            dioxide or nitrogen and liquid carbon dioxide.
AU CUI W
   SHU Y
   LU Y
   CUI M
   YAO F
   QIU X
   LIANG C
   ZHANG H
   YAN J
   ZHU D
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201814973Q
AB    NOVELTY - Very low water containing carbon dioxide                fracturing fluid comprises 3-10 wt.% water,                0.01-0.05 wt.% hydrophobic group-containing anionic                polymer, 30-50 wt.% gaseous carbon dioxide or                nitrogen and liquid carbon dioxide (balance                amount).
   USE - The carbon dioxide fracturing fluid is useful                for improving tight gas and shale gas reservoir                e.g. dense oil, and large volume reconstruction of                unconventional gas reservoir (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide fracturing fluid solves                problem of water-free fracturing fluid which is                difficult to carry thickened sand and technical                problem of large amount of water consumption in                reconstruction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing very low water containing carbon                dioxide fracturing fluid comprising (i) adding                hydrophobic group-containing anionic polymer in                water, stirring and swelling to obtain polymer                solution, (ii) mixing gaseous carbon dioxide or                nitrogen gas with polymer solution to obtain carbon                dioxide foam, and (iii) injecting carbon foam in a                container at 0-20 degrees C and sealing under                pressure of 4-5 MPa, then adding liquid carbon                dioxide into the container, and stirring for 0.5-10                minutes to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of carbon dioxide fracturing fluid for                improving tight gas and shale gas reservoir e.g.                dense oil, and large volume reconstruction of                unconventional gas reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide foam is                  prepared by mixing gaseous carbon dioxide or                  nitrogen gas and polymer solution through a foam                  generator. In step (ii), the carbon dioxide foam                  is injected into a closed container at 20 degrees                  C. The use method comprises reservoir                  reconstruction includes adding hydrophobic                  group-containing anionic polymer in water to                  obtain polymer solution, then mixing polymer                  solution with gaseous carbon dioxide or nitrogen                  gas through the foam generator, braking and                  mixing with supporting agent into a well, and                  then modifying the reservoir with liquid carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic                  group-containing anionic polymer is partially                  hydrolyzed polyacrylamide, the hydrophobic group                  is 12-22C alkyl hydrophobic groups and has                  molecular weight of 10-500, and the anionic group                  is carboxylic acid or sulfonic acid. The use                  method comprises ratio of supporting agent to                  sand is 2-25:1.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN US2021170331-A1; AU2020281119-A1; JP2021090904-A; CN113019069-A; GB2592715-A; AU2020281119-B2
TI Carbon dioxide capturing system used for e.g.            thermal power plant, comprises absorber to bring            process target gas absorbing liquid into contact with            each other, regenerator to release carbon dioxide, and            cleaner to receive condensed water.
AU KITAMURA H
   HANDA Y
   UDATSU M
   UTATSU M
AE TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 2021630367
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide capturing system (S1)                comprises an absorber (2) to bring a process target                gas containing carbon dioxide and an absorbing                liquid into contact with each other, and to                discharge the absorbing liquid having absorbed the                carbon dioxide and an absorber discharge gas                containing the process target gas from which the                carbon dioxide has been removed, a regenerator (5)                to release the carbon dioxide from the absorbing                liquid having been discharged from the absorber,                and to discharge the absorbing liquid having                released the carbon dioxide and a regenerator                discharge gas containing the carbon dioxide, a                cooler to cool the regenerator discharge gas, and a                cleaner (8) to receive condensed water generated                from the regenerator discharge gas cooled by the                cooler and a gaseous cooled regenerator discharge                gas, and to clean the cooled regenerator discharge                gas by a cleaning liquid.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capturing system used for                thermal power plant, steel mill, trash burning                site, and manufacturing facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide capturing system is capable                of effectively cleaning a gas, and has reduced                manufacturing cost with high manufacturing                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide capturing system comprises an                absorber to bring a process target gas containing                carbon dioxide and an absorbing liquid into contact                with each other, and to discharge the absorbing                liquid having absorbed the carbon dioxide and an                absorber discharge gas containing the process                target gas from which the carbon dioxide has been                removed, a regenerator to release the carbon                dioxide from the absorbing liquid having been                discharged from the absorber, and to discharge the                absorbing liquid having released the carbon dioxide                and a regenerator discharge gas containing the                carbon dioxide, a cooler to cool the regenerator                discharge gas, and a cleaner to receive condensed                water generated from the regenerator discharge gas                cooled by the cooler and a gaseous cooled                regenerator discharge gas, and to clean the cooled                regenerator discharge gas by a cleaning liquid,                where the cleaner comprises a liquid reservoir (R1)                to store the condensed water, and a liquid                reservoir (R2) to store the cleaning liquid having                cleaned the cooled regenerator discharge gas.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for operation                of a carbon dioxide capturing system, which                involves cooling the regenerator discharge gas to                separate the regenerator discharge gas into                condensed water and a gaseous cooled regenerator                discharge gas, storing the condensed water in a                liquid reservoir (R1), cleaning the cooled                regenerator discharge gas by a cleaning liquid, and                storing, in a liquid reservoir (R2), the cleaning                liquid having cleaned the cooled regenerator                discharge gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon                dioxide capturing system.
   Carbon dioxide capturing system (S1)
   Process exhaust gas line (1)
   Absorber (2)
   Regenerator (5)
   Cleaner (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Apparatus: The liquid reservoir                  (R2) is located on a downstream side of the                  liquid reservoir (R1). The liquid reservoir (R1)                  and (R2) are partitioned by a weir. The carbon                  dioxide capturing system comprises a one-way                  mechanism that allows the cleaning liquid to flow                  from the liquid reservoir (R2) into the liquid                  reservoir (R1), while limits flowing of the                  condensed water from the liquid reservoir (R1)                  into the liquid reservoir (R2). The one-way                  mechanism is constituted by a discharge structure                  configured to discharge the condensed water                  stored in the liquid reservoir (R1) to the                  outside of the liquid reservoir (R1), when a                  liquid level of the condensed water stored in the                  liquid reservoir (R1), which is detected by a                  condensed-water level gauge, reaches a                  predetermined value or more. The discharge                  structure discharges the condensed water at least                  any one of the absorber, the regenerator and a                  process flow path through which the absorbing                  liquid flows between the absorber and the                  regenerator. The liquid reservoir (R2) is located                  above the liquid reservoir (R1). The cleaning                  liquid is circulated such that, after the cooled                  regenerator discharge gas has been cleaned above                  the liquid reservoir (R2) by the cleaning liquid                  having been discharged from the liquid reservoir                  (R2) to the outside, the cleaning liquid having                  cleaned the cooled regenerator discharge gas is                  stored in the liquid reservoir (R2). The                  circulation path of the cleaning liquid is                  replenished with makeup water, when a liquid                  level of the cleaning liquid stored in the liquid                  reservoir (R2), which is detected by a                  cleaning-liquid level gauge, reaches a                  predetermined value or less.

PN CN114015345-A
TI Water-curing system anti-sagging polyurethane waterproof coating comprises polyether resin, plasticizer, dispersant, inorganic filler, anti-settling agent, carbon dioxide absorbent, curing agent, catalyst, and solvent, and ensures enhanced anti-sagging effect under low viscosity.
AU XUE W
   SONG S
   XU W
AE SHANGHAI SKSHU WATERPROOF TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022285864
AB    NOVELTY - A water-curing system anti-sagging polyurethane waterproof coating comprises 20-28 pts. wt. polyether resin, 16-24 pts. wt. plasticizer, 0.2-0.3 pt. wt. dispersant, 35-40 pts. wt. inorganic filler, 0.2-0.8 pt. wt. anti-settling agent, 4-8 pts. wt. carbon dioxide absorbent, 6.2-7 pts. wt. curing agent, 0.05-0.1 pt. wt. catalyst, and 8-12 pts. wt. solvent, where the curing agent is at least one or more of 80% 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, 100% 4-toluene diisocyanate, 50% 2,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate and isophorone diisocyanates.
   USE - Water-curing system anti-sagging polyurethane waterproof coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating ensures enhanced anti-sagging effect under low viscosity, and due to the combination of polyether resin, carbon dioxide absorbent and curing agent, water is added only to accelerate the interaction between the chemical bonds in the coating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation method of water-curing system anti-sagging polyurethane waterproof coating, which involves (S1) mixing polyether resin, plasticizer and dispersant at 150-250 rpm, heating at 83-87degreesC, adding inorganic filler and anti-settling agent and stirring at 450-550 rpm for 8-11 minutes, heating at 105-115degreesC to obtain mixed material, (S2) vacuum dehydrating the obtained mixed material at 105-115degreesC until the water content of mixed material is less than 0.5%, (S3) cooling the dehydrated mixed material to 75-83degreesC, adding carbon dioxide absorbent and stirring uniformly, and adding curing agent, and reacting for 2-3 hours at 75-83degreesC to obtain reaction product, and (S4) cooling the reaction product to 58-62degreesC, adding catalyst and solvent, stirring for 10-20 minutes, and vacuuming for more than 30 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyether resin resin is at least one or more of polyether 2000D, polyether 1000D, polyether 330N and polyether M500. The plasticizer is at least one or more of dioctyl terephthalate, chlorinated paraffin, dioctyl adipate and dioctyl phthalate. The dispersant is at least one or more of branched polycarboxylic acid, linear alkyl amide, graft acrylic acid copolymer or long-chain polyurethane dispersant. The anti-settling agent is at least one or more of bentonite, magnesium aluminum silicate, polyurea rheological agent and polyamide wax.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent is at least one or more of magnesium oxide and calcium oxide. The catalyst is at least one or more of organic bismuth, organic tin and organic bismuth.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is at least one or more of No.150 solvent oil, No.120 solvent oil, No.100 solvent oil, No.200 solvent oil or alkane solvent.

PT P
PN CN113044841-A
TI Method for secondary utilization of carbon monoxide emitted during coking process, involves passing waste gas into gas separation layer, and separating carbon monoxide in mixed gas based on adsorbability of carbon monoxide to obtain carbon monoxide by carbon monoxide adsorption layer.
AU JIANG H
   GUO Y
AE SUZHOU WUYUN MINGTAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202178668F
AB    NOVELTY - The method (200) involves determining (201) the carbon monoxide emissions of the coking enterprise. The carbon monoxide secondary utilization devices are installed (202) at the exhaust gas outlets of the coking enterprises, according to the determined carbon monoxide emissions of the coking enterprises. The waste gas discharged by the coking enterprise is passed (203) into the gas separation layer. The remaining gases include carbon dioxide, hydrogen, nitrogen and methane. The adsorption element depressurizes and evacuates the carbon monoxide and portion of the remaining gas to obtain a mixed gas of the depressurized carbon monoxide. The carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is separated and removed based on the adsorbability of carbon dioxide to obtain a second mixed gas by the carbon dioxide adsorption layer. The carbon monoxide in the second mixed gas is separated based on the adsorbability of carbon monoxide to obtain separated carbon monoxide by the carbon monoxide adsorption layer.
   USE - Method for secondary utilization of carbon monoxide emitted during coking process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the determination and utilization of the carbon monoxide emission in the target area and improves the resource utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electronic device for secondary utilization of carbon monoxide emitted during coking process; and
   (2) a computer-readable medium storing program for secondary utilization of carbon monoxide emitted during coking process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for secondary utilization of carbon monoxide emitted during coking process. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Method for secondary utilization of carbon monoxide emitted during coking process (200)
   Step for determining carbon monoxide emissions of coking enterprise (201)
   Step for installing carbon monoxide secondary utilization devices at exhaust gas outlets of coking enterprises, according to the determined carbon monoxide emissions of coking enterprises (202)
   Step for passing waste gas discharged by coking enterprise into gas separation layer (203)

TI Device useful for reducing greenhouse gas emissions in ship combined with exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) and intelligent control by exhaust recycling (iCER), comprises e.g. exhaust gas receiver for temporarily storing exhaust gas.
AU NAMBYUNGTAK
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 202130872G
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises an exhaust gas receiver for temporarily storing exhaust gas exhausted from each cylinder of a ship engine (10) to remove pulsation; a supercharger (120) that compresses and supplies combustion air by the exhaust gas supplied from the exhaust gas receiver; a cleaning unit (130) for injecting cleaning water into the exhaust gas supplied through supercharger to remove sulfur oxide (SOx) and chute for cleaning, and circulating the cooling water to cool the exhaust gas; a carbon-dioxide absorption unit configured to absorb and remove carbon-dioxide by injecting an absorption liquid into the exhaust gas passing through the cleaning unit; an absorbent liquid manufacturing unit for preparing a high-concentration carbon-dioxide absorbing liquid and supplying it to carbon-dioxide absorbing unit; and an absorbent liquid regeneration unit (160) for regenerating absorbent liquid and ammonia by reacting aqueous ammonium salt solution with aqueous divalent metal hydroxide solution.
   USE - The device is useful for reducing greenhouse gas emissions in ship combined with exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) and intelligent control by exhaust recycling (iCER) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device absorbs and converts greenhouse gas emission into a material that does not affect the environment to prevent corrosion of the engine and improves combustion quality, increase engine efficiency by iCER, reduces methane slip, recycles and reuse sthe absorbent, reduces the absorption performance of the absorbent, and prevents the loss of absorption liquid due to the spontaneous evaporation of the high-concentration absorption liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for ship equipped with the device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of device for reducing greenhouse gas emissions in ship combined with exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) and intelligent control by exhaust recycling (iCER) (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   Supercharger (120)
   Cleaning unit (130)
   Absorbent liquid regeneration unit (160)

TI Removing acid gases from fluid stream involves            contacting fluid stream with absorbent comprising            diluent and amine alcohol compounds to form treated            fluid stream and laden absorbent.
AU HOLCOMBE T W
   INGRAM T
   PANCHENKO A
   ERNST M
   SIEDER G
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2021268833
AB    NOVELTY - Removing acid gases from a fluid stream                comprises contacting the fluid stream with an                absorbent to obtain a treated fluid stream and a                laden absorbent, where the absorbent comprises at                least one diluent and amine alcohol compounds                (I).
   USE - The method is useful for removing acid gases                from a fluid stream, preferably for selective                removal of hydrogen sulfide from a fluid stream                comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide (all                claimed), where the acid gases comprises carbon                dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, carbon                disulfide, hydrogen cyanide, carbon oxy sulfide or                mercaptans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: involves the hydroxy groups that                can reduce the volatility of the amine and,                consequently, reduces amine losses during                operation; involves the absorbents which exhibits a                high cyclic capacity and a low volatility; provides                a gas treating process based on solvents with an                enhanced thermal stability which can be operated a                higher temperatures over longer period of times;                provides a gas treating process with a high                selectivity for the removal of hydrogen sulfide                from gaseous mixtures comprising both hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide which also allows for                the removal of other sulfur components which may be                additionally comprised in gaseous mixtures                comprising both hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide; and allows for the removal of mercaptans                in such a selective gas treatment process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing acid gases from a fluid stream                comprises contacting the fluid stream with an                absorbent to obtain a treated fluid stream and a                laden absorbent, where the absorbent comprises at                least one diluent and amine alcohol compounds of                formula (R2-C(R1)(R2)-N-(CH2CH2-S-)nCH2CH2-O-R4)                (I).
   R1 = 1-3C-alkyl;
   R2 = 1-3C-alkyl;
   R3 = H or 1-3C-alkyl;
   R4 = H or 1-3C-alkyl; and
   n = 1-4.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing (I) by converting an amine of formula                (R1-C(R2)(R3)-NH2) (II) with an alcohol of formula                (HO-(CH2CH2-S-)n-CH2CH2-O-R4) (III) in the liquid                phase and in the presence of a catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: (I) is                  2-(2-(tert-butylamino)ethylsulfanyl)ethanol. The                  diluent comprises water. The absorbent                  additionally comprises an acid. The diluent                  comprises a non-aqueous organic solvent. The                  organic solvent comprises 4-10C alcohols,                  ketones, esters, lactones, amides, lactams,                  sulfones, sulfoxides, glycols, poly- alkylene                  glycols, di- or mono(1-4C-alkyl ether) glycols,                  di- or mono(1-4C-alkyl ether) poly-alkylene                  glycols, cyclic ureas and/or thioalkanols. The                  absorbent comprises at least one activator                  comprising sterically unhindered primary amine                  and/or a sterically unhindered secondary amine.                  The activator is piperazine. The laden absorbent                  is regenerated by means of at least one of the                  measures of heating, decompressing and stripping                  with an inert fluid. The use of the absorbent is                  for removal of acid gases from a fluid stream or                  the use of absorbent is for selective removal of                  hydrogen sulfide from a fluid stream comprising                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Heterogeneous catalyst for decomposition of formic acid into hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide, comprises an organo-ruthenium complex comprising an amino or imino group, where a nitrogen atom of the amino or imino group is immobilized.
AU WERGHI B
   BASSET J
   HUANG K
   PUMP E
   BENDJERIOUSEDJERARI A
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 202057239P
AB    NOVELTY - Heterogeneous catalyst comprises an organo-ruthenium complex comprising an amino or imino group, where a nitrogen atom of the amino or imino group is immobilized to a tetra-coordinated aluminum atom grafted to a surface of an inorganic oxide.
   USE - Heterogeneous catalyst for decomposition of formic acid into hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The heterogeneous catalyst has the formula (M-O-)2(X)Al-(N(RR'R'')Rum), where M is a metal of an inorganic oxide support, and X is a hydride or halide and R and R' are independently selected from a hydrogen atom or a substituted or unsubstituted alkyl, heteroalkyl, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl, and heteroaryl group, R'' selected from a substituted or unsubstituted alkyl, heteroalkyl, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, aryl, and heteroaryl group and m is 1 or 2. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for making a heterogeneous catalyst for decomposition of formic acid into hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide, which involves providing an inorganic oxide support comprising at least one bipodal and tetrahedral aluminum atom on an accessible surface of the inorganic oxide, immobilizing an organo-ruthenium complex comprising an accessible amino or imino nitrogen atom by forming a chemical bond between the nitrogen atom and the bipodal and tetrahedral aluminum atom and heating the immobilized organo-ruthenium complex at a temperature sufficient to stabilize the chemical bond; and
   (2) a method for generating electricity, which involves contacting formic acid, formate or a mixture with a heterogeneous catalyst to form hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide, where heterogeneous catalyst comprises an organo-ruthenium complex comprising an amino or imino group, where a nitrogen atom of the amino or imino group is immobilized to a tetra-coordinated aluminum atom grafted to a surface of an inorganic oxide, delivering the hydrogen gas to a fuel cell and oxidizing the hydrogen gas in the fuel cell to generate electricity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic oxide support is a porous material selected from the group consisting of silica, alumina, silica-alumina, a metallic surface, a metal-organic framework and a zeolite. The inorganic oxide support is a fibrous silica nanosphere. The organo-ruthenium complex is a ruthenium PN3 pincer-type complex having the structure of formula (I).R1 and R2=each independently, a substituted or unsubstituted, alkyl(c not less than 12), aryl(c not less than 12), or aralkyl(c not less than 2) group;R3 and R4=if present each independently, a substituted or unsubstituted, alkyl(c not less than 12), aryl(c not less than 12), or aralkyl(c not less than 2) group;R5=substituted or unsubstituted, alkyl(c not less than 12), aryl(c not less than 12), or aralkyl(c not less than 2) group;Z=CR6, N or P;R6=hydrogen atom or, a substituted or unsubstituted;T=N, NR7, C, or CR8;R7 and R8=each independently a hydrogen, or, a substituted or unsubstituted, substituted or unsubstituted, alkyl(c not less than 12), aryl(c not less than 12), or aralkyl(c not less than 2) group;L=anionic ligand or a neutral ligand;n=0, 1, or 2;Q=P or N and optionally where and Q, together, form a 5 or 6 membered heterocyclic ring; where the heterocyclic ring can optionally be substituted with one or more heteroatoms and or one or more sites of the heterocyclic ring are substituted with one or more, substituted or unsubstituted alkyl(c not less than 12), aryl(c not less than 12), or aralkyl(c not less than 2) group, where heterocyclic ring can have a fused ring attached thereto, provided when T and Q form a 5 or 6 membered heterocyclic ring, one or both of R3 and/or R4 are not present and designates a single bond or a double bond;R1 to R6=independently selected from hydrogen, substituted or unsubstituted alkyl, and substituted or unsubstituted aryl;X1=a halide, a hydride, or a formate ion;L=a neutral ligand or an anionic ligand; andn=0, 1 or 2.Preferred Components: The organo-ruthenium complex is a bidentate ruthenium /V,/V'-diimine ligand of formula (II). Preferred Process: The e tetra-coordinated aluminum is grafted to the surface of the inorganic oxide support by a process comprising reacting a dehydroxylated inorganic oxide support with diisobutyl aluminum hydride to form a bipodal and tetrahedral isobutyl-aluminum complex, and heating the support to form a bipodal and tetrahedral hydride- aluminum complex.

PN CN110835291-A
TI Preparing estragole comprises e.g. washing and            drying raw materials, grinding, adding alcohol,            soaking, filtering, heating to separate alcohols, and            collecting intermediate products and performing vacuum            distillation.
AU XU G
   LI G
   RAO Z
AE JIANGXI EAST FLAVOR & FRAGRANCE CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202018828V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing estragole comprises e.g. (i) washing                and drying raw materials, grinding into powder                using grinder, then adding alcohol and soaking to                obtain initial material solution, (ii) filtering                and applying certain pressure to obtain filtrate,                (iii) adding filtrate into vacuum distillation                unit, heating to separate alcohols and collecting                intermediate products, (iv) placing the collected                intermediate products in extraction kettle, then                preheating the extraction kettle, when reaching                predetermined temperature, opening the carbon                dioxide gas cylinder for air supply and turning on                high-pressure pump to increase the pressure,                adjusting the pressure and temperature and                controlling carbon dioxide flow and starting                circular extraction at constant temperature and                performing pressure extraction and discharging to                obtain primary estragole and (v) placing primary                estragole in distillation column and performing                second vacuum distillation.
   USE - The estragole is useful in spices, food and                beverage industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process and low                production cost; and is convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing estragole comprises (i) washing and                drying raw materials, grinding into powder using                grinder, then adding alcohol and soaking for                certain period of time to obtain initial material                solution, (ii) filtering with 300-400 mesh filter                cloth and applying certain pressure to obtain                filtrate, (iii) adding filtrate into vacuum                distillation unit, heating to 70-85 degrees C to                separate alcohols and collecting intermediate                products, (iv) placing the collected intermediate                products in extraction kettle, then preheating the                extraction kettle, when reaching predetermined                temperature, then opening the carbon dioxide gas                cylinder for air supply, and turning on                high-pressure pump to increase the pressure,                adjusting the pressure and temperature, and                controlling carbon dioxide flow and starting                circular extraction at constant temperature and                performing pressure extraction for 1-2 hours and                discharging to obtain primary estragole and (v)                placing primary estragole in a distillation column                and performing second vacuum distillation to obtain                final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material is                  green pepper or star anise. The alcohol substance                  is ethanol and/or isopropanol. Preferred Method:                  In step (ii): the pressure is 0.3-0.5 MPa. In                  step (iv), the adjusting pressure is 10-20 MPa,                  the temperature is 40-55 degrees C and the carbon                  dioxide flow rate is 50-80 l/hour. The vacuum                  distillation process comprises carrying out first                  vacuum distillation under the pressure of less                  than 100 Pa, controlling temperature of the                  kettle at 50-120 degrees C, and collecting the                  intermediate products and then carrying out                  second distillation under reduced pressure,                  re-rectifying the obtained intermediate under the                  pressure of less than 300 Pa, adjusting the                  kettle temperature to 85-105 degrees C, then                  controlling the top temperature at 62-75 degrees                  C and collecting to obtain estragole.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

PN CN110482605-A; CN110482605-B
TI Preparing sodium alum using chromite as a raw material involves mixing chromite, soda ash, sodium hydroxide, water and heating to obtain a mixed liquid material, and reacting the mixed liquid material at an oxygen.
AU GUAN Q
   ZHANG G
   CHEN Y
   HE L
   MIAO R
   NING P
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2019A04272
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sodium alum using chromite as a raw material involves mixing chromite, soda ash, sodium hydroxide, and water at a mass ratio of 1: 1-1.1: 0.4-0.5: 5 and heating to 100 degrees C to obtain a mixed liquid material. The mixed liquid material is reacted at an oxygen pressure of 3-3.5 megapascal (MPa) and a temperature of 200-230 degrees C for 2-3 hours. The oxygen is change to carbon dioxide gas, the pressure is 1.2-1.3 Mpa, the temperature is 60-70 degrees C, and the reaction takes 2-3 hours to obtain the mixed product. The supernatant separate from the mixed product. The supernatant is at a temperature of 10-15 degrees C to obtain white sodium bicarbonate crystals. The solution was concentrated and cooled again to obtain the sodium alum product after separating the sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing sodium alum using chromite as a raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sodium alum using chromite as a raw material has realizes efficient self-recycling of carbon dioxide (CO2), soda ash, and thermal energy, and has reduces waste of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for sodium alum using chromite preparation device, which comprises:
   (A) mixing device, a stirring device, a horizontal reactor, a waste liquid tank, oxygen tank, carbon dioxide gas tank, decomposition tank, separation tank, multiple pumps;
   (B) the mixing device is provided with a stirring device, and the mixing device is provided with 4 feeding ports and one discharging port;
   (C) the discharge port is connected to the mixing liquid port on the upper portion of the horizontal reactor through a pump; and
   (D) the horizontal reactor is also provided with more than one upper exhaust port, oxygen inlet, carbon dioxide inlet, supernatant liquid outlet, discharge port, lower discharge port with a valve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass percentage content of elemental chromium (Cr) in chromite is 68%, the purity of soda ash is 99%, and the purity of sodium hydroxide is greater than 96%. Preferred Condition: The sodium bicarbonate crystals are decomposed at 100 degrees C to obtain sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is returned to use, and the sodium carbonate is returned to use. The residue of the mixed product is washed 1-2 times with water it is then used for iron extraction, and the washing liquid returns.

TI Method for detecting occupancy in residential building based on e.g. passive infrared sensor signal, involves setting passive infrared signal-based re-probability to one when passive infrared signal of threads is not specific value.
AU LEE K
AE UNIV PUKYONG NAT (UYPN-C)
GA 201975362G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring a passive infrared (PIR) signal of a thread among multiple threads in real time by a PIR sensor. Concentration change is determined for calculating concentration change amount by a calculation unit. Determination is made whether concentration of carbon dioxide is increased at first present time by the calculating unit when concentration change exceeds zero. Concentration-based loss is determined at second present time2 when concentration-based re-probability is greater than 0.5. PIR signal-based re-probability is set to 1 when the PIR signal of the threads is not 0.2.
   USE - Method for detecting occupancy in a residential building based on carbon dioxide concentration and PIR sensor signal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating the PIR signal-based re-probability rate by applying a preset PIR signal-based attenuation rate, determining preset carbon dioxide based on probability of loss of a previous point in time deducted from concentration decay rate By calculating the concentration-based re-probability and performing accurate occupancy determination by differently setting the concentration decay rate according to preset conditions so as to prevent generation of mistake when concentration is changed, avoid misjudgment in specific situation and re-acknowledge occupancy detection according to sensitivity of the sensor, detection range and degree of movement of the occupants.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for detecting occupancy based on carbon dioxide concentration and PIR sensor signal. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Step for setting concentration decay rate in advance to minimum concentration decay rate when first condition is set (S17)
   Step for determining whether second condition is set in advance when specific condition is not-first condition (S19)
   Step for setting concentration decay rate in advance to maximum concentration attenuation rate in second condition (S20)
   Step for setting concentration decay rate in advance to default concentration decay rate when first and second conditions are excluded (S22)

TI Nutritive granular carbon dioxide generating agent comprises calcium carbonate, ammonium sulfate and mixed liquid.
AU LIU W
   LIU H
   ZHOU X
AE BEIJING RONGKUN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   NINGXIA KEDA PHOTOVOLTAIC AGRIC RES INST (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201910871C
AB    NOVELTY - Nutritive granular carbon dioxide generating agent comprises 45 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 55 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate and 20-30 pts. wt. mixed liquid and the mixed liquid comprises trace element chelating fertilizer liquid and binding liquid having a mass ratio of 1:4. The trace element chelated fertilizer liquid comprises 5-8 pts. wt. ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, 0.01-0.05 pts. wt. zinc sulfate powder, 0.05-0.1 pts. wt. borax powder, 0.02-0.1 pts. wt. ammonium molybdate powder and 11.75-14.92 pts. wt. water. The binding liquid comprises 5-10 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), 5-10 pts. wt. corn starch and 60-70 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Used as nutritive granular carbon dioxide generating agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The agent uses easily available raw materials and the method is convenient for promotion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the nutritive granular carbon dioxide generating agent comprising (i) taking ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, zinc sulfate powder, borax powder, ammonium molybdate powder and water in a mass ratio of 5-8:0.01-0.05:0.05-0.1:0.02-0.1:11.75-14.92, uniformly mixing above materials, reacting at 60-80 degrees C, stirring at a speed of 80 rotations/minute for 20-30 minutes to obtain trace element chelating fertilizer solution; (ii) uniformly mixing polyvinyl alcohol, corn starch and water in a mass ratio of 5-10:5-10:60-70, reacting at 60-80 degrees C, stirring at 80 rotations/minute for 20-30 minutes to obtain binding liquid; (iii) uniformly mixing trace element chelating fertilizer solution and binding liquid in a mass ratio of 1:4 to obtain mixture; and (iv) uniformly mixing calcium carbonate and ammonium sulfate in a mass ratio of 45:55, drying, pulverizing the above materials, passing via 60-80 mesh sieve, uniformly mixing, adding to the granulator, spraying mixture obtained in the step (iii) of 20-30% of the mass of the raw material and granulating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid is trace element chelating agent, zinc sulfate is ordinary agricultural zinc sulfate, the borax is ordinary agricultural borax, the ammonium molybdate is ordinary agricultural ammonium molybdate, the polyvinyl alcohol has a molecular weight of 1300-1800, the corn starch is ordinary corn starch having a grain size of 50-80 mesh, the ammonium sulfate is ordinary agricultural ammonium sulfate and the calcium carbonate is ordinary limestone.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PT P
PN CN108796163-A; CN108796163-B
TI Controlling high temperature fire point in blast furnace jet impact zone involves using carbon dioxide, based on metallurgical reaction of converter steel making molten pool, where ratio of carbon dioxide in blowing gas is controlled.
AU ZHU R
   HAN B
   WEI G
   LIU R
   DONG K
   WU W
   LV M
   FENG C
   JIANG J
   DONG J
   WU X
   WANG Y
   HU S
   ZHU C
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201892689G
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling high temperature fire point in a blast furnace jet impact zone involves using carbon dioxide, based on the metallurgical reaction characteristics of a converter steel making molten pool, where the ratio of carbon dioxide in the blowing gas is dynamically controlled by a time-division period to control the converter blowing process, temperature distribution of the oxygen lance jet impact zone and the size of the fire point. The early stage of blowing, the proportion of the oxygen gun jet impact zone in the high temperature fire point area is controlled to 20-30 wt.%, then satisfying the rapid temperature rise and slag formation of the molten pool, and silicon, manganese are used to suppress the released reaction heat effectively.
   USE - Method for controlling high temperature fire point in blast furnace jet impact zone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to control high temperature fire point in a blast furnace jet impact zone, which is suitable for hot metal blowing at a temperature higher than 1250 degrees C and applicable to 30-400 t converter steel making process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The proportion of the oxygen gun jet impact zone in the high temperature fire point area in middle of blowing is controlled to 20-40%, which satisfies the decarburization and temperature rise of the molten pool, the molten pool is strengthened and the material between the fire point and the molten pool is accelerated, energy transfer is carried out to reduce the heat loss caused by the heat taken away by the flue gas. The proportion of the oxygen gun jet impact zone in the high temperature fire point area at the end of the blowing process is controlled to 5-10%, which meets the requirements of the end molten steel temperature and phosphorus content, metal yield is increased and the molten steel quality is improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pre-blowing period is controlled to 0-5 minutes, to ensure that the molten pool is rapidly heated and slag, then the oxidation of the molten pool element is suppressed, heat radiation of the fire point is reduced, and volume of the high temperature fire point is controlled. The oxygen lance is lowered to 1.6-2.2 m from the molten steel surface, carbon dioxide injection ratio is controlled to 10 to 20%, oxygen supply pressure of the gas is controlled to 0.8 to 0.95 MPa, and blowing is started. The middle of the blowing time is controlled to 5-13 minutes, high temperature reaction characteristics of carbon dioxide are used to strengthen the molten pool agitation, accelerate the internal material and energy convection in the molten pool, and reduce the heat taken away by the flue gas, where the oxygen lance is lowered to 1.2-1.8 m from the molten steel surface, carbon dioxide injection ratio is reduced to 3-10 vol.% during the blowing process, and the blowing is continued and after 13 minutes of blowing period carbon dioxide weak oxidizing property is used to reduce the heat release of the oxygen oxidizing molten pool element. The oxygen lance is lifted to a distance of 1.7-2.2 m from the molten steel surface, and the proportion of carbon dioxide in the blowing gas is controlled to 10-30% for blowing, through the sub-gun and detection is carried out to complete the blowing end point control. The high temperature fire point is controlled by the carbon dioxide in the oxygen lance of the converter, where the high temperature fire point area refers to a temperature higher than 2100 degrees C. The oxygen jet pulsing volume approximation calculation is carried out by using the equation v=2 pi r2h/3, and jet and the melt are radius of the circle, where r=0.63( rho t/ rho s)1/6(~o.d)1/3(H/ beta )1/2, h=d sq.root( rho beta ~o2/H rho .g), when the pool is in contact for the jet impact depth, Where rho is the oxygen gas outlet gas density, kg/m3, rho s is steel liquid density in kg/m3, ~o is the jet exit velocity in m/second, d is the jet exit diameter, H is the blowing gun position, beta is a constant, determined by the jet Mach number 8, and g is the acceleration of gravity in m2/second. The blowing gas is mixed by a gas static mixer, where the carbon dioxide gas pressure is higher than the oxygen pressure by 0.1 to 0.2 MPa, the uniformity coefficient of the mixing of oxygen and carbon dioxide is less than equal to 99%.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A05; M24-B02C; M24-C07; M24-C09; M24-D02; M24-D07; M24-E
