PN CN108676620-A
TI Essence oil and gas liquid separation method for plants, involves providing bottom end of first separating mechanism with liquid discharging mechanism, and mounting side wall of cylinder with heat dissipation fan.
AU LIU Z
   YANG D
AE LIU Z (LIUZ-Individual)
GA 201884270X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing the carbon dioxide gas carrying the essential oil into the gas-liquid separation device separating the essential oil and the carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide discharged is introduced into distilled water, and the residual essential oil in the carbon dioxide is absorbed by distilled water. The essential oil obtained is passed into a purification device to purify the essential oil. The top of the cylinder (1) is fastened to the sealing cover (2). The second separating mechanism (5) is mounted at an outlet end of the first separating mechanism (3) used for secondary collection of essential oils. The second separating mechanism and the outlet pipe (7) are in communication with each other. The bottom end of the first separating mechanism is provided with the liquid discharging mechanism configured to discharge essential oils. The side wall of the cylinder is mounted with the heat dissipation fan (9) for reducing the temperature inside the cylinder.
   USE - Essence oil and gas liquid separation method for plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The V-shaped separation plate can split the gas to both sides, and the essential oil is adsorbed on the separation plate under the action of inertia, thus realizing secondary collection of the essential oil and increasing the extraction amount. The scraping mechanism can be started to scrape off the essential oil on the separation plate, so that the essential oil can flow quickly into the U-shaped tube. The essential oil is automatically discharged through the first drain pipe to realize automatic draining and reduce gas turbulence when the U-shaped pipe reaches the position of the first drain pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view of a gas-liquid separation device used in the plant essence oil and gas liquid separation method.
   Cylinder (1)
   Sealing cover (2)
   First separating mechanism (3)
   Second separating mechanism (5)
   Outlet pipe (7)
   Heat dissipation fan (9)

PN CN108104815-A
TI Large-thick and hard coal-seam blasting pressure relief gas drainage coal caving mining method involves arranging generator in hole to perform mining, blasting, high-gas pumping and discharging parallel working procedure of surface.
AU WU X
   SHANG J
   GE S
   MENG S
   XUE K
   ZHANG M
   CAO X
   DU X
AE MENG S (MENG-Individual)
GA 201845416N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging a top coal caving working surface (11) at a bottom portion of a large-thick and hard coal-seam. A top coal caving working surface transporting lane (10) and a coal caving working surface return wind alley (13) are obliquely and respectively provided with a lower lane (9) and an upper lane (7) to reach a top portion of the coal-seam. The top portion of the coal-seam is provided with high-processing lanes (1) in parallel manner. A connecting roadway (2) is formed between the high-processing lanes. An adjusting window (8) is arranged in the connecting roadway to control ventilation. A high-gas extracting pipe (3) is arranged in the high-processing lanes. The connecting roadway is provided with a drill site (4). A drilling hole (5) is formed by using the drill site. A liquid carbon dioxide generator (6) is arranged in the hole to perform coal mining, carbon dioxide blasting, high-gas pumping and discharging parallel working procedure of the working surface.
   USE - Large-thick and hard coal-seam blasting pressure relief gas drainage coal caving mining method.

PN CN107812539-A
TI Preparing copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid loaded platinium nano-alloy catalyst for alkylation of methanol, involves adding platinum(II) acetylacetonate and iron(II) acetylacetonate precursor into reaction kettle.
AU GAO G
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 2018239449
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid loaded platinium nano-alloy catalyst involves adding the 50mg platinum(II) acetylacetonate and iron(II) acetylacetonate precursor into 20ml reaction kettle at weight ratio of 1:1, adding the 20g copper-BTC (benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid) nano-porous material in the reaction kettle, adding 500 mu L tetrahydrofuran and sealing the reaction kettle under pressed 150 atmospheric of supercritical carbon dioxide. The reaction kettle is heated to 60 degrees C for 2 hours, slowly released to obtain platinum loaded iron nano-porous material.
   USE - Method for preparing copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid loaded platinium nano-alloy catalyst for alkylation of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid loaded platinium nano-alloy catalyst has short technical route and improved xylene yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid loaded platinium nano-alloy catalyst involves adding the 50mg platinum(II) acetylacetonate and iron(II) acetylacetonate precursor into 20ml reaction kettle at weight ratio of 1:1, adding the 20g copper-BTC (benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid) nano-porous material in the reaction kettle, adding 500 mu L tetrahydrofuran and sealing the reaction kettle under pressed 150 atmospheric of supercritical carbon dioxide. The reaction kettle is heated to 60 degrees C for 2 hours, slowly released to obtain platinum loaded iron nano-porous material. The obtained product is put into the reaction kettle, added 1mL tetrahydrofuran borane and sealed the reaction kettle under pressed 150 atmospheric of supercritical carbon dioxide. The reaction kettle is heated to 80 degrees C for 2 hours, naturally cooled to room temperature, slowly released normal pressure. The obtained product is pressed under 80mL of supercritical carbon dioxide in the reaction kettle at rate of 0.5ml per minute for 2 hours. The reaction is stopped to obtain copper-benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid loaded platinium nano-alloy catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The Cu-BTC nano-porous material is prepared by adding the copper-II-nitrate trihydrate, 1,3,5-benzene tricarboxylic acid, ethanol and water as the solvent into teflon reactor at ratio of 1:0.56:55.6:186 at room temperature under ultrasonic irradiation treatment for 5 minutes. The pre-treated teflon reactor is installed on microwave reactor under irradiation of microwave of 2.45 gigaHertz for 2 minutes at 140 degrees C for 30 minutes followed by crystallization reaction and cooled to room temperature. The obtained product is filtered by filter paper to obtain a powder product, washed for 3 times with ethanol to purify the obtained powder product and dried at 70-100 degrees C to obtain Cu-BTC nano-porous material.

TI Passivating rice bran lipase under the action of            supercritical carbon dioxide solvent and pressure            comprises treating rice bran, preserving heat sensitive            component, stabilizing rice bran, and inactivating rice            bran lipase.
AU YU D
   WANG T
   ZHANG X
   PEI X
   CHEN S
   GENG H
   CHENG J
   JIANG L
   QIN L
   WANG L
AE UNIV NORTHEAST AGRIC (UNEA-C)
GA 201995537V
AB    NOVELTY - Passivating rice bran lipase under the action                of supercritical carbon dioxide solvent and                pressure comprises treating the rice bran by using                supercritical carbon dioxide solvent synergistic                pressure, passivating rice bran lipase, preserving                heat sensitive component, stabilizing rice bran,                and inactivating rice bran lipase under                supercritical carbon dioxide solvent synergistic                pressure by cleaning, screening, crushing and                sieving the rice bran, placing the rice bran in a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and                adjusting the pressure to supercritical state,                placing the rice bran in the supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction device, adjusting the carbon                dioxide pressure, and reacting, treating the rice                bran with supercritical carbon dioxide solvent in                combination with pressure treatment for determining                nitrogen solubility index (NSI), and studying the                storage stability of rice bran after supercritical                carbon dioxide solvent in combination with pressure                treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for passivating rice bran                lipase under the action of supercritical carbon                dioxide solvent and pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses carbon dioxide gas which is                widely used in food processing; has low cost, and                high purity components; uses carbon dioxide gas                which is safe and easy to handle; can greatly                change the conformation of the enzyme and                inactivate it by passivation; can determine the NSI                value of stabilized rice bran, i.e. 80%; can                greatly improve the utilization rate of rice bran.                The rice bran: has free fatty acid content of less                than 5% after 8 weeks of storage, and has peroxide                value within the threshold range of 5                mmol/kg.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Passivating rice bran lipase under the action                of supercritical carbon dioxide solvent and                pressure comprises treating the rice bran by using                supercritical carbon dioxide solvent synergistic                pressure, passivating rice bran lipase, preserving                heat sensitive component, stabilizing rice bran,                and inactivating rice bran lipase under                supercritical carbon dioxide solvent synergistic                pressure by (a) cleaning, screening, crushing and                sieving the rice bran, (b) placing the rice bran in                a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device,                and adjusting the pressure to make the system reach                to supercritical state, (c) placing the rice bran                in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                device, adjusting the carbon dioxide pressure to                30-50 MPa, and reacting at 35-55 degrees C for                60-180 minutes, (d) treating the rice bran with                supercritical carbon dioxide solvent in combination                with pressure treatment for determining nitrogen                solubility index (NSI), and (e) studying the                storage stability of rice bran after supercritical                carbon dioxide solvent in combination with pressure                treatment.

PT P
PN EP3992268-A1; WO2022090118-A1
TI Making solid recovered fuel pellets used to make hydrogen and/or carbon dioxide enriched syngas, by e.g. torrefacting pellets at torrefaction temperature and milling charred pellets in first milling step to coarsely milled charred pellets.
AU EURLINGS J T G
AE RWE GENERATION NL BV (RHWL-C)
   RWE GENERATION NL BV (RHWL-C)
GA 202258186L
AB    NOVELTY - Processing solid recovered fuel pellets (117), comprises: (a) torrefacting the pellets at a torrefaction temperature of 250-300 degrees C generating charred pellets (201) and torrefaction gas (202); (b) milling the charred pellets in a first milling step to coarsely milled charred pellets (207); (c) removing metal residues (206) from the coarsely milled charred pellets; (d) milling the coarsely charred pellets to finely milled charred pellets; (e) gasifying the finely milled charred pellets to raw syngas in an entrained flow gasifying process; (f) performing carbon monoxide shift reactions to the raw syngas creating shifted syngas; (g) removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from the shifted syngas creating a syngas; and (h) generating a product gas stream rich in hydrogen by purifying the syngas.
   USE - The method is useful for processing solid recovered fuel pellets (claimed) which is used to produce hydrogen and/or carbon dioxide enriched syngas by a torrefaction of the solid recovered fuel pellets with a subsequent gas treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows the transformation of the municipal solid waste into hydrogen with a high yield instead of landfilling or incinerating the municipal solid waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a plant for the processing solid recovered fuel pellets to a product gas stream rich in hydrogen, comprising a torrefaction unit for torrefying the solid recovered fuel pellets to charred pellets, a second mill for milling the charred pellets to coarsely milled charred pellets, a third metal removal unit comprising an eddy current for removing metal residues from the coarsely milled charred pellets, a third mill for milling the coarsely milled charred pellets to finely milled charred pellets, a gasification unit comprising an entrained flow gasifier for gasifying the finely milled charred pellets to raw syngas, a carbon monoxide shift unit for performing carbon monoxide shift reactions to the raw syngas creating shifted syngas, a gas cleaning unit comprising adsorbing means for removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from the shifted syngas and a hydrogen separator for separating hydrogen from a purge gas generating a product gas stream rich in hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a visualization of a pelletizing facility.
   Fuel pellets (117)
   Charred pellets (201)
   Torrefaction gas (202)
   Metal residues (206)
   Milled charred pellets (207)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The torrefaction gas generated in step (a) is thermally cracked for the generation of a torrefaction syngas, where the torrefaction syngas is mixed with the raw syngas generated in step (e) before step (f). In the step (b) the charred pellets are milled to a particle size of less than or equal to 10 mm. In the step (c) the metal residues are removed using an eddy current technology. In the step (d) the coarsely milled charred pellets are milled to a particle size of less than or equal to 500 mm. In the step (h) carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide are removed from the syngas by adsorption. In the step (g) hydrogen is separated from the syngas using a pressure swing adsorption system creating the product gas stream rich in hydrogen and a purge gas. At least a part of the purge gas is provided to the carbon monoxide shift reaction in the step (e). Preferred Components: A part of the purge gas is used as fuel gas. The plant further comprises a torrefaction gas processing unit for thermally cracking torrefaction gas generatable in the torrefaction unit. The hydrogen separator comprises pressure swing adsorption means.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN CN114011230-A; CN216458004-U
TI System for capturing carbon dioxide based on liquid-solid phase separation, has liquid outlet of clear liquid mixing tank connected with liquid inlet of flue gas condenser whose liquid outlet is connected with liquid inlet of last stage absorption tower.
AU YE Q
AE JIAXING TANBUSHOU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAX-Non-standard)
GA 202234054Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a slurry mixing tank (SM) whose outlet is provided with a slurry pre-heater (HE4) and a barren-rich liquid heat exchanger (HE5). The inlet of pre-heater is connected with outlet of mixing tank. The outlet of pre-heater is connected with inlet of barren-rich liquid heat exchanger. The outlet of barren-rich liquid heat exchanger is connected with inlet of a steam desorption tower (DS). The liquid outlet of steam desorption tower is connected with liquid inlet of an air desorption tower (DA). The air outlet of air desorption tower is connected with air inlet of boiler air pre-heater. The liquid outlet of air desorption tower is connected with lean liquid inlet of pre-heater. The lean liquid outlet of pre-heater is connected with inlet of clear liquid mixing tank (LM). The liquid outlet of clear liquid mixing tank is connected with liquid inlet of a flue gas condenser (HE1). The liquid outlet of gas condenser is connected with liquid inlet of last stage absorption tower.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide based on liquid-solid phase separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide capture system can reduce the size of the corresponding device, reduce carbon footprint, and save the energy consumption needed by the whole system, so as to reduce the CO2 capture cost. The CO2 concentration in the rich slurry to be regenerated is greatly enriched, the pressure of the stripping can be very high, reducing the trapping energy consumption and subsequent CO2 compression work requirement. The secondary gas obtained from the air outlet of the air desorption tower contains a small amount of CO2, entering the boiler air pre-heater, the CO2 content in the flue gas after boiler combustion is improved, the CO2 load amount in the barren liquid after deep desorption is further reduced, and the absorption length is increased.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon dioxide capturing system based on liquid-solid phase separation. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Air desorption tower (DA)
   Steam desorption tower (DS)
   Flue gas condenser (HE1)
   Slurry pre-heater (HE4)
   Barren-rich liquid heat exchanger (HE5)
   Clear liquid mixing tank (LM)
   Slurry mixing tank (SM)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC J01-E03; J01-E02A; E11-Q02C; E31-N05D
IP B01D-053/62; B01D-053/78; B01D-053/96

PT P
PN CN113969305-A
TI Fastly detecting colony total number in sauce production process comprises e.g. using distilled water comprising peptone, sodium chloride, disodium hydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and L-alanine as solvent, adding Tween emulsifier, and adding flavoring sauce.
AU CHEN S
   XU Y
   KE Y
   LUO Q
   XIAN Y
   CHEN J
   HUANG Q
   LIANG F
   SHENG Y
   XIONG R
   LI J
AE GUANGDONG MEIWEIXIAN FLAVORING FOODS CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022226486
AB    NOVELTY - Fastly detecting colony total number in sauce production process comprises (i) preparing resuscitation culture medium by taking distilled water as a solvent, where each 1000 ml distilled water comprises 10-11 g peptone, 5-6 g sodium chloride, 9-10 g disodium hydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate, 1.5-2 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1-2 g L-alanine, and adding Tween (Carbomer) emulsifier, (ii) adding flavoring sauce to be tested into resuscitation medium to dilute and oscillate, and incubating for 1-2 hours to obtain resuscitation raw liquid, and (iii) using BioLumix microbial real-time fluorescence photoelectric detection system to measure time DT required for carbon dioxide to reach set value in the resuscitation raw liquid per unit volume, and obtaining total number of colonies corresponding to time DT according to the established corresponding relationship between time required for carbon dioxide in original solution of different concentrations of resuscitation liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for fastly detecting colony total number in sauce production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: quickly and accurately detects the result in 24 hours; and improves the production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fastly detecting colony total number in sauce production process comprises (i) preparing resuscitation culture medium by taking distilled water as a solvent, where each 1000 ml distilled water comprises 10-11 g peptone, 5-6 g sodium chloride, 9-10 g disodium hydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate, 1.5-2 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1-2 g L-alanine, and adding Tween (Carbomer) emulsifier simultaneously, (ii) adding flavoring sauce to be tested into the resuscitation medium to dilute and oscillate, and then incubating for 1-2 hours to obtain the resuscitation raw liquid, and (iii) using BioLumix microbial real-time fluorescence photoelectric detection system to measure time DT required for carbon dioxide to reach the set value in the resuscitation raw liquid per unit volume, and obtaining total number of colonies corresponding to the time DT according to the established corresponding relationship between the time required for carbon dioxide in original solution of different concentrations of resuscitation liquid to reach the set value per unit volume and the total number of colonies in the sauce.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Tween (Carbomer) emulsifier is Tween-60 (Polyoxyethylene sorbitan mono-stearate) and Tween-80 (Non-ionic surfactant (5). The method uses phosphate-buffered saline solution.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PT P
PN CN113416137-A
TI Preparing benzyl salicylate by performing            efficient dehydration of sodium phenolate solution,            performing carboxylation, preparing crude benzyl            salicylate by reacting sodium salicylate and benzyl            chloride under catalysis of diethylamine and refining            benzyl salicylate by distilling and separating.
AU SONG G
   SHEN H
   YANG Y
   LI X
AE NINGXIA WANXIANGYUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2410L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing benzyl salicylate                involves (1) performing efficient dehydration of a                sodium phenolate solution by adding a sodium phenol                solution into a medium-pressure kettle, removing                part of water under reduced pressure, adding                phenol, where part of the phenol and water form a                binary azeotrope and separating the binary                azeotrope to obtain a dry expanded compound                composed of phenol and sodium phenolate, (2)                performing a carboxylation by introducing carbon                dioxide into the expanded compound, reacting sodium                phenolate in the expanded compound with carbon                dioxide to generate sodium salicylate and                separating phenol which is not involved in the                reaction under a negative pressure condition after                the reaction is ended to obtain sodium salicylate,                (3) preparing benzyl salicylate by reacting sodium                salicylate and benzyl chloride under the catalysis                of diethylamine and washing and separating and (4)                refining benzyl salicylate by distilling by steam                and separating impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing benzyl                salicylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accelerates the reaction rate,                shortens the reaction time, improves production                efficiency, reduces production cost, removes                impurities to effectively improve the purity of the                benzyl salicylate and provides the product with                fresh and elegant fragrance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing benzyl salicylate                involves (1) performing efficient dehydration of a                sodium phenolate solution by adding a sodium phenol                solution into a medium-pressure kettle, removing                part of water under reduced pressure, adding                phenol, where part of the phenol and water form a                binary azeotrope and separating the binary                azeotrope under a negative pressure condition to                obtain a dry expanded compound composed of phenol                and sodium phenolate, (2) performing a                carboxylation reaction by introducing carbon                dioxide into the expanded compound, reacting sodium                phenolate in the expanded compound with carbon                dioxide to generate sodium salicylate and                separating phenol which is not involved in the                reaction under a negative pressure condition after                the reaction is ended to obtain sodium salicylate,                (3) preparing benzyl salicylate by reacting sodium                salicylate and benzyl chloride under the catalysis                of diethylamine and washing the reaction product                with water and separating into layers to obtain                crude benzyl salicylate and (4) refining benzyl                salicylate by distilling by steam under high vacuum                conditions and separating impurities to obtain a                benzyl salicylate refined product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves (0) preparing the sodium phenol solution                  by reacting phenol and a sodium hydroxide                  solution. The binary azeotrope separated in the                  step (1) is used in the step (0) to react with                  the sodium hydroxide solution to prepare the                  sodium phenol solution, so as to realize the                  recovery and utilization of phenol, where phenol                  separated in the step (2) is used in the step (1)                  to realize the recycling of phenol. The step (2)                  involves adopting the pressure of the                  carboxylation reaction of 0.6-0.8 MPa. The step                  (4) involves adopting the pressure of 20-30Pa,                  where the vapor pressure is 1-2 kg. Preferred                  Composition In the step (1), phenol and sodium                  phenol are added in a molar ratio of (0.8-1.5):1,                  preferably 1:1. In the step (S3), the molar ratio                  of sodium salicylate, benzyl chloride and                  diethylamine is 1.081:1:0.094. Preferred                  Property: In the step (1), the purity of phenol                  added is greater than or equal to 99%.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)
MC B10-E02; N05-D; N07-D04
IP C07C-069/84; C07C-067/10; C07C-067/48

PT P
PN KR2060908-B1
TI Measuring cytotoxicity of burn ointment test substance by culturing cell line, placing formulation for toxicity test, injecting test substance, reacting mixture, incubating it, and confirming cytopathogenic effect.
AU SHIN B C
   MO S H
   CHOI Y
AE ARCA EIR INC (ARCA-Non-standard)
GA 202002815V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for measuring cytotoxicity of burn ointment test substance involves (a) culturing cell line in advance using Fibra-cel (RTM: Solid-support growth matrix) plate, and preparing Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) cell culture medium, (b) placing formulation required for toxicity test into biosafety workbench, subdividing DMEM cell culture medium, and sequentially diluting prepared burn ointment test substance, (c) removing DMEM cell culture medium of 24 well-plate in which cell line is cultured, and placing DMEM cell culture medium in the negative control well, (d) injecting serially diluted ointment test substance of 0.25 ml per well into 24 well-plate based on dilution order, reacting it at 60 degrees C, 10 degrees C and 35 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator respectively, and preparing DMEM cell culture medium, and (e) adding 1 ml DMEM cell culture medium to each well and incubating it for 14 days in 5% carbon dioxide incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring cytotoxicity and activation of burn ointment test substance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for measuring cytotoxicity of burn ointment test substance involves (a) culturing cell line in advance using Fibra-cel (RTM: Solid-support growth matrix) plate until cells reach 70% or more growth, and preparing Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) cell culture medium using circulating water bath at 37 degrees C for 15 minutes or more, (b) placing formulation for toxicity test into biosafety workbench, subdividing DMEM cell culture medium into 5 ml round tubes by 2 ml, and sequentially diluting prepared burn ointment test substance from 20 to 2-10 by 2 ml, (c) removing DMEM cell culture medium of 24 well-plate in which cell line is cultured, and placing DMEM cell culture medium in the negative control well, (d) injecting serially diluted ointment test substance of 0.25 ml per well into 24 well-plate based on dilution order, reacting it at 60 degrees C, 10 degrees C and 35 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator respectively, and preparing DMEM cell culture medium using circulating water bath at 37 degrees C for 15 minutes or more, and (e) adding 1 ml DMEM cell culture medium to each well and incubating for 14 days in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 35 degrees C and confirming that the well shows 50% cytopathogenic effect (CPE) per well under microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves placing Fibra-cel (RTM: Solid-support growth matrix) plate culture into each 12 well-plate or 24 well-plate for incubating UV-treated Fibra-cel (RTM: Solid-support growth matrix), preparing cell counted suspension based on total volume to be cultured, adding cell suspension to 50 ml conical tube, adjusting total volume of DMEM cell culture medium, adding 2 ml cell suspension into each well with Fibra-cel (RTM: Solid-support growth matrix), and observing cell adhesion after 2-3 days of incubation at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator. Preferred Components: The burn ointment test materials comprises 70-80 wt.% petroleum jelly, 0-10 wt.% lanolin, 0.001-0.01 wt.% beta carotene, 0.1-5 wt.% safflower, 0.05-0.5 wt.% ursolic acid, 1-2.5 wt.% ethyl alcohol, 0.05-2 wt.% benzyl alcohol and 0.05-0.5 wt.% chitosan chloride.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A09; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B01B; B04-C02E3; B04-F01; B11-C08E1; B12-K04E3; B12-M02B; B14-S18; D05-H09; A10-E04A; A10-E09; A12-E13; A12-W11L; S03-E09F

PN CN109751021-A; CN209603953-U
TI Simulation device for gas-enhanced coal-bed            methane development, has data system that comprises            data collecting box to collect flow rate/pressure data            of gas, and computer to draw displacement flow rate            curve of methane gas in coal sample.
AU DU Z
   ZHANG X
   HUANG Q
   GAO F
   XIE B
   ZHAO J
   GUO J
   WANG X
   LIU C
   ZHANG K
   TIAN C
AE LUOYANG INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (LUOY-C)
GA 201944449A
AB    NOVELTY - The simulation device has a coal bed                simulating system that comprises a case slit for                containing the coal sample (48). A injection system                comprises a methane cylinder (36), the nitrogen gas                cylinder (35), a carbon dioxide cylinder (34) and                the gas pressure pump (33). A first reference                cylinder (31), a second reference cylinder (29), a                third reference cylinder (27) contains pressurized                methane, nitrogen and carbon dioxide. The methane                gas cylinder, the nitrogen gas cylinder and a                carbon dioxide cylinder are connected with the                outlet and inlet of the gas distribution tank (25)                through the gas pressure pump. The data system                comprises a data collecting box and a computer. The                data collection box is used to collect the flow                rate and pressure data of the gas at the inlet and                outlet of the box casing (47). The computer is                connected with the data collecting box and used to                draw the displacement gas flow rate curve of                methane gas in the coal sample according to the                flow rate and pressure data.
   USE - Simulation device for gas-enhanced coal-bed                methane development.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas distribution irrigation used in the                device can realize the configuration of mixed gas                of different components and different proportions,                and provide relevant gas for the research of                multi-component gas injection and enhanced coal-bed                methane development. The experimental sample used                in the device is a cracked coal sample, which                accurately simulates the actual mining conditions                after adsorbing methane. The gas pressure                regulating valve used in the device can stabilize                the gas pressure and realize the steady state                adsorption of the gas containing the cracked coal                sample. The device is compact in structure, easy to                assemble, low in cost and easy to                manufacture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                simulation method for injecting gas-enhanced                coal-bed methane development by using simulation                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                portion of simulation device.
   Gas distribution tank (25)
   Third reference cylinder (27)
   Second reference cylinder (29)
   First reference cylinder (31)
   Gas pressure pump (33)
   Carbon dioxide cylinder (34)
   Nitrogen gas cylinder (35)
   Methane cylinder (36)
   Box casing (47)
   Coal sample (48)

PN CN109755502-A; CN109755502-B
TI Preparing silicon carbon cathode lithium-filled            pole piece comprises e.g. drying and mixing silicon            carbon cathode material and conductive agent, and            adding polyvinylidene fluoride N-methylpyrrolidone glue            to mixed powder.
AU HU Q
   ZHANG Q
   LING B
AE LONG POWERS TECHNOLOGY NINGXIA CO LTD (LONG-Non-standard)
   BLIVEX ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BLIV-Non-standard)
GA 201944361K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon carbon cathode                lithium-filled pole piece comprises e.g. drying and                mixing a silicon carbon cathode material and a                conductive agent to obtain a mixed powder, adding                polyvinylidene fluoride N-methylpyrrolidone glue to                the mixed powder, dispersing and infiltrating,                dispersing uniformly to obtain a silicon cathode                suspension, adding metal lithium powder, silicon                elemental material, graphite and carbon nanotubes                to the reaction vessel, adding a polyfluoro organic                compound, introducing carbon dioxide gas to replace                the air in the reaction vessel, and controlling and                maintaining the temperature and pressure in the                reaction vessel, converting carbon dioxide into a                supercritical state in a period of reaction, and                forming a lithium-rich mixture, adding the above                lithium-replenishing mixture to the                N-methylpyrrolidone glue of polyvinylidene fluoride                to disperse to form a lithium-replenishing slurry,                and uniformly mixing prepared lithium-recommended                slurry.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon                carbon cathode lithium-filled pole piece.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the occurrence of                agglomeration; and improves the processing                stability of the slurry, and adopts the electrical                conductivity, which is nearly doubled when compared                with the pole piece prepared by the ordinary                lithium-removing process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing silicon carbon cathode                lithium-filled pole piece comprises (i) drying and                mixing a silicon carbon cathode material and a                conductive agent to obtain a mixed powder, adding                polyvinylidene fluoride N-methylpyrrolidone glue to                the mixed powder, dispersing and infiltrating,                dispersing uniformly to obtain a silicon cathode                suspension; (ii) adding metal lithium powder,                silicon elemental material, graphite and carbon                nanotubes to the reaction vessel, adding a                polyfluoro organic compound, introducing carbon                dioxide gas to replace the air in the reaction                vessel, and controlling and maintaining the                temperature and pressure in the reaction vessel,                converting carbon dioxide into a supercritical                state in a period of reaction, and forming a                lithium-rich mixture; (iii) adding the above                lithium-replenishing mixture to the                N-methylpyrrolidone glue of polyvinylidene fluoride                to disperse to form a lithium-replenishing slurry;                and (iv) uniformly mixing lithium-recommended                slurry prepared in the step (iii) and                silicon-carbon cathode suspension prepared in the                step (i) under an argon atmosphere, coated on a                cathode current collector, and rolling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  silicon carbon cathode material and the                  conductive agent are dry blended to obtain a                  mixed powder, the mass of quality is: 95-99.5                  wt.% silicon carbon anode material, 0.5-5 wt.%                  conductive agent. In the step (i), the silicon                  carbon cathode material is silicon, silicon                  oxide, or silicon oxide and graphite. The                  conductive agent is conductive graphite,                  conductive carbon black, ketjen black (RTM: Super                  conductive carbon black), and carbon fiber. In                  the step (i), the weight ratio of conductive                  agent to polyvinylidene fluoride PVDF is                  1:1-1.5:1. In the step (ii), the polyfluoro                  organic compound is difluorochloromethane,                  1,1,2-trifluorotrichloroethane, and                  perfluorocarbon, dimethylcyclobutane and                  polyfluorobenzene. In the step (2), the mass                  fraction of metal lithium powder, silicon                  elemental, graphite, carbon nanotubes and                  polyfluoro organic compounds are: 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  metal lithium powder, 1 pts. wt. silicon, 0.1-2                  pts. wt. graphite parts, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. carbon                  nanotubes, and 0.1-10 pts. wt. polyfluoro organic                  compound. In the step (ii), the solid content of                  polyvinylidene fluoride of N-methyl pyrrolidone                  solution is 7-8%. Preferred Method: In the step                  (ii), the lithium prepared from the mixture to                  the reaction temperature is 40-80 degrees C, and                  the pressure is 2-10 MPa, and reaction time is                  1-8 hours. In the step (iv), the mass ratio of                  lithium and silicon-carbon cathode suspension is                  0.1:9.9-3:7. . In the step (iv), the rolling is                  cold rolling mode, controlling rolling                  temperature is greater than or equal to 45                  degrees C.

PN CN108949485-A
TI Making method of cassava carambola vinegar by drying pollen, introducing argon gas for protection, grinding, cleaning carambola, cutting, crushing with plum, adding ethanol solution, extracting and separating carbon dioxide and ethanol.
AU XIE Z
AE GUANGXI CHISHENG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A04923
AB    NOVELTY - Making method of cassava carambola vinegar by drying pollen, placing into high energy nano-impact tank, introducing argon gas for protection, and grinding to obtain nano-scale pollen; cleaning carambola, cutting, crushing with plum, putting crushed mixture into extracting tank, adding ethanol solution, extracting, and separating carbon dioxide and ethanol to obtain carambola extracting solution; selecting fresh cassava root blocks, cleaning, removing peel and impurity, cutting into small pieces, cleaning, soaking into cold water, taking out, steaming to obtain cooked cassava block, crushing, adding activated yeast, fully mixing, and fermenting to obtain fermented mash; and adding fermented mash and carambola extracting solution into sealed mixer, adding acetic acid bacteria, uniformly stirring, fermenting, stirring and introducing nitrogen for protection, adding saline, sealing and storing, filtering, adding filtrate with purified water and nano-scale pollen, and sterilizing.
   USE - Method for making cassava carambola vinegar.
   ADVANTAGE - The vinegar is suitable for all ages and easy to eat, and has clear color and high nutritive value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making method of cassava carambola vinegar involves:
   (A) drying pollen to water content of 8-10%, placing into high energy nano-impact tank, introducing argon gas for protection, and grinding for 3-4 hours to obtain nano-scale pollen;
   (B) cleaning carambola, cutting into block, crushing with plum into presser, putting crushed mixture into extracting tank, adding ethanol solution, extracting at 20-25 degrees C for 80-100 minutes at pressure of 25-30 MPa and carbon dioxide volume flow of 120-180 ml/minute, and separating carbon dioxide and ethanol to obtain carambola extracting solution;
   (C) selecting fresh cassava root blocks, cleaning, removing peel and impurity, cutting into small pieces, cleaning, soaking into cold water for 70-80 minutes, taking out, steaming for 30-40 minutes to obtain cooked cassava block, crushing, adding activated yeast, fully mixing, and fermenting at 29-30 degrees C for 4-5 days to obtain fermented mash; and
   (D) adding fermented mash and carambola extracting solution into sealed mixer, adding acetic acid bacteria, uniformly stirring, fermenting for 15-18 days, stirring 1-2 times every day in fermentation process, stirring and introducing nitrogen for protection, adding saline, sealing and storing, filtering after 32-40 days, adding filtrate with purified water and nano-scale pollen, and sterilizing at 80-95 degrees C for 1-2 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The vinegar comprises 2-3 pts. wt. pollen, 20-25 pts. wt. carambola, 8-12 pts. wt. plum and 50-60 pts. wt. cassava.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethanol solution is 56-60% ethanol solution.

TI Manganese iron-based catalyst for carbon dioxide            hydrogenation, is magnetic ferric oxide nano-sphere            with uniform surface modified by manganese, which is            prepared by dissolving ferric trichloride in glycol and            adding sodium acetate.
AU WANG C
   TIAN Z
   MA L
   CHEN L
   LIU Q
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG Q
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 201880288D
AB    NOVELTY - Manganese iron-based catalyst is magnetic                ferric oxide nano-sphere with uniform surface                modified by manganese, manganese agent and low                carbon olefinis 2-4C olefin. The particle diameter                of ferric oxide nano-ball is 80-120 nanometer. The                amount of manganese agent is 5-20 wt.%.
   USE - Manganese iron-based catalyst is used for                carbon dioxide hydrogenation to produce 2-4C low                carbon olefins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The manganese iron-based catalyst has good                stability and has good industrial application                prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing catalyst, which                involves using hydro-thermal synthesis to prepare                uniform grain diameter of dissolving ferric                trichloride in glycol, adding sodium acetate,                ethylenediamine and polyethylene pyrrolidone, where                ratio of ferric trichloride, sodium acetate and                ethylenediamine is 1:8-9:14-15, adding 1.0-1.3                times polyethylene pyrrolidone of ferric                trichloride, stirring to dissolve, transferring                obtained mixed solution to hydrothermal kettle,                sealing at 180-200 degrees C for 6-24 hours,                cooling to room temperature, using de-ionized water                and absolute ethyl alcohol to wash, drying at 60                degrees C to surface of ferric oxide nano-ball to                obtain ferric oxide nano ball with even particle                diameter, dispersing ferric oxide nanoballs in                solvent, performing ultrasonic treatment for 1-3                hours, putting in oven, drying at 120 degrees C for                6-24 hours, taking out grinding, where solvent is                de-ionized water, ethanol or glycol, pre-processing                ferric oxide nano ball surface loaded different                content, dispersing in manganese nitrate solution,                ultrasonic stirring, standing by repeating                manganese ion dispersed ferric oxide nano-sphere                outer surface, putting into 60 degrees C oven to                dry, transferring to 200 degrees C in oven for 12                hours and grinding to obtain catalyst; and
   (2) a method for preparing low-carbon olefin,                which involves mixing catalyst by tabletting,                granulating to 60-80 meshes quartz sand, filling to                fixed bed reactor under hydrogen gas, heating at                400 degrees C for 5 hours, switching gas to                hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas, pressure                is 2 mega pascal, heating at 320-380 degrees C,                where raw material gas hourly space velocity is                1200-4000 per hours under condition of carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to prepare low-carbon olefin,                where carbon olefin is ethylene, propylene and                butylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of hydrogen                  and carbon dioxide is 3:1.

TI Preparing diblock polymer containing ionic liquid as carbon dioxide adsorbent and foam nucleating agent comprises e.g. subjecting reversible addition-fragmentation chain transfer polymerization reagent, monomer I and initiator and mixing.
AU HE Z
   CHEN W
   YAN R
AE UNIV TAIZHOU (UYTZ-C)
GA 201879528D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing diblock polymer containing ionic liquid as carbon dioxide adsorbent and foam nucleating agent comprises e.g. subjecting reversible addition-fragmentation chain transfer polymerization reagent, monomer I, initiator and solvent I to reversible addition fragmentation chain transfer reaction under nitrogen protection, stirring the reaction, cooling, terminating the reaction to synthesize the first block, then adding monomer II and initiator to the reaction solution, then reacting to synthesizing the second block under the same conditions, and vacuum drying resulting reactant to obtain block polymer of monomer I and monomer II, placing block polymer in a round bottom flask, adding solvent II and monomer III and stirring the reaction, cooling and adding salt to the reaction solution and continuing the reaction, pouring reactant into an alcohol solvent to precipitate a product, suction filtering the resulting mixture, and vacuum drying filter cake.
   USE - The diblock polymer containing ionic liquid is useful as an additive for polymer supercritical carbon dioxide foaming, and preparing polymer microporous foaming material.
   ADVANTAGE - The diblock polymer containing ionic liquid: has increased bubble nucleation rate of more than 3 times, and reduced cell size to less than 2 mu m.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing diblock polymer containing ionic liquid as carbon dioxide adsorbent and foam nucleating agent, comprises subjecting reversible addition-fragmentation chain transfer polymerization reagent, monomer I, initiator and solvent I to reversible addition fragmentation chain transfer reaction under nitrogen protection, and stirring the reaction at 70-90 degrees C for 15-30 hours and then cooling to 0-5 degrees C, terminating the reaction to synthesize the first block, then adding monomer II and initiator to the reaction solution, then reacting to synthesizing the second block under the same conditions, and vacuum drying the resulting reactant to obtain a block polymer of monomer I and monomer II, placing block polymer in a round bottom flask, adding solvent II and monomer III and stirring the reaction at 70-90 degrees C for 15-30 hours, then cooling to 20-30 degrees C and adding salt to the reaction solution and continuing the reaction for 15-30 hours, pouring reactant into an alcohol solvent to precipitate a product, suction filtering the resulting mixture, and vacuum drying the filter cake to obtain diblock polymer of monomer I and an ionic liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is of the formula A+B(-), where B(-) is tetrafluoroborate (BF4(-)), hexafluorophosphate (PF6(-)) or bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide (TF 2 N-) and A(+) is amine compound of formula (I), 3-methyl-1H-imidazol-3-ium compound of formula (II), phosphorus compound of formula (III), and 1-methylpyridinium compound of formula (IV). Preferred Components: The RAFT agent is cyanomethyl dodecyl trithiocarbonate and cyanoisopropyl dithiobenzoate. The monomer I is styrene, methyl methacrylate , acrylonitrile, methyl acrylate or ethyl acrylate. The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile and 2,2'-azobis(2,4-dimethyl)valeronitrile. The solvent I is toluene, xylene, dichloromethane and ethyl acetate. The monomer II is p-chloromethylstyrene monomer and dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate. The solvent II is N,N-dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide. The monomer III is trimethylamine, tributylphosphine, N-methylimidazole, N-butylimidazole, N-ethylpyridine. The salt is sodium tetrafluoroborate, potassium hexafluorophosphate or lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide.R1 , R3, R5 and R7 = 2-8C fatty alkenyl having double bond alkenyl groups or 8-10C aromatic alkenyl; andR2, R4, and R6 = 1-8C aliphatic hydrocarbon group or 6-8C aromatic hydrocarbon group.

TI Multi-layered pressure-swing adsorption system for purifying gaseous feed containing carbon dioxide in cyclic adsorption process has adsorbent layers with core-in-shell composite adsorbent having carbon dioxide removal adsorbent and binder.
AU ZHENG J
   STEPHENSON N A
   PONTONIO S J
   SCHOTZ C D
   BARRETT P A
AE ZHENG J (ZHEN-Individual)
   STEPHENSON N A (STEP-Individual)
   PONTONIO S J (PONT-Individual)
   SCHOTZ C D (SCHO-Individual)
   BARRETT P A (BARR-Individual)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
GA 201850628F
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-layered pressure-swing adsorption (PSA) system for purifying gaseous feed containing less than or equal to 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) in a cyclic adsorption process comprises greater than or equal to 2 adsorbent layers, where 1st layer comprises water vapor removal adsorbent and 2nd layer comprises CO2 removal adsorbent. Adsorbent is a core-in-shell composite adsorbent comprising inert dense core, and adsorptive shell comprising greater than or equal to 1 zeolite-containing CO2 removal adsorbent and binder, where core diameter ratio is 50-90%; attrition loss of core-in-shell composite adsorbent is less than or equal to 2 wt.%; and volumetric performance of adsorbent is greater than or equal to 0.7 bed-size factor (BSF) (m3/s.m2).
   USE - A multi-layered pressure-swing adsorption (PSA) system for purifying gaseous feed containing less than 5% carbon dioxide in a cyclic adsorption process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The attrition-resistant core-in-shell composite adsorbent maintains good physical strength and exhibits higher adsorption performance for removing gaseous impurities from feed gas streams in adsorption processes. The attrition resistant core-in-shell composite adsorbent compositions offer significantly improved working capacity compared to conventional alumina-zeolite only composites or mixtures. The low-cost core-in-shell composite adsorbents exhibit good attrition resistance and significantly improved PSA performance. The addition of the binder to the composite improves the attrition resistance properties of the composite, potentially suppressing undesirable loss of adsorbent during loading and while in use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) cyclic adsorption process (M1) for removing water and CO2 from gaseous water and CO2-containing feed stream which involves contacting the feed stream with the adsorption system;
   (2) core-in-shell composite adsorbent for use in purifying a process gas stream containing less than or equal to 5% CO2 in an adsorption process;
   (3) cyclic adsorption process (M2) for removing CO2 from gaseous feed stream containing water and CO2 which involves contacting feed stream with the adsorbent and recovering the CO2-depleted stream; and
   (4) cyclic gas adsorption process (M3) for removing CO2 from gas stream having less than or equal to 5% CO2, which involves contacting gas stream with multilayer adsorption system comprising greater than or equal to 2 layers and recovering the CO2 depleted gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the cyclic test system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Binder is a clay selected from attapulgite, sepiolite, kaolin, and/or halloysite. Adsorptive shell contains greater than or equal to 10 wt.% binding agent with respect to shell material. Zeolite is type X and has silica/alumina (SiO2/Al2O3) ratio of less than or equal to 2.5, preferably approximately 2. The 1st layer is activated alumina, and the inert dense core in the core-in-shell adsorbent of the 2nd layer is sand, preferably naturally occurring sand. Preferred Properties: Volumetric performance of core-in-shell adsorbent is greater than or equal to 0.76 BSF (m3/s.m2). Core diameter ratio is 60-80%. Preferred Processes: In method (M3), adsorption process is selected from PSA, temperature swing adsorption (TSA), and vacuum pressure swing adsorption (VPSA) processes, preferably PSA pre-purification process for removal of water and CO2 in air prior to a cryogenic air separation process.

PT P
PN CN107722330-A; CN107722330-B
TI Preparation of plastic film used in greenhouse, involves liquefying carbon dioxide into liquid, adding e.g. polyvinyl chloride, mica particles and foaming agent, forming film, coating premix containing matrix resin, extruding and crimping.
AU CHEN Q
   ZAN H
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
   SHANDONG LONGCHANG PLASTIC IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201817157D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of plastic film involves liquefying carbon dioxide into liquid, adding tetrahydrofuran, mixing, adding polyvinyl chloride, ultrafine mica particles and 4,4'-oxydibenzenesulfonyl hydrazide foaming agent, stirring, reducing the reaction system pressure to predetermined speed at constant temperature, forming a coating film, coating a premix prepared by mixing matrix resin, calcium carbonate, light stabilizer, antioxidant, anti-fogging agent and lubricant, extruding, cooling the resultant film, corona-treating and crimping.
   USE - Preparation of plastic film used in greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides plastic film having excellent infrared-blocking effect, dispersibility, thermal insulation performance and mechanical property, by energy-saving and environmentally-friendly process. The supercritical fluid does not undergo phase transition during the treatment of the mica particles in the film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of plastic film involves liquefying carbon dioxide into liquid under high pressure and low temperature, adding tetrahydrofuran, mixing to form a mixed solvent, such that the volume ratio of liquid carbon dioxide and tetrahydrofuran is 1:2-2:1, adding 2-4 pts. wt. polyvinyl chloride, mixing to completely dissolved, adding 5-7 pts. wt. ultrafine mica particles, and 1-3 pts. wt. 4,4'-oxydibenzenesulfonyl hydrazide foaming agent, stirring uniformly, adjusting the temperature and pressure of the resultant solution, maintaining the resultant solution in a supercritical state, opening a pressure relief valve and reducing the reaction system pressure to predetermined speed at constant temperature, such that polyvinyl chloride is gradually precipitated and deposited on the surface of mica particles and foaming agent to form a coating film, evenly coating a premix prepared by mixing 75-85 pts. wt. matrix resin, 2-3 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, light stabilizer, 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. antioxidant, 4-6 pts. wt. anti-fogging agent and 0.2-0.4 pt. wt. lubricant, extruding in an extrusion film blowing machine at high-temperature heating, such that gas generated by the foaming of foaming agent promotes the uniform dispersion of mica particles into the interior of the resin, cooling the resultant film, corona-treating and crimping. The light stabilizer is prepared by mixing 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. hindered amine stabilizer, 0.2-0.3 pt. wt. UV absorber, and 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. energy quencher.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The ultrafine mica particles have a particle size of 100-500 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antioxidant is chosen from pentaerythritol tetrakis(( beta )-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl) propionate), n-octadecyl ( beta )-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate, N'N,-bis-(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionyl)hexamethylenediamine and N,N'-bis(( beta )-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoyl)propanehydrazide. The hindered amine stabilizer is at least one of 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidinyl benzoate, styrene-methacrylic acid copolymer or tris(1,2,2,6,6-pentamethylpiperidyl)phosphite. The UV absorber is at least one of phenyl ortho-hydroxybenzoate, 2,4-dihydroxybenzophenone, 2-hydroxy-4-methoxybenzophenone, 2-hydroxy-4-n-octyloxybenzophenone, resorcinol or 4-benzoyloxy-2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine. The energy quencher is at least one of 2,2,-thiobis(4-tert-octylphenoloxy)nickel, nickel dibutyldithiocarbamate or nickel bis(monoethyl(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxylbenzyl)phosphonate).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The matrix resin is low-density polyethylene or polypropylene. The lubricant is at least one of paraffin wax, polyethylene wax, low-molecular weight polyethylene, or oxidized polyethylene wax.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN US2021322921-A1; WO2021210271-A1; AU2021256299-A1
TI Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus for recovering            carbon dioxide in flue gas generated by fuel            combustion, has discharge channel through which            circulating water circulating through cooling unit and            circulation channel is discharged to outside of            system.
AU KISHIMOTO S
   KAMIJO T
   MIYAMOTO O
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 2021B84969
AB    NOVELTY - The recovery apparatus (1) has an absorption                unit that comprises a carbon dioxide absorbing                section (21) that brings the gas cooled in the                cooling unit into contact with an absorbent and                causes the absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide                included in the gas, and a washing section (22)                that washes the gas. A regeneration unit (40)                removes carbon dioxide. A discharge channel is                provided through which the circulating water                circulating through the cooling and circulation                channels is discharged to outside of a system. The                cooling unit cools the gas. The supply channel is                provided with a capture unit that captures                impurities in the circulating water distributed                through the supply channel.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus for                recovering carbon dioxide in flue gas generated by                fuel combustion in factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The amount of water inside the system is                maintained within a predetermined range, and the                concentration of the absorbent in the predetermined                range is maintained. The concentration of carbon                dioxide in the absorption tower can be suitably                absorbed. The carbon dioxide recovery apparatus is                provided with a cooling device that cools the flue                gas, and a carbon dioxide absorption tower that                absorbs carbon dioxide, and thus the amount of                cooling water for use in the whole apparatus can be                decreased. The water balance of the system can be                maintained within the preset range, so that the                carbon dioxide can be efficiently absorbed in the                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide recovery apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus (1)
   Carbon dioxide absorbing section (21)
   Washing section (22)
   Absorbent pump (24)
   Regeneration unit (40)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN JP2020084184-A
TI Reduction of carbon dioxide in steel industry, involves reacting carbon dioxide with carbon components and hydrogen to convert into carbon monoxide, and reforming unreacted portion of coke oven gas in catalyst furnace to carbon monoxide.
AU NAKAO K
   SUZUKI K

AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of carbon dioxide involves supplying carbon dioxide to a carbonization chamber filled with coal in a coke oven, reacting carbon dioxide with carbon components and hydrogen existing in the carbonization chamber to convert into carbon monoxide, supplying coke oven gas containing an unreacted portion of carbon dioxide, to the carbonization chamber and supplying to a catalyst furnace provided with a catalyst, and reforming unreacted portion of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide.
   USE - Reduction of carbon dioxide used in steel industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reduction of carbon dioxide by reducing large amount of carbon dioxide at room temperature, by utilizing sensible heat of the coke oven, catalyst, and coke oven gas, and increasing calories of the coke oven gas, by deducting energy for heating carbon dioxide at room temperature to reaction temperature, and newly injected energy required for endothermic reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for coke manufacture installation (1), which consists of a carbonization chamber to which carbon dioxide is supplied with respect to filled coal, and producing coke from coal, a coke oven (10) in which the carbon monoxide is produced by reacting carbon component and hydrogen, a catalyst furnace (20) in which the catalyst is provided inside, supplying coke oven gas containing unreacted portion to the chamber with the catalyst, and reforming by carbon dioxide, and a carbon dioxide supply control unit (50) which controls supply amount of carbon dioxide to the chamber according to reacting degree of the reaction in inside of the chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view of the typical structure of the coke manufacture installation.
   Coke manufacture installation (1)
   Coke oven (10)
   Catalyst furnace (20)
   Controlling valves (30,40)
   Control apparatus (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The space velocity of the coke oven gas containing carbon dioxide supplied to catalyst furnace is 250-2000 hour-1. Preferred Process: The catalyst reforming step involves further supplying carbon dioxide with respect to catalyst furnace, with the coke oven gas containing unreacted portion of the carbon dioxide. The reduction method is performed in the carbonization chamber is provided with an discharge port for discharging coke manufactured from coal, and a riser for discharging coke oven gas containing unreacted carbon dioxide, supplying carbon dioxide in chamber to inside of the chamber from predetermined position on the discharge port side with respect to riser, supplying carbon dioxide to the further top space of the chamber, filling calcium component prior to filling coal in the chamber, after filling coal, installing calcium-containing material on the surface layer of the filled coal, prior filling coal, filling formic acid in chamber. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of carbon dioxide in chamber to carbon component in the coke oven gas is 0.2-2.0. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide supplied to the catalytic furnace with respect to a carbon component in the coke oven gas is 0.1-1.0. When the ash of the coal filled in the carbonization chamber is measured by a fluorescent X-ray analysis, the total concentration of calcium oxide and magnesium oxide obtained ash composition, is 3 %mass or more, ferric oxide is 5 %mass or more.

PN CN110590093-A
TI Treating sludge, by selecting number of sludge, obtaining sludge sanitary landfill as comparison benchmark, premising mass balance rule, calculating direct and indirect emissions, and calculating emission reduction.
AU GUO Q
   MA Y
   HAN X
   SONG S
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT URBAN WATER (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A8615N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating sludge based on carbon dioxide emission reduction, involves selecting number of sludge, obtaining sludge sanitary landfill as comparison benchmark to calculate carbon dioxide production amount, premising mass balance rule based on the accounting boundary conditions under condition of quality conservation, calculating all direct and indirect emissions in each sludge treatment and disposal process carbon dioxide emissions generated, reducing carbon dioxide emissions by carbon sinks generated by final energy, according to the carbon dioxide emissions generated by the sanitary landfill process as comparison benchmark, performing carbon dioxide emissions generated by different sludge treatment and disposal processes, calculating emission reduction according to the carbon dioxide emission reduction amount of different sludge treatment and disposal processes, the corresponding sludge treatment and disposal process is selected for sludge treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for treating sludge (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for treating sludge based on carbon dioxide emission reduction, involves selecting number of sludge, obtaining sludge sanitary landfill as comparison benchmark to calculate carbon dioxide production amount in case of sludge sanitary landfill, premising mass balance rule based on the accounting boundary conditions under condition of quality conservation, calculating all direct and indirect emissions in each sludge treatment and disposal process carbon dioxide emissions generated, reducing carbon dioxide emissions by carbon sinks generated by final energy, according to the carbon dioxide emissions generated by the sanitary landfill process as comparison benchmark, performing carbon dioxide emissions generated by different sludge treatment and disposal processes, each of which needs to be calculated to reduce emissions to obtain each required calculation carbon dioxide emission reductions for different sludge treatment and disposal processes, calculating emission reduction according to the carbon dioxide emission reduction amount of different sludge treatment and disposal processes, the corresponding sludge treatment and disposal process is selected for sludge treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method involves calculating carbon dioxide generation amount in case of sludge sanitary landfill using default value model of the IPCC. The IPCC default value model is as follows: ECO2=Wx DOCx DOCfx (1-MCFx F) x (44/12), where the W is quality of the selected sludge, DOC is the degradable organic carbon, DOCf is the degradable organic carbon actually decomposed, MCF is the methane correction factor, F is the volume ratio of CH4 in the generated gas, and 44/12 is carbon dioxide/carbon molecular weight ratio. The value of the degradable organic carbon DOC is 40-50%. The recommended value of the actually decomposed degradable organic carbon DOCf is 50%. The value of the methane correction factor MCF is 0.4-1. The recommended value of the volume ratio F of methane in the generated gas is 1. The accounting boundary conditions include carbon source and a carbon sink including direct emissions, indirect emissions, and carbon sinks. The method involves considering the accounting boundary conditions under the condition of conservation of mass, calculating power consumption generated during the sludge treatment equivalent carbon dioxide emissions EPE, where the specific carbon dioxide E0 in the biogas is produced during the treatment process, the carbon dioxide Eb produced after the methane is combusted in the biogas, and the reduced carbon dioxide emissions Em after the biogas produced is used for power generation. The carbon dioxide emission generated by the sanitary landfill process as a comparison benchmark is subtracted from each need to calculate emission reduction amount of carbon dioxide emissions generated by each accounting boundary of the treatment and disposal process is the carbon dioxide emission reduction of each treatment and disposal process that needs to be calculated.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of carbon dioxide by producing low-carbon olefins from methanol and/or dimethyl ether comprising contacting raw material gas containing methanol and/or dimethyl ether with catalyst in reactor to obtain low-carbon olefin-containing material I and carbon catalyst, separating material I to obtain product low-carbon olefin, sending carbon catalyst to regenerator, contacting with oxygen, water vapor and carbon dioxide regenerated gas to obtain material II, and burning to obtain product carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The regenerated gas comprises 1-80 wt.% water vapor, 1-80 wt.% carbon dioxide and 20-50 wt.% oxygen. The purity of carbon dioxide after burning of material II is 90-99%. The catalyst is fluidized bed catalyst containing silicoaluminophosphate-34 (SAPO-34) molecular sieve. Preferred Method: The regenerator has temperature of 550-750 degrees C and apparent pressure of 0.01-0.3 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The olefin comprises ethylene, propylene and/or butene.

PN CN113583428-A; CN113583428-B
TI Carbon dioxide-based polyurethane composite            material comprises carbon dioxide-based diols,            diisocyanates, carbon dioxide-based aqueous            polyurethane emulsions, chain extenders, organometallic            catalysts and natural polymer materials.
AU YUAN J
   LIU W
   XIE Z
   LU D
   DING Z
   SONG L
   LIU B
   WANG Y
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 2021C84487
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polyurethane composite                material comprises carbon dioxide-based diols,                diisocyanates, carbon dioxide-based aqueous                polyurethane emulsions, chain extenders,                organometallic catalysts and natural polymer                materials. The carbon dioxide-based aqueous                polyurethane emulsion is prepared using the carbon                dioxide-based diol and the diisocyanate as raw                materials.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide-based polyurethane                composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material is economical and has high                performance and high content of natural polymer                material to improve the biodegradable efficiency of                the composite material, and keeps good mechanical                property, and increases the compatibility of the                natural polymer material with the polyurethane                elastomer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composite material, comprising (i)                stirring and mixing the natural polymer material                and the carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane                emulsion, allowing to stand and drying to obtain a                mixture; (ii) adding carbon dioxide-based diol,                diisocyanate and organometallic catalyst into the                reaction vessel to obtain prepolymer A, dropping                the chain extender into the prepolymer A and                stirring, mixing, vulcanizing, and crushing to                obtain thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer                pellets; and (iii) mixing thermoplastic                polyurethane elastomer pellets and the mixture at                high speed, extruding and pelletizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The material comprises                  100 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based diols, 20-70                  pts. wt. diisocyanates, 1-50 pts. wt. carbon                  dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane emulsions,                  5-50 pts. wt. chain extenders, 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  organometallic catalysts and 5-60 pts. wt.                  natural polymer materials. The carbon                  dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane emulsion                  comprises 40-80 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based                  diol, 10-40 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 2-15 pts. wt.                  hydrophilic agent, 0-40 pts. wt. organic solvent,                  0-5 pts. wt. organic metal catalyst, 5-30 pts.                  wt. diluent, 2-6 pts. wt. neutralizer, and                  100-300 pts. wt. deionized water. The natural                  polymer material is corn starch, sweet potato                  starch, wheat starch, cross-linked modified                  starch, acetylated starch, hydroxypropyl starch,                  lignin, chitin, protein and/or chitosan. The                  diisocyanate is diphenylmethane diisocyanate,                  toluene diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate                  and/or isophorone isocyanate, the carbon                  dioxide-based diol is polypropylene carbonate or                  polyethylene carbonate, the chain extender is                  ethylene glycol, butylene glycol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, 1,6 hexanediol,                  1,3-propanediol, 1,2-propanediol,                  trimethylpentanediol, di-n-butylamine,                  ethylenediamine, tetraethylenepentamine and/or                  diethylenetriamine. The organometallic catalyst                  is stannous octoate, stannous isooctanoate,                  dibutyltin dilaurate and/or organic                  bismuth.Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  stirring and mixing the natural polymer material                  and the carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane                  emulsion for 10-40 minutes, allowing to stand for                  2-10 hours and drying 50-100degrees Celsius for                  is 1-10 hours to obtain a mixture. The step (ii)                  comprises adding carbon dioxide-based diol,                  diisocyanate and organometallic catalyst into the                  reaction vessel and reacting at 70-100degrees                  Celsius for 2-8 hours to obtain prepolymer A,                  dropping the chain extender into the prepolymer A                  and stirring at 50-80degrees Celsius for 20-60                  minutes, mixing, vulcanizing at 70-120degrees                  Celsius, and crushing to obtain thermoplastic                  polyurethane elastomer pellets. The preparation                  of the carbon dioxide-based aqueous polyurethane                  emulsion comprises (a) adding carbon                  dioxide-based diol, diisocyanate,                  2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, organic solvent and                  organometallic catalyst into the reaction vessel                  and reacting at 70-100degrees Celsius for 2-8                  hours to obtain prepolymer B; and (b) cooling the                  prepolymer B at 30-60degrees Celsius,                  sequentially adding acetone, triethylamine and                  deionized water, and dropping diethylamine,                  stirring for 10-30 minutes and reacting at                  50-90degrees Celsius for 1-6 hours.
