PN US2019367390-A1; US11040898-B2
TI Carbonating a carbon dioxide sequestration            comprises using chelating agent, extracting divalent            ions from brine solution, using weak acid, regenerating            the agent and forming divalent ion salt solution and            introducing carbon dioxide.
AU SANT G
   WEI Z
   WANG B
   LA PLANTE E C
   SIMONETTI D
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 2019A1652S
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonating a carbon dioxide sequestration                comprises: using a chelating agent, extracting                divalent ions from a brine solution as complexes of                the chelating agent and the divalent ions; using a                weak acid, regenerating the chelating agent and                producing a divalent ion salt solution; and                introducing carbon dioxide to the divalent ion salt                solution to induce precipitation of the divalent                ions as a carbonate salt.
   USE - The methods are useful for carbonating a                carbon dioxide sequestration and mineralization of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods are stable and environmentally                acceptable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                mineralization of carbon dioxide (M2) comprising                combining water with carbon dioxide to produce a                carbon dioxide solution, introducing an                ion-exchanger to the carbon dioxide solution to                induce exchange of alkali metal cations included in                the ion-exchanger with protons included in the                carbon dioxide solution and to produce a                bicarbonate salt solution of the alkali metal                cations, and introducing a brine solution to the                bicarbonate salt solution to induce precipitation                of divalent ions from the brine solution as a                carbonate salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the                  step of extracting the divalent ions includes                  introducing the chelating agent to the brine                  solution, followed by subjecting the brine                  solution to filtration, where subjecting the                  brine solution to filtration is performed by                  ultrafiltration, nanofiltration or reverse                  osmosis; the step of subjecting the brine                  solution to filtration includes producing a                  retentate solution including the complexes of the                  chelating agent and the divalent ion; and the                  step of regenerating the chelating agent includes                  introducing the weak acid to the retentate                  solution to induce precipitation of the chelating                  agent and to produce the divalent ion salt                  solution, where the weak acid has a pKa greater                  than 3.6 at 298 K; and the step of regenerating                  the chelating agent includes adjusting the pH of                  the retentate solution to less than or equal to                  4. The method (M1) further comprises adjusting                  the pH of the divalent ion salt solution to above                  3.6, before introducing the carbon dioxide. The                  step of introducing the carbon dioxide includes                  inducing precipitation of at least one of calcium                  carbonate or magnesium carbonate. The method (M2)                  further comprises: recovering the heterogeneous                  ion exchanger by filtration; and regenerating the                  heterogeneous ion exchanger by exposing the                  heterogeneous ion exchanger to an alkali metal                  cation solution. Preferred Components: The                  ion-exchanger is a heterogeneous ion-exchanger.                  The heterogeneous ion-exchanger is a                  polymer-supported ion-exchanger. The                  heterogeneous ion-exchanger is a silicate mineral                  to support ion exchange reaction.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110124481-A; CN211216131-U
TI Active packaging material for simultaneously absorbing oxygen and carbon dioxide, comprises oxygen absorbing package, carbon dioxide absorbing package and elastic strip.
AU LIU J
AE JIANGSU OUKAI PACKAGING TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201972832N
AB    NOVELTY - An active packaging material comprises oxygen absorbing package, carbon dioxide absorbing package and elastic strip, where the oxygen absorbing package and carbon dioxide absorbing package are fixedly connected by the elastic strip, inside of the oxygen absorbing package and the carbon dioxide absorbing package are filled with deoxidizing agent and carbon dioxide removing agent respectively, fixed partition plate is embedded in center of top end of the oxygen absorbing package and the carbon dioxide absorbing package, multiple venting holes are uniformly drilled in the fixed partition plate, side of top end of the oxygen absorbing package is fixedly connected with upper sealing ring and side of top end of the carbon dioxide absorbing package is fixedly connected with lower sealing ring.
   USE - Active packaging material for simultaneously absorbing oxygen and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The active packaging material effectively maintain its ability to completely absorb oxygen and carbon dioxide from food bag, prevents internal deoxidizing agent and dragged carbon dioxide particles from falling outward without affecting internal and external permeability, increases type of gas absorption, has better fresh-keeping effect, and sealing effect, before usage and is small in size and convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An active packaging material comprises oxygen absorbing package, carbon dioxide absorbing package and elastic strip, where the oxygen absorbing package and carbon dioxide absorbing package are fixedly connected by the elastic strip, inside of the oxygen absorbing package and the carbon dioxide absorbing package are filled with deoxidizing agent and carbon dioxide removing agent respectively, fixed partition plate is embedded in center of top end of the oxygen absorbing package and the carbon dioxide absorbing package, multiple venting holes are uniformly drilled in the fixed partition plate, side of top end of the oxygen absorbing package is fixedly connected with upper sealing ring, side of top end of the carbon dioxide absorbing package is fixedly connected with lower sealing ring, two fixed partitions plates are respectively located inside the upper sealing ring and the lower sealing ring, single-sided gas permeable plate is embedded in interior of the upper sealing ring and the lower sealing ring, top end of the upper sealing ring is cut with annular sealing groove and the annular sealing groove matches the lower sealing ring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active packaging material comprises partitioning gas permeable plate and transparent block, which are made of aerogel materials of nanoporous network structure. The active packaging material comprises adaptive seal ring including outer elastomeric bladder and non-Newtonian fluid, where the outer elastomeric bladder is wrapped outside the non-Newtonian fluid and outer surface of the outer elastomeric bladder is fixedly connected to inner wall of the annular sealing groove, where oxygen absorbing package, carbon dioxide absorbing package and outer surface of the outer elastomeric bladder is coated with layer of 0.07-0.12 mm thick LINE-X coating. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide removing agent comprises 40-70 mass parts reduced iron powder, 1-5 mass parts alkali metal halide, 5-15 mass parts water retention agent, 0-6 mass parts gas adsorbent, 6-20 mass parts water, 1-30 mass parts calcium hydroxide and sodium-based alkali metal hydroxide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   SEO P

   LEE K
   ZHANG H
AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 2022A3644F
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling radiocarbon, comprises heating                liquid mixture containing organic compound                containing radiocarbon and metal ion in the form of                droplets to obtain metal or metal oxide and carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The radiocarbon is useful as raw material for                producing isotope marker for medicinal and drug                development.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recycles, and reduces radioactive                waste; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing a carbonate containing radiocarbon,                comprising mixing carbon dioxide and aqueous sodium                hydroxide solution obtained by above method to                produce sodium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid mixture                  comprises 10-99.9999 wt.% organic compound                  containing radiocarbon and 0.0001-90 wt.% metal                  ion based on the total weight of the liquid                  mixture. In the reducing                  atmosphere, the reducing gas includes hydrogen,                  methane, ammonia or formed by a mixed gas of                  carrier gas including nitrogen, or argon. The                  oxidizing atmosphere is formed by oxygen or                  compressed air. Preferred Method: The heating is                  carried out by spray pyrolysis. The droplet has                  an average particle diameter of 0.1-1000 mu m.                  The heating is carried out at 200-1400 degrees C.                  The heating in the droplets form is carried out                  in a reducing atmosphere or an oxidizing                  atmosphere. The method further comprises                  producing a carbonate by mixing sodium carbonate                  with barium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, and/or                  strontium hydroxide to obtain barium carbonate,                  calcium carbonate, and/or strontium                  carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN KR2022107444-A
TI Preparing highly conductive porous activated            carbon comprises carrying out hybrid activation            treatment of petroleum pitch using hydrocarbon gas,            carbon-dioxide and water vapor as activation            source.
AU ROH K C
   LEEJUNGHAN
AE KOREA INST CERAMIC ENG & TECHNOLOGY (KOCE-C)
GA 2022971725
AB    NOVELTY - Producing porous activated carbon comprises:                (a) preparing petroleum-based pitch, and (b)                carrying out hybrid activation treatment of the                petroleum pitch using hydrocarbon gas,                carbon-dioxide and water vapor as an activation                source.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly                conductive porous activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; and solves the                environmental pollution problem in prior. The                porous activated carbon has electrical conductivity                of 2.7-3.3 S/cm under the condition of 100-120                kgf.cm-2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for preparing highly conductive porous                activated carbon (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  petroleum based pitch includes pitch derived from                  residual oil generated in the petrochemical                  industry, and has softening point of                  200-300&#176; C. In the step (b), the                  hydrocarbon gas includes methane, ethane,                  propane, butane, pentane, hexane, acetylene,                  cyclopropene, butadiene and/or pentyne. Preferred                  Method: In the step (b): the hybrid activation                  process is carried out at 800-1000&#176; C at                  a rate of 2-10&#176; C/minute for 30-120                  minutes under inert atmosphere at 100-700 cc/mm,                  where inert gas includes argon, helium and/or                  nitrogen; and the hydrocarbon gas, carbon dioxide                  and water vapor are supplied at a speed of                  200-500 cc/mm. The hydrocarbon gas in the hybrid                  activation process is used as a carrier gas so                  that water vapor generated by ultrasonically                  vibrating the water in the ultrasonic atomizer                  flows into the furnace. The method further                  comprises (c) cooling the synthesized porous                  activated carbon at a rate of 5-20&#176;                  C/minute to obtain porous activated carbon having                  a specific surface area of 1,500-3,500                  m2/g.
   EXAMPLE - 5 g petroleum pitch was placed in a crucible                and then placed into a reactor of heat treatment                device. The left side of tubular furnace was filled                with distilled water to the outlet to check whether                the gas escaped and an inert atmosphere was                maintained by flowing argon gas at 300 cc/minutes                flow rate for 10 minutes before the heat treatment.                Then heated to 900&#176; C at a rate of                10&#176; C/minutes. Ethane, carbon-dioxide and                steam were passed at a flow rate of 300cc/minutes                for 1 hour to provide pores to the petroleum pitch.                The high-conductivity carbon generated by the                decomposition of ethane was formed on the surface                of the activated carbon, then cooled to room                temperature at a rate of 10&#176; C/minutes to                obtain the highly conductive porous activated                carbon.

PN CN113831961-A
TI Walnut oil extracting comprises breaking the skin            of the walnut and collecting the walnut kernel,            crushing the walnut kernel to obtain walnut pieces,            adding the walnut in a sealed container, filling carbon            dioxide into the sealed container and            pressurizing.
AU ZHOU L
   DU Y
   DU J
   RAO L
   WANG Y
   LIAO X
   ZHAO L
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 202208493R
AB    NOVELTY - Walnut oil extracting comprises breaking the                skin of the walnut and collecting the walnut                kernel, crushing the walnut kernel to obtain walnut                pieces, adding the walnut in a sealed container,                filling carbon dioxide into the sealed container,                pressurizing and pressure relief processing, and                pressing and refining the product obtained in the                step (1) to obtain the walnut oil.
   USE - The method is useful for walnut oil                extracting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a walnut oil extraction rate of                more than 75 %, is economical and is suitable for                industrial application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The grain diameter of the                  crushed walnut is 10-60 meshes. The grain                  diameter of the crushed walnut is 10-30 meshes.                  The volume of the filled carbon dioxide to the                  volume of the crushed walnut is 1: (1 -9). The                  volume of the filled carbon dioxide to the volume                  of the crushed walnut is 1: (1-3). The                  pressurizing treatment is 5-30degrees Celsius,                  the time is 5-20 minutes, and the pressure is                  50-300 MPa. The pressurizing treatment is                  20-30degrees Celsius, the time is 5-15 minutes,                  and the pressure is 50-150 MPa. The pressure                  relief rate is a rate of 100-200 MPa/s; the                  airtight container is a sealable nylon                  polyethylene bag. The pressing is carried out by                  a screw press, and the spiral rotating speed is                  5-20 rotations/minutes. The method specifically                  comprises breaking the skin of walnuts to collect                  walnut kernels, pulverizing the walnut kernels to                  10-30 meshes to obtain broken walnuts, placing                  the broken walnuts In a nylon polyethylene bag,                  the nylon polyethylene bag is filled with carbon                  dioxide, and the ratio of the volume of the                  carbon dioxide filled to the volume of the                  crushed walnuts is 1: (1-3), sealing and placing                  at 20-30degrees Celsius and the pressure of                  50-150 MPa, processing for 5-15 minutes, and then                  the pressure is relieved at a rate of 100-200                  MPa/s, the product obtained in the previous step                  is pressed by a screw press, and the screw speed                  is 5-20 rotations/minutes to obtain crude oil,                  the crude oil is refined to obtain the walnut                  oil.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

The nano bismuth element in the form of nanoparticles, nanosheets and/or nanowires is left for 6-20 days, preferably 12-24 hours, in a glass container.

PN CN110373267-A; CN110373267-B
TI New additive i.e. polyoxyethylene ether compound useful for extracting plant essential oils.
AU SHI Q
   XU X
   LIU S
   ZHANG Q
   CHAI G
   XI H
   MAO J
   FAN W
   LIU J
   SONG Y
   ZHANG W
   HU J
   QU Z
   YANG W
   JI L
   HUO X
AE ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
   ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
GA 201990253J
AB    NOVELTY - Additive i.e. polyoxyethylene ether compound, is new.
   USE - The additive is useful for extracting plant essential oils (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method yields high quality plant essential oils and avoids the loss of aroma substances caused by the removal of additives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Additive i.e. polyoxyethylene ether compound of formula (RO(CH2CH2O)nH) (I), is new.
   R = 6-12C-containing aliphatic group; and
   n = degree of polymerization 5-10.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for extracting plant essential oil, comprising adding the additive and the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent into the plant raw material to perform plant essential oil extraction treatment, where the additive is added in an amount of 0.01-1 wt.% of the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyoxyethylene ether compound is linear alkyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, linear olefin polyoxyethylene ether, linear alkyne polyoxyethylene ether, branched alkyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, branched olefin polyoxyethylene ether or branched alkyne polyoxyethylene ether. The structure of the branched alkyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether is ((CH3(CH2)m1)(CH3(CH2)m2)CHCH2O(CH2CH2O)5-10H), where the sum of m1 and m2 is 2-8 and the m1 and m2 are 0 or a positive integer. The plant material is root, stem, leaves, flower, fruit and/or peel of fresh plants. The polyoxyethylene ether compound is 2-methylpentanol polyoxyethylene octyl ether (sic), n-hexyl polyoxyethylene pentyl ether(sic), n-hexyl polyoxyethylene decyl ether(sic), 2-ethylbutanol polyoxyethylene pentyl ether(sic), n-heptanol polyoxyethylene hexyl ether(sic), 4-alkenyl heptanol polyoxyethylene pentyl ether(sic), n-octanol polyoxyethylene pentyl ether(sic), n-octanol polyoxyethylene nonyl ether(sic), 3-n-propenyl nonanol polyoxyethylene hexyl ether(sic), 3-n-propyl octanol polyoxyethylene decyl ether, n-decanol polyoxyethylene pentyl ether(sic), 2-n-butylheptanol polyoxyethylene octyl ether(sic), 3-alkenyl undecanol polyoxyethylene heptyl ether(sic), 2-methylundecyl penta(polyoxyethylene ether)(sic), n-hexanol penta(polyoxyethylene ether)(sic), 2-propyl octanol polyoxyethylene octyl ether(sic), 2-alkynyl heptanol polyoxyethylene pentyl ether (sic) or 2-n-pentylhexanol polyoxyethylene hexyl ether(sic). The ratio of the plant material and the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent is 1:8-1:12 g/ml. Preferred Method: The method comprises placing the plant material and the additive in an extraction kettle, extracting the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent at 35-60 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours at 8-30 MPa to obtain an extract and subjecting the extract to separation and purification treatment to obtain the plant essential oil. The method of separating and purifying the extract comprises passing the extract in the first stage separator for cooling treatment and passing into the second stage separator to remove carbon dioxide to obtain the plant essential oil.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN110151633-A
TI Preparing facial mask liquid useful for removing acne, comprises e.g. drying grape seeds, hemp leaves, smashing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, performing cavitation jet treatment and sterilizing.
AU CAO L
   LIU X
   HUANG L
   GENG K
AE HEILONGJIANG YANGGUANG IND HEMP RES INST (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201977363M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing facial mask liquid comprises (i) taking grape seeds, hemp leaves, drying, smashing and sieving, (ii) taking the powder, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain mixture of hemp oil and anthocyanin, (iii) mixing the mixture with Bletilla striata powder, honeysuckle extract, pearl powder, and honey and performing cavitation jet treatment to obtain a mixed emulsion, and (iv) sterilizing the mixed emulsion.
   USE - The facial mask liquid is useful for removing acne (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The facial mask liquid has good water storage performance and is not easy to dry during use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the pulverization is ultrafine pulverization, and the pulverization is carried out to 600-800 meshes sieve and the sieving is over 600 meshes sieve. The Bletilla striatapowder is obtained by crushing a white mesh through 800 mesh sieve. In the step (iii), the mixed material comprises 0.2-0.4 wt.% Bletilla striata powder, 1-2 wt.% honeysuckle extract, 3-5 wt.% pearl powder, 2-5 wt.% honey and the balance is the mixture obtained in the step (ii). Preferred Method: In the step (i), the drying is low-temperature drying, the drying temperature is 45-55 degrees C and the drying time is 6-24 hours. In the step (ii), the CO2 supercritical extraction temperature is 20-30 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 28- 32 MPa, and the extraction time is 2-3 hours. In the step (iii), the method for preparing the honeysuckle extract is soaking the honeysuckle in water, heating, extracting by ultrasonication, filtering the filtrate, and concentrating, where the mass ratio of the honeysuckle to the water is (8-9):(500-1000), the heating is carried out to a temperature of 40-60 degrees C, the ultrasonic extraction has an extraction power of 100-200 W and an extraction time of 20-30 minutes, and the concentrate is concentrated to a concentration of 2-5 times the mass of the raw material honeysuckle. In the step (iii), the cavitation jet treatment pressure is 0.4-0.6 MPa and the cavitation time is 30-40 minutes. In the step (4), the sterilization is high-pressure pulse sterilization, bipolar positive and negative pulses, the electric field strength is 32-36 kV/cm, the processing time is 290-298 mu s, the water bath temperature is 32-34 degrees C, the pulse width is 2 Ns, and the pulse frequency is 200 Hz.
   ACTIVITY - Antiseborrheic; Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. GA 2018A2576L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of superhydrophobic glass fiber membrane, involves preprocessing glass fiber membrane by soaking in an alkaline solution for 2-4 hours, ultrasonically washing with deionized water until neutral, and drying, immersing the preprocessed glass fiber membrane in 0.5-2% methyl potassium silicate for 2-4 hours, taking out the membrane, placing vertically in carbon dioxide atmosphere for 3-5 hours, and drying.

AE HUBEI JINGHONG CHEM CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 201884761J

Preferred Conditions: The ratio of epoxycyclopentane, magnesium powder and trimethylsilyl chloride is 1: 1-1.5:1.5-2. The reaction temperature is 45-60 degrees C.

TI Steam plasma generating gas evaporation method, involves decomposing steam-volatile organic pollutant into carbon dioxide and water, determining waste water organic pollutant removal rate, and performing steam condensation process.

GA 201884629L

AE UNIV YEUNGNAM RES COOP FOUND (UYYE-C)
GA 2021912040
AB    NOVELTY - Bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite (1) comprises a carbon dioxide absorption coating layer (12) formed on a dielectric bead (11), includes a carbon dioxide absorption layer (13) formed on one region of the dielectric bead; a metal layer (15) formed on the carbon dioxide absorption layer and including chalcogenide; and an organic compound layer formed to surround the dielectric bead, the carbon dioxide absorption layer, and the metal layer.
   USE - The bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite is useful for plasma reactor, filter device (all claimed) and air purifier.
   ADVANTAGE - The bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite has excellent carbon dioxide absorption and reduction performance, and improved lifespan; utilized in air purifier efficiently reduces carbon dioxide and does not emit additional pollutants; and exhibits carbon dioxide absorption ability by controlling the oxygen content of the gas during particle production to control the oxygen vacancy level.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) plasma reactor comprising a chamber including an inlet through which the outside air is introduced, an outlet through which the bet is discharged, and a receiving portion formed between the inlet and the outlet, at least one bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite located in the receiving portion, and a wire electrode passing through the chamber and positioned in a direction from the inlet to the outlet;
   (2) filter device comprising a chamber including an inlet through which the outside air is introduced, an outlet through which the bet is discharged, and a receiving portion formed between the inlet and the outlet, at least one bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite located in the receiving portion, and an air ionizer positioned at the inlet and configured to supply ions into the accommodating part; and
   (3) roll-to-roll process of bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite, comprising forming a coating layer sequentially including carbon dioxide absorption layer, metal layer and organic compound layer on a general hemisphere of a spherical dielectric bead, forming a coating layer sequentially including carbon dioxide absorption layer, metal layer and organic compound layer on the other hemisphere of the dielectric bead coated with the one hemisphere, chalcogenizing the metal layer by irradiating light on the dielectric bead on which the coating layer is formed, and carrying out post-treatment by removing the solvent on the dielectric bead on which the coating layer is formed, and washing and drying the dielectric bead on which the coating layer is formed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite.
   Bead-type carbon dioxide absorbing composite (1)
   Dielectric bead (11)
   Carbon dioxide absorption coating layer (12)
   Carbon dioxide absorption layer (13)
   Metal layer (15)

PN JP2020175344-A
TI Immobilizing carbon dioxide used for producing alkaline earth metal carbonate, by obtaining alkaline earth metal oxide from brackish water containing alkaline earth metal, and reacting alkaline earth metal oxide with gas with carbon dioxide.
AU MYERS C A
   NAKAGAKI T
AE UNIV WASEDA GH (UYWS-C)
   IDEMITSU KOSAN CO LTD (IDEK-C)
   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
GA 2020A5892A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for immobilizing carbon dioxide involves (a) obtaining an alkaline earth metal oxide from brackish water containing an alkaline earth metal, and (b) reacting the alkaline earth metal oxide with a gas containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is used for immobilizing carbon dioxide on alkaline earth metals for producing alkaline earth metal carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances the carbon dioxide reduction capacity while considering carbon dioxide emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The brackish water is brackish water obtained from at least one chosen from seawater, salt lake and industrial wastewater. The brackish water is at least one chosen from brackish water obtained from a desalination apparatus using seawater, brackish water obtained from a process of making salt from seawater, and brackish water obtained from a process of recovering lithium from a salt lake.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: a hydrogen chloride reusing step of using at least a part of the hydrogen chloride recovered by the hydrogen chloride recovery step in the alkali metal removing step; and reusing the hydrate of the alkaline earth metal chloride obtained by using at least a part of the hydrate of the obtained alkaline earth metal chloride in the hydrogen chloride recovery step, after drying the brine that has undergone the hydrogen chloride recovery step. The step (a) comprises concentrating step, alkali metal removal step, hydrogen chloride recovery step and drying and thermal decomposition step. The alkali metal removing step comprises adding a gas containing hydrogen chloride to brackish water which has undergone the concentration step to remove the alkali metal chloride. The hydrogen chloride recovery step comprises adding a hydrate of alkaline earth metal chloride to the brackish water that has undergone the alkali metal separation step to recover hydrogen chloride. The drying and thermal decomposition step comprises drying the brine which has undergone the hydrogen chloride recovery and further heating to obtain an alkaline earth metal oxide. Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal comprises magnesium. The brackish water that has undergone the hydrogen chloride recovery step contains a slurry of alkaline earth metal chloride.

PN US2022267242-A1
TI Producing calcium oxide and citric acid from            alkaline earth cation-citrate anion salt, for use in            enhanced oil recovery and other carbon dioxide            utilization and/or conversion applications, involves            reacting calcium cation-citrate anion salt aqueous            sulfur dioxide, separating calcium sulfite solid.
AU NOVEK E
AE INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2022A9579S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium oxide and citric acid from                alkaline earth cation-citrate anion salt involves                reacting a calcium cation-citrate anion salt with                aqueous sulfur dioxide to produce solid calcium                sulfite and an aqueous citric acid solution,                separating the calcium sulfite solid from the                aqueous citric acid solution and calcining the                calcium sulfite solid to produce calcium oxide,                cement, and/or its combination.
   USE - Method for producing calcium oxide and citric                acid from an alkaline earth cation-citrate anion                salt, for use in enhanced oil recovery and other                carbon dioxide utilization and/or conversion                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has enabled to the generation of                high quality, high concentration, and/or high                purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The method further involves calcining the                  alkaline-earth-sulfite solid to produce sulfur                  dioxide. The method further involves absorbing                  the sulfur dioxide in water to produce aqueous                  sulfur dioxide. The method further involves                  desorbing residual aqueous sulfur dioxide from                  the aqueous citric acid solution. The method                  further involves reacting the calcium oxide with                  water to produce calcium hydroxide. The method                  further involves reacting to produce heat and                  where the heat is used to desorb sulfur dioxide,                  separate citric acid, separate water, dry calcium                  sulfite, and/or any combination. The method                  further involves reacting the calcium hydroxide                  with a solution comprising citric acid to form                  calcium citrate. The carbonate and/or bicarbonate                  salt comprises an aqueous solution comprising                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, and/or its                  combination.

PN JP2022122449-A
TI Supplying haloolefin-containing composition            containing haloolefin and carbon dioxide from first            containment facility to second containment facility,            involves maintaining haloolefin-containing composition            in first accommodation facility in single-phase state            from start of supply to end of supply.
AU HAYAMI H
   CHINSOGA T
   FUKUSHIMA M
AE AGC INC (ASAG-C)
GA 2022B2298U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for supplying a haloolefin-containing                composition containing a haloolefin and carbon                dioxide from a first containment facility to a                second containment facility, involves maintaining                the haloolefin-containing composition in the first                accommodation facility in a single-phase state from                the start of supply to the end of supply.
   USE - The method is used for supplying a                haloolefin-containing composition in working fluids                including refrigerants for refrigerators,                refrigerants for air conditioners, working fluids                for power generation systems, and working fluids                for latent heat transfer devices, secondary cooling                media, foaming agents, diluent solvents, cleaning                agents, propellants, extinguishing agents,                sterilizing agents, gas dielectrics, and etching                gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method suppresses fluctuations in                composition when the composition is supplied from a                first accommodation facility to a second                accommodation facility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The single-phase state                  is a superheated steam state, a supercritical                  state or a supercooled state. Preferred Method:                  The supply method further involves heating,                  pressurizing or cooling the haloolefin-containing                  composition in the first accommodation facility                  between the start of supply and the end of                  supply. The state changes from one state are                  chosen from a superheated steam state, a                  supercritical state and a supercooled state to                  another state from the start of the supply to the                  end of the supply. In the pressure-specific                  enthalpy diagram of the haloolefin-containing                  composition, from the start of supply to the end                  of supply, the temperature in the first                  accommodation facility is controlled so that the                  temperature in the first accommodation facility                  is always equal to or higher than the t according                  to the change in the density d over time by                  monitoring the mass w, where t is the temperature                  at the intersection of the saturation line and                  the contour line of the density d calculated from                  the volume of the first accommodation facility                  and the mass w of the haloolefin-containing                  composition in the first accommodation facility.                  In the pressure-specific enthalpy diagram of the                  haloolefin-containing composition, when the                  temperature at the junction of the saturation                  line and the isothermal line is T, the                  temperature in the first accommodation facility                  is maintained at T or more from the start of                  supply to the end of supply. Preferred Device:                  The second accommodation facility comprises                  refrigeration and air conditioning equipment,                  heat transport equipment, power equipment, molten                  metal equipment, cleaning equipment,                  semiconductor equipment, sterilization equipment,                  fire extinguishing equipment, aerosol containers,                  or cylinder containers.

PN CN114917955-A
TI Nitrogen-containing metal-organic frameworks            material used as catalyst, prepared by placing            nitrogen-containing ligand, zinc fluoride tetrahydrate            and water in three-necked flask, stirring, putting in            high-temperature oil bath, heating, refluxing,            centrifuging, washing and drying obtained solid.
AU HAN Z
   CHEN Q
   LIU L
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 2022B0409J
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen-containing metal-organic frameworks                material preparation comprises placing                nitrogen-containing ligand, zinc fluoride                tetrahydrate and water in three-necked flask,                stirring and mixing, putting in a high-temperature                oil bath and continue heating, refluxing for 12                hours, centrifuging, washing and drying the                obtained solid to obtain a nitrogen-containing                metal-organic frameworks material.
   USE - The nitrogen-containing MOFs material is used                as catalyst in preparing cyclic carbonate by                catalyzing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-containing MOFs material exhibits                good catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                nitrogen-containing metal-organic frameworks (MOFs)                material as catalyst in preparing cyclic carbonate                by catalyzing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the                  high-temperature oil bath is 160&#8451; at                  2-8&#8451;.min-1heating rate. The method of                  preparing cyclic carbonate by catalyzing carbon                  dioxide comprises adding nitrogen-containing MOFs                  material in the container containing epoxy                  compound, adding or not adding co-catalyst,                  introducing carbon dioxide, stirring, heating and                  reacting for 24 hours at 40-100&#8451;.Preferred Components: The                  nitrogen-containing ligand is 1,2,4 triazole,                  3-amino-1,2,4-triazole or 3,5-diamino                  1,2,4-triazole. The epoxy compound is                  epichlorohydrin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of                  nitrogen-containing ligand and zinc fluoride                  tetrahydrate is 1:1. The concentration of carbon                  dioxide is 10-100 vol.%, preferably 10                  vol.%.Preferred Components: The co-catalyst is                  tetrabutylammonium bromide.

PN KR2022108367-A; US2022241751-A1
TI Manufacturing chitosan-biocha composite fiber for            phosphorus removal in water comprises pyrolyzing paper            mill sludge in carbon dioxide or nitrogen gas            atmosphere to produce biochar, and mixing bio-cha with            chitosan solution.
AU OK Y
   JOE Y L
   YOON-E C
   KUMUDUNI P
   KIM S
   CHOI Y E
   CHO Y R
   OK Y S
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202298317C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing chitosan-biocha composite fiber                for phosphorus removal in water comprises (i)                pyrolyzing paper mill sludge in a carbon dioxide                (CO2) or nitrogen (N2) gas atmosphere to produce                biochar; (ii) mixing the bio-cha prepared in step                (i) with a chitosan solution, adding ferric                chloride hexahydrate (FeCl3 6H2O), and preparing a                mixed solution while stirring; (iii) carrying out                chitosan gelation by spinning mixture into sodium                hydroxide solution using a needle; and (iv)                crosslinking chitosan gel product in a                glutaraldehyde solution, washing and neutralizing                to obtain a chitosan-biocha composite fiber.
   USE - The chitosan-biocha composite fiber is useful                for removing phosphorus in water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can adsorb phosphorus in water                efficiently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                phosphorus adsorbent composition, comprising                chitosan-biocha composite fiber prepared by above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) thermal                  decomposition is carried out at 580-620 degrees C                  at a heating rate of 13-18 degrees C/minute. In                  the step (iv) cross-linking is carried out for                  1-3 hours at 20-25 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: In the step (ii), chitosan solution                  is 3-5% (w/w) chitosan solution prepared with an                  acetic acid solution. In the step (ii),                  FeCl3-6H2O is added so as to be 2-4 times the                  weight of the biochar. In step (iii), sodium                  hydroxide solution is a 1.5-2.5 M sodium                  hydroxide solution. The phosphorus adsorbent                  composition removes phosphorus in water. The                  phosphorus adsorbent composition has an increased                  phosphorus adsorption efficiency under acidic                  conditions. The acidic condition is a pH of                  2-4.
   EXAMPLE - Two types of biochar was prepared from PMS,                biochar was produced at 600?OC at a heating rate of                15?OC/minute under CO2 and N2 environment. 4% (w/w)                chitosan solution was prepared using a 5% (v/v)                acetic acid solution. The prepared chitosan                solution (115 g) was mixed with 3.22 g each biochar                of BC-N and BC-C. 10 g FeCl3-6H2O was added to the                chitosan/biochar suspension with vigorous stirring.                The mixture was then extruded with a 2M sodium                hydroxide solution (14 pH) through a 0.2 mm                diameter plastic hub needle to undergo chitosan                gelation. Chitosan-biocha composite fibers (FBC)                were cross-linked in a glutaraldehyde solution (0.6                ml/l) at 25 degrees C for 2 hours. The FBCs were                rinsed many times with deionized water until a                neutral pH was reached and then freeze-dried after                the crosslinking reaction. The chitosan-biocha                composite fibers produced from the biochar of BC-N                and BC-C, respectively, were named FBC-N and FBC-C,                respectively.

AB    NOVELTY - Nitrifying biogas involves dissolving                impurities in absorption liquid by introducing                biogas and the absorption liquid into an absorption                tower. PN US2021229075-A1; WO2021154910-A1; US11338270-B2
TI Performing autothermal reforming over modified red mud catalyst composition involves providing methane feed with oxygen and carbon dioxide to react over the modified red mud catalyst composition to produce synthesis gas.
AU ALBUALI M A
   BAMAGAIN R
   ALSOLAMI B H
   FADHEL B A
   ALSOLAMI B
   FADHEL B
   ALBUALI M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO LAW DEPT (SAOI-C)
GA 202187142W
AB    NOVELTY - Performing autothermal reforming over modified red mud catalyst composition involves providing methane feed with oxygen and carbon dioxide to react over the modified red mud catalyst composition at increased temperature and increased pressure to produce synthesis gas comprises H_2 and CO. The composition comprises red mud material produced from alumina extraction process from bauxite ore, nickel oxide, nickel oxide present at between 5-40 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition and Periodic Table Group VIB metal oxide, Group VIB metal oxide present at between 1-30 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition.
   USE - Method for performing autothermal reforming over modified red mud catalyst composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform autothermal reforming over modified red mud catalyst composition that improves catalytic activity for autothermal reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Group VIB metal oxide comprises metal selected from chromium, molybdenum, and tungsten. The composition includes component selected from Fe_2O_3, Al_2O_3, SiO_2, Na_2O, CaO, and TiO_2. Preferred Conditions: The majority of the particles of the composition has particle size of less than about 70 micrometer. The nickel oxide is present at between about 10-30 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition. The Group VIB metal oxide is present at between about 1-20 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition. The methane conversion is greater than at least about 45% for 6 hours. The molar ratio of methane, carbon dioxide and oxygen is 2:1:1. The produced H_2 is 18 mol.% of produced products from the reaction for 5 hours. The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of the modified red mud catalyst composition is between 50-90 m^2/g.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E31-A01; H04-F06; H04-E04; H04-F02E; H04-F05; J04-E04; J04-E01; J04-E11; N06-F; N03-D01; N03-D02; N03-C02; N07-J

PT P
PN WO2021130128-A1; CN114846050-A; EP4081573-A1
TI Method for producing polyhydroxy alkanoate for            production of e.g. foils, involves growing bacteria            under heterotrophic conditions in media, and            cultivating bacteria under atmosphere of carbon            dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen at preset pressure.
AU FANTINEL F
   CARFAGNINI A
AE CO2BIOCLEAN GMBH (COTW-Non-standard)
   CO2BIOCLEAN GMBH (COTW-Non-standard)
GA 2021741563
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing polyhydroxy alkanoate                (PHA) involves growing bacteria under heterotrophic                conditions in a media, and cultivating the bacteria                under autotrophic conditions under an atmosphere of                carbon dioxide, hydrogen and below 10% (v/v) oxygen                at a pressure of at least 1 barg.
   USE - Method for producing polyhydroxy alkanoate                e.g. polyhydroxy butyrate and polyhydroxy hexanoate                for production of granulates, master batch, coating                materials, foils, films, laminates, fibers, molded                components, molded articles, injection molded                articles, extrudates, containers, packaging                materials, particles, beads, micro beads and                medicine dispensers (all claimed). Uses include but                are not limited to automobile components, transport                and/or communication, industrial equipment, machine                and plant construction components, household                appliances, medical devices, and electric or                electronic components.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides biodegradable                PHA polymer with unique properties at high                rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The bacteria is a wild                  type bacteria, preferably Cupriavidus necator.                  Preferred Conditions: The bacteria are grown                  under exponential growth conditions. The pressure                  for cultivating step is preferably 2-20                  barg.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of carbon                  dioxide used is 2-25% (v/v). The content of                  hydrogen used is 50-92% (v/v).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon source used                  in the above method is selected from sugars,                  polyols, or organic acids and their salts or                  esters.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A85 (Electrical applications.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

TI Preservation of nutrients of stored meat by            Internet of things based Machine learning enabled            technology comprising e.g. temperature and humidity            sensor, carbon dioxide sensor, methane sensor, pH            sensor, LCD and micro-controller.
AU NARAYANAN A E
   RASMIKA A S
   AKASH A
   MONISHA J
   SIVAKUMAR R
AE NARAYANAN A E (NARA-Individual)
   RASMIKA A S (RASM-Individual)

GA 2021771804
AB    NOVELTY - Preservation of nutrients of the stored meat                by Internet of things based Machine learning                enabled technology comprising temperature and                humidity sensor (DHT11), carbon dioxide sensor                (MQ135), methane sensor (MQ7), pH sensor, LCD                display, micro-controller (Arduino UNO (RTM:                Open-source electronics prototyping platform)), and                Global system for Mobile Communication                module.
   USE - The method is useful for preservation of                nutrients of the stored meat by Internet of things                based Machine learning enabled technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is reliable and economical;                efficiently verifies food quality to prevent food                borne disease; and utilizes the device, which is                small and portable.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a block diagram of method of                preservation of nutrients of the stored meat by                Internet of things based Machine learning enabled                technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The Arduino UNO (RTM:                  Open-source electronics prototyping platform) is                  programmed using Embedded C programming language.                  The sensors comprise temperature and humidity                  sensor, carbon dioxide sensor, methane sensor, pH                  sensor to measure the quality of food, a                  temperature sensor to sense the temperature of                  the food, and a pH sensor to test the salt                  content of the food, where a standard pH value is                  stored in the IOT server.

PT P
PN WO2021110588-A1; EP3831920-A1
TI Controlling ammonia content in anaerobic medium of anaerobic process, by subjecting organic material to anaerobic treatment, solubilizing carbon dioxide into liquid medium, and subjecting liquid medium to anaerobic treatment.
AU HADDAD M
   PARDO P
AE SUEZ GRP (SUEZ-C)
GA 202163105A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling ammonia (NH3) content in an anaerobic medium of an anaerobic process involves (a) providing organic material, (b) subjecting organic material to an anaerobic treatment, for producing a digestate and biogas, (c) solubilizing carbon dioxide (CO2) from a CO2-containing gas into a liquid medium comprising at least one microorganism population, for providing a CO2-enriched liquid medium, and (d) subjecting at least part of the CO2-enriched liquid medium to the anaerobic treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling NH3 content in anaerobic medium of anaerobic process, and producing methane and VFAs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains NH3 content in digestion medium outside of its toxicity range, and produces methane and VFAs with improved production and yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an installation for NH3 content control in an anaerobic tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves injecting a CO2-containing gas into a membrane reactor configured to-be-placed inside a liquid bath comprising a liquid medium, where liquid medium comprises at least one microorganism population, membrane reactor comprises at least one hollow fiber arranged in a way to contact the membrane reactor with the liquid medium, and a biofilm is formed by at least one microorganism population around the at least one hollow fiber. The method further involves (e) recirculating at least a part of the digestate into the liquid medium, (f) subjecting organic material to a thermal treatment for generating at least syngas, where CO2-containing gas comprises syngas, (g) separating CO2 from the biogas produced during the anaerobic treatment for providing a first CO2 enriched gas and a second CH4-enriched gas, and providing first CO2 enriched gas of separating CO2 from the biogas as the CO2-containing gas, (h) controlling a quantity of CO2 injected during injection of CO2 into the membrane reactor as a function of a NH3 concentration during the anaerobic treatment, and (i) controlling a quantity of CO2 injected during injection of CO2 into the membrane reactor as a function of a NH3 concentration in the at least a part of the digestate re-circulated into the liquid medium of the liquid bath. The thermal treatment is a hydrothermal gasification, and method further involves producing an inorganic solid residue and liquid product (F1) containing biodegradable carbons i.e. volatile fatty acids (VFAs), and subjecting the liquid product to the anaerobic treatment.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN112811456-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate with high            specific surface area, comprises e.g. introducing            calcium hydroxide slurry into carbonization reactor,            adding nucleating agent, stirring at high speed, and            adding calcium hydroxide slurry.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG Z
   LI J
   WU C
   LEI T
   HU J
   LI Y
   HAN Y
AE JIANGXI SHITONG CALCIUM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202158524L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate with high                specific surface area, comprises (i) introducing                the calcium hydroxide slurry with temperature of                20-25 degrees C into the carbonization reactor,                continuously adding nucleating agent, introducing                high pressure pure carbon dioxide gas, stirring at                high speed, and generating calcium carbonate                crystal nucleus, and (ii) controlling the                temperature of the carbonization reactor below 35                degrees C, adding calcium hydroxide slurry with                temperature of 10-15 degrees C into the                carbonization reactor, continuously adding crystal                nucleus growth control agent, introducing pure                carbon dioxide gas, and stirring at low                speed.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano                calcium carbonate with high specific surface                area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for nano                calcium carbonate with high specific surface area                prepared according to above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  high-pressure pure carbon dioxide gas is                  introduced, the pressure in the carbonization                  reaction is maintained at 0.45-0.55 MPa, and the                  reaction time is controlled within 5 minutes. In                  the step (i), and (ii), the mass percentage                  concentration of the calcium hydroxide slurry is                  8-10%. In the step (i), the filling amount of the                  calcium hydroxide slurry is 45-55% of the volume                  of the carbonization reactor. In the step (ii),                  the adding amount of the calcium hydroxide slurry                  is 25-35% of the volume of the carbonization                  reactor. In the step (i), the stirring speed is                  920-1000 revolutions/minutes. In the step (ii),                  the stirring speed is 60-100 revolutions/minutes.                  In the step (i), the nucleating agent is ammonium                  carbonate, and the added amount of the nucleating                  agent is 0.45-0.55% of the mass of the produced                  dry-based nano calcium carbonate. The method                  further comprises (iii): adding composite surface                  treatment agent to the prepared high specific                  surface area nano calcium carbonate slurry for                  surface treatment, adding composite surface                  treatment agent is 5-8% dry basis nano calcium                  carbonate, where the composite surface treatment                  agent comprises stearic acid, cocoa oil and                  caustic soda. The step (iii) specifically                  comprises adding the prepared high specific                  surface area nano calcium carbonate slurry into                  the activation reaction kettle, heating the high                  specific surface area nano calcium carbonate                  slurry to 75-85 degrees C, adding composite                  surface treatment agent into the activation                  reaction kettle at a speed of 600-700                  revolutions/minute for 1.5-2.5 hours, where the                  mass ratio of stearic acid, cocoa butter and                  caustic soda is 4.5-5.5:4.5-5.5:0.5-1.5.

PT P
PN CN112625069-A; CN112625069-B
TI New pyrenetetrapyrazole-ligand-based cobalt metal-organic framework material used in selective adsorption and separation of gases such as carbon dioxide, methane, and nitrogen.
AU XIE Y
   LI T
   KONG X
   HE T
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2021404074
AB    NOVELTY - A pyrenetetrapyrazole-ligand-based cobalt metal-organic framework material (I) is new.
   USE - New pyrenetetrapyrazole ligand-based cobalt metal-organic framework material is used in selective adsorption and separation of gases such as carbon dioxide, methane, and nitrogen (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A pyrenetetrapyrazole-ligand-based cobalt metal-organic framework material of formula: Co(A)(CH3COO)2(H2O)(CH3CN)2 (I) is new.
   A = 1,3,6,8-tetra(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrenyl organic ligand.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the pyrenetetrapyrazole-ligand-based cobalt metal-organic framework material, which involves dissolving 1,3,6,8-tetra(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrene and 0.05 mmol cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in 1-8 mL N,N-dimethylformamide solution under sealed condition, adding 10-120 mu L acetonitrile and acetic acid, and performing solvothermal reaction to obtain metal-organic framework crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The metal-organic framework material has a three-dimensional framework structure and belongs to an orthorhombic system with a space group of Pnma and unit cell parameters a of 20.0553(7) Angstrom , b of 22.0562(7) Angstrom , c of 7.7763(2) Angstrom , and alpha , beta and gamma of 90 degrees . In the metal-organic framework, the cobalt ion is six-coordinated and the surrounding coordination is 4 nitrogen atoms derived from four different pyrene tetrapyrazole ligand and 2 oxygen atoms derived from acetate ions and water molecules in the reaction system, and the bond length of cobalt-oxygen bond (Co-O1) is 2.0797(35) Angstrom , bond length of cobalt-oxygen bond (Co-O3) is 2.0523(40) Angstrom , bond length of two identical cobalt-nitrogen bonds (Co-N1) is 2.1626(32) Angstrom , and bond length of two identical cobalt-nitrogen bonds (Co-N3) is 2.1792(31) Angstrom . The metal-organic framework has a one-dimensional circular channel, after removing the coordinated acetate ions and water molecules, along a crystallographic a-axis, with a diameter of 6 Angstrom and there are two types of one-dimensional channels in the c-axis direction and the cross-sectional dimensions are approximately 5 Angstrom . Preferred Composition: Each pyrene tetrapyrazole ligand is regarded as a 4-linked linker from a topological point of view and four-coordinated cobalt ions are regarded as 4-connected nodes, and these two types of structural units are alternately connected to form a (4,4)-connected network. The 1,3,6,8-tetrakis(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrene organic ligand is of formula (I). The molar ratio of 1,3,6,8-tetra(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrene and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate is 1:(1-5). Preferred Process: The organic ligand is obtained by adding liquid bromine to a pyrene and nitrobenzene solution at 80 degrees C, vigorously stirring, heating the mixture to 120 degrees C, and maintaining for 12 hours to perform Suzuki's reaction, adding pyrazole borate protected by tetrahydropyran in 1,4-dioxane and water, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium and potassium carbonate, sealing, vacuuming, stirring the mixed solution at 100 degrees C for 48 hours under inert gas protection to obtain 1,3,6,8-tetra(1-(tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrene, heating and refluxing 1,3,6,8-tetra(1-(tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrene in a hydrochloric acid ethanol solution for deprotection to obtain 1,3,6,8-tetra(1H-pyrazol-4-yl)pyrene. Preferred Condition: The solvothermal reaction is performed at 80-120 degrees C for 24-48 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI New compound i.e. 5-(2,6-diamino-4-carboxyphenoxy)isophthalic acid useful in preparing metal complex for carbon dioxide adsorption.
AU ZHANG X

AE UNIV DEZHOU (UYEZ-C)
GA 202137142P
AB    NOVELTY - Compound i.e. 5-(2,6-diamino-4-carboxyphenoxy)isophthalic acid (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing metal complex for carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) solves the problem that the limited adsorption capacity of metal-organic framework materials in the existing technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Compound i.e. 5-(2,6-diamino-4-carboxyphenoxy)isophthalic acid (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) metal complex comprising taking (I) as ligand and metal complex formed by taking metal ion as center, where the metal ion is copper ion; and
   (3) preparing metal complex, comprising mixing ligand, metal salt and solvent in a reaction kettle, sealing and reacting at 80-100 degrees C for 16-48 hours, where the molar ratio between ligand and metal salt is 1:0.5-1:4, and the solvent volume ratio between N,N-dimethylacetamide and water is 2:0.2-2:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises reacting 4-chloro-3,5-dinitro benzoic acid (II) with dimethyl 5-hydroxyisophthalate (III) to prepare intermediate product i.e. 4-(3,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)phenoxy)-3,5-dinitrobenzoic acid (IV), then carrying out ester hydrolysis to obtain intermediate product i.e. 5-(4-carboxy-2,6-dinitrophenoxy)isophthalic acid (V), and then carrying out hydrogenation reaction to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The intermediate product (IV) is prepared by stirring anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide, potassium hydroxide and (III) under the inert atmosphere for 0.5-2 hours, then adding (II) to dissolve in dimethyl sulfoxide, heating the reaction mixture at 100-150 degrees C, heating and stirring under the protection of an inert atmosphere at 100-150 degrees C for 3-6 hours, stopping heating after a raw material disappears in the detection reaction, cooling to room temperature, pouring the reaction mixture into ice water, adding hydrochloric acid to acidify and adjust the pH value to 2.5-3.5, stirring for 0.5-1.5 hours, filtering, water washing and drying to obtain crude product, purifying and drying the obtained crude product to obtain yellow solid product i.e. 4-(3,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)phenoxy)-3,5-dinitrobenzoic acid (IV), where the mass ratio between potassium hydroxide, (III) and (II) is 6-18:10-30:12-36, the volume ratio of first addition of anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide and second addition of dimethyl sulfoxide is 80-150:10-30, and the inert atmosphere is argon atmosphere. The intermediate product (V) is prepared by stirring the mixture of (IV) and methanol, then adding potassium hydroxide solution, heating and refluxing for 5-7 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding water, then adding hydrochloric acid to acidify and adjust the pH value to less than 1, allowing to stand, filtering, and water washing to obtain pure yellow solid product i.e. 5-(4-carboxy-2,6-dinitrophenoxy)isophthalic acid (V), where the mass ratio between (IV) and potassium hydroxide is 60-180:3-6. The hydrogenation reaction comprises mixing (V) with methanol, adding palladium on carbon catalyst after completely dissolving the solid raw materials, performing hydrogen replacement, stirring the reaction overnight under oil bath heating at 50-100 degrees C under 1-6 atmospheric pressure, cooling to room temperature, then opening the reaction kettle cover, filtering, washing with methanol and concentrating to obtained grey solid product i.e. 5-(2,6-diamino-4-carboxyphenoxy)isophthalic acid (I), where the mass ratio between (V) and palladium on carbon catalyst is 6-25: 0.025-0.1, the palladium on carbon catalyst has 5-20 wt.% palladium, the hydrogenation reaction is carried out in a pressure-resistant reaction kettle. The purification step comprises mixing crude product (IV) with methanol, then fully dissolving the mixture under the ultrasonic waves, then allowing to stand, washing with small amount of methanol to obtain pure 4-(3,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)phenoxy)-3,5-dinitrobenzoic acid (IV). In the metal complex, the ligand and metal ion form metal complex in multidentate coordination manner.

AB    NOVELTY - Removing organic matter in mother liquor of Bayer process involves providing carbonation decomposition of Bayer process seed separation mother liquor; transporting to carbon separation tank; introducing carbon dioxide in carbon separation tank for carbonation decomposition to obtain carbon separation slurry; providing solid-liquid separation of obtained carbon slurry, where obtained solid phase is aluminum hydroxide material, and obtained liquid phase is carbon mother liquor; sintering carbon component mother liquor in clinker kiln after evaporation and batching process.
   USE - Method for removing organic matter in mother liquor of Bayer process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for removing organic matter in mother liquor of Bayer process helps in removing soluble organic matter easily which is harmful to Bayer process and is recycled efficiently with low consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The quality of Bayer process seed mother liquor is 0.2-10% total seed mother liquor in Bayer process for alumina production process. The sodium aluminate concentration is 69-113 g/L in terms of alumina concentration. The concentration of sodium hydroxide is 130-200 g/L in terms of concentration of caustic sodium oxide. The sodium carbonate concentration is 5-20 g/L in terms of sodium oxide concentration. The concentration of organic sodium salt is 4-20 g/L in terms of total organic carbon. The m3 of Bayer process seeding mother liquor consumes 100-1000m3 carbon dioxide. The concentration of alumina in carbon slurry is 0-70 g/L, and the carbonation decomposition rate is 38-100%. The Bayer process mother liquor is mixed with other materials in alumina production process and then carbonized and decomposed, and sintered 0-90 wt.% other materials. The caustic soda concentration in sintering method seed mother liquor or aluminum hydroxide filter washing liquid is 80-150 g/L, and the alumina concentration is 40-90 g/L. The solid-liquid separation of carbon slurry is filter carbon slurry to obtain solid phase material and liquid phase material. The average particle size of aluminum hydroxide material is 70-150 microns with 0-3 wt.% impurity content. The sintering process is carried out in aerobic environment where clinker kiln sucks air under negative pressure, and the temperature is 1300-1600 degrees C. The concentration of Na2O in mother liquor of sintering method is 160-270 g/L, and concentration of Al2O3 is 10-45 g/L. The organic matter in mother liquor of carbon fraction is converted into caustic which becomes substitute for sintering method. Preferred Components: The Bayer process seed separation mother liquor includes aqueous solution of sodium aluminate, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and organic sodium salt. Preferred Composition: The sintering method raw material slurry is prepared by batching which includes 30-45 wt.% liquid phase, 8-20 wt.% calcium oxide (CaO), 10-22 wt.% sodium oxide (Na2O), 5-10 wt.% silicon oxide (SiO2), 1-6 wt.% ferric oxide (Fe2O3), 18-30 wt.% aluminum oxide (Al2O3). The liquid phase components includes 5-100 wt.% carbon component mother liquor, and 0-95 wt.% sintering method mother liquor.

GA 202097884A
AB    NOVELTY - Gas separating syngas applying differences in                water solubilities of syngas components, comprises:                producing a product gas comprising hydrogen and                carbon dioxide from a hydrocarbon fuel source;                separating hydrogen from the product gas to create                a hydrogen product stream and a byproduct stream by                solubilizing components in water that are more                soluble in water than hydrogen; injecting the                byproduct stream into a reservoir containing mafic                rock; and allowing components of the byproduct                stream to react in situ with components of the                mafic rock to precipitate and store components of                the byproduct stream in the reservoir.
   USE - The method is useful for gas separating syngas                applying differences in water solubilities of                syngas components.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for gas separation of syngas applying                differences in water solubilities of syngas                components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of separating                  includes: using at least one vertical scrubbing                  tower with countercurrent flow of the product gas                  and water, the product gas flowing at 20 degrees                  C, where at least 50% carbon dioxide and 95%                  hydrogen sulfide are removed from the product gas                  and separated from the hydrogen product stream by                  being solubilized in the countercurrent flow of                  water; and using at least two vertical scrubbing                  towers in series with countercurrent flow of the                  product gas and water. Before the step of                  injecting the byproduct stream into the                  reservoir, the byproduct stream is further                  treated to separate and purify carbon dioxide                  from other components to increase carbon dioxide                  concentration of the byproduct stream for                  injection into the reservoir. The method further                  comprises: liquefying carbon dioxide in the                  byproduct stream for injection into the                  reservoir; reacting the separated hydrogen with                  nitrogen to form compressed liquid ammonia; and                  transporting the compressed liquid ammonia and                  returning the compressed liquid ammonia to                  hydrogen and nitrogen via electrolysis for use of                  hydrogen as a hydrogen fuel source. The step of                  producing a product gas includes steam reforming                  or partial oxidation. The step of allowing                  components of the byproduct stream to react in                  situ with components of the mafic rock produces                  precipitates comprises calcium carbonates,                  magnesium carbonates and/or iron                  carbonates.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
