PN CN111438373-A
TI Preparation of copper-silver core-shell structured bimetallic spherical nanoparticles used for forming catalyst, involves using carbon monoxide gas, heating copper core, injecting oleylamine solution of silver organic compound, and reacting.
AU LU Z
   ZHAO H
   QIN J
   LIANG W
AE UNIV DATONG (UDAT-C)
GA 2020717673
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of copper-silver core-shell structured bimetallic spherical nanoparticles involves using carbon monoxide gas as a reducing agent under oil bath conditions, heating a copper core by two-step method, injecting an oleylamine solution of a silver organic compound, continuously reacting, washing the reaction product with n-hexane by ultrasonic centrifugation, and dispersing the obtained product in n-hexane.
   USE - Preparation of copper-silver core-shell structured bimetallic spherical nanoparticles used for forming catalyst used in carbon dioxide conversion application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of copper-silver core-shell structured bimetallic spherical nanoparticles with controlled size, improved carbon dioxide electroreduction catalytic ability and high product selectivity. The nanoparticles are easily oxidized and deactivated as compared to existing non-noble metal catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves preparing copper core by mixing 26 mg copper organic compound, 0-180 mu l diphenyl ether and 9 ml oleylamine in a three-necked flask, placing in 100 degrees C oil bath, using a vacuum exhaust system to completely exhaust oxygen in the system, feeding carbon monoxide gas at a flow rate of 90 ml/minute, increasing the reaction temperature to 220 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 10 degrees C/6 minutes, maintaining at 220 degrees C for 2 hours, and cooling to 30 degrees C, and injecting 5 ml oleylamine solution of silver organic compound having a concentration of 1.1 mg/ml at a flow rate of 0.5 ml/hour into the copper core using a peristaltic pump, continuously introducing carbon monoxide, maintaining the temperature of the reaction system at 30 degrees C, adding the silver organic compound, continuously reacting for 1 hour, cooling the reaction product to room temperature, washing the reaction product three times with n-hexane by ultrasonically-centrifuging at 10000 rpm, and dispersing the obtained product in n-hexane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The copper organic compound is copper acetylacetonate (Cu(acac)2). The silver organic compound is silver trifluoroacetate (AgCF3COO).
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)

   YAN H
   WANG H
   MA Y
   LIU X
AE CINF ENG CO LTD (CINF-Non-standard)
   UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2020677691
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a waste power lithium battery with dry ice. An internal fixing reaction kettle is fixed with a crushing device to obtain mixed material. The mixed material is mixed and crushed by the crushing device. Temperature of the fixed kettle is adjusted to 40-60 degrees centigrade to obtain mixed liquid.    USE - The catalyst is useful for decomposing radish sludge (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst completely decomposes organic matter of radish sludge into water and carbon dioxide, and shortens treatment time, and reduces transportation and storage costs, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for decomposing radish sludge, comprising mixing 45-55 wt.% catalyst with 45-55 wt.% sludge and maintaining temperature of 60-100 degrees C to decompose organic sludge into water and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The enzyme-activated water comprises 50-70 wt.% enzyme including diastases, amylase, amyloglucosidase, trypsin, pepsin, rennet, papain, protease, glucose isomerase, lipase, cellulase and/or pectinase, 15-25 wt.% water and 15-25 wt.% sawdust. Preferred Method: The porous kaolin is obtained by mixing kaolin with water in weight ratio of 1:1, reacting for 20-30 hours, and rapidly cooling at -75 degrees C to -85 degrees C when water is saturated in inter-laminar structure and pores, creating new pores as moisture expands or expanding existing pores. The microbial agent is mixture of cocoite having water content of 38-43%, and obtained by inoculating Aerobacter, Bacillus, Pseudomonas, Rhodopseudomonas, Nitrosomonas, and/or Nitrobacter in petri-dish in incubator at 43-47 degrees C for 12 hours, inoculating the cultured strain into nutrient broth, incubating at 200-250 revolutions/minute at 43-47 degrees C for 6-10 hours in shaker incubator, and centrifuging at 3500-4500 revolutions/minute for 20-40 minutes to obtain microbial strain.
   EXAMPLE - Typical environmentally-friendly catalyst comprised of 50 wt.% degrading enzyme, 25 wt.% water and 25 wt.% sawdust.

GA 2019961298

Preferred Method: The preparation of chitosan oligosaccharide quaternary ammonium Schiff base comprises dissolving chitooligosaccharide and glycidyl trimethylammonium chloride in water and heating and stirring, adding solution of benzaldehyde in methanol dropwise and stirring is continued; or dissolving 6 pts. wt. chitosan oligosaccharide and 9 pts. wt. glycidyl trimethylammonium chloride in 150 pts. wt. water, heating to 50 degrees C, stirring for 30 minutes, heating to 80 degrees C for 10 hours, adding mixed solution of 5 pts. wt. benzaldehyde and 10 pts. wt. methanol dropwise, stirring for 24 hours and purifying. In the step (a) the heating temperature is 50-60 degrees C. In the step (b) continuously stirring for 0.5-1 hour at a stirring speed of 800-1000 revolutions/minute, allowing the mixture to stand and cooling to room temperature.

AE CHEN D (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201942895L
AB    NOVELTY - Foamed polyethylene terephthalate film comprises repeating A/B structures, where the layer-A includes 99-99.5 pts. wt. polyethylene terephthalate resin and 0.5-1 pts. wt. chain extender. The layer-B includes 100 pts. wt. polyethylene terephthalate resin with supercritical carbon dioxide at 40 degrees C/8 MPa to 45 degrees C/10 MPa.
   USE - Used as a foamed polyethylene terephthalate film.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed polyethylene terephthalate film has good heat-insulating property, uniform thickness, heat-resistant, good flexibility and is suitable for economical continuous mass production.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The upper and lower surfaces of foamed polyethylene terephthalate film are A-layers. The number of A-layer is one more than the number of B-layer. The foamed polyethylene terephthalate film is a microlayer film having 101-201 layers. The A-layer polyethylene terephthalate resin has intrinsic viscosity of 0.65-0.82 dl/g. The B-layer polyethylene terephthalate resin has intrinsic viscosity of 0.75-1.15 dl/g. The chain extender is phthalic anhydride, pyromellitic anhydride or naphthalene tetraacetic anhydride. Preferred Method: The A-layer extruder has first zone temperature of 220-245 degrees C, second zone temperature of 250-255 degrees C, third zone temperature of 255-260 degrees C, fourth zone temperature of 255-260 degrees C, fifth zone temperature of 260-265 degrees C and filter temperature of 260-265 degrees C. The B-layer extruder has first zone temperature of 220-245 degrees C, second zone temperature of 235-245 degrees C, third zone temperature of 240-245 degrees C, fourth zone temperature of 245-250 degrees C, fifth zone temperature of 245-250 degrees C and filter temperature of 250-255 degrees C. The microlayer dispenser temperature used for microlayer co-extrusion is 260-265 degrees C. The microlayer dispenser has flow path gap of not greater than 0.3 mu m, and the corresponding gap of the A layer is three quarters to one quarter of the B-layer. The microlayer co-extrusion has mold temperature of 250-260 degrees C and slab temperature of 25-35 degrees C.

GA 2019205669

PN CN109354049-A; CN109354049-B
TI Preparation of intercalated hydrotalcite stabilizer used for resin, involves mixing solid magnesium source and aluminum source, adding water to obtain reaction liquid, reacting, pumping carbon dioxide, heating, and cooling.
AU DAI B
   ZHANG S
   GONG T
   LIU C
   LIU Y
   HAN L
   LUO B
   CAO H
AE HUBEI BENXING NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 201920677E
AB    NOVELTY - An intercalated hydrotalcite stabilizer is prepared by mixing 2:1 mass ratio of solid magnesium source and aluminum source, adding 3-4 times water to obtain reaction liquid, reacting at rate of 150-200 revolutions/minute at 90-100 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, pumping 50-70% carbon dioxide, heating at 150-200 degrees C for 5-10 hours, cooling to 90 degrees C, adding 4-10 times stearic acid or stearate, reacting for 1-2 hours, obtaining crude solid product, filtering, and drying to obtain solid crude product, taking filtrate, and repeatedly using to prepare reaction solution.
   USE - Method for preparing intercalated hydrotalcite stabilizer used for resin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has low reaction temperature and environment-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The intercalated hydrotalcite stabilizer is prepared by mixing 2:1 mass ratio of solid magnesium source comprising magnesium hydroxide and magnesium oxide, and aluminum hydroxide, aluminum source, obtaining 20 wt.% suspension according to mol ratio of 1-2:3-2:2, adding 3-4 times water, shearing into 300 nm particle diameter D50. taking as reaction liquid, reacting at rate of 150-200 revolutions/minute at 90-100 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, pumping 50-70% carbon dioxide, heating at 170-180 degrees C for 8 hours, cooling to 90 degrees C, adding 4-10 times stearic acid or stearate, reacting for 1-2 hours, obtaining crude solid product, filtering, and drying to obtain solid crude product, taking filtrate, and repeatedly using to prepare reaction solution.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Capturing acid gas e.g. carbon dioxide involves            contacting acid gas with amino acid-free capture            composition comprising salt(s) of carboxylic acid.
AU BARNES D
   SCHOOLDERMAN C
   JAKAB G
   RAYNER C
   JAKOB G
   REINER C
AE C-CAPTURE LTD (CCAP-Non-standard)
   C-CAPTURE LTD (CCAP-Non-standard)
   C-CAPTURE LTD (CCAP-Non-standard)
GA 2019139380
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing acid gas in a composition involves                contacting the acid gas with a capture composition.                The capture composition comprises salt(s) of                carboxylic acid dissolved in a solvent system                comprising water, and is free of amino acids or its                salt(s).
   USE - Capturing acid gas e.g. carbon dioxide gas                (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to                power stations, cement, manufacture, iron and steel                manufacture, glass making, brewing, syngas                processes, natural and biogas purification and                other chemical processes such as ammonia                production, hydrogen production, power-to-gas (e.g.                Sabatier reaction) as well as any other acid gas                producing industrial, commercial or domestic                processes, smaller scale specialist such as                submarines, spacecraft, natural gas sweetening,                biogas upgrading and desulfurization.
   ADVANTAGE - The capturing method is very simple,                economical , has excellent capture efficiency, uses                less hazardous reagents, and reduces emissions and                degradation issues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The base is                  carbonate, optionally lithium, potassium, sodium,                  magnesium and/or calcium carbonate. The                  carboxylic acid is metal salt, preferably                  potassium salt. Preferred Property: The acid gas                  capture occurs at 60-100 degrees C and 0-1                  bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The salt of                  carboxylic salt is single salt of carboxylic                  acid. The single salt is salt of 1-4C or 5-6C                  aliphatic carboxylic acids, which are optionally                  straight chained or branched. The composition                  further comprises base additive which is a salt                  of phenol optionally substituted by halo, 1-4C                  alkyl, nitro, cyano, 1-4C -haloalkyl,                  O-1-4Chaloalkyl or additional aromatic rings to                  make polycyclic phenol. Preferred Property: The                  concentration of single salt is greater than                  6M.

PT P
PN CN109304134-A
TI Preparation of alumina-zirconia aerogel composite material involves pouring zirconia sol containing alcohol solvent into mold, gelating, aging, soaking fiber composite in alcohol solvent and introducing carbon dioxide to remove solvent.
AU HUANG J
AE ZHANGJIAGANG MUHE NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLO (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201916113G
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of alumina-zirconia aerogel composite material involves pouring zirconia sol containing alcohol solvent into mold containing alumina fiber, sealing, gelating at room temperature, aging for 24-48 hours in water bath at 70-80 degrees C, and drying to obtain alcohol gel/alumina/zirconia fiber composite, placing alcohol gel/alumina/zirconia fiber composite into supercritical dryer, soaking fiber composite in alcohol solvent, introducing excess liquid carbon dioxide at 4-8 degrees C to remove alcohol solvent and water, adjusting the temperature and pressure conditions in the supercritical dryer to 32-36 degrees C and 7.5x 106-8.0x 106 Pa.
   USE - Preparation of alumina-zirconia aerogel composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of alumina-zirconia aerogel composite material with nanoporous network structure, high heat resistance and high strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The zirconia sol is prepared by mixing zirconium salt, alcohol solvent and deionized water in amount ratio of (0.1-1):(1-10):(5-15) and magnetically stirring for 24 hours, and leaving still. The alumina fiber is prepared by taking a certain amount of crystalline aluminum chloride, adding deionized water, and uniformly mixing to obtain aluminum chloride solution, adding aluminum powder to aluminum chloride solution at ratio of (1:2)-(1:4), stirring, and heating and refluxing in constant temperature water bath at 80-100 degrees C for 20 hours to colloidal mother liquor, adding fiber forming additive to the colloidal mother liquor, stirring uniformly and concentrating at constant temperature of 40-100 degrees C for a certain time to obtain alumina sol, fiberizing alumina sol at room temperature and drying to obtain alumina gel fiber and heat-treating alumina gel fiber at 1200-1400 degrees C to obtain an alumina fiber. Drying is carried out by supercritical drying using carbon dioxide or ethanol as drying medium. Preferred Components: The zirconium salt is zirconium oxychloride octahydrate or zirconia dinitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol or n-butanol.

PT P
PN CN109135030-A; CN109135030-B
TI Polyolefin photoquantum gas permeable membrane            comprises polyolefin resin mixture, inorganic filler            and auxiliary agent, the inorganic filler includes            photo quantum material.
AU SONG Y
   XU J
AE CHENGDU YUNDUANZHILIAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201907264H
AB    NOVELTY - Polyolefin photoquantum gas permeable membrane                comprises 35-50 wt.% polyolefin resin mixture,                45-60 wt.% inorganic filler and 3-5 wt.% auxiliary                agent, the inorganic filler includes a photo                quantum material.
   USE - Used as polyolefin photoquantum gas permeable                membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The permeable membrane: has excellent                biological activity; promotes the diffusion of                oxygen and carbon dioxide, which dilutes the carbon                dioxide produced by the plant respiration; reduces                propagation of anaerobic bacteria and plant                spoilage; delays spoilage and realizes high quality                fresh-keeping effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing Polyolefin photoquantum gas permeable                membrane, comprising mixing inorganic filler,                polyolefin resin mixture and auxiliary agent to                obtain granular master batch, dehumidifying and                drying the granular master batch, subjecting to                melt plastication and extrusion, film casting or                film forming, preheating, stretching, cooling                setting, corona treatment, detecting thickness,                winding and trimming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyolefin resin                  mixture includes 85-90 wt.% linear low density                  polyethylene, 6-9 wt.% high density polyethylene,                  and 4-6 wt.% polypropylene. The inorganic filler                  includes 20-40 wt.% light quantum powder, 40-55                  wt.% inorganic oxide, and 20-40 wt.% inorganic                  acid salt. The photon quantum material is a                  graphene photo quantum dot. The inorganic oxide                  is silica, titanium dioxide, alumina, and/or                  zirconium dioxide, the inorganic oxide has                  particle diameter of 20-100 nm. The inorganic                  acid salt is calcium silicate, calcium carbonate,                  potassium aluminum phosphate, and/or barium                  sulfate, the inorganic acid salt has particle                  size of less than 2,500 mesh. The auxiliary agent                  includes 40-60 wt.% coupling agent, 20-30 wt.%                  dispersant, 10-15 wt.% antioxidant, and 10-15                  wt.% processing auxiliary agent. The coupling                  agent is aluminum lanthanum organometallic                  complex coupling agent or rare earth coupling                  agent, the dispersing agent includes 40-50 wt.%                  oxidized polyethylene wax, 25-30 wt.% higher                  alcohol, and 25-30 wt.% polyethylene wax, the                  antioxidant is thioester antioxidant or                  pentaerythritol ester antioxidant, the processing                  auxiliary agent uses stearic acid processing                  auxiliary agent. Preferred Method: The granular                  master batch preparation comprises (1) adding                  inorganic filler to the high-mixer at speed of                  400-600 revolutions/minute for 15-20 minutes, and                  heating to 100-105 degrees C, (2) adding coupling                  agent mixed coating treatment for 10-15 minutes,                  adding dispersing agent to stir at speed of                  400-600 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes, and                  raising the temperature to 130 degrees C, (3)                  adding polyolefin resin mixture, antioxidant, and                  processing auxiliary agent, stirring and mixing                  until the paste is formed, and (4) placing in an                  extrusion granulator, melt extruding by screw                  extrusion, plasticized granulating, hot cutting,                  and air cooling to form granular master                  batch.

PN CN109053002-A
TI Preparation of highly hydrated active silicon bead involves adding aqueous glass alkali slag and polycrystalline silicon powder waste to vertical mill, supplying carbon dioxide, reacting, and controlling fineness of resultant product.
AU CAI L
   CAI M
   LI K
   XU D
   LU X
AE JIANGSU HENGDA HIGH PERFORMANCE MATERIALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201902282P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of highly hydrated active silicon bead involves adding aqueous glass alkali slag and polycrystalline silicon powder waste in a weight ratio of 3-4:1 to vertical mill at blowing temperature of 500-600 degrees C, supplying 15-20 vol.% carbon dioxide, reacting, converting sodium silicate hydrate in the slag into sodium carbonate and silica, crystallizing, decomposing hydrogen peroxide in the waste into water and oxygen, grinding, obtaining alkali slag with particle size of 10-30 mu m, and silicon powder waste with particle size of 1-3 mu m, combining alkali slag and silicon powder waste, and controlling fineness of resultant to 45 mu m.
   USE - Preparation of highly hydrated active silicon bead.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient preparation of highly hydrated active silicon bead.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The silica beads are added to cement in an amount of 10-20 wt.%.

PT P
PN CN108935971-A
TI Preparing feed containing chitosan oligosaccharides comprises e.g. crushing shrimp head, adding deionized water, passing carbon dioxide, heating, concentrating in vacuum concentrator, inoculating microorganisms, stirring and pulverizing.
AU MAO X
   SUN J
   ZHAO T
   XUE C
AE UNIV CHINA OCEAN (UYOC-C)
GA 2018A0802W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing feed containing chitosan oligosaccharides comprises (i) drying, crushing shrimp head, adding deionized water, uniformly mixing to prepare homogenate, and storing in steel reactor, (ii) passing carbon dioxide into the steel reactor, sealing, and heating the steel reactor in an oil bath, (iii) taking the heated shrimp head homogenate, and concentrating in a vacuum concentrator, (iv) sterilizing the concentrated shrimp head material at 121 degrees C for 15-30 minutes, inoculating the microorganisms for fermentation, and (v) adding 0.5-5 times deionized water of the fermented material into a stirrer, stirring, drying and pulverizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing feed containing chitosan oligosaccharides.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids using a large amount of acid and alkali in the process, and is economical.

PN DE102018200027-A1; KR2018079925-A; US2018185792-A1; KR1927889-B1; US10537855-B2
TI Preparing decadodecasil 3R type zeolite membrane, comprises depositing zeolite particles on substrate to form seed layer, preparing hydrophobic membrane by adding substrate to synthetic precursor and performing hydrothermal synthesis.
AU CHOI J
   LEE K Y
   KIM E
   KIM E J
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2018524169
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a decadodecasil 3R (DDR) type zeolite membrane, comprises: (a) forming a seed layer by depositing DDR type zeolite particles on a porous substrate; and (b) preparing a hydrophobic membrane by adding the substrate on which the seed layer is formed to a silica source synthetic precursor containing a methyltropinium salt and performing a hydrothermal synthesis.
   USE - The DDR type zeolite membrane is useful for separating carbon dioxide from a mixture of a small molecule comprising methane, nitrogen, oxygen, ethene, ethane, propene, and propane, and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces hydrophobic DDR zeolite membrane with high reproducibility and high separation performance under moisture-containing feed conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for DDR type zeolite membrane prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic membrane has out-of-plane orientation. The methyltropinium salt includes methyltropinium iodide, methyltropinium fluoride, methyltropinium chloride, methyltropinium bromide and methyltropinium hydroxide. The silica source synthetic precursor comprises silicon dioxide, methyltropinium salt, sodium hydroxide and water at a molar ratio of 100:1-1000:0-1000:10-100000. The porous substrate is made of alumina, polypropylene, polyethylene, polytetrafluoroethylene, polysulfone, polyimide, silica, glass, mullite, zirconia, titania, yttria, ceria, vanadia, silicon, stainless steel and carbon. Preferred Method: The method, after step (b), further comprising a step (c) of activating the membrane by calcining the membrane at 300-900 degrees C for 1-240 hours. The step (b) is performed at 70-250 degrees C for 1-960 hours. The DDR type zeolite particle in the step (a) is obtained by adding DDR particles obtained by a non-seeded growth method to a synthetic precursor containing 1-adamantanamine, ethylenediamine, silicon dioxide and water at a ratio of 1-100:10-1000:100:100-100000 and performing hydrothermal synthesis at 100-200 degrees C for 1-240 hours.
   EXAMPLE - DDR zeolite particles (obtained by adding adamantylamine to ethylenediamine and mixing with each other, additionally performing sonication for 1 hour, adding the solution to deionized water and CAB-O-SIL (RTM: Fumed silicas), adding the synthetic precursor prepared to silicon oil bath and heating to 95 degrees C, stirred for 1 hour. Then performing hydrothermal synthesis at 160 degrees C for 4 days, performing vacuum filtration, and synthesizing DDR zeolite particles) were deposited on a porous alpha -alumina disc. Then 0.05 g sintered DDR zeolite particles were added to 40 ml ethanol, to obtain a coating dispersion. The coating dispersion was contacted with a polished side of alpha -alumina disc for about 30 seconds, the disc was taken out and allowed to dry for 30 seconds. The process was repeated about 4 times, DDR zeolite particles were attached to a surface of the alumina disc. The alpha alumina disc was put into the synthetic precursor containing methyltropinium iodide (MTI) and hydrothermal synthesis was performed, and the hydrophobic DDR zeolite membrane having a continuous out-of-plane orientation was synthesized.

PN WO2018107450-A1
TI Producing propiolactone compound electrochemically comprises reacting first reactant having ethylenically unsaturated carbon-carbon double bond with second reactant being carbon dioxide in presence of electrolyte, solvent and catalyst.
AU SCHWIEDERNOCH R
   MIGNANI G
   WU M
   LIEBENS A T
AE RHODIA OPERATIONS (RHOD-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 201849428R
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical process for producing a propiolactone compound comprises reacting a first reactant being a compound having at least one ethylenically unsaturated carbon-carbon double bond, with a second reactant being carbon dioxide, in the presence of an electrolyte, a solvent and a catalyst. The catalyst comprises metal clusters comprising at least one metal element in elemental form. The metal element comprises elements of group IA, IIA, IIIA, IVA, VA, VIA or VIIA, elements of groups IB, IIB, IIIB, IVB, VB, VIB, VIIB or VIIIB, lanthanides and/or actinides.
   USE - The process is useful for electrochemical process for producing a propiolactone compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is one-step simple electrochemical process for producing propiolactone compounds with desired characteristics e.g. inexpensiveness, high selectivity and conversion, and ease of handling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises metal clusters comprising at least one metal element in elemental form, where the metal element is nickel, cobalt, tin, iron, aluminum, chromium, platinum, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, iridium, silver, gold, cerium, bismuth, manganese, rhenium or copper. The average particle diameter of metal clusters is 1.5-3 nm. The electrolyte is alkali metal salt, alkaline earth metal salt or alkylammonium salt. The cathode of electrochemical reactor comprises at least one element comprising carbon, iron, silver, nickel, ruthenium, iridium, osmium, manganese, lanthanum, cobalt and/or cerium. The anode of electrochemical reactor comprises at least one element comprising carbon, iron, palladium, platinum, ruthenium, gold, rhodium, iridium, bismuth, tin and/or boron.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In process, a stabilizer comprising tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide, tetrahydrothiophene, alkylammonium salts e.g. tetraalkylammonium halides, tetraalkylammonium perchlorates or tetraalkylammonium tetrafluoroborate is further employed. The potential to produce propiolactone compound is -1 to -3V. The current density to produce propiolactone compound is -1 to -5 mA/cm2. Preferred Components: The first reactant is a substituted alkylene compound of formula (R1-C(R2)=C(R3)-R4) (I) and the propiolactone compound produced is a substituted cyclic compound of formula (II) or (III), or the first reactant is a substituted diene compound of formula (R5-C(R6)=C(R7)-C(R8)=C(R9)-R10) (IV) and the propiolactone compound produced is a substituted alkylene substituted-cyclic compound of formula (V) or (VI).R1-R4 = H or 1-11C hydrocarbyl, preferably H, 1-6C alkyl, 2-6C alkenyl or phenyl, where at least one of R1-R4 in (I) is linear 1-4C alkyl; andR5-R10 = H or 1-11C hydrocarbyl, where at least one of R5-R10 in (IV) is linear 1-4C alkyl.The first reactant is 1,3-butadiene, 2,3-dimethyl-1,3-butadiene, isoprene, myrcene, styrene, alpha -methyl styrene or 1-pheny-1,3-butadiene. The propiolactone compound produced is 3-methyl-3-(prop-1-en-2-yl)oxetan-2-one, 3-(4-methylpent-3-en-1-yl)-3-vinyloxetan-2-one, 3-(6-methylhepta-1,5-dien-2-yl)oxetan-2-one, 3,3-dimethyl-4-(3-methylenepent-4-en-1-yl)oxetan-2-one, 3-vinyloxetan-2-one, 3-methyl-3-vinyloxetan-2-one, 3-(prop-1-en-2-yl)oxetan-2-one, 3-methyl-3-phenyloxetan-2-one, 3-styryloxetan-2-one or 4-phenyl-3-vinyloxetan-2-one. The solvent comprises water, methanol, ethanol, propanol, acetone, acetonitrile, acetic acid, tetrahydrofuran, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethylsulfoxide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethyl carbonate, nitromethane, propylene carbonate, ethylene carbonate, tetraalkylammonium halides, tetraalkylammonium perchlorates or tetraalkylammonium tetrafluoroborates.
   EXAMPLE - Propiolactone synthesis was carried out using nickel catalyst followed directly by applying a potential of -2V at a current density of -2.3 mA/cm2 for 95 hours. After synthesis, dimethylformamide was removed under vacuum. The residue was acidified with 2M hydrochloric acid for 6 hours and the product was extracted with dimethyl ether and dried over sodium sulfate. The products (propiolactone and hex-3-enedioic acid) were isolated by removing diethyl ether.
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate, by mixing calcium sulfate, water and ammonium bicarbonate solution, crushing, adding calcium oxide powder to vessel with supercritical carbon dioxide, ultrasonically processing and collecting solid.
AU JIANG Y
AE JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 2018503691
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate involves mixing calcium sulfate and water, adding ammonium bicarbonate solution, heating at 15-35 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, filtering, heating filtrate at 80-90 degrees C for 2-4 hours, washing filtrate, drying, calcining at 650-750 degrees C for 1-2 hours, crushing, sieving through 300-350-mesh sieve to obtain calcium oxide powder, adding calcium oxide powder to a vessel containing supercritical carbon dioxide, ultrasonically processing at 45-55 degrees C and pressure of 75-80 atm for 60-120 minutes, connecting vessel to dust collecting device and collecting solid.
   USE - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of nano-calcium carbonate with improved dispersibility and purity and low agglomeration index and impurity content.

TI Fish cooling plant on the fishing vessels.
AU NOVIKOV E V
   NEVEROV E N
AE UNIV KEMEROVO (UYKE-Non-standard)
GA 201855679N
AB    NOVELTY - Present invention relates to refrigerating equipment, to the fish cooling plants on the fishing vessels. Fish cooling plants on the fishing vessels includes fish receiving devices, fish unloading devices, carbon dioxide circulation circuit, heat insulated chamber, conveyor for the fish moving along the chamber, equipped with drive from the electric motor with the possibility of the rotation speed adjustment. Heat insulated chamber is equipped with a distribution manifold with nozzles for the carbon dioxide supply to the located on the conveyor fish, at that, the tight contact between the product individual parts on the conveyor is provided by pre-pressing with the roller, which height relative to the conveyor is preset by means of an adjustment bar. Fish freezing process occurs in a short period of time, increasing the plant productivity.
   USE - Refrigerating equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical solution allows to improve the fish quality, to reduce the number of auxiliary equipment and to optimize the cooling agent consumption.

PN CN107720014-A
TI Green vegetable fresh-keeping storage method, involves dipping washed vegetables into ozone-containing water, spraying water on vegetables in carriage, and inputting carbon dioxide gas into carriage for reducing oxygen content in carriage.
AU LEE S
AE MEISHAN DONGPO DEXIANG VEGETABLES PROFESSIONAL COOP (MEIS-Non-standard)
GA 2018179468
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sorting harvested vegetables. Damaged harvested vegetables are removed. Vegetable roots are cut by using a high-temperature-sterilized cutter. The vegetables are soaked into salt water, where temperature of the salt water is measured for about 30 to 35 degrees centigrade. Washed vegetables are dipped into ozone-containing water. Bundles of the vegetables are suspended. Water is sprayed on the vegetables in a carriage, where temperature in the carriage is about 2 to 8 degrees centigrade. Carbon dioxide gas is input into the carriage to reduce oxygen content in the carriage.
   USE - Green vegetable fresh-keeping storage method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables prolonging green vegetable refreshing time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a harvested green vegetable.

AB    NOVELTY - A system for recovering sulfur from acid gas feed comprises Claus process (100) which converts a combined Claus feed (74) to produce a Claus outlet gas stream (10) and a recovered sulfur stream (12) where combined Claus feed includes acid gas feed (2) and absorption process outlet stream where acid gas feed includes hydrogen sulfide such that acid gas feed has hydrogen sulfide concentration, absorption process outlet stream includes sulfur dioxide, Claus outlet gas stream includes sulfur-containing compounds, hydrogen sulfide, and sulfur dioxide and recovered sulfur stream includes elemental sulfur; thermal oxidizer (200) which converts sulfur-containing compounds and hydrogen sulfide in Claus outlet gas stream to sulfur dioxide to produce a thermal oxidizer outlet stream, and includes sulfur dioxide and water vapor; and gas treatment unit (350) which cools thermal oxidizer outlet stream to produce a process condensed water stream and dehydrated stream containing sulfur dioxide.
   USE - A system for recovering sulfur from acid gas feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can improve the capability of sulfur dioxide (SO2)-selective absorption process and can reduce the costs to build and operate, improving the overall economics of sulfur recovery system. Sulfur recovery from Claus unit is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system further includes membrane sweeping unit which is connected to gas treatment unit, separates sulfur dioxide in dehydrated stream to produce a sweep membrane residue stream and sulfur dioxide enriched air feed, and includes membrane where sulfur dioxide permeates through where sweep membrane residue stream includes sulfur dioxide; and sulfur dioxide absorption process which is connected to the membrane sweeping unit, produces absorption process outlet stream and stack feed (70) and separates sulfur dioxide from the sweep membrane residue stream (60), where stack feed is below an allowable sulfur dioxide emission limit. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system for recovering sulfur from acid gas feed (2) comprising Claus process (100) which converts combined Claus feed (74) to produce Claus outlet gas stream (10) and recovered sulfur stream (12) and includes acid gas feed and absorption process outlet stream where acid gas feed comprises hydrogen sulfide such that acid gas feed has hydrogen sulfide concentration, absorption process outlet stream comprises sulfur dioxide, Claus outlet gas stream comprises sulfur-containing compounds, hydrogen sulfide, and sulfur dioxide, recovered sulfur stream comprises elemental sulfur; thermal oxidizer (200) which is connected to Claus process, converts sulfur-containing compounds and hydrogen sulfide in Claus outlet gas stream to sulfur dioxide to produce a thermal oxidizer outlet stream having sulfur dioxide and water vapor; gas treatment unit (350) which is connected to thermal oxidizer that cools thermal oxidizer outlet stream to produce a process condensed water stream and dehydrated stream where dehydrated stream comprises sulfur dioxide; membrane sweeping unit (600) which is connected to gas treatment unit, separates sulfur dioxide from sweep membrane feed to produce a sweep membrane residue stream and sulfur dioxide enriched air feed, where sweep membrane feed is separated from dehydrated stream, membrane sweeping unit comprises a membrane in which sulfur dioxide permeates through; pressure differential driven membrane unit which is connected to gas treatment unit and separates sulfur dioxide from pressure differential driven membrane feed to produce a pressure driven residue stream and pressure driven permeate recycle stream where pressure differential driven membrane feed is separated from the dehydrated stream, pressure differential driven membrane unit comprises pressure driven membrane, pressure driven membrane has a permeate side and feed side; recycle pressure treatment unit which is connected to pressure differential driven membrane unit and increases the permeate pressure of the pressure driven permeate recycle stream to above atmospheric pressure; and sulfur dioxide absorption process (700) which is connected to membrane sweeping unit and pressure differential driven membrane feed and produces absorption process outlet stream and stack feed and separates sulfur dioxide from residue stream, where residue stream comprises sweep membrane residue stream and pressure driven residue stream and stack feed is below an allowable sulfur dioxide emission limit; and
   (2) system for recovering sulfur from acid gas comprising feed treatment unit which condenses water vapor in acid gas feed to produce feed recovered water stream and carbon dioxide membrane feed where carbon dioxide membrane feed comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, acid gas feed has hydrogen sulfide concentration; carbon dioxide membrane unit which is connected to feed treatment unit, separates carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide membrane feed to produce a carbon dioxide permeate and a hydrogen sulfide retentate, comprises a carbon dioxide-selective membrane; feed pressure treatment unit which is connected to carbon dioxide membrane unit, increases carbon dioxide permeate pressure of carbon dioxide permeate to produce a carbon dioxide enriched feed; Claus process which is connected to feed pressure treatment unit, converts enriched acid gas stream to produce a Claus outlet gas stream and recovered sulfur stream where enriched acid gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide retentate and absorption process outlet stream and absorption process outlet stream comprises sulfur dioxide, Claus outlet gas stream comprises sulfur-containing compounds, hydrogen sulfide, and sulfur dioxide; gas treatment unit which is connected to thermal oxidizer that cools thermal oxidizer outlet stream to produce process condensed water stream and dehydrated stream; membrane sweeping unit which is connected to gas treatment unit and includes membrane where sulfur dioxide permeates through; and sulfur dioxide absorption process which is connected to membrane sweeping unit, produces absorption process outlet stream and a stack feed, and separates sulfur dioxide from sweep membrane residue stream, where stack feed is below an allowable sulfur dioxide emission limit.

PN CN114291958-A; CN216639187-U
TI Treatment system for salty wastewater, has first nanofiltration unit, connected to waste water pretreatment unit, second freezing crystallization unit is respectively connected with first nanofiltration unit, second nanofiltration unit, ammonium sulfate evaporation and crystallization unit.
AU FANG Z
   HU J
AE MEIJING BEIJING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (MEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202253965Y
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment system for salty wastewater, has wastewater pretreatment unit (30), first nanofiltration unit (31), connected to the waste water pretreatment unit, second freezing crystallization unit (36) is respectively connected with the first nanofiltration unit, the second nanofiltration unit (38), the ammonium sulfate evaporation and crystallization unit, and the ammonium chloride evaporation and crystallization unit (43, and is used to intercept the nanofiltration of the first sodium filtration unit. Second nanofiltration unit is connected to the second freezing and crystallization unit. The nanofiltration retentate is returned to the second freezing and crystallization unit and/or or the wastewater pretreatment unit. The ammonium chloride evaporation and crystallization unit is connected with the second nanofiltration unit. Ammonium nitrate freezing crystallization unit is connected with the ammonium chloride evaporative crystallization unit.
   USE - Treatment system for salty wastewater and waste gas containing carbon dioxide (Claimed). Can also be used in producing sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - Organically combined with carbon dioxide (CO2) emission reduction, absorb CO2 from exhaust gas, reduce CO2 emissions, and which has significant environmental and economic benefits. The system and method treat high-concentration brine, and recovering sulfate, chloride and nitrate in wastewater in a targeted manner, without generating unusable mixed salt, and obtaining high-quality additional products, realizing resource treatment of salty wastewater without secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) Treatment method of salty wastewater, which involves: i. wastewater pretreatment step is to pretreat the salty wastewater to remove organic matter, suspended solids and impurities therein, and to reduce the hardness, in the first nanofiltration step, nanofiltration is performed on the effluent of the wastewater pretreatment step; ii. in the second freezing crystallization step, the nanofiltration retentate of the first sodium filtration step, the crystallization mother liquor of the ammonium sulfate evaporative crystallization step, the nanofiltration retentate of the second nanofiltration step, and the ammonium chloride evaporated crystallization mother liquor of the crystallization step is subjected to freezing crystallization treatment and sodium sulfate crystals are recovered and the effluent is discharged to the second nanofiltration step at the same time; iii. in the second nanofiltration step, nanofiltration is performed on the effluent of the second freezing and crystallization step, returning the nanofiltration retentate to the second freeze crystallization step and/or pretreatment step; iv. ammonium chloride evaporation crystallization step, evaporative crystallization is carried out to the effluent of the second nanofiltration step, ammonium chloride crystals are recovered, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously and ammonium nitrate freezing crystallization step, adding demineralized water to the evaporative crystallization mother liquor of the ammonium chloride evaporative crystallization step and carrying out freezing crystallization treatment, recovering ammonium nitrate crystals, and returning the effluent to the ammonium chloride evaporation and crystallization step at the same time.
   (2) Treatment system for waste gas containing carbon dioxide.
   (3) Method for treating waste gas containing carbon dioxide (CO2), which involves: i. subjecting waste gas containing CO2 to CO2 absorption treatment, and at the same time, the excess of CO2 is ensured; ii. the tail gas of the first absorption step is again subjected to CO2 absorption treatment with ammonia water and at the same time, transporting part of the absorption circulating solution to the first absorption step; iii. subjecting the tail gas of the second absorption step to washing treatment, and discharging the washing solution to the outside at the same time; iv. using demineralized water to wash the tail gas of the first tail gas washing step, and the washing circulation solution is transported to the first tail gas washing step; v. subjecting the sodium bicarbonate slurry formed in the first absorption step to centrifugal separation treatment, and recovering the sodium bicarbonate crystals, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously; vi. in the first freezing crystallization step, subjecting the centrifugal mother liquor of the sodium bicarbonate centrifugal separation step to freezing crystallization treatment, and recovering the mixed crystals of sodium bicarbonate and ammonium bicarbonate, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously; vii. performing evaporative crystallization process on the frozen crystallization mother liquor of the first frozen crystallization step, the ammonium sulfate crystals are recovered, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously; and viii. sodium bicarbonate and ammonium bicarbonate mixed crystals of the first freezing and crystallization step, the efflux washing solution of the first tail gas washing step, and preparing sodium sulfate crystals into solutions and sent to the first absorption within the steps.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the comprehensive utilization and treatment system for CO2-containing waste gas and salt-containing wastewater.
   Wastewater pretreatment unit (30)
   First nano-filtration unit (31)
   Second freezing crystallization unit (36)
   Second nano-filtration unit (38)
   Ammonium chloride evaporation and crystallization unit (43)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the first absorption step, the waste gas containing carbon dioxide (CO2) is subjected to CO2 absorption treatment, and at the same time, the excess of CO2 is ensured. In the second absorption step, the tail gas of the first absorption step is again subjected to CO2 absorption treatment with ammonia water, and at the same time, part of the absorption circulating solution is transported to the first absorption step. In the first tail gas washing step, the tail gas of the second absorption step is subjected to washing treatment, and the washing solution is discharged to the outside at the same time. In the second tail gas washing step, demineralized water is used to wash the tail gas of the first tail gas washing step, and the washing circulation solution is transported to the first tail gas washing step. In the sodium bicarbonate centrifugal separation step, the sodium bicarbonate slurry formed in the first absorption step is subjected to centrifugal separation treatment, and the sodium bicarbonate crystals are recovered, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously. In the first freezing crystallization step, the centrifugal mother liquor of the sodium bicarbonate centrifugal separation step is subjected to freezing crystallization treatment, and the mixed crystals of sodium bicarbonate and ammonium bicarbonate are recovered, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously. In the ammonium sulfate evaporation and crystallization step, the evaporative crystallization process is performed on the frozen crystallization mother liquor of the first frozen crystallization step, the ammonium sulfate crystals are recovered, and the mother liquor is discharged simultaneously. The sodium sulfate solution preparation step, the sodium bicarbonate and ammonium bicarbonate mixed crystals of the first freezing and crystallization step, the efflux washing solution of the first tail gas washing step, and the sodium sulfate crystals are prepared into solutions and sent to the first absorption within the steps. The temperatures of the first absorption step and the second absorption step are both 30-40 degreesC. The temperature of the first freezing crystallization step is 5-10 degreesC. The hardness of the effluent from the wastewater pretreatment step is less than 1 milligrams per liter (mg/L) (calculated as calcium carbonate), and the silt density index (SDI) is less than 3. Reverse osmosis concentration step, performing reverse osmosis desalination treatment on the permeated liquid of the nanofiltration membrane in the first nanofiltration step, and performing freeze crystallization treatment on the reverse osmosis concentrated water in the second freezing crystallization step. The temperature of the second freeze crystallization step is -5-5degreesC. The temperature of the freezing crystallization step of ammonium nitrate is 10-20 degreesC. The temperatures of the first absorption step and the second absorption step are both 30-40 degreesC, temperature of the first freezing crystallization step is 5-10 degreesC. Preferred Composition: In the ammonium nitrate freezing and crystallization step, the addition amount of the desalinated water is 40-120 wt.% of the flow rate of the evaporated mother liquor, more preferably 100-120 wt.%. The concentration of ammonium nitrate in the discharged mother liquor of the ammonium chloride evaporation and crystallization step is 1400-6000 grams per liter (g/L), preferably 4000-5000 g/L.

PT P
PN CN113584330-A
TI Recycling rhenium from carbon dioxide and oxygen leaching uranium leaching solution, by co-adsorbing uranium and rhenium in leaching solution with exchange resin, washing uranium and rhenium in resin, extracting rhenium, adding potassium chloride, and crystallizing to obtain potassium perrhenate.
AU WANG X
   ZHANG C
   DU Z
   YUAN Y
   YAO Y
   ZHENG J
   WEN Z
   XU G
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2021D1005A
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling rhenium from carbon dioxide and oxygen in-situ leaching uranium leaching solution involves: (1) introducing leaching solution containing uranium and rhenium into an ion exchange tower, and co-adsorbing uranium and rhenium with anion exchange resin; (2) washing uranium in resin; (3) transforming rhenium-containing resin obtained in step (2), and introducing adsorption tail liquid obtained in step (1) to obtain rhenium-carrying resin; (4) re-adsorbing uranium and rhenium to obtain rhenium-saturated resin; (5) pretreating rhenium-saturated resin; (6) leaching rhenium in resin to obtain a qualified rhenium solution and poor rhenium resin; (7) transforming poor rhenium resin; (8) extracting rhenium from qualified rhenium solution with an extractant; (9) reverse extracting rhenium to obtain a back extraction liquid; and (10) heating and evaporating back extraction liquid, and adding saturated potassium chloride to freeze and crystallize to obtain potassium perrhenate.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling rhenium from carbon dioxide and oxygen in-situ leaching uranium leaching solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the concentration of rhenium in the leaching solution by more than 30000 times, and ensures rhenium recovery rate of more than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling rhenium from carbon dioxide and oxygen in-situ leaching uranium leaching solution involves: (1) introducing leaching solution containing uranium and rhenium as an original adsorption solution into an ion exchange tower for a preset contact time, and co-adsorbing uranium and rhenium in the leaching solution with anion exchange resin, and stopping adsorption when the concentration of uranium in the adsorption tail liquid is basically the same as the concentration of uranium in the original adsorption solution, and the uranium on the resin has reached saturation; (2) washing uranium in resin by uranium leaching on the uranium saturated resin obtained in step (1), introducing a uranium leaching agent into the ion exchange tower for a preset contact time, leaching to obtain a qualified uranium solution and rhenium-carrying resin, eluting the uranium on the resin without eluting rhenium, and using the qualified uranium solution used to prepare a uranium product; (3) transforming the rhenium-containing resin obtained in step (2), and introducing a preset volume of the adsorption tail liquid obtained in step (1) to obtain rhenium-carrying resin after transformation; (4) re-adsorbing uranium and rhenium by repeating the steps (1)-(3) on the transformed rhenium-carrying resin obtained in step (3) until the concentration of rhenium in the adsorption tail liquid is basically the same as the concentration of rhenium in the original adsorption solution, and the rhenium has reached saturation, performing step (2) on the resin, and washing the uranium leaching agent between the resins with clear water to obtain a rhenium-saturated resin; (5) pretreating the rhenium-saturated resin by unloading the rhenium-saturated resin obtained in step (4) from the ion exchange tower to a tank, adding hydrochloric acid solution to fully react bicarbonate ion and carbonate ion adsorbed on the resin with an acid until no more bubbles are generated, and loading the treated rhenium saturated resin into the ion exchange tower; (6) leaching rhenium in resin by rhenium leaching on the treated rhenium-saturated resin obtained in step (5), introducing a rhenium leaching agent into the ion exchange tower according to a preset contact time, and leaching to obtain a qualified rhenium solution and a poor rhenium resin; (7) transforming the poor rhenium resin obtained in step (6), washing the rhenium eluent between the resins with clean water, and then introducing the adsorption tail liquid obtained in step (1), finally obtaining the transformed rhenium-poor resin, returning to the step (1) and continuously adsorbing; (8) extracting rhenium from the rhenium qualified solution obtained in step (6) with an extractant including amine extractant N235 (trialkylamine), a phase modifier including sec-octanol, and a diluent including sulfonated kerosene under preset conditions in an extraction mixing clarification tank to obtain a loaded organic phase; (9) reverse extracting rhenium from the loaded organic phase obtained in step (8), washing the loaded organic phase with clear water, then using a preset concentration of sodium hydroxide solution for back extraction, returning the poor organic phase to step (8) and continuously extracting to obtain a back extraction liquid; and (10) heating and evaporating the back extraction liquid obtained in step (9) until the volume of the liquid phase becomes small, and adding saturated potassium chloride to freeze and crystallize at a low temperature to obtain potassium perrhenate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The anion exchange resin in step (1) is strongly basic anion exchange resin D261.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of sodium chloride in the uranium eluent in step (2) is 50-100 g/L. The concentration of sodium bicarbonate is 5-20 g/L. The concentration of hydrochloric acid in step (5) is 10-20 g/L. The volume ratio of hydrochloric acid solution to resin is 1/2-2/1. The concentration of the back extraction liquid in step (9) is 0.5-4 mol/L.Preferred Components: The rhenium eluent in step (6) is 6-8 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume fraction of the extractant N235 (trialkylamine) in step (8) is 3-10%. The volume fraction of phase modifier is 1-5%.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC A04-C; A04-D09; A12-M03; A12-W12F; M25-G25; M25-G20; M25-G04; M25-B03; M25-A01

PT P
PN GB2585644-A
TI Producing syngas comprises e.g. providing gas mixture including recovered oxygen and carbon dioxide, providing gas mixture to gasifier for gasification of input fuel comprising feedstock to produce syngas, and cooling syngas.
AU MKUSHI G H M
AE LFEOG LTD (LFEO-Non-standard)
GA 202116462B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing syngas, comprises: (a) providing a gas mixture including oxygen and carbon dioxide recovered from the step (c); (b) providing the gas mixture to a gasifier for gasification of input fuel comprising feedstock to produce syngas; (c) cooling the syngas using all or a fraction of the recovered carbon dioxide in the step (d) also resulting in heated recovered carbon dioxide; (d) scrubbing the producer gas to remove carbon dioxide from the syngas and capturing carbon dioxide from the removed carbon dioxide; and repeating the steps (a)-(d), where in the step (c) the syngas is cooled using carbon dioxide as captured at the step (d), resulting in heated carbon dioxide. less than
   USE - The method is useful for producing syngas (claimed) from any carbonaceous feedstock (containing carbon) including hydrocarbonaceous feedstock (containing carbon and hydrogen) with carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen as bulk components.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safer than heating oxidizer gas mixtures including air or oxygen, because there is no safety consequence to a failure of the heat exchanger tubes between the syngas and the oxidizer when the oxidizer is carbon dioxide, and also safer than a heat recovery boiler because it can operate at lower pressures than an equivalent steam system and has lower capex than a steam system; minimizes the presence of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, condensable water and hydrocarbons (tars); improves performance of the oxidant stream through the presence of oxygen and carbon dioxide as oxidants; enables a smaller gasification reaction vessel; provides smaller equipment and reduces capital costs at the heat recovery stage; provides smaller equipment downstream of the system due to the lower concentration contaminant and the diluent gases reduces capital costs for subsequent plant equipment; and allows acceptance of hydrocarbonaceous waste and biomass derived feedstocks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for producing a syngas, comprising: a gas mixer for receiving a gas mixture comprising oxygen; a gasifier for gasification, using the gas mixture of input fuel comprising feedstock to produce syngas; means for cooling the syngas; a scrubber for scrubbing the syngas to remove carbon dioxide from the producer gas; means for capturing carbon dioxide from the removed carbon dioxide; and means for circulating the captured carbon dioxide to the means for cooling, where the means for cooling are configured to cool the syngas using the captured carbon dioxide, thus heating the captured carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b) the syngas is filtered to remove particulate. In the step (b) the syngas is conditioned to achieve a target syngas composition, where the conditioning comprises at least one of adding carbon dioxide as captured at the step (d), adding gas mixture from the step (a), adding oxidant gas and adding steam. In the step (c) the syngas is cooled to a temperature less than a boiling point of water in the prevailing operating conditions. In the step (d) scrubbing is in at least one gas/liquid contactor for removing condensable water-soluble gases, ash particulate and condensable hydrocarbons. In the step (d) at least 10% of carbon dioxide present in the syngas is removed.Preferred Components: In the step (a) the gas mixture: includes air and heated carbon dioxide recovered from the step (c); comprises air and carbon dioxide as captured at the step (d); and is provided using oxygen enriched air. The oxygen is present in the gas mixture in a concentration greater than 22% on a molar basis. In the step (a) the gas mixture is at a temperature less than 150 degrees Celsius. The gas mixture has a target gas mixture composition determined by a control system. The feedstock is hydrocarbonaceous feedstock. The system further comprises: a control system for setting a target gas mixture composition for the gas mixture; an oxygen enriching element for increasing oxygen concentration in air comprised in the gas mixture; a filtering element for filtering the syngas; and a conditioner for conditioning the syngas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI Reduction of carbon dioxide and metal-containing            dust generated during combustion of e.g. oil, involves            passing off-gas containing carbon dioxide and metal            dust through sulfated microbial reactor to remove            carbon dioxide and metal dust.
AU LA Y H
   CHO K K
   LA YEON H
   LUO L
   ZHAO G
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 2019260941
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of carbon dioxide and                metal-containing dust involves passing off-gas                containing carbon dioxide and metal-containing dust                through a sulfated microbial reactor to remove                carbon dioxide and metal-containing dust in the                off-gas.
   USE - Reduction of carbon dioxide and                metal-containing dust in off-gas generated during                combustion of oil, liquefied natural gas, peat,                coal, diesel fuel, and kerosene fuel in thermal                power plant, automobile, and industrial                facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient reduction of                carbon dioxide and metal-containing dust from                off-gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The sulfated                  microorganism is chosen from Acidianus, Aquifex,                  Hydrogenobacter, Thiobacillus, Thiomicrospira,                  Sulfurimonas, Halothiobacillus,                  Acidithiobacillus, and Thermithiobacillus.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sulfated                  microorganism oxidizes sulfur to produce sulfuric                  acid using carbon dioxide as a carbon source. The                  obtained sulfuric acid and the metal contained in                  the dust react to form a metal sulfate. The metal                  is chosen from magnesium, calcium, vanadium,                  chromium, manganese, cobalt, nickel, zinc,                  copper, tin, lead, niobium, molybdenum,                  ruthenium, vanadium, gallium, strontium, barium,                  titanium, zirconium, and strontium.

PT P
PN JP2020069417-A
TI Carbon-dioxide separation composition comprises polyethylene polyamine, alumina, and silica.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202037598Q
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide separation composition comprises a polyethylene polyamine, a zinc compound, and 30-95 wt.% water.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide separation composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-dioxide separation composition comprises alumina, which accelerates absorption and diffusion of carbon dioxide. Change in quality of silica is prevented.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises water. The composition comprises 0.1-20 wt.% alumina with respect to silica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compounds: The polyethylene polyamine is diethylenetriamine, N-(2-aminoethyl)piperazine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexamine and/or hexaethyleneheptamine.

PN WO2022207439-A1
TI Solution used to prepare hollow fiber membrane for            fluid separation, preferably organic solvent            nanofiltration or gas separation, preferably methane,            comprises polyhydroxyamide and alcohol comprising            isopropanol, n-propanol, or ethanol.
AU PETERS L
   LUGSTEIN M
   STAUDINGER S
   LEHNER D
   MAURER C
   BALSTER J
   LEE K P
AE EVONIK FIBRES GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 2022C7606C
AB    NOVELTY - Solution comprises 0.05-7.5 pts. wt. a                polyhydroxyamide and 99.95-92.55 pts. wt. at least                one alcohol comprising isopropanol, n-propanol, or                ethanol, where the polyhydroxyamide has a number                average molecular weight (Mn) of 10000-120000                g/mol, preferably 35000-70000 g/mol, determined                according to the method given in the description,                and a polydispersity index of 2-9, preferably                3-7.
   USE - The solution is useful for preparing a hollow                fiber membrane which is used for fluid separation,                preferably organic solvent nanofiltration or gas                separation, more preferably for the separation of                dioxygen and nitrogen gas or methane from carbon                dioxide, more preferably methane from carbon                dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solution provides hollow fiber membrane                which: has a permeance of methane of 2-40 GPU, more                preferably 5-30 GPU and a permeance of carbon                dioxide of 120-1200 GPU, more preferably 150-500                GPU and a pure gas selectivity carbon                dioxide/methane of 30-50, more preferably 35-45,                determined according to the method; and shows                almost the same mechanical characteristics as the                hollow fiber membrane used as hollow fiber support                but has significantly better gas separation                properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   preparing (P1) a hollow fiber membrane,                comprising coating a hollow fiber on the outside                with the solution;
   hollow fiber membrane comprising a support                hollow fiber comprising polyimide and a separation                layer on the outer surface of the hollow fiber                cylinder comprising polybenzoxazole; and
   preparing (P2) the solution, comprising (a)                providing the alcohol, (b) providing the                polyhydroxyamide, and (c) dissolving the                polyhydroxyamide in the alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process (P1)                  comprises a further step of thermal treatment,                  conducted after the coating step. The thermal                  treatment is done at a temperature of                  275-450&#176; C, more preferably                  300-370&#176; C, most preferably                  330-370&#176; C and for at least 1 minutes,                  more preferably at least 15 minutes, even more                  preferably 15-180 minutes., and most preferably                  30-120 minutes. Before the coating step an                  earlier coating step is using a siloxane or                  siloxane solution before the coating step with                  the solution to obtain a gutter layer. The                  process (P2) further comprises (d) filtering                  obtained solution. The dissolving step is                  performed under stirring. Preferred Components:                  The polyhydroxyamide is of formula                  (HO-CO-R1-CO-(-NH-R2-NH-CO-R1-CO-NH-R2-NH)n-CO-R1-CO-OH)                  (I).n=10-10000, preferably 10-5000;R2=hydrocarbon group comprising at least two                  hydroxy-groups, and optionally at least one halo,                  more preferably F, preferably                  2-hydroxy-4-[2,2,2-trifluoro-1-[3-hydroxyphenyl]-1-(trifluoromethyl)ethyl]phenyl;                  andR1=hydrocarbon group, more preferably a                  hydrocarbon group comprising 2-12C, most                  preferably 4-8C, preferably phenyl.The hollow fiber used is a polyimide fiber,                  having a permeance of 500 GPU N, preferably                  2000-20000 GPU N and the hollow fiber material                  has a glass transition temperature Tg of greater                  than 300&#176; C, preferably Tg of greater                  than 350 determined according to the method. The                  separation layer has a thickness of 20-10000 nm,                  more preferably 100-3000 nm. The support hollow                  fiber has a permeance of 500 GPU N, preferably                  2000-20000 GPU N and the hollow fiber material                  has a glass transition temperature Tg of greater                  than 300&#176; C, preferably Tg of greater                  than 350 determined according to the method. The                  hollow fiber membrane comprises a silicone                  comprising gutter layer between the surface of                  the support hollow fiber and the separation                  layer, preferably having an average thickness of                  10-900 nm. A portion of the gutter layer is                  present within the support hollow fiber and a                  portion of the gutter layer is outside of the                  support and the conditions having: the portion of                  the gutter layer outside of the support has an                  average thickness (GLe) of 10-900 nm; and the                  portion of the gutter layer present within the                  support has an average thickness (GLi) of 10-350%                  of GLe, are satisfied.
   EXAMPLE - 1000 ml Flask was dried in a nitrogen gas                stream. 36.63 g                4,4'-(Hexafluoroisopropylidene)bis(2- aminophenol)                and 300 g dry N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) were                filled into the flask and the solution was cooled                down to 10&#176; C using a cooling bath. Then                44.5 g chlorotrimethylsilane were added dropwise                via a syringe for 10 minutes. After the complete                addition of 35.7 g chlorotrimethylsilane pyridine                were added. The reaction mixture was heated to room                temperature and stirred in an nitrogen gas stream                for 120 minutes. Then the solution was cooled down                to 10&#176; C using a cooling bath and 20.3 g                isophthalic acid chloride were added as solid and                rinsed with 100 g NMP. After 30 minutes, 17.4 g                pyridine was added. The reaction mixture was cooled                for another 60 minutes and then stirred over night                at room temperature. The viscous reaction mixture                was precipitated in water and the resulting polymer                granules were washed with water and then dried at                80&#176; C with circulating air to obtain                polyhydroxyamide. 2 g Polyhydroxyamide was solved                under vigorous stirring in 98 g isopropanol. The                solution was filtered before use with a 3                &#956; m paper filter.

GA 2022A4175A
AB    NOVELTY - In-situ synthesis of carbon-based single-atom                nanosheet involves mixing ferrocene and                2-methylimidazole to obtain a mixture (A), mixing                and grinding zinc oxide, sodium chloride and                calcium chloride to obtain a mixture (B), adding                the mixture (A) and the mixture (B) to a container                spaced apart, passing argon gas into the container,                blowing the mixture (A) into the mixture (B)                through the argon gas, performing gradient                calcining, cooling to obtain initial carbon-based                single-atom nanosheet containing sodium                chloride/calcium chloride mixed salt, washing the                initial carbon-based single-atom nanosheets                containing sodium chloride/calcium chloride mixed                salt with deionized water, stirring,                suction-filtration, and vacuum-drying.

   ADVANTAGE - The method provides in-situ synthesized                carbon-based single-atom nanosheet with high                electro-catalytic reduction carbon dioxide                activity.    1. the carbon-based single-atom nanosheet;                and

The electrocatalytic reduction                is performed using a three-electrode system                comprising the carbon-based single-atom nanosheet                adhered to carbon paper as a working electrode,                silver/silver chloride as a reference electrode,                and platinum wire as a counter electrode.

The                nickel-doped bismuth is obtained by                electro-reduction of the nickel-doped bismuth                salt/bismuth carbonate nanosheet precursor. In the                electric reduction process bismuth oxycarbonate is                reduced to metal bismuth.
   USE - Method for nickel-doped bismuth salt/bismuth                carbonate nanosheet precursor in carbon dioxide                electroreduction high-efficiency formic acid                reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to control, and can                mass produce. The size of the catalyst reaches                nanometer level thickness. The introduction of                nickel controls the p rail of the bismuth catalyst,                the electron density at the Fermi level is                enhanced, and it is good for reducing the energy                barrier of carbon dioxide reduction. The catalyst                is applied to carbon dioxide reduction to prepare                formic acid, which solves the problem of high                over-potential, low catalytic activity and low                selectivity.

The catalyst                  electrode is prepared by taking the slurry and                  applying it on the carbon paper electrode                  dropwise, and after naturally drying, the                  negative electrode of the electrolytic cell is                  obtained. The mol ratio of the nickel salt                  and bismuth salt is 3: 97, 5: 95, 7: 93, 10: 90.    LI C
   WANG Y
   LIANG D
   CHEN M
AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
GA 202252583H
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst used for preparing synthesis gas by reforming methane and carbon dioxide, uses clay-based ordered mesoporous molecular sieve as carrier to load nickel, manganese and zirconium as active metal. The carrier is obtained by using inorganic acid acidified attapulgite as silicon source and template agent under alkaline condition by hydrothermal crystallization. The loading amount of active metals is 1-15 wt.% nickel, 1-5 wt.% manganese and 1-5 wt.% zirconium.
   USE - The catalyst is used for preparing synthesis gas by reforming methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product uses nickel, manganese and zirconium three metal compound, improves anti-sintering ability and removing carbon deposition ability and anti-carbon and anti-sintering ability, reduces production cost, and increases service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a method for preparing catalyst comprising acidifying attapulgite under mineral acid concentration of 3.5- mol/L at 150-200degrees Celsius for 10-15 hours, filtering, washing and drying to obtain attapulgite-based silicon source, fully stirring silicon source with template agent and sodium hydroxide, using acid titration to adjust pH value to stable at 10-12, sealing suspension for hydrothermal treatment, centrifuging, drying, calcining to obtain attapulgite-based ordered mesoporous molecular sieve carrier, fully mixing nickel-manganese-zirconium-containing metal precursor salt solution with citric acid, adding to attapulgite-based silicon source suspension, fully stirring to gel, evaporating, drying, grinding and sieving, and calcining; and (2) an application of catalyst comprising reducing catalyst by hydrogen/nitrogen atmosphere and catalytic coal mine gas-carbon dioxide reforming in micro-fixed bed reactor to produce synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic acid is hydrochloric acid and/or sulfuric acid. The template agent is hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide. The mass ratio of attapulgite-based silicon source and template agent is 1:1.58-1.94.Preferred Method: Reduction is done at 600-800degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours at 100-150 mL/min of 10-15 vol.% hydrogen/nitrogen gas stream. Catalytic coal mine gas-carbon dioxide reforming to produce synthesis gas is done at 400-700degrees Celsius at catalyst dosage of 0.1-0.5g, feeding molar ratio of methane and carbon dioxide at 1-1.2, air feeding space velocity of 10000 mL/h-gcat. The nickel-manganese-zirconium-containing metal precursor salt is respectively nitrate hexahydrate, hexahydrate manganese nitrate and zirconium nitrate pentahydrate. The molar ratio of metal ion and citric acid in precursor salt solution is 1:1-2. The method involves acidifying attapulgite, filtering, washing and drying to obtain attapulgite-based silicon source, fully stirring silicon source with template agent and sodium hydroxide at 30-40 degrees Celsius for 6-12 hours, using acid titration to adjust pH value to stable, sealing suspension for hydrothermal treatment at 100-180degrees Celsius for 24-96 hours, centrifuging, drying at 100degrees Celsius for 8-12 hours, calcining at 550-650degrees Celsius for 6-8 hours at 2-4degrees Celsius/minute to obtain attapulgite-based ordered mesoporous molecular sieve carrier, fully mixing nickel-manganese-zirconium-containing metal precursor salt solution with citric acid, adding to attapulgite-based silicon source suspension, fully stirring to gel, evaporating at 70-90degrees Celsius, drying to 100degrees Celsius for 8-12 hours, grinding and sieving, and calcining to 500-700degrees Celsius for 3-5 hours at 2-4degrees Celsius/minute.
