The processors monitor a supply                load of electricity and a consumptive load of the                electricity by an integrator application, and                identify an excess energy condition based on the                supply load. The processor directs an air scrubber                to utilize the routed excess electricity as a power                source to remove carbon dioxide from ambient air,                and directs enhanced coal bed methane (ECBM) well                to produce methane that is displaced by the removed                carbon dioxide in response to a deficient energy                condition.    USE - The fully biodegradable foaming material is used in children toy, medical material and other fields.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying is carried out for 8-12 hours at 60-80degrees Celsius. In the step (1), the mixing is as follows: mixing for 8-10 minutes in the internal mixer with the rotating speed of 60-80 revolutions/minute. The sample plate is made of 1-2 mm on the 140-180degrees Celsius of flat vulcanizing instrument. The temperature of the reactor is 160-180degrees Celsius, the pressure is maintained at 20-30 MPa, soaking the sample plate in the supercritical carbon dioxide for 4-5 hours, reducing the temperature to 120-140degrees Celsius and releasing the pressure in the reactor to be normal pressure. In the step (3), the mold heat treatment is carried out at 150-200degrees Celsius.

TI Tungsten trioxide nanosheet with electron-rich            surface as catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of            carbon dioxide, has pit structure, and is prepared by            adding sodium tungstate to uniformly mixed solution of            deionized water and nitric acid.
AU YANG P
   ZHAO W
   GAO S
AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 2021C54693
AB    NOVELTY - Tungsten trioxide nanosheet with electron-rich                surface has pit structure.
   USE - Tungsten trioxide nanosheet with electron-rich                surface as catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tungsten trioxide nanosheet with                electron-rich surface reduces carbon dioxide to                methanol and methane with more practical value by                use of light energy. The output of methanol can                reach 17 mol/(g.hour) and the output of methane can                reach 6.6 mol/(g.hour). The catalytic activity is                high, the stability is good, and it is prepared in                large quantity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) method for preparing electron-rich                tungsten trioxide (WO3) nanosheet, which involves:                (a) adding sodium tungstate to the uniformly mixed                solution of deionized water and concentrated nitric                acid, (b) stirring at room temperature for 12-24                hours, (c) washing the precipitate to neutrality,                (d) freezing, (e) drying for 5-10 hours to obtain                the precursor, (f) installing the obtained                precursor in a quartz boat, (g) placing in a tube                furnace, (h) heating to 400-500degrees Celsius in a                hydrogen-argon mixed atmosphere or an air                atmosphere, (i)calcining for 10-40 minutes to                obtain tungsten trioxide nanosheet with                electron-rich surface;
   (2) method of using tungsten trioxide                nanosheet with electron-rich surface, which                involves: (a) dispersing uniformly the                electron-rich tungsten trioxide nanosheet in an                aqueous ethanol solution, (b) coating on the glass                plate, (c) drying, (d) placing in a quartz reactor,                (e) adding deionized water to the quartz reactor,                (f) introducing the carbon dioxide for                photocatalytic reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The size of the pit                  structure is 3-10 nm. The average heating rate in                  the hydrogen-argon mixed atmosphere is                  40-60degrees Celsius/minute. The volume fraction                  of hydrogen in the mixture of hydrogen-argon is                  5-20 vol.%. The flow rate of the hydrogen and                  argon mixed gas is 10-40 mL/minute.

PN KR2021060722-A
TI Production of bimetallic blue titania complex used as photocatalyst, involves e.g. mixing blue titania nanoparticles with platinum precursor solution, performing photo-deposition process on suspension, filtering, washing and drying.
AU IN S I
   KIM H
   HWANG Y J
   SORCARSAURAV
AE DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 202159126J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of bimetallic blue titania complex involves (i) preparing blue titania nanoparticles (BT), (ii) preparing a suspension by mixing the blue titania nanoparticles with a platinum precursor solution, performing a photo-deposition process on the suspension to prepare a blue titania composite (Pt-BT) on which platinum nanoparticles are deposited, (iii) preparing a blue titania composite dispersion by dispersing the platinum nanoparticles-deposited blue titania complex (Pt-BT) in a mixed solvent of water and methanol, (iv) preparing a suspension by mixing a copper precursor solution with the blue titania composite dispersion, subjecting the suspension to a photo-deposition process to deposit copper nanoparticles and preparing bimetallic BT composite comprising platinum nanoparticles and copper nanoparticles are combined with bimetallic nanoparticles, and (v) filtering, washing and drying.
   USE - Production of bimetallic blue titania complex used as photocatalyst for converting carbon dioxide into methane and ethane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of bimetallic blue titania complex having excellent stability and reusability, and high photoconversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) bimetallic blue titania complex, which comprises blue titania nanoparticles (BT), and bimetallic nanoparticles photo-deposited on the surface of the blue titania nanoparticles, which are formed by combining 0.20-1.50 wt.% platinum nanoparticles and 0.40-2.0 wt.% copper nanoparticles; and
   (2) photoconversion of carbon dioxide, which involves using carbon dioxide is photoconverted into methane and ethane by gas phase reaction of carbon dioxide and water vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The blue titania nanoparticles of the step (i) is prepared by mixing nanoparticles and a reducing agent to prepare a mixture, heating the mixture under an inert gas at a temperature of 280-420 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, and washing the heated mixture with a solvent and drying at a temperature of 70-110 degrees C for 9-15 hours. The step (ii) and (iv) are prepared by stirring under closed system and dark conditions. When the light conversion experiment of carbon dioxide to methane and ethane by gas phase reaction of carbon dioxide and water vapor performed for 1.5 hours, the methane yield of 40% or more, and the ethane yield (AQY) of 3.0% or more, and the total yield for methane and ethane is 43%. When the light conversion experiment of carbon dioxide to methane and ethane by gas phase reaction of carbon dioxide and water vapor performed for 6 hours, the maximum light conversion efficiency of sunlight into fuel for methane is 2.5-3.3%, and ethane is 0.25-0.50%. Preferred Composition: The platinum precursor solution contains 0.05-0.50 pts. wt. platinum precursor based on 100 pts. wt. of the solvent. The blue titania composite dispersion contains 15-50 ml water and 3-20 ml methanol per 100 mg of the blue titania complex on which platinum nanoparticles are deposited. The copper precursor solution contains 0.05-1.25 pts. wt. copper precursor based on 100 pts. wt. of the solvent. Preferred Components: The platinum precursor comprises at least one of chloroplatinic acid, potassium chloride platinate, and cisplatin. The copper precursor includes at least one selected from copper nitrate, copper(II) acetylacetonate and copper sulfate.

AU BAEDONGMIN
   CHOJOONGGIL
AE DT & BIO (DTBI-Non-standard)
   HEXHIVE CO LTD (HEXH-Non-standard)
GA 202141358U
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has an omnidirectional photographing unit (110) installed inside a vehicle and includes a single lens module for photographing a 360 degree direction. An image processing unit (120) generates an omnidirectional plane image by image processing the captured image of the omnidirectional photographing unit. A communication module transmits the omnidirectional plane image in real time. A data server (300) receives the omnidirectional plane image transmitted from the communication module, recognizes the object of the omnidirectional plane image in real time, and feeds back the recognized object information to the image processing unit. A carbon dioxide sensor (130) senses the concentration of carbon dioxide inside the vehicle. The data server feeds back information for varying the stored image frame for area in consideration of the movement of the object or the speed of the vehicle to the image processing unit after dividing the captured image into multiple images.
   USE - 360-degree black box system for high-definition video transmission.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the power consumption of the vehicle photographing apparatus due to processing. The system helps to safe driving by warning when the carbon dioxide concentration exceeds the reference value.

AE NANJING YOUDENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202134713T
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (1) mixing 50-70 wt.%                diatom shell, 10-30 wt.% mesoporous silica, and                10-20 wt.% all-silica zeolite uniformly, the                preferred ratio is 50 wt.%:30 wt.%:20 wt.%, 70                wt.%:10 wt.%: 20 wt.% or 70 wt.%:20 wt.%:10 wt.%,                (2) selecting solid-state lasers, fiber lasers, or                carbon dioxide lasers, preferably a carbon dioxide                laser with a laser wavelength in the infrared                region, and the laser wavelength is 10.6 nm, the                operating power is 40-100 watts, the spot diameter                is 10-50 microns, the laser scanning speed is 10-50                cm/s, and the resolution is 400-500 DPI, and (3)                carrying out software control powder spreading,                where the thickness of the powder layer is set to                150-200 microns, preferably 150 microns, 170                microns, 200 microns, using the carbon dioxide                laser to reserve large pores when selecting areas                and sinter layer by layer to obtain cross-scale                porous ceramics.

   ADVANTAGE - The porous ceramic has a multi-level pore                structure of macropores (greater than 50 nm),                mesopores (2-50 nm), and micropores (less than                2nm); and realizes a cross-scale bionic structure                on three scales of macroscopic, mesoscopic, and                microscopic. PN WO2021025856-A1; US2021039052-A1; US11471839-B2
TI Thin film composite gas separation membrane for            e.g. oxygen/nitrogen separation, comprises polyether            block amide copolymer coating layer and nanoporous            asymmetric support membrane having nanopores and            gelatin polymers inside nanopores.
AU DONG X
   LIU C
   TRAN H
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 202115043U
AB    NOVELTY - A thin film composite gas separation membrane                comprises a polyether block amide copolymer coating                layer and a nanoporous asymmetric support membrane.                The skin layer surface of the support membrane has                nanopores and gelatin polymers inside the                nanopores.
   USE - Thin film composite gas separation membrane                for oxygen/nitrogen separation, hydrogen sulfide                and carbon dioxide separation from natural gas,                carbon dioxide removal from flue gas, fuel gas                conditioning, hydrogen/methane, polar molecules,                and ammonia mixtures with methane, nitrogen or                hydrogen and other light gases separations, natural                gas liquids recovery and hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide removal from natural gas. Uses                include but are not limited to natural gas                upgrading applications to remove carbon dioxide and                hydrogen sulfide impurities such as off-shore                gas-processing platforms, floating liquefied                natural gas (FLNG), and floating, processing,                storage and offloading (FPSO) vessel                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has high performance, high                permeability and selectivity, excellent mechanical                stability, ease of processability, decreased cost,                and provides high purity product gas with increased                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of the gas separation                membrane, which involves casting a flat sheet,                nipping an aqueous solution of 0.01-1 wt.% gelatin,                incorporating high hydrogen sulfide or methane                selective gelatin polymers into nanopores, drying,                coating using a polyether block amide copolymer                solution containing 0.2-5 wt.% polyether block                amide copolymer, and alcohol and/or water;                and
   (2) gas separation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The support membrane                  comprises a polymeric membrane material chosen                  from polysulfone, polyethersulfone,                  polyacrylonitrile, polyimide, polyetherimide,                  cellulose acetate, and/or cellulose triacetate.                  The gelatin polymer is a type-A gelatin derived                  from acid-cured tissue, or a type-B gelatin                  derived from lime-cured tissue. The polyether                  block amide copolymer comprises a polyamide                  segment and a polyether segment. Preferred                  Process: The process involves producing a residue                  stream from a membrane (A) containing a low                  hydrogen sulfide content and sending into a                  membrane (B) to remove carbon dioxide.

AB    NOVELTY - This utility model claims an air conditioner and its trans-critical carbon dioxide air source heat pump system. The trans-critical carbon dioxide air source heat pump system comprises a compressor with an inlet and an outlet, an air cooler, a heat regenerator, an electronic expansion valve and an evaporator. the air cooler comprises a cooling medium passage and a cooling passage for transferring heat with the cooling medium passage. The evaporator comprises a heat exchange tube. The defrosting efficiency of the trans-critical carbon dioxide air source heat pump system is high.

PN CN111772065-A
TI Aerated banana vinegar flavored beverage comprises            banana vinegar, concentrated fruit juice, mogroside,            citric acid, carbon dioxide, and purified water.
AU WEI L
   YANG C
   ZHENG B
   HUANG J
   SUN Q
   CHEN Z
   DU L
AE GUANGXI AGRIC VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A4059K
AB    NOVELTY - Aerated banana vinegar flavored beverage                comprises 3.5-5 wt.% banana vinegar, 1.5-5 wt.%                concentrated fruit juice, 0.03-0.06 wt.% mogroside,                0.2-0.5 wt.% citric acid, 1-2 wt.% carbon dioxide,                and 100 wt.% purified water.
   USE - Used as the aerated banana vinegar flavored                beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage: uses carbon dioxide isobaric                filling process to combine the high value of banana                vinegar, concentrated fruit juice, and functional                sugar; is uniform, clear and transparent liquid;                has no delamination in long-term storage, has                unique, sweet and sour flavor, and has combined                deliciousness with low sugar and low calories; and                gives people a different taste and conforms to                pursuit high-quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the beverage comprising (a) taking fresh                Momordica grosvenori for microwave sterilization,                adding into a steaming tank, decocting for 3-6                times by using water for 2-4 hours, and extracting                to obtain a crude extract, then clarifying the                crude extract at room temperature with a clarifying                agent, passing through a macroporous resin column,                eluting with an ethanol gradient, drying and                superfinely crushing to obtain mogroside, (b)                mixing banana vinegar, concentrated fruit juice,                mogroside and citric acid, then adding some                purified water to obtain thick slurry, (c) freezing                the remaining pure water at 1-5 degrees C to obtain                ice pure water, (d) sterilizing the thick slurry at                85-95 degrees C, and then immediately cooling to                room temperature, (e) mixing the thick slurry and                pure ice water with carbon dioxide mixer, and (f)                using equal pressure filling machine for filling                and sealing the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The aerated banana                  vinegar flavored beverage comprises 5 wt.% banana                  vinegar, 2.5 wt.% concentrated fruit juice, 0.045                  wt.% mogroside, 0.2 wt.% citric acid, 1.3 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, and 100 wt.% purified water.                  Preferred Components: The concentrated fruit                  juice comprises concentrated lemon juice, litchi                  juice, guava juice, pear juice, longan juice,                  pineapple juice, mango juice, apple juice,                  strawberry juice, grape juice and/or passion                  fruit juice. The mass content of the thick slurry                  is 14.29-33.33%. The weight ratio of thick slurry                  to pure ice water is 1:2-6. The pressure of                  carbon dioxide is 0.15-0.3 MPa. Preferred Method:                  The step (f) before filling comprises                  sequentially filtering the mixed and formulated                  flavored beverages through diatomaceous earth,                  polypropylene membrane and polyethersulfone                  filter element. The filling is carried out under                  the pressure of 0.15-0.25 MPa.

PT P
PN US2019359902-A1; US11370982-B2
TI Production of jet fuel or diesel, involves            reacting carbonaceous material with e.g. steam to            produce product gas, reacting compressed gas with            catalyst to produce Fischer Tropsch products, and            upgrading.
AU CHANDRAN R
   BURCIAGA D A
   LEO D M
   FREITAS S R
   NEWPORT D G
   MILLER J K
   HARRINGTON K E
   ATTWOOD B C
AE THERMOCHEM RECOVERY INT INC (THER-Non-standard)
GA 201999589R
AB    NOVELTY - Production of jet fuel and/or diesel from                carbonaceous material (500) involves (a) providing                a source of material, (b) densifying material, (c)                reacting the material with steam and carbon dioxide                in an endothermic reaction to produce a product gas                which includes at least carbon dioxide, (d) cooling                the gas in heat exchanger, (e) scrubbing the gas in                a scrubber, (f) compressing the gas, (g) removing                carbon dioxide from the gas, and recycling at least                a portion of the removed carbon dioxide as a                reactant in step (c), (h) reacting the gas with a                catalyst to produce one or more Fischer Tropsch                products including tail gas, (i) upgrading products                to produce jet fuel and/or diesel fuel and naphtha,                and recycling at least a portion of the tail gas                produced in step (h) as a fuel source to generate                heat for the endothermic reaction in step (c),                and/or recycling at least a portion of the naphtha                produced in step (i) as a fuel source to generate                heat for the endothermic reaction in step                (c).
   USE - Production of jet fuel and/or diesel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, and                rapid production of jet fuel and/or diesel. The jet                fuel/diesel on combustion does not emit greenhouse                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the mass                flow regulation of feedstock deliver system.
   Carbonaceous material (500)
   Feedstock preparation (1000)
   Feedstock delivery (2000)
   Product gas generation (3000)
   Primary gas clean up (4000)
   Compression (5000)
   Secondary gas clean up (6000)
   Downstream synthesis system (7000)
   Upgrading (8000)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  recycling both tail gas produced in step (h) and                  naphtha produced in step (i), as fuel sources to                  generate heat for the endothermic reaction in                  step (c), reducing a pressure of the at least a                  portion of the carbon dioxide removed in step (g)                  to form a reduced-pressure carbon dioxide, and                  mixing the reduced-pressure carbon dioxide with                  the carbonaceous material at a carbonaceous                  material to gas weight ratio that is less than                  about 50:1, prior to reacting in step (c),                  introducing at least a portion of the tail gas                  produced in step (h) along with an                  oxygen-containing gas, into at least one heat                  exchanger, combusting the tail gas and the                  oxygen-containing gas within the heat exchanger                  to form a combustion stream; and indirectly                  transferring heat from the combustion stream to                  the reactants in step (c) to effectuate                  endothermic thermochemical reaction, combusting a                  fuel source in the heat exchanger to indirectly                  heat the carbonaceous material, steam and carbon                  dioxide, and scrubbing the product gas to remove                  benzene, toluene, phenol, styrene, xylene, and                  cresol. The step (c) involves reacting the steam                  with the carbonaceous material in a weight ratio                  of 0.125:1-3:1, preferably more than 0:1 to less                  than 0.5:1, in presence of the particulate heat                  transfer material in stages to produce product                  gas, providing indirect heat for the endothermic                  reaction by heating the carbonaceous material,                  steam, and carbon dioxide with a heat exchanger,                  converting carbon in the carbonaceous material at                  a carbon conversion rate of 50-99%. The                  particulate heat transfer material promotes the                  endothermic reaction between the carbonaceous                  material and steam. The step (c) involves                  reacting steam with 104-625 tons per day of                  carbonaceous material. Preferred Components: The                  fuel source comprises natural gas, flue gas. The                  product gas comprises hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide, and benzene, toluene, phenol, styrene,                  xylene, and cresol. Preferred Conditions: The                  reaction between steam and carbonaceous material                  is performed at velocity of 0.6-1.2 ft/second,                  preferably 50-300 ft/second.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

PT P
PN US2019112574-A1; CN109666631-A
TI Enhancing an activity of stem cells involves culturing stem cell clusters for three to eleven days in a sealed container filled with a culture medium, under a condition in which no oxygen and carbon dioxide are supplied.
AU XU R
   JIANG B
   YAN L
   LI E
AE UNIV MACAU (UYMA-Non-standard)
   UNIV MACAU (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 201934563Y
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing an activity of stem cells involves culturing stem cell clusters for three to eleven days in a sealed container filled with a culture medium, under a condition in which no oxygen and carbon dioxide are supplied.
   USE - Method for enhancing an activity of stem cells and treating a subject for central nervous disease(s) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to enhance an activity of stem cells, where stemness and apoptosis resistance of the stem cells are improved, thus significantly improving the therapeutic effect of the stem cells after transplantation into a human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a formulation for treating a central nervous disease or diseases, which involves culturing neural stem cells or mesenchymal stem cells for three to eleven days in a sealed container filled with a culture medium, under a condition in which no oxygen and carbon dioxide are supplied, removing the culture medium therefrom, and then resuspending the obtained stem cell clusters in a saline injection solution; and
   (2) a method for treating a subject for central nervous disease(s), which involves culturing stem cell clusters for three to eleven days in a sealed container filled with a culture medium, under a condition in which no oxygen and carbon dioxide are supplied, and then injecting the stem cell clusters into the subject through intrathecal injection or orthotopical brain injection, where the stem cells are either one or both of mesenchymal stem cells and neural stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The culture is performed for three to seven days. The stem cells comprise mesenchymal stem cells, neural stem cells, pluripotent stem cells, adipose stem cells, muscle stem cells, and/or cardiac stem cells. The stem cell clusters are obtained by aggregating the stem cells under a suspension culture condition to form cell clusters, and where the stem cells are cultured with a 3D suspension culture system to form cell clusters. A diameter of the cell clusters is between 30 mu m and 250 mu m. The stem cells are suspension-cultured in an incubator at 37 degrees C for 24-48 hours and aggregated to form cell clusters, or where the stem cells are suspension-cultured under a condition of 20-37 degrees C and spontaneously aggregate to form cell clusters. The container is a plastic container, and the plastic container is made of polyethylene, polypropylene or melamine. The stem cells are packaged at an amount of 1 million to 2 million cells per milliliter of culture medium. The method involves preparing a stem cell formulation therefrom.
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

TI Removable fiberoptic adapter for intubating            patient by e.g. laryngeal mask airway, for ventilation,            has adapter mounting tube oriented away from oxygen            port and carbon dioxide outlet, and adapter plug for            engaging implement inlet.

   ANIGATI C
AE ANIGATI E (ANIG-Individual)
   ANIGATI C (ANIG-Individual)
GA 2019258471
AB    NOVELTY - The adapter has an implement inlet (4)                centrally connected to a grasping flange (5). The                implement inlet is oppositely positioned to an                airway tube (1) about the grasping flange. A                mounting flange (6) is laterally and perimetrically                connected to the airway tube. An oxygen port (2)                and a carbon dioxide outlet (3) are positioned                between the grasping flange and the mounting                flange. An adapter mounting tube (8) is adjacently                connected to the mounting flange. The airway tube                is concentrically positioned within the adapter                mounting tube. The adapter mounting tube is                oriented away from the oxygen port and the carbon                dioxide outlet. An adapter plug (7) selectively                engages the implement inlet.
   USE - Removable fiberoptic adapter for intubating a                patient by a respiratory-device e.g. laryngeal mask                airway (LMA) tube and ventilating airway mask, for                non-invasive ventilation under anesthesia or                unconscious.
   ADVANTAGE - The adapter is opened and disengaged from a                LMA tube or an adapter cuff when a fiber-optic                scope and an endotracheal tube are inserted, so                that users realizes better flexibility in                positioning the tubes within the LMA tube or the                adapter cuff. The adapter forms a set of tactile                grooves so as to effectively improve users grip                radially across the plug flange.

TI Method for tempering organic product in enclosed            structure, involves maintaining liquid at predefined            temperature by circulating liquid from enclosed            structure through heating unit and back into enclosed            structure.
AU ALVA J S
   ALVA V R
   ALVA J
   ALVA V
AE ALVA J S (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA V R (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA J S (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA V R (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA J (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA V (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA V R (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA J S (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA J (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA V (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA J (ALVA-Individual)
   ALVA V (ALVA-Individual)
GA 201913984Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing (802) the                organic product through an access unit of the                enclosed structure into the liquid. The access unit                is opened during the introduction and is closed.                The liquid is maintained at a predefined                temperature by circulating the liquid from the                enclosed structure through a heating unit and back                into the enclosed structure to increase a                temperature of the liquid up to a predefined                temperature. The heating unit is provided to supply                heat to the liquid to increase the temperature up                to the predefined temperature. The carbon dioxide                gas is supplied into the enclosed structure for a                time period. The organic product is removed through                the access unit of the enclosed structure, when a                core temperature of the organic product reaches the                predefined temperature. The circulation of the                liquid and the supply of carbon dioxide gas is                ceased, before removing of the organic                product.
   USE - Method for tempering organic product in                enclosed structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains the freezing solvent at                the predefined temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for tempering organic product in enclosed                structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart illustrating                the method for tempering organic product in                enclosed structure.
   Step for introducing the organic product                through an access unit of the enclosed structure                into the liquid (802)
   Step for circulating liquid from the enclosed                structure (804)
   Step for agitating the liquid by using                agitator placed within the liquid (806)
   Step for initiating supply of carbon dioxide                gas through a gas permeable structure (810)
   Step for ceasing supply of carbon dioxide                into the enclosed structure (812)

PN CN109298015-A; CN109298015-B
TI Carbon dioxide high pressure environment coal heating gasification combustion experiment device, has air pump arranged between carbon monoxide analyzer and synthesis detecting instrument such that air pump recycles gas in device main body.
AU LI Z
   SASAKI H
   ZHANG X
   SUGAI Y
   WANG Q
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201914579B
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an air tank connected with an air inlet pipeline in an experimental device main body through a pressure valve and a three-way interface. The inlet pipeline is provided with a first air pressure valve. A bottom center of a high pressure reaction kettle is provided with a ceramic disk, where a coal sample is placed on the ceramic disk. A cylindrical open container is arranged on the ceramic disk. A carbon dioxide laser beam heating device is arranged on the pressure reaction kettle. A flue gas filter is connected with an oxygen monitoring apparatus, a carbon monoxide analyzer and a synthesis detecting instrument. An air pump is arranged between the carbon monoxide analyzer and the synthesis detecting instrument such that the air pump continuously recycles gas in the experimental device main body.
   USE - Carbon dioxide high pressure environment coal heating gasification combustion experiment device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can realize rapid heating of coal samples in a short time such that temperature of the coal sample rises rapidly to target temperature, thus shortening heating time, and avoiding slow oxidation combustion of coal and improving gasification efficiency of the coal. The device can test normal pressure or high pressure so as to study gasification efficiency of the coal under different conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a carbon dioxide high pressure environment coal heating gasification combustion experiment device.

PN CN109265723-A
TI Producing composite foamed granule useful for preparing a thermoplastic elastomer composite foamed material having a magnetic therapeutic effect comprises e.g. mixing thermoplastic granules and magnetically permeable materials.
AU QIN L
   MA W
   ZHANG C
   LIANG Y
   SHI Z
AE NINGBO GMF NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201912402M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing composite foamed granule comprises (i) mixing thermoplastic granules and magnetically permeable materials into granulation to obtain composite particles to be foamed, and placing the resulting composite particles to be foamed into an autoclave, passing carbon dioxide gas into the autoclave, adjusting the pressure and temperature of the autoclave, maintaining carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, performing pressure infiltration, and performing rapid pressure relief and heat foaming to obtain finished product.
   USE - The granule is useful for preparing a thermoplastic elastomer composite foamed material having a magnetic therapeutic effect (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The granule: has light weight and high resilience of the conventional thermoplastic elastomer foaming material, and specific physiotherapy effect; improves the physiological function of the human body; and can be widely applied to the fields of soles, insoles, mattresses and protective gears.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) preparing a thermoplastic elastomer composite foamed material comprising (a) mixing the thermoplastic elastomer particles and the magnetic conductive material into a granulation to obtain a composite particle to be foamed, (b) placing the resulting composite particles to be foamed in an autoclave, passing carbon dioxide gas into the autoclave, adjusting the pressure and temperature of the autoclave, maintaining carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, performing pressure infiltration, and performing rapid pressure relief, heat foaming, and composite foaming granules, and (iii) subjecting the resulting composite foamed granules to steam compression molding to obtain finished product; and
   (2) a thermoplastic elastomer composite foamed material having a magnetic therapeutic effect prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the thermoplastic elastomer is polyurethane-based thermoplastic elastomer, polyolefin-based thermoplastic elastomer, polyester-based thermoplastic elastomer or styrene-based thermoplastic elastomer. The magnetic conductive material is a magnetic conductive powder. The magnetically permeable material is added in an amount of 5-10 wt.% based on the total weight of the composite particles to be foamed. The particle size of the composite particles to be foamed is about 4 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mixing and granulation of the mixture is carried out by using a single screw extruder of phi -150 and L/D of 15:1, and set the single screw extruder body temperature at 150-160 degrees C, 160-190 degrees C, 170-200 degrees C, and 175-205 degrees C, and the head temperature is 180-210 degrees C. In the step (ii), the pressure is 7.38-30 MPa, the temperature is 31-80 degrees C, and the permeation time is 1.5-6 hours. The heating foaming temperature is 90-160 degrees C, and the heating foaming time is 30-90 seconds.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.)

AB    NOVELTY - The device (200) has a lid structure (210) for defining a first opening (212) and a second opening (214). An input tube (220) includes a first end (222) and a second end (224) that form a flow conduit for pressurized carbon dioxide into a storage container. A vent tube (230) is sized to pass through and seal within the second opening defined by the lid structure. The vent tube includes a first end (232) and a second end (234) that form a flow conduit for gaseous carbon dioxide out of the storage container, where the first end of the vent tube is sized to fit within the storage container and coupled with a lower vent tube. The lid structure and the vent tube are formed from one of plastic and polymer.

PT P
PN CN108499568-A; CN108499568-B
TI Nickel-based catalyst for reforming methane by pressurized carbon dioxide, is selected from silicon dioxide and titanium dioxide, and active component is nickel-iron or nickel-cobalt and auxiliary agent is selected from cerium oxide.
AU LIU Z
   XIAO Y
   LI T
   SHI X
   SONG Y
   YANG C
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 201872471N
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-based catalyst is selected from silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide and titanium dioxide, and active component is nickel, nickel-iron or nickel-cobalt, and auxiliary agent is selected from cerium oxide, zirconium oxide or cerium-zirconium oxide (CexZr1-xO2), where x ranges from 0.3-0.7 and content of active component is 5-15 wt.%, and content of auxiliary agent is 2-9 wt.%. The catalyst is prepared by dissolving active component precursor, auxiliary precursor and carrier precursor in ethanol, and then adding aqueous solution of coordination-combustion agent, stirring mixture at room temperature for 2-4 hours, distilling off solvent to obtain viscous liquid, heating liquid to obtain solid powder by combustion and polishing uniformly, and then calcining in air atmosphere at 500-800 degrees C for 3-6 hours, and cooling to room temperature naturally, tableting, granulating, and passing through 40-60 mesh sieve to obtain catalyst.
   USE - Nickel-based catalyst for reforming methane by pressurized carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-based catalyst has high methane and carbon dioxide conversion to carbon dioxide reformed methane under pressurized condition, high activity, high stability, high carbon and anti-sintering ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Where coordination-combustion agent is selected from glycine, alanine, threonine, serine, ethylenediamine, citric acid, urea and trimesic acid. The amount of agent added is 0.5-2 times of total amount of metal element in precursor of active component and total amount of metal element or/and metalloid element in carrier precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass of catalyst is 100 wt.%, content of active component is 10 wt.% and content of auxiliary agent is 4-7 wt.%. The coordination-adding combustion improver is active component precursor in metal element, and total molar weight of auxiliary agent in precursor metal element and carrier in precursormetal element is 1-1.5 times. Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is cerium-zirconium oxide (CexZr1-xO2). The coordination-combustion improver is selected from glycine, alanine and threoninein. The active component is nickel, active component precursor is nickel nitrate or nickel oxalate, and active component is nickel-iron, precursor of active component is mixture of nickel nitrate and nickel oxalate and any one of ferric nitrate, iron oxalate and ferric ammonium citrate, when the active component is nickel-cobalt, the active group of precursor is mixture of nickel nitrate and nickel oxalate with any one of cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetylacetonate and cobalt oxalate. The auxiliary agent is cerium oxide, auxiliary agent precursor is selected from cerium nitrate or ammonium cericnitrate, when auxiliary agent is zirconium oxide, auxiliary agent precursor is zirconium nitrate or zirconium oxynitrate and when auxiliary agent is cerium-zirconium oxide, auxiliary agent precursor is selected from cerium nitrate, ammonium cerium nitrate and mixture of zirconium nitrate and zirconium oxynitrate. The carrier precursor is selected from methyl silicate, ethylsilicate, propyl silicate, butyl silicate, aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum nitrate, boehmite, one of tetrabutyl titanate and isopropyl titanate. Preferred Condition: The catalyst in air atmosphere is performed at 650-750 degrees C for 3-6 hours.

PT P
PN CN108187716-A
TI Nitrogen-doped carbon material-loaded multi-metal            catalyst used in preparation of synthesis gas by            reforming methane and carbon dioxide, comprises            nitrogen-doped carbon material as carrier, and nickel,            iron and cobalt as active components.
AU ZHANG G
   DUAN G
   CHENG H
   XU Y
   LIU J
   WANG L
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201850866D
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen-doped carbon material-loaded                multi-metal catalyst comprises 70-95 %mass                nitrogen-doped carbon material as a carrier, and                5-30 %mass nickel, iron and cobalt as active                components.
   USE - Nitrogen-doped carbon material-loaded                multi-metal catalyst is useful in the preparation                of synthesis gas by reforming methane and carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-doped carbon material-loaded                multi-metal catalyst is simple and economical to                prepare, and has high methane conversion rate of                over 90%, carbon dioxide conversion rate of over                89%, a hydrogen selectivity of over 93% and carbon                monoxide selectivity of over 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the method for preparing the                nitrogen-doped carbon material-loaded multi-metal                catalyst, which involves carbonizing low-grade                lignite and coconut shells as raw materials,                carrying out activation, immersing the materials in                hydrochloric acid or nitric acid for 10-24 hours,                washing with deionized water and drying at 80-100                degrees C for 24 hours to obtain carbon material                carrier, continuously subjecting nitrogen source to                nitrogen source treatment to obtain nitrogen-doped                carbon carrier, loading nickel, iron and cobalt                active components on the nitrogen-doped carbon                carrier, filtering, drying and calcining to obtain                a catalyst, and carrying out nitrogen source                treatment to obtain the nitrogen-doped carbon                material loaded multi-metal catalyst; and
   (2) application of nitrogen-doped carbon                material loaded multi-metal catalyst in the                preparation of synthesis gas by reforming methane                and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method                  involves mixing low-grade lignite and coconut                  shell in a mass ratio of 1:1-2, crushing the                  mixture to a particle size of 20-100 mesh,                  extruding the crushed mixture into blocks,                  carbonizing at 400-600 degrees C for 9-12 hours,                  crushing the carbonized product into an amorphous                  granular substance with a diameter of smaller                  than 3 mm, activating the amorphous granular                  substance by 10-30 %mass aqueous ammonia at                  500-900 degrees C to obtain a crude carbon                  material product, immersing the crude carbon                  material product in hydrochloric acid or nitric                  acid for 10-24 hours, washing with deionized                  water to a pH value of 6-7 and drying in an oven                  at 80-100 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a                  carbon material carrier, (b) taking nitrogen from                  the vessel, adding deionized water, heating,                  stirring under microwave condition to dissolve                  nitrogen source, adding carbon material carrier,                  adding deionized water, continuously stirring and                  reacting at 80-90 degrees C for 20-40 minutes to                  obtain reactants, transferring the reactants at a                  high-temperature resistant reaction vessel,                  vacuum-drying at 120-18 degrees C for 16-20                  hours, cooling to room temperature, cooling,                  filtering reactant, repeatedly washing the filter                  cake 2-4 times with deionized water and anhydrous                  diethyl ether, washing, drying at 40-50 degrees C                  for 6-10 hours and cooling to obtain                  nitrogen-doped carbon material, where the mass                  ratio of nitrogen source to carbon material                  carrier is 1:1-2, (c) immersing nitrogen-doped                  carbon material in a mixed solution of nickel                  nitrate, iron nitrate and ammonium heptamolybdate                  under ultrasonic negative pressure at an                  ultrasonic frequency of 20-100 kHz and a pressure                  of 80-100 kPa for 10-12 hours, filtering, drying                  at 80-100 degrees C for 8-12 hours, calcining in                  a muffle furnace at 500-700 degrees C for 4-5                  hours to obtain catalyst intermediate, where the                  molar ratio of nickel nitrate, iron nitrate and                  ammonium heptamolybdate is                  1.05-1.15:0.2-0.3:0.03-0.05 and the ratio of                  nitrogen-doped carbon material to impregnation                  liquid is 10:30-60 ml, and (d) mixing the                  catalyst intermediate with nitrogen dopant,                  stirring for 1.5 hours, placing it in a high                  temperature reactor, introducing an inert gas                  into high-temperature resistant reactor for 5                  minutes, heating at a temperature of 800-1000                  degrees C and a heating rate of 10-25 degrees                  C/minute for 0.8-1 hour, naturally cooling to                  room temperature to obtain a reactant, washing                  the reactant with deionized water and drying to                  obtain the catalyst. Preferred Components: The                  nitrogen source in the step (b) is ammonia gas,                  aqueous ammonia, urea and aniline. The nitrogen                  dopant in the step (d) is ammonium chloride,                  ammonium nitrate or ammonium nitrite. The inert                  gas in the step (d) is helium, argon or helium.                  The flow rate of the inert gas is 500-1000                  l/minute. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio                  of the nitrogen material in the carbon material                  to the nitrogen source is 1:0.05-0.5. The                  nitrogen element mass ratio of the catalyst                  intermediate to the nitrogen dopant is                  1:0.05-0.5. Preferred Method: The application                  method involves carrying out reforming reaction                  on methane/carbon dioxide at a temperature of 900                  degrees C under normal pressure, where the volume                  ratio of methane/carbon dioxide is 1:1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU SCHIFFMANN O
AE STEAG NEW ENERGIES GMBH (STGG-C)
GA 201818797K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding the ash (1) with a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream (2) in order to reduce the solubility of impurities from the ash. The ash is carbonated by the supply of the gas stream and is brought into a mold, in which the impurities contained in the ash such as heavy metals, lead and zinc, have a lower solubility from the ash. The gas stream is an exhaust gas stream of combustion process, and power plant (3).
   USE - Method for treatment of ash e.g. rust or boiler ash from thermal recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The convection belt dryer has the feature that the moist material is hardly subjected to any mechanical stress, as a result of which the gas from the gas stream remains dust-free. The gas is passed counter currently through the belt of the convection belt dryer, or in cross-flow through the perforated belt of the convection belt dryer. The overflow of the moist material over the flow that the pressure loss is lower, so that higher flow rates can be achieved. The flow through the moist material, allows a better mass transfer. The ash is supplied to reduce the solubility of impurities from the ash carbon dioxide having gas stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for treatment of ash.

GA 202250075A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of a carbonate of group (II) element involves introducing carbon dioxide into an alkaline adsorbent containing alkali and adsorbing it, adjusting the pH of the alkaline adsorbed solution that has adsorbed carbon dioxide to 10 or more, and adding a salt of a group (II) element to the alkaline adsorbed solution to produce a carbonate of the group (II) element.
   USE - Manufacture of carbonate of group (II) element for immobilizing greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of carbonate of group (II) element with excellent conversion efficiency and immobilizes the carbon dioxide.

   Carbon dioxide fixation device (20)

Preferred Composition: The amount of substance of the carbon dioxide introduced into the alkaline adsorbent is 50-90% with respect to the amount of substance of the alkali in the alkaline adsorbent. Preferred Properties: The pressure at which the carbon dioxide is introduced as the mixed gas calculated using partial pressure of carbon dioxide and total pressure of the mixed gas is 10-80%, and satisfy the relation (partial pressure of carbon dioxide/total pressure of the mixed gas)x 100. Preferred Components: The alkali is chosen from sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, and potassium carbonate. The salt of the group (II) element is chosen from calcium chloride, calcium nitrate, calcium sulfate, magnesium chloride, magnesium nitrate, and magnesium sulfate. Preferred Apparatus: The carbon dioxide fixation system further comprises an electrodialysis device (30) that produces an acid and an alkali by electrodialyzing the obtained liquid after separating the carbonate of the second group element, and a unit for supplying the alkali to the carbon dioxide adsorbing device.

PT P
PN CN113477223-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorption material            used in separation and recovery of carbon dioxide,            involves carbonizing high-molecular weight polymer,            activating, stirring activated material, weak base            solution and acetone and reacting.
AU ZHANG L
   DENG M
AE SHAANXI LINGSHENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (SHAA-Non-standard)
   XUNYANG LINGSHENG NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLO (XUNY-Non-standard)
GA 2021B85689
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorption                material involves (1) performing high-temperature                carbonization on high-molecular weight polymer                under protection of nitrogen, then performing                high-temperature activation in steam atmosphere to                obtain activated materials, and (2) functionalizing                activated material by adding activated material and                weak base solution to acetone, stirring, reacting                and volatilizing the acetone. The high-molecular                weight polymer is styrene, acrylic acid or                acrylonitrile series macroporous adsorption                resin.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorption                material used in separation and recovery of carbon                dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared carbon dioxide adsorption                material has high selectivity to carbon dioxide and                high adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the carbon dioxide adsorption material in                separation and recovery of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (1) involves                  passing nitrogen into container filled with                  high-molecular weight polymer, heating to                  100-800degrees Celsius at rate of 1-10degrees                  Celsius/minute under protection of nitrogen for                  carbonization, further heating to 800-1500degrees                  Celsius at rate of 1-10degrees Celsius/minute,                  activating in steam atmosphere for 10 minutes to                  5 hours, replacing steam with nitrogen, lowering                  temperature to room temperature, washing the                  product with acid and lye, washing with water to                  neutrality, and drying. The application method of                  carbon dioxide adsorption material involves                  pre-treating exhaust gas containing carbon                  dioxide by desulfurization, denitration and dust                  removal process, adsorbing and separating the                  pretreated carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas                  through adsorption column filled with carbon                  dioxide adsorbent material under water vapor                  pressure of 0.1-1 MPa, eluting adsorption column                  with lye at room temperature and pressure to                  obtain eluate, passing carbon dioxide gas to the                  obtained eluate until the solution becomes                  neutral, heating and evaporating the eluate to                  prepare carbonate.Preferred Condition: The step (2) is carried                  out at 25-60degrees Celsiusfor 3-8 hours.Preferred Component: The weak base solution                  is one or more of ethylene glycol amine,                  triethanolamine, and                  N-methyldiethanolamine.

PN CN114180604-A
TI Producing active magnesium oxide, involves pulping magnesium hydroxide filter cake to obtain magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid, introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide, filtering to obtain magnesium bicarbonate-containing heavy magnesium water, and pyrolyzing.
AU SHI J
   LI R
   SHEN J
   GENG Q
AE ANHUI JINHE IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202243157F
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing active magnesium oxide involves: adding water into waste residue containing basic magnesium chloride and organic impurities, heating, and separating organic solvent to obtain turbid liquid containing magnesium chloride, magnesium hydroxide and partial impurities; adding hydrogen peroxide into the turbid solution, and filtering to obtain a magnesium hydroxide filter cake and a magnesium chloride mother solution; putting the magnesium hydroxide filter cake into a reaction kettle, adding water, stirring and pulping to obtain uniform magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid; introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into the turbid magnesium hydroxide solution to carry out carbonization reaction, and filtering to obtain magnesium bicarbonate-containing heavy magnesium water; introducing steam into the heavy magnesium water for heating and pyrolysis to obtain basic magnesium carbonate suspension; and filtering to obtain basic magnesium carbonate, and calcining.
   USE - Method for producing active magnesium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-activity magnesium oxide takes basic magnesium carbonate waste residue and synthetic ammonia purge gas generated in spice production as raw materials, thus not only solving the problem of recycling magnesium resources, but also creating economic benefits, and avoiding the pollution of the waste residue on soil and surface water and the influence of carbon dioxide gas directly discharged into the atmosphere on the ecological environment. The pressure carbonization method is adopted, so that the carbonization time is shortened, the utilization rate of carbon dioxide is increased, the energy consumption is saved, the equipment investment is reduced, and the economic and ecological benefits are remarkable.

TI Method of co-producing X-type zeolite by using            blast furnace slag to mineralize carbon dioxide,            involves uniformly mixing fine grinding of blast            furnace and ammonium sulfate, adding sulfuric acid            solution and magnetic stirring at certain            temperature.

   WU H
   LIU W
   YANG C
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202210575E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves uniformly mixing the fine                grinding of 150m of blast furnace residue and                ammonium sulfate according to a certain proportion,                and adding sulfuric acid solution and magnetic                stirring at a certain temperature, and filtering to                obtain sulfate leaching liquid. Proper ammonia                water is added into the lixivium and pH is adjusted                so as to make the silicon and aluminum precipitate                to form silicon-aluminum gel. The aluminum                hydroxide, sodium hydroxides, sodium silicate are                dissolved according to the certain proportion in                proper water solution, and fully stirred to obtain                white sol. The solid product is washed with                de-ionized water to obtain the X-type zeolite, and                ammonia water is added into the semi-water gypsum                leaching residue. PN WO2021041218-A1
TI Graft copolymer for a composition used for article e.g., filter comprises semi-crystalline base polymer having amorphous domain and crystalline domain, and grafted polymer that selectively grafts onto semi-crystalline base polymer.
AU LESSER A J
   CAMARDA D S
   LAMPE M J
   STOLL K H
   GABRIEL C
   DESBOIS P
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   UNIV MASSACHUSETTS (UMAC-C)
GA 202122262L
AB    NOVELTY - A graft copolymer comprises a semi-crystalline base polymer having an amorphous domain and crystalline domain; where the semi-crystalline base polymer comprises an abstractable hydrogen; and grafted polymer that selectively grafts onto the semi-crystalline base polymer in the amorphous domain.
   USE - A graft copolymer for a composition (claimed) used for article e.g., filter, membrane, electrochemical cell (e.g., fuel cell), and consumer product.
   ADVANTAGE - Utilizing supercritical carbon dioxide as the solvent can dissolve the grafting monomer and can cause swelling of the amorphous domains. This combination of effects can allow for the grafting monomer to easily dissolve into the amorphous domains of the base polymer. The method of grafting can be used to easily upcycle semi-crystalline polymeric waste in a cost-effective, sustainable way.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for forming a graft copolymer which involves forming a mixture comprising a semi-crystalline base polymer, grafting monomer, and initiator; where the semi-crystalline base polymer comprises an abstractable hydrogen; where the semi-crystalline base polymer comprises an amorphous domain and a crystalline domain; introducing carbon dioxide to the mixture in a closed chamber; and reacting the grafting monomer at temperature and pressure conditions above the supercritical point of carbon dioxide to form a supercritical carbon dioxide, but below a melting temperature of the crystalline domain of the semi-crystalline base polymer to selectively polymerize the grafts in the amorphous domain to form the graft copolymer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view of the selectively grafted copolymer.
   Grafted side chains (g)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixture further comprises a co-solvent capable of dissolving the grafting monomer. The co-solvent comprises greater than or equal to 1 of 1-4 carbon-containing (1-4C) alkyl alcohol, acetone, or dimethyl formamide. The initiator comprises greater than or equal to 1 of acetylcyclohexanesulfonyl peroxide, diacetyl peroxydicarbonate, diethyl peroxydicarbonate, dicyclohexyl peroxydicarbonate, di-2-ethylhexyl peroxydicarbonate, tert.-butyl perneodecanoate, 2,2'-azobis(methoxy-2,4-dimethylvaleronitrile), tert.-butyl perpivalate, dioctanoyl peroxide, dilauroyl peroxide, 2,2'-azobis(2,4-dimethylvaleronitrile), tert.-butylazo-2-cyanobutane, dibenzoyl peroxide, tert.-butyl per-2-ethylhexanoate, tert.-butyl permaleate, 2,2-azobis(isobutyronitrile), bis(tert.-butylperoxy) cyclohexane, tert.-butyl peroxyisopropylcarbonate, tert.-butyl peroxybenzoate, tert.-butyl peracetate, 2,2-bis(tert.-butylperoxy) butane, dicumyl peroxide, di-tert.-amyl peroxide, di-tert.-butyl peroxide, p-methane hydroperoxide, pinane hydroperoxide, cumene hydroperoxide, or tert.-butyl hydroperoxide. The ratio by volume of co-solvent to grafting monomer in the mixture is 5:1-1:5, 3:1-1:3, or 2:1-1:2; the amount of initiator 0.05-1 mol.% based on the total number of moles of the grafting monomer; the mixture comprises 10-99 wt.% grafting monomer polymer based on the total weight of the mixture; or the mixture comprises 1-80 wt.% semi-crystalline base polymer based on the total weight of the mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The semi-crystalline base polymer comprises greater than or equal to 1 of polyamide, polyester (e.g., poly(ethylene terephthalate), poly(butylene terephthalate), or poly(trimethylene terephthalate)), poly(lactic acid), polyketone, polyacetal (e.g., polyoxymethylene), or polyurethane. The semi-crystalline base polymer comprises greater than or equal to 1 of polyamide or a polyester. The semi-crystalline base polymer comprises greater than or equal to 2 different blocks of different repeat units; where greater than or equal to 1 block forms the amorphous domain and a different block forms the crystalline domain. The grafted polymer is derived from greater than or equal to 1 of olefin, non-conjugated diene, monovinylidene aromatic monomer (e.g., styrene, alpha -methylstyrene, o-methyl styrene, p-methyl styrene, tert.-butylstyrene, vinyltoluene, or dichlorostyrene), methacrylic acid, an alkyl (meth)acrylate monomer, a trialkoxysilane (meth)acrylate, acrylonitrile, vinyl acetate, N-vinylpyrrolidinone, 2-isopropenyl-2-oxazoline, a vinyl halide, or a vinyl-terminated siloxane. The graft copolymer comprises 10-95 w.t%, or 20-80 wt.% grafted polymer and 5-90 wt.%, or 20-80 wt.% semi-crystalline base polymer based on the total weight of the graft copolymer. The composition further comprises greater than or equal to 1 of polymer different from graft copolymer or additive. Preferred Parameters: The graft copolymer has a crystallinity of 5-40 wt.%, or 5-30 wt.% based on the total weight of the graft copolymer. The semi-crystalline polymer is in the form of a powder, bead, pellet or strand or is an article. The semi-crystalline polymer is in the form of a uniaxially or biaxially stretched film. Preferred Process: Reacting comprises greater than or equal to 1 of free radical polymerization, ring opening metathesis polymerization, or living free radical polymerization. Method further includes removing a residual grafting monomer from the graft copolymer, optionally by washing or drying the graft copolymer. The method further comprises reacting a second grafting monomer at temperature and pressure conditions above the supercritical point of carbon dioxide to form supercritical carbon dioxide and above the melting temperature of crystalline domain of semi-crystalline base polymer.

PT P
PN CN112430532-A
TI Producing earthworm stock solution, by pouring carbon dioxide gas into closed reaction tank containing purified water, earthworm and sugar, fermenting mixture, cooling, stirring cooled earthworm stock solution, and collecting solution.
AU HAO Z
   BO J
   CAO R
   LI Q
   WANG T
   LI F
   GAO Y
AE TANGSHAN YINLING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
   TANGSHAN XINGAINIAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 202126006J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing earthworm stock solution by performing carbon-dioxide anaerobic fermentation involves (a) putting purified water, earthworm and sugar into a closed reaction tank, (b) pouring carbon dioxide gas into the closed reaction tank, (c) adjusting pressure and temperature in the closed reaction tank, performing fermentation, and obtaining the earthworm stock solution, (d) cooling the original earthworm solution in the reaction tank after the fermentation is completed, (e) stirring and separating the cooled earthworm stock solution to obtain earthworm stock solutions of different densities, and (f) collecting and saving the earthworm stock solutions of different densities.
   USE - The method is useful for producing earthworm stock solution by performing carbon-dioxide anaerobic fermentation. The system is useful for operating method for preparing earthworm stock solution (claimed). The earthworm stock solution is used in field including medical treatment, cosmetics, feed, fertilizer and preservative.
   ADVANTAGE - The method retains beneficial component including protein and amino acid in earthworm liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system, which comprises gas cylinder and reaction tank, where (i) the gas cylinder is in communication with the air inlet of the reaction tank, and (ii) the reaction tank is provided with a centrifugal stirring device, a heating wire and a cooling tube, and the centrifugal stirring device is arranged in the center of the reaction tank, the heating wire is arranged at the bottom of the reaction tank, and the cooling pipe is arranged around the outer wall of the reaction tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding earthworm and purified water in a ratio of 1:1-1:2, and using 1-5 %mass sugar with respect to total weight of earthworm and purified water. The step (c) involves maintaining pressure in reaction tank to 0.1-0.5 Mp and temperature in reaction tank to 25-40 degrees C during fermentation process, and performing fermentation for 3-6 hours. In step (d), temperature in reaction tank is less than or equal to 20 degrees C, after cooling. In step (e), earthworm stock solution comprises a supernatant liquid with a density of 0.6-1 g/cm3, clear liquid with a density of 1-1.5 g/cm3 and solid-liquid mixture with a density of 1.5-2 g/cm3, after stirring and separation. The step (f) involves storing the earthworm stock solution in an environment protected from light and at a temperature of less than 30 degrees C. Preferred Device: The top of the reaction tank is provided with a feed port, and the side wall is provided with a several liquid discharge ports. The reaction tank is also provided with a pressure gauge, a thermometer and an observation port.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Chicken house-greenhouse gas integrated system comprises carbon dioxide separating tank composition and oxygen separation tank composition arranged between vegetable greenhouse and chicken house, and gas emitted from vegetable greenhouse.
AU YU W
   CAO F
   WANG G
   CAI J
   CHEN J
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2020425620
AB    NOVELTY - Chicken house-greenhouse gas integrated system comprises a carbon dioxide separating tank composition (3) and an oxygen separation tank composition (4) arranged between a vegetable greenhouse (1) and a chicken house (2) connected through a transmission pipe I (5) and a transmission pipe II forming a gas passage. The carbon dioxide separating tank composition includes multiple medicine pots, a gas washing tank I, a gas washing tank II and a liquid inlet pipe. A chemical liquid is transferred between the medicine pot, the gas washing tank I and the gas washing tank II through the two liquid inlet pipes. A gas washing tank IV and a gas washing tank V are connected by the transmission pipe II. A gas is emitted from the vegetable greenhouse and successively passes through the transmission pipe II enters a gas washing tank III, the gas washing tank IV and the gas washing tank V and transfer to the chicken house through the transmission pipe II at output end of the gas washing tank V.
   USE - Used as chicken house-greenhouse gas integrated system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system purifies the rich oxygen gas in the vegetable greenhouse, removes the harmful gas and sends to the closed chicken house, solves the adverse effects of harmful gases in their respective environments, and provides powerful environmental conditions for growth of the other party's producers.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an overall structure of the chicken house-greenhouse gas integrated system.
   Vegetable greenhouse (1)
   Chicken house (2)
   Carbon dioxide separating tank composition (3)
   Oxygen separation tank composition (4)
   Transmission pipe I (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide separation tank are arranged with aqueous solution of cuprous chloride and copper chloride hydrochloric acid solution. The oxygen separating tank are respectively arranged with copper chloride cuprous chloride hydrochloric acid solution, aqueous solution and sodium hydroxide solution.

PN CN111088024-A
TI Washing agent for carbon dioxide oil displacement            method, comprises cationic surfactant which is at least            one of quaternary ammonium salts, and quaternary            ammonium bases, and anionic surfactant, which is            phenoxybenzene derivative.
AU SHA O
   LI Y
   ZHANG W
   SHEN Z
   HE X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 202038812W
AB    NOVELTY - A washing agent comprises cationic surfactant                and anionic surfactant in molar ratio of                (1:0.01)-(1:100). The cationic surfactant is at                least one of quaternary ammonium salts, and                quaternary ammonium bases. The anionic surfactant                is phenoxybenzene derivative (I).
   USE - Washing agent for carbon dioxide oil                displacement method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high temperature high salt washing agent                overcomes the problem that the surfactant can                achieve ultra-low interfacial tension in the                existing oil reservoir environment of the oil                field, and the problem of low crude oil stripping                ability and low oil washing efficiency on the rock,                and improves the recovery factor in oil                fields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A washing agent comprises cationic surfactant                and anionic surfactant in molar ratio of                (1:0.01)-(1:100). The cationic surfactant is at                least one of quaternary ammonium salts, and                quaternary ammonium bases. The anionic surfactant                is phenoxybenzene derivative of formula (I).
   R1 = 8-30C aliphatic group;
   R-2 = H or 8-30C aliphatic group;and
   M, M' = cations or cationic groups.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of washing agent, which                involves dissolving anionic surfactant and cationic                surfactant in water separately, mixing, and                stirring mixed solution of anionic and cationic                composite surfactant prepared; and
   (2) carbon dioxide flooding method, which                involves injecting oil displacement agent                comprising 0.01-5 %mass washing agent and carbon                dioxide into oil reservoir, contacting with crude                oil, and displacing crude oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The quaternary ammonium                  salt is chosen from tetraalkyl quaternary                  ammonium salts. The quaternary ammonium base is                  chosen from tetraalkyl quaternary ammonium bases.                  Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide flooding                  method preferably involves dissolving anionic                  surfactant and cationic surfactant in water,                  mixing to obtain washing agent, mix washing agent                  with 92-99.98 %mass water, stirring for 1-3 hours                  to obtain oil displacing agent, using water to                  displace the oil until it is oil-free under the                  reservoir temperature conditions at 60-120                  degrees C, transferring the oil displacing agent                  prepared, and alternately injecting carbon                  dioxide and oil displacing agent, till the water                  content is 100%.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = dodecyl, hexadecyl or octadecyl;
   R2 = H, dodecyl, hexadecyl or                octadecyl;and
   M, M' = Na+, NH4+, Ca2+, Mg2+ or Al3+.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

AU WANG J

AE CHENGDU TAIMENG SOFTWARE CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020387708
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a control device (2) that is arranged inside a housing. A flow measuring device (5) is provided at the air inlet of the measuring device and is connected to the collecting pipe. A collection pipeline (6) includes two gas delivery ports at the outlet end and the return end for storing the exhaled gas of the subject. The gas outlet of the collection pipe is connected to the gas inlet of an oxygen concentration measuring device (3). The outlet end of the oxygen concentration measuring device is connected to the inlet end of a carbon dioxide concentration measuring device (4). The outlet end of the carbon dioxide concentration measuring device is connected to the return end of the collection pipe.    USE - Basic metabolic measurement device.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaled gas flow rate, exhaled carbon dioxide concentration, and exhaled oxygen concentration of the human body can be measured in real time, and the respiratory quotient and metabolic rate can be calculated in combination with the body weight and height.
