PT P
PN US2020124330-A1; US10663201-B2
TI Carbon dioxide refrigeration system used for            cooling temperature controlled display devices, has            controller that operates pressure control valve to            drive pressure of refrigerant leaving gas            cooler-condenser to supercritical pressure.
AU HAYES N M
AE HILL PHOENIX INC (DOVE-C)
GA 2020317953
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a gas cooler-condenser                (2) that removes heat from a refrigerant and that                discharges the refrigerant into a high pressure                conduit. A temperature sensor (31) is located along                high pressure conduit, and measures a temperature                of refrigerant leaving the gas cooler-condenser. A                pressure sensor (34) is located along high pressure                conduit, and measures a pressure of refrigerant                leaving the gas cooler-condenser. A pressure                control valve regulates pressure of refrigerant                leaving the gas cooler-condenser. A controller (50)                determines whether refrigerant leaving the gas                cooler-condenser is in subcritical region based on                measured temperature or pressure of refrigerant,                and calculates a supercritical pseudo-saturated                pressure as a function of the summed temperature.                The controller operates pressure control valve to                drive the pressure of refrigerant leaving the gas                cooler-condenser to supercritical pseudo-saturated                pressure corresponding to the summed                temperature.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system used for                cooling temperature controlled display devices such                as cases and merchandisers in supermarkets and                other similar facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - High side pressure setpoint is generated to                ensure that the carbon dioxide refrigerant exiting                gas cooler/condenser with desired amount of                subcooling. Well-defined and widely-accepted                saturated temperatures and pressures where                evaporation and condensation processes can occur                can be realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                carbon dioxide refrigeration system.
   Gas cooler-condenser (2)
   Temperature sensor (31)
   Pressure sensor (34)
   Controller (50)
   Refrigeration system (100)

PN WO2020072630-A1; CA3115215-A1; AU2019351922-A1; IN202147019570-A; BR112021006463-A2; EP3860367-A1; US2022000129-A1; JP2022512592-W; EP3860367-A4
TI Product comprises flexible packaging, and cut            potato product provided within flexible packaging,            where potato product includes moisture and fat.
AU RIZVI S S H
   SARAN V P
   RIZVI S S
AE UNIV CORNELL (CORR-C)
   UNIV CORNELL (CORR-C)
   UNIV CORNELL (CORR-C)
   UNIV CORNELL (CORR-C)
GA 202027599G
AB    NOVELTY - Product comprises a flexible packaging, and a                cut potato product provided within the flexible                packaging, where the potato product comprises a pH                below 4.6 throughout the cut potato product, where                the pH varies less than about 0.15 throughout the                cut potato product. The potato product includes                65-85 wt.% moisture and 0-2 wt.% fat.
   USE - Product.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has equilibrium pH below 4.6,                shelf stability, color profile, fat to moisture                ratio, texture profile, cooking profile, and flavor                profile, saves cost for material by utilizing                sufficient breathable material to avoid bursting of                the bags during the process, and has significantly                less energy requirements and eliminates the                requirements for the frozen or refrigerated supply                chain post processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for making a product, which involves:
   (A) cutting a potato to form a cut potato                product;
   (B) blanching the cut potato product;
   (C) disposing the cut potato product in a                package;
   (D) adding processing aids to the                package;
   (E) treating the cut potato product in a                chamber with a supercritical carbon dioxide under                conditions to prevent outflow of supercritical                carbon dioxide from the chamber during the process                to infuse the processing aids into the cut potato                product;
   (F) depressurizing the treated cut potato                product to trap the processing aids;
   (G) depressurizing the cut potato product at                atmospheric pressure; and
   (H) sealing the portion of the package to                permit passage of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of                the method for producing a shelf stable potato fry                product.
   Sorting and washing (105)
   Peeled (110)
   Pre-heating and cutting (115)
   Blanching (120)
   Processing super critical carbon dioxide                (145)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The flexible packaging                  lacks an acidic solution suspension. The                  processing aids include nisin, distilled water                  vinegar, vinegar, lemon juice, lemon juice                  concentrate, apple juice, apple juice                  concentrate, cumin seed, ginger, garlic, lactic                  acid, gluconic acid, malic acid, peroxyacetic                  acid, tartaric acid, acetic acid, acetic acid                  derivatives, sodium bisulfate,                  gluconodeltalactone, citric acid, buffer of                  lactic acid, buffer of gluconic acid, buffer of                  malic acid, buffer of peroxyacetic acid, buffer                  of tartaric acid, buffer of acetic acid, buffer                  of acetic acid derivatives, buffer of citric                  acid, oleoresins, vegetable oil, canola oil,                  truffle oil, onion extract, clove, clove                  extracts, paprika extracts, cumin, cumin                  extracts, deionized water, and distilled                  water.

TI Carbon-dioxide recovery type fuel cell power            generation system, includes flow volume control valve            which adjusts flow volume of air electrode off gas            supplied to deoxygenation unit which separates oxygen            from air electrode off gas.
AU KAWABATA Y
   MATSUZAKI Y
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
   TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 202017257W
AB    NOVELTY - The fuel cell power generation system (10A)                has a fuel cell (12,14) to which a fuel electrode                off gas is sent from a fuel electrode (12A,14A),                and air electrode gas is sent from an air electrode                (12B,14B). A branch air electrode off-gas flow path                (P6) branches a portion of air electrode off gas                sent from the air electrode. A deoxygenation unit                (24) is connected with the branch air electrode                off-gas flow path, and separates oxygen from the                air electrode off gas. A flow volume control valve                (42) adjusts the flow volume of the air electrode                off gas supplied to the deoxygenation unit. A                combustion unit (22) carries out the combustion                reaction of the oxygen separated in the                deoxygenation unit and the fuel electrode off gas.                A condenser (26) separates carbon-dioxide rich gas                from the combustion off gas sent from the                combustion unit.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide recovery type fuel cell power                generation system for generating electric power                using carbon compound fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - High-concentration carbon dioxide can be                recovered from fuel electrode off gas with simple                adjustment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                fuel cell power generation system. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
   Fuel cell power generation system                (10A)
   Fuel cell (12,14)
   Fuel electrode (12A,14A)
   Air electrode (12B,14B)
   Combustion unit (22)
   Deoxygenation unit (24)
   Condenser (26)
   Flow volume control valve (42)
   Branch air electrode off-gas flow path                (P6)

PN KR2018109829-A; KR2001986-B1
TI Method and apparatus useful for producing lithium carbonate comprises supplying lithium hydroxide solution to reaction vessel, supplying normal carbon dioxide gas to lithium hydroxide solution surface, and stirring.
AU JUNG W C
   LEE S M
   CHOI J H
   PARK K S
   PARK S K
   LEE H W
   PARK W K
   KIM D H
   KO K K
   JUNG W
   PARK K
   PARK S
   PARK W
   KIM D
   KO K
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
   RES INST IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (POSC-C)
GA 201879905M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lithium carbonate comprises (a) supplying a lithium hydroxide solution to a reaction vessel, (b) supplying normal carbon dioxide gas to the solution surface of the lithium hydroxide solution in the reaction vessel, and (c) stirring the lithium hydroxide solution so that the solution surface of the lithium hydroxide solution remains in a V-shape when the normal-pressure carbonic acid gas is supplied into the reaction vessel under atmospheric pressure.
   USE - The method and apparatus are useful for producing lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and apparatus prevent vapor phase carbonation and wall adhesion in atmospheric pressure environment, and improve production yield of lithium carbonate, and ensure effective and accurate reaction control means by confirming highest temperature through temperature monitoring and terminating reaction after 10% of the time reaching the maximum temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for lithium carbonate producing apparatus comprising a reaction vessel (100) for containing a lithium hydroxide solution and carrying out the reaction under atmospheric pressure, a lithium hydroxide solution supply pipe connected to the reaction vessel, a pressurized carbon dioxide gas storage tank connected to the reaction vessel for supplying atmospheric carbon dioxide gas to the solution surface of the lithium hydroxide solution in the reaction vessel, and a stirrer (200) provided in the reaction vessel for stirring the lithium hydroxide solution so that the solution surface of the lithium hydroxide solution maintains V-shape.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the lithium carbonate producing apparatus.
   Reaction vessel (100)
   Stirrer (200)
   Rotary shaft (210)
   Drive motor (220)
   Impeller (230)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the pressure of the atmospheric carbon dioxide gas is set to pressure higher than the pressure in the reaction vessel, preferably 1.1-1.5 bar. In the step (a), the lithium hydroxide solution is supplied to the reaction vessel in strong base state at pH of 11-12. The step (b) comprises decompressing the carbon dioxide gas to normal carbon dioxide gas, and controlling the supply of the atmospheric carbon dioxide gas supplied into the reaction vessel. In the step (c), the lithium hydroxide solution in the reaction vessel is stirred by rotation of the impeller of the stirrer, preferably at rotation speed of 200-800 revolutions/minute. The method further comprises (d) discharging the generated lithium carbonate to the outside of the reaction vessel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pressure of the atmospheric carbon dioxide gas is set to pressure higher than the pressure in the reaction vessel. The supply pipe supplies the lithium hydroxide solution in strong basic state of pH 11 to pH 12 to the reaction vessel. The carbon dioxide gas storage tank is connected to a decompression device. The stirrer comprises a drive motor (220) provided at the upper end of the rotary shaft (210), and at least one impeller (230) installed at a lower end of the rotary shaft. The rotary shaft has rotating speed of 200-800 revolutions/minute. The atmospheric carbon dioxide storage tank is connected to the reaction vessel by a connection pipe. The connection pipe is provided with a connection pipe opening/closing control valve. The apparatus further comprises a discharge pipe for discharging lithium carbonate in the reaction vessel is provided at a lower end of the reaction vessel. The discharge pipe is provided with a discharge pipe opening/closing control valve.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 201878361R
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of a waste-derived carbon                dioxide-activated clinker involves firing nodules                at 1000-1100 degrees C for a time sufficient to                obtain carbon dioxide-reactive clinker phases                within the nodules, cooling the clinker nodules and                reducing to powder to obtain a clinker powder. The                nodules are agglomerates of a stoichiometric mix of                uniformly-sized powders of municipal solid waste                (MSW) incineration residues. The stoichiometric mix                comprises 35-45 wt.% calcium oxide, 2-8 wt.%                aluminum oxide, and 12-20 wt.% silicon dioxide, and                remaining optionally comprising oxides or compounds                of magnesium, iron, sodium, potassium, phosphorus,                sulfur, carbon and chlorine. The final                stoichiometric mix comprises 1-10 wt.% sulfur, 2-20                wt.% carbon and 2-15 wt.% chlorine.
   USE - Synthesis of waste-derived carbon                dioxide-activated clinker used for producing                building products for non-reinforced                low-load-bearing or non-load-bearing concrete                precast applications (all claimed). Uses include                but are not limited to dry-cast or precast products                such as pavers, interlock, bricks, retaining walls,                architectural stone, masonry units and lightweight                aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The clinker can achieve rapid binding strength                from carbonation, as well as additional latent                strength from subsequent hydraulic reactivity. The                presence of certain oxides can be used in lending a                fluxing effect, to aid with firing efficiency and                also in lending a doping effect to help stabilize                desired mineral species.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) primed bottom ash; and
   (2) manufacture of building units.

TI Combined carbon dioxide laser tube has total reflection cavity, folding cavity and window cavity which are respectively connected to gas storage pipe through gas return pipe, and gas storage pipe which is provided with exhaust port.
AU ZHOU X
AE JIAXING HUAYAN LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAX-Non-standard)
GA 201871705T
AB    NOVELTY - The laser tube has a total reflection mirror (22) which is mounted in total reflection chamber (21). The total reflection mirror is connected to a discharge tube (4). A window coupling lens (32) facing the discharge tube is installed in a window cavity (31). Each of discharge tubes is placed in a water-cooled tube (41). Two ends of water-cooling tube are provided with inlet and outlet nozzles (42). Two ends of discharge tube are provided with connecting elements (6) for connecting folding cavity (51), total reflection chamber and the window cavity. A positive electrode (61) is arranged on the connecting element at one end of the discharge tube, and a negative electrode (62) is arranged on the connecting element at other end. The total reflection cavity, folding cavity and the window cavity are respectively connected to a gas storage pipe (1) through a gas return pipe (11). The gas storage pipe is provided with an exhaust port (12). The connecting element is made of metal.
   USE - Combined carbon dioxide laser tube.
   ADVANTAGE - The combined carbon dioxide laser tube achieves good stability and uses root discharge tubes to provide prerequisite for improving the laser power.

PN CN108405549-A; CN108405549-B
TI Method for controlling fouling and plugging of            landfill by using slag and landfill gas, involves            replacing slag in treatment room after slag in            treatment room is treated for predetermined days, and            transporting slag to landfill.
AU XU Q
   WANG Q
   SHAO M
   WU H
AE UNIV PEKING SHENZHEN GRADUATE SCHOOL (UYPK-C)
GA 2018660166
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves discharging an incinerated                slag to a slag landfill layer (5) in a treatment                room (16). The treatment room is sealed. The                landfill gas is transported into the treatment room                through the intake pipe. The landfill gas reacts                with the slag. The carbon dioxide concentration in                the gas of the gas port is detected, after a                certain period of reaction. The landfill gas                returns from the pipeline back to the treatment                room to continue the reaction, when the carbon                dioxide concentration is higher than 20%. The                landfill gas after the reaction is collected, when                the carbon dioxide concentration is lower than 20%.                The slag in the treatment room is replaced, after                the slag in the treatment room is treated for 3-5                days. The slag after the reaction is transported to                the landfill for landfill.
   USE - Method for controlling fouling and plugging of                landfill by using slag and landfill gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The problem of scaling and clogging of slag                and domestic garbage is solved. The removal                efficiency of carbon dioxide is improved. The                quality of landfill gas is purified. The                utilization efficiency of landfill gas is improved.                The treatment of incinerator slag resources is                simultaneously performed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for controlling fouling and plugging of landfill by                using slag and landfill gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device for preventing and controlling fouling of a                landfill.
   Discharge gate (1)
   Discharge platform (3)
   Slag discharge (4)
   Sand landfill layer (5)
   Treatment chamber (16)

PN KR2018074630-A; KR1938521-B1
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide electric power system for preventing low temperature corrosion, has recuperator arranged for heating working fluid through compressor that is supplied to heat exchangers, heated and branched to turbine.
AU CHA S H
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201854132D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor (100) that compresses working fluid (4A,4B,7B). Several heat exchangers (300,310,330) are provided for receiving heat from an outer heat source and heating up the working fluid. Multiple turbines (400,410,430) are driven with the working fluid. Recuperators (200,210,230) for heat-exchanging the working fluid are passed through the turbine. The working fluid is passed through the compressor to cool the working fluid passing through the turbine and heat the working fluid passing through the compressor. A condenser (500) is provided for cooling the working fluid cooled by the recuperators to supply the working fluid to the compressor. The two recuperators are arranged parallel to each other. The working fluid heated by the recuperator through the compressor is supplied to other one of the heat exchangers, heated and branched to the turbine.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide electric power system for preventing low temperature corrosion.
   ADVANTAGE - The low temperature corrosion phenomenon of the external heat exchanger is reduced and the lifetime of the external heat exchanger is increased. The reliability of the supercritical carbon dioxide electric power system and external heat exchanger is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide electric power system for preventing low temperature corrosion.
   Working fluids (4A,4B,7B)
   Compressor (100)
   Recuperators (200,210,230)
   Heat exchangers (300,310,330)
   Turbines (400,410,430)
   Condenser (500)

TI System for power generation, has recycle line which introduces portion of regenerated sorbent from calciner to hydrogen generator, and power generator which receives and utilizes portion of hydrogen product from gas/solids separator.
AU SUBBARAMAN G
   MAYS J A
   STANIS R J
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201846691J
AB    NOVELTY - The system (210) has a compact hydrogen generator (212) which contains a quantity of a sorbent material. A feed material produces hydrogen product and carbon dioxide, and the sorbent material absorbs carbon dioxide and forms a used sorbent. A gas/solids separator (239) is connected to the compact hydrogen generator to separate the hydrogen product and the used sorbent. A calciner (272) is connected to the gas/solids separator to heat the used sorbent to desorb carbon dioxide from the used sorbent to produce regenerated sorbent. A recycle line is provided to introduce at least a portion of the regenerated sorbent from the calciner to the compact hydrogen generator. At least one power generator receives and utilizes at least a portion of the hydrogen product from the gas/solids separator to produce power. The compact hydrogen generator comprises a fluidized bed, sorbent enhanced reformer.
   USE - System for power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The compact hydrogen generator for the economical production of electricity can be advantageously utilized. The capital and operating costs can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for power generation from natural gas through use of hydrogen fuel; and
   (2) a method for producing power through hydrogen gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the system for converting natural gas assets to electric power, hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   System for power generation (210)
   Compact hydrogen generator (212)
   Desulfurizer unit (222)
   Gas/solids separator (239)
   Calciner (272)

TI Herbicidal composition useful for controlling weeds, comprises mixed oil of cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract.
AU HAN J K
   LIM B H
   PARK S A
AE PACIFIC SCI CO (PACI-Non-standard)
GA 2018460554
AB    NOVELTY - Herbicidal composition comprises mixed oil of cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract.
   USE - The herbicidal composition is useful for controlling weeds.
   ADVANTAGE - The herbicidal composition is environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing herbicidal composition, comprising either: (a) drying cottonseed, clove flower, garlic, lavender leaves, cinnamon bark and Yucca roots at low-temperature, (b) pulverizing the dried cottonseed, clove flower, garlic, lavender leaves, cinnamon bark and Yucca roots to obtain mixture, (c) drying the mixture, (d) subjecting the dried mixture to supercritical extraction method using carbon dioxide to obtain an extract, and (e) separating carbon dioxide from the extract to prepare the mixed oil of cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract; (i) drying cottonseed, clove flower, garlic, lavender leaves, cinnamon bark and Yucca roots at low-temperature, (ii) pulverizing the dried cottonseed, clove flower, garlic, lavender leaves, cinnamon bark and Yucca roots to obtain pulverized product, and (iii) subjecting the pulverized product to supercritical extraction method using carbon dioxide to obtain an extract; or (A) drying cottonseed, clove flower, garlic, lavender leaves, cinnamon bark and Yucca roots at low-temperature, (B) pulverizing the dried cottonseed, clove flower, garlic, lavender leaves, cinnamon bark and Yucca roots to obtain pulverized product, (C) subjecting the pulverized product to supercritical extraction method using carbon dioxide to produce cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract, and (D) mixing the extracted cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed oil comprises cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract in weight ratio of 5-25:15-25:5-15:15-25:5-15. Preferred Method: The cottonseed oil, clove oil, garlic oil, lavender oil, cinnamon oil and Yucca extract are extracted by supercritical extraction method. The step (d) is carried out at 40-65 degrees C for 2-5 hours under pressure of 150-200 bar. The steps (a),(i) and (A) are carried out at low-temperature of 30-40 degrees C. The extract in step (iii) is obtained by preparing primary extract by supercritical extraction under pressure of 150-200 bar for 3-5 hours, and preparing a secondary extract by supercritical extraction at 40-50 degrees C for 2 hours under pressure of 15-20 MPa.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.)

TI Composition useful for preparing one-step embryonic culture fluid, comprises alpha-lipoic acid, acetyl L-carnitine or acetyl L-carnitine hydrochloride composite.
AU YU Y
   DAI Z
   WANG J
   JIA X
   MI Y
   LIU H
   YAN F
   MI L
AE CHENGDU AIVFO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201841366K
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises ( plus minus )- alpha -lipoic acid, acetyl L-carnitine or acetyl L-carnitine hydrochloride composite in molar ratio of 0.055-0.098:0.044-0.078.
   USE - The composition is useful for preparing one-step embryonic culture fluid (claiemd).
   ADVANTAGE - The embryonic culture fluid retains the active components secreted into the culture fluid by the embryos; can protect cells and inhibit the decomposition and use of harmful products; and avoids possible mechanical damage during embryo transfer with the problem of low blastocyst formation rate due to stress caused by different culture environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) one-step embryonic culture fluid comprising the composition and a basic culture medium composition, where the molar ratio of the composition to the basic culture medium is 0.055-0.098:0.044-0.078:135.251-167.614; and
   (2) preparing embryonic culture fluid, comprising (i) preparing mixed solution I by mixing sodium chloride, potassium chloride, magnesium sulfate, glucose, gentamicin, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, sodium pyruvate, alanyl glutamine, taurine, D-sorbitol, calcium chloride, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium bicarbonate, ( plus minus )- alpha -lipoic acid and acetyl L-carnitine hydrochloride or acetyl L-carnitine with an aqueous medium, (ii) washing mixing solution I with carbon-dioxide shielding gas, (iii) adding the above-mentioned components of amino acids and human serum albumin during the process of flushing carbon-dioxide shielding gas to obtain mixed solution II, (iv) filtering and sterilizing mixed solution II in a carbon-dioxide incubator with a 0.2 mu m membrane filter for 5-10 hours, and (v) assembling the well-balanced solution II aseptically in the carbon-dioxide protective gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of ( plus minus )- alpha -lipoic acid and acetyl L-carnitine or acetyl L-carnitine hydrochloride is 0.065-0.098:0.054-0.078. The basic culture medium comprises an inorganic salt buffer solution, amino acids, EDTA, sodium pyruvate, alanyl glutamine, taurine, glucose, D-sorbitol, human serum albumin and bactericide. The molar ratio of inorganic salt buffer solution, amino acids, EDTA, sodium pyruvate, alanyl glutamine, taurine, glucose and D-sorbitol, is 98.303-120.12:1.2-3.8:0.009-0.012:9.459-11.561:24.741-30.239:0.432-0.528:0.9-1.1:0.207-0.254. The human serum albumin content in 100 ml of one-step embryo culture fluid is 0.45-0.55 g/l, and the content of bactericide in 100ml one-step embryo culture fluid is 45-55mg/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic salt buffer solution comprises sodium chloride, potassium chloride, magnesium sulfate, calcium chloride, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and sodium bicarbonate in a molar ratio of 89.937-109.923:5-6.083:0.9-1.1:1.62-1.98:0.135-0.165:0.135-0.165:0.576-0.704. Preferred Method: In the steps (ii) and (v), the amount of carbon-dioxide passed is 6-7 vol.%, respectively. The equilibrium condition in the incubator in step (iv) is 37 degrees C, 5-10 hours in 6% carbon-dioxide incubator, and pH adjusted to 7.20-7.40.

PT P
PN DE102016119010-A1; WO2018065253-A1; CN109803916-A; BR112019006972-A2; EP3523237-A1; US2020172386-A1; EP3523237-B1; US11040863-B2
TI Method for tapping carbonated beverage from dispensing system, involves making carbonated beverage to experience pressure drop above equilibrium pressure of carbonated beverage when filter flows through.
AU SONNENREIN G
AE BIOLOGIC GMBH (BIOL-Non-standard)
   SONNENREIN G (SONN-Individual)
   SONNENREIN G (SONN-Individual)
   SONNENREIN G (SONN-Individual)
GA 2018275879
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing carbonated beverage (19) through a line (3) through a flow regulator (5) and a filter (7) arranged downstream of the flow regulator. The carbonated beverage is made to experience a pressure drop above the equilibrium pressure of the carbonated beverage when the filter flows through. The flow rate by the quantity regulator is set to 2 L/min. The carbonated beverage is provided with carbon dioxide content of 6 g/L. the flow regulator and the filter are arranged in a cartridge unit (25).
   USE - Method for tapping carbonated beverage from dispensing system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Since the sum of the ambient pressure and the dynamic pressure is above the equilibrium pressure of the carbon dioxide in the carbonated beverage at its given temperature, the equilibrium pressure of dissolved carbon dioxide in water at 8 degrees C is about 1.6 bar. Thus, the back pressure of more than 0.6 bar resulting from passing through the filter is still required in order to avoid a sudden relaxation of the carbonated liquid in the flow regulator to a pressure which is below the equilibrium pressure of the solution dissolved in the carbonated liquid carbon dioxide. Hence, the tapping method is realized in a simple manner by selecting the filter and suitable dimensioning.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbonated beverage dispensing system; and
   (2) a cartridge unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic views of the dispensing system.
   Line (3)
   Flow regulator (5)
   Filter (7)
   Carbonated beverage (19)
   Cartridge unit (25)

TI Electrocatalyst used as cathode catalyst for            carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises metal organic            compounds one or two and one or more aromatic compounds            containing hydroxyl.
AU ZHONG H
   ZHANG H
   QIU Y
   LI X
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201825227J
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalyst comprises metal organic                compounds one or two and one or more aromatic                compounds containing hydroxyl.
   USE - Electrocatalyst used as a cathode catalyst for                carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction                in process of electrochemical reduction of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrocatalyst improves activity and                selectivity of catalyst, strength and reduces                electronic structure of carbon-nitrogen bond to                increase number of active sites, and adjusts                adsorption strength of reactants and intermediate                products on catalyst surface, increases activity                and selectivity of catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing electrocatalyst, which                involves:
   (A) taking the metal organic compound, and                adding 10-50 times solvent 1, which is selecting                from one or more of N-methylpyrrolidone,                dimethylacetamide, dimethylformamide, triethyl                carbonate, dimethylsulfoxide or acetone, and mixing                uniformly under ultrasonic waves to obtain solution                A;
   (B) taking hydroxyl aromatic compounds, and                adding 10-50 times solvent 2, which is selecting                from mixture of alcohol, selecting from one or more                of ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, ethylene glycol or                glycerol, and n-hexanol, water or alcohol water,                ratio of alcohol to water in the mixture is 1:4,                and mixing uniformly under ultrasonic waves to                obtain solution B;
   (C) adding dropwise obtained solution B to                solution A, and treating under nitrogen protection                at 100-140 degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain                composite; and
   (D) cooling the obtained composite to room                temperature, filtering, washing the material, and                subjecting to ball milled at 200-600 revolution per                minute for 0.5-8 hours to obtain desired                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The electrocatalyst                  contents hydroxyl aromatic compound of 0.5-20                  wt.%. Preferred Components: The metal organic                  compound is selected from tetra-(4-carboxyphenyl)                  porphyrin iron, porphyrin iron, iron cyanide,                  cyanide cobalt, manganese cyanide, vitamin B12,                  one or two or more of cobalt porphyrins. The                  aromatic compound containing hydroxyl selected                  from is phenol, trichlorophenol, resorcinol,                  quinol, catechol, phloroglucinol, one or two or                  more of cresols.

GA 201833134X
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing the concentration of gaseous acid anhydrides, preferably carbon dioxide concentration, in off-gases, comprises contacting off-gases containing the acid anhydrides with an absorbent as a solution of alkali metal hydroxide and/or carbonates, where gaseous acid anhydrides are bound to form salt including sodium bicarbonate, and optionally a two-phase post-reaction mixture is formed, from which after the removal of the precipitated sodium bicarbonate, post-reaction liquors are formed.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing the concentration of gaseous acid anhydrides, preferably carbon dioxide concentration, in off-gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing the concentration of gaseous acid anhydrides, preferably carbon dioxide concentration, in off-gases, comprises contacting off-gases containing the acid anhydrides with an absorbent as a solution of alkali metal hydroxide and/or carbonates, where gaseous acid anhydrides are bound to form salt including sodium bicarbonate, and optionally a two-phase post-reaction mixture is formed, from which after the removal of the precipitated sodium bicarbonate, post-reaction liquors are formed. The absorbent solution essentially comprises a mixture of alkali metal hydroxides and additionally non-absorbing salts including halides (preferably chlorides), sulfates, nitrates of alkali and/or ammonium metals, and the total salt concentration in the absorbent is at least 5 wt.% and the concentration of alkali metal hydroxides in the absorbent is at least 0.1 M, and sodium is present in the absorbent in a molar amount, and the regeneration of alkalinity in post-reaction liquids is preferably carried out using liquid/liquid extraction.

PN CN111013188-A; WO2021128978-A1; CN111013188-B
TI Entrained pharmaceutical method based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by mixing medicine materials with centrifugal slow-release ball, heating carbon dioxide, fully mixing with remaining entrainer, extracting, and condensing.
AU HUANG X
AE JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202034085G
AB    NOVELTY - Entrained pharmaceutical method based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction involves grinding and crushing medicinal materials to be extracted, injecting portion of entrainer into centrifugal slow-release ball, and mixing with medicine materials; heating carbon dioxide to enter supercritical state, and fully mixing with remaining entrainer; controlling flow of supercritical carbon dioxide, extracting, applying externally uniform rotating magnetic field, stirring centrifugal slow-release ball and medicine materials with supercritical carbon dioxide, and continuously extracting; entering effective ingredients in medicine materials into separator, heating, recovering supercritical carbon dioxide to normal, and resolving and separating from active ingredient; and taking out carbon dioxide, condensing, recycling, recovering carbon dioxide, and collecting high-purity active ingredients without impurities.
   USE - The method is used for entrained pharmaceutical based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method strengthens extraction performance, improves extraction efficiency, avoids high energy consumption of gas compression, extracts biologically active ingredients in medicine materials, and increases enhances solubility of active ingredients in medicinal materials and sensitivity to temperature and pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Entrained pharmaceutical method based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction involves:
   (A) grinding and crushing medicinal materials to be extracted, injecting portion of entrainer into centrifugal slow-release ball, adding medicine materials into extraction kettle, and mixing with centrifugal slow-release ball;
   (B) using booster pump to heat carbon dioxide in air storage tank through heater to enter supercritical state, pumping into mixer, fully mixing with remaining entrainer, and opening valve to enter extraction kettle;
   (C) controlling flow of supercritical carbon dioxide to 5-10 L/hour, extracting at 35-50 degrees C at pressure of 10-25 MPa, applying externally uniform rotating magnetic field to extraction kettle, stirring centrifugal slow-release ball and medicine materials with supercritical carbon dioxide, and continuously extracting for 5-8 hours;
   (D) entering effective ingredients in medicine materials into separator, heating, recovering supercritical carbon dioxide to normal, and resolving and separating from active ingredient; and
   (E) taking out carbon dioxide from separator, condensing by condenser, returning to air storage tank for recycling, recovering carbon dioxide, and collecting high-purity active ingredients without impurities from bottom of extraction kettle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Separating is done at 10-20 degrees C at pressure of 8-10 Mpa.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Preparing tungsten oxide/antimony sulfide heterojunction electrode useful for photoreduction of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. growing antimony sulfide on tungsten oxide nanosheet, and carrying out one-time annealing reaction.
AU LI G

   LI W

AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 2019651791
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tungsten oxide/antimony sulfide heterojunction electrode comprises growing antimony sulfide on tungsten oxide nanosheet by mixed deposition of antimony chloride and sodium thiosulfate, and carrying out one-time annealing reaction.
   USE - The electrode is useful for photoreduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, mild and efficient; and produces electrode having excellent visible light absorbing performance and good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) stirring 1-2 g antimony chloride and 4-6 ml acetone, and preparing as solution A, adding 7-8 g sodium thiosulfate and 40-60 ml ice water, mixing and stirring, and preparing as solution B, mixing the solution A and solution B, adding 135-155 ml ice water, and preparing into strontium sulfide precursor liquid, and (ii) placing conductive glass containing tungsten oxide nanosheets in the antimony sulfide precursor solution, immersing at 4 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain antimony sulfide film on the conductive glass substrate, and annealing under the protection of argon at 300-400 degrees C for 20-40 minutes.

AU GONG H
   LI C
AE HANGZHOU YUANSHENGTAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019132651
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating domestic edible Spirulina comprises (i) taking the Spirulina, inoculating into the culture solution, stirring, and distributing algae species in the culture solution; (ii) cultivating under light intensity of 3-6 million lux at 25-35 degrees C, supplementing carbon dioxide, and cultivating to stabilize the regular harvest of Spirulina cells.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating domestic edible Spirulina (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves nutrient utilization rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the culture solution has pH of 9-11, and the seed density optical density value is 0.2-0.3, the culture solution comprises 16-17 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 0.3-0.7 g/l dipotassium phosphate, 2-3 g/l sodium nitrate, 0.5-1.5 g/l sodium chloride, 0.1-0.3 g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.005-0.015 g/l iron sulfate, 0.5-1.5 g/l potassium sulfate, 0.02-0.06 g/l calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.06-0.1 g/l EDTA and replace Zarrouk culture base every 0.5-1.5 months. In the step (i), trace elements include boron, manganese, zinc, molybdenum, and copper are also added into the culture solution. The trace element includes 2.76-2.96 mg/l boric acid, 1.7-1.9 mg/l manganese chloride tetrahydrate, 0.12-0.32 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.005-0.015 mg/l molybdenum trioxide, and 0.07-0.09 mg/l copper sulfate pentahydrate. The trace element includes vanadium, nickel, tungsten, titanium, and copper are also added into the culture solution. The trace element includes 20.9-24.9 mg/l ammonium metavanadate, 45.8-49.8 mg/l nickel sulfide heptahydrate, 15.9-19.9 mg/l sodium tungstate, 35-45 mg/l titanium sulfate, and 3.4-5.4 mg/l cobalt nitrate hexahydrate. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the illumination time is 18-24 hours, and the light quality is white light or pink light, the carbon dioxide is replenished for every 1-2 hours, and the flow rate is 20-40 cm/second, the algal cells are harvested when the optical density value is more than 0.35-0.55, and the optical density value after the algae removal is controlled to 0.2-0.25.

PN CN107698777-A; CN107698777-B
TI New copper-coordinated porous polymer used as photocatalyst for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
AU FAN W
   KOU J
   LIU F
   XU Q
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV YUNNAN NORMAL (UNYN-C)
   UNIV YUNNAN (UNYN-C)
GA 201816345T
AB    NOVELTY - A copper-coordinated porous polymer is new.
   USE - Copper-coordinated porous polymer used as photocatalyst for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer has three-dimensional frame structure porosity of 51.9%, decomposing temperature of 305 degrees C, easy preparation process, and high product purity and yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A copper-coordinated porous polymer of formula (Cu3(Tra)2O2).7H2O)n is new.
   Tra=organic ligand 1,2,4-triazole negative ion proton; and
   n=not defined.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of copper-coordinated porous polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The copper-coordinated porous polymer is prepared by mixing 1,2,4- triazole-3-carboxylic acid, 5-amino-1,2,4-triazole-3- carboxylic acid, and copper(II) chloride in distilled water and stirring; hydrothermal reacting at 150-180 degrees C for 24~-8 hours and slowly cooling to room temperature; washing with ethanol and naturally drying; and vacuum drying at 110 degrees C. Preferred Components: The ratio of 1,2,4- triazole-3-carboxylic acid, 5-amino-1,2,4-triazole-3- carboxylic acid, and copper(II) chloride is 1:1:1-1:1:2.

TI Carbon dioxide absorbent composition for carbon            dioxide capture, comprises N-alkylaminoalkanol; one or            more polyhydroxyamine-based compound selected from            polyhydroxyamine-based compounds; and one or more            selected from ethylenediamine and            diethylenetriamine.
AU CHOI S
   AHN H
   KIM Y

   KYUNG D

   NAH I W

   AHN H G
   CHOI S I
AE KOREA LAND & HOUSING CORP (KOLA-Non-standard)
   KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
   KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
   KOREA LAND & HOUSING CORP (KOLA-Non-standard)
   KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
   KOREA LAND & HOUSING CORP (KOLA-Non-standard)
GA 202295112W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent composition comprises                N-alkylaminoalkanol (I); one or more                polyhydroxyamine-based compound selected from                polyhydroxyamine-based compounds (II); and one or                more selected from ethylenediamine and                diethylenetriamine.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbent composition for                carbon dioxide capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent composition has                superior carbon dioxide absorption capacity,                absorption rate and recyclability, has remarkably                lower absorbent recycling temperature as compared                to the existing absorbents such as                monoethanolamine, reduces total energy consumption                in the capturing process. In addition, since carbon                dioxide is recovered at low recycling temperature,                contamination by water or absorbent vapor may be                prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide absorbent composition comprises                N-alkylaminoalkanol of formula (I); one or more                polyhydroxyamine-based compound selected from                polyhydroxyamine-based compounds of formula (II);                and one or more selected from ethylenediamine and                diethylenetriamine.
   n and m=1-10;
   R=hydrogen (H), methyl (-CH3), ethyl                (-CH2CH3), propyl (-CH2CH2CH3), hydroxymethyl                (HO-CH2-), hydroxyethyl (HO-CH2CH2-), hydroxypropyl                (HO-CH2CH2CH2-), 4-10C alkyl, 4-10C cycloalkyl,                4-10C hydroxyalkyl or 4-10C hydroxycycloalkyl;                and
   X=Cl, Br, I and OH, the repeating unit n is                from 1-10.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a                method for preparing the carbon dioxide absorbent                composition, which involves dissolving and mixing                the N-alkylaminoalkanol, one or more                polyhydroxyamine-based compound selected from the                group consisting of the polyhydroxyamine-based                compounds, and the one or more selected from the                group consisting of ethylenediamine and                diethylenetriamine in a solvent; and
   (2) a method for carbon dioxide capture, which                involves capturing carbon dioxide using the carbon                dioxide absorbent composition.
                                                                                                                                                                                 
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent                  composition comprises one or more selected from a                  group consisting of water, ethylene glycol,                  glycerol and n-butyl alcohol.Preferred Process: The method further                  involves producing the polyhydroxyamine-based                  absorbent from an amine precursor; an aldehyde                  precursor; and an acid or alkali through                  condensation. The method for carbon dioxide                  capture involves absorbing carbon dioxide from a                  carbon dioxide-comprising gas mixture using the                  carbon dioxide absorbent composition; and                  deaerating the absorbed carbon dioxide from the                  carbon dioxide absorbent. The carbamate is not                  formed after the absorption of carbon dioxide.                  The carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is one                  or more selected from a group consisting of                  exhaust gas, natural gas and sewage disposal                  plant biogas.Preferred Condition: The absorption                  temperature is 10-60&#176; C.
   EXAMPLE - The example illustrates the preparation of                carbon dioxide absorbent. The 5 g of the prepared                polyhydroxyamine-based compound and 2 g of                ethylenediamine (purchased from Sigma-Aldrich) or                diethylenetriamine (purchased from Daejung                Chemicals) were mixed with 63 g of water and                completely dissolved while maintaining temperature                at 80&#176; C for 1 hour. Then, an absorbent                was prepared by mixing with 30 g of an                N-alkylaminoalkanol (purchased from                Sigma-Aldrich).

TI Method for distributing energy from energy            production facility to site of customer in need of            heating and cooling, involves compressing, condensing            and cooling vaporized carbon dioxide to predetermined            conditions to provide heating at site.
AU GEINOZ F I
   CUENI M
   YAKOB K
   AIGNAS G F
   MARCEL Q
   CAMERAN J
   JACOB K
AE MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
   MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
   MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
   MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
GA 202142418F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating carbon dioxide                in liquid form at a temperature between about                4degrees C and 15degrees C at the energy production                facility thorugh pipes (103) under a pressure                sufficient to maintain the carbon dioxide in a                liquid state at that temperature. The hot water is                generated at a temperature of 30degrees C at the                energy production facility. The liquid carbon                dioxide and the hot water is piped to the site. The                liquid carbon dioxide is evaporated to carbon                dioxide gas to provide cooling at the site. The                evaporated carbon dioxide is allowed to expand. The                expanded carbon dioxide is warmed with the hot                water. The vaporized carbon dioxide is compressed,                condensed and cooled to the predetermined                conditions using the rejected heat to provide                heating at the site.
   USE - Method for distributing energy from energy                production facility to site of customer such as                large apartment, retail center, office or                industrial park in need of heating and                cooling.
   ADVANTAGE - The end user allows the liquid carbon dioxide                to evaporate to obtain cooling, thus removing from                a local heat exchanger the heat of vaporization of                the carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for distributing thermal energy from energy                production facility to site of customer in need of                heating and cooling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                distribution system.
   Pipe (103)
   Ground-coupled heat exchangers                (104,110)
   Carbon dioxide injector (106)
   Outlet (112)
   Carbon dioxide recovery unit (115)

AE INNER MONGOLIA SYNTHETIC CHEM IND RES (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 201914792G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing high energy propellant prepared into granular. Diameter of the granular is determined as lesser than 5 mm. The granular is placed in an extract kettle. A temperature pressure control device, the extraction kettle and a carbon dioxide recycling device are connected with each other by a pipeline. Ultra-critical carbon dioxide is placed into the extraction kettle for extraction process. The ultra-critical carbon dioxide in the extraction kettle is collected. The ultra-critical carbon dioxide is passed into a separator through a pressure reducing valve for reducing pressure. Gaseous carbon dioxide is dried. The dried gaseous carbon dioxide is inputted into an extraction device, where the high energy propellant is composite solid propellant containing nitrate plasticizer, and the plasticizer is nitrate, nitroglycerin, 1, 2, 4-butantriol nitric acid ester, triethylene glycol nitrate and diglycol nitrate.
   USE - Method for recycling high energy carbon dioxide propellant solid and explosive dangerous fire waste in plasticizer and stabilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing carbon dioxide as extraction solvent so as to avoid generation of secondary pollution and avoiding complicated post-processing in components recovering process, realizing recycle of high energy carbon dioxide propellant solid and explosive dangerous fire waste in plasticizer and stabilizer in a simple and easy manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a method for recycling high energy carbon dioxide propellant solid and explosive dangerous fire waste in plasticizer and stabilizer. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a liquid nitrogen tank car (4), a liquid nitrogen pump Lorry (3), a nitrogen pressurizing device (2) sequentially connected by a pipeline. The nitrogen pressurizing device is connected with a carbon dioxide storage tank (1) through a carbon dioxide storage tank vapor pipeline (5). The carbon dioxide storage tank is connected with a circulating cooling device (7) through a carbon dioxide storage tank liquid phase pipeline (6). The circulating cooling device is connected with a sand mixing device (14) through a sealed sand device liquid inlet pipeline (13). The sand mixing device is connected with a fracturing Lorry (9) by fracturing an upper water pipeline (8).
   USE - Liquid carbon dioxide dry sand-fracturing system.

   USE - Catalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst effectively reduces the carbon dioxide even at low temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon dioxide reduction method, which involves reducing carbon dioxide using the catalyst, and performing catalyst reduction reaction for reducing the catalyst; and
   (2) carbon dioxide reducing device, which consists of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal oxide (A) comprises iron. The metallic compound (B1) comprises at least one metal element chosen from beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium, and zinc. The compound (B1) is a metal oxide. The catalyst comprises both the metallic compound (B1) and the platinum compound (B2), and further comprises a carrier (C) which carries the transition metal oxide (A) and the metallic compound (B). Preferred Process: The catalyst is oxidized by reduction of carbon dioxide and reduction by hydrogen. Preferred Condition: The method is carried out at 700 degrees C or less. The reaction temperature in the method is more than the equilibrium reaction start temperature of the metallic compound (B1). Preferred Apparatus: The device consists of reactor (R1), a carbon dioxide supply device, a hydrogen supply device, and a changeover system. The reactor (R1) is connected by turns with a carbon dioxide supply device and hydrogen supply device with a changeover system. The device further consists of reactor (R2) and the reactors (R1) and (R2) are connected by turns with a carbon dioxide supply device and hydrogen supply device with a changeover system.

   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing thiourea using petrochemical waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, saves energy, has high product yield and purity, satisfy market's quality requirements for thiourea, and ensures quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide,ammonia and catalyst is 1: 1.1-1.5: 2-2.2: 0.1-0.2. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) passing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide into reactor and adding catalyst, (ii) heating up to first reaction temperature to perform first stage reaction, and (iii) cooling to 25 degrees C for solid-liquid separation, adding liquid to reactor, passing ammonia gas to second reaction temperature to perform second-stage reaction, reacting and cooling. The first reaction temperature is 400-600 degrees C. The second reaction temperature is 170-200 degrees C. The time of the first section reaction is 0.5-2 hours and second section of reaction time is 5-15 hours.

AU LAI Y
AE MINGYOU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MING-Non-standard)
GA 201910186U
AB    NOVELTY - Production of brown algae extract seasoning comprises performing first extraction procedure on brown algae by carbon dioxide supercritical extractor to obtain first extract, performing second extraction procedure to obtain second extract, performing third extraction procedure to obtain third extract, combining first, second and third extracts, freezing and vacuum spraying.
   USE - Method for producing brown algae extract seasoning used for nutritional supplement (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first extraction procedure is carried out at 45-70 degrees C for 2-6.5 hours with extraction pressure of 20-35 MPa, separation pressure of 0-7 MPa and carbon dioxide flowrate of 15-45 L/hour. The second extraction procedure is carried out at 30-60 degrees C for 2-3.5 hours with extraction pressure of 20-35 MPa, separation pressure of 1-6 MPa and carbon dioxide flowrate of 10-60 L/hour. The third extraction procedure is carried out at 30-60 degrees C for 1-3 hours with extraction pressure of 30-50 MPa, separation pressure of 3-9 MPa and carbon dioxide flowrate of 20-60 L/hour. The method further comprises pre-treating by heating brown algae at 45-55 degrees C before performing first extraction process, performing enzymatic hydrolysis, stirring for 4-5 hours, centrifuging, collecting upper layer of floating matter and drying to make water content less than 10%. The enzymatic fermentation is carried out by adding kelp gum. Preferred Components: The brown algae is kelp, wakame (kelp bud), risotto, Porphyra, alginate, sea sedge or hornwort. PN CN108729218-A; CN108729218-B
TI Processing method for making cellulose fibers by pre-swelling or pretreating cellulose fibers, adding additive to increase solubility of antioxidant drug in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and then immersing cellulose fiber in fluid.
AU ZHU W
   WANG H
   LONG J
   XIAO H
   SHI M
AE BINZHOU LOFTEX IND CO LTD (BINZ-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   NANTONG CELLULOSE FIBERS CO LTD (CNTA-C)
   ZHEJIANG JINCAI NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201887211B
AB    NOVELTY - Processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology for making cellulose fibers involves: (1) pre-swelling or pretreating the cellulose fibers; (2) adding an additive to increase the solubility of antioxidant drug in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and dissolving a hydrophilic drug, which is not soluble in supercritical fluid in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (3) adding the antioxidant drug to a medicine tank of a high-pressure equipment, removing air, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure in the equipment to 8-30 MPa to obtain a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and immersing the cellulose fiber in the carbon dioxide fluid; and (4) introducing the antioxidant drug into the pre-swollen cellulose fiber, carrying out relative movement between the drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and the cellulose fibers, and leaving still the antioxidant drug in the amorphous zone inside the cellulose fibers after pressure relief.
   USE - The processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology is useful for making cellulose fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The processing method is capable of making the cellulose fibers with excellent anti-oxidation function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology for making cellulose fibers involves: (1) pre-swelling or pretreating the cellulose fibers; (2) adding an additive to increase the solubility of antioxidant drug in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and using supercritical carbon dioxide/reverse micelle method to dissolve the hydrophilic drug, which is not soluble in supercritical fluid in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (3) adding the antioxidant drug to a medicine tank of a high-pressure equipment, removing air, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure in the equipment to 8-30 MPa and temperature to 32-120 degrees C to obtain a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and immersing the cellulose fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; and (4) introducing the antioxidant drug into the pre-swollen cellulose fiber by dissolving in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, continuously carrying out relative movement between the drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and the cellulose fibers, and leaving still the antioxidant drug in the amorphous zone inside the cellulose fibers after pressure relief to form a drug-loaded cellulose fiber capable of sustained release.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is chosen from methanol, ethanol and/or acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The cellulose fibers include natural cellulose fibers and regenerated cellulose fibers. The natural cellulose fibers include cotton fibers and hemp fibers. The regenerated cellulose fibers comprise viscose fiber, high wet modulus viscose fiber and solvent spun regenerated cellulose fibers. Preferred Method: The pre-swelling method involves: (A) preparing 30-80 %mass N-methylmorpholine-N-oxide aqueous solution, immersing the cellulose fiber in the aqueous solution, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and aqueous solution is 1:15-1:35, and setting the temperature at 60-90 degrees C for 10-80 minutes; (2) preparing 10-20% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, immersing the cellulose fiber in the aqueous solution, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and aqueous solution is 1:25-1:40, and setting the temperature at -10 degrees C to 20 degrees C for 10-80 minutes; and (3) preparing an aqueous ionic liquid 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride solution with a water content of 2-5%, immersing the cellulose fiber in the ionic liquid, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and ionic liquid is 1:25-1:40, and setting the temperature at 60-120 degrees C for 10-80 minutes. The pretreatment involves carrying out active grafting on the cellulose fibers.
DC F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)
MC F03-C07; F03-E01
IP D06M-101/06; D06M-013/152; D06M-013/165; D06M-013/342; D06M-013/352; D06M-013/368; D06M-023/00

PN US2019062236-A1; WO2019043560-A1; CA3074035-A1; AU2018323000-A1; KR2020040864-A; CN111094220-A; US10662127-B2; EP3676237-A1; VN71556-A; JP2020532540-W; CN111094220-B; AU2018323000-B2
TI Producing chemical from ethane comprises e.g.            reacting ethane with carbon dioxide in reactor in            presence of catalyst, removing water and optionally            condensates present in reaction product stream and            compressing the product stream.
AU BEAUCHAMP D
   LU X
   RAFATI M
   LV X
   XIJIA L
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 201919633W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing chemical from ethane comprises: e.g.                providing ethane and carbon dioxide into reactor at                molar ratio so that amount of provided carbon                dioxide is in excess of stoichiometrically required                amount for complete reaction with ethane; reacting                ethane with carbon dioxide in reactor in presence                of catalyst to form reaction product stream                comprising e.g. ethylene; passing the reaction                product stream through primary heat exchanger to                withdraw heat from it; removing water and                optionally any further condensates present in                reaction product stream; compressing the reaction                product stream to pressure of at least 20 bar;                separating carbon dioxide from the reaction product                stream in separation unit to provide upgraded                stream comprising ethylene; heating at least                portion of carbon dioxide separated from reaction                product stream using heat withdrawn from the                reaction product stream to form stream of heated                carbon dioxide; and recycling stream of heated                carbon dioxide back into the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for chemical production                from ethane (claimed), where the chemical is                ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the energy requirement for                the chemical production; prevents catalyst                deactivation; consumes carbon dioxide and can                generate other valuable commodity materials,                including hydrogen gas (e.g. via a water gas shift                reaction) and/or methanol (e.g. via reverse water                gas shift followed by methanol synthesis); and                utilizes carbon dioxide as a soft oxidant to                perform oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane, thus                achieving ethylene production through ethane carbon                dioxide cracking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing chemical from ethane comprises:                providing ethane and carbon dioxide into a reactor                at a molar ratio so that the amount of provided                carbon dioxide is in excess of the                stoichiometrically required amount for complete                reaction with the ethane; reacting the ethane with                the carbon dioxide in a reactor in the presence of                a catalyst to form a reaction product stream at a                temperature of greater than or equal to 450 degrees                C comprising at least ethylene, water, and carbon                dioxide; passing the reaction product stream                through a primary heat exchanger to withdraw heat                from it; removing water and optionally any further                condensates present in the reaction product stream;                compressing the reaction product stream to a                pressure of at least 20 bar; separating carbon                dioxide from the reaction product stream in a                separation unit to provide an upgraded stream                comprising ethylene; heating at least a portion of                the carbon dioxide separated from the reaction                product stream using the heat withdrawn from the                reaction product stream to form a stream of heated                carbon dioxide; recycling the stream of heated                carbon dioxide back into the reactor; and further                processing the upgraded stream comprising ethylene                to provide at least ethylene as a produced                chemical. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                a system for chemical production from ethane                comprising a catalytic reactor configured for                reacting ethane with carbon dioxide at a                temperature of greater than or equal to 450 degrees                C to form a reaction product stream including at                least ethylene and carbon dioxide, an ethane line                configured for delivery of ethane into the                catalytic reactor, a carbon dioxide line configured                for delivery of carbon dioxide into the catalytic                reactor, a primary heat exchanger configured to                receive the reaction product stream from the                catalytic reactor and withdraw heat from it, a                gas-liquid separation unit configured for removal                of water and optionally other condensates from the                reaction product stream, a compressor configured                for compressing the reaction product stream to a                pressure of at least 20 bars, a carbon dioxide                separation unit configured for receiving the                reaction product stream after at least one stage of                compression and for separating at least a portion                of the carbon dioxide from the reaction product                stream to provide an upgraded stream comprising                ethylene, and a line configured for delivering at                least a portion of the carbon dioxide separated                from the reaction product stream in the carbon                dioxide separation unit to the reactor while being                heated with at least a portion of the heat                withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the                primary heat exchanger.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system comprises:                  a secondary heat exchanger, where the line                  configured for delivering at least a portion of                  the carbon dioxide separated from the reaction                  product stream in the carbon dioxide separation                  unit to the reactor passes through the secondary                  heat exchanger for heating against a line passing                  a heated circulating stream from the primary heat                  exchanger; a line heater configured for heating                  at least one of the ethane line and the carbon                  dioxide line; at least one line configured for                  delivering a heated stream from the primary heat                  exchanger to the line heater; at least one line                  configured for delivering a heated stream from                  the primary heat exchanger for transfer of heat                  to at least one of the reactor, a carbon dioxide                  source and an ethane source; a thermal energy                  source configured for heating the reactor; and at                  least one of components configured for receiving                  the upgraded stream comprising ethylene, a                  compressor configured for compressing the                  upgraded ethylene stream to a pressure of at                  least 10 bar, an adsorber configured for                  adsorbing any water in the upgraded ethylene                  stream, a refrigeration unit configured to cool                  the upgraded ethylene stream to a temperature of                  less than -50 degrees C, a de-methanizer unit, a                  de-ethanizer unit, and a C2 splitter unit. The                  line configured for delivering at least a portion                  of the carbon dioxide separated from the reaction                  product stream in the carbon dioxide separation                  unit to the reactor is specifically configured                  for delivering at least a portion of the carbon                  dioxide into at least one of the carbon dioxide                  line and the ethane line. The thermal energy                  source comprises at least one of a concentrated                  solar energy heater, a combustion heater and an                  external industrial heat source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reactor is a fixed bed                  reactor catalytic reactor or a fluidized bed                  catalytic reactor. The reaction product stream is                  at a temperature of 500-800 degrees C. The                  reaction product stream comprises at least 10                  (preferably 10-60) mass% carbon dioxide based on                  the total mass of the reaction product exiting                  the reactor. The primary heat exchanger is a                  transfer line exchanger (TLE). The reaction                  product stream exiting the primary heat exchanger                  is at a temperature of 200-400 degrees C. The                  step of removing water and optionally any further                  condensates present in the reaction product                  comprises passing the reaction product stream                  through a condensing unit. The reaction product                  stream is cooled in the condensing unit to                  ambient temperature. The step of heating at least                  a portion of the carbon dioxide separated from                  the reaction product stream using the heat                  withdrawn from the reaction product stream                  comprises passing the carbon dioxide through a                  secondary heat exchanger against a circulating                  stream that is heated in the primary heat                  exchanger using the heat withdrawn from the                  reaction product stream. The step of recycling                  the stream of heated carbon dioxide back into the                  reactor comprises at least one of injecting the                  stream of heated carbon dioxide directly into the                  reactor, injecting the stream of heated carbon                  dioxide into a carbon dioxide source, injecting                  the stream of heated carbon dioxide into a line                  delivering carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide                  source to the reactor, injecting the stream of                  heated carbon dioxide into an ethane source,                  injecting the stream of heated carbon dioxide                  into a line delivering ethane from an ethane                  source to the reactor. The portion of the stream                  of heated carbon dioxide is passed through a line                  heater configured for transfer of heat from the                  stream of heated carbon dioxide to one or more                  streams being passed into the reactor. The                  portion of the heat withdrawn from the reaction                  product stream in the primary heat exchanger is                  used for heating at least one of the reactor, a                  carbon dioxide source, a carbon dioxide line                  delivering carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide                  source to the reactor, an ethane source, an                  ethane line delivering ethane from an ethane                  source to the reactor. The portion of the heat                  withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the                  primary heat exchanger is used for heating at                  least one of a pressurized steam stream and a                  pressurized carbon dioxide stream for use in                  power generation in a closed loop or semi-open                  loop power production system where a working                  stream is repeatedly compressed and expanded for                  power production. The portion of the heat                  withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the                  primary heat exchanger is used for heating a                  steam stream that is injected into the reactor.                  The method further processing the upgraded                  ethylene stream comprises at least one of passing                  the upgraded ethylene stream through an adsorber                  to adsorb any water in the upgraded ethylene                  stream, passing the upgraded ethylene stream                  through a refrigeration unit to cool the upgraded                  ethylene stream to a temperature of less than -50                  degrees C, passing the upgraded ethylene stream                  through a de-methanizer unit, passing the                  upgraded ethylene stream through a de-ethanizer                  unit, passing a mixture of ethane and ethylene                  from the de-ethanizer unit into a C2 splitter                  unit. The method comprises injecting steam into                  the reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
