PT P
PN CN111072054-A
TI Preparing gypsum and ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum, by adding sodium carbonate solution to phosphogypsum solution, adding hydrochloric acid to filter cake, adding sodium sulfate to calcium chloride solution and drying precipitate.
AU SUN C
   ZHENG Z
   PENG K
   LIU R
   YANG B
   CHEN M
   ZHU Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHAO Y
   LIANG J
   LIU Q
   TONG H
AE ANHUI LIUGUO CHEM IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202038045H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-purity gypsum powder and by-product ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum, involves (a) mixing crushed phosphogypsum and deionized water, and mixing sodium carbonate and deionized water, (b) adding sodium carbonate solution to phosphogypsum solution, and collecting sodium sulfate solution and primary filter cake, (c) adding primary filter cake to deionized water, and uniformly stirring, dripping dilute hydrochloric acid to filter cake solution, and collecting the gaseous carbon dioxide, (d) adding sodium sulfate solution to obtained calcium chloride solution, reacting, and drying white precipitate, (e) cooling the saturated sodium chloride solution, introducing ammonia gas and carbon dioxide, and collecting sodium bicarbonate precipitate and mixed filtrate, (f) heating sodium bicarbonate precipitate, and decomposing, and (g) introducing ammonia gas, precipitating ammonium chloride precipitate, filtering and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity gypsum powder and by-product ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and environmentally-friendly, adopts recyclable raw materials, ensures high product purity, solves the problem of comprehensive utilization of phosphogypsum, and utilizes the resources of phosphogypsum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing high-purity gypsum powder and by-product ammonium chloride from phosphogypsum, involves (a) mixing crushed phosphogypsum and deionized water in a mass ratio of 1:5-1:6, and uniformly stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1 hour to obtain a phosphogypsum solution, taking phosphogypsum and sodium carbonate in a mass ratio of 5:3-10:7, and mixing sodium carbonate and deionized water in a mass ratio of 1:2-1:5 to obtain a saturated sodium carbonate solution, (b) slowly adding sodium carbonate solution to phosphogypsum solution, stirring while adding to obtain white precipitate and sodium sulfate solution, stopping dripping process of sodium carbonate solution when no new white precipitate is added, filtering, and collecting sodium sulfate solution and primary filter cake, (c) adding primary filter cake to deionized water in a mass ratio of 1:5-1:6, adding deionized water to primary filter cake, and uniformly stirring to obtain a filter cake solution, dripping 10 %mass dilute hydrochloric acid to filter cake solution, stirring while adding to obtain carbon dioxide gas and calcium chloride solution, stopping adding hydrochloric acid when bubbles are no longer generated, collecting the generated gaseous carbon dioxide, adding for re-use in step (e), filtering the impurity filter residue and collecting a calcium chloride solution, (d) adding sodium sulfate solution to obtain calcium chloride solution, reacting to obtain white precipitate and sodium chloride solution, filtering, collecting sodium chloride solution and white precipitate, drying the white precipitate at 50-70 degrees C for 2.5-4 hours to obtain calcium sulfate dihydrate, (e) taking phosphogypsum and sodium chloride in a mass ratio of 2:1-5:2, adding sodium chloride to the sodium chloride solution, heating and evaporating to form a saturated sodium chloride solution, cooling the saturated sodium chloride solution to 25-35 degrees C, filtering, collecting filtrate, introducing ammonia gas, where the volume ratio of filtrate and ammonia gas of 1:800-1:850 to obtain a saturated ammonium salt solution, introducing carbon dioxide, where the volume ratio of filtrate and carbon dioxide is 1:120-1:150, precipitating sodium bicarbonate to obtain sodium chloride and ammonium chloride mixed solution, filtering, and collecting sodium bicarbonate precipitate and mixed filtrate, (f) heating obtained sodium bicarbonate precipitate at 250-275 degrees C for 2-2.5 hours, decomposing to generate sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide, collecting carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate, adding sodium carbonate for reuse in step (a), and adding carbon dioxide gas for reuse in step (e), and (g) introducing ammonia gas, where the volume ratio of mixed filtrate and ammonia gas is 1:150-1:180, adding sodium chloride, where mixed filtrate and sodium chloride are present in a mass ratio of 18:1-20: 1, reducing the temperature to 5-10 degrees C, precipitating ammonium chloride precipitate, filtering, collecting filtrate and filter residues, drying the filter residues at 80-85 degrees C to obtain ammonium chloride crystals, where the filtrate is saturated sodium chloride solution, which is added for reuse in reaction system of step (e).
DC E35; E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN CN107581463-A
TI Deodorizing carbon dioxide-flash device comprises            placing aquatic product protein under environment            conditions, discharging air in reaction environment,            heating and pressurizing the aquatic product, stirring            and treating product.
AU HOU H
   LI B
   SONG S
AE UNIV CHINA OCEAN (UYOC-C)
GA 2018068321
AB    NOVELTY - Deodorizing carbon dioxide-flash device                comprises (i) placing the aquatic product protein                under environment conditions at 9-11 degrees C, and                discharging the air in the reaction environment;                (ii) heating and pressurizing the aquatic product                under the conditions of high temperature and high                pressure and stirring to release the pressure; and                (iii) treating the product of the step (ii) at high                temperature and high pressure under negative                pressure conditions and converting to saturated                steam.
   USE - The method is useful for deodorizing carbon                dioxide-flash device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances high density of carbon                dioxide-flash device, device has simple process; is                easy to control process flow, has low energy                consumption, convenient reaction and flash and                pipeline flushing, no dead corner inside structure,                sanitary and has no potential safety hazard, good                deodorizing efficacy of device, removes fishy smell                and peculiar smell material, is                environmentally-friendly, has no release of                non-toxic gas and product has no pollution,                utilized for aquatic product, smell juice, beverage                dairy materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon dioxide-flash deodorization device,                comprising a reaction kettle (1), a flash tank                (13), condenser (14), a carbon dioxide bottle (15),                water tank (10), a vacuum pump, material                circulating pump. The reaction kettle at the top is                equipped with a safety valve, an emptying valve I,                a stirrer and an electric contact pressure gauge.                The bottom is equipped with a filter screen I and a                heater I. The filter screen I is located above the                heater I. The left side of the reaction kettle is                further equipped with a control box. The kettle                body wall is provided with an air inlet I. The                middle part of the inner wall is provided with a                discharging port. The carbon dioxide cylinder is                installed on the left side of the control box. PN RU2775732-C1
TI Oxygen-fuel power plant has development of power            plants with low emissions of harmful substances into            the atmosphere. The oxygen-fuel power plant.
AU KINDRA V O
   KOMAROV I I
   ZLYVKO O V
   OSIPOV S K
AE UNIV MPEI NAT RES (UYMP-Non-standard)
GA 202295708F
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the field of electric                power industry and can be used in the development                of power plants with low emissions of harmful                substances into the atmosphere. The oxygen-fuel                power plant contains a compressor (1), a combustion                chamber (2), a fuel compressor (3), an air                separation unit (4), a gas turbine (5), a waste                heat boiler (6), which is made in the form of two                heat exchangers - gas-water double-flow a heat                exchanger (7) containing a hot gas circuit of the                heat carrier (8) and a cold water circuit of the                heat carrier (9), as well as a gas-air double-flow                heat exchanger (10) containing a hot gas coolant                circuit (11) and a cold carbon dioxide coolant                circuit (12), a cooler-separator (13), a                multi-stage compressor with intermediate cooling                (14). The steam circuit consists of a steam turbine                (15) containing high (16), medium (17) and low (18)                pressure cylinders, a condenser (19), the first                condensate pump (20) and a mixing type low pressure                heater (21), a second condensing pump (22) and a                mixing type low pressure heater (23), a third                condensing pump (24), a group consisting of three                surface type low pressure heaters (25), a deaerator                (26), a feed pump (27), a group, consisting of                three surface-type high-pressure heaters (28),                carbon dioxide turbine (29), additional condenser                (30), additional pump (31), first (32), second (33)                and third (34) electric generators. The outlet of                the hot gas circuit of the heat carrier (8) of the                gas-water double-flow heat exchanger (7) of the                waste heat boiler (6) is connected to the inlet of                the hot gas circuit of the heat carrier (11) of the                gas-air double-flow heat exchanger (10) of the                waste heat boiler (6), the outlet of which, in                turn, is connected to cooler-separator inlet (13).                The condenser (19) is connected in series with the                first condensate pump (20), the first low pressure                heater mixing type (21), second condensing pump                (22), second mixing type low pressure heater (23),                third condensing pump (24), group consisting of                three surface type low pressure heaters (25),                deaerator (26), feed pump (27), a group consisting                of three surface-type high-pressure heaters (28).                The first (21) and second (22) mixing-type                low-pressure heaters are connected to the                low-pressure outlets of the low-pressure cylinder                (18) of the steam turbine (15), a group of                surface-type low-pressure heaters (25) is connected                to the low-pressure outlets of the low-pressure                cylinder (18 ) and the medium pressure cylinder                (17) of the steam turbine (15), a group of                surface-type high pressure heaters (28) is                connected to the high pressure outlets of the high                pressure cylinder (16) and the medium pressure                cylinder (17) of the steam turbine (15). The output                of the cold carbon dioxide circuit of the heat                carrier (12) of the gas-air double-flow heat                exchanger (10) of the waste heat boiler (6) is                connected in series with the carbon dioxide turbine                (29), additional condenser (30), additional pump                (31), whose output is connected to the inlet of the                cold carbon dioxide circuit of the heat carrier (                12) gas-air two-flow heat exchanger (10) of the                waste heat boiler (6).
   USE - Electric power industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the net electrical efficiency of an                oxygen-fuel power plant.

An                end of the output pipeline is communicated with a                working medium input port of a medium temperature                evaporator (6). Second regulating valve (11-2) and                first expansion valve (12-1) are connected in                series to the output pipeline. PN CN113621822-A
TI Recycling manganese from electrolytic manganese            slag, involves mixing electrolytic manganese slag and            water, washing and filter-pressing, discharging dry            residue, mixing filtrates, reacting carbon dioxide            micro-bubbles with manganese ions, precipitating,            pressing, and separating solid and liquid.
AU HAN B
   LAI X
   LAI Z
AE SHENZHEN YUANYU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D11585
AB    NOVELTY - A method for recycling manganese from                electrolytic manganese slag, involves (A) mixing                electrolytic manganese slag and water into a                stirring mixer, (B) washing and filter-pressing the                slurry r, adding the filter cake to a stage-(II)                washing and filter press for washing and filter                pressing, washing and filter-pressing the filter                cake, and discharging the dry residue, (C) adding                the stage-(II) filtrate to a stirring mixer, and                adding the stage-(III) filtrate to the stage-(I)                washing and filter press, (D) adding the stage-(I)                filtrate to a carbonization reactor, sending the                carbon dioxide gas into a micro-nano generator,                sending the loaded micro-bubbles from into                carbonization reactor, so that the carbon dioxide                micro-bubbles fully react with manganese ions in                the stage-(I) filtrate to generate manganese                carbonate, and (E) precipitating the stage-(I)                filtrate after passing through carbonization                reactor, washing and filter-pressing supernatant,                and separating solid and liquid.
   USE - Method for recycling manganese from                electrolytic manganese slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process technique,                convenient operation and safety, excellent                processing and recycling effect, and low processing                cost. The recovery rate of soluble manganese is                more than 75%. The purity of the recovered product                manganese carbonate reaches more than 99%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A method for recycling manganese from                electrolytic manganese slag, involves (A) adding                electrolytic manganese slag and water into a                stirring mixer according to proportion to mix to                form slurry, (B) adding the slurry to a stage-(I)                washing and filter press for washing and filter                pressing, adding the filter cake from the stage-(I)                washing and filter press to a stage-(II) washing                and filter press for washing and filter pressing,                adding the filter cake from the stage-(II) washing                and filter-press to a stage-(III) washing and                filter press to wash and filter press, and                discharging the dry residue from the stage-(III)                washing and filter press, (C) adding the stage-(II)                filtrate from the stage-(II) washing and                filter-press to a stirring mixer, and adding the                stage-(III) filtrate from the stage-(III) washing                and filter press to the stage-(I) washing and                filter press, (D) adding the stage-(I) filtrate                from the stage-(I) washing and filter press to a                carbonization reactor, sending the carbon dioxide                gas into a micro-nano generator, sending the loaded                micro-bubbles from the micro-nano generator into                the carbonization reactor, so that the carbon                dioxide micro-bubbles fully react with the                manganese ions in the stage-(I) filtrate to                generate manganese carbonate, and (E) adding the                stage-(I) filtrate after passing through the                carbonization reactor to a high-efficiency                precipitator for static precipitation, adding the                supernatant in the high-efficiency precipitator to                the stage-(II) washing and filter press and                stage-(III) washing and filter press, and adding                the sediment in the high-efficiency precipitator to                a solid-liquid separating device to carry out                solid-liquid separation, where the solid separated                from the solid-liquid is manganese carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressures of the                  stage-(I) washing and filter press, the                  stage-(II) washing and filter press, and the                  stage-(III) washing and filter press are 0.5-1                  MPa, 0.8-1.5 MPa, and 1-2 MPa,                  respectively.

PT P
PN CN112944371-A
TI Harmless treatment of organic waste liquid, involves generating high temperature mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and water by combustion furnace, performing organic waste liquid combustion followed by cooling mixed gas.
AU SHI J
   ZENG G
AE CHONGQING GUANTONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2021733567
AB    NOVELTY - Organic waste liquid harmless treatment method involves pressurizing an organic waste liquid of a waste liquid storage tank to an atomizing nozzle of a combustion furnace by a booster pump or utilizing high-speed compressed air to suck the organic waste liquid into the mist chemical sprinkler to atomize all organic waste liquid. Organic waste liquid combustion is performed by thermally cracking atomized organic waste liquid by the high temperature heat generated by the fuel in the combustion furnace, and fully mixing and burning in the combustion furnace with the oxygen component in the excess air sent by the self-burning fan to control the combustion at 1100 degrees C in the temperature room, and completely burning organic components in the organic waste liquid to produce carbon dioxide and water followed by cooling high-temperature mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and water produced by the combustion furnace to below 200 degrees C by the atomized cooling water of the exhaust gas cooling.
   USE - Method for harmless treatment of organic waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing complete and harmless treatment of the organic waste after fully atomizing and discharging tail gas without residual waste liquid and waste residue so as to improve processing result safety and environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Organic waste liquid harmless treatment method involves pressurizing an organic waste liquid of a waste liquid storage tank to an atomizing nozzle of a combustion furnace by a booster pump or utilizing high-speed compressed air to suck the organic waste liquid into the mist chemical sprinkler to atomize all organic waste liquid. Organic waste liquid combustion is performed by thermally cracking atomized organic waste liquid by the high temperature heat generated by the fuel in the combustion furnace, and fully mixing and burning in the combustion furnace with the oxygen component in the excess air sent by the self-burning fan to control the combustion at 1100 degrees C in the temperature room, and completely burning organic components in the organic waste liquid to produce carbon dioxide and water followed by cooling high-temperature mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and water produced by the combustion furnace to below 200 degrees C by the atomized cooling water of the exhaust gas cooling and venting system and discharging into the atmosphere. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a harmless organic waste liquid treatment device, which includes a gas delivery pipeline (1), an air supply device (2), a waste liquid pressurized supply pipeline (3), a combustion furnace (4) and exhaust gas cooling Venting system (5), where combustion furnace includes a combustion chamber and exhaust gas discharge channel. The combustion chamber is equipped with a combustion-supporting nozzle and an atomizing nozzle, and a gas delivery pipe. The circuit and the air supply device are connected to the combustion nozzle, the waste liquid pressurizing supply pipe is connected to the inlet of the atomizing nozzle, and the exhaust gas cooling and venting system is connected to the exhaust gas discharge on channel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a harmless organic waste liquid treatment device.
   Gas delivery pipeline (1)
   Air supply device (2)
   Waste liquid pressurized supply pipeline (3)
   Combustion furnace (4)

PT P
PN CN112875726-A; CN112875726-B
TI Preparing sodium bicarbonate and calcium sulfate            dihydrate involves taking sodium sulfate and ammonium            bicarbonate, crystallizing using same ion effect            produced by sodium ions, filtering, adding calcium            salt, mixing, and filtering.
AU WANG X
   WANG Y
   FAN Y
   MENG Y
   WANG M
AE NINGBO FMR ENVIRON & TECH CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2021643641
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sodium bicarbonate and calcium                sulfate dihydrate involves: (1) taking purified                sodium sulfate solution as a raw material and                ammonium bicarbonate as a metathesis reactant,                controlling the molar ratio of sodium ion/ammonium                ion at greater than 1, using same ion effect                produced by sodium ions to force sodium bicarbonate                to crystallize, filtering to obtain sodium                bicarbonate crystals and crystallization mother                liquor (A), adding liquor (A) to mirabilite                crystal, filtering to obtain mirabilite and mother                liquor (B), and using liquor (B) to produce calcium                sulfate dihydrate; and (2) adding soluble calcium                salt to the liquor (B) or volatilization residue                obtained in step (1), mixing sulfate ion with                calcium ion to form calcium sulfate precipitate,                filtering to obtain calcium sulfate dihydrate                product and precipitated liquid, adding calcium                oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to the precipitated                liquid, heating to remove ammonia, and filtering to                obtain calcium salt solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sodium                bicarbonate and calcium sulfate dihydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical                preparation of sodium bicarbonate and calcium                sulfate dihydrate with high quality and additional                value, is convenient to operate, and recycles                utilization of industrial waste containing sodium                sulfate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sodium bicarbonate and calcium                sulfate dihydrate by using sodium sulfate involves:                (1) taking purified sodium sulfate solution as a                raw material and ammonium bicarbonate as a                metathesis reactant, controlling the molar ratio of                sodium ion/ammonium ion at greater than 1, using                the same ion effect produced by sodium ions to                force sodium bicarbonate to crystallize out,                filtering to obtain sodium bicarbonate crystals and                crystallization mother liquor (A), directly                crystallizing the liquor (A), or adding to                mirabilite crystal at -14 degrees C to 14 degrees C                for 2-12 hours, filtering to obtain mirabilite and                crystallization mother liquor (B), returning the                mirabilite to be used as raw material for                utilization, using the liquor (B) to produce                calcium sulfate dihydrate, or using a mixed gas of                carbon dioxide and ammonia as metathesis reactant,                controlling molar ratio of ammonia/sodium at                greater than 1, introducing carbon dioxide                pressure, acidifying until pH is 3.5-6.9, using the                same ion effect produced by bicarbonate-ions to                force sodium bicarbonate to crystallize, separating                to sodium bicarbonate crystals and acidified                liquid, depressurizing the acidified liquid to                force the dissolved carbon dioxide and ammonia to                volatilize, returning the vaporized carbon dioxide                and ammonia to be used as metathesis reactants, and                using the volatile residue to produce calcium                sulfate dihydrate; and (2) adding soluble calcium                salt as a precipitant to the liquor (B) or                volatilization residue obtained in step (1)                according to the molar ratio of divalent calcium                ion/sulfate ion of 1, mixing sulfate ion with                calcium ion to form calcium sulfate precipitate,                filtering to obtain calcium sulfate dihydrate                product and precipitated liquid, adding calcium                oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to the resulting                precipitated liquid, heating to remove ammonia,                controlling the pH of the solution at greater than                or equal to 10.5, returning the volatilized ammonia                to step (1), introducing the final solution                obtained by purging ammonia into carbon dioxide to                adjust the pH to 7-9, filtering to obtain calcium                salt solution, and returning the resulting calcium                salt solution to be used as a precipitant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The purified sodium                  sulfate solution is 80-480 g/l sodium sulfate                  solution obtained by adding sodium carbonate                  and/or sodium hydroxide to sodium sulfate                  solution, adjusting the pH to 8.5-9.5, purifying                  and transforming or removing impurities, and                  filtering. The soluble calcium salt is calcium                  chloride, calcium bromide, calcium nitrate,                  calcium nitrite, calcium chlorate, calcium                  bromate, calcium acetate, and/or calcium                  perchlorate. The effective utilization rates of                  sodium sulfate, ammonium bicarbonate, carbon                  dioxide and ammonia, calcium oxide and/or calcium                  hydroxide are all greater than or equal to 96.5%.                  The purity of calcium sulfate dihydrate product                  is greater than or equal to 99%. The purity of                  sodium bicarbonate product is greater than or                  equal to 99.5%.

PN CN111138658-A; CN111138658-B
TI Preparing two-block non-ionic fluorine containing short chain surfactant by non-isocyanate route preparation method comprises e.g. reacting certain amount of methoxypoly(ethylene glycol) glycidyl ether with tetrabutylammonium iodide.
AU JIN Y
   ZHOU Y
   JIN H
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202042458V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing two-block non-ionic fluorine containing short chain surfactant by non-isocyanate route preparation method comprises e.g. (i) adding certain amount of methoxypoly(ethylene glycol) glycidyl ether, tetrabutylammonium iodide, and high fluorine tert-butanol into a 80 ml high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide until the pressure reached 100 bar, heating at 80 degrees C for 16 hours to obtain crude product, (v) vacuum-treating at 60 degrees C for 16 hours to remove residual carbon dioxide and high-fluoro-tert-butanol to obtain prepolymer, (vi) adding certain amount of the prepolymer and short-chain fluoro containing amine to a three-necked flask equipped with a stirrer and thermometer, heating at 50-70 degrees C while stirring, and reacting for 6-8 hours, and (vii) cooling the obtained product to below 40 degrees C, adding certain amount of distilled water and stirring for 0.5 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing two-block non-ionic fluorine containing short chain surfactant by non-isocyanate route preparation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves the non-isocyanate route synthesis of two-block non-ionic fluorine-containing short-chain surfactant containing polyurethane structure; and avoids the use of toxic isocyanate monomers; has simple process, high product yield, produces les by-products. The surfactant: has excellent micelle stability, excellent biocompatibility and biodegradability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing two-block non-ionic fluorine containing short chain surfactant by non-isocyanate route preparation method comprising (i) placing activated 3A molecular sieve in short-chain fluoro- containing amine, sealing for overnight, and removing water, (ii) vacuum distilling off methoxypoly(ethylene glycol) glycidyl ether under reduced pressure at 100-120 degrees C to remove water, where the vacuum pressure is 0.009 MPa, (iii) drying three-necked flask, stirrer and feeding tube at 100-120 degrees C for 2-4 hours, taking out, placing in a dryer, and cooling, (iv) adding certain amount of methoxypoly(ethylene glycol) glycidyl ether, tetrabutylammonium iodide, and high fluorine tert-butanol into a 80 ml high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide until the pressure reached 100 bar, heating at 80 degrees C for 16 hours to obtain crude product, (v) vacuum-treating at 60 degrees C for 16 hours to remove residual carbon dioxide and high-fluoro-tert-butanol to obtain prepolymer, (vi) adding certain amount of the prepolymer and short-chain fluoro containing amine to a three-necked flask equipped with a stirrer and thermometer, heating at 50-70 degrees C while stirring, and reacting for 6-8 hours, and (vii) cooling the obtained product to below 40 degrees C, adding certain amount of distilled water and stirring for 0.5 hours to obtain final product. The raw material of surfactant comprises 1-1.5 wt.% short-chain fluoro-containing amine, 21-45 wt.% methoxypoly(ethylene glycol) glycidyl ether, 0.74-1.11 wt.% tetrabutylammonium iodide, 0.47-0.71 wt.% high fluorine tert-butanol, 10-18 wt.% carbon dioxide and 100-500 wt.% distilled water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The short-chain fluoro-containing amine is 1H,1H-undecafluorohexylamine, 1H,1H-perfluoropentylamine and/or 2,2,3,3,4,4,4-heptafluorobutylamine. The number average molecular weight of methoxypoly(ethylene glycol) glycidyl ether is 350, 550 or 750.

PN CN110452794-A
TI Flavoring and brewing dry white wine comprises            adding grapes into fermentation tank, inoculating            yeast, crushing, adding pectinase, blending using e.g.            bentonite, three-stage activation, thermal and cold            stabilizing treatment.
AU WEI H
   DUAN W
   LIU C
   SHAO L
   JIANG W
   MENG J
   ZHANG Q
   SUN B
   YAN X
   JIANG H
   ZHANG L
   SONG J
   WANG Q
AE QINGDAO HUADONG WINERY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201997999V
AB    NOVELTY - Flavoring and brewing dry white wine comprises                (i) de-stemming the harvested grapes without                crushing, (ii) adding the grape into a fermentation                tank, pumping water, inoculating yeast, adding the                processed grapes, controlling the temperature,                determining the alcohol content, (iii) crushing,                removing grape skin and grape seeds, separating the                flowing juice, storing and processing, (iv) adding                the grape juice into a clarification tank, adding                pectinase, blending the white grape juice using                bentonite, polyvinylpolypyrrolidone (PVPP) and                casein, separating the clarified grape juice from                the suspended matter and adding into the fermenting                tank pre-filled with carbon dioxide and (v) heating                the clarified grape juice, adding white yeast after                three-stage activation, controlling the                fermentation temperature, maintaining sugar                content, performing cross-flow filtration and                subjecting the dry white wine to thermal                stabilization process and cold stabilizing                treatment.
   USE - The method is useful in flavoring and brewing                dry white wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine: has strong aroma, complex flavor;                ensures strong typicality; and has improved health                care function. The method: enhances the release of                aroma and extraction of resveratrol from the                peel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flavoring and brewing dry white wine comprises                (i) de-stemming the harvested grapes without                crushing, (ii) adding the grape after processing                into a fermentation tank, pumping water (3 vol.%)                at the bottom of fermentation tank, inoculating 30                ppm yeast or adding same variety grape juice (10                vol.%) to generate carbon dioxide gas, filling the                fermentation tank with carbon dioxide to maintain                the dipping process in an anaerobic environment,                adding the processed grapes and closing the tank,                controlling the temperature during dipping process                at 16-24 degrees C or 24-35 degrees C for 15 days                or 10 days, filling the tank with carbon dioxide                gas every day to maintain the pressure at 105 KPa,                determining the alcohol content in the tank, when                the alcohol content is 2-5 vol.% discharge the                tank, (iii) crushing the grapes at 12 degrees C,                removing grape skin and grape seeds, separating the                flowing juice from the crushed juice, storing and                processing separately, (iv) adding the grape juice                into a clarification tank, adding pectinase,                controlling the temperature at 15-18 degrees C,                taking the clarified sample from the top of the                tank, blending the white grape juice using                bentonite, polyvinylpolypyrrolidone (PVPP) and                casein for 4 hours to obtain clarified grape juice,                separating the clarified grape juice from the                suspended matter when the turbidity of the grape                juice reaches 100-250 NTU and adding the clarified                grape juice into the fermenting tank pre-filled                with carbon dioxide and (v) heating the clarified                grape juice at 14 degrees C, adding 250 ppm white                yeast after three-stage activation, controlling the                fermentation temperature at 12-15 degrees C,                maintaining the sugar content to decrease by 2                Brix/24 hour, stopping when the sugar content is                less than or equal to 4Brix to obtain dry white                wine, performing cross-flow filtration and                subjecting the dry white wine to thermal                stabilization process and cold stabilizing                treatment. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included                for dry white wine prepared by using the above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises                  adjusting the pressure-reducing valve of the                  fermentation tank, maintaining the inflation                  pressure at 0.1-0.2 MPa, filling carbon dioxide                  gas (3 times) from the bottom of the fermentation                  tank, then filling from the top of the                  fermentation tank, adding the processed grapes                  and closing the tank. The process of three-stage                  activation in step (v) comprises (a) preparing                  warm water by heating at 40 degrees C, adding                  active dry yeast uniformly in the warm water in a                  ratio of 10:1, melting the active dry yeast                  particles completely in warm water, allowing it                  to stand and avoid stirring the yeast solution,                  observing for the production of bubbles where the                  foam fills the entire liquid surface, thus                  completing the primary stage activation to obtain                  a primary yeast activated solution, (ii) adding                  clarified grape juice to the primary yeast                  activated solution, allowing to stand for                  carrying out secondary activation, where the                  yeast solution releases dense carbon dioxide                  bubbles which completely covers the surface to                  obtain a secondary activation liquid and (iii)                  adding same volume of clarified grape juice into                  secondary activation liquid, allowing to stand                  for third-stage activation, measuring the                  temperature of yeast activation solution and                  completing the third-stage activation when the                  temperature difference between yeast activation                  solution and the grape juice in the tank is less                  than or equal to 10 degrees C. The thermal                  stabilization process comprises adding white wine                  completely into a storage tank after                  fermentation, adding bentonite for carrying out                  thermal stabilization process and adding 60 ppm                  sulfur dioxide and storing. The cold stabilizing                  treatment comprises drying the white wine at -6                  degrees C for 2 weeks-1 month, filtering the                  sample while cold and cooling at -4 degrees C for                  3 days to determine the cold stabilization end                  time, observing for tartar precipitation after 3                  days and stopping the process. The step (ii)                  comprises low temperature freezing by adding                  grapes after removing the stem into the tank,                  adding 40-60 ppm sulfur dioxide and ice, reducing                  the temperature of the grapes to -4 degrees C and                  maintaining for 24 hours to obtain the grape                  mash, returning the temperature of the grape mash                  to greater than or equal to 0 degrees C, where                  the amount of grape mash is 70 vol.% and                  increasing the temperature of the grape mash in                  the tank to greater than or equal to 16 degrees                  C. The step (i) comprises harvesting the grapes                  and pre-cooling at 4 degrees C for 12 hours by                  placing in a cold storage room before                  de-stemming. The step (ii) comprises adding wholw                  and unfrozen grapes (10 vol.%) at the top of the                  tank. The step (iv) comprises adding 10-20 ppm                  impregnated enzyme to the grape juice,                  controlling the temperature at 8 degrees C,                  performing cold soaking for 2 days under the                  protection of the inert gas.

PN CN110255596-A
TI Continuous carbonation of calcium carbonate            comprises e.g. mixing calcium carbonate crystal nucleus            with slaked lime slurry to form mixture and, pumping            slurry continuously into top of reactor and introducing            carbon dioxide.
AU ZHAO J
AE CHANGSHU MEITE CALCIUM IND CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201991963G
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous carbonation of calcium carbonate                comprises (i) mixing calcium carbonate crystal                nucleus having a particle diameter of 1.5-2 mu m                with the slaked lime slurry to form a mixture and                the calcium carbonate crystal nucleus accounts for                5-10% in the slurry, pumping the slurry                continuously into the top of the reaction tank                while carbon dioxide is introduced from the bottom                of the reaction tank, (ii) pumping the reacted                calcium carbonate slurry from the bottom into the                refining tank by using a pump, and setting a                conductivity meter in the lower part of the                reaction tank to monitor the conductivity of the                pumping material, and there is a quantitative                relationship between the concentration of slaked                lime in the slurry and the conductivity of the                slurry, where the difference between the                conductivity of the slurry pumped into the reaction                tank.
   USE - The method is useful for continuous                carbonation of calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high degree of automation,                high yield and good quality; and is easy to                control, continuous and stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuous carbonation of calcium carbonate                comprises (i) mixing calcium carbonate crystal                nucleus having a particle diameter of 1.5-2 mu m                with the slaked lime slurry to form a mixture and                the calcium carbonate crystal nucleus accounts for                5-10% in the slurry, pumping the slurry                continuously into the top of the reaction tank                while carbon dioxide is introduced from the bottom                of the reaction tank, (ii) pumping the reacted                calcium carbonate slurry from the bottom into the                refining tank by using a pump, and setting a                conductivity meter in the lower part of the                reaction tank to monitor the conductivity of the                pumping material, and there is a quantitative                relationship between the concentration of slaked                lime in the slurry and the conductivity of the                slurry, where the difference between the                conductivity of the slurry pumped into the reaction                tank, the conductivity of the finished calcium                carbonate slurry, and the conductivity of the                slurry pumped into the refining tank serves as a                control mechanism and transmitting the conductivity                signal to the PLC control system, and the PLC                control system is used to separately control the                flow rate of the slurry entering the reaction tank,                the flow rate of the slurry pumped into the                refining tank, and the flow rate of the slurry                pumped out of the refining tank, and keeping the                liquid level in the reaction tank and the refining                tank always within the preset range, (iii) pumping                the calcium carbonate slurry at the bottom of the                reaction tank into the top of the refining tank by                using a pump, and simultaneously introducing carbon                dioxide from the bottom of the refining tank,                monitoring the pH in the reaction tank in real                time, and adjusting the amount of carbon dioxide to                ensure the pH in the reaction tank is 7-8.5, and                (iv) pumping out the finished calcium carbonate                from the refining tank and recycle the above                process.

PN EP3524597-A1; EP3524597-B1
TI Revamping melamine purification section of high pressure melamine plant by installing line feeding stream of water added with ammonia to quencher, dismissing stripper and heat exchanger, and redirecting stripping medium to quencher.
AU DI CARLO G
   SCOTTO A
   GAMBA S
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 201969648G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for revamping a melamine purification section of a high pressure melamine plant, comprises: installing a line feeding a stream of water, possibly added with ammonia, to the quencher, where at least a portion of the stream of water is heated inside the second heat exchanger; dismissing the stripper; dismissing the first heat exchanger; redirecting the stripping medium to the quencher; installing a line for exporting the second melamine-containing stream from the quencher; and dismissing the absorber. The high pressure melamine plant comprises: a quencher fed with a first melamine-containing stream from the synthesis section and an aqueous solution comprising ammonia and carbon dioxide; a stripper fed with a second melamine-containing stream from the quencher and with a stripping medium; a first heat exchanger; and an absorber.
   USE - The method is useful for revamping a melamine purification section of a high pressure melamine plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the undesired sequence of desorption and absorption taking place, respectively, in the stripper and the absorber because the ammonia content in the absorber is minimized, and the carbon dioxide content in the effluent from the stripper is minimized; does not saturate the liquid stream leaving the quencher with ammonia and carbon dioxide, which provides for less off-gas extracted from the subsequent stripping and a lower content of ammonia and carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution collected from the absorber; and exports a portion of the aqueous solution from the absorber from the purification section for further use in the process, so that the water input for the quencher is provided by a stream of water recycled from the plant, where the quencher does not provide any gaseous stream and no off-gas are recovered from it.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for revamping a melamine purification section of a high pressure melamine plant, comprises: installing a line feeding a stream of water, possibly added with ammonia, to the quencher, where at least a portion of the stream of water is heated inside the second heat exchanger; dismissing the stripper; dismissing the first heat exchanger; redirecting the stripping medium to the quencher; installing a line for exporting the second melamine-containing stream from the quencher; and dismissing the absorber. The high pressure melamine plant comprises: a quencher fed with a first melamine-containing stream from the synthesis section and an aqueous solution comprising ammonia and carbon dioxide, where melamine is dissolved and off-gas are extracted; a stripper fed with a second melamine-containing stream from the quencher and with a stripping medium, where purified melamine is obtained and vapors are extracted; a first heat exchanger, where the vapors are at least partially condensed by heat exchange with an aqueous solution, providing a condensed stream; and an absorber fed with the condensed stream from the first heat exchanger and providing an aqueous solution, which is at least partially used for condensing the vapors in the first heat exchanger, re-condensed in a second heat-exchanger and recycled to the quencher.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The line for injecting ammonia to the stream of water is installed.

PN CN109511497-A
TI Method for controlling greenhouse gas, involves selecting soil type of test field first used for field trial, preparing Clinoptilolite, determining fluxes of sulfur, carbon dioxide, methane and determining ammonium and nitrate nitrogen.
AU CHEN T
   LIANG Q
   WANG S
   SHA Y
   ZHAO Q
   LIU G
   ZHANG F
   WANG Z
   WANG T
   GUO C
   XIA G
   SUN S
   WANG L
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV SHENYANG AGRIC (USAG-C)
GA 201929606T
AB    NOVELTY - A greenhouse gas controlling method involves selecting soil type of test field first used for field trial, provincial or county type soil of test area, selecting second test field in a contiguous paddy field with a total area of 0.5-1 hectares. Clinoptilolite is prepared and applied together with the base fertilizer before turning over the ground. Greenhouse gas fluxes of sulfur, carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen oxide are determined. Static box is installed in the middle of the lysimeter, the frequency of gas sampling is once a week, and the sampling is increased one time before the fertilization week and the dry and wet alternate irrigation water surface disappears, collect 60 ml gas in a 60 mL syringe and transfer to a 100 ml vacuum aluminum bag, followed by measuring carbon dioxide using American Agilent Gas Chromatograph (7890B), methane and nitrogen oxide greenhouse gases, and determining ammonium and nitrate nitrogen.
   USE - Method for controlling greenhouse gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing the nitrification-denitrification by reducing the nitrate ion in the soil by the clinoptilolite, thus reducing the nitrogen oxide emission, and breaking the relationship between the methane and nitrogen oxide emissions in the paddy field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A greenhouse gas controlling method involves selecting soil type of test field first used for field trial, provincial or county type soil of test area, selecting second test field in a contiguous paddy field with a total area of 0.5-1 hectares, and third experimental field far away from houses, highways, ancient trees and cemeteries, fourth experimental field having an area of 500-1000 square meters, which is convenient for drainage, and good light conditions. Clinoptilolite is prepared and applied together with the base fertilizer before turning over the ground, where the amount of clinoptilolite is 5-10 tons/ha, and the size of clinoptilolite is 20 or 80 mesh, and the paddle field is turned over and mixed. Normal fertilization is performed in the second year but no zeolite. 5 points are taken in the plum blossoms every 7 days after rice transplanting, and the redox potential of the cultivated layer soil is directly measured in the field by CN61M/FJA3 automatic oxidation reduction potential depolarization tester. Greenhouse gas fluxes of sulfur, carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen oxide are determined. Static box is installed in the middle of the lysimeter, the frequency of gas sampling is once a week, and the sampling is increased one time before the fertilization week and the dry and wet alternate irrigation water surface disappears, where gas collection time is 8:00 in the morning. 00-11:00, after the box is closed, collect 60 ml gas in a 60 mL syringe at 0, 10, 20, 30, 40 min and transfer to a 100 ml vacuum aluminum bag, followed by measuring carbon dioxide using American Agilent Gas Chromatograph (7890B), methane and nitrogen oxide greenhouse gases, determining ammonium and nitrate nitrogen in surface soil and leachate using a German SEAL AA3 continuous flow analyzer (AA3 Auto Analyzer 3).

PN CN109275832-A
TI Drying seafood mushroom by carrying out low-temperature cold air drying by adopting hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying coupling carbon dioxide source heat pump, removing impurities and processing fresh seafood mushroom subjected pre-freezing.
AU LAI P
   CHEN J
   TANG B
AE FUJIAN ACAD AGRIC SCI AGRIC ENG TECHNOLO (IAMV-C)
GA 201915981R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for drying a seafood mushroom involves carrying out low-temperature cold air drying by adopting a hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying coupling carbon dioxide source heat pump, removing impurities and processing the fresh seafood mushroom subjected to pre-freezing to obtain the finished product, where the step of hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying method involves carrying out stepwise heating of the inner heating plate and stepwise changing the temperature and vacuum degree by (i) drying at -10 degrees C for 2-4 hours and a vacuum degree of 20-35 Pa in stage (A), (ii) drying at 65-70 degrees C for 18-20 hours and the vacuum degree of 30-50 Pa in stage (B), (iii) drying at 30-35 degrees C for 2-4 hours and the vacuum degree of 20-30 Pa in stage (C) and (iv) drying at 25-28 degrees C for 1-3 hours and the vacuum degree of 15-25 Pa in stage (D).
   USE - The method for drying a seafood mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the defects of serious browning and shrinkage of the dried seafood mushroom, compensates the shortcomings of long vacuum freeze-drying, retains the appearance, shape, flavor and color of fresh seafood mushroom, reduces energy consumption, production cycle and production cost, adopts hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying in the evaporation period of 35-90% with large moisture and removes most of the water in a short time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for drying a seafood mushroom involves carrying out low-temperature cold air drying by adopting a hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying coupling carbon dioxide source heat pump, removing impurities and processing the fresh seafood mushroom subjected to pre-freezing to obtain the finished product, where the step of hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying method involves carrying out stepwise heating of the inner heating plate and stepwise changing the temperature and vacuum degree by involving (i) drying at -10 degrees C for 2-4 hours and a vacuum degree of 20-35 Pa in stage (A), (ii) drying at 65-70 degrees C for 18-20 hours and the vacuum degree of 30-50 Pa in stage (B), (iii) drying at 30-35 degrees C for 2-4 hours and the vacuum degree of 20-30 Pa in stage (C) and (iv) drying at 25-28 degrees C for 1-3 hours and the vacuum degree of 15-25 Pa in stage (D), and the low-temperature cold air drying of the carbon dioxide source heat pump directly circulates the seafood mushroom which is subjected to hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying into a heat pump low-temperature cold air dryer which uses carbon dioxide as a drying medium to perform cycle drying, where the drying process is carried out at 25-28 degrees C for 7-9 hours, the circulating air volume is 2000-2800 m3/hour and the oxygen content in the drying chamber is 8-12%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The moisture content of the seafood mushroom after the hot plate-vacuum freeze-drying is 30-35%. The dried seafood mushroom has a moisture content of less than 10%.

PN CN108728288-A; WO2019214692-A1
TI Preparing organic selenium-enriched black rice beer includes washing barley, black kerneled rice, black rice, hops, soaking in water, germinating, drying, grinding, saccharifying, filtering, heating, fermenting and filling carbon dioxide.
AU LI C
AE LI C (LICC-Individual)
GA 2018888272
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic selenium-enriched black rice beer comprises using barley, black kerneled rice, black rice, hops, washing the barley and black rice, soaking in water, allowing for germination, drying, removing roots, passing to a malt barn, grinding to obtain a malt flour and black kerneled rice to obtain a black kerneled rice flour, adding separately the malt flour and black kerneled rice flour into a saccharifying pot, adding e.g. selenium-enriched mineralized water, heating, boiling respectively to obtain malt flour saccharification juice and black kerneled rice saccharification juice, mixing the malt flour saccharification juice and black kerneled rice saccharification juice, filtering to obtain wort, heating wort, filtering again, cooling the filtered hot wort to the inoculation temperature, filling sterile air, passing into a fermentation tank for fermentation, cooling, filling carbon dioxide, precipitating and filtering the liquor filled with carbon dioxide, and preparing beer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing organic selenium-enriched black rice beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The beer: is rich in nutrition as it contains black kerneled rice, black rice and barley; has very rich content of protein, vitamin, mineral and trace elements; has low alcohol content and calories; does not accumulate heat in the body like ordinary beer; does not cause fat accumulation, obesity, increased blood pressure and blood lipid, cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases; and reaches the national green organic selenium-enriched food certification standard with great healthcare functions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing organic selenium-enriched black rice beer comprises (a) using 30-40 wt.% barley, 50-55 wt.% black kerneled rice, 10-15 wt.% black rice, 1-5 wt.% hops, where the selenium content in barley is 0.05 mg/kg, in black kerneled rice is 0.18 mg/kg, and in black rice is 0.22 mg/kg, (b) washing the barley and black rice, soaking in water for 2 days, and allowing for germination, (c) drying the barley and black rice after germination, removing roots and passing to a malt barn, (d) grinding to obtain a malt flour and grinding black kerneled rice to obtain a black kerneled rice flour, (e) the saccharification process specifically comprising (e1) adding separately the malt flour and black kerneled rice flour into a saccharifying pot, adding selenium-enriched mineralized water, pH regulator, enzyme preparation and hops, adjusting the pH to 5.4-5.8, heating and boiling for 60-70 minutes respectively to obtain malt flour saccharification juice and black kerneled rice saccharification juice, where the selenium-enriched mineralized water contains 0.021 mg/kg selenium, and (e2) mixing the malt flour saccharification juice and black kerneled rice saccharification juice, (f) filtering the mixture obtained after saccharification to obtain wort, (g) heating wort to 76-78 degrees C and adjusting the wort pH value to 5.2-5.4, then filtering again, cooling the filtered hot wort to the inoculation temperature, filling sterile air and passing into a fermentation tank for fermentation, (h) cooling the fermented wine liquor and filling carbon dioxide, and (i) precipitating and filtering the liquor filled with carbon dioxide, and preparing beer, where the selenium content in the beer is 0.018-0.03 mg/kg. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for organic selenium-enriched black rice beer can be prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The enzyme preparation in the step (e1) is yeast. The inoculation temperature in the step (g) is 8-10 degrees C. Preferred Method: The method comprises removing iron impurities from barley, black rice and black kerneled rice before grinding through initial cleaning and magnetic separation by permanent magnet roller and also by self balancing vibration sieve. The enzyme preparation respectively added in the step (e1) is 0.07-0.09% of the total mass of the malt powder and black kerneled rice saccharification juice. The step (f) specifically comprises (f1) pumping the mixture obtained after saccharification into a filter tank for raw juice filtration, and (f2) spraying the filter tank twice for washing, adding the water obtained by washing tank into the filtered raw juice of step (f1) and boiling to obtain wort.

AB    NOVELTY - Energy is integrated into a power cycle during carbon dioxide production by combusting fuel and oxygen in a reactor to produce mixture of carbon dioxide and water and form heat of reaction; capturing heat of reaction; converting heat of reaction into electrical energy; feeding electrical energy into power cycle; and purifying and recovering carbon dioxide.
   USE - Integration of energy into a power cycle during production of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - By capturing the thermal energy of the heat of reaction, less energy needs to be generated for the power cycle for processing the flue gas stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of carbon dioxide and power which involves combusting fuel and oxygen in a reactor to produce flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and contaminants, and a heat of reaction; recovering heat of reaction from reactor and producing electricity; integrating the electricity into a power cycle that is used to process the flue gas; and removing contaminants from the carbon dioxide and recovering purified carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: Power cycle is electricity that is used in separating and purifying the mixture of carbon dioxide and water. Fuel is selected from hydrogen and natural gas selected from natural gas, compressed natural gas, and liquefied natural gas. Oxygen is from an air separation device. Heat of reaction is from super-stoichiometric reaction or sub-stoichiometric reaction. Preferred Processes: Heat of reaction is converted into electrical energy by feeding to a compressor and expander. Electrical energy is used to power downstream separation and purification systems. The mixture of carbon dioxide and water is fed to a water separation system. Carbon dioxide from water separation system is fed to a system for removing nitrogen oxides and sulfur oxides. Carbon dioxide is fed to a device for removing oxygen and inert compounds. Purified carbon dioxide is fed to a compressor and recovered. Preferred Devices: Reactor is a boiler. The boiler is in fluid communication with greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers to provide steam to the boiler. A heat exchanger is in fluid communication with water separation system and with greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers providing steam to boiler. A heat exchanger is in fluid communication with the device to remove oxygen and inert compounds and with the boiler.

TI High-efficiency capturing method of carbon dioxide            from flue gas, by removing moisture and particle            impurities from gas, introducing into adsorption tower            filled with carbon dioxide adsorption material for            adsorbing carbon dioxide, and introducing hot stream            into adsorption tower for desorbing.
AU MIAO J
AE JIANGSU JIUYA MACHINERY TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022576179
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency capturing method of carbon                dioxide from flue gas involves removing moisture                and particulate impurities from the flue gas by                using a flue gas treatment system, then introducing                into an adsorption tower filled with carbon dioxide                adsorption material for adsorbing carbon dioxide at                0.3-1 MPa and 25-60degrees Celsius for 0.1-5 hours                and capturing, and introducing 120-180degrees                Celsius hot stream into the adsorption tower for                desorbing for 2-5 hours and analyzing. The filling                volume of the carbon dioxide adsorption material is                60-85% of the volume of the adsorption tower.
   USE - High-efficiency capturing method of carbon                dioxide from flue gas .
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains carbon dioxide with high                purity, and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the carbon dioxide adsorption material involves:                  (1) evenly mixing 0.05-1.5 mol/L, preferably 1.1                  mol/L titanium tetrachloride solution, absolute                  ethanol and deionized water, adding ZSM-5 type                  zeolite, adjusting the pH of the solution to                  8-10, preferably 9, heating and reacting at                  80-120degrees Celsius, preferably 90-100degrees                  Celsius for 1-3 hours, preferably 2-3 hours; and                  (2) adding rare earth metal oxide and                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate to                  the obtained reaction solution, mixing,                  ultrasonically stirring for 2-3 hours, then                  filtering, washing the filter cake to neutral,                  drying in an oven at 110-130degrees Celsius, and                  grinding to a particle size of 4-10 mm.Preferred Conditions: The adsorption step is                  preferably carried out at 0.7-0.85 MPa and                  45-50degrees Celsius for 2 hours. The temperature                  of the hot steam is preferably 150-160degrees                  Celsius, and the desorption time is preferably 2                  hours. Preferred Components: The rare earth metal                  oxide is lanthanum oxide, cerium oxide, or erbium                  oxide, preferably lanthanum oxide.Preferred Composition: The filling volume of                  the carbon dioxide adsorption material is                  preferably 75-80% of the volume of the adsorption                  tower. The mass ratio of titanium tetrachloride                  solution, absolute ethanol, deionized water and                  ZSM-5 type zeolite is (2-3):(1-5):(2-3):1,                  preferably 3:5:2:1. The mass ratio of rare earth                  metal oxide, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium                  tetrafluoroborate and ZSM-5 type zeolite is                  (0.03-0.5):(0.5-1.5):1, preferably                  0.03:1.2:1.

   USE - Electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction catalyst electrode for preparing electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction apparatus, and reducing electrochemical carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction catalyst electrode can be self-activated by selectively adsorbing and stabilizing metal cation impurities present in an electrolyte solution at a single atom level, thus improves catalyst performance and durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction catalyst electrode, which involves dispersing a binder including carbon catalyst particles and nonionic conductive polymers doped with heteroelements having a non-shared electron pair in a solvent to provide a mixed solution, snd applying the mixed solution on a substrate to provide a catalyst electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: Electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction catalyst electrode comprise heteroelement having a non-shared electron pair including at least one element selected from a group 15 element, a group 16 element, and a group 17 element. The heteroelement having an unshared electron pair comprises at least one element selected from nitrogen, phosphorus, sulfur, and fluorine. The carbon catalyst particles contain at least one selected from graphite, graphene, carbon nanotubes, carbon nanofibers, activated carbon, carbon black, acetylene black, and fullerene. The nonionic conductive polymer is selected from polyphenylene vinylene, polynaphthalene vinylene, polymethyl methacrylate, polystyrene, polycaprolactone, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, polyethylene oxide, polypropylene oxide, polyalkyl oxide, polyoxyethylene oxide, polyethylene oxide-propylene oxide copolymer, cellulose, methylcellulose, ethylcellulose, methylhydroxyethylcellulose, methylhydroxypropylcellulose, hydroxyethylcellulose, carboxymethylcellulose, carboxymethylhydroxyethyl cellulose, and derivatives of those, preferably double bond chain in a backbone structure. It further comprises a metal cation impurity adsorbed at the site where heteroelement having the unshared electron pair is present. The metal cation impurity contains at least one metal cation selected from iron (Fe), nickel (Ni), zinc (Zn), and copper (Cu). Preferred Compositions: The doping amount of heterogeneous element is in the range of 1-10 atomic.% based on the total number of carbon atoms of the carbon catalyst particles and the heterogeneous element. The weight ratio of carbon catalyst particles and nonionic conductive polymer is 1:100-100:1. Preferred Conditions: The method further involves contacting the catalyst electrode with an electrolyte solution and performing a carbon dioxide reduction reaction.

GA 2020397051
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting volatiles from the Cannabis with carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) providing a source of Cannabis, where the Cannabis includes volatiles comprising terpenes, wax, or oil; and (b) after step (a), extracting the volatiles from the Cannabis with carbon dioxide to form a carbon dioxide and volatiles mixture according to a method including (b1) separating the terpenes from the Cannabis at a first temperature and a first pressure, and (b2) after step (b1), separating the oil and the wax from the Cannabis at a second temperature and a second pressure, where the second temperature is greater than the first temperature, the second pressure is greater than the first pressure, and the carbon dioxide and volatiles mixture includes carbon dioxide, oil, or wax.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting volatiles from the Cannabis with carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes minimal water and environmental impact.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises: (c) after step (b), removing the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide and volatiles mixture to produce extracted volatiles, where the extracted volatiles includes oil and wax; (d) after step (c), mixing the extracted volatiles with ethanol to produce an ethanol and volatiles mixture; (e) after step (d), cooling the ethanol and volatiles mixture to form a cooled ethanol and volatiles mixture; (f) after step (e), filtering the cooled ethanol and volatiles mixture to form a filtered ethanol and volatiles mixture; (g) after step (f), evaporating the ethanol from the filtered ethanol and volatiles mixture under vacuum conditions to produce concentrated volatiles and vaporized ethanol; (h) after step (g), condensing the vaporized ethanol to produce liquid ethanol and non-condensable gas, where the non-condensable gas has a reduced amount of ethanol vapor relative to the vaporized ethanol; and (i) after step (h), introducing the non-condensable gas to a vacuum system. In step (g) evaporating the ethanol with: a wiped-film evaporator; a falling film tubular evaporator; a rising film tubular evaporator; a forced circulation evaporator; a thermal vapor recompression evaporator; a mechanical vapor recompression evaporator; or a distillation column. In step (g) evaporating the ethanol from the filtered ethanol and volatiles mixture with: an electrically-heated evaporator; or a steam-heated evaporator. In step (i), the vacuum system includes a liquid-ring vacuum pump. In step (h), the non-condensable gas includes at least one nitrogen, air, and/or carbon dioxide. In step (a), the Cannabis includes ground Cannabis, where the ground Cannabis includes bacteria and fungi. Preferred Components: The source of Cannabis includes: ground hybrid asexually cloned Cannabis comprising a hybrid between Cannabis sativa subspecies sativa and Cannabis sativa subspecies indica; or decarboxylated of Cannabis. The ground hybrid asexually cloned Cannabis includes the terpenes. The terpenes include at least three terpenes including limonene, humulene, pinene, linalool, caryophyllene, eucalyptol, nerolidol, or phytol. The source of Cannabis is derived from Cannabis plants grown indoors, or Cannabis plants grown outdoors. The source of Cannabis includes ground Cannabis flowers, or ground Cannabis leaves, where the ground Cannabis leaves are derived from Cannabis leaves comprising palmately compound leaves with 5-11 serrated leaflets per leaf.

PN JP6817485-B1; JP2021116221-A; JP7008305-B2
TI Fixing device of carbon dioxide, has carbon dioxide fixative charging unit arranged such that electric power generated by solar power generation is provided for electrolysis in electrolytic chamber.
AU 
AE SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO IND CO LTD (KOBM-C)
   SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO SANGYO KK (SHIA-C)
GA 202108410E
AB    NOVELTY - The fixing device (1) has a reaction vessel (10), a carbon dioxide fixative charging unit (20A,20B), and a gas-liquid mixing unit. An electrolysis chamber includes an anode chamber and a cathode chamber. Liquids are sent from the reaction chamber to the electrolysis chamber and from the electrolysis chamber to the reaction chamber, respectively. The carbon dioxide fixative is reacted with carbon dioxide in the reaction chamber. The carbon dioxide fixative after the reaction is sent from the reaction chamber to the electrolysis chamber. The carbon dioxide fixative after the reaction is electrolyzed in the electrolytic chamber. The carbon dioxide fixative after electrolysis is sent from the electrolytic chamber to the reaction chamber. The carbon dioxide fixative after electrolysis is reused as the carbon dioxide fixative in the reaction chamber. The electric power generated by solar power generation is used for the electrolysis in the electrolytic chamber.
   USE - Fixing device of carbon dioxide used in thermal power plant for treatment of combustion exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The new method for fixing carbon dioxide is provided. The carbon dioxide is fixed in a more stable state, which becomes easy to handle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide fixative charging unit charges the carbon dioxide fixative into the reaction vessel. The reaction vessel includes a reaction chamber (11) and an electrolysis chamber (12). The reaction chamber contains the carbon dioxide fixative. The gas-liquid mixing unit is capable of mixing gas containing carbon dioxide with the carbon dioxide fixative contained in the reaction chamber, and is inserted into the reaction chamber. The insertion end is coated with at least one of a water repellent and an electrically negative material. The electric power generated by a photovoltaic power generation device is used for the electrolysis in the electrolytic chamber. The hydrogen generated in the electrolysis chamber is supplied to the hydrogen fuel cell. The electrolysis is possible by utilizing the electric power of the hydrogen fuel cell in the electrolytic chamber. The electric power generated by the solar power generation is supplied to the storage battery. The electrolysis is possible by utilizing the electric power of the storage battery in the electrolytic chamber. A switching unit switches the electric power used in the electrolysis chamber. The switching is switching to any one or a combination of two or three selected from the group consisting of the power generated by the photovoltaic power generation, the power of the hydrogen fuel cell, and the power of the storage battery. The gas-liquid mixing unit is inserted into the reaction chamber and provided with several holes at the insertion end. The gas is released from the holes into the carbon dioxide fixative in the reaction chamber. The gas-liquid mixing unit includes a liquid circulation flow path and a pump. The liquid circulation flow path includes a liquid suction end portion and a liquid discharge end portion. The liquid suction end is inserted into the electrolysis chamber. The liquid discharge end is inserted into the reaction chamber. The carbon dioxide fixative is sucked from the liquid suction end portion with the pump, and the sucked carbon dioxide fixative is discharged from the liquid discharge end portion. The liquid circulation flow path further includes a gas-liquid mixing element. The gas-liquid mixing element mixes gas with the liquid flowing through the liquid circulation flow path. The reaction vessel comprises a first reaction vessel and a second reaction vessel. The first reaction vessel comprises the reaction chamber. The second reaction vessel includes the electrolysis chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the fixing device of carbon dioxide.
   Fixing device of carbon dioxide (1)
   Reaction vessel (10)
   Reaction chamber (11)
   Electrolysis chamber (12)
   Carbon dioxide fixative charging unit (20A,20B)

GA 202221447B

   USE - As embryonic neural stem cell in vitro culture medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The medium expands and purifies neural stem cells in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for embryonic neural stem cell in vitro culture method, which involves (a) inoculating 5x105 cells/5 ml embryonic neural stem cells into the embryonic neural stem cell in vitro medium in a T25 culture flask and culturing under carbon dioxide in a cell incubator and (b) adding medium into the T25 culture flask once in every 2-3 days, shaking and continuing culturing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The medium comprises serum-free medium i.e. Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12), 20 ng EGF and 10 ng bFGF. Preferred Method: The method involves taking aborted human embryos of 6-10 weeks gestational age, separating tissues from fetal cerebral cortex, sequentially subjecting the tissues to trypsinization and mechanical pipetting to obtain embryonic neural stem cells in the form of single-cell suspension and then inoculating in the medium. The step (a) or (b) involves culturing at 36.5-37.5degrees Celsius, preferably 37degrees Celsius under 4.5-5.5%, preferably 5% carbon dioxide for 7-10 days. The step (b) involves taking the T25 culture flask out from the incubator and incubating in an ultra-clean bench for the supplementation.

   SONG J

   USE - The method is useful in forming complex stitch net by volume fracturing.

The section plug comprises a front supporting agent section plug with smaller grain diameter and sand, and a back supporting agent section plug with larger grain diameter and sand.
