TI Alcohol amine chain extender has good water resistance, high elongation at break, high tensile strength and high thermal stability.
AU WANG J
   LI X
   KANG M
   ZHAO Y
   FENG Y
   LI Q
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202020919F
AB    NOVELTY - Alcohol amine chain extender.
   USE - Alcohol amine chain extender.
   ADVANTAGE - The alcohol amine chain extender has good water resistance, high elongation at break, high tensile strength and high thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing alcohol amine chain extender, which involves:
   (A) adding diglycidyl ether and catalyst to a high-pressure reaction kettle at 90-150 degrees C;
   (B) passing carbon dioxide continuously for 25-45 hours;
   (C) filtering the catalyst to obtain cyclic carbonate compound;
   (D) adding cyclic carbonate compound to the reactor at 30-120 degrees C under stirring for 3-15 hours; and
   (E) distilling excess diamine compound under reduced pressure for 3 -20 hours to obtain alcohol amine chain extender.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diglycidyl ether is ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, butanediol diglycidyl ether, bisphenol A diglycidyl ether, hexanediol diglycidyl ether, neopentyl glycol diglycidyl ether, diethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether or polypropylene glycol diglycidyl glycidyl ethers. The catalyst is a metal salt, a quaternary ammonium salt, an amino alcohol, and a macroporous basic anion exchange resin, zinc bromide and ionic liquid composite catalyst, magnesium-aluminum composite oxide, and Lewis acid metal zinc halide. The metal salt is lithium bromide, lithium chloride, potassium bromide, potassium iodide or sodium bromide. The quaternary ammonium salt is tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium iodide or tetrabutyl ammonium chloride. The macroporous basic anion exchange resin is a strongly basic styrene ion exchange resin or an acrylic ion exchange resin. The styrene-based ion exchange resin is D296, D201 or 201asterisk7 D301. The acrylic ion-exchange resin is D314, D311 or D318. The diamine compound comprises hexamethylene diamine, p-phenylenediamine, propylene diamine, butanediamine, ethylenediamine, pentanediamine, octanediamine, m-phenylenediamine or cyclohexanediamine. The polymer polyol is polyether diol, polyester diol, and biomass material polyol. The polyether glycol is polytetramethylene ether glycol, polyoxypropylene glycol, polyoxyethylene glycol, polybutadiene polyols. The polyester diol is a polyadipate, a poly- eta -caprolactone diol, or a polycarbonate diol. The polyadipates are polyethylene adipate, polybutylene adipate, polyadipate diethylene glycol-propylene glycol, polyethylene adipate-butanediol, polyhexane adipate, polyethylene adipate-neopentyl glycol. The biomass material is castor oil or vegetable oil. The diisocyanate compound is an aromatic diisocyanate compound, an aliphatic diisocyanate compound, or a modified diisocyanate compound. The aromatic diisocyanate compound is toluene diisocyanate, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, p-phenylene diisocyanate or 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate. The aliphatic diisocyanate compound is 1,6-hexanediisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, or 4,4'-dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate. The modified diisocyanate compound is liquefied 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, carbamate ester-modified isocyanate, dimer, trimer, biuret-modified isocyanate, carbodiimide-modified isocyanate, or blocked modified isocyanate. The solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone, N, N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, acetonitrile or nitromethane.

AU OH J E
   IL S
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 202020519H
AB    NOVELTY - Binder composition comprises 100 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-40 pts. wt. calcium oxide and 1-20 pts. wt. formate.
   USE - The composition is useful in producing binder (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The binder composition reduces voids, improves initial strength and long-term strength over time, exhibits strength equal to or higher than that of the cement-based binder, replaces conventional cement-based binder, and is environmentally friendly. The method generates less carbon dioxide, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) producing binder composition, comprising (a) preparing binder composition by mixing 100 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-40 pts. wt. quicklime and 1-20 pts. wt. formate, and (b) mixing the composition with water to cure; and
   (2) binder obtained by curing above binder composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The formate is calcium formate, sodium formate and/or potassium formate. The quicklime is activator which induces pozzolanic reaction of fly ash. The fly ash has average particle diameter of 1-500 mu m. The C-S-H peak, the alumina-iron oxide-sulfate (AFm phase) peak, the katoite peak, and the calcite peak is measured during X-ray diffraction spectrum measurement, after curing the composition. The composition is reduced amount of aluminum after curing compared to before curing. The binder has multiple pores having size of less than or equal to 20 nm, and porosity of less than or equal to 50%. The binder comprises the bonding material aged for more than 25 days after starting curing, and compressive strength of greater than or equal to 13 Mpa. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 45-70 wt.% silicon dioxide,15-30 wt.% aluminum oxide , 5-15 wt.% iron(III) oxide,1-10 wt.% calcium oxide, 0.1-3 wt.% potassium oxide, 0.1-3 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.1-3 wt.% sodium oxide, 0.1-2 wt.% titanium dioxide, 0.1-2 wt.% sulfur trioxide and 0.1-2 wt.% phosphorus pentoxide. Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises mixing 20-50 pts. wt. water based on 100 pts. wt. composition. The step (b) comprises performing at 40-80 degrees C and a relative humidity of greater than or equal to 80%.
   EXAMPLE - 85 pts. wt. Fly ash, 15 pts. wt. quicklime and 1 pts. wt. calcium formate were mixed to prepare the binder composition.

   USE - Catalyst composition used for manufacturing                polyalkylene carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst composition provides easy                manufacture of polyalkylene carbonate and improved                reactivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst composition comprises polynuclear                metal complex of formula (A): LM1mM2n, and an                alcohol compound of formula (B): R1OH.
   M1 = metal(s) of group 12;
   M2 = metal chosen from 3 group metal and                lanthanoids;
   L = ligand of M1 and M2;
   m,n = 1 or more;and
   R1 = alkyl or aryl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) polyalkylene carbonate of formula (III);                and
   (2) manufacture of polyalkylene carbonate                (III), which involves reacting carbon dioxide and                epoxide in presence of the catalyst.

GA 2018411140
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving Dendrobium involves                sealing Dendrobium in a two-way stretching                polypropylene film and storing in the refrigeration                environment of -5 degrees C to 0 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving                Dendrobium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method preserves Dendrobium using                packaging bag, which provides certain air                permeability, and an environment having low oxygen                and high carbon dioxide, inhibits a respiratory                effect of the Dendrobium, and effectively delays                aging, when placed in a low temperature storage                environment. The method preserves Dendrobium in a                refrigerated environment of -5 degrees C to 0                degrees C and slows the Dendrobium becoming                mildewy, prolongs the storage time of the                Dendrobium, and maintains the commercial and                nutritional value of the Dendrobium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Dendrobium is                  tiled in at least one layer in a two-way                  stretching polypropylene film and placing                  moisture-absorbent in a sealed space formed by a                  two-way stretching polypropylene film. The each                  layer of tiled Dendrobium is covered with a                  moisture-absorbent. The moisture-absorbent covers                  the tiled Dendrobium is a newspaper. The                  moisture-absorbent comprises a food desiccant.                  The food desiccant is placed at the bottom of the                  bottom of tiled Dendrobium. The weight ratio of                  the tiled Dendrobium and the food desiccant is                  50:1. The tiled Dendrobium are tiled to form at                  least two layers with a support structure between                  the layers for separating the tiled Dendrobium of                  adjacent layers from each other to avoid                  crushing. Preferred Method: The method involves                  placing Dendrobium together with two-way                  stretching polypropylene film in the packaging                  bag, sealing the packaging bag and storing in a                  refrigeration environment. The temperature of the                  refrigerator compartment is controlled at -4                  degrees C to 0 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU ZHU B
   ZOU X
   LIANG J
   CHEN L
AE CHINA GEZHOUBA GROUP CEMENT CO LTD (CECG-C)
GA 201808622Y
AB    NOVELTY - A modified silicate cement clinker comprises                75-85 wt.% limestone, 1-15 wt.% siliceous material,                0.5-15 wt.% aluminum material, 0.5-3 wt.% iron raw                material, and 2-5 wt.% industrial gypsum.
   USE - Modified silicate cement clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The clinker reduces energy consumption,                reduces carbon dioxide emission, high early                strength and high later strength, fast setting                time, and low sintering temperature and volume                shrinkage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of modified silicate cement clinker                comprising taking limestone, siliceous material,                aluminum raw material, ferrous raw material and                industrial gypsum, crushing, homogenizing and                storing; grinding and placing in storehouse; and                sending into precalcining kiln, calcining at                1300-1400 degrees C for 8-10 minutes, and                cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The modified silicate                  cement clinker comprises 78 wt.% limestone, 10                  wt.% siliceous material, 8 wt.% aluminum                  material, 2 wt.% iron raw material, and 3 wt.%                  industrial gypsum. The industrial gypsum is                  desulfurized gypsum, phosphogypsum, fluorogypsum                  or salt gypsum. The limestone comprises 48-53                  wt.% calcium oxide. The siliceous material is                  silica, sandstone, shale or slag. The siliceous                  material comprises 65-90 wt.% silicon dioxide.                  The aluminum raw material is clay, shale, kaolin,                  coal gangue or pulverized coal ash. The iron raw                  material is sulfuric acid residue, converter                  slag, iron ore or iron tailings. The industrial                  gypsum is desulfurized gypsum, phosphogypsum,                  fluorogypsum and/or salt gypsum. The clinker                  comprises 60-70 wt.% tricalcium silicate, 10-20                  wt.% dicalcium silicate, 5-10 wt.% tetracalcium                  aluminoferrite, 3-8 wt.% calcium aluminate, and                  0.5-1.5 wt.% calcium oxide.

PN CN114618259-A
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas,            involves setting air passage and flue gas passage,            connecting air passage with air compressor, and            sequentially connecting flue air channel with carbon            dioxide condenser through pipeline.
AU ZHANG X
   WANG L
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
GA 2022830040
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting an air passage and                a flue gas passage. The air passage is connected                with an air compressor that compressed air cooler                compressed air storage and a compressed air                expansion machine through a pipeline. The flue air                channel is sequentially connected with a carbon                dioxide condenser through the pipeline. A carbon                dioxide liquefier is connected in parallel. The                compressed air expander is used with the expansion                work that is generated by the compressed air                expansion to drive the flue gas compressor. The low                temperature air output is used to provide cold                source for the carbon dioxide condenser and the                carbon dioxide solidifying device by the compressed                air expander. The air is compressed in the air                passage, cooling, storing, expanding, and                outputting low temperature air. The low temperature                air is discharged after cooling the carbon dioxide                condenser and carbon dioxide solidifying device in                the flue gas passage.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide in flue                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing the capture of                carbon dioxide in flue gas efficiently. The method                allows the carbon dioxide solidifying device                to-be-connected with the heat collecting inlet of                the compressed air cooler in parallel, so that the                heat sink and compressed air are not connected in a                non-direct contact type heat exchange, thus                realizing cold recycling, and hence realizing cold                energy recovery in an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the method for capturing carbon dioxide in flue                gas. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Air compressor (1)
   Air cooler compressed air (2)
   Compressed air reservoir (3)
   Compressed air expander (4)
   Flue gas processor (5)
   Smoke gas compressor (6)
   Heat collecting inlet (7)
   Carbon dioxide condenser (8)
   Smoke gas compressor using carbon dioxide                solidifying device (9-1)

PN CN113877539-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide solid adsorbent used as carbon dioxide absorbent in an air direct capture carbon dioxide device, comprises e.g. dissolving organic amine in water, stirring, adding porous carrier material to solution, immersing, evaporating aqueous solution to dryness, and drying the sample.
AU ZHANG K
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG C
   ZHANG S
   WANG F
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202211477T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide solid adsorbent comprises (1) dissolving organic amine in water to obtain solution A, adding metal salt to solution A, stirring for 1-5 hours, preferably 2-3 hours to obtain solution B, where the viscosity of solution B is 0.1-50 MPa.s lower than that of solution A, preferably 0.1-10 MP.s (the viscosity of the solution is tested at 25degrees Celsius), and (2) adding the porous carrier material to the prepared solution B, stirring and immersing for a period of time, evaporating the aqueous solution to dryness, and then drying the sample to obtain a carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide solid adsorbent, which is used as a carbon dioxide absorbent in an air direct capture carbon dioxide device, a flue gas carbon dioxide removal device, an air purification device or a vehicle exhaust carbon dioxide capture device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide solid adsorbent has high dispersibility of organic amines in the carrier pores due to the introduction of metal salts; enhances mass transfer; shows a faster kinetic adsorption rate at 15% carbon dioxide concentration; and can reach kinetic adsorption rate 63 mg/g/minute; and has industrial application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The stirring and dipping time is 6-48 hours. The drying temperature is 70-110degreesC. The drying time is 1-24 hours.Preferred Components: The organic amines include ethylenediamine, diethanolamine, polyethyleneamine, pentaethylenehexamine, polypropyleneimine, polyethyleneimine, tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine, and/or diethylenetriamine. The metal salts include cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt chloride hexahydrate, nickel nitrate hexahydrate, nickel chloride hexahydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zinc chloride, and/or magnesium chloride hexahydrate. The porous carrier material is mesoporous silica, porous silica gel, sepiolite, porous resin, activated carbon, SAB-15 molecular sieve and/or other porous materials. The mass ratio of the organic amine to water is 1:1-100, preferably 1:5-40. The molar ratio of the organic amine to the metal salt is 1:0.01-0.2, preferably 1:0.05-0.1. The mass ratio of the porous carrier material to the solution B is 1:1-80, preferably 1:5-40.

GA 2020331354

A carbon dioxide gas after the phase change is injected (S3) in a predetermined direction with a predetermined discharge amount to obtain thrust. The carbon dioxide is contained in a thermally insulated container in a liquid phase at a predetermined temperature.

The requirements of promoting physical advancement in different fields are met.

PN CN111116322-A
TI Extracting cannabidiol useful in cosmetics, and health-care and medicine products comprises e.g. preparing carbon dioxide, raising temperature and pressure to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide liquid, and drying industrial hemp leaves.
AU LI R
   MA W
   HUANG S
   WEN X
   WANG Y
AE YUNNAN JINBIDI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202042656J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting cannabidiol comprises e.g. preparing carbon dioxide in compression chamber, cooling, increasing pressure, converting carbon dioxide gas to liquid, raising temperature and pressure until obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide liquid, maintaining density to fill supercritical carbon dioxide liquid to container, drying and baking industrial hemp leaves, maintaining water content, vacuum drying, crushing dried industrial hemp leaves in crusher, placing pretreated industrial hemp leaf in extraction kettle with supercritical carbon dioxide liquid, extracting to obtain extracting liquid, introducing ethanol to extraction kettle to improve solubility of extract, entering extraction liquid to rectification column, adjusting pressure and temperature to improve extraction rate, separating plant extract from carbon dioxide liquid, entering supercritical fluid to separation tank through expansion valve, returning carbon dioxide gas to main tank, and discharging cannabidiol.
   USE - The cannabidiol is useful in cosmetics, health-care products, and medicine products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly, has good manufacturability, and realizes organic biological extraction. The cannabidiol extract has 90% yield with 20-30% cannabidiol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting cannabidiol comprises (1) preparing carbon dioxide in compression chamber, cooling to less than -56 degrees C, increasing pressure to more than 75 MPa, converting carbon dioxide gas to liquid, raising temperature to more than 31 degrees C and raising pressure to more than 7.38 MPa until obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide liquid, and maintaining density to fill supercritical carbon dioxide liquid to container, where the supercritical carbon dioxide liquid is suitable for chemical extraction without denaturing and damage, (2) drying and baking industrial hemp leaves in full bloom period, maintaining water content within specific range and lime soil content less than required range, vacuum drying to specific moisture content, and crushing dried industrial hemp leaves in crusher, (3) placing pretreated industrial hemp leaf in extraction kettle with supercritical carbon dioxide liquid, extracting at specific temperature and pressure to obtain extracting liquid, introducing specific amount of ethanol through solvent pump to extraction kettle to improve solubility of extract and extraction rate, controlling valve to place extraction liquid in separation kettle, adjusting pressure and temperature to separate extraction liquid, entering extraction liquid to rectification column, and adjusting pressure and temperature in rectification column to improve extraction rate of cannabidiol, (4) reversing carbon dioxide liquid to low-pressure separator through valve control, separating plant extract from carbon dioxide liquid, entering supercritical fluid to separation tank through expansion valve, reducing pressure to obtain carbon dioxide liquid gas, and separates liquid gas from extract, and (5) returning carbon dioxide gas to main tank, discharging cannabidiol from separator under reduced pressure, and returning carbon dioxide gas to extraction kettle through compressor for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (2), the moisture content of baked industrial hemp leaves is 15-20% with lime soil content less than 5%; the vacuum drying is carried out at 150-180 degrees C for 20-40 minutes to a moisture content of 5-8%; the industrial hemp leaves are crushed to 10-15 meshes. In the step (3), extraction is carried out at 50 degrees C with a pressure of 30 MPa; the separation of extract is carried out at 50 degrees C with a pressure of 12 MPa; the pressure in rectification column is 15 MPa with a temperature of 50 degrees C.

PN CN114618509-A

AU JIN T
AE SHANGHAI NIPUDUN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022893533
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction                catalyst comprises a ferrous salt and molybdate                loaded on fine-grained volcanic rock powder which                is porous and has a large specific surface area to                form a catalytic system comprising iron,                molybdenum, iron molybdate and various elements in                volcanic rock. The iron molybdate can improve the                stability of an active portion of the                catalyst.
   USE - Carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared iron molybdate can improve the                stability of the catalyst using iron and molybdenum                as active elements. The catalyst is obtained using                volcanic rock powder which is easily obtained from                nature as a carrier, so that the process of                synthesizing methane from carbon monoxide and                hydrogen is inhibited, and the efficiency of                reducing carbon dioxide by hydrogen is improved.                The preparation of the catalyst is simplified, and                the cost is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                preparation of carbon dioxide hydrogenation                reduction catalyst, which involves (s1) dissolving                molybdate in water to form molybdate solution, (s2)                adding volcanic rock powder into water to form a                turbid solution, (s3) slowly pouring molybdate                solution into the powder turbid solution, stirring                for more than 12 hours, filtering, cleaning and                drying at 60 &#176;C for more than 12 hours to                obtain molybdate-loaded dry powder, (s4) dissolving                ferrous salt into water to form ferrous solution,                (s5) adding molybdate-loaded dry powder into water                to form a turbid solution, and (s6) slowly pouring                ferrous solution into the turbid solution loaded                with the molybdate dry powder, stirring for more                than 12 hours, filtering, cleaning, drying at 60                &#176;C for more than 12 hours to obtain the                iron and molybdate loaded dry powder, and calcining                at 650 &#176;C for more than 6 hours. The                concentration of molybdate in step (s1) is 1-1.5 %,                volcanic rock powder in step (s2) is 3-5 %, ferrous                salt in step (s4) is 0.3-0.6 %, and molybdate                loaded dry powder in step (s5) is 3-5 %. In step                (s6), the ratio of molybdate to iron is                1.5:1-2.5:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The volcanic rock powder                  is prepared by grinding alkaline volcanic rock                  such as andesite, diatomaceous earth, and                  zeolite.Preferred Property: The volcanic rock powder                  has a particle size of 10-50 &#956;m.Preferred Components: The powder is mainly                  composed of silica, and contains oxides such as                  alkaline earth metals, alkali metals, iron, and                  aluminum.

The catalyst structure comprises a carrier having a porous structure comprising a zeolite-type compound, and at least one catalyst substance contained in the carrier. The carrier has a passage that communicates with each other inside, and the catalyst substance is metal fine particles of a non-precious metal and is present in at least the passage of the carrier.

PN CN110524781-A
TI Method for foaming thermoplastic polyurethane sheet, involves heating thermoplastic polyurethane raw material to molten state, followed by heat molding, cutting, sealing in autoclave and introducing supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU XU S
   CHEN J
AE XIAMEN FENGTE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
   XU S (XUSS-Individual)
GA 2019A38237
AB    NOVELTY - A thermoplastic polyurethane sheet foaming method involves heating the thermoplastic polyurethane raw material to a molten state and heat molding to obtain the thermoplastic polyurethane original plate. The thermoplastic polyurethane original plate is cut according to design requirements to obtain a first thermoplastic polyurethane board. The first thermoplastic polyurethane sheet is placed in an autoclave, followed by sealing, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into the autoclave to a pressure of 8-30 MPa, raising the temperature, evacuating carbon dioxide after holding at a constant temperature, introducing supercritical nitrogen into the autoclave, raising the temperature to 50-85 degrees C, after maintaining the pressure, quickly discharging nitrogen and taking out the swollen first thermoplastic polyurethane sheet from the autoclave to obtain a second thermoplastic polyurethane sheet. The second TPU sheet is placed in a constant temperature device to obtain a foamed product.
   USE - Method for foaming thermoplastic polyurethane sheet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables foaming the thermoplastic polyurethane sheet with better product flatness and plumpness in convenient and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A thermoplastic polyurethane sheet foaming method involves heating the thermoplastic polyurethane raw material to a molten state and heat molding to obtain the thermoplastic polyurethane original plate. The thermoplastic polyurethane original plate is cut according to design requirements to obtain a first thermoplastic polyurethane board. The first thermoplastic polyurethane sheet is placed in an autoclave, followed by sealing, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into the autoclave to a pressure of 8-30 MPa, raising the temperature to 50-85 degrees C, quickly evacuating carbon dioxide after holding at a constant temperature for 1.5-5 hours, introducing supercritical nitrogen into the autoclave to a pressure of 5-20MPa, raising the temperature to 50-85 degrees C, after maintaining the pressure at a constant temperature for 0.5-2 hours, quickly discharging nitrogen and taking out the swollen first thermoplastic polyurethane sheet from the autoclave to obtain a second thermoplastic polyurethane sheet. The second TPU sheet is placed in a constant temperature device at 80-120 degrees C for 0.5-10 minutes to obtain a foamed product.

TI Method for determining subject experiencing anaerobic exercise during exercise session and modifying subsequent exercise session, involves administering bicarbonate salt, monitoring expired air followed by measuring ratio of carbon dioxide.
AU JAVITT N B
   PINCHOT R
AE PINCHOT R (PINC-Individual)
GA 201900026Q
AB    NOVELTY - A subject experiencing anaerobic exercise determining and subsequent exercise session modifying method involves administering a bicarbonate salt comprising a carbon-13 isotope prior to the exercise session to the subject. The expired air from the subject during the exercise session is monitored followed by measuring a ratio of carbon dioxide comprising the carbon-13 isotope to carbon dioxide comprising a carbon-12 isotope in the monitored expired air at time instants, to obtain ratio measurements over a period of the exercise session, recording ratio measurements, and determining an inflection point in the carbon-13 isotope to carbon-12 isotope ratio measurements, and modifying a subsequent exercise session, relative to the exercise session, based on the indication of anaerobic exercise.
   USE - Method for determining subject experiencing anaerobic exercise during an exercise session and modifying subsequent exercise session (claimed), and altering exercise sessions and/or training periods to produce optimal results for the subject, e.g. increased level of overall fitness.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving performance, and fitness/exercise regimen pattern of the subject.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A subject experiencing anaerobic exercise determining and subsequent exercise session modifying method involves administering a bicarbonate salt comprising a carbon-13 isotope prior to the exercise session to the subject. The expired air from the subject during the exercise session is monitored followed by measuring a ratio of carbon dioxide comprising the carbon-13 isotope to carbon dioxide comprising a carbon-12 isotope in the monitored expired air at time instants, to obtain ratio measurements over a period of the exercise session, recording ratio measurements, and determining an inflection point in the carbon-13 isotope to carbon-12 isotope ratio measurements, where an increase in the carbon-13 isotope to carbon-12 isotope ratio after the inflection point indicates that the subject is experiencing anaerobic exercise during the exercise session, and modifying a subsequent exercise session, relative to the exercise session, based on the indication of anaerobic exercise. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for determining the effectiveness of a training period in a subject.

PN CN108410217-A
TI Preparing environmentally friendly decorative paint comprises e.g. preparing carbon-doped titanium dioxide particles, adding calcium oxide and then introducing carbon dioxide gas, adding a dispersant and stirring.
AU JIANG Y
AE JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 201865905E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing environmentally friendly decorative paint comprises e.g. (i) adding isopropyl titanate into closed reaction vessel, then heating, introducing ozone to maintaing the pressure of the closed reactor at 5-10 atmospheres, and placing in a sealed sintering furnace, then introducing carbon dioxide and calcining to obtain carbon-doped titanium dioxide particles, (ii) adding calcium oxide into slurry, then introducing carbon dioxide gas until the solution to completely clarify and then adding a dispersant, while adding carbon-doped titanium dioxide particles to clear solution and then stirring to completely dispersed, heating, then reacting, adding diatomaceous earth, stirring for 15-30 min and adding inorganic pigment and filtering to obtain first filtrate and first filter residue, and (iii) calcining first filter residue obtained in step (ii) at 600-650 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, then crushing and sieving to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing environmentally friendly decorative paint.
   ADVANTAGE - The decorative paint: purifies indoor air and does not release of harmful gases includes formaldehyde; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing environmentally friendly decorative paint comprises (i) adding isopropyl titanate into closed reaction vessel, then heating to 250-320 degrees C and introducing ozone to maintaing the pressure of the closed reactor at 5-10 atmospheres, and placing in a sealed sintering furnace, then introducing carbon dioxide and calcining at 600-650 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours to obtain carbon-doped titanium dioxide particles, (ii) adding calcium oxide into slurry, then introducing carbon dioxide gas until the solution to completely clarify and then adding a dispersant, while adding carbon-doped titanium dioxide particles to clear solution and then stirring to completely dispersed, heating to 90-95 degrees C, then reacting for 1.5-2 hours, adding diatomaceous earth, stirring for 15-30 min and adding inorganic pigment and filtering to obtain first filtrate and first filter residue, and (iii) calcining first filter residue obtained in step (ii) at 600-650 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, then crushing and sieving by 150-200 mesh sieve to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), the number of moles of ozone introduced is 8-10 times the number of moles of isopropyl titanate, and the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of the sintering furnace is greater than 99.5 wt.%. In step (ii), the inorganic pigment is iron oxide red, iron oxide yellow and/or iron oxide black. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the mass ratio between calcium oxide, carbon-doped titanium dioxide particles and diatomaceous earth is 2-4:1:1-2, the dispersing agent is sodium hexametaphosphate, and the concentration of dispersing agent is 0.1-0.2 wt.%; the stirring speed is 200-250 revolutions/minute; the diatomaceous earth has a heavy metal content of less than 20 parts per million; and the specific surface area is greater than 50 m2/g. In step (ii), the first filtrate is returned for slurrying together with calcium oxide. In step (iii), the first filter residue is passed through nitrogen gas during the calcination process.

PT P
PN CN107565138-A; CN107565138-B
TI Preparing lithium carbon dioxide battery anode material comprises e.g. dissolving polyvinylpyrrolidone in absolute ethanol, dissolving manganese acetate tetrahydrate in mixed solution 1 and placing obtained precursor in tube furnace.
AU LIU X
   LU S
   LEI X
   DING Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 201804622W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium carbon dioxide battery anode material comprises e.g. (i) dissolving polyvinylpyrrolidone in absolute ethanol to obtain mixed solution 1, (ii) dissolving manganese acetate tetrahydrate in mixed solution 1 to obtain mixed solution 2, (iii) magnetically stirring mixed solution 2 at 40-80 degrees C, refluxing for 3-5 hours, naturally cooling to obtain white precipitate, centrifuging resulting precipitate, washing with absolute ethanol, drying at 50-100 degrees C for 6-12 hours to obtain powdery precursor, and (iv) placing obtained precursor in tube furnace, heating and incubating.
   USE - The anode material is useful in catalyst manganese(III) oxide (Mn2O3) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation, low cost; is easy to obtain and product is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lithium carbon dioxide battery anode material comprises (i) dissolving polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) in absolute ethanol to obtain mixed solution 1, where adding 0.5-1 g PVP for every 50-100 ml absolute ethanol, (ii) dissolving manganese acetate tetrahydrate (Mn(CH3COO)2.4H2O) in mixed solution 1 to obtain mixed solution 2, where mass ratio between PVP and Mn(CH3COO)2.4H2O is 0.5-1:1-2, (iii) magnetically stirring mixed solution 2 at 40-80 degrees C, refluxing for 3-5 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain white precipitate, then centrifuging resulting precipitate, washing with absolute ethanol, and drying at 50-100 degrees C for 6-12 hours under vacuum to obtain powdery precursor, and (iv) placing obtained precursor in tube furnace under air atmosphere, heating at rate of 0.5-2 degrees C/minute at 500-700 degrees C, and incubating for 2-4 hours to obtain lithium carbon dioxide cathode catalyst Mn2O3. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of lithium carbon dioxide battery anode in catalyst Mn2O3 comprising mixing prepared Mn2O3, carbon material and binder, and pressing resulting mixture on carbon paper under pressure of 1-4 MPa to obtain lithium-ion battery anode, where carbon material is acetylene black, Ketjen black and carbon nanotube, binder is polytetrafluoroethylene, mass percentage of carbon material in mixture is 20-60%, mass percentage of binder is 5-20% and Mn2O3 (balancing amount).

PT P
PN CN110982513-A; CN110982513-B
TI Preparing fluorescent carbon dot useful e.g. in            detecting cell activity comprises mixing citric acid,            adding co-solvent dimethyl sulfoxide, preparing            precursor solution, centrifuging, dialyzing and            performing rotary evaporation.
AU LI C
   GUO Y
   SUN Y
   QU L
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202033147Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fluorescent carbon dot comprises (a)                mixing citric acid, triethylenetetramine and                fluorescein mixed solution in absolute ethanol,                adding co-solvent dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO),                preparing a precursor solution and placing in a                microwave reaction pipe, (b) adding the microwave                reaction pipe in step (a) in the microwave reactor                to allow the precursor solution to react under                heating conditions and naturally cooling the                product after the reaction to room temperature, (c)                centrifuging the product cooled in the step (b) at                6000 revolutions/minute, removing the large                particles and dialyzing with a dialysis membrane to                obtain the purified carbon dots solution, (d)                performing rotary evaporation to the carbon dots                solution obtained in the step (c) to obtain                fluorescent carbon dots.
   USE - The fluorescent carbon dot is useful in                detecting cell activity, distinguishing normal                cells from cancer cells and specific cell membrane                targeting of liver cancer cells (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is easy to operate, easy to obtain                raw materials and environmentally friendly. The                carbon dot: has potential application value in the                fields of biology and medical treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                detecting cell activity using fluorescent carbon                dot comprising (a) inoculating the cells in a                confocal dish, incubating at 37 degrees C, 5%                carbon dioxide with saturated humidity for 12-24                hours in a cell culture box to obtain culture                solution A, where the cell is at least one of a                HeLa cell, A549 cell, MCF-7 cell, 4T1cell and                Raw264.7 cell, (b) adding fluorescent carbon dots                into the culturing solution A obtained in the step                (a), incubating at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide                with saturated humidity for 4 hours in a cell                culture box to obtain culture liquid B, (c) adding                the lysosomal staining agent LysoTracker (RTM:                Fluorophore linked to a weak base) Deep Red to the                culture liquid B obtained in the step (b),                incubating for 15 minutes to obtain the culture                solution C, (d) adding the lysosomal staining agent                MitoTracker (RTM: Red-fluorescent dye) Deep Red to                the culture solution B obtained in the step (b),                incubating for 15 minutes to obtain the culture                liquid D, (e) adding nuclear staining reagent                NucRed (RTM: Cell impermeant stain) Live 647 to the                culture solution B obtained in the step (b),                incubating for 15 minutes to obtain the mixed                solution E, (f) adding the ice-cold methanol to the                culture solution B obtained in the step (b) and                incubating for 5 minutes to obtain the mixed                solution F and (g) washing the cells using                phosphate buffer solution, adding fresh phosphate                buffer solution and using laser confocal microscope                to shoot image. The process of distinguishing                normal cells from cancer cells using fluorescent                carbon dot comprises (i) inoculating the cells in a                confocal dish and incubating in a cell incubator at                37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide with saturated                humidity for 12-24 hours to obtain culture medium                A, where the cells are 3t3 cells or 7702 cells,                (ii) adding fluorescent carbon dots to the culture                solution A obtained in step (i) and incubating in a                cell incubator at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide                with saturated humidity for 4 hours to obtain                culture solution B and (iii) rinsing the cells with                phosphate buffer and adding fresh phosphate buffer,                where the laser confocal microscope is useful for                imaging. The process of specific cell membrane                targeting of liver cancer cells using fluorescent                carbon dot comprises (i) inoculating the cells in a                confocal dish and incubating in a cell incubator at                37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide with saturated                humidity for 12-24 hours to obtain culture medium                A, where the cells are HepG-2 cells or SMMC-7721                cell, (ii) adding fluorescent carbon dots to the                culture solution A obtained in step (i) and                incubating in a cell incubator at 37 degrees C, 5%                carbon dioxide with saturated humidity for 4 hours                to obtain culture solution B and (iii) rinsing the                cells with phosphate buffer and adding fresh                phosphate buffer, where the laser confocal                microscope is useful for imaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises                  heating at 100-140 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The                  dialysis bag specification is 1 KDa for 72 hours.                  Preferred Components: The molar mass ratio of                  citric acid, triethylenetetramine and fluorescein                  in the step (a) is 1:1-5:1-5. The fluorescence                  intensity decreases, when the pH increases at 460                  nm and the fluorescence intensity increases the                  pH increases at 520 nm. The cell culture medium                  in the confocal dish includes Dulbecco's modified                  eagle medium (DMEM) high glucose medium, 10%                  volume fetal bovine serum, 100 mu g/ml penicillin                  and 100 mu g/ml streptomycin. The final                  concentration of fluorescent carbon dots after                  addition is 50-100 mu g/ml.

PN KR2022037651-A; KR2446795-B1
TI Method of decomposition of carbon dioxide            comprises contacting reducing gas with metal oxide to            reversibly activate metal oxide having oxygen atom            vacancies, decomposing carbon dioxide into carbon            monoxide.
AU NAMSUNGCHAN
   JANG J T
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2022447415
AB    NOVELTY - Method of decomposition of carbon dioxide                comprises contacting a reducing gas with a metal                oxide to reversibly activate the metal oxide having                oxygen atom vacancies, decomposing carbon dioxide                into carbon or carbon monoxide by contacting the                activated metal oxide having oxygen atom vacancies                with a carbon dioxide-containing gas. The metal                oxide is a compound (I).
   USE - The method is useful for decomposition of                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and suitable for                large-scale system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of decomposition of carbon dioxide                comprises contacting a reducing gas with a metal                oxide to reversibly activate the metal oxide having                oxygen atom vacancies, decomposing carbon dioxide                into carbon or carbon monoxide by contacting the                activated metal oxide having oxygen atom vacancies                with a carbon dioxide-containing gas. The metal                oxide is a compound of formula (AB1O3) (I).
   A = Sr, and a part contains or does not                contain Ba, Ca, and Mg; and
   B1 = Fe, and a part contains or does not                contain Zr.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon dioxide decomposition device, comprising a                reactor located in a furnace and having an                activated metal oxide, a reducing gas source and a                carbon dioxide-containing gas source, a first                regulator for controlling the flow rate of                supplying the reducing gas to the reactor in                contact with the activated metal oxide to reduce it                to a metal oxide having a vacancy of oxygen atoms,                and a second regulator for controlling the flow                rate of supplying the carbon dioxide-containing gas                to the reactor to decompose the carbon dioxide into                carbon or carbon monoxide by bringing the carbon                dioxide-containing gas into contact with the                reduced metal oxide having a vacancy of the oxygen                atom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is                  strontium ferrate having a perovskite structure.                  The reducing gas is a by-product gas (coke oven                  gas). The carbon dioxide-containing gas utilizes                  furnace gas or converter gas. Preferred Method:                  The method is carried out with a reaction                  pressure of less than 0.1 bar. The carbon dioxide                  decomposition proceeds at 250-800 degrees C. The                  conversion rate for decomposing the carbon                  dioxide into carbon or carbon monoxide is greater                  than or equal to 80% at 500-800 degrees C to                  decompose 200-2000 Nm3/hour mixed gas containing                  carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The method is                  conducted in a continuous flow system including a                  catalytic reactor for injecting a reducing gas to                  the catalytic reactor to activate the metal                  oxide.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of preparation of                strontium ferrate that do not support the                claims.

PN CN113433297-A; CN214310500-U; CN113433297-B
TI Method for detecting concentration of carbon dioxide in soil by using carbon dioxide concentration detector in real-time, involves detecting concentration of carbon dioxide in storage cavity in real time by adopting carbon dioxide detector to obtain detection result.
AU YIN D
   SHI Z
   HE S
   XU L
   XU B
   ZHAO W
   LIANG B
AE WEIFANG ZHENGTONG ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (WEIF-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG YISIMAN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   WEIFANG HUIJINHAI IOT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WEIF-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG HUIJINHAI SMART AGRIC RES INST (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG HUIJINHAI SMART AGRIC RES INST (SHAN-Non-standard)
   WEIFANG HUIJINHAI IOT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WEIF-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4748E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves embedding a carbon dioxide concentration detector in soil to be detected. The storage cavity of the carbon dioxide concentration detector is vacuumized to form negative pressure in the storage cavity. The real-time air pressure in the storage cavity is detected. The real-time air pressure is compared with the minimum pressure preset threshold. The gas in the soil is allowed to enter the storage cavity through an air inlet hole (4) in the carbon dioxide concentration detector. The real-time air pressure in the storage cavity is detected. The real-time air pressure is compared with a maximum pressure preset threshold at any moment. The concentration of the carbon dioxide in the storage cavity is detected in real time by adopting a carbon dioxide detector to obtain a detection result.
   USE - Method for detecting concentration of carbon dioxide in soil by using carbon dioxide concentration detector in real-time.

AB    NOVELTY - An absorbing liquid is prepared by dissolving                linear monoamine, diamine, and an ether                bond-containing compound (I) in water.
   USE - Absorbing liquid used in removal apparatus of                carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide for                absorbing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide                from gas (all claimed) such as exhaust gas of                boiler of thermal power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbing liquid has excellent absorption                of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide during                regeneration of the absorption liquid by multiply                entangled components with one another by being                dissolved in water and due to their synergistic                effect, and reduces the amount of water vapor used                when the absorbent is regenerated in the carbon                dioxide recovery facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An absorbing liquid is prepared by dissolving                linear monoamine, diamine, and an ether                bond-containing compound of formula:                R1-O-(R2-O)n-R3 (I) in water.
   R1 = 2-4C hydrocarbyl;
   R2 = propylene;
   R3 = H;and
   n = 1-3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) removal apparatus of carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide, which comprises an absorption                tower that removes carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide by bringing a gas containing carbon dioxide                and/or hydrogen sulfide into contact with an                absorbing liquid, and an absorption liquid                regeneration tower (20) for regenerating a solution                having absorbed carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen                sulfide in which the solution regenerated by                removing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide in                the absorption liquid regeneration tower is reused                in the absorption tower; and
   (2) removal method of carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide, which involves bringing a gas                containing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide                into contact with an absorbing liquid, regenerating                a solution having absorbed carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide and reusing the solution                regenerated by removing carbon dioxide and/or                hydrogen sulfide in the absorption liquid                regeneration tower in the absorption tower.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide recovery apparatus.
   Waste gas (14)
   Absorbing liquid (17)
   Carbon dioxide absorption tower (18)
   Absorption liquid regeneration tower                (20)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ether                  bond-containing compound (I) is preferably                  propylene glycol alkyl ether, in which the alkyl                  ether in the propylene glycol alkyl ether is                  ethyl ether, propyl ether or butyl ether. The                  absorbing liquid is prepared by dissolving linear                  monoamine, diamine, and an ether bond-containing                  compound of formula: R4-O-(R5-O)n-R6 (II). The                  ether bond-containing compound (II) is preferably                  ethylene glycol alkyl ether acetate in which the                  alkyl ether in the ethylene glycol alkyl ether                  acetate is methyl ether or ethyl ether. The                  linear amine contains at least one of a primary                  linear monoamine, a secondary linear monoamine,                  and a tertiary linear monoamine. The diamine is a                  primary linear polyamine, and a secondary linear                  polyamine. The composition comprises 40-60 wt.%                  linear monoamine and diamine (in total). The                  mixing ratio of the sum of diamine and the ether                  bond-containing compound (I) to the linear                  monoamine is 0.35-1.1. The weight ratio of the                  ether bond-containing compound (I) and diamine                  linear monoamine is 0.99-7.8. The mixing ratio of                  the sum of diamine and the ether bond-containing                  compound (II) to the linear monoamine is                  0.35-1.1. The weight ratio of ether                  bond-containing compound (II) and diamine linear                  monoamine is 0.99-7.8.R4 = acetyl;R5 = ethylene;andR6 = alkyl.Preferred Conditions: The absorption                  temperature of the absorption tower is 30-80                  degrees C, and the regeneration temperature of                  the absorption liquid regeneration tower is 110                  degrees C or more. The partial pressure of carbon                  dioxide at the inlet of the absorption tower is                  low.

PT P
PN CN114210716-A
TI Method for high-efficiency curing and synergistic carbon fixation of heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash comprises performing three-stage countercurrent washing on fly ash, adding ground fly ash into carbon fixation bin with stirring equipment, pouring fly ash mixture, dehydrating and drying.
AU YAN J
   LI X
   CHEN J
   LIN X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2022466633
AB    NOVELTY - Method for high-efficiency curing and synergistic carbon fixation of heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash comprises performing three-stage countercurrent washing on the fly ash, controlling the liquid-solid ratio to 3:1-5:1, subjecting the fly ash washed with water to filter press treatment, drying at 90-105degrees Celsius, grinding the dried fly ash, adding the ground fly ash into carbon fixation bin with stirring equipment, adding water, starting the stirring equipment, monitoring the carbon dioxide concentration in the exhaust gas of the carbon-fixing silo in real time, ending carbon fixation reaction when the concentration rises to the concentration of carbon dioxide contained in the feed flue gas which indicates that the carbon dioxide capture capacity of the fly ash in the carbon sequestration bin is significantly reduced, pouring the fly ash mixture in the carbon fixation bin into air-drying box, dehydrating and drying at room temperature to obtain carbon fixation product.
   USE - The method is useful for high-efficiency curing and synergistic carbon fixation of heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high-efficiency curing and co-carbon fixation of waste metal of burned fly ash. The method does not need to add any additive, the treatment cost is low, at normal temperature and normal pressure; the process flow is simple, the process has no harmful substance generation, low carbon and environment-friend; can be directly installed in the power plant waste realizing semi-automatic production; and can improve fly ash capturing carbon dioxide capability, realizing carbon dioxide permanent sealing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for high-efficiency curing and synergistic carbon fixation of heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for high-efficiency curing and synergistic carbon fixation of heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN113373539-A; CN113373539-B
TI Preparing polyvinyl alcohol fiber useful for            preparing fiber-reinforced concrete comprises e.g.            adding polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane into dissolution            kettle containing dimethylformamide and water, and            introducing carbon dioxide gas.
AU WANG Y
   LI W
   HE J
   ZOU L
   WU X
   SHANG S
   SUN W
   WANG C
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2021A8168D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-strength and high-modulus                polyvinyl alcohol fiber comprises adding polyvinyl                alcohol/dodecane into dissolution kettle containing                dimethylformamide and water, heating to swelling in                stages, then introducing carbon dioxide gas into                dissolution kettle to obtain a certain pressure,                completely dissolving polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane,                then discharging carbon dioxide gas to obtain                polyvinyl alcohol spinning solution, then dry-wet                spinning polyvinyl alcohol spinning solution to                obtain high-strength and high-modulus polyvinyl                alcohol fiber, the polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane is                under the condition of concentrated sulfuric acid                catalysis, polyvinyl alcohol and n-dodecanoic acid                obtained by esterification reaction, or using                polyvinyl alcohol and sodium hydroxide solution as                raw material for synthesizing polyvinyl alcohol                sodium, then adding iodine dodecane and dibutyl                sulfate into polyvinyl alcohol sodium obtaining by                substitution reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyvinyl                alcohol fiber, which is useful for preparing                fiber-reinforced concrete (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fiber: has high-strength, high-modulus,                good breaking strength, elastic modulus, good water                resistance, and reinforcing effect; reduces                production of concrete crack; and prevents concrete                crack has been generated further expanding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-strength and high-modulus                polyvinyl alcohol fiber comprises adding polyvinyl                alcohol/dodecane into dissolution kettle containing                dimethylformamide and water, heating to swelling in                stages, then introducing carbon dioxide gas into                dissolution kettle to obtain a certain pressure,                completely dissolving polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane,                then discharging carbon dioxide gas to obtain                polyvinyl alcohol spinning solution, then dry-wet                spinning polyvinyl alcohol spinning solution to                obtain high-strength and high-modulus polyvinyl                alcohol fiber, the polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane is                under the condition of concentrated sulfuric acid                catalysis, polyvinyl alcohol and n-dodecanoic acid                obtained by esterification reaction, or using                polyvinyl alcohol and sodium hydroxide solution as                raw material for synthesizing polyvinyl alcohol                sodium, then adding iodine dodecane and dibutyl                sulfate into polyvinyl alcohol sodium obtaining by                substitution reaction, the breaking strength of the                high-strength and high-modulus polyvinyl alcohol                fiber is 12.6-14.2 cN/dtex, the elastic modulus is                319-334 cN/dtex, and the softening coefficient of                the high-strength and high-modulus polyvinyl                alcohol fiber is 0.85-0.95.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The process flow of                  dry-wet spinning is: polyvinyl alcohol high solid                  content spinning solution is extruding through                  the spinneret to form a spinning stream, the                  spinning stream enters the coagulation bath                  through the air layer, once stretching, three                  extractions, three heats stretch, where the                  process parameters of dry and wet spinning are:                  the diameter of the spinneret hole is 0.45-0.55                  mm, and the extrusion rate of the spinneret hole                  is 4.5-4.8 ml/minutes, the height of the air                  layer is 10-12 cm, the temperature of the                  coagulation bath is -6&#176; C to                  -3&#176; C, the curing time of the                  coagulation bath is 1.5-2 minutes, the multiple                  of one stretch is 2.6-3 times, the temperature of                  the first extraction is 68-70&#176; C, the                  time is 6-8 hours, the temperature of the second                  extraction is 72-75&#176; C, the time is 8-10                  hours, and the temperature of the third                  extraction is 75-80&#176; C, the time is                  10-12 hours, the temperature of the first heat                  stretching is 185-195&#176; C, the stretching                  ratio is 2-2.5 times, the temperature of the                  second heat stretching is 195-205&#176; C,                  the stretching ratio is 2.5-3 times, the                  temperature of the third heat stretching                  205-210&#176; C, stretching ratio 3-3.5                  times. The heating in stages is as follows:                  heating to 35-40&#176; C from room                  temperature and maintaining for 10-20 minutes,                  heating to 45-50&#176; C and maintaining for                  20-30 minutes. The degree of polymerization of                  polyvinyl alcohol is 3200-3500, and the degree of                  alcoholysis is 98-99%, the pressure formed after                  carbon dioxide gas is passing into the dissolving                  kettle is 1.2-1.6 MPa, after completely                  dissolving polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane and before                  discharging carbon dioxide gas, placing                  dissolving kettle at 50-55&#176; C for 50-80                  minutes, and the carbon dioxide gas discharge                  rate is 18-22 l/minutes. The solid content of the                  polyvinyl alcohol spinning solution is 26-30                  wt.%, the viscosity of the spinning solution when                  the polyvinyl alcohol/dodecane is completely                  dissolving and stably placing is 1800-2000 Pa.s,                  and the viscosity of the spinning solution after                  completely discharging carbon dioxide gas is                  2000-2200 Pa.s. The mass ratio of                  dimethylformamide to water is 7-8:2-3. The mass                  ratio of polyvinyl alcohol and n-dodecanoic acid                  is 1:0.01-0.05, the concentration of sulfuric                  acid is 1-2.5 wt.% of the total amount of                  polyvinyl alcohol, n-dodecanoic acid,                  dimethylformamide and water, and the                  concentration of sulfuric acid is 96-98 wt.%, the                  temperature of the esterification reaction is                  80-90&#176; C, and the reaction time is 6-8                  hours. The synthesis of sodium polyvinyl                  alcoholate with polyvinyl alcohol and sodium                  hydroxide solution as raw materials is carrying                  out under a nitrogen atmosphere, the reaction                  temperature is 120-130&#176; C, and the                  reaction time is 5-6 hours, the concentration of                  the NaOH solution is 16-20 wt.%, and the mass                  ratio of polyvinyl alcohol to sodium hydroxide is                  1-2:25-35, the temperature of adding iododecane                  and dibutyl sulfate to the sodium polyvinyl                  alcoholate for substitution reaction is                  70-80&#176; C, and the time is 4-6 hours, the                  molar ratio of sodium polyvinyl alcoholate to                  iododecane is 1-2:15-20, and the amount of                  dibutyl sulfate is 1.5-2.5 wt.% sodium polyvinyl                  alcoholate and iododecane. The fiber-reinforced                  concrete is soaking in water, and the retention                  rates of breaking strength after 20 days, 50 days                  and 80 days are 99.2-99.8%, 98.2-99.2% and                  96.2-98.2%, respectively.

PT P
PN CN113070068-A
TI Preparing indium oxide-copper oxide photocatalyst by stirring indium chloride tetrahydrate and trisodium citrate dihydrate in water for hydrothermal reaction, centrifuging, annealing, dissolving hexamethylenetetramine in water, heating with indium oxide and copper nitrate trihydrate, and reacting.
AU NIU L
   HAN D
   CHEN K
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 202187054G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of indium oxide-copper oxide photocatalyst, involves (a) adding indium chloride tetrahydrate and trisodium citrate dihydrate to water and stirring, then adjusting the pH of the solution to 6.4-6.6, subjecting the obtained solution to a hydrothermal reaction, after reacting, naturally cooling it to room temperature, collecting the final product by centrifuging the mixture, washing and drying, annealing, and then naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain spherical indium oxide, and (b) dissolving hexamethylenetetramine in water to obtain solution (A), adding the indium oxide and copper nitrate trihydrate prepared in step (a) to water to obtain solution (B), after stirring the solution (B) for a period of time, dripping the solution (A) to solution (B), heating the mixed solution at 70 degrees C for 3 hours, after reacting, naturally cooling the system to room temperature, collecting the final product by centrifugation, washing and drying to obtain the photocatalyst.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of indium oxide-copper oxide photocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction for preparing carbon monoxide and methanol (all claimed). The photocatalyst is used as a photocatalyst for photocatalytic fuel production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method supports the indium oxide nanospheres on the copper oxide nanoflowers through a simple wet chemical preparation method. This unique structure makes the composite catalyst have significant advantages such as large specific surface area, many exposed adsorption sites, small photo-generated electron migration distance, and improved separation and migration performance. The prepared photocatalyst has excellent carbon monoxide generation rate than pure indium oxide, and further converts carbon dioxide into methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing carbon monoxide and methanol by reducing carbon dioxide, involving adding the indium oxide-copper oxide photocatalyst and the sodium sulfite solution to the reactor, and then introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor under a partial pressure of 0.4 atm, and using a xenon lamp as a light source to perform photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to obtain carbon monoxide and methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction in step (a) is a reaction at 130 degrees C for 24 hours. In step (a), the stirring is performed for 30 minutes, and the pH value of the solution is adjusted with aqueous ammonia. The washing in step (a) refers to washing with water and ethanol, and the drying is drying at 65 degrees C for 6 hours. In step (a), the annealing refers to annealing at 500 degrees C in air for 3 hours. The stirring time in step (b) is 30 minutes. The washing in step (b) refers to washing with water and ethanol, and the drying is drying at 65 degrees C for 6 hours. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the molar ratio of indium chloride tetrahydrate and trisodium citrate dihydrate is 1:1. In step (b), the molar ratio of indium oxide spheres and copper nitrate trihydrate is (1-3):(3-1), preferably 2:3, 2:1, 1:1, 3:2 or 1:2.

PN CN112442419-A
TI Preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, by wall-breaking Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, quick-freezing and thawing, crushing, extracting wall-broken spore powder in tank, filtering and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU CHEN Y
AE JIANGSU ZIBUTANG PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021259927
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, involves wall-breaking G.lucidum spore powder, and extracting broken G.lucidum spore powder to obtain G.lucidum spore oil, performing multi-time quick-freezing and thawing operation on G.lucidum spore powder, and crushing G.lucidum spore powder using roller press to obtain wall-broken G.lucidum spore powder, extracting by placing wall-broken G.lucidum spore powder in extraction tank having three extraction tank comprising first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank, connecting extraction tanks in series, sequentially washing first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank, washing, filtering and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using pure water to obtain pure G.lucidum spore oil.
   USE - Method for preparing G.lucidum spore oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts multiple water washing process, which completely separates the extract of G.lucidum spore powder from broken wall, and realizes cost-effective, high-efficiency and rapid preparation of G.lucidum spore oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing G.lucidum spore oil, involves wall-breaking G.lucidum spore powder, and extracting broken G.lucidum spore powder to obtain G.lucidum spore oil, performing multi-time quick-freezing and thawing operation on G.lucidum spore powder, and crushing G.lucidum spore powder using roller press to obtain wall-broken G.lucidum spore powder, extracting by placing wall-broken G.lucidum spore powder in extraction tank having three extraction tank comprising first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank, connecting extraction tanks in series, sequentially washing first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank, washing, filtering and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using pure water to obtain pure G.lucidum spore oil, where supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 32-35 degrees C under extraction pressure of 15-16 degrees C, separating at 30-32 degrees C under separation pressure of 6-6.5 MPa at carbon dioxide flow rate of 40-45 l/hour, and extracting for 3-4 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The wall breaking process involves performing wall breaking for more than 5 times, quick freezing G.lucidum spore powder at-20 degrees C for more than 30 minutes, thawing for 5-20 minutes, and cycling for more than 5 times. Preferred Conditions: The linear speed of front roller is 18.65 m/minute, the roller speed ratio is 1:1.27. The method involves maintaining first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank at constant temperature, and effectively dissolving the extract of G.lucidum spore powder. The method involves controlling temperature of first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank at 65-70 degrees C, 60-65 degrees C and 55-60 degrees C. The method involves equipping first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank with stirring paddles, and during washing, turning on stirring paddle for stirring pure water for accelerating dissolution of extract of G.lucidum spore powder. The stirring speeds of first extraction tank, second extraction tank and third extraction tank are 600-800 rpm, 400-600 rpm and 300-400 rpm, and stirring for 0.5-1 hour. The method involves after completing washing, filtering using filter membrane and centrifuging for completely separating solution of G.lucidum spore oil extract, and extracting the solution by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain pure G.lucidum spore oil.

PN US2020282180-A1; WO2020180483-A1
TI Tube tip detection system for detecting tube            misplacement in patient airway, has enteral tube which            is provided with tip and feedback from sensing            component is displayed on feedback display to indicate            to user of main portion.
AU ASHTAROLNAKHAI S
AE AVENT INC (AENT-C)
   AVENT INC (AENT-C)
GA 202088088R
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a enteral tube (102)                which is provided with a tip (106). A first sensing                component (108) is mounted at the tip. The feedback                from the first sensing component is displayed on                the feedback display (110) to indicate to a user of                the main portion whether the tip is misplaced in a                patient's airway. The first sensing component is a                micro-electro-mechanical system (MEMS) component.                The MEMS component comprises an infrared carbon                dioxide sensor. The infrared carbon dioxide sensor                comprises an infrared emitter and an infrared                receiver. The feedback from the infrared carbon                dioxide sensor indicates the tip is entering the                airway when the infrared carbon dioxide sensor                senses a carbon dioxide concentration of 30 mmHg                and 35 mmHg. A second sensing component is mounted                along the length at a position spaced apart from                the tip.
   USE - Tube tip detection system for detecting tube                misplacement in patient's airway.
   ADVANTAGE - The tube tip detection system provides enteral                tubes with sensing components to detect whether the                tube tip is being misplaced in the patient's airway                to avoid such misplacement. The system allows                real-time, bedside verification of the tube                placement, which can save time and money compared                to existing system. The system reduce the                occurrence of misplaced enteral tubes, such as                nasogastric or orogastric feeding tubes, thus                reducing complications from administering fluid to                a patient through a misplaced tube. The recognition                of the airway when placing an enteral tube is an                important way to prevent harm to a patient. The tip                of the tube should be placed at the proper location                within the patient's body because erroneous                placement of the tube tip injure or harm the                patient, if the tube is misplaced into the                patient's airway.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for detecting tube misplacement in patient's                airway.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                tube tip detection system for detecting tube                misplacement in patient airway.
   Tube tip detection system (100)
   Enteral tube (102)
   Tip (106)
   First sensing component (108)
   Feedback display (110)
