PT P
PN CN111444627-A
TI Processing energy saving optimization comprise e.g. establishing quality of air conditioned rooms and energy balance model, and quality of air handling unit and energy balance model, and converting output variables water and carbon dioxide.
AU YU J
   ZHANG R
   ZHAO A
   LIU Q
   XIE Y
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 202071624M
AB    NOVELTY - Processing energy-saving optimization comprise (i) establishing quality of air-conditioned rooms and energy balance model, (ii) establishing quality of air handling unit (AHU) and energy balance model, (iii) converting output variables water and carbon dioxide content of model established in steps (i) and (ii) into humidity and ppmV values, (iv) determining conveying temperature and flow restriction between room and the AHU system, dynamically restricting valve and restricting temperature, humidity and air quality by time variation, (v) setting reasonable comfortable area by using predicted mean vote- predicted percentage dissatisfied (PMV-PPD) index, and (vi) taking steps (i)-(v) as constraints, setting objective function of model-based comfort area optimization method, setting point tracking method, optimizing the control variables, giving temperature and temperature requiring to meet comfort requirements and minimizing energy consumption, humidity and carbon dioxide content.
   USE - The method is useful for comfort zone based on indoor quality control model (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: meets comfortability and significantly saves energy and energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing energy-saving optimization comprise (i) establishing the quality of air-conditioned rooms and energy balance model, (ii) establishing the quality of air handling unit (AHU) and energy balance model, (iii) converting the output variables water and carbon dioxide content of the model established in the steps (i) and (ii) into humidity and ppmV values, (iv) determining the conveying temperature and flow restriction between the room and the AHU system according to the external environment volume flow Fa(h), room input temperature Tin(h), room humidity R(h), and pressure P(h) between the room and the AHU system, dynamically restricting the valve and restricting the temperature, humidity and air quality by time variation, (v) setting a reasonable comfortable area by using predicted mean vote- predicted percentage dissatisfied (PMV-PPD) index, and (vi) taking the steps (i)-(v) as constraints, setting the objective function of the model-based comfort area optimization method, setting point tracking method, optimizing the control variables, giving the temperature and temperature requiring to meet comfort requirements and minimizing energy consumption, humidity and carbon dioxide content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the quality dynamic model of the air-conditioned room is established according to the formula as given in the specification, where the V is the total volume of the building area, the concentration of i component Cin in the Ciz room is the concentration of i in the inlet air, gi is the component generation rate of each person, Fin is the inlet volume flow rate, Fo is the outlet volume flow, ph is the number of personnel, i=1 means water, and i=2 means carbon dioxide. The energy dynamic model of the air-conditioned room is established according to the formula as given in the specification, where the c is the heat capacity of air under standard conditions, T is the room temperature, w is the heat transfer coefficient of the wall, m is the total mass of the building area, H is time, rho is the air density under standard conditions, Fin is the inlet volume flow, Fo is the outlet volume flow, A is the wall area, Ta is the ambient temperature, q is the heat gain per capita in the building, p is the occupancy of the room, p=1 means the space is occupied, p=0 means the space is not occupied, and ph is the number of personnel. In the step (ii), the mass dynamic model of AHU is established according to the formula Fo(h)+Fa(h)=Fe(h)+Fin(h)mim(h)=Fin(h)Cin(h)+Fe(h )Ciz(h)-Fa(h)Cia(h)-Fo(h)Ciz(h), where the mim is the mass removal rate in AHU, the carbon dioxide mass removal rate when i=2, Ciz is the concentration of i in the indoor space and Cia is the concentration of i in the outside air. The energy dynamic model of the AHU is established according to the formula Ql(h)=mimCQs(h)=Fin(h) rho cTin(h)- rho c(Fo(h)T(h)+Fa(h)Ta(h)-Fe(h)T(h))Q= Ql+Qs where the Ql is the increasing/decreasing latent heat in AHU, C is the latent heat of condensation, Qs is sensible heat, mim is the mass removal rate in AHU, The carbon dioxide mass removal rate when i=2, Q is the total energy, Fin is the inlet volume flow rate, rho is the air density under standard conditions, c is the air heat capacity under standard conditions, Tin enters the temperature of the room, Fo is the outlet volume flow rate, T is the room temperature, Ta is the ambient temperature, Fa is the volume flow of the external environment, and Fe is the exhaust air volume flow. In the step (iii), the water content is converted into the humidity equation as the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the air is converted into a ppmV value according to the formula as given in the specification, where the C1z is the concentration of water in the indoor space, C1s is the saturation concentration of water, MC is the molecular weight of carbon dioxide, C2z is the concentration of indoor carbon dioxide, P is the internal pressure of the building system, and Ru is the universal gas constant. In the step (iv), the temperature conveying and flow rate restriction of the AHU system is determined according to the formula Tin,L less than or equal to Tin(h) less than or equal to Tin,HFo,L less than or equal to Fo(h) less than or equal to Fo,HFin,L less than or equal to Fin(h) less than or equal to Fin,HFe,L less than or equal to Fe(h) less than or equal to Fe,HFin,L less than or equal to Fa(h) less than or equal to Fin,H, where the Tin,L is the lowest value of the room input temperature, Tin, H is the highest value of the room input temperature, Tin(h) entering the temperature of the room, Fo, L is the lowest value of the volume flow in the external environment, Fo, H is the highest value of the volume flow of the external environment, Fo(h) is the volume flow of the external environment, Fin,L is the lowest value of the room entrance volume flow, Fin, H is the highest value of the room entrance volume flow, Fin(h) is the volume flow rate of the room entrance, Fe, L is the lowest value of exhaust air volume flow, Fe and H are the highest values of exhaust volume flow, Fe(h) is the exhaust air volume flow rate, and Fa(h) is the volume flow rate of the external environment. The dynamically restricting of the valve according to the formula as given in the specification, where the Delta Fin, H is the change in volume flow rate at the entrance of the room, Delta Fo,H is the change in volume flow rate of the room outlet, Delta Fa, H is the change in volume flow of the external environment, Delta Fe, H is the highest value of exhaust air volume flow, Fin(h) is the volume flow rate of the room entrance, Fo(h) is the volume flow at the room outlet, Fa(h) is the volume flow of the external environment, and Fe(h) is the exhaust air volume flow rate. The temperature, humidity and air quality adopt time varying restricting is calculating according to the formula TLp(h)+(1-p(h))Ts,L less than or equal to T(h) less than or equal to THp(h)+(1-p(h))Ts,HRLp(h)+(1-p(h)) Rs,L less than or equal to R(h) less than or equal to RHp(h)+(1-p(h))Rs,HppmV2(h) less than or equal to ppmV2,Hp(h)+(1-p(h))ppmVs,2, where the TL is the lowest value of indoor temperature, Ts, L is the lowest indoor temperature under the set point strategy, TH is the highest indoor temperature, Ts, H is the highest indoor temperature under the set point strategy, RL is the lowest value of indoor humidity, Rs, L is the lowest indoor humidity under the set point strategy, RH is the highest indoor humidity value, Rs, H is the highest indoor humidity under the set point strategy, ppmV2(h) is the indoor carbon dioxide content, ppmV2, H is the highest indoor carbon dioxide content and ppmVs,2 is the indoor carbon dioxide content under the set point strategy. In the step (vi), the objective function of the point tracking method is specifically setting according to the formula as given in the specification, where the T is the room temperature, Ts is the indoor temperature under the set point strategy, R(h) is the indoor humidity, Rs(h) is the indoor humidity under the set point strategy, P(h) is the pressure in the room, Ps(h) is the room pressure under the set point strategy, ppmV2(h) is the indoor carbon dioxide content and ppmVs,2(h) is the indoor carbon dioxide content under the set-point strategy.

PN WO2019023532-A1; US2020181782-A1
TI Electrochemical-reductive carboxylation of unsaturated organic substrate for producing dicarboxylic organic product, involves electrochemically reducing organic substrate in reactant medium containing carbon dioxide, with cathode and anode.
AU REDKO M
   SAFFRON C M
   JACKSON J E
AE UNIV MICHIGAN STATE (UNMS-C)
   UNIV MICHIGAN STATE (UNMS-C)
GA 201912229K
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical-reductive carboxylation of unsaturated organic substrate involves (a) providing a reactant medium comprising a water-immiscible, ionically-conductive, aprotic organic liquid, an unsaturated organic substrate reactant, and a carbon dioxide reactant, (b) providing a product medium comprising water, (c) electrochemically reducing the unsaturated organic substrate in the reactant medium with a cathode in the reactant medium and an anode in the product medium, to form a dicarboxylic organic product corresponding to the unsaturated organic substrate, and (d) recovering the dicarboxylic organic product in the product medium.
   USE - Electrochemical-reductive carboxylation of unsaturated organic substrate for producing dicarboxylic organic product e.g. naphthalenedicarboxylic acid, furandicarboxylic acid, thiophenedicarboxylic acid, pyrroledicarboxylic acid, adipic acid, suberic acid, sebacic acid, 1,12-dodecanedioic acid, and phthalic acid (all claimed) e.g. terephthalic acid used as starting material for synthesizing polyethylene terephthalate used for producing polyester fibers and carbonated beverage bottles. The adipic acid is used in nylon synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical since it uses renewable feedstock, gaseous carbon dioxide and renewable electricity, and efficiently provides dicarboxylic organic product with high yield and high selectivity. The carbon dioxide gas formed in the product medium can be transferred back to the reactant medium, for example, as dispersed bubbles buoyantly traveling from the product medium to the reactant medium when the product medium is the denser medium, and hence carbon dioxide reactant can be recycled and recovered without being lost or discharged as formic acid waste. The insoluble or only sparingly soluble ionic liquid can limit the loss and/or transport of the ionic liquid to the aqueous phase during the reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reaction system for electrochemical reductive carboxylation of unsaturated organic substrate, which comprises a reaction vessel having interior volume and defining a product region and a reactant region, in the interior volume, the cathode positioned in the reactant region, the anode positioned in the product region, an electrical power source in electrical contact with the cathode and the anode, a source of reactant medium in fluid communication with the reactant region of the reaction vessel, and a source of product medium in fluid communication with the product region of the vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cathode comprises tin, bismuth, gallium, indium, copper, silver, gold, cadmium, mercury and/or lead, and the anode comprises nickel, stainless steel and/or ruthenium-doped titania.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic liquid is chosen from ionic liquid, organic solvent comprising dissolved electrolyte, liquid polymer comprising dissolved electrolyte, and water-insoluble polymeric electrolyte. The ionic liquid comprises a cation chosen from substituted ammonium cation, substituted phosphonium ion, substituted sulfonium ion and substituted aromatic heterocyclic ring having quaternary ammonium cation(s), and a counter anion to the cation. The unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises aromatic hydrocarbon substrate, heteroaromatic hydrocarbon substrate, alkylenic hydrocarbon substrate, and/or alkylynic hydrocarbon substrate. When the unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises substituted or unsubstituted benzene, the dicarboxylic organic product comprises phthalic acid, when the unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises substituted or unsubstituted naphthalene, the dicarboxylic organic product comprises naphthalenedicarboxylic acid, when the unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises substituted or unsubstituted furan, the dicarboxylic organic product comprises furandicarboxylic acid, when the unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises substituted or unsubstituted thiophene, the dicarboxylic organic product comprises thiophenedicarboxylic acid, when the unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises substituted or unsubstituted pyrrole, the dicarboxylic organic product comprises pyrroledicarboxylic acid, and when the unsaturated organic substrate reactant comprises ethylene, acetylene and/or 1,3-butadiene, the dicarboxylic organic product comprises adipic acid, suberic acid, sebacic acid and/or 1,12-dodecanedioic acid. The reactant medium is free from water. The reactant medium and product medium further comprise supporting electrolyte. Preferred Process: The step (c) further involves forming formic reaction product, and recovering the formic reaction product in the product medium. The method further involves electrochemically oxidizing the formic reaction product in the product medium with the anode, thereby forming carbon dioxide as an oxidation product, and recovering carbon dioxide from the product medium in the reactant medium. The reactant medium and the product medium are in direct liquid-liquid contact.

TI Producing synthetic natural gas in which calorific value, involves separating biomass and solid carbon into water gas shift one after synthetic gas, converting first flow synthetic gas, gasifying and reacting synthetic gas.
AU MOON D J
   YANG E H

   NOH Y S
   PARK J I

AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2018784211
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthetic natural gas in which calorific value, involves separating biomass and solid carbon into the water gas shift one after the synthetic gas. The first flow the converted synthetic gas, gasified and reacted the synthetic gas of the first flow at methanation to manufacture synthetic natural gas, separated the converted synthetic gas to the first flow and the second flow. The carbon dioxide is separated from the above-mentioned converted synthetic gas, treated the separated carbon dioxide with the carbon material, reverse boudouard reaction is performed and carbon monoxide is manufactured. The synthetic gas of the second flow and carbon monoxide are mixed to prepare raw material. The raw material is reacted to prepare hydrocarbon compound, and hydrocarbon compound and synthetic natural gas are mixed.
   USE - Method for producing synthetic natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce synthetic natural gas which has good calorific value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a manufacturing device, which comprises separation of carbon dioxide unit which includes coal, gasification unit gasifying at least one fuel among the biomass and solid carbon and manufactures the synthetic gas, water gas shift unit, the synthetic gas separation unit which separates the converted synthetic gas according to the first flow and the second flow, and the methanation unit which is reacted the synthetic gas of the first flow at the methanation and manufactures the synthetic natural gas and separates the carbon dioxide from the separation unit and the synthetic gas which is positioned between the water gas shift unit and is converted, and the water gas shift unit supplies water to the synthetic gas and reacted, reverse bud reaction unit for supplying the carbon material to the separated carbon dioxide and reacts and manufactures the carbon monoxide, preparing raw material unit for mixing the synthetic gas of the second flow and carbon monoxide; the reaction unit which is provided with the raw material from the preparing raw material unit and reacts and manufactures the hydrocarbon compound and the mixed unit for mixing the hydrocarbon compound and synthetic natural gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The calorific value is increased, converted synthetic gas is separated to the first flow and the second flow, and the volume ratio of the second flow and the first flow separates so that 9:1-6:4. The synthetic gas separation unit separates the synthetic gas in which the volume ratio of the second flow and the first flow is 9:1-6:4. The synthetic gas of the second flow and carbon monoxide are mixed in mole ratio of 2.0-3.0. Preferred Condition: The hydrocarbon compound is liquefied petroleum gas, and is liquefied petroleum gas orders the raw material to perform Fischer-Tropsch reaction (fischer-tropsch synthesis reaction) and represented by linear formula such as (2n+1)H2 and nCO are reacted to form CnH2n and nH2O, where n is 1-20.The manufacturing method mixes the synthetic gas of the second flow and carbon monoxide H2/CO in in mole ratio of 2.0-3.0, and reacted the raw material to manufactures liquefied petroleum gas. The hydrocarbon compound is the dimethylether, and dimethyl ether reacted the raw material, which is represented by formula such as 3H2 (+ ) 3CO are reacted to form CH3OCH3 (+ ) CO2. The synthetic gas of the second flow and carbon monoxide are mixed in mole ratio of 1.0-2.0, and reacted the raw material and dimethylether. The calorific value at reaction temperature of the reverse unit or reaction is 700 to 2000 degrees C. The reaction temperature is 700 to 2000 degrees C, the reverse bud reaction unit reacts the carbon dioxide and carbon material.

PT P
PN WO2022084996-A1
TI Producing ammonia comprises contacting water, iron-containing material, and carbon dioxide source selected from carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide precursor with nitrogen in reactor to produce ammonia.
AU VUKSAN S
AE TYKHE TECH PTE LTD (TYKH-Non-standard)
   SAAR R (SAAR-Individual)
GA 2022571085
AB    NOVELTY - A process for producing ammonia comprises contacting water, an iron-containing material, and a carbon dioxide source selected from carbon dioxide and a carbon dioxide precursor with nitrogen in a reactor to produce ammonia.
   USE - The process is used for producing ammonia and for recycling of coal combustion products and in carbon dioxide capture and storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is performed in the absence of electrolysis, in the absence of irradiation in a closed reactor and absence of external energy. The process provides a cost-effective procedure for the preparation of ammonia. The ammonia has high purity, and the process is economically and environmentally beneficial. The nitrogen and carbon dioxide used as starting material are not required to have high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises contacting a mixture of water, the iron-containing material and the carbon dioxide source for a period of time followed by admixing of nitrogen and producing ammonia. The process is performed in a single step in a closed reactor. The process further comprises a step of collecting the produced ammonia. The process is performed in the absence of electrolysis, in the absence of irradiation in a closed reactor and absence of external energy. The process is performed at a temperature of 290 degrees C or less, preferably -5 to 50 degrees C. The process further comprises recycling iron waste and producing an iron oxide, iron carbonate or both. The process is a batch and continuous production process. The process is performed at a pressure of 100-350 Bar. The process further comprises adding an anti-caking agent to the reactor. The process further comprises capturing of carbon dioxide. At least one of the water, the nitrogen, the iron-containing material and the carbon dioxide source is pre-treated prior to the step of contacting. Preferred Components: The water is selected from tap water, sea water, partially purified water, deionized water, distilled water, brackish water, and waste water. The water is in a liquid state. The iron-containing material is selected from an iron metal, an iron alloy, an iron complex, an iron oxide, an iron carbonate, and their mixture, preferably iron metal. The iron-containing material comprises iron-containing coal combustion product. The iron-containing material is in the form of a powder, shavings, bars, chips, or granules. The carbon dioxide source is a carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide gas is originated from at least one of pure industrial carbon dioxide, flue gas, a carbon dioxide-producing plant, and atmospheric carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide source is dry ice. The carbon dioxide precursor is selected from carbonic acid, a carbonate, a bicarbonate, and their mixture. The anti-caking agent is selected from tricalcium phosphate, powdered cellulose, magnesium stearate, sodium ferrocyanide, potassium ferrocyanide, calcium ferrocyanide, calcium phosphate, sodium silicate, silicon dioxide, calcium silicate, magnesium trisilicate, talc powder, sodium aluminosilicate, potassium aluminum silicate, calcium aluminosilicate, bentonite, aluminum silicate, stearic acid, polydimethylsiloxane, and their mixture. The gas mixture is air. Preferred Property: The iron-containing material is in the form of a powder having an average particle size of 1.5-4.5 mu m. Preferred Composition: The nitrogen is provided as a gas mixture comprising at least 1 wt.% nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - Three hundred and twenty milliliters (320 ml) of water were mixed with 1000 g of iron (Fe, 99.9% purity, particle size: 2.5-3.5 microns) in a 1000 ml reactor at room temperature (25 degrees C). Following the mixing, 78 g of carbon dioxide and 130 g of nitrogen were added to the reactor. The reactor was kept sealed for 72 hours leading to the formation of ammonia, iron(II) oxide and iron carbonate. No external energy was supplied.

PN US2021268480-A1; WO2021178369-A1; US11426708-B2
TI Forming hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide involves            using red mud as catalyst, impregnating red mud with            potassium to create potassium promoted catalyst and            forming higher carbon number products from carbon            dioxide.
AU FADHEL B A
   GASCON J
   SHTERK G
   RAMIREZ A
   RUSSKIKH A
   ALSOLAMI B H
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 2021A1807X
AB    NOVELTY - Forming hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide                involves using red mud as catalyst, impregnating                red mud with potassium to create potassium promoted                catalyst and forming higher carbon number products                from carbon dioxide, using potassium promoted                catalyst.
   USE - Method for forming hydrocarbons from carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to form hydrocarbons from                carbon dioxide that improves catalytic                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: a method for making reverse water gas                shift/Fischer-Tropsch catalyst for forming higher                carbon number products from carbon dioxide, which                involves impregnating red mud with potassium to                form potassium promoted catalyst; and#a catalyst                for forming higher carbon number products from                carbon dioxide, which comprises red mud comprises                iron and aluminum, and impregnated potassium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The potassium                  precursor comprises potassium carbonate, or                  potassium hydroxide, or both. The catalyst                  comprises titanium. The red mud comprises                  gibbsite, perovskite, hematite, or cancrinite, or                  any combinations. The red mud comprises Saudi                  Arabian red mud. Preferred Conditions: The higher                  carbon number products comprise 2-4C olefins. The                  catalyst further comprises 2 wt.% impregnated                  potassium. Preferred Process: The method involves                  using the potassium promoted catalyst to                  facilitate reverse water-gas shift reaction                  between CO2 and hydrogen to form carbon monoxide                  (CO) and water, using the potassium promoted                  catalyst to facilitate a Fischer-Tropsch reaction                  between the CO and the hydrogen to form the                  higher carbon number products. The method                  involves using the potassium promoted catalyst to                  facilitate reaction between carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen to form the higher carbon number                  products. The method involves impregnating the                  red mud with potassium from potassium carbonate                  using an incipient wetness impregnation. The                  method involves impregnating the red mud with                  potassium precursor, evaporating liquid water                  from the impregnated red mud at elevated                  temperature of about 70?oC and drying the                  impregnated red mud, placing impregnated red mud                  in heating device, ramping temperature of the                  heating device to maximum temperature of between                  50-200?oC at rate of between 0.5?oC per minute                  and about 20?oC per minute and holding the                  temperature at the maximum temperature for about                  12 hours. The method involves mixing the red mud                  with potassium precursor in water solution. The                  method involves evaporating liquid water from the                  impregnated red mud at an elevated temperature of                  about 70?oC and drying the impregnated red mud by                  placing the impregnated red mud in a heating                  device, ramping a temperature of the heating                  device to maximum temperature of between                  50-200?oC at rate of between about 0.5-20?oC per                  minute and holding the temperature the maximum                  temperature for about 12 hours. The method                  involves impregnating the red mud with the                  potassium to form the potassium promoted catalyst                  with 2 wt.% potassium.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN JP2021095357-A
TI Producing carbon dioxide derivative by absorbing carbon dioxide in amine solution containing amine, adding olefin compound and acid catalyst, heating to produce carbamic acid ester, and heating carbamic acid ester while dealcoholizing.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202170297G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing carbon dioxide derivative involves (i) absorbing carbon dioxide in amine solution containing amine, (ii) adding olefin compound and acid catalyst to amine solution that has absorbed carbon dioxide and heating at 80 degrees C or high to produce a carbamic acid ester, and (iii) heating carbamic acid ester at 100 degrees C or high while dealcoholizing, or heating at 100 degrees C or high in presence of alcohol to produce isocyanate or carbonic acid ester.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbon dioxide derivative.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces carbon dioxide derivative from carbon dioxide with low energy, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The amine solution absorbs carbon dioxide in the presence of a non-aqueous solvent. Preferred Components: The amine comprises primary amine such ethanolamine, alkylamine, or alkylenediamine. The non-aqueous solvent is chosen from olefin compound and/or ether compound. The acid catalyst comprises solid acid such as inorganic acid such aluminum chloride, zinc chloride and boron fluoride, mineral acid such sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid, methanesulfonic acid, trifluoromethane sulfonic acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid, trifloroacetic acid, ion exchange resin, zeolite and silica-alumina. The olefin compound is chosen from ethylene, propylene, isobutene, cyclohexene, and/or styrene.

TI Decomposition of formic acid in production of            hydrogen, involves using catalyst prepared by            dispersing platinum fine particles in water-soluble            polymer polyvinylpyrrolidone and mixing with solution            of poly(diallyldimethylammonium chloride) to decompose            formic acid into hydrogen and carbon dioxide.

   KANEI M

   TOMISHIMA H

   AKEDA H

   AMAO Y
AE IIDA GROUP HOLDINGS CO LTD (IIDA-Non-standard)
   UNIV PUBLIC CORP OSAKA (UYPU-Non-standard)
GA 2022B9512W
AB    NOVELTY - Decomposition of formic acid involves using a                catalyst prepared by dispersing platinum fine                particles in a water-soluble polymer                polyvinylpyrrolidone and mixing with a solution of                cationic polymer, preferably                poly(diallyldimethylammonium chloride) to decompose                formic acid into hydrogen and carbon dioxide under                deoxidation condition of normal temperature and                normal pressure.
   USE - Decomposition of formic acid in production of                high-purity hydrogen used as fuel of                automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables decomposition of formic                acid with suppressed side reactions, and without                requiring a catalyst having a complicated                structure. The method can improve the efficiency of                hydrogen generation as compared to the existing                method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a formic                acid decomposition apparatus (10), which comprises                a storage unit (11) for storing formic acid, a                reaction unit (12) for decomposing formic acid                supplied from the storage unit into hydrogen and                carbon dioxide, and a control unit (13) for                controlling the reaction unit under deoxidation                condition of normal temperature and normal                pressure. The reaction unit comprises the                catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                structure of the formic acid decomposition                apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   10Formic acid decomposition apparatus
   11Storage unit
   12Reaction unit
   13Control unit

TI Digital-intelligent MINI farm structure, has            planting frame fixed in box body and provided with            base, upright post fixed on left and right sides of            base and planting layer that is fixed between two            upright posts.
AU KANG X
   SUN Z
   WANG W
AE CHONGQING BACHENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022B9222Q
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a planting frame fixed in a                box body. The planting frame comprises a base, an                upright post fixed on left and right sides of the                base and a planting layer that is fixed between the                two upright posts. The planting layer is fixed                along a height direction. The planting frame is                provided with multiple planting holes. A LED                lighting system comprises an illumination sensing                sensor and a set of multi channel LED lamp. The box                body is divided into a planting chamber and a                storage chamber through the planting layer. A                carbon dioxide generator increases carbon dioxide                concentration in the planting chamber. The carbon                dioxide generator is fixed in the storage                chamber.
   USE - Digital-intelligent MINI farm structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure creates a vertical water culture                system by optimizing illumination, environment and                water supply different control factors so as to                shorten plant growth period, thus increasing plant                nutrient content and adjusting effect of plant                taste. The structure reduces requirement of labor,                thus improving intelligent level and real-time                analyzing plant growth state by artificial                intelligence image identification of a video remote                monitoring system to adjust and control in time to                reach better growth state.

PN CN111691060-A
TI Preparing high polymer fibers, by transporting            polymer and plasticizer to supercritical kettle,            introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, stabilizing            to form spinning fluid, and performing phase separation            to transform into cured polymers.
AU ZHU M
   XIANG H
   WANG Q
   PAN W
   ZHOU J
   HU Z
   YU S
   YANG L
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2020953454
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high polymer fibers based                on the instantaneous pressure-releasing spinning                method, involves sequentially (a) transporting the                polymer and plasticizer to the supercritical                kettle, and introducing supercritical carbon                dioxide under stirring to form a supercritical                spinning fluid after stabilizing, and (b)                undergoing the supercritical spinning fluid to                rapid phase separation from the supercritical state                under a pressure difference of 100-200 times, in an                open atmosphere where the supercritical spinning                fluid is instantaneously released at a speed of                200-300 m/second through a spinning nozzle, and                transforming into carbon dioxide gas, plasticizing                additives and cured high polymers, and forming the                cured high polymer into polymer fibers under the                action of the plasticizer replacement and the                instantaneous pressure relief draft in the above                process.
   USE - The method is used for preparing high polymer                fibers based on the instantaneous                pressure-releasing spinning method, where the                polymer fiber is used for preparing polymer                non-woven fabric (all claimed) preparation which is                suitable for limited-time medical protective                clothing application.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method obtains higher spinning                pressure difference and spinning speed than the                flash evaporation method, and avoids the recovery                and treatment of organic solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a high                polymer fiber is made by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The plasticizer                  includes composite additives of dioxane,                  cyclohexane, dichloromethane, isopropanol, and/or                  ethylene glycol monomethyl ether.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The temperature                  environment of the supercritical kettle is                  110-190&#176; C. The pressure of the                  supercritical fluid is 8-18 MPa. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of the high polymer                  and the plasticizing additive is (70-95):(5-30).                  Preferred Components: The high polymer is                  polyolefin, polyester or polyamide. The                  polyolefin is one of polypropylene and                  polyethylene. The polyester is one of                  polyethylene terephthalate, polytrimethylene                  terephthalate, polybutylene terephthalate,                  polylactic acid, polyhydroxy fatty acid ester,                  polyglycolic acid, or polybutylene succinate. The                  polyamide is one of polyamide 6, polyamide 66,                  polyamide 56, and polyamide 1010. Preferred                  Properties: The diameter of the polymer fiber is                  0.5-5 &#956; m.

PN CN111545209-A
TI Synthesis of copper/zinc oxide catalyst based on            hard template copper oxide nanosheet used e.g. for            carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation to prepare            methanol, by mixing hard template copper oxide            nanosheet with deionized water and heating.
AU YAO J
   ZHANG Z
   ZHANG X
   JIA N
AE ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020819653
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of copper/zinc oxide catalyst based                on hard template copper oxide nanosheet involves                mixing hard template copper oxide nanosheet with                deionized water at mass ratio of 1:1000-1:2000,                stirring for 2-5 hours at speed of 30-60                revolutions/minute (rpm), fully dispersing hard                template copper oxide nanosheet in deionized water,                heating solution to 50-80 degrees C, adding zinc                chloride at speed of 30-60 rpm, completely                dissolving in solution, stirring for 2-5 hours,                gradually changing solution color from black to                gray, separating and precipitating by filtering,                cleaning to remove un-reacted zinc ion using                excessive deionized water, washing remaining gray                sample using deionized water, vacuum drying and                crushing.
   USE - The method is used for synthesizing                copper/zinc oxide catalyst based on hard template                copper oxide nanosheet for carbon dioxide catalytic                hydrogenation to prepare methanol and development                and application of green environment-friendly                energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic activity                and stability, uses safe and easy to obtain raw                material medicine, and exhibits higher catalytic                reaction activity at 425 degrees C, carbon dioxide                conversion rate reaching to greater than or equal                to 83%, carbon monoxide yield reaching to greater                than or equal to 31% and methanol yield to greater                than or equal to 52%.

TI Producing mesoporous silica foam useful for            producing carbon dioxide adsorbent and rare earth metal            ion recovery agent, comprises extracting sodium            silicate solution from thermal power plant fly ash, and            preparing mesoporous silica foam.
AU AHN W
   ZHANGSIQIAN
   YI Y R
   YU G S
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 202015124A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing mesoporous silica foam, comprises                (a) extracting sodium silicate solution from                thermal power plant fly ash, and (b) preparing                mesoporous silica foam from sodium silicate                solution, triblock copolymer (P123) of                poly(ethylene glycol)-block-poly(propylene                glycol)-block-poly(ethylene glycol),                1,3,5-trimethylbenzene and inorganic acid.
   USE - The mesoporous silica foam is useful for                producing carbon dioxide adsorbent and rare earth                metal ion recovery agent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mesoporous silica foam has excellent                surface area, and provides carbon oxide adsorbent                and a rare earth metal ion recovery agent having                excellent recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing carbon dioxide adsorbent,                comprising (i) producing mesoporous silica foam,                and (ii) dispersing the prepared mesoporous silica                foam in an organic solvent containing                polyethyleneimine and evaporating the solvent;                and
   (2) producing rare earth metal ion recovery                agent, comprising (1) producing mesoporous silica                foam, (2) dispersing the prepared mesoporous silica                foam in organic solvent containing                (3-aminopropyl)triethoxy silane (APTES) and                evaporating the solvent to prepare a mesoporous                silica foam impregnated with APTES, and (3)                dispersing mesoporous silica foam impregnated with                APTES in ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid anhydride                under organic solvent to prepare mesoporous silica                foam impregnated with EDTA.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic acid                  includes hydrochloric acid, bromic acid, sulfuric                  acid and/or nitric acid. The amount of inorganic                  acid is 0.2-0.65 pts. wt. based on 1 pts. wt.                  sodium silicate solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  mixing fly ash with hydrochloric acid in weight                  ratio of 1:1-1:5, mixing and fusing the                  acid-treated fly ash with inorganic base                  including sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  and lithium hydroxide in weight ratio of 1:1-1:5                  at 500-700 degrees C, and separating the                  supernatant solution containing sodium silicate                  by mixing the fly ash fused with the inorganic                  base with 3-5 times excess amount of water and                  stirring at room temperature. Preferred                  Components: The amount of 1,3,5-trimethylbenzene                  is 0.07-0.3 pts. wt. based on 1 pts. wt. sodium                  silicate solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The amount of                  poly(ethylene glycol)-block-poly(propylene                  glycol)-block-poly(ethylene glycol) is 0.05-0.2                  pts. wt. based on 1 pts. wt. sodium silicate                  solution.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - A Sevelamer carbonate is prepared by (a) contacting an aqueous alkaline solution of polyallylamine hydrochloride with epichlorohydrin at pH greater than or equal to 12; (b) maintaining the reaction mixture under stirring until the formation of gel; (c) optionally washing the gel with greater than or equal to 1 of water miscible solvent(s) and water; (d) suspending the obtained gel in water; (e) treating the suspension with carbon dioxide in water to obtain Sevelamer carbonate; and (f) optionally washing the Sevelamer carbonate with greater than or equal to 1 of water miscible solvent(s) and water.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The aqueous alkaline solution of polyallylamine hydrochloride is prepared by dissolving the polyallylamine hydrochloride in water to obtain aqueous solution of polyallylamine hydrochloride, and treating with solution of alkali or alkaline earth metal hydroxides. Preferred Components: The alkali or alkaline earth metal hydroxides is selected from sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, and magnesium hydroxide. Preferred Parameters: The pH is 12-14, preferably 13-14. Reaction mixture in step (b) is maintained for 30 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water miscible solvent is selected from 1-4 carbon-containing (1-4C) alcohol, dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide, and acetonitrile. Water miscible solvent in step (c) comprises isopropanol. Water miscible solvent in step (f) comprises methanol.

PT P
PN CN109630078-A; CN109630078-B
TI Method for designing process parameter for generating carbon dioxide in layer used in reservoir water flooding development, involves establishing calculation model for injection pressure of gas generating agent and gas releasing agent.
AU WANG T
   XU J
   LI X
   LI J
   FENG Q
   ZHENG Y
   FENG X
   HUANG Z
AE CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL CORP (CNOO-C)
   CHINA OILFIELD SERVICES LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 2019385303
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves simplifying the actual oil layer into a multi-oil layer geological model containing a chasing layer and a plugging layer. The dosage of the gas-producing agent and the gas-releasing agent is dynamically divided, and the difference in the water absorption index of each small layer after the regulation and control is analyzed. The calculation model for the amount of gas injector injection, the amount of gassing agent injected, the time of injection of the gas injector, the injection time of the gas release agent, and the number of injected plugs are established when the coefficient of variation of the water absorption index is minimized. The calculation model for the injection pressure of the gas generating agent and the gas releasing agent is established.
   USE - Method for designing process parameter for generating carbon dioxide in layer used in reservoir water flooding development (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The injection amount of agent and the calculation of number of injection slugs can be achieved. The real-time predictive calculation of injection pressure during construction can be achieved. The method is more compatible and more scientific.

PN EA31411-B1
TI Obtaining extract from vegetable mass of hemp used to reduce nausea, involves e.g. extracting aerial part of raw material with liquefied carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions as fluid with co-solvents and separating primary extract.
AU KULMANOV SERIK D
AE DALAPHARM KAZAKHSTAN PHARM CO LLP (DALA-Non-standard)
GA 2019231707
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining an extract from the vegetable mass of hemp, comprises: extracting the aerial part of the raw material with liquefied carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions as a fluid with co-solvents, at a pressure of 75-100 bar and a temperature of 30-40 degrees C to obtain a primary extract, and separating the primary extract and subjecting to dissolution and purification from ballast substances, where the co-solvent is an aqueous solution of acetone or an aqueous solution of ethyl alcohol; further concentrating the primary extract by evaporation at 30-40 degrees C in carbon dioxide under a pressure of less than 0.5 bar; and removing the ballast substances by dissolving the concentrated extract in a solvent, and separating the ballast substances, followed by distilling off the solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining an extract from the vegetable mass of hemp (claimed), where the obtained extract is useful e.g. in stimulation of appetite, to reduce nausea, vomiting, and seizures and muscle spastic spasm in multiple sclerosis and transverse paralysis, and to provide relief from pain and migraines. Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the extract with improved yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide and co-solvents are passed through vegetable raw materials at a speed of 80-100 g/minute. The aqueous solution of acetone is used at 10-50 vol.%. The aqueous solution of ethyl alcohol is used at 10-50 vol.%. The acetone is used as a solvent in the purification of the concentrated extract from the ballast substances. The concentrated extract after separation of the ballast substances is additionally dried at 30-40 degrees C, in carbon dioxide under a pressure of less than 0.5 bar.
   ACTIVITY - Antimigraine; Analgesic; Anticonvulsant; Antiemetic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN108579719-A
TI Preparing nano cerium-zirconium solid solution composite oxide comprises preparing reaction solution by cerium salt and zirconium salt, adding precipitator for hydro-thermal reaction, and calcining the hydrothermal product.
AU HE H
   ZHANG Y
   LIANG Z
   SHAN W
   WANG M
AE NINGBO URBAN ENVIRONMENT OBSERVATION (NING-Non-standard)
   INST URBAN ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (CUBN-C)
GA 2018797946
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano cerium-zirconium solid solution composite oxide comprises (i) preparing reaction solution by cerium salt and zirconium salt, and adding precipitator for hydro-thermal reaction, and (ii) calcining the hydrothermal product to obtain nano cerium-zirconium solid solution composite oxide.
   USE - The catalyst used for equivalent combustion of natural gas vehicle tail gas or simulated flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite oxide comprised catalyst can improve the catalytic performance, conversion of methane under high carbon dioxide conditions, and high nitric oxide and carbon monoxide conversion efficiency in equivalent combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalyst comprising the nano cerium-zirconium solid solution composite oxide as the carrier and active component loaded on the carrier, the active component is noble metal, preferably palladium, the loading amount of the active component is 0.5-2%, preferably 1.5%; and
   (2) use of catalyst for equivalent combustion of natural gas vehicle tail gas or simulated flue gas, which mainly comprises methane, nitric oxide, carbon monoxide, water and carbon dioxide, where the equivalent ratio oxygen, the amount of water and carbon dioxide is 10% by weight, methane concentration is 700 ppm, nitric oxide concentration is 700 ppm, and carbon monoxide concentration is 4000 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises after the hydrothermal reaction in the step (i), calcining in step (ii) is carried out before step (i): washing and drying the hydrothermal product, preferably, step (i) drying temperature is 60 degrees C. In the step (i) cerium salt and zirconium salt is cerous nitrate; the precipitator is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or barium hydroxide; the molar ratio of the total molar amount of the precipitator of cerium and zirconium is 1:35-1:70. In the step (i), the hydrothermal reaction temperature is 90-180 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C; the hydro-thermal reaction time is 10-24 hours, preferably 12 hours. In the step (ii) the calcining temperature is 450-600 degrees C, preferably 550 degrees C; in the step (ii), the calcining time is 2-6 hours, preferably 4 hours; the calcination is carried under the air atmosphere. The method comprises using cerium nitrate and zirconium nitrate, preparing reaction solution, then adding precipitator sodium hydroxide, performing hydrothermal reaction at 100 degrees C for 12 hours, filtering the hydrothermal product, washing and drying at 60 degrees C overnight, at 550 degrees C for 4 hours, calcining to obtain nano cerium-zirconium solid solution composite oxide. Preferred Components: The cerium-zirconium solid solution contains a surface oxygen vacancies, and trivalent cerium enriched surface; the cerium-zirconium solid solution is cerium-zirconium complex represented by CexZr(1-x)O2.x = 0-1, preferably 0.9.The cerium-zirconium solid solution composite oxide is in the range of 10-70 nm.

PN US2021178324-A1
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from effluent gas            stream, involves reversibly adsorbing carbon dioxide            from effluent gas stream using adsorption material,            generating adsorption material with improved carbon            dioxide capacity and renewing adsorber.
AU WENZ G B
   MCDONALD T M
AE MOSAIC MATERIALS INC (MOSA-Non-standard)
GA 2021688896
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide from an effluent gas                stream involves contacting an effluent gas stream                with an adsorption material to reversibly adsorb                carbon dioxide from the effluent gas stream and                generating an adsorption material with improved                carbon dioxide capacity, removing a major portion                of carbon dioxide from the stream, retaining the                major portion on an amine-functionalized solid                sorbent of the adsorption material prior to                regeneration process to renew the adsorption                material. The adsorption material is an                amine-functionalized solid sorbent comprising a                diamine-appended metal-organic framework comprising                several diamine ligands, polytopic organic linkers,                and metal ions. The solid sorbent is present in the                form of a packed bed of solids chosen from                particles, granules, and agglomerates. The improved                carbon dioxide capacity is achieved by means of                pre-saturating the amine-functionalized solid                sorbent using water vapor prior to contact with the                effluent gas stream.
   USE - Method is useful for removing carbon dioxide                from effluent gas stream and gaseous                environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can rapidly and deeply capture                carbon dioxide using improved solid state sorbent                material and without the need to pretreat column                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for removal                of carbon dioxide from a gaseous environment by                means of direct air capture, which involves                exposing the adsorption material to the gaseous                environment to reversibly adsorb carbon dioxide                from the gaseous environment to generate an                adsorption material at least one improvement in                performance relating to carbon dioxide absorption,                removing a portion of carbon dioxide from the                gaseous environment, and retaining the portion of                carbon dioxide on the amine-functionalized solid                sorbent prior to regeneration process to renew the                adsorption material. The adsorption material is an                amine-functionalized solid sorbent comprising an                amine-appended metal-organic framework comprising                amine-containing ligands, polytopic organic                linkers, and metal ions. The amine-functionalized                solid sorbent is present in the form of an open bed                of solids chosen from particles, granules, and                agglomerates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diamine ligand is                  at least one chosen from ethylene diamine,                  N-methylethylenediamine, N-ethylethylenediamine,                  N,N-dimethylethylenediamine,                  N,N-diethylethylenediamine,                  di(N-methyl)ethylenediamine,                  N-isopropylethylenediamine,                  N,N-dimethyl-N-methylethylenediamine,                  di(N,N-dimethyl)ethylenediamine,                  N,N-diisopropylethylenediamine,                  2,2-dimethyl-1,3-diaminopropane, and                  1,3-diaminopentane. The polytopic organic linker                  is at least one chosen from                  4,4'-dihydroxy-(1,1'-biphenyl)-3,3'-dicarboxylic                  acid and its deprotonated mono-carboxylate form                  and its di-carboxylate form. The amine-containing                  ligand is diamine and/or triamine. The diamine is                  the diamine ligand. The triamine is at least one                  chosen from diethylenetriamine,                  N-(2-aminoethyl)-1,3-propanediamine,                  bis(3-aminopropyl)amine,                  N-(3-aminopropyl)-1,4-diaminobutane,                  triethylenetetramine,                  N,N'-bis(2-aminoethyl)-1,3-propanediamine,                  1,2-bis(3-aminopropylamino)ethane,                  N,N'-bis(3-aminopropyl)-1,3-propanediamine,                  N,N'-bis(3-aminopropyl)-1,4-diaminobutane, and                  tetraethylenepentamine.Preferred Process: The adsorption material                  for removing carbon dioxide from an effluent gas                  is pre-saturated by introducing water to the                  adsorption material in the form of water vapor                  introduced by means of injecting a neutral                  carrier gas with the water vapor to achieve a                  higher relative humidity level than the incident                  neutral carrier gas prior to injection of water,                  or the adsorption material for removing carbon                  dioxide from gaseous environment is pre-saturated                  by introducing water to the adsorption material                  in the form of liquid water, water vapor, steam,                  and/or ambient relative humidity to achieve a                  relative humidity level within the adsorption                  material that is at least equal to or greater                  than the equilibrium relative humidity level of                  the gaseous environment. The adsorption material                  is saturated by continuous introduction of water                  to the absorption material in the form of water                  vapor introduced by means of injecting the water                  vapor into the effluent gas stream prior to                  contact with the adsorption material to achieve a                  higher relative humidity level than the effluent                  gas stream prior to injection of water. The                  humidity level within the adsorption material is                  maintained at an optimum level during usage by                  continuously monitoring the relative humidity                  level of the treated effluent gas stream and                  adjusting the humidity level to the optimum level                  by changing the temperature of effluent gas                  stream, changing the temperature of the                  adsorption material, injecting water vapor to the                  effluent gas stream, removing water vapor from                  the effluent gas stream, changing velocity of the                  effluent gas stream, increasing the rate of                  carbon dioxide propagation by increasing the                  concentration of carbon dioxide, decreasing the                  rate of carbon dioxide propagation by decreasing                  the concentration of carbon dioxide, increasing                  the rate of water propagation by increasing the                  concentration of water, and/or decreasing the                  rate of water propagation by decreasing the                  concentration of water. The adsorption material                  is synthesized by grafting the diamine ligands                  onto a metal-organic framework comprising metal                  cations and polytopic organic linkers, by                  exposing an amount of the metal-organic framework                  to a solution comprising the polyamine ligand                  diluted with a solvent to form unactivated                  adsorption material. At least one improvement in                  performance relative to the activated adsorption                  material is achieved by maintaining an optimum                  level of relative humidity within the adsorption                  material during dynamic contact with the effluent                  gas stream. The adsorption material is                  regenerated in a repeatable cyclic process by                  adsorbing carbon dioxide from either an effluent                  gas stream or gaseous environment, regenerating                  the adsorbent material by applying heat to the                  adsorbent material, heating the effluent gas                  stream, heating an inert gas stream, and/or                  applying vacuum to the adsorbent material to                  release adsorbed carbon dioxide and water,                  flowing an inert gas stream containing water                  vapor over the adsorption material or contacting                  the adsorption material with water and/or water                  vapor to achieve an optimum level of relative                  humidity, and repeating the processes until                  acceptable extraction is achieved. Preferred                  Properties: The optimum level of relative                  humidity with respect to the water vapor is                  10-100% relative humidity. The optimum level of                  relative humidity with respect to water present                  on the amine-functionalized solid sorbent is 3.5                  mol% or more water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ions are at                  least one cationic metal ions chosen from                  magnesium, calcium, manganese, chromium, iron,                  cobalt, nickel, copper, and zinc.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   USE - Device for decarbonising and recycling carbon dioxide and liquefied natural gas rich in carbon dioxide gas and gas well (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device suitable for the separation and recovery of natural gas in various oil and gas wells, and the high-purity natural gas after the removal of carbon dioxide is used in the pipeline network, and at the same time, the separated carbon dioxide is liquefied and recovered or reinjected and buried to realize economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide-rich oil and gas wells natural gas decarbonization and carbon dioxide recovery and liquefaction process, which involves performing carbon dioxide removal process and carbon dioxide liquefaction process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of device for decarbonising and recycling carbon dioxide and liquefied natural gas rich in carbon dioxide gas and gas well.
   Water removal device (1.1)
   Filter D (1.10)
   Filter A (1.2)
   Compressor (1.3)
   Adsorption drying device A (1.4)
   Freeze drying device (1.5)
   Filter B (1.6)
   Activated carbon filter A (1.7)
   Filter C (1.8)
   Heater (1.9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: The steps of the carbon dioxide removal process involves entering natural gas of the oil and gas well to the water removal device along the tangent line of the water removal device. The liquid water in the natural gas of the oil and gas well is preliminarily separated. The natural gas of the oil and gas well after preliminary dewatering enters the filter A to filter out particulate impurities in the gas. The clean gas enters the compressor for pressurization, and compressed to a process pressure suitable for the work of the polymer material gas separation membrane module. The compressor is used for pressurization and directly enter the post-processing. The pressurized natural gas of the oil and gas well first enters the adsorption dryer A for deep water removal, so that the gas pressure dew point reaches -20 degrees C. The natural gas of the oil and gas well after the removal of water and heavy hydrocarbons then enters the filter B, the activated carbon filter A, the filter C, the heater, the filter D is further purified. The water, oil, solid particles and other substances remaining in the gas are removed, and heated the gas to the temperature required for gas separation in the polymer material gas separation membrane module. The gas separation and decarburization in the polymer material gas separation membrane module separates natural gas products and permeate gas with a carbon dioxide content of 2-5 wt.%. The steps of the carbon dioxide liquefaction process involves performing gas separation and decarburization in the polymer material gas separation membrane module. The carbon dioxide content in the permeate gas is greater than or equal to 80 wt.%, and the permeate gas is compressed to a suitable process pressure by the compressor. The filter E, the activated carbon filter B, the filter F, and the filter G are passed to purify and removed oil. The dew point in the gas is reduced to -40 degrees C after the deep dehydration treatment of the adsorption dryer B. The treated permeate gas containing carbon dioxide enters the refrigerator and is cooled to -35 degrees C, and then enters the gas-liquid separation device. The carbon dioxide content in the liquid carbon dioxide at the bottom of the gas-liquid separation device is greater than or equal to 96.0%, which flows into the carbon dioxide storage tank for storage. The gas at the top of the gas-liquid separation device is returned to the compressor inlet pipeline for the next step of carbon dioxide liquefaction treatment.

PN CN114046148-A
TI Method for improving water injection effect of            coal seam by using liquid carbon dioxide to crack and            increase infiltration, involves using mine geological            drilling machine or rock electric drilling construction            main drilling hole.
AU CHENG S
   ZHANG C
   LIU Y
   HE J
   WANG H
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 202229987W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing water injection effect                of coal seam by liquid carbon dioxide cracking and                increasing permeability, involves (a) using a                mining geological drilling rig or electric rock                drill to construct the main hole along the                inclination of the coal seam in the area in front                of the working face of the coal seam to be mined,                (b) using a horizontal directional drilling rig to                construct several branch boreholes, (c) laying                low-temperature water-injection steel pipes in the                main hole and branch holes, (d) constructing the                strain-crack-temperature monitoring hole along a                certain inclination angle at the midpoint of the                connecting line of each adjacent main hole, (e)                connecting the low temperature resistant water                injection steel pipe in the main borehole through                the quick connector, (f) monitoring the evolution                of coal and rock fractures through the coal-rock                strain-crack-temperature monitoring hole, and (g)                sampling the construction inspection hole.
   USE - Method for improving water injection effect of                coal layer by liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) cracking                and seepage increasing used in mine dust control                and power disaster prevention field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the coal seam                fluence and wettability, which is not only good for                improving the coal bed water injection                dust-reducing efficiency, it also can reduce the                coal layer stress concentration and effectively                relieve the coal impact tendency and coal and gas                outburst danger, it has wide applicability in the                mine dust control and power disaster prevention                field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for increasing water injection effect                of coal seam by liquid carbon dioxide cracking and                increasing permeability, involves (a) using a                mining geological drilling rig or electric rock                drill to construct the main hole along the                inclination of the coal seam in the area in front                of the working face of the coal seam to be mined,                where the drill hole length (L) is the length of                the working face (L1) to a parameter which varies                with the permeability of the coal seam and the                drilling direction (S), and S is generally taken as                20 m, for the coal seam with weak permeability, (b)                taking the main borehole as the center of the                circle, when the main borehole reaches the design                depth, and using a horizontal directional drilling                rig to construct several branch boreholes evenly                along the 360degrees direction of the coal seam, to                cover the penetration enhancement and wetting as                completely as possible area, (c) laying                low-temperature water-injection steel pipes in the                main hole and branch holes, after the drilling is                withdrawn, arranging the external outlet of the                low-temperature water-injection steel pipe with a                pressure measuring port, which is connected to an                external pressure sensor, using the device for                pressure monitoring, exposing the low                temperature-resistant water injection steel pipe to                an appropriate length from the coal wall to                facilitate subsequent pipeline connection, and                sealing the main borehole by grouting sealing                method, (d) constructing the                strain-crack-temperature monitoring hole along a                certain inclination angle at the midpoint of the                connecting line of each adjacent main hole, and                installing a special hole sealer at the orifice,                after the construction of the inclined hole is                completed, implanting the sensing optical cable                into the inclined borehole by using a polyvinyl                chloride pipe, performing full-hole grouting, after                the orifice is closed, so that the sensing optical                cable and coupling the coal rock mass as a whole,                cold-connecting after the sensing optical cable and                the communication optical cable, over heading along                the side of the roadway and leading to the main                road to be connected with Brillouin light time                domain reflection data acquisition instrument, (e)                connecting the low temperature resistant water                injection steel pipe in the main borehole through                the quick connector, arranging high pressure hose,                low temperature resistant metal hose and liquid                carbon dioxide storage tank in the return air                tunnel, transporting the liquid carbon dioxide to                the coal seam to be mined by cryogenic liquid                booster pump, connecting quick connector with a                flow meter to monitor the liquid carbon dioxide                delivery flow, setting a pressure gauge above the                storage tank, the pressure sensor and the safety                valve, monitoring and adjusting the pressure of the                liquid carbon dioxide in the storage tank,                maintaining the liquid carbon dioxide injection                pressure at a level moderately higher than the coal                seam gas pressure, and providing the transportation                pipeline with a one-way valve, to prevent the                backflow of liquid carbon dioxide due to factors                such as temperature difference and pressure                difference, (f) monitoring the evolution of coal                and rock fractures through the coal-rock                strain-crack-temperature monitoring hole,                solidifying the cement slurry in the monitoring                hole, obtaining the initial strain distribution of                each sensing optical cable through the strain                distribution of the sensing optical cable,                obtaining the analysis of the change                characteristics and the position of the breakpoint                of the optical cable for expansion and development                of the coal-rock fracture network, fracturing,                closing the booster pump and the shut-off valve,                stopping the liquid carbon dioxide injection, and                connecting the water injection pipeline to the                water injection pipeline after the coal seam, which                is naturally warmed up to carry out the dynamic and                static pressure water injection operation, and (g)                sampling the construction inspection hole around                the water injection hole to examine the moisture                increase of the coal sample, during the water                injection process, where the moisture increase of                the coal sample is 2% as the criterion for                effective wetting, and a cycle of water injection                work is completed.

PT P
PN CN113821937-A
TI Predicting carbon dioxide enhanced gas reservoir exploitation and sequectioning involves using molecular dynamics method to construct structure model of rock pore, performing structure optimization and using giant Monte Carlo method.
AU ZHOU J
AE HUANENG GROUP TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION CENT (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 202210146Q
AB    NOVELTY - Predicting carbon dioxide enhanced gas reservoir exploitation and sequectioning involves using molecular dynamics method to construct structure model of rock pore, performing structure optimization, using a giant Monte Carlo method to simulate the adsorption process of carbon dioxide and methane gas in the rock pore structure model, calculating to obtain methane single component under different pressure intensity, adsorption isotherm of carbon dioxide single component in rock pore, obtaining the methane density in the pore before and after the depressurization from the adsorption isotherm according to the initial pressure and the pressure amplitude of the reservoir, calculating according to the Peng-Robinson state equation to obtain the system pressure after injecting carbon dioxide, obtaining the residual methane density and sealing carbon dioxide density in the pore from the adsorption isotherm line.
   USE - Method for predicting carbon dioxide enhanced gas reservoir exploitation and sequectioning.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can simulate the production process composed of multiple carbon dioxide huff-puff stage, and can quantitatively predict the methane recovery rate and CO2 storage rate of each stage. Based on this, the whole exploitation path can be optimized under the precondition of considering economical efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Predicting carbon dioxide enhanced gas reservoir exploitation and sequectioning involves using molecular dynamics method to construct structure model of rock pore, performing structure optimization, using a giant Monte Carlo method to simulate the adsorption process of carbon dioxide and methane gas in the rock pore structure model, calculating to obtain methane single component under different pressure intensity, adsorption isotherm of carbon dioxide single component in rock pore, obtaining the methane density in the pore before and after the depressurization from the adsorption isotherm according to the initial pressure and the pressure amplitude of the reservoir, calculating according to the Peng-Robinson state equation to obtain the system pressure after injecting carbon dioxide, obtaining the residual methane density and sealing carbon dioxide density in the pore from the adsorption isotherm line, reducing the system, obtaining the residual methane density and sealing carbon dioxide density in the pore by adsorption isotherm, calculating methane recovery rate and carbon dioxide sealing rate, obtaining the residual methane density and carbon dioxide density, p in the pore of each stage, calculating to obtain methane recovery rate and carbon dioxide sealing rate of each stage and optimizing the gas reservoir production path according to the recovery rate of methane in each stage and the sealing rate of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The structure model of methane and carbon dioxide molecule is firstly constructed, the structure optimization is carried out, and then the simulation of the adsorption process is carried out. The adsorption process of the carbon dioxide and methane gas in the rock pore structure model is simulated by the giant Monte Carlo method, the adsorption amount of the carbon dioxide and methane gas in the rock pore space is obtained by different pressure intensity, obtaining adsorption isotherm under the same temperature and different pressure, carrying out curve fitting to the data to obtain the adsorption isotherm model. The structure model of rock pore is constructed according to the influence factor data collected in the reservoir. The influence factor in the reservoir comprises water content, salt content and pore characteristics. The adsorption data obtained by mixing the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and methane with different proportions forms multiple groups of adsorption isotherm to obtain adsorption spectrum, the methane recovery rate and the carbon dioxide storage rate in each stage are calculated according to the adsorption spectrum.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); T01 (Digital Computers)
