PN WO2020116866-A1; KR2020067563-A; EP3862640-A1; US2022010993-A1; EP3862640-A4
TI Air conditioner for creating pleasant indoor environment in room, has processor that controls carbon dioxide removing unit and oxygen generating unit to discharge filtered carbon dioxide and residue together outdoors.
AU LIM J
   KIM K
   KIM S
   PARK S
   YANG H
   LEE K
   LEE S
   LEE J
   JUNG M
   HAN J
   SOO L J
   KIM K M
   SEULKIRO K
   YANG H S
   YEONG L S
   LEE J H
   JEONG M K
   HAN J S
AE SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 202050706V
AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioner has an indoor unit (10) having a carbon dioxide removing unit that filters carbon dioxide from indoor air and supplies it indoors, and discharges the filtered carbon dioxide outdoors. An outdoor unit (20) has an oxygen generator for separating oxygen from the outdoor air introduced from the outside and supplying it to the room, and discharging the separated residue from the outdoor air. A processor controls the carbon dioxide removing unit and the oxygen generating unit to discharge the filtered carbon dioxide and the residue together outdoors. The carbon dioxide removing unit includes an adsorbent made of a zeolite, a metal organic framework (MOF), and a porous carbon material.
   USE - Air conditioner for creating pleasant indoor environment in room.
   ADVANTAGE - The indoor carbon dioxide is first removed, and the carbon dioxide and the remaining substances are discharged together while an oxygen generator is operated, thus enabling an efficient operation and facilitating pipe installation work by means of a simple pipe structure. The air conditioner creates a comfortable indoor environment by removing carbon dioxide from indoor air and supplying oxygen to the indoor room, has a simple structure and is capable of efficiently discharging residues separated from removed carbon dioxide and oxygen. The residues such as nitrogen separated from oxygen and the eliminated carbon dioxide are discharged together, and the separated oxygen from the oxygen generating unit is introduced into the room to efficiently manage indoor air. The number of related units is reduced when driving the carbon dioxide removing unit and the oxygen generating unit separately, thus overcoming the space limitation of indoor units and outdoor units. The piping structure is simple and the piping installation is convenient. The piping structure is simple and the pipe installation is convenient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control method of an air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of the carbon dioxide removal unit and the oxygen generating unit of the air conditioner. (Drawing includes non-English Language text)
   Indoor unit (10)
   Carbon dioxide removal unit (19)
   Outdoor unit (20)
   Oxygen generation unit (28)
   Ejection unit (102)

PN CN215975849-U
TI Carbon dioxide culture box with uniform gas distribution, has circulating fan that drives gas in box housing to be conveyed from bottom of ventilating pipe to top, so that carbon dioxide accumulated at bottom is uniformly distributed.
AU GUO X
   WANG X
   ZHANG L
AE GUANGZHOU MINGYUTAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022409866
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide culture box with uniform gas distribution, comprising a box body and a cabin door set on the box body; the box body and the cabin door are closed to form a cabin; the top part of the box body is provided with multiple top carbon dioxide sensors, the bottom is provided with multiple bottom carbon dioxide sensors, and the box body is vertically provided with a ventilation pipe and a circulating fan, the circulating fan respectively connected with the top carbon dioxide sensor and the bottom carbon dioxide sensor signal; the circulating fan drives the gas in the box body to be conveyed from the bottom of the ventilation pipe to the top, so that the carbon dioxide accumulated at the bottom is uniformly distributed in the box body. The utility model uses the top carbon dioxide sensor and the bottom carbon dioxide sensor, when sensing the top and bottom carbon dioxide concentration is too large, starting the circulating fan drives the gas in the box from the bottom of the ventilation pipe to the top conveying, for a period of time until the concentration difference is reduced to acceptable range. automatic control, the concentration of carbon dioxide in each part in the cabin is the same.

PN CN113713571-A; CN113713571-B
TI Enhancing desorption of carbon dioxide by            chromium-containing monoatomic fluid, comprises passing            industrial flue gas into absorption chamber containing            chromium-containing monoatomic fluid, and performing            carbon dioxide desorption under action of electric            energy, thermal energy and chemical energy.
AU ZHANG C
   WANG J
   ZHOU C
   ZHANG J
   YU Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021E8526L
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing the desorption of carbon dioxide by                chromium-containing monoatomic fluid, comprises                passing the industrial flue gas into the absorption                chamber containing chromium-containing monoatomic                fluid, absorbing carbon dioxide in the flue gas,                releasing nitrogen, passing the chromium-containing                monoatomic fluid after absorbing the flue gas into                the reactor, and performing carbon dioxide                desorption under the action of electric energy,                thermal energy and chemical energy, where carbon                dioxide-containing monoatomic fluids include                polyaniline and piperidinol. The                chromium-containing monoatomic fluid is prepared by                utilizes polyvinylidene fluoride,                dimethylformamide, polyaniline, nitroxide radical                piperidinol and monoethanolamine. The chromium                single-atom catalyst is chromium-carbon nitride                (Cr-C3N4).
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing desorption                of carbon dioxide by chromium-containing monoatomic                fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The monoatomic fluid contains chromium atoms,                which strengthens the thermoelectric effect of the                monoatomic fluid. The strong thermoelectric effect                can enhance carbon dioxide desorption, which can                greatly reduce the desorption temperature and                reduce energy consumption. The method for enhancing                carbon dioxide desorption with Cr-containing                monoatomic fluid can capture carbon dioxide with                high efficiency and low energy consumption, and has                important application value for large-scale carbon                dioxide capture in industry.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural diagram of the                method for enhancing desorption of carbon dioxide                by chromium-containing monoatomic fluid (Drawing                includes non-English language text).

TI Treatment of carbon dioxide from thermal power            plant, involves preparing aqueous dispersion containing            magnesium hydroxide and acetonitrile and contacting            with gas containing carbon dioxide to react magnesium            hydroxide and carbon dioxide.
AU OCHIAI S
AE JONQUELLE CONSULTING KK (JONQ-Non-standard)
GA 202182499W
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of carbon dioxide involves preparing                an aqueous dispersion containing magnesium                hydroxide and 10-40 wt.% acetonitrile, and                contacting the aqueous dispersion and a gas                containing carbon dioxide to react magnesium                hydroxide and carbon dioxide. The aqueous                dispersion further comprises 2-8 wt.%                hexamethylphosphoric triamide, and 30-90 wt.% of at                least one hydroxide chosen from zinc hydroxide,                sodium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide.
   USE - Treatment of carbon dioxide generated by                burning liquefied natural gas, liquefied petroleum                gas, heavy oil, gasoline, light oil and coal during                thermal power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats carbon dioxide by finely                adjusting the processing speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                aqueous dispersion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction of magnesium                  hydroxide and carbon dioxide is performed while                  stirring the aqueous dispersion. The aqueous                  dispersion is obtained by dispersing magnesium                  oxide in water to obtain a dispersion and adding                  acetonitrile, hexamethylphosphoric triamide, and                  hydroxide to the dispersion. Treatment of carbon                  dioxide further involves heating the reaction                  product of magnesium hydroxide and carbon dioxide                  in a magnesium metal to form graphene. Preferred                  Property: The magnesium oxide has an average                  particle diameter (D50) of 100 nm or less.                  Preferred Components: The gas containing carbon                  dioxide is derived from natural gas.

AU MA S
AE TIANJIN YOUAICHUANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201848593X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a fan for transmitting air into a reaction tank to react with solution in the reaction tank when an intake valve and an exhaust valve are opened and a vent valve is closed. An electric heating tube heats the solution such that the carbon dioxide is released by starting the fan, opening the ventilation valve and closing the exhaust valve, where the carbon dioxide is conveyed into the greenhouse when delivering the carbon dioxide into the greenhouse is needed. The fan is connected with a top of the greenhouse.

PT P
PN CN111809017-A; CN111809017-B
TI Increasing yield of chromium in an electric furnace involves smelting last furnace for steel, adding steel of lime into the furnace, adding carbon powder steel during the melting process to melt the scrap steel materials.
AU CHEN L
   WANG J
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020A5707K
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing yield of chromium in an electric furnace involves smelting last furnace for 10t steel, adding 18-25kg/t steel of lime into the furnace, where the scrap steel materials and alloy materials total 40-50t, adding carbon powder 40-60kg/t steel during the melting process to melt the scrap steel materials, blowing oxygen into the furnace at the same time, where the oxygen blowing amount is 10-30 m3/t steel, and the oxygen pressure is 0.8-1.2 mega Pascal. The nitrogen is used from the beginning of smelting to the 1/3 stage, and the flow rate is 0.06-0.25 Nm3/t minutes, the carbon dioxide is used in the 1/3-2/3 stage of blowing, and the flow is 0.10-0.35 Nm3/t minutes, used mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon, the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to argon is 4:1, and the flow rate is 0.2-0.45 Nm3/t minutes.
   USE - Method for increasing yield of chromium in an electric furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to increases yield of chromium in an electric furnace controls the adding amount of ferrosilicon and adding time, combines with the control of bottom blowing and improves the recovery rate of chromium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Increasing yield of chromium in an electric furnace involves smelting last furnace for 10t steel, adding 18-25kg/t steel of lime into the furnace, where the scrap steel materials and alloy materials total 40-50t, adding carbon powder 40-60kg/t steel during the melting process to melt the scrap steel materials, blowing oxygen into the furnace at the same time, where the oxygen blowing amount is 10-30 m3/t steel, and the oxygen pressure is 0.8-1.2 mega Pascal. The nitrogen is used from the beginning of smelting to the 1/3 stage, and the flow rate is 0.06-0.25 Nm3/t minutes, the carbon dioxide is used in the 1/3-2/3 stage of blowing, and the flow is 0.10-0.35 Nm3/t minutes, used mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon, the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to argon is 4:1, and the flow rate is 0.2-0.45 Nm3/t minutes, added carbon powder 1-2kg/t steel to the ladle at the stage of 2/3 to before tapping, added 1.5-2.2kg/t steel of ferrosilicon. The basicity of steel slag is controlled at 2.8-03.2, and the smelting end temperature is controlled at 1650-1680 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The amount of alloy material added is determined according to the composition of the steel type. Preferred Components: The alloy material is nickel, chromium, silicon, manganese, molybdenum, vanadium, titanium, niobium, boron or lead. The lime is an active lime containing 85-90% calcium oxide ands the scrap steel material is scrap steel with iron content of 80%. The carbon powder is carbon powder with carbon content of 95%. The ferrosilicon contains 75% silicon, 22% iron, and the rest are unavoidable impurities.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC M24-A05; M24-B02D; M24-C; M24-D07; M24-E; X25-C01; X25-Q01

PT P
PN WO2020160644-A1; AR118018-A1; AU2020218378-A1; EP3921403-A1; EP3921403-A4; US11466237-B2; US2019169555-A1
TI Preserving aroma of fermentable beverage in            fermentation apparatus involves fermenting beverage in            fermentation container, producing gaseous headspace            mixture, gaseous headspace mixture includes carbon            dioxide gas, locating aromatic fluid.
AU JONES R L
AE AROMALOC INC (AROM-Non-standard)
   AROMALOC INC (AROM-Non-standard)
   AROMALOC INC (AROM-Non-standard)
GA 202076703P
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving aroma of fermentable beverage in                fermentation apparatus involves fermenting the                beverage in fermentation container, producing                gaseous headspace mixture. The gaseous headspace                mixture includes carbon dioxide gas and aromatic                fluid in headspace is located above the beverage                contained in the fermentation container. The                gaseous headspace mixture has first concentration                of aromatic fluid. The gaseous headspace mixture is                moved into upstream portion of flow passage into                contact with feed side of rubbery membrane. The                rubbery membrane stripping portion of the aromatic                fluid from the gaseous headspace mixture,                concentration of the aromatic fluid is reduced in                the gaseous headspace mixture in the upstream                portion of the flow passage. The first portion of                the gaseous headspace mixture is discharged from                the fermentation apparatus.
   USE - Method for preserving aroma of fermentable                beverage in fermentation apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to preserve aroma of                fermentable beverage in fermentation apparatus that                improves quality of the fermentable beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preserving aroma of fermentable beverage in                fermentation apparatus involves fermenting the                beverage in fermentation container, producing                gaseous headspace mixture. The gaseous headspace                mixture includes carbon dioxide gas and aromatic                fluid in headspace is located above the beverage                contained in the fermentation container. The                gaseous headspace mixture has first concentration                of aromatic fluid. The gaseous headspace mixture is                moved into upstream portion of flow passage into                contact with feed side of rubbery membrane. The                rubbery membrane stripping portion of the aromatic                fluid from the gaseous headspace mixture,                concentration of the aromatic fluid is reduced in                the gaseous headspace mixture in the upstream                portion of the flow passage. The first portion of                the gaseous headspace mixture is discharged from                the fermentation apparatus. The first portion has                second concentration of aromatic fluid less than                the first concentration. The carbon dioxide gas is                diffused from second portion of the gaseous                headspace mixture through rubbery membrane. The                diffused carbon dioxide gas entrains aromatic fluid                at permeate side of the rubbery membrane. The                concentrated gaseous mixture is moved and comprises                diffused carbon dioxide gas and aromatic fluid                entrained at permeate side of the rubbery membrane,                through downstream portion of the flow passage out                of the flow passage and into headspace of the                fermentation container. The concentrated gaseous                mixture has third concentration of aromatic fluid                greater than first concentration. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for a fermentation apparatus for                preserving aroma of fermentable beverage, which                comprises fermentation container includes gas exit                port and gas re-entry port, locating gas re-entry                port downstream of the gas exit port, flow passage                includes upstream portion which is connecting to                gas exit port and downstream portion connecting to                gas re-entry port.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The membrane is                  silicone rubber membrane. Preferred Conditions:                  The aromatic fluid includes hydrophobic compounds                  and hydrophilic compounds, and membrane has                  greater permeability to hydrophobic compounds                  than to hydrophilic compounds. Preferred Process:                  The method involves diffusing carbon dioxide gas,                  generating pressure differential across the                  rubbery membrane. The downstream gas pressure at                  the permeate side is less than upstream gas                  pressure at the feed side, generating vacuum                  pressure of between 200-500 mbar absolute to the                  permeate side. The method involves fermenting                  beverage generates carbon dioxide gas at first                  volumetric rate, discharging carbon dioxide gas                  from fermentation apparatus at second volumetric                  rate, and second volumetric rate is substantially                  the same as the first volumetric rate, dissolving                  the portion of the aromatic fluid from the                  gaseous headspace mixture into the rubbery                  membrane. The aromatic fluid includes hydrophobic                  compounds and hydrophilic compounds, stripping                  removes 50% of the hydrophilic compounds from                  aromatic fluid of the gaseous headspace mixture                  in the upstream portion of the flow                  passage.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC A06-A00E2; A12-H; A12-W11A; D05-A04D; D05-B02; D05-E; D05-F

PN WO2020142487-A1
TI Producing methanol involves e.g. catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture in CPO reactor reacting under near-isothermal conditions, via catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction to produce syngas.
AU CHINTA S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   CHINTA S (CHIN-Individual)
GA 202061911W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol comprises: (a) reacting under near-isothermal conditions, via a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction, a CPO reactant mixture in a CPO reactor to produce syngas, where the CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons and oxygen, and the near-isothermal conditions comprise a temperature variation of less than plus minus 100 degrees C across the CPO reactor and/or a catalyst bed, and the catalyst bed comprises the CPO catalyst; (b) introducing at least a portion of the syngas to a methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream, where the methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, or hydrocarbons; and (c) separating at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream into a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where the crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water, the vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrocarbons.
   USE - The process is useful for producing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides for an on-stream factor of the methanol reactor that is greater than the on-stream factor of a methanol reactor; allows for controlling the composition of the syngas produced via CPO (e.g. by controlling CPO operational parameters), which in turn can lead to a decreased water content of the crude methanol stream; reduces the overall energy consumption in methanol production by reducing the water content in the crude methanol; and reduces the water content in the crude methanol by reducing the carbon dioxide content of the syngas i.e. introduced to the methanol reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing methanol comprises: (a) reacting under near-isothermal conditions, via a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction, a CPO reactant mixture in a CPO reactor to produce syngas, where the CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons and oxygen, the CPO reactor comprises a CPO catalyst, the syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water and unreacted hydrocarbons, and the near-isothermal conditions comprise a temperature variation of less than plus minus 100 degrees C across the CPO reactor and/or a catalyst bed, and the catalyst bed comprises the CPO catalyst; (b) introducing at least a portion of the syngas to a methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream, where the methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or hydrocarbons; and (c) separating at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream into a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where the crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water, the vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrocarbons, the crude methanol stream comprises water less than 10 wt.%, based on the total weight of the crude methanol stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The near-isothermal conditions: are provided by external heat exchange, external coolant fluid cooling, reactive cooling, liquid nitrogen cooling, cryogenic cooling, electric heating, electric arc heating, microwave heating, radiant heating, natural gas combustion, solar heating, infrared heating, and/or use of a diluent in the CPO reactant mixture, where the diluent comprises water, steam, inert gases, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide; and are provided by removal of process heat from the CPO reactor. The diluent contributes to the near-isothermal conditions by phase change and/or reactive heat exchange. The near-isothermal conditions comprise and optionally at least one CPO operational parameter comprises: a CPO feed temperature of 25-600 degrees C; a CPO effluent temperature of 600-1400 degrees C; a CPO pressure of 1-90 barg; a CPO contact time of 0.001 milliseconds to 5 second; a carbon to oxygen (C/O) molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of 0.5:1-3:1, where the C/O molar ratio refers to the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of oxygen in the reactant mixture; a steam to carbon (S/C) molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of 0.01:1 to less than 2.4:1, where the S/C molar ratio refers to the total moles of water in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture. The CPO effluent temperature is 750-1150 degrees C. The removal of heat comprises: heat removal via an endothermic dry reforming reaction between carbon dioxide and methane; and heat removal via an endothermic reforming reaction between water and methane. A portion of the hydrocarbons in the CPO reactant mixture undergo decomposition to carbon and hydrogen, where at least a portion of the carbon reacts with carbon dioxide in the CPO reactor to produce carbon monoxide, and at least a portion of the carbon reacts with water in the CPO reactor to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The process further comprises: separating at least a portion of the vapor stream into a hydrogen stream and a residual gas stream, where the hydrogen stream comprises at least a portion of the hydrogen of the vapor stream, and the residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrocarbons; and recycling at least a portion of the hydrogen stream to the methanol reactor; and separating at least a portion of the crude methanol stream in a distillation unit into a methanol stream and a water stream, where the distillation unit comprises at least one distillation columns. A CPO effluent temperature is 850-1050 degrees C. The number of distillation columns is lower when compared to the number of distillation columns in an otherwise similar process that introduces to a methanol reactor a syngas comprising carbon dioxide in greater than or equal to 5 mole%. Preferred Components: The syngas: comprises carbon dioxide is 0.1-5 mole%; is characterized by a carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide (CO/CO2) molar ratio of greater than or equal to 5; is characterized by an M ratio of 1.6-2.2, where the M ratio is a molar ratio defined as (H2-CO2)/(CO plus minus CO2); and is characterized by an M ratio that varies by less than 20% from a target M ratio under the near-isothermal conditions for a given set of reactor operating conditions, where the M ratio is a molar ratio defined as (H2-CO2)/(CO+CO2). The hydrocarbons comprise methane, natural gas, natural gas liquids, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) off gas, refinery process gases, stack gases, and/or fuel gas from fuel gas header. The reactor operating conditions comprise a CPO pressure of less than about 30 barg; and/or a carbon to oxygen (C/O) molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of less than about 2.2: 1, where the C/O molar ratio refers to the total moles of carbon of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of oxygen in the reactant mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109232917-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide indicating type color            developing nanocellulose hydrogel comprises e.g.            concentrating oxidized nanocellulose, dissolving in            ethanol solution, ultra sonicating, immersing in carbon            dioxide indicator solution.
AU LU P
   LIU R
   LIU X
   WU M
   WANG L
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 201913308V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide indicating type color                developing nanocellulose hydrogel comprises (i)                mixing nanocellulose colloid,                2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxide powder and                sodium bromide powder at room temperature and                diluting with distilled water to a solids content                of 1%, adding 0.4-4 pts. wt. sodium hypochlorite                solution with chlorine concentration of 112 g/l,                adding 10 pts. wt. absolute ethanol until the pH of                the system maintained to 10, centrifugation at high                speed, (ii) concentrating the oxidized                nanocellulose by a solvent evaporation method to an                oxidized nanocellulose suspension, dropping zinc                chloride solution to the top of the oxidized                nanocellulose suspension, soaking in deionized                water for 5 minutes to obtain a nanocellulose                hydrogel, (iii) mixing methyl red powder and bromo                thymol blue powder, dissolving the mixed powder in                ethanol solution, magnetically stirring, immersing                nanocellulose hydrogel in the CO2 indicator                solution for 10 hours, and taking out.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide indicating type color developing                nanocellulose hydrogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogel has wide application prospect in                intelligent packing field of food freshness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide indicating type color                developing nanocellulose hydrogel comprises (i)                mixing 100 pts. wt. nanocellulose colloid, 0.08                pts. wt. 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxide                (TEMPO) powder and 0.52 pts. wt. sodium bromide                powder at room temperature and diluting with                distilled water to a solids content of 1%, adding                0.4-4 pts. wt. sodium hypochlorite solution with                chlorine concentration of 112 g/l, adjusting the pH                of the system to 10 with a hydrochloric acid                solution with a concentration of 0.5 mol/l, keep                the reaction temperature at 25 degrees C,                decreasing the pH of the system during the                reaction, maintaining the pH system of 10 by                dropping 0.5 mol/l sodium solution, adding 10 pts.                wt. absolute ethanol until the pH of the system                maintained to 10, centrifugation at high speed                until the supernatant droplets plus nitric acid and                silver nitrate solution do not appear flocculent                precipitation to obtain oxidized nanocellulose,                (ii) concentrating the oxidized nanocellulose by a                solvent evaporation method to an oxidized                nanocellulose suspension having a solid content of                1-5% without stirring, dropping 2 mol/l zinc                chloride solution to the top of the oxidized                nanocellulose suspension, allowing to stand for 48                hours to form hydrogel, soaking in 3 times                deionized water for 5 minutes to obtain a                nanocellulose hydrogel, (iii) mixing methyl red                powder and bromo thymol blue powder at a mass ratio                of 3:2, dissolving the mixed powder in ethanol                solution having a volume concentration of 70%,                magnetically stirring at normal temperature for 1                hour, ultra sonicating for 10 minutes, immersing                the nanocellulose hydrogel in the carbon dioxide                indicator solution for 10 hours, and taking                out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  nanocellulose colloid is prepared by mechanical                  grinding using a raw material of bleached                  softwood fiber, bleached hardwood fiber or                  bleached bagasse fiber to obtain a mass                  concentration of 5%, nanocellulose diameter of                  2-20 nm, and a length of 0.1-100 mu m                  nanocellulose colloid.

PN CN113999725-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of walnut oil useful in walnut oil deep processing field comprises e.g. drying walnut kernels, performing primary, secondary pulverization, heating liquid carbon dioxide, pressurizing and separating walnut oil, and recycling resulting carbon dioxide gas.
AU TANG C
AE TANG C (TANG-Individual)
GA 202225970X
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of walnut oil comprises e.g. subjecting raw walnut kernels to drying, performing primary pulverization, peeling, secondary pulverization, and vibrating sieve operations to obtain walnut kernel powder raw materials, placing walnut powder into extraction kettle, heating the liquid carbon dioxide and pressurizing to reach a supercritical state through the extraction system, feeding the carbon dioxide in supercritical state into the extraction kettle to contact with the raw material of walnut kernel powder, dissolving the walnut oil component in walnut kernel powder in the supercritical fluid, feeding supercritical fluid sequentially to the separation heater, separating the walnut oil, discharging, recycling the resulting carbon dioxide gas to the extraction system through the carbon dioxide recovery subsystem, re-cooling to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of walnut oil, which is useful in walnut oil deep processing field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem that the existing extraction process walnut kernel pre-processing technique is complex and carbon dioxide gas loss is large, and resulting in high cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of walnut oil comprises subjecting raw walnut kernels to drying before extraction, performing primary pulverization, peeling, secondary pulverization, and vibrating sieve operations through the raw material pretreatment subsystem to obtain walnut kernel powder raw materials with specified mesh fineness, placing the walnut kernel powder raw material processed by the raw material pretreatment subsystem into the extraction kettle in the extraction system, during extraction, heating the liquid carbon dioxide and pressurizing to reach a supercritical state through the extraction system, and feeding the carbon dioxide in supercritical state into the extraction kettle to contact with the raw material of walnut kernel powder, dissolving the walnut oil component in the raw material of walnut kernel powder in the supercritical fluid, feeding supercritical fluid sequentially to the separation heater and the separator from the outlet of the extraction kettle to realize temperature-raising and depressurization separation, separating the walnut oil, discharging from the bottom of the separator into the purification tank, recycling the resulting carbon dioxide gas to the extraction system through the carbon dioxide recovery subsystem, re-cooling to obtain liquid carbon dioxide and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical state temperature is 50degrees Celsius, the pressure is 35 MPa. The state temperature of the separator is 50degrees Celsius, the pressure is 8 MPa. In the process, recycling the carbon dioxide sub-system the extracting kettle after finishing working, recycling the carbon dioxide gas in the extracting kettle until the recycling pressure in the extracting kettle is less than or equal to 0.8 MPa.Preferred Components: The extracting system is provided with three extracting kettles in parallel and synchronously extracted. The extracting system is connected in series with three groups of separating heater and separator for continuously separating the walnut oil.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing common linoleic acid by electrocatalytic auxiliary safflower seed oil in supercritical carbon dioxide environment comprises using homemade electrocatalytic reactor, adding nitrogen and carbon dioxide instead of the catalyst activation step in the traditional conjugate method, carrying out activation of electrochemically catalyzed conjugate catalysts, transferring the oil phase and catalyst of the cathode to another reactor through valve, adding carbon dioxide, and performing conjugate method to obtain safflower seed oil, where the safflower seed oil rich in conjugated linoleic acid is prepared by e.g. (i) filling the homemade electrochemical catalytic reactor with nitrogen and carbon dioxide, when the nitrogen pressure is 1-3 MPa and the carbon dioxide pressure is 6-10 MPa, carrying out carbon dioxide solubility in electrolyte and conductivity of electrolyte, (ii) adjusting the electrocatalytic current.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing common linoleic acid by electrocatalytic auxiliary safflower seed oil in supercritical carbon dioxide environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the reaction rate; shortens the hydrogenation reaction time; and reduces the reaction temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing common linoleic acid by electrocatalytic auxiliary safflower seed oil in supercritical carbon dioxide environment comprises using homemade electrocatalytic reactor, adding nitrogen and carbon dioxide instead of the catalyst activation step in the traditional conjugate method, carrying out activation of electrochemically catalyzed conjugate catalysts, transferring the oil phase and catalyst of the cathode to another reactor through valve, adding carbon dioxide, and performing conjugate method to obtain safflower seed oil, where the safflower seed oil rich in conjugated linoleic acid is prepared by (i) filling the homemade electrochemical catalytic reactor with nitrogen and carbon dioxide, when the nitrogen pressure is 1-3 MPa and the carbon dioxide pressure is 6-10 MPa, carrying out carbon dioxide solubility in electrolyte and conductivity of electrolyte, (ii) adjusting the electrocatalytic current (100-200 mA) under specific carbon dioxide and nitrogen pressure conditions, reacting at 60-120 degrees C for 1-3 hours, stirring at speed of 200-400 revolution/minute (rpm), and determining the optimal reaction conditions, (iii) taking safflower seed oil, carrying out electrocatalysis, conjugating under the conditions of carbon dioxide under pressure of 8 MPa at 120 degrees C, stirring at rate of 400 rpm, and reacting for 8 hours, determining conjugated linoleic acid in safflower seed oil by electrocatalytically assisted conjugate reaction, and(iv) analyzing the fatty acid component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), adjusting the nitrogen pressure to 1.5-2.5 MPa, adjusting the carbon dioxide pressure to 7-9 MPa, and studying the solubility of carbon dioxide in the electrolyte and the conductivity of the electrolyte. In the step (ii), adjusting the electrocatalytic current to 120-180 mA, and determining the content of conjugated linoleic acid in safflower seed oil. In the step (ii), adjusting the reaction temperature to 80-100 degrees C, and measuring the content of conjugated linoleic acid in the electrocatalytic safflower seed oil. In the step (ii), adjusting the stirring speed to 250-350 rpm, and measuring the content of conjugated linoleic acid in the electrocatalytic safflower seed oil. In the step (ii), adjusting the reaction time to 1.5-2.5 hours, and determining the content of conjugated linoleic acid in the electrocatalytic safflower seed oil.

PN CN114909117-A
TI Carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid system            has liquid carbon dioxide, fracturing fluid,            sand-carrying fluid and displacement fluid, the liquid            carbon dioxide is used for pretreatment of the            formation by injecting into the formation before            fracturing and creating a fracture.
AU NIU Q
   CAI W
   YU S
   XU Y
   AN Y
   WANG Z
   SHI J
AE SICHUAN ANSHUO PETROLEUM ENG TECHNOLOGY (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A95120
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid                system has liquid carbon dioxide, fracturing fluid,                sand-carrying fluid and displacement fluid. The                liquid carbon dioxide is used for pretreatment of                the formation by injecting into the formation                before fracturing and creating a fracture. The                fracturing fluid is used to inject into the                formation for fracturing and creating fractures.                The fracturing fluid includes carbon dioxide less                water fracturing fluid and filtration slug, and the                carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid and                filtration slug are used for alternate injection                into the formation. The filter slug is composed of                carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid and                proppant. The sand-carrying fluid is injected into                the fractured formation to play a supporting role;                the sand-carrying fluid is composed of carbon                dioxide (CO2) less water fracturing fluid and                proppant. The displacement fluid is used to                displace the liquid in the formation.
   USE - Carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid                system has low viscosity of liquid carbon dioxide,                limited sand-carrying capacity, poor fracturing                effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid                system has liquid carbon dioxide, fracturing fluid,                sand-carrying fluid and displacement fluid. The                liquid carbon dioxide is used for pretreatment of                the formation by injecting into the formation                before fracturing and creating a fracture. The                fracturing fluid is used to inject into the                formation for fracturing and creating fractures.                The fracturing fluid includes carbon dioxide less                water fracturing fluid and filtration slug, and the                carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid and                filtration slug are used for alternate injection                into the formation. The filter slug is composed of                carbon dioxide less water fracturing fluid and                proppant. The sand-carrying fluid is injected into                the fractured formation to play a supporting role;                the sand-carrying fluid is composed of carbon                dioxide (CO2) less water fracturing fluid and                proppant. The displacement fluid is used to                displace the liquid in the formation. The                displacement fluid is used in combination with                CO2less water fracturing fluid and water-based                fracturing fluid. The CO2less water fracturing                fluid consists of 20-25 % water-based fracturing                fluid and 75-80% liquid CO2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                supercritical CO2quasi-dry sand fracturing method,                which involves i. injecting liquid CO2into the                formation to pretreat the formation; ii.                alternately injecting CO2low-water fracturing fluid                and filtering slug into the formation to create                fractures, press open the formation, and form                artificial fractures; iii. injecting sand-carrying                liquid into the stratum after fracture-making in                step ii. for support; iv. the displacement fluid is                used to displace the liquid in the formation; v.                the CO2low-water fracturing fluid is used to                replace half of the wellbore volume, and then the                CO2 low-water fracturing fluid was stopped, and                only water-based fracturing fluid is used to                replace it in place.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The water-based                  fracturing fluid comprises 1-3 wt.% drag reducing                  agent, 0.1-0.5 wt.% anti-swelling agent, 1-3 wt.%                  carbon dioxide (CO2) thickener and the remainder                  of the water composition. The filter slug is                  composed of 95-97 wt.% CO2less water fracturing                  fluid and 3-5 wt.% proppant. The sand-carrying                  fluid includes two kinds, and the first                  sand-carrying fluid is composed of 95-97 wt.%                  CO2low-water fracturing fluid and 40-70-mesh 3-5                  wt.% proppant. The second sand-carrying fluid is                  composed of 95-97 wt.% CO2low-water fracturing                  fluid and 3-5 wt.% proppant with a particle size                  of 30-50 mesh. The top CO2 low-water fracturing                  fluid used in the displacement fluid is composed                  of 1-3 wt.% drag reducing agent, 0.1-0.5 wt.%                  anti-swelling agent and the balance of water. The                  CO2water-less fracturing fluid and the filtration                  reducing slug are added in the fracturing fluid                  with filtration reducing fibers with a mass                  percentage content of 2 wt.% relative to their                  own. The sand-carrying liquid is added with 2                  wt.% filter-reducing fibers relative to its own                  mass percentage. Preferred Method: The step iii.                  involves injecting the first sand-carrying fluid                  to support the main fractures and the main                  fracture network structure of artificial                  fractures, and then follow the second                  sand-carrying fluid to support the fracture                  openings, the first sand-carrying fluid is                  composed of 95-97 wt.% carbon dioxide low-water                  fracturing fluid and 40-70-mesh 3-5% proppant.                  The second sand-carrying fluid is composed of                  95-97 wt.% carbon dioxide low-water fracturing                  fluid and 30-50-mesh 3-5 wt.% proppant in                  particle size. The injection displacement of the                  liquid CO2, fracturing fluid, sand-carrying fluid                  and/or displacement fluid is 5-8 m3/minutes.                  Preferred Condition: The proppant has a particle                  size of 40-70 mesh in the filter plug.

PN CN113083315-A; CN113083315-B
TI Coral-shaped three-dimensional supported non-precious metal alloy catalyst useful in alcoholysis of ammonia borane for producing hydrogen, comprises carrier and active ingredients including copper, cobalt, nickel and molybdenum.
AU XIAO X
   HUANG Z
   CHEN H
   CHEN L
   HE J
   QIN H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021799081
AB    NOVELTY - Coral-shaped three-dimensional supported non-precious metal alloy catalyst comprises a carrier and active ingredients uniformly loaded on the carrier. The carrier is an activated carbon nanosphere with a size of 20-25 nm, and the active ingredient is an alloy nanoparticle composed of copper, cobalt, nickel, and molybdenum with a size of 2-3 nm. The catalyst is prepared by e.g. heating the activated carbon BP-2000 in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, cooling to obtain carbon dioxide activated carbon powder CAC, preparing metal precursor solution-A and metal precursor solution-B, transferring carbon dioxide activated carbon powder CAC and metal precursor solution-A and B into mortar to obtain mixture, uniformly coating the mixture on a carbon cloth, drying to obtain precursor carbon cloth, triggering thermal shock of the precursor carbon cloth, and decomposing metal salts of copper, cobalt, nickel and molybdenum to form alloy nano particles and scraping the powder on the carbon cloth.
   USE - The alloy catalyst is useful in alcoholysis of ammonia borane for producing hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, and uses easily available raw materials and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coral-shaped three-dimensional supported non-precious metal alloy catalyst comprises a carrier and active ingredients uniformly loaded on the carrier. The carrier is an activated carbon nanosphere with a size of 20-25 nm, and the active ingredient is an alloy nanoparticle composed of copper, cobalt, nickel, and molybdenum with a size of 2-3 nm. The non-precious metal alloy catalyst is prepared by (a) heating the activated carbon BP-2000 in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, cooling to obtain carbon dioxide activated carbon powder CAC, which is the carrier, (b) dissolving copper chloride, cobalt chloride, and nickel chloride in absolute ethanol to obtain metal precursor solution-A and dissolving sodium molybdate in deionized water to obtain metal precursor solution-B, (c) transferring carbon dioxide activated carbon powder CAC and metal precursor solution-A and B into a mortar for grinding and mixing to obtain mixture, uniformly coating the mixture on a carbon cloth and drying at 45-55 degrees C to obtain precursor carbon cloth, and (d) connecting the precursor carbon cloth to the copper electrode through an aluminum fixture, placing it in a quartz cover and externally connecting the direct current power supply and a capacitor, charging the capacitor using direct current power supply, converting the electric energy into a high temperature shock, triggering the thermal shock of the precursor carbon cloth, and decomposing metal salts of copper, cobalt, nickel and molybdenum to form alloy nano particles and scraping the powder on the carbon cloth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (b) the molar ratio of copper, cobalt, nickel and molybdenum in the active components is 1:1:1:1. The ratio of the amounts of copper chloride, cobalt chloride, and nickel chloride in the metal precursor solution-A to the volume of absolute ethanol is 3.75 mmol: 50 ml. The volume ratio of molybdenum in the metal precursor solution-B to deionized water is 1.25 mmol: 50 ml. In the step (c) the mass ratio of the carbon dioxide activated carbon powder CAC to the volume of the metal precursor solutions-A and B is 72 mg:1.5 ml: 1.5 ml. The mass ratio of the coral-shaped three-dimensional load metal alloy is 796 m2/g, and the apparent activation energy is 18.97 kJ/mol. Preferred Method: In the step (a) the heating rate of activated carbon BP-2000 is 10 degrees C/minute. In the step (c), the drying time is 0.5-1.5 hours. In the step (d) the direct current power supply is set to a pulse voltage of 20 V and the current source is set to 3.6 A.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating (S101) the molar flow rate of each component of the furnace top waste gas supplied to the removal apparatus. The molar flow rate of carbon dioxide is calculated (S102) in the adjustment furnace top waste gas discharged from the removal apparatus from the molar flow rate of carbon dioxide in the furnace top waste gas calculated, and carbon dioxide removal rate of the removal apparatus. The component and flow volume of the adjustment furnace top waste gas which are blown in into the blast furnace from the tuyere is set (S105) as gas blowing-in conditions for the blast-furnace mathematical model from the component and flow volume of the adjustment furnace top waste gas which are calculated.

The method can suppress that the inside of the blast furnace becomes low temperature, and can suppress reduction disintegration.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory diagram illustrating an object blast furnace. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Step for calculating component and flow volume of the adjustment furnace top waste gas which are discharged from the removal apparatus from an above-mentioned molar flow rate (S104)
   Step for setting component and flow volume of adjustment furnace top waste gas blown in into blast furnace from tuyere as gas blowing-in conditions for blast-furnace mathematical model (S105)

GA 202012710G
AB    NOVELTY - A facility for membrane permeation treatment                of feed gas flow containing greater than or equal                to 1 methane and carbon dioxide, comprises                compressor for compressing feed gas flow; 1st                membrane separation unit which receives a flow of                gas coming from the compressor and supply a 1st                permeate and a 1st retentate; a 2nd membrane                separation unit which receives 1st retentate and                supply a 2nd permeate and a 2nd retentate; a 3rd                membrane separation unit which receives 1st                permeate and supply a 3rd permeate and a 3rd                retentate; a 4th membrane separation unit which                receives the 3rd retentate and supply a 4th                permeate and a 4th retentate; a gas analyzer which                measures a methane (CH4) concentration of 3rd                permeate and/or of 4th permeate; and greater than                or equal to 1 heat exchanger for adjusting a                temperature of 1st retentate at an inlet of 2nd                membrane separation unit as a function of measured                CH4 concentration, where each membrane separation                unit comprises greater than or equal to 1 membrane                that is more permeable to carbon dioxide than to                methane.
   USE - A facility for membrane permeation treatment                of feed gas flow containing methane and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The facility obtains a methane stream at                constant concentration such that the equipment that                uses the biomethane has a consistent                operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for membrane                permeation treatment of feed gas flow containing                methane and carbon dioxide, which involves                compressing the feed gas flow with a compressor;                receiving a flow of gas coming from the compressor                at 1st membrane separation unit that supplies 1st                permeate and 1st retentate; receiving the 1st                retentate at 2nd membrane separation unit that                supplies a 2nd permeate and a 2nd retentate;                receiving the 1st permeate at a 3rd membrane                separation unit that supplies a 3rd permeate and a                3rd retentate; receiving the 3rd retentate at a 4th                membrane separation unit that supplies a 4th                permeate and a 4th retentate; with a gas analyzer,                measuring a methane (CH4) concentration of the 3rd                permeate, a CH4 concentration of 4th permeate, or a                CH4 concentration of a flow of gas that 3rd                permeate and/or 4th permeate; comparing the                measured CH4 concentration with a setpoint CH4                concentration and determining a difference; and                adjusting a temperature of 1st retentate at an                inlet of 2nd membrane separation unit with greater                than or equal to 1 heat exchanger as a function of                the measured CH4 concentration in such a way to                reduce the determined difference, where each                membrane separation unit comprises greater than or                equal to 1 membrane that is more permeable to                carbon dioxide than to methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Devices: The heat exchanger                  receives a secondary flow having a temperature                  higher or lower than a temperature of 1st                  retentate. Membrane separation unit uses a                  membrane with a selectivity different from the                  selectivity of the membranes of the other                  membrane separation units. Membrane separation                  unit comprises greater than or equal to 2                  membranes with a same selectivity. Membrane                  separation unit comprises greater than or equal                  to 2 membranes with different selectivities.                  Preferred Process: The fourth retentate is                  recycled to the compressor. Each of the membranes                  used in the membrane separation units has a same                  selectivity. Method includes adjusting the                  temperature of 1st retentate by heat exchange                  between a secondary flow having a temperature                  higher or lower than the temperature of the first                  retentate. Adjusting step comprises increasing or                  decreasing the temperature. Steps of comparing,                  determining and adjusting are automatically                  performed by programmable controller.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feed gas flow is                  biogas.

PN CN110746324-A
TI Decomposition process for urea production, involves feeding mixture comprising urea and free ammonia to decomposition tower, heating, distilling, separating gas-liquid mixture, and recovering mixed gas comprising ammonia, and water.
AU ZHAO S
   LIU S
   LIU C
   LEI L
   LI X
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202014109L
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature and low-pressure decomposition process for urea production, involves (i) feeding reaction mixture comprising urea, ammonium carbamate, water and free ammonia to packing section at the upper portion of the low-pressure decomposition tower and performing mass heat exchange, (ii) heating solution from upper packing section, decomposing ammonium carbamate in the solution into ammonia and carbon dioxide and distilling off free ammonia, (iii) separating gas-liquid mixture, (iv) feeding solution after gas-liquid separation into low-pressure decomposition tower, stripping lower packing by carbon dioxide from the bottom of the low-pressure decomposition tower and stripping remaining free ammonia in the solution by gas, and (v) feeding mixed gas comprising ammonia, carbon dioxide and water obtained by thermal decomposition and gas extraction flows from the top of the low-pressure decomposition tower to the low-pressure absorption system for recovery.
   USE - Low-temperature and low-pressure decomposition process for urea production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables energy-saving, low-temperature and low-pressure decomposition process for urea production with improved efficiency. The low-pressure decomposition process used in the method has low investment, and improved ammonium carbamate decomposition rate and total ammonia evaporation rate, and reduces steam consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low-temperature and low-pressure decomposition process for urea production, involves (i) feeding reaction mixture comprising urea, ammonium carbamate, water and free ammonia after decompression from medium-pressure decomposition tower to packing section at the upper portion of the low-pressure decomposition tower and performing mass heat exchange under the pressure of 0.25 MPa, (ii) heating solution from upper packing section enters from the bottom of the low-pressure decomposition heater to 130-135 degrees C, heating lower portion of the heater by desorption hydrolysis solution, heating upper portion by steam, decomposing ammonium carbamate in the solution into ammonia and carbon dioxide and distilling off free ammonia, (iii) separating gas-liquid mixture obtained by heating and decomposition by a low-pressure decomposition heater, mixing gas and desorption hydrolysis through the riser pipe and feeding to the upper packing section of the low-pressure decomposition tower for heat exchange, (iv) feeding solution after gas-liquid separation from the low-pressure decomposition heater into low-pressure decomposition tower, stripping lower packing by carbon dioxide from the bottom of the low-pressure decomposition tower and stripping remaining free ammonia in the solution by gas, and (v) feeding mixed gas comprising ammonia, carbon dioxide and water obtained by thermal decomposition and gas extraction flows from the top of the low-pressure decomposition tower to the low-pressure absorption system for recovery. The free ammonia content of the solution in the concentrated urea obtained from the bottom of the low-pressure decomposition column is less than 0.6 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Arrangement: The low-pressure decomposition tower is divided into two sections comprising an upper packing section and a lower packing section. The medium pressure decomposition tower is in communication with the upper packing section on the low pressure decomposition tower. A solution outlet is arranged on one side of the upper packing section. The solution outlet is connected to the bottom of the two-stage heating low-pressure decomposition heater through a pipeline. The outlet at the top of the two-stage heating low-pressure decomposition heater is connected to the inlet on the side of the upper packing section through a lift pipe. A desorption hydrolysis gas inlet is provided in the middle of one side of the low-pressure decomposition column, and a carbon dioxide gas stripping distributor is arranged in the lower packing section.

PN CN109321225-A; CN109321225-B
TI Carbon dioxide foaming agent system useful for high temperature and high salt reservoirs comprises gemini surfactant, hydrophobically modified nano-silica, and mineralized water.
AU MA L
   LI X
   HE Z
   TAN J
   LIU X
   WANG S
   YANG T
   XU C
   REN J
   ZHANG T
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019172643
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide foaming agent system comprises 0.01-0.30 wt.% gemini surfactant, 0.10-0.50 wt.% hydrophobically modified nano-silica, and 99.89-99.20 wt.% mineralized water, where the gemini surfactant is prepared by yellow crystal A and white solid B in the mass ratio (4-10):1.
   USE - The foaming agent system is useful for high temperature and high salt reservoirs (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces gas oil mobility ratio, improves carbon dioxide foam flooding efficiency and overcome the problem of gas enthalpy control of carbon dioxide foam flooding technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide foaming agent system suitable for high temperature and high salt oil reservoir comprising (i) placing amount of yellow crystal A and the amount of potassium carbonate in a flask or beaker, using acetone-water mixed solution having a volume ratio (1-3):1 as a solvent, stirring and dissolving, and then heating to 50-100 degrees C, dissolving, slowly adding dropwise 30 ml volume ratio (1-3):1 white solid B of acetone-water mixed solution to dissolve, stirring reaction at 50-100 degrees C for 8-15 hours, reacting, distilling solvent under reduced pressure, adding 50-200 g of ethanol, removing impurities by filtration to obtain a colorless transparent liquid, removing ethanol under reduced pressure to obtain a gemini surfactant, (ii) providing 0.01-2 g nano-silica in a flask or beaker, stirring, heating to 50-80 degrees C, maintaining at a constant temperature for 10-20 minutes, adding 0.1-50 g ethanol into flask, preparing 4.8 wt.% nano silica emulsion, stirring and dispersing for 10 minutes, adding 0.18-36 g modifier dimethyldichlorosilane at one time, slowly adding 0.1-50 g modifier auxiliary water, reacting for 18-24 hours, washing suspension with anhydrous ethanol for 3-4 times, drying to constant weight to obtain hydrophobically modified nano-silica, (iii) providing 5-15 g potassium chloride,0.001-0.5 g sodium sulfate, 0.001-0.5 g sodium bicarbonate, 15-50 sodium chloride, 15-50 g calcium chloride and 0.001-1 g magnesium chloride dissolving into 1 l deionized water, stirring for 10-60 minutes, uniformly mixing , completely dissolving to obtain 99.89-99.20 wt.% mineralized water required to formulate foaming agent system, (iv) adding 0.01-0.30 wt.% gemini surfactant into 99.89-99.20 wt.% mineralized water, stirring for 50-150 minutes, uniformly mixing, adding 0.10-0.50 wt.% prepared hydrophobically modified nano-silica, stirring for 10-60 minutes, uniformly mixing to obtain carbon dioxide foaming agent system suitable for use in high temperature and high salt reservoirs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The yellow crystal A is prepared by mixing dodecyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, chloroform and thionyl chloride in the mass ratio (1-5):(1-5):1. The white solid B is prepared by mixing ethylenediamine, potassium carbonate, a solvent and sodium 2-bromoethylsulfonate in the mass ratio 1:(1-5):(50-55):(5-10). The molecular weight of dodecyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether is 318.56-537.92. The hydrophobically modified nano-silica has a particle size of 10-100 nm, and the degree of modification ranges is 30.2-76.4%. The mineralized water has a salinity of 10000-80000 mg/l. Preferred Method: In the step (i) the yellow crystal A is prepared by taking amount of dodecyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether in a flask, adding amount of chloroform as a solvent, and heating to 35-60 degrees C, slowly dropping amount of thionyl chloride, carrying out a reaction for 1-5 hours, and distilling under reduced pressure to obtain yellow crystal A. In the step (i), the white solid B is prepared by taking amount of ethylenediamine and potassium carbonate in a flask, using 30-60 ml mixture of acetone and water in a volume ratio of (1-3):1 as a solvent, adding 30 ml volume ratio (1~3):1 ethanol-water mixture to dissolve, stirring and dissolving, raising temperature to 30-60 degrees C, slowly adding 30 ml volume ratio (1-3):1 acetone-water mixed solution, dissolving amount of sodium 2-bromoethylsulfonate, carrying out reaction at 30-60 degrees C for 5-20 hours, stopping reaction, and carrying out distillation under reduced pressure to obtain white solid B.

GA 201865836M
AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioner has an adsorption tower through which the gas to be treated flows. A heat-generating portion (20) and a heat-transmitting portion (30) are provided to the adsorption material-filled portion. The heat-transmitting portion is extended in the flow direction of the gas to be treated. The adsorption material is provided for adsorbing carbon dioxide, when the gas to be treated is contacted, and desorbing the carbon dioxide adsorbed by the adsorption material when heated.

The heat generating portion is located in the upstream in the distribution direction of the process target gas. The process target gas is circulated from the downward direction to upper direction in the adsorption material-filled portion. The heat-transmitting portion is provided with an axial portion (31) extended in the distribution direction of the process target gas in the adsorption material-filled portion. A thermal-diffusion portion (32) is extended towards the direction away from the axial portion. The heat-transmitting portion is provided with several thermal-diffusion portions along extension direction of the axial portion. The thermal-diffusion portion is located in the distance from the heat generating portion among several thermal-diffusion portions whose length in the orthogonal direction orthogonal to the distribution direction is longer than the thermal-diffusion portion located near the heat generating portion. The thermal-diffusion portion is extended in extension direction of the axial portion. The thermal-diffusion portion is arranged along the circumferential direction of the axial portion. The cooling element is extended towards the direction away from an outer wall (40). A cooling element (45) is located in the distance from the heat generating portion. The porosity of the adsorption material-filled portion is 60-90 %. The granulated material of the adsorbent is contained with cerium oxide and binder. AE ZHAN H (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201828028W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ammonia alcohol synthesis by combination of carbon black gas and coke oven gas involves preventing air from entering the coke oven exhaust gas by coke oven gas blower using sealed isolation chamber, and pressurized coke oven exhaust gas undergoes cooling and dehydration, wet removing sulfide /~100 partspermillion. The electric dust capture method to remove solid impurities in gas, pressurizing to 0.3-0.8 megaPascal, pressure swing adsorption method to remove carbon dioxide, methane and C2+ gas to form methane depleted gas and methane gas.
   USE - Preparing ammonia alcohol synthesis using combination of carbon black gas and coke oven gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ammonia alcohol synthesis by combination of carbon black gas and coke oven gas involves preventing air from entering the coke oven exhaust gas by coke oven gas blower using sealed isolation chamber, and pressurized coke oven exhaust gas undergoes cooling and dehydration, wet removing sulfide below 100 partspermillion. The electric dust capture method to remove solid impurities in gas, pressurizing to 0.3-0.8 megaPascal, pressure swing adsorption method to remove carbon dioxide, methane and C2+ gas to form methane depleted gas and methane gas. The methane-enriched gas is sent chemical raw materials or continues to purified as liquefied natural gas/compresses natural gas, methane dep gas is pressurized to 0.8 megaPascal or more, and pressure swing adsorption method continues to remove carbon monoxide to form lean carbon monoxide gas (concentration is lower than the decarbonized gas after conversion) and rich where carbon monoxide rich gas sent to carbon black exhaust gas cabinet. The carbon black exhaust gas fan is sealed by isolation chamber to prevent air from entering the carbon black exhaust gas after the pressure is applied, the carbon black exhaust gas undergoes cooling and dehydration, and wet removing sulfides to /~50 partspermillion, non-electrical dust capture method to remove solid impurities in the gas, pressurize to conversion system, and then send to the pressure swing adsorption decarburization device to remove all machine sulfides, unsaturated hydrocarbons, C2+ saturated hydrocarbons, benzene and similar gases, portion of methane where removing ratio is controlled, most of carbon dioxide, portion of nitrogen where reduction ratio of nitrogen is based on ammonia synthesis of the back section. The hydrogen-nitrogen ratio of device is required to form conversion decarbonization gas and high carbon dioxide gas, high carbon dioxide gas is sent to post-treatment device. The decomposed carbon gas is mixed with lean carbon monoxide gas to form a gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide where concentration is lower than 2% of amino alcohol synthesis gas.

PN CN113877390-A
TI Fixing carbon based on biology, involves using carbon capture process, obtaining carbon dioxide gas source, and making crop in second sealed space for second photosynthesis to obtain second tail gas.
AU LIU H
   LV X
   LIU Y
AE SPIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO LTD (CPIC-C)
GA 202211515Q
AB    NOVELTY - Fixing carbon based on biology involves using carbon capture process to obtain carbon dioxide gas source; passing through pressure control unit, where carbon dioxide concentration of carbon dioxide source is not greater than 1400 ppm; using algae in first sealed space for first photosynthesis, and discharging first tail gas; using crop in second sealed space to perform second photosynthesis to obtain second tail gas; using at least portion of carbon dioxide of first photosynthesis source is derived from second end gas or derived from carbon dioxide from source; using portion of second photosynthesis source to derive from first end gas or derived from carbon dioxide in source.
   USE - Method for fixing carbon based on biology, where carbon is carbon dioxide and carbon sequegstore, carbon dioxide stored in oil well, coal bed and deep sea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to realize grading and fixing carbon dioxide, improves biological carbon amount and solid carbon efficiency, where efficiency of method is improved for more than 10 times.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of biological-based carbon fixation method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method for fixing carbon involves using carbon dioxide flow sensor to monitor concentration of carbon dioxide in first tail gas, when concentration of carbon dioxide in first tail gas is 400-800 ppm, making first tail gas participate in second photosynthesis, when concentration of carbon dioxide in first tail gas is 800-1200ppm, making first tail gas participate in first photosynthesis again; when concentration of carbon dioxide in first tail gas or second tail gas is not less than 400 ppm then method for fixing carbon based on biology further involves concentrating first tail gas or second tail gas to obtain carbon dioxide concentrated gas and taking carbon dioxide concentrated gas as at least portion of raw material gas for preparing carbon dioxide gas source or returning carbon dioxide concentrated gas as at least portion of carbon dioxide source of first photosynthesis to first closed space and returning to second sealed space as at least portion of carbon dioxide source of second photosynthesis, when concentration of carbon dioxide in first tail gas or concentration of carbon dioxide in second tail gas is less than 400ppm, then emptying first or second tail gas; concentrating selected from adsorption-desorption method and compression-decompression method; performing first photosynthesis which is carried out by one or more reactors in series and in parallel and loaded with algae and culture solution; using carbon capture process by capturing carbon dioxide gas source from mixed gas contains carbon dioxide-containing feed gas and mixed gas, where raw material gas contains carbon dioxide is selected from air and combustion tail gas, pressure of first and second closed space are both negative pressure, difference between pressure of first closed space and atmospheric pressure of second closed space is not greater than 10 Pascal; controlling pressure of first and second closed space by pressure stabilizing exhaust device, where pressure stabilizing exhaust device comprises first pressure stabilizing exhaust device and second pressure stabilizing exhaust device, first sealed space is provided with first carbon dioxide inlet and first pressure stabilizing exhaust device set at opposite angle, second sealed space is provided with second carbon dioxide inlet and second pressure stabilizing exhaust device set at opposite angle, first pressure stabilizing exhaust device is provided with first carbon dioxide flow sensor, second pressure stabilizing exhaust device is provided with second carbon dioxide flow sensor, preferably first sealed space is set in first sealed space or first pressure stabilizing exhaust device of first sealed space is connected with second carbon dioxide inlet of second sealed space.Preferred Components: The algae is selected from natural algae or synthetic algae in first photosynthesis. The algae is selected from cyanobacteria and cyanobacteria comprises spirulina, chlorella, micronechococcus and polychlorella. The carbon dioxide gas source with different concentrations is supplied according to growth stage of algae. The first photosynthesis and second photosynthesis are carried out under action of natural light source and artificial light source and artificial light source is powered by clean energy source. The clean energy is selected from photovoltaic power generation, wind energy generation, geothermal energy generation, biomass power generation or energy obtained by hydraulic power generation. The first and second closed spaces are formed by insulating material and heat storage material. The heat storage material is cement, mortar, hydrated salt and organic alcohol. The photovoltaic device is set on top of first sealed space and second sealed space, on upper portion of first or second sealed space to sunny side surface of second sealed space and angle of photovoltaic device and horizontal direction is adjusted according to angle of light incident into first or second sealed space. The carbon capture process is selected from liquid amine adsorption method, solid film adsorption method or carbonate aqueous solution electrolysis method. The electrolysis process is staged electrolysis process.Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide gas source is obtained by using carbonate water solution electrolysis method, where voltage of electrolysis bath is 2-3 Volt, and the current density is 1000-10000 ampere (A)/m2, the pH of carbonate aqueous solution is 7 to 10, the concentration of carbonate in carbonate aqueous solution is 1 to 10 mol/L. The electrolysis process is carried out at normal pressure or 2-40 bar.

When the gas comprising carbon dioxide is supplied to the inside (bore) of the membrane, the solution comprising the metal ions is supplied to the outside (shell) of the membrane. When the gas comprising carbon dioxide is supplied to the outside (shell) of the membrane, the solution comprising the metal ions is supplied to the inside (bore) of the membrane.
   USE - System is used in mineral carbonation (claimed), and separation and conversion of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively minimizes interface resistance between gas and liquid, and maximizes transfer efficiency of carbon dioxide.

The hollow fiber membrane comprises a high-density layer and a porous layer. The porous layer is stacked on the high-density layer, or the high-density layer is stacked on the porous layer. At least one of the high-density layer and the porous layer comprises intrinsic microporous polymer. The gas comprising carbon dioxide is supplied so as to contact the porous layer, and the solution comprising metal ions is supplied so as to contact the high-density layer. The hollow fiber membrane is hydrophobic.

   YANG Z
