   USE - The method is useful for promoting microbial                hydrino conversion based on porous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous material composite bacterial agent                biofilm complex can realize the rapid conversion of                carbon dioxide and exogenous hydrogen in the biogas                into methane, so the methane concentration in the                biogas is increased to more than 98%, and increases                the methane production rate by more than 70%                compared with the conventional suspension culture                method. The method greatly improves the calorific                value of the biogas and enhances the practicability                of the biogas, reduces the emission of a large                amount of carbon dioxide, and yields good economic                and environmental benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The hydrino conversion                  compound bacterial agent is two types of                  bacterial groups in a cooperative relationship,                  namely the volatile fatty acid oxidizing bacteria                  responsible for the degradation of acetic acid,                  propionic acid, and butyric small-molecule                  volatile fatty acids in the process of                  hydrinoalkane conversion, and the volatile fatty                  acid oxidizing bacteria responsible for                  converting hydrogen and Composition of                  hydrogenotrophic methanogens that convert carbon                  dioxide to methane; where the fatty acid                  oxidizing bacteria are composed of Petrimonas and                  Geobacter; the hydrotrophic methanogens consist                  of Methanobacterium and Methanobrevibacter. The                  culture medium comprises 1-2 wt.% calcium                  carbonate, 1-3 wt.% potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 1-2 wt.% potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 1-2 wt.% ammonium chloride, 1-3 wt.%                  yeast powder, and is water (balance), pH is                  7-8.Preferred Method: The biological carrier is                  that the porous material is treated with 1-10%                  dilute acid for 12-24 hours, then washed with                  deionized water to about pH 7, keeping at                  121degrees Celsius for 30 minutes. In the method,                  the porous material after pretreatment, the                  culture medium and the concentrated solution of                  the composite bacterial agent obtained after                  centrifugation, and then controlling the inflow                  ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to be 4:1                  (vol./vol.), and the total gas flow is per liter                  working volume is feeding with 1-5 ml/minute, and                  keeping the incubation temperature at 35degrees                  Celsius, then culturing for 36-72 hours, the                  composite bacterial agent forms a microbial film                  on the surface of the porous material; where the                  porous material after pretreatment and the                  concentrated solution of the composite bacterial                  agent obtained after centrifugation account for                  10-30% of the quality of the culture medium                  respectively. The composite biofilm-porous                  material complex is transferred into hydrino                  conversion reactor (1-2 l), filled to 2/3 of the                  reactor volume, and biogas (carbon dioxide                  concentration is 2/3) is introduced from the                  bottom of the reactor 45-50%) and hydrogen gas                  mixture, the inflow ratio of hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide is controlled at 4:1 (vol./vol.), and the                  flow rate is controlled at 1-5 ml/minutes to                  carry out continuous and stable methane                  production.

PT P
PN CN113117672-A
TI Branched alkane reforming photo-thermal catalyst used in reforming synthetic gas of photo-thermal catalytic methane carbon dioxide, prepared by loading rhodium nanoparticles on titanium dioxide carrier, and reducing rhodium/titanium dioxide.
AU YUN R
   JIANG P
   GE H
   QIN N
   XU C
   MA X
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202184076Y
AB    NOVELTY - Branched alkane reforming photo-thermal catalyst, prepared by using immersion method to load rhodium nanoparticles (Rh NPs) on the titanium dioxide (TiO2) carrier, then reducing the rhodium/titanium dioxide (Rh/TiO2) obtained by calcining at different temperatures by hydrogen to obtain the defect Rh/TiO2, and obtaining the catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in reforming synthetic gas of photo-thermal catalytic methane carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adjust optical property of the catalyst; adjust and control the photo-thermal catalytic activity; is simple; has relatively mild reaction condition and social benefit; and is economical. The catalyst is built in the self-designed reaction device; reaches the purpose of high-efficiency photo-thermal catalytic branched alkane reforming synthetic gas (H2, CO) under the excitation of the full-wave band light xenon lamp; has excellent processing performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of branched alkane reforming photo-thermal catalyst in reforming synthetic gas of photo-thermal catalytic methane carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprising (i) preparing TiO2 carrier by hydrolysis reaction of isopropyl titanate: firstly, taking deionized water in a beaker, continuously stirring and soaking isopropyl titanate in ice water bath, continuously stirring, then centrifuging and separating the obtained suspension to obtain white solid, using deionized water to wash for 5 times, placing in a 80 degrees C vacuum drying oven, drying for 12 hours, grinding into powder and placing in a muffle furnace, calcining at high temperature for 30 minutes, (ii) preparing Rh/TiO2 catalyst by taking TiO2 sample prepared in the step (i), adding into a beaker, then adding rhodium metal salt solution and deionized water, magnetically stirring for 12 hours, placing the beaker in 60 degrees C water bath, stirring and drying, completely evaporating the water in the beaker, placing the beaker with a sample in a vacuum drying oven at 60 degrees C, drying for 12 hours, then scraping the completely dried sample, grinding into powder, placing in a muffle furnace to specify the temperature, calcining for 6 hours, (iii) preparing the Rh/TiO2 catalyst in the step (ii) by adding the Rh/TiO2 sample prepared in the step (ii) in the tubular furnace and raising the temperature to the appointed temperature to reduce for 2 hours in the hydrogen atmosphere; preparing to obtain the catalyst. The step (i) comprises placing isopropyl titanate and deionized water in the ice water bath at 0 degrees C and stirring for 30 minutes. The step (i) comprises high temperature calcining at 350-550 degrees C at a rate of 1 degrees C/minute. The step (ii) comprises calcining at 350-550 degrees C at a rate of 2 degrees C/minute. The step (iii) comprises reducing temperature in the hydrogen atmosphere to 350-550 degrees C at a heating rate of 10 degrees C/minute. The use method specifically comprises adding 0.2 g branched alkane reforming photothermal catalyst with filter to 60 meshes in the customized 2x 1.5x 0.1 cm of rectangular quartz three-way reactor, introducing the branched alkane gas with mol ratio of 1:1 under normal pressure and full band xenon lamp mW/cm, analysing the photo-thermal catalytic product by gas chromatography, and performing qualitative and quantitative by retention time and peak area. Preferred Components: The volume ratio of isopropyl titanate and deionized water in step (i) is 5:1-1:1. The amount of Rh-NPs in the Rh/TiO2 catalyst in step (ii) is 0.5-5 wt.%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN111519206-A; CN111519206-B
TI Copper-based composite film catalyst used in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction to prepare ethylene, comprises thin film substrate with micropores, copper film, cobalt phthalocyanine compound thin film, and carbon material layer.
AU KONG X
   KE J
   CHI M
   GENG Z
   ZENG J
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2020795652
AB    NOVELTY - Copper-based composite film catalyst comprises a thin film substrate with micropores, a copper film provided on the thin film substrate, a cobalt phthalocyanine compound thin film provided on the copper film, and a carbon material layer provided on the phthalocyanine compound film.
   USE - The copper-based composite film catalyst is used in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reactions (claimed) to prepare ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based composite film catalyst has hydrophobicity, and improves the current density and Faraday efficiency in the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare ethylene, thus improving the reaction activity and selectivity of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the copper-based composite film catalyst, comprising (a) obtaining a thin film substrate compounded with a copper film by magnetron sputtering on a thin film substrate with micropores, and (b) sequentially spraying the cobalt phthalocyanine compound and the carbon material on the film substrate compounded with the copper film to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The loading amount of the cobalt phthalocyanine compound in the copper-based composite film catalyst is 0.05-0.1 mg/cm2. The loading amount of carbon material in the copper-based composite film catalyst is 0.05-0.2 mg/cm2. Preferred Conditions: The current of the magnetron sputtering is 40-80 mA, and the time of the magnetron sputtering is 50-80 minutes. The pressure of the argon gas during the magnetron sputtering is 2x 10-3 to 3x 10-3 mbar. Preferred Components: The cobalt phthalocyanine compound film is a cobalt phthalocyanine film, and the carbon material layer is a carbon black layer. Preferred Property: The average diameter of the micropores of the thin film substrate is 1-2 mu m. The thickness of the copper film is 400-800 nm.

PN CN111514716-A; CN212881806-U; WO2021243726-A1
TI Desulfurization, denitrification, demercuration            purification of flue gas by dissolving carbon dioxide            from flue gas in water from gas phase for carbonation            reaction, and catalyzing percarbonamide in bicarbonate            of two-phase balance system.
AU LI J
   LI T
AE SHENZHEN AINUO IND CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN QINHONG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN AINUO IND CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN ENERGY CHEM IND CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020795845
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization, denitrification,                demercuration purification of flue gas involves                dissolving carbon dioxide from flue gas in water                from gas phase for carbonation reaction,                establishing gas/liquid two-phase balance system,                catalyzing percarbonamide in bicarbonate of                two-phase balance system, decomposing in aqueous                solution to form weakly alkaline absorption liquid                containing strong oxidative free radicals, and                quickly desulfurizing, denitrifying and                demercuration processing flue gas.
   USE - The method is used for desulfurizing,                denitrifying, demercuration purifying flue                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes fast flue gas integrated                desulfurization, denitration and                demercuration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desulfurization, denitrification,                demercuration purification of flue gas involves                dissolving carbon dioxide from flue gas in water                from gas phase for carbonation reaction,                establishing gas/liquid two-phase balance of                CO2-H2CO3-HCO3-system, catalyzing percarbonamide                CO(NH2)2H2O2 in bicarbonate (HCO3-) of two-phase                balance system, decomposing in aqueous solution to                form weakly alkaline absorption liquid containing                strong oxidative free radicals, and quickly                desulfurizing, denitrifying and demercuration                processing flue gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for desulfurization, denitrification,                demercuration purification device of flue                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weakly alkaline                  absorption liquid containing strong oxidative                  free radical is peroxide radical ion, hydroxyl                  free radical, carbonate anion free radical,                  hydrogen peroxide free radical, superoxide anion                  free radical, urea, hydrogen peroxide and/or                  bicarbonate radical and homogeneous mixed                  solution with pH of 7-9.

PN CN111514932-A
TI Preparation of polyionic liquid catalyst for            catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare            cyclic carbonate, by dissolving polyamino            functionalized ionic liquid in water, then adding            dialdehyde, stirring, centrifuging and vacuum            drying.
AU SHI L
   YI Q
   WU Y
   ZHANG Q
   LI D
   GAO L
   SHAN Y
   QI K
   WANG X
   LIU T
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2020787592
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyionic liquid catalyst                involves taking water as a medium having a pH range                of 5-14, polyamino functionalized ionic liquid and                dialdehyde in a molar ratio is 1:(0.5-2) as raw                materials, dissolving 10-40 mmol polyamino                functionalized ionic liquid in water at room                temperature, adjusting the pH to 5-14, then adding                5-80 mmol dialdehyde, stirring at room temperature                for 2 hours, so that a large number of flocs                appear, centrifuging to separate solids, and vacuum                drying at 50 degrees C for 24 hours to get                mesoporous polymer material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyionic                liquid catalyst, which is useful for catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic                carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of the                polyionic liquid catalyst under mild catalytic                condition with high recycling utilization rate and                without using cocatalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the polyionic liquid catalyst for catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The polyamino                  functionalized ionic liquid is prepared by                  dissolving polyamino functionalized ionic liquid                  monomer in water, adding an initiator                  azobisisobutyronitrile, where the number of moles                  of initiator is 0.05% of the monomer, and                  reacting at 60-80 degrees C for 4-12 hours.                  Preferred Components: The polyamino                  functionalized ionic liquid monomer is                  1-vinyl-3-ethylaminoimidazolium chloride,                  1-vinyl-3-ethylaminoimidazolium bromide,                  1-vinyl-3-propylaminoimidazolium bromide and/or                  1-vinyl-3-butylaminoimidazolium bromide. The                  dialdehyde is succinic aldehyde, glutaraldehyde,                  adipaldehyde, 1,4-phthalaldehyde and/or                  4,4'-biphenyl dialdehyde.

PN CN111359651-A; CN111359651-B
TI Preparing a transition metal-nitrogen coordinated carbon gel electrocatalyst useful in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide, comprises e.g. dissolving pyridine carboxylic acid in inorganic alkali solution.
AU HOU Y
   ZHANG Y
   YANG B
   LI Z
   LEI L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020638021
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a transition metal-nitrogen coordinated carbon gel electrocatalyst comprises (i) dissolving pyridine carboxylic acid in an inorganic alkali solution, adding a transition metal salt, and dissolving by ultrasonic to form a transition metal-nitrogen coordinated organic monomer solution; (ii) adding the solution of pyrrole and ammonium persulfate to the organic monomer solution of step (i) in sequence to polymerize at low temperature to form a gel; and (iii) washing the gel, drying, roasting nitrogen, pickling, and drying.
   USE - The transition metal-nitrogen coordinated carbon gel electrocatalyst is useful in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The transition metal-nitrogen coordinated carbon gel electrocatalyst: has clear coordination structure, and is applied to electrocatalyze carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide, has high activity and high selectivity, and ensures that the maximum Faraday efficiency of carbon monoxide can reaches to 96%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for transition metal-nitrogen coordinated carbon gel electrocatalyst, is prepared by the preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar ratio of the inorganic base to the pyridinecarboxylic acid is 0.5-1.5:1 according to the molar ratio of the hydroxide in inorganic base and the carboxy group in pyridinecarboxylic acid. In step (i), the transition metal salt is a chloride salt, a nitrate salt and a sulfate salt of the transition metal, the transition metal includes copper, manganese, iron, cobalt and nickel. In the step (ii), the mass concentration of pyrrole in pyrrole solution is 40-200 mg/ml, and the solvent is isopropanol, methanol, ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol. In the step (ii), the mass concentration of pyrrole in the pyrrole solution is 40-200 mg/ml, and the solvent is isopropanol, methanol, ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol. In the step (ii), the mass concentration of ammonium persulfate in the ammonium persulfate solution is 200-400 mg/ml and the solvent is water or an alcoholic solvent. In the step (ii), the low temperature is 0-10 degrees C, and the polymerization time is 2-8 hours. In the step (iii), the temperature of nitrogen heating is 700-1000 degrees C, the heating time is 8-12 hours, and the heating rate is 3-7 degrees C/minute. In the step (iii), the transition metal is nickel, and the temperature of nitrogen roasting is 800-1000 degrees C.

PN CN110876954-A; CN110876954-B
TI Preparing foamed metal composite electrocatalyst            for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction,            by taking metal composite electrocatalyst composed of            metal nanoparticles supported on MXene/carbon nitride            carrier.
AU LI C
   WANG Y
   CHAO Z
AE UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 202022231C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing foamed metal composite                electrocatalyst involves taking metal composite                electrocatalyst composed of metal nanoparticles                supported on MXene/carbon nitride carrier, where                the MXene/carbon nitride carrier is prepared by                adding melamine to a small layer of MXene                dispersion with a concentration of 0.2-10 mg/ml,                stirring, freezing in liquid nitrogen for 5-20                minutes, freeze-drying the liquid nitrogen mixture                at -15 to -50 degrees C to obtain Mxene/melamine,                and calcining the obtained Mxene/melamine under an                inert atmosphere at 500-600 degrees C for 1-4 hours                to obtain MXene/carbon nitride carrier, and the                mass ratio of small layer MXene and melamine is                1:2-1:25.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing foamed                metal composite electrocatalystis for                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrocatalyst has high catalytic                activity, high stability and high selectivity, and                improves the energy efficiency of carbon dioxide                utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a foamed                metal composite electrocatalyst prepared by the                above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The small layer MXene                  material is a sheet-shaped material obtained by                  intercalating or peeling a multilayer MXene                  formed by acid etching to less than 10 layers.                  The metal nanoparticles are supported on the                  MXene/carbon nitride carrier by adding                  MXene/carbon nitride carrier to deionized water                  to prepare a dispersion of 0.2-3 mg/ml, adding                  metal precursor and citric acid to the                  dispersion, dripping 0.5 mol/l sodium borohydride                  solution at a speed of 10-120 day/minute,                  stirring and reacting the mixture, filtering,                  washing and drying to obtain metal composite                  electrocatalyst, where the mass ratio of metal                  precursor and MXene/carbon nitride carrier is                  1:1-1:6, the mass ratio of metal precursor and                  citric acid is 1:10-1:100, and the volume ratio                  of sodium borohydride solution and MXene/carbon                  nitride carrier dispersion is 1:2-1:25. Preferred                  Components: The MXene comprises titanium carbide                  of formula: Ti3C2, tantalum carbide of formula:                  Ta4C3, niobium of formula: Nb4C3, chromium of                  formula: Cr2C or titanium of formula: Ti2C. The                  metal precursor is a metal nitrate or a chloride.                  The catalyst is a composite electrocatalyst                  having a layer of carbon nitride is uniformly                  coated on the surface of the layered MXene to                  form an MXene/carbon nitride carrier, obtaining                  metal composite electrocatalyst after the metal                  nanoparticles are supported on the outer layer of                  carbon nitride, where the MXene/carbon nitride                  carrier has a foam structure as a whole and                  holes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal                  nanoparticles are metal nanoparticles with                  electrocatalytic activity. The metal comprises                  gold or silver.

PT P
PN CN110551263-A
TI New carbon dioxide stimulus-responsive multi-block polymer useful in tumor detection kit and fluorescent probe for tumor detection and for fluorescent diagnosis of cancer cells and tissues.
AU WANG Y
   ZHOU G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA NORMAL (USCN-C)
GA 2019A5792M
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide stimulus-responsive multi-block polymer (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in tumor detection kit and fluorescent probe for tumor detection (claimed); and for fluorescent diagnosis of cancer cells and tissues.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) have excellent supramolecular assembly characteristics and biocompatibility, forms polymer assembly which causes aggregation-induced luminescence and emits bright blue light under high-energy light (ultraviolet light).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide stimulus-responsive multi-block polymer of formula (I) are new.
   m = 1-50;
   n = 1-100;
   p = 1-50;
   A = 2-(4-(1,2,2-triphenylvinyl)phenoxy)ethyl 2-methylbutanoate moiety of formula (II);
   a, b = 1-20;
   B1 = (Z)-N,N-dimethyl-N'-((1-(4-propylbenzyl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methyl)acetimidamide moiety of formula (III); and
   C1 = 2-methoxyethyl 2-methylbutanoate moiety of formula (IV).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) fluorescent probe for tumor detection comprising multi-block polymer; and
   (3) tumor detection kit comprising fluorescent probe for tumor detection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) placing flask containing zinc and tetrahydrofuran in ice water bath, adding titanium tetrachloride for reflux, then adding mixed solution of benzophenone and 4-hydroxybenzophenone, refluxing and purifying to obtain 1-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1,2,2-triphenylethylene, (ii) mixing 1-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1,2,2-triphenylethylene with sodium carbonate, 11-bromo-1-undecanol, and potassium iodide, reacting at 100-110 degrees C, and purifying to obtain 1-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-1,2,2-triphenylethylene, (iii) adding 1-(4-ethoxyphenyl) -1,2,2-triphenylethylene and methacryloyl chloride into the dichloromethane solution, stirring the reaction thoroughly to obtain 4-undecyloxytetraphenyl vinyl methacrylate, (iv) reacting 4-undecyloxytetraphenylvinyl methacrylate and 2-(dodecylthiocarbonthiothio)-2-methylpropionic acid at 50-70 degrees C, and then purifying to obtain polytetrafluoroethylene, (v) performing polymerization reaction between the polytetrafluoroethylene and 4-vinylbenzyl chloride to obtain poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl chloride) block polymer, (vi) polymerizing poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl chloride) block polymer and poly(ethylene oxide) precursor to obtain poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl azide)-b-poly(ethylene oxide) block polymer, (vii) reacting poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl azide)-b-poly(ethylene oxide) block polymer with sodium azide, (viii) mixing propynylamine with N, N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal to obtain N'-propyne-N,N-dimethylacetamide, (ix) polymerizing poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl azide)-b-poly(ethylene oxide) with N'-propyne-N, N-dimethylacetamide to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In steps (i), (ii), and (iii), the purification is performed by column chromatography. In steps (i), (ii), and (iii), prior to purification by column chromatography, extraction and drying are performed. The multi-block polymer has number average molecular weight of 20,000-100,000. In step (i), the stoichiometric ratio between benzophenone and 4-hydroxybenzophenone is 1:1; in step (ii), the chemical equivalent ratio between 1-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-1,2,2-triphenylethylene, 11-bromo-1-undecanol and potassium iodide is 1:1.2:0.005; in step (iii), the stoichiometric ratio between 1-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-1,2,2-triphenylethylene and methacryloyl chloride is 4:1; in step (iv), the stoichiometric ratio between 4-undecyloxytetraphenyl vinyl methacrylate to 2-(dodecylthiocarbonthiothio)-2-methylpropionic acid is 2.5:1; in step (v), stoichiometric ratio between polytetrafluoroethylene and 4-vinylbenzyl chloride is 1:0.135; in step (vi), the stoichiometric ratio of the poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl chloride)block polymer and poly(ethylene oxide) precursor is 1:4; in step (vii), the stoichiometric ratio between poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl chloride)block polymer and sodium azide is 1:11.5; in step (viii), the stoichiometric ratio between propynylamine and N, N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal is 1:2.4; and in step (ix), the stoichiometric ratio between poly(polytetrafluoroethylene)-b-(4-vinylbenzyl azide)-b-poly(ethylene oxide) block polymer to the N'-propyne-N, N-dimethylacetamide is 1:1. Preferred Method: In steps (v), (vi), and (ix), the polymerization reaction adopts reversible addition-fragmentation chain transfer polymerization method. In step (viii), the mixed reaction of propynylamine and N, N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal is performed under the protection of a protective atmosphere.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

TI Treating inorganic waste through exchange of materials, comprises e.g. preparing waste gypsum and slag, introducing ammonia, carbon dioxide and water into waste gypsum, and performing carbonation reaction to obtain ammonium sulfate.
AU LEE S W
   BANG J H
   CHAE S C
   BANG J
   CHAE S
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 201946104H
AB    NOVELTY - Treating inorganic waste through exchange of materials, comprises (a) preparing waste gypsum and slag, (b) introducing ammonia, carbon dioxide and water into waste gypsum, and performing carbonation reaction to obtain ammonium sulfate and calcium carbonate, (c) separating and recovering ammonium sulfate and calcium carbonate, (d) introducing ammonium sulfate and water into the slag to prepare slurry, separating metal ions and ammonia, (e) introducing carbon dioxide and water into metal ions, performing carbonation reaction to collect carbonate, and (f) introducing ammonium sulfate generated in the step (c) into step (d) and generating metal ions and ammonia, transferring ammonia in the step (d) into step (b), and performing carbonation reaction to generate ammonium sulfate and calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for treating inorganic waste through exchange of materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be performed simultaneously, recovers calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate with high purity, recovers ammonium sulfate with increased nitrogen content, which can be used as fertilizer, and is economical and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The waste slag is conditioned by drying at 80-100 degrees C, pulverizing, granulating the pulverized resultant using 200 mesh. The ammonia is formed by adding and dissolving ammonia solution into ammonia and water generated from the step (e). The added amount of ammonia is 1-5 equivalents into waste gypsum. The slag is conditioned by milling using a ball-mill, pulverizing and granulating the pulverized resultant using 200 mesh. The separation of metal ions and ammonia are carried out by heating at 200-400 degrees C, and separating in form of ammonia gas. The carbonation reaction of step (e) and (f) is carried out under pressure of 1-50 bar. Preferred Components: The waste gypsum is discharged from coal-fired power plant, and the slag is discharged from steel mill. The slag is steel slag containing calcium or magnesium. The coal-fired power plant and the steel mill are adjacent to each other. The metal ion is made of calcium or magnesium. The carbonate is calcium carbonate or magnesium carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2018234971-A1
TI Production of syngas for producing e.g. methanol, involves reacting reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and oxygen, in reactor comprising catalytic partial oxidation catalyst to produce syngas comprising hydrogen, and carbon dioxide.
AU CHINTA S
   AL-GHAMDI M
   PANT A
   EDREES R
   AL-AMAR M
   AL-HAGBANI S
   RABIE A
   KOLAH A
   BINKHAMIS S
   AL-TURKISTANI S
   NARAYANASWAMY R
   RAJAB K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2019001284
AB    NOVELTY - Production of syngas involves reacting a reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and oxygen, in a reactor comprising catalytic partial oxidation catalyst, by a catalytic partial oxidation reaction under non-adiabatic and near-isothermal conditions at contact time of 0.001 mseconds to 5 seconds, preferably 0.001-5 mseconds to produce syngas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and unreacted hydrocarbons.
   USE - Production of syngas used as intermediate for producing methanol (claimed), ammonia, and synthetic petroleum products used as lubricant and fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple and can be easily adapted to the various syngas compositions required for downstream process, and does not produce additional hydrogen, thereby conserving hydrogen feedstock, such as natural gas. The use of carbon dioxide diluent can decrease usage amount of coke, thus decreasing catalyst deactivation. The syngas is used in methanol production, which can reduce recycle streams due to containing required amount of hydrogen, and also reduce amount of water in crude methanol product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reactant mixture further comprises a diluent comprising water, steam, inert gases, nitrogen, and/or carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of methane and oxygen in the reaction mixture is 1:1-3:1. The molar ratio of difference of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the sum of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the syngas is 1.6-2.2, which varies by less than 20% from a desired ratio under the near-isothermal conditions for a given set of reactor operating conditions. The syngas has hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio that is increased when compared to a hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of a syngas produced by similar process conducted with a reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and oxygen without the water and/or steam diluent, and has a hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio that is decreased when compared to a hydrogen to carbon monoxide molar ratio of a syngas produced by similar process conducted with a reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and oxygen without the carbon dioxide diluent.

   ZHANG C
   MA W
   YU S
   LIU K
AE NINGBO GMF NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)

GA 201869002R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coated ultra-light high elastic resilience composite foaming material involves placing thermoplastic elastomer particles into high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction kettle, controlling pressure and temperature of pressure reaction kettle in which carbon dioxide is present in supercritical state, performing de-compression process, heating and foaming to obtain foaming particle, steaming and molding, performing steam pressing of foaming particles to prepare foaming material, coating material in on surface to obtain coated ultra-light foaming material.
   USE - Method for preparing coated ultra-light high elastic resilience composite foaming material used in building, industrial equipment and some fields with requirements for mechanical properties, resilience and flame retardancy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare coated ultra-light high elastic resilience composite foaming material has low coefficient of heat conductivity, low density, high rebound resilience, wear resistance, folding resistance, strong mechanical property, good low temperature performance, heat insulating performance and good flame retardant property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic elastomer is selected from polyurethane thermoplastic elastomer, polyolefinbased thermoplastic elastomer, polyester thermoplastic elastomer and styrene thermoplastic elastomer. Preferred Conditions: The diameter of thermoplastic elastomer particles are 4 mm. The pressure is 7.38-30 megaPascal, temperature is 31-80 degrees C and penetration time is 1.5-6 hours. The foaming temperature is 90-160 degrees C and the foaming time is 30-90 s. The foam particle foaming multiplying power is 4-10 times. The temperature of steam compression molding is 120-200 degrees C, pressure is 0.1-1 megaPascal and time is 2-6 minute. The thickness of low thermal conductivity material is 3-10 mm.

PN CN108440486-A
TI Synthesizing propylene carbonate by adding propylene, oxidizing agent, nano oxide and acetylacetone salt to reaction kettle, then dissolving in solvent, stirring and cooling, introducing dried carbon dioxide gas into kettle and treating.
AU CHEN T
   CHAN S
   BAO G
   ZHENG X
   WANG J
   YANG S
   CHEN Z
   LI H
   XU H
   HUANG R
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   UNIV ANQING NORMAL (AQNU-C)
GA 201868201A
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing propylene carbonate involves: (a) carrying out oxidation reaction by adding propylene, oxidizing agent, nano oxide and acetylacetone salt in a molar ratio of 1:(1.1-2.2):(0.001-0.005):(0.001-0.005) to reaction kettle, then dissolving the mixture in a solvent, stirring the reaction mixture at 50-90 degrees C for 1-2 hours and cooling to room temperature; and (b) carrying out esterification reaction by introducing dried carbon dioxide gas into the reaction kettle, controlling the reaction temperature at 100-130 degrees C under closed condition, stirring the reaction mixture for 4-6 hours, cooling to room temperature, slowly discharging unreacted carbon dioxide gas, and then treating to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The one-pot method can synthesize the propylene carbonate with improved production efficiency and without performing separation and purification treatment on the intermediate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano oxide is chosen from molybdenum trioxide, iron(II,III) oxide, nickel(II) oxide and/or manganese tetroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (1), norbornene (optionally) and piperidine are further added to the reaction kettle. The step (2) involves, after the carbon dioxide gas is introduced, controlling the reaction pressure in the reaction kettle at 2-10 atm. Preferred Components: The oxidizing agent is m-chloroperbenzoic acid. The acetylacetonate salt is of structural formula (I). The solvent is chosen from N,N-dimethylformamide, toluene and/or propylene carbonate.M(n+) = Ni2+, Mn2+, Fe2+, Cu2+ or Co2+.

PT P
PN WO2022216221-A1
TI Preparing aluminum trihydrate comprises mixing            aluminum dross with sodium hydroxide to form mixture            and filtering to obtain first filtrate and first            residue, diluting the filtrate with water, and mixing            the filtrate with carbon dioxide.
AU PANDEY T B
   CHENG C L J
AE JTS OPTIMAX PTE LTD (JTSO-Non-standard)
GA 2022C9058G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum trihydrate (ATH) comprises:                mixing aluminum dross with sodium hydroxide at a                first pre-determined temperature to form a mixture,                where the aluminum dross has an aluminum oxide                content of &#8805; 30% based on the total                weight of the aluminum dross; filtering the mixture                to obtain a first filtrate and a first residue;                diluting the first filtrate with water; and mixing                the first filtrate with carbon dioxide at a second                pre-determined temperature to obtain ATH residue                and a second filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ATH which                is useful in fire retarded polymers for electronics                and automotive industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces ATH may subsequently                converted to aluminum; recovers aluminum from                aluminum dross in an environmentally friendly                manner; utilizes carbon dioxide, to reduce carbon                emissions; minimizes waste; and reduces loss of                resources to landfill; is a safe method and                contributes to keeping the method economical; and                enables production of other useful                by-products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aluminum dross has                  an aluminum oxide content of 60-90% based on the                  total weight of the aluminum dross. The aluminum                  dross is powdered aluminum dross. The aluminum                  dross has an average particle size of 0.002-10                  mm. The mixture comprises aluminum dross and                  sodium hydroxide in a ratio of 1:0.2-1:8.                  Preferred Method: The first pre-determined                  temperature is 50-100&#176; C. The diluting                  comprises diluting the first filtrate by a                  dilution factor of 1.5-4. The mixing the first                  filtrate with carbon dioxide comprises flowing                  carbon dioxide into the first filtrate at a                  flowrate of 9-20 kg/minute. The second                  pre-determined temperature is 40-100&#176; C.                  The mixing the first filtrate with carbon dioxide                  comprises flowing carbon dioxide into the first                  filtrate for 60-180 minutes. The mixing the first                  filtrate with carbon dioxide reduces pH of the                  first filtrate to 7-8.6. The method is performed                  at atmospheric pressure. The method further                  comprises: evaporating the second filtrate to                  form sodium bicarbonate; and heating the sodium                  bicarbonate to form sodium carbonate. The                  evaporating comprises vacuum evaporation.
   EXAMPLE - 100 g Aluminum dross powder (comprised of 82%                aluminum oxide, 1.62% calcium oxide, 0.39% barium                oxide, 1.53% iron(III) oxide, 1.39% titanium                dioxide, 0.13% potassium oxide, 0.11% nickel oxide,                0.1% chromium(III) oxide, 0.07% zinc oxide, 0.03%                strontium oxide, 0.05% copper oxide, 0.07%                zirconium oxide, 4.49% silicon dioxide, 6.41%                magnesium oxide, 0.56% sulfur trioxide, 0.04%                manganese oxide, 0.94% sodium carbonate and 0.07%                vanadium(V) oxide) was then added to sodium                hydroxide solution in a ratio of 3:2 and was                constantly stirred. The mixture was maintained at                100&#176; C for 2 hours. After 2 hours, sodium                aluminate liquor was obtained. The liquor was                filtered out from the reaction oxide residue (ROR)                cake. The sodium aluminate liquor had a                concentration of 68% to remain soluble in water.                Carbon dioxide gas was then pumped into the                filtered sodium aluminate liquor to form carbonic                acid and carbonic acid. Due to the high alkalinity                of the liquor, the carbonic acid neutralized the                sodium hydroxide that kept the sodium aluminate                soluble. Therefore, the sodium aluminate                dissociated back to sodium hydroxide and aluminum                trihydrate. As there was insufficient sodium                hydroxide to keep the aluminum trihydrate soluble,                the aluminum trihydrate precipitated out as a runny                white precipitate product.

PN CN114163395-A
TI Synthesizing chiral oxazolinone derivative useful            in preparing linezolid and rivaroxaban, comprises            reacting primary amine, glycidyl derivatives and carbon            dioxide in presence of bifunctional quaternary ammonium            salt phase transfer catalyst (i.e. urea, thiourea or            squaramide-containing catalyst).
AU LI M
   ZHANG D
   JIANG R
   LIU S
   BAI Y
   ZHANG S
   WANG P
AE UNIV PLA AIR FORCE MEDICAL (UYFM-C)
GA 202242037A
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing chiral oxazolinone derivative                comprises reacting primary amine, glycidyl                derivatives and carbon dioxide at 70-100degrees                Celsius for 24-48 hours under the catalysis of                bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt phase                transfer catalyst to obtain chiral oxazolinone                derivatives, where the bifunctional quaternary                ammonium salt phase transfer catalyst is a urea,                thiourea or squaramide-containing catalyst.
   USE - The chiral oxazolinone derivative is useful in                preparing linezolid and rivaroxaban                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide activation                and immobilization, phase transfer catalysis and                multi-component reactions, which all belong to the                category of environmentally friendly organic                synthesis, which is in line with the current                development trend of environmentally friendly                chemistry; involves an atom-economical reaction,                which provides a shortcut for the environmentally                friendly synthesis of medicines containing chiral                oxazolinone skeletons, e.g. linezolid and                rivaroxaban.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) Oxazolinone derivative prepared by above                method;
   (2) Use of chiral oxazolinone derivative in                preparing linezolid and rivaroxaban.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material ratio of                  the primary amine, the glycidyl derivative and                  the bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt phase                  transfer catalyst is 1:1-1.1:0.01-0.05; the                  amount of carbon dioxide is 1-5 atm. The                  synthesis of the chiral oxazolinone derivatives                  is carried out in tetrahydrofuran or                  N-methylpyrrolidone or under solvent-free                  conditions. The bifunctional quaternary ammonium                  salt phase transfer catalyst is e.g.                  1-benzyl-1-(2-(3-(3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)ureido)ethyl)pyrrolidinium                  bromide,                  1-benzyl-1-(2-(3-(3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)thioureido)ethyl)pyrrolidinium                  bromide and                  1-benzyl-1-(2-(2-(3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylamino)-3,4-dioxocyclobut-1-enylamino)ethyl)pyrrolidinium                  bromide. The primary amine is compound of formula                  (R1-NH2) (I). The glycidol derivative is e.g.                  (R)-2-(phenoxymethyl)oxirane,                  (R)-2-((2-methoxyphenoxy)methyl)oxirane,                  (R)-2-((2-fluorophenoxy)methyl)oxirane,                  (R)-2-((2-chlorophenoxy)methyl)oxirane and                  (R)-oxiran-2-ylmethyl benzoate.R1 = 3-12C alkyl, 3-8C cycloalkyl, benzene                  derivative of formula (a), pyrrole derivative of                  formula (b), furan derivative of formula (c),                  thiophene derivative of formula (d) or pyridine                  derivative of formula (e);RX and RX' = H, 1-5C alkyl, 3-8C cycloalkyl,                  halogen, cyano, nitro, hydroxy, alkoxy, acetyl,                  carboxy, pyrrolidinyl, morpholinyl or                  piperidinyl.Preferred Method: The linezolid is prepared                  by (1) taking carbon dioxide as a raw material                  and tetrahydrofuran as a solvent, heating and                  reacting at 70-100degrees Celsius for 24-48 hours                  in the presence of a bifunctional quaternary                  ammonium salt phase transfer catalyst to obtain                  linezolid key intermediate C i.e.                  (S)-2-((3-(3-fluoro-4-morpholinophenyl)-2-oxooxazolidin-5-yl)methyl)isoindoline-1,3-dione,                  (2) adding the linezolid key intermediate C                  obtained in step (1) into the methylamine-alcohol                  solution, then heating to reflux for 1-3 hours,                  cooling to room temperature, then carrying out                  acidification using 1-3 mol/l hydrochloric acid                  solution to obtain                  (S)-5-(aminomethyl)-3-(3-fluoro-4-morpholinophenyl)oxazolidin-2-one                  (D), where the amount of methylamine in the                  methylamine-alcohol solution is 30-40%, (3)                  mixing pyridine, acetic anhydride and the D                  obtained in step (2) under -10-0degrees Celsius                  condition, taking dichloromethane as solvent,                  heating to room temperature and reacting for 2-4                  hours to obtain linezolid; in, The ratio of the                  amount of compound D to the substance of pyridine                  and acetic anhydride is 1:5:1-1.05. The                  rivaroxaban is prepared by (1) taking carbon                  dioxide as raw material and N-methylpyrrolidone                  as solvent, heating and reacting at 70-100degrees                  Celsius for 24-48 hours in the presence of a                  bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt phase                  transfer catalyst to obtain the key rivaroxaban                  intermediate F i.e.                  (S)-2-((3-(4-morpholinophenyl)-2-oxooxazolidin-5-yl)methyl)isoindoline-1,3-dione,                  (2) adding F prepared in step (1) into a                  methylamine-alcohol solution in an argon                  atmosphere, then heating to reflux for 1-3 hours                  and cooling to -10-0degrees Celsius, then                  acidifying with 1-3 mol/l hydrochloric acid                  solution to obtain compound G i.e.                  (S)-5-(aminomethyl)-3-(4-morpholinophenyl)oxazolidin-2-one                  hydrochloride, where the amount of methylamine in                  the methylamine-alcohol solution is 30-40%, (3)                  mixing triethylamine, 5-chlorothiophene-2-formyl                  chloride and compound G obtained in step (2) in                  an argon atmosphere under the condition of                  0degrees Celsius and using dichloromethane as a                  solvent, heating to room temperature and reacting                  for 2-4 hours to obtain rivaroxaban, where then                  substance amount ratio of compound G,                  triethylamine and 5-chlorothiophene-2-carbonyl                  chloride is 1:5:1-1.05.

TI Dual phase membrane for separating carbon dioxide            from feed gas, comprises solid porous support phase            providing porous solid matrix of connected open pores,            and molten salt phase contained within pores.
AU MERRILL M
   KELLY J
AE LUNA INNOVATIONS INC (OPTA-C)
GA 202227678B
AB    NOVELTY - A dual phase separation membrane (10)                comprises a porous support providing a solid phase                (12) containing a matrix of connected pores, and a                liquefiable ion transport phase (14) within the                pores of the support. The ion transport phase                comprises at least one alkali metal hydroxide, and                at least one oxide ion transport agent for                providing a source of ions selected from borates,                nitrates, phosphates, vanadates, niobates and                sulfates.
   USE - Dual phase membrane for separating (claimed)                gaseous species e.g. carbon dioxide from feed gas                e.g. flue gas of industrial facility e.g. chemical,                petroleum and power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The dual phase active membrane enables the                high performance separation of carbon dioxide from                a feed gas throughout a relatively wide range of                relevant applied temperatures and other operational                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separation of gaseous species from feed gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a highly schematic                cross-sectional view of the membrane.
   Dual phase membrane (10)
   Sides (10a,10b)
   Solid phase (12)
   Liquefiable ion transport phase (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least one oxide ion                  transport agent is a compound in a meta, ortho,                  pyro or poly state, preferably an alkali or an                  alkaline earth metal salt of a borate, nitrate,                  phosphate, vanadate, niobate or sulfate. The                  alkali metal hydroxide is selected from sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium                  hydroxide, rubidium hydroxide and/or cesium                  hydroxide. The ion transport phase further                  comprises a melt temperature depression component                  comprising alkali earth metal hydroxide(s),                  alkali metal nitrate(s) and/or alkali earth metal                  nitrate(s), preferably beryllium hydroxide,                  magnesium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, strontium                  hydroxide, barium hydroxide, lithium nitrate,                  sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate, rubidium                  nitrate, cesium nitrate, beryllium nitrate,                  magnesium nitrate, calcium nitrate, strontium                  nitrate and/or barium nitrate. The support                  comprises a metal or ceramic material, preferably                  nickel-chromium based alloys, stainless steels,                  zirconium oxides, cerium oxides, magnesium                  oxides, aluminum oxides, lanthanum oxides,                  samarium oxides, gadolinium oxides, iron oxides,                  calcium carbonates, silicon oxides and silicon                  carbides. The feed gas comprises carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Composition: The ion transport phase                  contains 1-30 mol% oxide ion transport agent.                  Preferred Property: The pores have an average                  pore size of 10 nm to 1 mm.

AU MA X
   SONG T

   CHENG N
   CHEN Q
AE JINNAIYUAN HENAN IND TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JINN-Non-standard)
GA 202236096X
AB    NOVELTY - Hardening accelerator comprises 5-6 pts. wt.                calcium hydroxide, 2-3 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1.5-2                pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 3-4 pts. wt. Portland                cement and 1-2 pts. wt. cross-linking agent. The                hardening accelerator is prepared by (i) grinding                calcium hydroxide, Portland cement, magnesium oxide                and zinc oxide respectively into powder, and (ii)                uniformly mixing the ground materials, adding                crosslinking agent to obtain hardening                accelerator.
   USE - Used as hardening accelerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The accelerator: avoids problem of long                blowing hardening time and slow coagulation speed;                increases the sensitivity of phenolic resin binder                to carbon dioxide gas hardening; shortens the                blowing hardening time; improves the process                strength of the adhesive; has the functions of heat                generation, reaction cross-linking, and dispersion                enhancement; increases the plasticity of phenolic                resin, and strength; improves the hygroscopicity of                carbon dioxide hardening phenolic resins; can reach                the technical level of initial strength                greater-than 1.8MPa, final strength greater-than                5.2 MPa; and creates more favorable conditions for                the popularization and application of carbon                dioxide gas hardening phenolic resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crosslinking agent is                  prepared by (ii.i) placing acetic acid solution                  and chitosan into a three-necked flask, adding                  purified carbon nanotubes after tube opening,                  stirring and refluxing, drying and grinding to                  obtain catalyst E, (ii.ii) adding chloroplatinic                  acid isopropanol solution to absolute ethanol,                  taking catalyst E powder and mixing, then adding                  ethanol, and distilling under reduced pressure to                  obtain catalyst F, (ii.iii) taking 9-decen-1-ol                  and catalyst F and placing in a three-necked                  flask at room temperature in the fume hood,                  adding methyldichlorosilane under the protection                  of nitrogen, reacting at room temperature under                  magnetic stirring to obtain intermediate A,                  (ii.iv) adding intermediate A to the there-necked                  flask, adding propanol to the there-necked flask                  under the protection of N, heat the reaction                  under magnetic stirring, cooling and filtering to                  obtain intermediate B, and (ii.v) adding                  methacryloyl chloride to intermediate B, react at                  room temperature under magnetic stirring, and                  filter to obtain the crosslinking agent. The                  method specifically comprises (i) taking calcium                  hydroxide, portland cement, magnesium oxide and                  zinc oxide, and grinding into powder, and (ii)                  uniformly mixing the ground calcium hydroxide,                  portland cement, magnesium oxide and zinc oxide,                  and adding a crosslinking agent to obtain the                  hardening accelerator.Preferred Components: In the step (ii.i),                  the mass fraction of the acetic acid solution is                  1.5 wt.%, and the dosage ratio of the acetic acid                  solution, chitosan and carbon nanotubes is 50 ml:                  0.8 g: 8 g. In the step (ii.ii), the                  concentration of chloroplatinic acid isopropanol                  solution is 0.0386 mol/l. The dosage ratio of the                  isopropanol solution of chloroplatinic acid,                  absolute ethanol, catalyst E and ethanol is 3                  ml:30 ml:1 g:10 ml. In the step (ii.iii), the                  dosage ratio of 9-decen-1-ol, catalyst F and                  methyl dichlorosilane is 34 g:4 g:27.09 g. In the                  step (ii.v), the dosage ratio of 9-decen-1-ol,                  catalyst F and methyl dichlorosilane is 34 g:4                  g:27.09 g.

PN CN113717382-A; CN113717382-B
TI New covalent triazine polymer or metallized            covalent triazine polymer used in photocatalytic carbon            dioxide reduction, comprises porphyrin, pyrimidine and            triazine structural units.
AU SHE Y
   LI Y
   WANG K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2021E58962
AB    NOVELTY - A covalent triazine polymer or metallized                covalent triazine polymer (I) or (II), is new. The                polymer comprises porphyrin, pyrimidine and                triazine structural units. The metal ion in                metallized covalent triazine polymer is cupric ion,                divalent cobalt ion, ferrous ion, divalent platinum                ion, divalent palladium ion or divalent nickel                ion.
   USE - New polymer (I) or (II) used in photocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymers have excellent light absorption                property and carbon dioxide adsorption and                conversion performance due to high nitrogen content                and high-density metal distribution in                polymer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A covalent triazine polymer or metallized                covalent triazine polymer of formula (I) or (II),                is new. The polymer comprises porphyrin, pyrimidine                and triazine structural unit. The metal ion in                metallized covalent triazine polymer is cupric ion,                divalent cobalt ion, ferrous ion, divalent platinum                ion, divalent palladium ion or divalent nickel                ion.
   R1 = group of formula (i); R2 = group of                formula (ii); and R3 = H, Br, NH2, CH3 or                COOH.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the polymer (I) or (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The preparation of                  polymer (I) or (II) involves reacting                  cyano-containing porphyrin and 2-cyanopyrimidine                  or its derivatives with trifluoromethanesulfonic                  acid as catalyst in chloroform as solvent under                  nitrogen protection to carry out cyano                  cyclization reaction to obtain covalent triazine                  polymer (I), heating the polymer (I) and metal                  salt in solvent, so that metal ions are                  coordinated with the polymer to obtain                  high-density metallized covalent triazine polymer                  (II).M = Cu2+, Co2+, Fe2+, Pt2+, Pd2+ or                  Ni2+.Preferred Process: The preparation involves                  dissolving cyano-containing porphyrin and                  2-cyanopyrimidine or its derivative in chloroform                  under nitrogen protection to obtain solution (A),                  dissolving trifluoromethanesulfonic acid in                  chloroform at 0degrees Celsius under nitrogen                  atmosphere to form solution (B), dripping                  solution (A) into solution (B) using syringe                  under stirring condition, reacting at -5degrees                  Celsius to 10degrees Celsius in nitrogen                  atmosphere for 2-4 hours, heating the reaction                  mixture to room temperature, reacting at room                  temperature for 10-13 hours, adding aqueous                  ammonia to neutralize the mixture to neutrality,                  stirring for 1.5-2.5 hours, filtering to obtain                  purple glossy crude product, washing and                  filtering with dichloromethane and acetone in                  order until the filtrate is colorless, drying at                  75-85degrees Celsius for 11-13 to obtain covalent                  triazine polymer, adding covalent triazine                  polymer and metal salt to three-necked flask                  under nitrogen protection, adding solvent (A)                  using syringe, reacting under stirring and                  heating condition for 8-30 hours, cooling to room                  temperature, removing the solvent by filtration                  to obtain a crude product, washing and filtering                  with solvent until the filtrate is colorless and                  vacuum drying at 75-85degrees Celsius for 11-13                  hours to obtain metallized covalent triazine                  polymer.Preferred Condition: The cyano cyclization                  reaction temperature is 5-10degrees Celsius. The                  solvent input ratio is 15-25 mL/g, preferably 20                  mL/g of the total amount of covalent triazine                  polymer and metal salt.Preferred Component: The metallized covalent                  triazine polymer is chosen from polymers of                  formulae (1)-(4).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The derivative of                  2-cyanopyrimidine is 2-cyanopyrimidine,                  2-cyano-5-bromopyrimidine,                  2-cyano-5-aminopyrimidine,                  2-cyano-5-methylpyrimidine or                  2-cyano-5-carboxypyrimidine. The cyano-containing                  porphyrin is                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-cyanophenyl)porphyrin or                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(3-cyanophenyl)porphyrin. The                  solvent (A) is N,N-dimethylformamide,                  acetonitrile or benzonitrile.Preferred Composition: The ratio of                  cyano-containing porphyrin and 2-cyanopyrimidine                  or its derivative is 1:0.1-10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal salt is                  cupric chloride, cobalt chloride, ferrous                  chloride, platinum chloride, palladium chloride                  or nickel chloride.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113633671-A
TI Preparing bone-penetrating and dredging collateral            liquid containing Acronychia pedunculata, comprises            crushing dried Acronychia pedunculata, introducing            carbon dioxide gas into extraction kettle with            Acronychia pedunculata powder and filtering.
AU LIU H
AE HAINAN CHANGYONGHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D7130W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bone-penetrating and dredging                collaterals liquid containing Acronychia                pedunculata comprises (1) taking Acronychia                pedunculata, removing impurities, and drying; (2)                crushing dried Acronychia pedunculata, and passing                through 50 meshes sieve; (3) filling extraction                kettle with carbon dioxide, and discharging air in                the extraction kettle; (4) placing Acronychia                pedunculata powder in extraction kettle, and                introducing carbon dioxide gas for extraction to                obtain Acronychia pedunculata essential oil; and                (5) dissolving 50-60 pts. wt. Acronychia                pedunculata essential oil, 20-40 pts. wt. borneol,                and 20-40 pts. wt. menthol in 50-60 pts. wt.                glycerol, adding 70-80 pts. wt. polyethylene                glycol, uniformly stirring, and filtering to obtain                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                bone-penetrating and dredging collateral liquid                containing Acronychia pedunculata.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid has smaller side effect and                protects the skin.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for preparing bone-penetrating and                dredging collateral liquid containing Acronychia                pedunculata (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (4), the                  pressure in the extracting kettle is 25-30 MPa,                  preferably 28 MPa, the temperature is                  60-70degrees Celsius, preferably 65degrees                  Celsius and the extracting time is 180-210                  minutes, preferably 195 minutes. The extracting                  process is dynamic extraction and static                  extraction. The dynamic extraction time is                  140-150 minutes and the static extraction time is                  40-60 minutes. In the step (5), the 30 pts. wt.                  Acronychia pedunculata essential oil, 30 pts. wt.                  borneol and 30 pts. wt. menthol is dissolved in                  50 pts. wt. glycerol, and 70 pts. wt.                  polyethylene glycol is added. In the step (1),                  the electric heating constant-temperature blast                  drying box is used to dry the Acronychia                  pedunculata. In the step (1), the medicine                  crusher is used to crush Acronychia                  pedunculata.Preferred Components: The capacity of the                  extraction kettle is 1 l, and the Acronychia                  pedunculata is added to the extraction kettle is                  75-80 g, preferably 80 g. The Acronychia                  pedunculata is inclined leaves yellow                  sandalwood.

TI Manufacture of calcium carbonate used as e.g. inorganic filler for paper, involves reducing pH of calcium carbonate aqueous dispersion by adding acidic substance other than carbon dioxide under atmospheric pressure, and increasing pH of dispersion to grow calcium carbonate particles.
AU KEZUKA Y
AE SHIRAISHI CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (SHIR-Non-standard)
   SHIRAISHI CENT LAB CO LTD (SHIR-Non-standard)
   SHIRAISHI CENT LAB CO LTD (SHIR-Non-standard)
   SHIRAISHI CENT LAB CO LTD (SHIR-Non-standard)
GA 2021B6731S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of calcium carbonate involves reducing a pH of a calcium carbonate aqueous dispersion to 5.5-9 by adding an acidic substance other than carbon dioxide to the dispersion under atmospheric pressure, or adding acidic substance to the dispersion under a pressure of more than 1 atm to less than 10 atm, and increasing the pH of the dispersion to grow calcium carbonate particles.
   USE - Manufacture of calcium carbonate used as base material and inorganic filler. Uses include but are not limited to paper, rubber, sealing material, plastics, agriculture application, foodstuff application and cosmetic application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of lightweight calcium carbonate having large size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for crystal growth method of calcium carbonate, which involves reducing pH of the dispersion to 5.5-9 by adding acidic substance other than carbon dioxide, and increasing the pH of the dispersion to grow calcium carbonate particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves allowing the dispersion to stand, stirring the dispersion, depressurizing the dispersion, heating the dispersion, and increasing the pH of the dispersion by adding a basic substance to the dispersion. The decrease in pH of the dispersion and the increase in pH of the dispersion are repeated.

PN CN113441183-A; CN113441183-B
TI New alkali metal ions loaded crown ether organic            polymer useful as catalyst for coupling reaction of            carbon dioxide and epoxides to prepare cyclic            carbonates.
AU ZHANG Y
   YU Y
   LIU X
   CHEN M
   LUO R
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 2021B6125J
AB    NOVELTY - Alkali metal ions loaded crown ether organic                polymer (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as catalyst for coupling                reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxides to prepare                cyclic carbonates (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has simple catalytic conditions and high                repetitive utilization rate; is environmentally                friendly; and ensures high yield of catalyzed                formation of cyclic carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Alkali metal ions loaded crown ether organic                polymer of formula ((M-R1-(R)2)n) (I) is new.
   M = alkali metal ion, preferably Na+, K+ or                Cs+;
   R = amino or aldehyde groups containing                moieties of formula (1)-(8);
   R1 = amino or aldehyde groups containing crown                ethers of formula (9)-(11); and
   R'', R' = amino group or aldehyde group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) catalytic preparation of cyclic carbonate,                comprising (1) adding catalyst and epoxide into a                container, and introducing carbon dioxide for the                reaction, and the catalyst is (I), and (2) cooling                the reaction system, and filtering to obtain                filtrate as cyclic carbonate solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) performing first reflux                  reaction of amino or aldehyde groups containing                  crown ethers and amino or aldehyde groups                  containing compound in organic solution A, and                  performing first reflux reaction at 50-150degrees                  Celsius for 20-36 hours to obtain crown ether                  organic polymer, (ii) subjecting the crown ether                  organic polymer and halide of alkali metal to                  second reflux reaction in organic solution B,                  performing second reflux reaction at                  50-240degrees Celsius for 8-24 hours to obtain                  final product.Preferred Components: In the use method, the                  amount of (I) is 0.04-2.5 mol.% of the epoxide,                  and the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.2-3.5                  MPa. The epoxide is 7-oxabicyclo(4.1.0)heptane,                  or substituted oxirane compound of formula (1a)                  or substituted 2-phenyloxirane compound of                  formula (1b).R2 = H, halo, alkyl, unsaturated ether bond                  or phenoxy group; andR3 = H, halo, alkyl or alkoxy.

PN CN113332985-A
TI Catalyst useful for methane and carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction, comprises metal nickel as catalyst active component uniformly dispersed in silicon dioxide as carrier.
AU GUO Y
   DING X
   WANG Y
   LIU X
   YUE S
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021A44733
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises metal nickel as catalyst active component and silicon dioxide as carrier, where the metal nickel nano-particles are uniformly dispersed in the silicon dioxide carrier and covered by silicon dioxide, and the molar ratio of nickel to silicon dioxide is 1:100-1:0.5, preferably 1:20-1:2, and the average particle diameter of the metal nickel particles is not greater than 3 nm at the high load amount.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methane and carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst ensures that the nickel particles are highly uniformly dispersed under the premise of large nickel load amount (i.e. 0.1-66.7 wt.%), and coated by silicon dioxide carrier, thus ensures high reaction activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising adopting coprecipitation method or sol-gel method, preferably sol-gel method; and
   (2) application of the catalyst in methane and carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt includes nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, nickel sulfate and/or nickel acetate. The silicon source includes ethyl orthosilicate, silica sol and/or sodium silicate. The alkali liquor includes sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, ammonia, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate and/or ammonium carbonate, preferably ammonia. Preferred Method: The co-precipitation method comprises dissolving nickel salt in deionized water, then adding the silicon source into the nickel salt solution, and then reacting with alkali liquor to produce precipitate, drying the precipitate, roasting and reducing to obtain the catalyst. The sol-gel method comprises dissolving the nickel salt and complexing agent in the deionized water to obtain a solution, then adding the silicon source into the solution, stirring until a gel is formed, drying the gel, roasting and reducing to obtain the catalyst. In the application of the catalyst, the conversion of carbon dioxide reverse water gas is inhibited, and the conversion rate of methane and carbon dioxide are made similar, and the difference is within 2%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent includes citric acid, malic acid and/or tartaric acid, preferably citric acid.

PN CN113213498-A
TI Carbonizing and reusing cement-based material by mixing cement-based material powder e.g. cement powder and solution e.g. surfactant, introducing gas carbon dioxide, heating to react, mixing with soluble alkali, and solid-liquid separating.
AU SHEN P
   LU J
   XUAN D
   PAN Z
AE UNIV HONG KONG POLYTECHNIC (UYKP-C)
GA 2021939667
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonizing and reusing cement-based material involves mixing cement-based material powder and solution to obtain first mixture, introducing gas into first mixture, heating to react to obtain second mixture, mixing second mixture with soluble alkali, and solid-liquid separating to obtain calcium carbonate whisker solid material and material liquid. The material liquid is silicon, aluminate or aluminosilicate. The cement-based material powder comprises concrete sand powder, steel slag, cement powder and clinker powder. The solution comprises surfactant, calcium ion complexing agent and solvent. The surfactant is organic surfactant and/or inorganic surfactant. The gas comprises not less than 5% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is used for carbonizing and reusing cement-based material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves defect of low additional value utilization of cement-based material and solidifies carbon dioxide in industrial exhaust gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a calcium carbonate whisker material comprising calcium carbonate whisker material prepared by carbonizing and reusing cement-based material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The grain diameter of cement-based material powder is less than or equal to 0.15 mm. The cement powder and clinker powder comprise silicate cement and clinker, aluminate cement and clinker, sulfoaluminate cement and clinker, iron aluminate cement and clinker and fluoroaluminate cement and clinker. The concrete sand powder comprises waste concrete derived from silicate cement, aluminate cement, sulfoaluminate cement, iron aluminate cement and fluoroaluminate cement as gelled material, and alkali-activated concrete comprising waste concrete. The steel slag is steel slag by-product generated in steel-making process. The inorganic surfactant is soluble magnesium salt, magnesium hydroxide, phosphoric acid, soluble phosphate and/or soluble aluminum salt. The solvent is tap water, seawater, deionized water and/or medium water. The mass ratio of cement-based material powder to solution is 1:3-50. The mol concentration of inorganic surfactant is 0.01-10 mol/L. The organic surfactant accounts for 0.1-5% of solution. The calcium ion complexing agent accounts for 0.01-1% of solution. The soluble alkali is potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide. The mol concentration of soluble alkali is 0.5-10 mol/L. The material has purity of more than 50%, diameter of 50 nm to 2 mu m, and length-diameter ratio of 5-30. Preferred Method: Heating to react is done at 40-100 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours. The ventilation speed of gas is 0.01 L/minute/g. The gas is steel industrial exhaust gas, cement industrial exhaust gas, power industrial exhaust gas, chemical exhaust gas, non-steel metal product exhaust gas and/or glass and ceramic industrial exhaust gas. Mixing is done at 20-100 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic surfactant is sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and/or amino acid of phosphoric acid. The calcium ion complexing agent is EDTA, polyvinyl alcohol and/or triethanolamine.
