PN CN112251429-A
TI Preparation of zeolitic imidazolate framework            (ZIF)-8-formate dehydrogenase (FDH) nano hybrid            material used for catalyzing conversion of carbon            dioxide to formic acid, by obtaining ZIF-8-FDH            suspension and vacuum-drying precipitate.
AU WANG S
   XIU Y
   LI J
   YIN S
   YANG H
   JIA C
   CAO M
   XIA Y
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2021113344
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparation of zeolitic imidazolate                framework (ZIF)-8-formate dehydrogenase (FDH) nano                hybrid material, involves dissolving zinc acetate                dihydrate in water to obtain solution A, dissolving                2-methylimidazole in water, adding FDH powder,                mixing to obtain solution B, gradually adding                solution A to the solution B under stirring                process, obtaining ZIF-8-FDH suspension after                reaction, and vacuum-drying the precipitate for 12                hours after washing the suspension by                centrifugation to obtain ZIF-8-FDH nano hybrid                material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                ZIF-8-FDH nano hybrid material which is used for                catalyzing specific conversion of carbon dioxide to                formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) ZIF-8-FDH nano hybrid material prepared by                the method; and
   (2) use of the ZIF-8-FDH nano hybrid material,                for catalyzing specific conversion of carbon                dioxide to formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  zinc acetate dihydrate is 10-40 mM, and the                  concentration of 2-methylimidazole is 50-160 mM.                  The volume ratio of solution A and solution B is                  0.5-4. The concentration of the FDH is 0.05-0.5                  mg/ml. The reaction is performed at room                  temperature, and the reaction time is 12-48                  hours. The centrifugation speed of the ZIF-8-FDH                  suspension is 5000-12000 revolutions per minute                  (rpm) and the centrifugation time is 10-30                  minutes. The enzyme activity of FDH in the                  ZIF-8-FDH nano hybrid material is maintained at                  80% or more compared with the free enzyme, and                  the tolerance to the environment is                  improved.

TI Preparation of composite membrane for separation            of carbon dioxide from methane, involves forming            polymer membrane from polymer as support layer by            contacting polymer in solvent, and fabricating            polysaccharide by film casting surface layer on support            layer.
AU IBRAHIM N A
   MORADIHAMEDANI P
   YUSOF N A
AE UNIV PUTRA MALAYSIA (UYPM-C)
GA 2022954869
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation (500) of a composite membrane                involves forming (505) a polymer membrane from a                polymer as a support layer by contacting and                incorporating the polymer in a solvent, and                fabricating (510) a polysaccharide as a surface                layer by film casting the surface layer on the                support layer. The polymer is a thermoplastic                polymer and the polysaccharide is a linear                polysaccharide. The fabricated polysaccharide is                dip-coated onto an outer surface of the polymer                membrane as a coating layer. The gas purification                involves separating carbon dioxide from                methane.
   USE - Preparation of composite membrane (claimed)                for separation of carbon dioxide from                methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides composite membrane                exhibiting excellent separation factors.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermoplastic                  polymer is polysulfone. The linear polysaccharide                  is chitosan. Preferred Composition: 3 wt.% of                  chitosan and 20 wt.% of polysulfone is utilized                  to form the composite membrane. PN CN112029104-A; CN112029104-B
TI Preparing metal-organic framework film for gas            separation, involves drying membrane substrate, then            loading metal-organic framework material, and heat            treating metal-organic framework material-loaded            carrier with vapor formed by ligand.
AU YANG W
   ZHAO M
   BAN Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2020C40547
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-organic framework film                involves: (1) washing a membrane substrate with a                solvent and drying at 20-30 degrees C; (2) loading                a metal-organic framework material on the membrane                substrate; and (3) heat treating the metal-organic                framework material-loaded carrier with vapor formed                by ligand in a closed space.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                metal-organic framework film, which is useful for                gas separation, where separated gas is                hydrogen/carbon dioxide, hydrogen/nitrogen,                hydrogen/methane, hydrogen/propane,                hydrogen/butane, carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon                dioxide/methane, ethylene/ethane or propene/propane                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is solvent-free, simple and easy                to implement; has high repeatability; prepares new                hybrid film from more than one metal-organic                framework material; simultaneously realizes                chemical composition and pore channel composite                hybridization between a parent or seed material and                an epitaxial nascent material; and enables simple                and effective elimination of metal-organic                framework defects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic                  framework material is MOF-74-M, ZIF-8, ZIF-67,                  ZIF-4, HKUST-1, MOP-1 or MOF-5. The metal (M) is                  zinc, magnesium, manganese, cobalt, nickel or                  iron. The substrate is an aluminum oxide                  substrate, a stainless steel substrate, an                  anodized aluminum oxide substrate, a silicon                  oxide substrate, a zirconium oxide substrate, a                  titanium oxide substrate, a zinc oxide substrate                  or a foamed nickel substrate. The loading amount                  of the metal-organic framework material is 3-400                  g/m2. The mass ratio of the ligand to the                  metal-organic framework material is 15:1-600:1.                  Preferred Property: The particle size of the                  metal-organic framework material is 10-100000                  nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is water,                  a hydrocarbon solvent, a halogenated hydrocarbon                  solvent, an alcohol solvent, a                  nitrogen-containing organic solvent, a                  sulfur-containing organic solvent, an ester                  organic solvent, a phenolic organic solvent, an                  ether organic solvent or a ketone organic                  solvent. The ligand is an imidazole ligand or a                  carboxylic acid ligand, preferably imidazole,                  2-methylimidazole, benzimidazole,                  2-nitroimidazole, 2-ethylimidazole, pyrazine,                  terephthalic acid and 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic                  acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: When the organic ligand in                  the metal-framework material is the same as the                  ligand, a pure phase metal-organic framework film                  is obtained. When the organic ligand in the                  metal-framework material is different from the                  ligand, a hybrid metal-organic framework film is                  obtained. The loading step involves loading                  powder or sol of the metal-organic framework                  material onto the substrate by manual rubbing                  method, spraying method, spin coating method or                  hot pressing method, where the sol is a metal                  framework material dissolved in an organic                  solvent. Preferred Conditions: The drying step is                  carried out at 20-200 degrees C for 120-1440                  minutes. The heat treating step is carried out at                  80-300 degrees C for 120-14400 minutes.

TI Titanum-organic gels/aerogels useful in photo-induced water splitting, carbon dioxide reduction, and chemical cracking, comprises titanium-oxo clusters, organic ligand that coordinates with the cluster, and solvent.
AU KIM J G

   PARK K
   KO Y M
   HA J
   KIM B
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 2020B8326L
AB    NOVELTY - Titanum-organic gels (TOG) comprises titanium-oxo clusters; organic ligand that coordinates with the cluster; and solvent.
   USE - The TOG/TOA are useful in photo-induced water splitting, carbon dioxide reduction, and chemical cracking.
   ADVANTAGE - The TOG/TOA can reversibly converting to a gel and a sol state according to thermal and mechanical stimulation, and has photocatalytic properties or photochromic properties by photo-oxidation reduction in response to ultraviolet irradiation and air exposure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing titanium-organic gel comprising heating composition containing titanium-oxo cluster, organic ligand coordinating to the cluster, and solvent at greater than or equal to 40 degrees C;
   (2) titanum-organic aerogels (TOA) comprising titanium-oxo clusters, organic ligand that coordinates with the cluster, and solvent; and
   (3) producing TOA comprising performing carbon dioxide super-critical point dry (CPD) treatment on gelatinous product of composition containing titanium-oxo cluster, organic ligand coordinating to the cluster, and solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The TOG/TOA is capable of reversible gel-sol conversion by mechanical and thermal stimulation, and is reversible by photo-oxidation reduction due to UV irradiation and air exposure. The titanium-oxo cluster is substituted benzene-based compound (I). The TOG further comprises transition metals other than titanium. The TOA has hierarchical porosity, and Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of 500-900 m2/g; and is amorphous. Preferred Method: The heat treatment is carried out at 80-120 degrees C. The preparation of TOA further comprises obtaining gelated product through heat treatment of the composition at greater than or equal to 40 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: (I) is 4-aminobenzoate, benzenedicarboxylate and/or 4-t-butyl benzoate. The titanium-oxo cluster includes hexatianium hexaoxy(4-t-butyl benzoate) isopropoxide (Ti6O6(t-BBZ)10(OiPr)2) tianium hexaoxyhexa(4-t-butyl benzoate) isopropoxide (Ti6O6(t-BBZ)6(OiPr)6), and/or tianium(4-t-butyl benzoate) (Ti8O8(t-BBZ)16) (preferred). The organic ligand is benzene-based compound (II). The organic ligand has 1-4 -COOR. The solvent includes N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), N,N-diethylformamide (DEF), N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMA) and/or N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), preferably dimethylformamide and DMA. The organic ligand is 1,2,4,5-benzene tetracarboxylate. The TOG/TOA is formed from composition containing titanium-oxo cluster, organic ligand coordinating to the cluster, and solvent. The composition comprises titanium-oxo cluster and organic ligand in molar ratio of 1:1-1:15.R1-R6, R7-R12 = H, linear or branched alkyl, alkoxy, hydroxy, and/or coordination functional group i.e. -COOR, -COO-, -NH2, -NO2, -X, -SO3H, -PO3H and -PO(OH)2;R = H or alkyl; andX = halo.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111758869-A
TI Preparing instant Chrysanthemum powder for drink by performing primary and secondary supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on Chrysanthemum, dewaxing water-soluble extract, adding wall material, homogenizing, emulsifying and spray-drying.
AU CHEN J
   WENG S
   LI M
   WEI M
AE SHANGHAI XUMEI FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   KAIFENG LIJIA FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (KAIF-Non-standard)
GA 2020A4138F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing instant Chrysanthemum powder, involves taking Chrysanthemum, performing primary supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the Chrysanthemum, performing secondary supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain oil-soluble Chrysanthemum extract and water-soluble Chrysanthemum extract, dewaxing the water-soluble Chrysanthemum extract to obtain water-soluble flavoring components, using the water-soluble flavoring components as solvent and the oil-soluble Chrysanthemum extract as core material, adding or not adding wall material, homogenizing and emulsifying to obtain homogeneous liquid, and spray-drying the homogeneous liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing instant Chrysanthemum powder used for drink (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the residual organic solvents of the traditional process, shortens the production cycle, reduces oxidation of aroma components and destruction of heat-sensitive substances, and improves the utilization rate of Chrysanthemum raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) an instant Chrysanthemum powder prepared by the above method; and (2) a drink comprising the instant Chrysanthemum powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method of dewaxing treatment involves stirring and heating the water-soluble Chrysanthemum extract at 50-70 degrees C, completely liquefying the wax in the water-soluble Chrysanthemum extract, separating oil and water, and removing the lower aqueous phase to obtain the water-soluble flavoring component. Preferred Conditions: The primary supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at a extraction pressure of 12.5-20 MPa and extraction temperature of 30-45 degrees C. The secondary supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at a extraction pressure of 25-40 MPa and extraction temperature of 40-70 degrees C. The homogenizing and emulsifying process is carried out at 25-80 degrees C and homogenization pressure of 20-50 MPa. The spray-drying process is carried out at an inlet air temperature of 150-240 degrees C and outlet air temperature of 60-100 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the water-soluble flavoring component, the oil-soluble Chrysanthemum extract and the wall material during the homogeneous emulsification process is (40-70):(1-30):(5-35). Preferred Components: The wall material comprises maltodextrin, cyclodextrin, modified starch, maltose syrup, corn syrup, gelatin and/or Arabic gum.

PN CN111620844-A; CN111620844-B
TI Preparing alpha-acetyl-gamma-butyrolactone by            subjecting gamma-butyrolactone, acetate compound and            sodium alkoxide compound to acylation reaction, and            contacting with carbon dioxide gas in presence of water            to cause neutralization reaction.
AU GUO G
   LI Q
   YUAN Y
   XIA Y
   WAN T
   SI Y
AE JIANGXI TIANXIN PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI BONASAIEN PHARM R & D CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202087731C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alpha -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone                involves: (1) subjecting gamma -butyrolactone, an                acetate compound (I) and a sodium alkoxide compound                (II) to acylation reaction to a obtain material                containing alpha -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone                sodium salt; and (2) contacting the material                containing alpha -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone                sodium salt with carbon dioxide gas in the presence                of water to cause neutralization reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alpha                -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables safe preparation of alpha                -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone with high                yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alpha -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone                involves: (1) subjecting gamma -butyrolactone, an                acetate compound of formula: H3CCOOR1 (I) and a                sodium alkoxide compound of formula: R2ONa (II) to                acylation reaction to a obtain material containing                alpha -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone sodium salt;                and (2) contacting the material containing alpha                -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone sodium salt with                carbon dioxide gas in the presence of water to                cause neutralization reaction.
   R1, R2 = 1-4C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process of the                  acylation reaction, the compound (I) and an                  alcohol compound of formula: R1OH (III) or R2OH                  (IV) generated during the acylation reaction are                  distilled and recovered. The method further                  involves (3) separating the crude alpha -acetyl-                  gamma -butyrolactone in step (2) by vacuum                  distillation to obtain alpha -acetyl- gamma                  -butyrolactone. Preferred Conditions: In step                  (1), the temperature of the acylation reaction is                  60-100 degrees C, preferably 80-90 degrees C. The                  pressure of the acylation reaction is 0.01-0.6                  MPa, preferably 0.1-0.3 MPa. The acylation                  reaction time is 1-30 hours, preferably 3-15                  hours. In step (2), the pressure of carbon                  dioxide gas is 0.01-5 MPa, preferably 0.01-1 MPa.                  The temperature of the neutralization reaction is                  -10 degrees C to 80 degrees C, preferably 5-35                  degrees C. The neutralization reaction time is                  0.1-10 hours, preferably 0.5-5 hours. In step                  (3), the vacuum degree of the vacuum distillation                  is less than 1000 Pa. Preferred Components: In                  step (1), the molar ratio of gamma                  -butyrolactone, compound (I) and compound (II) is                  1:(2-15):(0.5-2), preferably 1:(3-12):(1-1.5). In                  step (2), the molar ratio of water to compound                  (II) in step (1) is (0.3-1):1, preferably                  (0.55-1):1.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, R2 = 1-2C alkyl.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing a compound (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of the compound (I) under mild reaction condition with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing an aryl carboxylic acid compound of formula (I) involves dissolving 1 equivalent arylboronic acid compound of formula (II), 1-2 equivalents alkoxide and 0.03-0.05 equivalent copper catalyst in an organic solvent, sealing a reaction system under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, stirring and reacting at 25-120 degrees C for 12-36 hours, and acidifying by adding an inorganic acid.
   Ar1 = aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method involves initially dissolving the compound (II), alkoxide and copper catalyst in the organic solvent under an inert atmosphere, then ventilating, sealing, stirring and reacting at 70 degrees C for 24 hours under carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding 1 mol/l hydrochloric acid for acidification after the reaction is completed, extracting with a neutral oxygen-containing extractant, washing with salt water, collecting the organic phase, concentrating under reduced pressure and isolating the product by using column chromatography. Preferred Components: The aryl group in the compound (II) is optionally substituted phenyl as shown in formula (III). The compound (II) is a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon boronic acid or a benzoheterocyclic boronic acid, preferably naphthalen-2-ylboranediol, anthracen-9-ylboranediol, 2H-1,3-benzodioxol-5-ylboranediol, 1H-indol-5-ylboranediol and quinolin-6-ylboranediol. The concentration of the arylboronic acid in the organic solvent is 0.1-2 mol/l. The copper catalyst is chloro(1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)imidazol-2-ylidene)copper(I). The alkoxide is an alkali metal salt of 1-4C alcohol. The organic solvent is N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran or acetonitrile.R = substituents on phenyl, where the substituents are H, halo, alkyl, acyl, ester, cyano, carboxy, carboxyalkyl, amino, amido, hydroxy, hydroxyalkyl, alkylthio, alkoxy or phenyl.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ar1 = aryl, preferably phenyl, fused ring aryl, biphenyl aryl, fluorenyl, furyl, thienyl, pyridyl, benzofuryl, benzothienyl or quinolinyl, where the aryl does not contain substituents or contain substituents including halo alkyl, acyl, ester, cyano, carboxy, carboxyalkyl, amino, amido, hydroxy, hydroxyalkyl, alkylthio or alkoxy.

PN CN110548488-A; CN110548488-B
TI Preparing biochar-based nitrogen-rich composite material to adsorb carbon dioxide, by pyrolyzing biomass, mixing metal organic framework material precursor solution with biochar and synthesizing metal framework material on biochar surface.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHANG J
   HUANG D
   SHAO J
   ZHANG S
   WANG X
   YANG H
   CHEN H
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019A7007S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing biochar-based nitrogen-rich composite material, involves (a) pyrolyzing a biomass to obtain biochar, (b) mixing metal organic framework material precursor solution comprising a metal salt solution obtained by dissolving metal salt in a solvent and an organic ligand solution obtained by dissolving organic ligand in the solvent, where the organic ligand contains a nitrogen element with the biochar to obtain a mixed solution, and (c) reacting the mixed solution obtained in step (b) under agitation conditions to synthesizes a metal organic framework material in situ on the surface of biochar, separating solid-liquid, washing the obtained solid, and drying to obtain the composite material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biochar-based nitrogen-rich composite material used to adsorb carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the carbon dioxide adsorption effect of the composite material, improves low specific surface area and nitrogen content, and solves an insufficient hardness of metal-organic framework for gas adsorption, and insufficient retention of intermolecular adsorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves pyrolzing at 400-800 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours under nitrogen, carbon dioxide or water vapor at a gas flow of 50-200 ml/minute, and heating at a rate of 5-20 degrees C/ ml/minute. Preferred Conditions: In step (c), the temperature is increased at a rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute, the reaction temperature is 30-200 degrees C, the reaction time is 3-24 hours, and the stirring rate is 100-1000 rpm. Preferred Properties: The biomass is granular, and has a particle size of 1-10 mm. Preferred Components: The biomass comprises bamboo dust, straw and/or wood dust.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt comprises zinc nitrate hexahydrate and/or cobalt nitrate hexahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand comprises is 2-methylimidazole and/or benzimidazole. The solvent comprises ethanol, methanol and N,N-dimethylformamide.

AU SHANG J
   ZHANG Y
AE SHANG J (SHAN-Individual)
GA 201982559D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing waste liquid involves placing waste liquid generated in the semiconductor grinding process in flocculating agent, settling, filtering to obtain processed grinding waste liquid, adding calcium hydroxide to the waste liquid, adjusting pH value, completely reacting, sedimenting, filtering to obtain pickling waste liquid, processing, charging heavy metal ion scavenger into processed pickling waste liquid, completely reacting, sedimenting the precipitate, filtering to obtain pickling waste liquid, processing, mixing the processed grinding waste liquid with pickling waste liquid, introducing carbon dioxide into the waste liquid, adjusting pH value to 5-6, completely reacting, sedimenting the mixture, filtering to obtain processed mixed waste liquid, adding nitric acid to the processed mixed waste liquid, removing carbon dioxide, heating, cooling, adding aqueous ammonia, evaporating and concentrating to obtain ammonium nitrate concentrate.

PN WO2019121873-A1; EP3694955-A1; EP3694955-B1; ES2881963-T3
TI Producing seal in soil material through injection device by reacting gaseous carbon dioxide and aqueous solution containing water glass to form gel.
AU WEISS M
   LIND J
   HENSEL P
AE WOELLNER GMBH (WOEL-Non-standard)
   WOELLNER GMBH (WOEL-Non-standard)
GA 201955469J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a seal in a soil material through an injection device involves reacting gaseous carbon dioxide and an aqueous solution containing water glass to form a gel.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a seal in a soil material, forming a sealing sole in an excavation and landfill and preventing entry or passage of groundwater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly, adopts no chemical such as metal salts in the form of a sodium aluminate solution in the soil, provides soft gel, preferably an elastic soft gel that is groundwater neutral and has a less solids content compared with hard gels, avoids associated problems of the transition from metal cations present in reactive into the groundwater or the surrounding soil material and disadvantages caused by hyperacidity or excessive alkalization and provides the seal with excellent long-term stability due to the formation of improved networks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of gaseous carbon dioxide as a reactive for producing the seal from the aqueous solution by injection in the soil material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves introducing gaseous carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution before and/or in the injection device. The method involves adjusting pH of a mixture of gaseous carbon dioxide and the aqueous solution to 6.5-10.5. The method involves introducing gaseous carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution continuously. The method involves introducing gaseous carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution laterally through an annular die and/or in countercurrent. The method involves compressing the aqueous solution by at least one pumping means. The mixture of gaseous carbon dioxide and the aqueous solution is injected into the soil material. Preferred composition: The aqueous solution comprises at least one water glass having a molar modulus of 2.5-4. The aqueous solution comprises at least one water glass comprising 0.5-5 wt.% silicon dioxide based on the total amount of the aqueous solution. The aqueous solution comprises at least one water glass comprising 1-6 wt.% solids based on the total amount of the aqueous solution.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109019593-A
TI Preparing waste ion exchange resin-based activated carbon used for adsorbing carbon dioxide, involves immersing waste ion exchange resin in anhydrous lower alcohol, and removing excess anhydrous lower alcohol for pickling.
AU YU Q
   LIU K
   WEI M
   QIN Q
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN CN (UNEN-C)
GA 2018A5215P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing waste ion exchange resin-based activated carbon involves immersing the waste ion exchange resin in an anhydrous lower alcohol, and removing the excess anhydrous lower alcohol for pickling, and then washing with water until neutral and drying to obtain a pretreated waste ion exchange resin. The pretreated waste ion exchange resin is heated and carbonized under a protective gas atmosphere to obtain a carbonized product. The carbonized product is placed in a potassium hydroxide solution for carrying out impregnation. The impregnated carbonized product is taken out. The obtained mixture is placed again in a protective gas atmosphere to be heated and activated, cooled, washed and dried to obtain waste ion exchange resin-based activated carbon.
   USE - Method for preparing waste ion exchange resin-based activated carbon used for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare waste ion exchange resin-based activated carbon in a cost-effective manner, where the waste ion exchange resin-based activated carbon ensures good carbon dioxide adsorption performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The pickling acid is 3-8% hydrochloric acid, preferably 5% hydrochloric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anhydrous lower alcohol is anhydrous ethanol, anhydrous methanol or anhydrous propanol.

PT P
PN CN108620018-A
TI Preparing high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent using dredging tank sludge comprises e.g. grinding dehydrated mud tank precipitate, adding hydrochloric acid-nitric acid mixture, grinding and mixing with solid phase of lithium nitrate.
AU WANG J
   YANG Y
   ZHANG T
   NING P
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201880006J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent using dredged pond bottom mud comprises (i) taking dehydrated mud tank precipitate, repeatedly filtering and washing for 3-5 times with deionized water, removing impurities, drying, and grinding into powder, (ii) adding 20-30 ml acid mixture per 1 g of the step (i) powder, adding the powder to the hydrochloric acid-nitric acid mixture, heating at 60-100 degrees C, condensing and refluxing for more than 12 hours, removing the supernatant, centrifuging and cleaning with deionized water until the supernatant is neutral, drying, and cooling and grinding to obtain sample containing silicon dioxide, and (iii) mixing the sample with the solid phase of lithium nitrate and drying, cooling and grinding, sintering, cooling, and grinding.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent using dredging tank sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; and realizes purpose of waste treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrochloric acid-nitric acid mixture is prepared by mixing hydrochloric acid and nitric acid in a ratio of 1:1-4:1 by volume. The SiO2-containing sample is mixed with LiNO3 in a molar ratio of SiO2 to LiNO3 of 1:1-1:10. The sintering process is carried out at 650-850 degrees C for more than 4 hours.

   HANEBUTH M

AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 201854842V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hydrocarbon (22) involves                producing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide under                addition of oxygen (5) in a reaction unit (C1),                carrying out fermentation using the carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen (4) in a                reaction unit (C2), providing the resulting biogas                (12) and oxygen and hydrogen produced by an                electrolyzer (3) to a biogas plant (11) as starting                materials into the units (C1) and (C2).
   USE - Preparation of hydrocarbon e.g. ethanol,                methanol, butyrate, formic acid, formate, acetyl                complex, coenzyme A activated acetate, acetone,                butanol, hexanol, propanol, 2,3-butanoldiol and                1,3-propanodiol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                preparation of hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus, which has reaction units (C1) and (C2),                biogas plant and the electrolyzer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show schematic views of the                apparatus for producing hydrocarbons.
   Electrolyzer (3)
   Hydrogen (4)
   Oxygen (5)
   Biogas plant (11)
   Biogas (12)
   Reaction units (C1,C2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reaction unit (C1)                  is chosen from an adiabatic fixed bed reactor, a                  honeycomb reactor, a fluidized bed reactor, and a                  tube bundle reactor. Preferred Components: An                  educt of the unit (C2) contains less than 1000                  ppmv oxygen and less than 1 vol.% methane. A                  buffer memory for the oxygen and hydrogen is used                  in the process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: A catalyst comprising                  nickel, cobalt, zinc, copper, and/or magnesium,                  titanium, platinum and/or a rare earth element                  chosen from cerium, yttrium or lanthanum, is                  present in the unit (C1).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the unit (C1),                  reforming is carried out autothermally using a                  reverse water gas shift reaction reactor, a steam                  reformer, a dry reformer or a gasifier such that                  the outlet temperature is 550-1000 degrees C,                  preferably 580-850 degrees C. The electrolyzer                  used in the process is supplied with power using                  a regenerative electrical energy unit. In the                  unit (C2), anaerobic gas fermentation using                  microorganisms chosen from Clostridium                  ljungdahlii, C.autoethanogenum, C.ragsdalei,                  C.carboxidivorans, Moorella thermoacetica,                  C.coskatti, M.thermoautotrophica, Acetobacterium                  woodii, and their co-cultures, is carried out.                  Preferred Composition: At least 60-80% oxygen                  generated from the electrolyzer is used in the                  process. The molar ratio of water to oxygen in                  the unit (C1) is 1.8-3.8. The gas from the unit                  (C2) contains 20-80% hydrogen.

   YU J
   LI Z
AE SHENZHEN INST ADVANCED TECHNOLOGY (CAAT-C)
GA 201839379W
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped diamond                electrode comprises a substrate. The substrate is                provided with the nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped                diamond film layer having flat structure layer                which is set at the surface of the substrate and                arranged with bump array structure on the surface                of the flat structure layer.
   USE - The electrode is useful in electrocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode has high hydrogen evolution                potential, strong electro-catalysis activity and                physical, chemical stability, very low resistance,                and high selectivity for catalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped diamond                electrode comprising providing substrate,                subjecting substrate to sand blasting treatment,                ultrasonic treatment and seed treatment, etching                nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped diamond film layer                to obtain bump array structure, and depositing                nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped diamond film layer                on surface of the substrate to obtain                electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped diamond                electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bump array                  structure is vertically arranged on the flat                  structure layer. The bump array is prismatic,                  acicular or spherical. The bump array is                  needle-shaped or spherical having curvature 1-25                  nm radius. The thickness of the flat structure                  layer is 100 nm to 5 mu m, and the height of the                  bump array is 50 nm to 2.5 mu m. Preferred                  Method: The deposition method specifically                  comprises placing material into hot wire chemical                  vapor deposition apparatus, introducing reaction                  gas comprising methane, hydrogen,                  trimethylborane, nitrogen, and argon filled with                  cyclopentadienyl nickel. In the process adopting                  coupling plasma etching method for etching                  nickel-boron-nitrogen co-doped diamond film                  layer, the etching process specifically comprises                  filling gas comprising hydrogen, argon, oxygen,                  helium, nitrogen, carbon tetra fluoride and/or                  sulfur hexafluoride at total flow rate of 50-300                  sccm, maintaining etching pressure at 0.5-10 Pa,                  power supply at 600-3500 W, etching power at                  50-350 W and etching for 0.5-10 hours. The                  etching process adopts electron cyclotron                  resonance microwave plasma chemical vapor                  deposition etching, the gas is hydrogen or                  mixture of hydrogen and argon gas, the etching                  gas pressure is 0.5-1.5 Pa, the etching bias is                  50- 250 V and the etch bias is 50-150 mA, and the                  etching time is 0.5-10 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally friendly rapid freezing type insecticide comprises liquid carbon dioxide, volatile organic solvent and Dalmatian pellitory extract. The insecticide primarily controls pests by rapidly freezing pests in volatile organic solvent, and secondarily controls pests by Dalmatian pellitory extract. The Dalmatian pellitory extract is obtained by collecting Dalmatian pellitory flower sample from May to June, cutting sample in predetermined length, drying, and impregnating dried Dalmatian pellitory in ethyl alcohol.

   ADVANTAGE - The insecticide is natural.

The Dalmatian pellitory extract is obtained by collecting Dalmatian pellitory flower sample from May to June, cutting sample in predetermined length, drying, and impregnating dried Dalmatian pellitory in ethyl alcohol in weight ratio of 1:1 for 24 plus minus 2 hours.

PN WO2018012536-A1; JP2019147740-A
TI Composition useful for controlling insect pest e.g. Lepidoptera pests, winged pests, mites, Hymenoptera pests and mites, comprises 2,3,5,6-tetrafluorobenzyl 2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate compound and liquefied carbon dioxide.
AU TANAKA T
   TANAKA Y
   YOSHIMURA K
AE SC ENVIRONMENTAL SCI CO LTD (ENVI-Non-standard)
   NIPPON EKITAN CORP (NIEK-Non-standard)
   SUMIKA ENVIRONMENTAL SCI CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   NIPPON EKITAN CORP (NIEK-Non-standard)
GA 2018063480
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a 2,3,5,6-tetrafluorobenzyl 2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate compound (I) and liquefied carbon dioxide.
   USE - The composition is useful for controlling insect pest, where the pest is inhabited in a tropical region (all claimed). The pest is chosen from Lepidoptera pests e.g. Indian mealmoth, Tinea translucens, and Tineola bisselliella, winged pests e.g. Culex pipiens pallens, C.tritaeniorhynchus, and C.quinquefasciatus, mites e.g. Tyrophagus putrescentiae, Dermatophagoides farinae, and D.pteronyssinus, Hymenoptera pests e.g. Monomorium pharaonis, Formica fusca japonica, and Ochetellus glaber, and Isoptera pests e.g. Reticulitermes speratus, Coptotermes formosanus, and Coptotermes gestroi.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is convenient and safe even under storage and transportation at environmental temperature of 30 degrees C or more, and is used without cooling and stirring.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composition comprises a 2,3,5,6-tetrafluorobenzyl 2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate compound of formula (I) and liquefied carbon dioxide.
   R1, R2= H, methyl, trifluoromethyl or Cl;and
   R3=H, methyl or methoxymethyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 0.001-10 weight/weight% ester compound (I).
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide; Acaricide. Test for controlling insect (e.g. Culex pipiens pallens) was conducted using the composition (spraying, 0.5 g). The composition resulted in insect mortality rate of 100%, after 24 hours.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition is applied by spraying at a dosage of 0.01-10 g/m2.
   EXAMPLE - 0.01 pt. wt. Metofluthrin was placed in a pressure-resistant container, and 99.99 pts. wt. liquefied carbonic acid gas was injected from the nozzle side after installing the pressure valve in the pressure-resistant container to obtain a composition.

TI Organic semiconductor device supercritical            processing method involves treating carbon dioxide with            substance in supercritical state to obtain carbon            dioxide in supercritical state, and subjecting organic            semiconductor device to impurity extraction by            supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU LIU K
   LI L
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV PEKING SHENZHEN GRADUATE SCHOOL (UYPK-C)
GA 202210855R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing (10) an organic                semiconductor device, a substance and carbon                dioxide. The supercritical treatment is performed                (20) on the substance, so that the substance is in                a supercritical state, where the critical                temperature of the substance is lower than the                critical temperature of the carbon dioxide. The                carbon dioxide is treated (30) with the substance                in a supercritical state to obtain carbon dioxide                in a supercritical state, where the substance is a                carbon tetrafluoride or nitrogen. The organic                semiconductor device is subjected to impurity                extraction (40) by supercritical carbon dioxide and                the material of the organic material structure is                polymethyl methacrylate.
   USE - Organic semiconductor device supercritical                processing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic material structure exhibits                electrical properties in the organic semiconductor                device, which can be further close to the ideal                model, and the influence of the impurity is                reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the organic semiconductor device supercritical                processing process. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)
   Step for providing organic semiconductor                device (10)
   Step for performing supercritical treatment                on substance (20)
   Step for treating carbon dioxide with                substance in supercritical state to obtain carbon                dioxide in supercritical state (30)
   Step for subjecting organic semiconductor                device to impurity extraction by supercritical                carbon dioxide (40)

PT P
PN WO2021148262-A1; CN115210204-A
TI Preparing methanol by introducing carbon dioxide            and hydrogen containing stream in methanol synthesis            unit, condensing methanol- and water-enriched crude            methanol stream, expanding, and separating high low-            and high-boiler streams.
AU VICARI M
   BRITZIUS S
   REINING S
   KATZ T
   GEIGER T
   MEYER G
   MAIER G
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202186949K
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol is prepared by (a) converting carbon                dioxide and hydrogen containing stream (I) in a                methanol synthesis unit (A) at 150-300 degrees C                and a pressure of 3-10 MPa (absolute) in the                presence of methanol synthesis catalyst to a                reaction mixture containing methanol, water, carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, dimethyl ether,                and methane, and condensing methanol- and                water-enriched crude methanol stream (II); (b)                expanding crude methanol stream (II) to a pressure                of 0.1-2 MPa (absolute) to obtain expansion gas                (IV) comprising carbon dioxide and methane and                degassed crude methanol stream (V) enriched with                methanol and water; (c) separating carbon dioxide-                and dimethyl ether-comprising low boiler stream                (VI) to obtain methanol- and water-enriched bottom                stream (VII); and (d) separating water-containing                high boiler stream (VIII) from bottom stream (VII)                in a further distillation apparatus (D) to obtain                methanol by distillation as stream (IX).
   USE - Preparation of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids emission of carbon dioxide                while reusing in the preparation of methanol. This                process is based on the continuously operated                methanol synthesis by a low-pressure process. The                process easily uses carbon dioxide of different                sources and qualities regarding its concentration                and possible accompanying compounds. Furthermore,                the process is to be easily performable and is to                largely use the apparatuses and interconnections in                established methanol synthesis processes customary                in the industry and which are based on the use of                synthesis gas, so that such plants can easily be                modified.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methanol is prepared by (a) converting carbon                dioxide and hydrogen containing stream (I) in a                methanol synthesis unit (A) at 150-300 degrees C                and a pressure of 3-10 MPa (absolute) in the                presence of methanol synthesis catalyst to a                reaction mixture containing methanol, water, carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, dimethyl ether,                and methane, condensing methanol- and                water-enriched crude methanol stream (II) out of                the reaction mixture, and conducting crude methanol                stream (II) and gaseous stream (III) comprising                carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and                methane out of the methanol synthesis unit (A); (b)                expanding crude methanol stream (II) in expansion                unit (B) to a pressure of 0.1-2 MPa (absolute), and                obtaining an expansion gas (IV) comprising carbon                dioxide and methane, and a degassed crude methanol                stream (V) enriched with methanol and water; (c)                separating carbon dioxide- and dimethyl                ether-comprising low boiler stream (VI) by                distillation from degassed crude methanol stream                (V) in a distillation apparatus (C), and obtaining                a methanol- and water-enriched bottom stream (VII);                (d) separating water-containing high boiler stream                (VIII) from the bottom stream (VII) in a further                distillation apparatus (D), and obtaining methanol                by distillation as stream (IX); (e) feeding carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, dimethyl ether, and                methane components of value in stream (III) and in                at least one of the two streams (IV) and (VI) to a                combustion unit (E) and combusting with supply of                an oxygenous gas (X) having an oxygen content of                30-100 vol.%, forming carbon dioxide-containing                flue gas (XI); (f) separating carbon                dioxide-enriched stream (XIII) from the carbon                dioxide-containing flue gas (XI) in a carbon                dioxide recovery unit (F) to form an off-gas stream                (XII); (g) recycling carbon dioxide-enriched stream                (XIII) separated off in the carbon dioxide recovery                unit (F) from stage (f) to the methanol synthesis                unit (A) as a carbon dioxide containing source of                stream (I); (h) supplying hydrogen feedstock (XIV)                to methanol synthesis unit (A) as a hydrogen                containing source of stream (I); and (i) supplying                carbon dioxide feedstock (XV) to the carbon                dioxide-containing flue gas (XI) and/or to the                methanol synthesis unit (A) as a carbon dioxide                containing source of stream (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: Copper- and                  zinc-containing heterogeneous catalyst is used as                  methanol synthesis catalyst in the methanol                  synthesis unit (A) in stage (a). Basic solvent                  used is an aqueous solution of an organic amine.                  Organic amine is monoethanolamine, piperazine,                  2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, triethylenediamine,                  N-methyldiethanolamine, or                  tert.-butylaminoethoxyethanol. Preferred Devices:                  Methanol synthesis unit (A) in stage (a)                  comprises a compressor for compression of stream                  (I), reactor for conversion of the compressed                  stream (I), condenser for condensing out stream                  (II), and conduit for recycling of uncondensed                  gas to the reactor. Preferred Processes: In stage                  (e), carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, dimethyl                  ether, and methane components of value in streams                  (III), (IV), and (VI) are fed to the combustion                  unit (E). Combustion unit (E) in stage (e) is                  supplied with an oxygenous gas (X) having an                  oxygen content of 90-100 vol.%. Combustion unit                  (E) in stage (e) comprises a combustion chamber                  and a condenser; water is condensed out of the                  combustion gas obtained in the combustion chamber                  in the condenser and conducted out of the                  combustion unit (E) as stream (XVI); and                  remaining gaseous stream constitutes the carbon                  dioxide-containing flue gas (XI). In carbon                  dioxide recovery unit (F) in stage (f), carbon                  dioxide is absorbed from the carbon                  dioxide-containing flue gas (XI) in an absorber                  in a basic solvent to form the off-gas stream                  (XII); carbon dioxide-enriched stream (XIII) is                  released from the carbon dioxide-laden solvent in                  a desorber; and carbon dioxide-depleted solvent                  is returned to the absorber. Carbon                  dioxide-enriched stream (XIII) from stage (f)                  comprises oxygen, and stream (XIII), before being                  recycled to methanol synthesis unit (A), is                  catalytically hydrogenated to deplete the oxygen.                  Carbon dioxide feedstock (XV) containing 5-95                  vol.% carbon dioxide based on the gaseous                  feedstock is supplied to the carbon                  dioxide-containing flue gas (XI). Carbon dioxide                  feedstock (XV) containing 95-100 vol.% carbon                  dioxide based on the gaseous feedstock is                  supplied to the methanol synthesis unit (A).                  About 50-100% of the carbon bound in methanol                  (IX) is based on carbon dioxide supplied by                  carbon dioxide feedstock (XV). Before stream                  (III) is fed to the combustion unit (E), hydrogen                  is separated off in a hydrogen recovery unit (G)                  and recycled to the methanol synthesis unit (A)                  of stage (a). Hydrogen is separated off by                  pressure swing adsorption in the hydrogen                  recovery unit (G).
   EXAMPLE - The example does not provide sufficient                details on the preparation of methanol, so it does                not support the claims.

PT P
PN CN113479922-A
TI Water washing-plasma melting recycling device for            recycling waste incineration fly ash, has solid-liquid            stirring tank, cleaning water tank, plasma melting            furnace, water washing tower, desulfurization tower,            and waste water collection tank.
AU LI Y
   TIAN J
   AI Y
   ZHANG B
   YOU R
   CHEN L
   JIANG Z
   YAN S
AE JIANGSU TIANYING ENVIRONMENTAL ENERGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU TIANYING PLASMA TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8696C
AB    NOVELTY - A water washing-plasma melting waste                incineration fly ash recycling device has 1                solid-liquid stirring tank, 2 cleaning water tank,                3 drier, 4 granulator, 5 plasma melting furnace, 6                power wave washing system, 8 water washing tower, 9                desulfurization tower, 10 carbon dioxide absorption                tower, 11 chimney, 12 diaphragm filter, 13 waste                water collection tank, 14 coagulation sedimentation                system, 16 intermediate water tank, 17 calcium                carbonate dehydrator, 18 gypsum dehydrator, 19 salt                water tank, 20 filtration system, 21 nanofiltration                membrane system, 22 bipolar membrane system, 23                liquid alkali storage tank, and 24 hydrochloric                acid storage tank.
   USE - Water washing-plasma melting recycling device                used for recycling waste incineration fly ash                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The water washing-plasma melting recycling                device realizes use of calcium ions in fly ash to                fix sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide, and realizes                resource utilization of large number of materials                in fly ash and effect of using waste to cure                waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A water washing-plasma melting waste                incineration fly ash recycling device has                solid-liquid stirring tank, cleaning water tank,                drier, granulator, plasma melting furnace, power                wave washing system, water washing tower,                desulfurization tower, carbon dioxide absorption                tower, chimney, diaphragm filter, waste water                collection tank, coagulation sedimentation system,                intermediate water tank, calcium carbonate                dehydrator, gypsum dehydrator, salt water tank,                filtration system, nanofiltration membrane system,                bipolar membrane system, liquid alkali storage                tank, and hydrochloric acid storage tank. The top                of the solid-liquid stirring tank is provided with                a water replenishment port. The solid-liquid                stirring tank is connected to the diaphragm filter                through a sludge pump. The diaphragm filter is                connected with the cleaning water tank through                cleaning water pump. The liquid outlet of the                diaphragm filter is connected with the waste water                collection tank. The bottom of the diaphragm filter                is provided with a filter cake outlet. The outlet                of the filter cake is connected with drier. The                drier is connected with the granulator. The drier                is connected with the cleaning water tank. The                granulator is connected with the plasma melting                furnace, power wave washing system, washing tower,                desulfurization tower, absorption tower and                chimney. The power wave washing system is connected                to the solid-liquid stirring tank through a branch                pipe. The wastewater collection tank, the                coagulation sedimentation system, and the                intermediate water tank are connected in sequence.                The bottom sludge of the coagulation sedimentation                system is connected to the solid-liquid stirring                tank through a pump. The intermediate water tank is                respectively connected with the desulfurization                tower and the carbon dioxide absorption tower. The                desulfurization tower is connected to the gypsum                dehydrator. The carbon dioxide absorption tower is                connected with the calcium carbonate dehydrator.                The gypsum dehydrator and calcium carbonate                dehydrator are respectively connected to the salt                water tank. The salt water tank, the filtration                system, the nanofiltration membrane system, and the                bipolar membrane system are connected in sequence.                The bipolar membrane system is respectively                connected with the liquid alkali storage tank and                the hydrochloric acid storage tank.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for recycling waste incineration fly ash of the                water washing-plasma melting waste incineration fly                ash recycling device, which involves:
   a. performing solid-liquid mixing fly ash and                cleaning liquid into solid-liquid stirring tank at                mass ratio of 1:1-6;
   b. stirring for 10-120 minutes to obtain a                mixed liquid and adjusting pH of the mixed liquid                to 8-13;
   c. cleaning fly ash by introducing the mixed                liquid into the diaphragm filter through feed pump                for dehydration and cleaning;
   d. discharging filtered filtrate into the                waste water collection tank and pre-pressing the                filter cake and turning on cleaning water                pump;
   e. using cleaning fluid to clean the                pre-pressed filter cake and squeezing the filter                cake for dehydration;
   f. collecting the cleaning liquid and                returning to the solid-liquid stirring tank to                reconstitute the fly ash;
   g. ash washing and drying to unload the                dehydrated filter cake and inputting into the drier                to obtain dry fly ash, where the moisture content                of the dry fly ash is 0-5% and the chlorine content                is less than 0.5%;
   h. discharging exhaust gas of the drier into                combustion chamber of the plasma melting furnace                after being condensed and discharging the condensed                water to cleaning water tank for recycling;
   i. mixing granulation additives and the dry                fly ash in the mass ratio of 1-30:100;
   j. granulating and molding using the                granulator, where the particle size of molded                particles is 1-300 mm;
   k. sending the molded particles into plasma                melting furnace to melt at 1200-1600degrees Celsius                to form slag and cooling the slag to form a glass                component;
   l. treating flue gas in plasma melting furnace                after being burned in the combustion chamber                sequentially enters the power wave washing                system;
   m. transporting acidic washing liquid in the                power wave washing system by a pump to the                solid-liquid stirring tank for adjusting the pH of                the mixed liquid;
   n. dehydrating by gypsum dehydrator to form a                gypsum product, entering calcium carbonate                dehydrator for dehydration and drying to obtain                calcium carbonate product;
   o. performing wastewater treatment by                discharging supernatant into intermediate water                tank after dosing coagulation sedimentation                treatment, pumping clear liquid in the intermediate                water tank into desulfurization tower and carbon                dioxide absorption tower through the pump;
   p. controlling pH value in desulfurization                tower and carbon dioxide absorption tower,                centrifuging the desulfurization and                decarbonization slurry in respective centrifuges                and flowing the clear liquid into salt water                tank;
   q. filtering the clear liquid in salt water                tank by the filtration system and entering the                nanofiltration membrane system to intercept sulfate                or carbonate;
   r. returning nanofiltration membrane                concentrated water in the membrane system to the                solid-liquid stirring tank as fly ash washing and                replenishing water;
   . entering clear liquid in the nanofiltration                membrane system to bipolar membrane system to                electrolyze hydrochloric acid and liquid caustic                soda;
   t. storing hydrochloric acid in hydrochloric                acid storage tank and using as acid-base regulator                of the mixed liquid in the solid-liquid stirring                tank;
   u. storing liquid caustic soda in the liquid                caustic soda storage tank and using as                desulfurization agent in the desulfurization tower                and carbon dioxide absorbent for the carbon dioxide                absorption tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cleaning liquid is                  one or more of inorganic waste acid, inorganic                  waste alkali, water, ammonia, sodium hydroxide,                  and sodium carbonate. The granulation additive is                  one or more of sand stone, diatomaceous earth,                  sodium carbonate, and sodium silicate.Preferred Composition: The content of                  chlorine salt in the clear liquid entering the                  bipolar membrane system is 5-20 wt.%.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); ; J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. MC D04-B10A; D04-A01P1; D04-A01F3; D04-A01E; D04-A01B; D04-B07; E32-A02; E33-A03; E33-D; E31-P03; E31-P05C; E31-F01C; E31-N05C; E31-B03D; E11-N; E11-Q02B; J01-E02H1; J01-E02B; J01-E01; J03-B03; J03-B02; L02-J02C; L02-C05; L02-A13; L02-A05; L02-A04; L02-A03; L02-A01; P43-U26; P43-E05; V05-F08E; V05-F05E5; V05-F05E3; V05-F05C; V05-F04

PT P
PN CN108033880-A
TI Preparing 1,2 propylene glycol by one-step synthesis of methyl ethyl carbonate from propylene oxide in parallel condition comprises e.g. vaporizing raw material liquid carbon dioxide and propylene oxide mixture through vaporizer V-1.
AU SHI L
   YU Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201839856S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,2 propylene glycol by one-step synthesis of methyl ethyl carbonate from propylene oxide in parallel condition comprises e.g. vaporizing raw material liquid carbon dioxide and propylene oxide mixture by metering pump P-1 through the vaporizer V-1 and entering bottom of first reactor T1; preheating by preheater V-2 after feeding from top of the first reactor; reacting two raw materials with each other in catalyst portion in the middle of first reactor to obtain first mixture, returning light components from the top of first reactor are carbon dioxide, propylene oxide, methanol and ethanol to the first reactor to continuing reaction and another part of higher boiling component includes dimethyl carbonate and methanol azeotrope, ethyl methyl carbonate and ethanol azeotrope and a small amount of diethyl carbonate and condensed via condenser E-1 and then adding into V-3 preheating tank for preheating and then pumping into pressurized rectification column T2 by P-2 metering pump.
   USE - The 1,2 propylene glycol is useful for preparing carbon fiber composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process; and the carbon fiber composite material: has smooth surface, high comprehensive mechanical performance and high tensile strength, high strength and light weight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 1,2 propylene glycol by one-step synthesis of methyl ethyl carbonate from propylene oxide in parallel condition, comprises one-step synthesis of ethyl methyl carbonate reaction as given in the specification, where the ethyl methyl carbonate production process includes vaporizing raw material liquid carbon dioxide and propylene oxide mixture by the metering pump P-1 through the vaporizer V-1 and enters the bottom of the first reactor T1; another way is the mixture of raw materials methanol and ethanol. preheating by the preheater V-2 after feeding from the top of the first reactor; reacting two raw materials with each other in the catalyst portion in the middle of the first reactor to obtain a first mixture, returning light components from the top of the first reactor are carbon dioxide, propylene oxide, methanol and ethanol to the first reactor to continue the reaction and another part of higher boiling component includes dimethyl carbonate and methanol azeotrope, ethyl methyl carbonate and ethanol azeotrope and a small amount of diethyl carbonate and condensed via condenser E-1 and then adding into V-3 preheating tank for preheating and then pumping into pressurized rectification column T2 by P-2 metering pump; condensing high-boiling components from the bottom of the first reactor are: 1,2-propanediol, diethyl carbonate in the E-2 condenser and then entering the top of the pressurized rectification column T2, and another part of the crude 1,2-propanediol directly into 1,2-propanediol batch distillation column T4 plus water treatment to obtain 1,2-propanediol fine; entering the composition of the pressurized rectification column T2, separating original atmospheric azeotropic composition under high pressure, and returning light components includes methanol and dimethyl carbonate azeotrope and ethanol to T1 first reactor to participate in the reaction, adding High boiling point composition includes ethyl methyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate and 1,2-propanediol T3 atmospheric distillation column to continue separating; E-3 condenser for cooling ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate, E-4 condenser for condensing 1,2-propanediol; entering high boiling component of the preheating tank V-4 into T3 atmospheric distillation column, and distilling ethyl methyl carbonate from the top of T3 column and condensing by E-6 to obtain pure ethyl methyl carbonate fine product; condensing mixture of diethyl carbonate and 1,2-propanediol obtained at the bottom of the T3 in an E-7 condenser into a T41, 2-propanediol batch rectification column; subjecting crude 1,2-propanediol with T41, 2-propanediol batch distillation column to obtain 1,2-propanediol, collecting diethyl carbonate collected from the top of the tower or returned to the T1 first reactor to continuously to participate the reaction; the vaporizer V1 temperature is 40-100 degrees C; at the same time, the metering pump is used to pump the liquid raw material methanol and ethanol with a molar ratio of 1:5-5:1; and the preheating tank V2 temperature is 25-60 degrees C; the apparatus for producing ethyl methyl carbonate comprises: T1 reaction first reactor having catalyst inlet arranged at intervals from top to bottom, raw materials for methanol and ethanol imports and raw materials for carbon dioxide and propylene oxide inlet and the top of the first reactor of T1 reaction has a first discharge port and the bottom of T1 reaction first reactor has a second discharge port; pressurized distillation column having two second feed ports connected to the first discharge port, and a lower discharge port of the pressurized distillation column having a second discharge port and the bottom of the pressurized rectification column has a third discharge port; T3 atmospheric distillation column having third feed port connected to the second and third discharge ports, fourth discharge port at the top of the column, and fifth discharge port communicating with T41,2-propanediol batch rectification column; the 1,2-propylene glycol batch rectification column has a fourth feed port connected to the fifth discharge port, the top of the 1,2-propylene glycol batch distillation column has a sixth discharge port and the lower portion has fifth feed port and the bottom portion has seventh discharge port, where the catalyst is multifunctional composite alkaline material, the catalyst carrier is a molecular sieve with microporous composite pores, and various metal elements are supported as active components, the catalyst performance is stable, and there is no deactivation phenomenon after 5000 hours of use and the amount is 0.1-3 wt.% total mass of raw material; the first mixture includes carbon dioxide and propylene oxide, methanol, ethanol, ethyl methyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate and 1,2-propanediol; the raw materials are propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol, the molar ratio between product 1,2-propanediol and carbonate is 1:1, the route can effectively produce a large quantity of co-produced 1,2-propanediol; where the one-step synthesis of ethyl methyl carbonate reaction as given in the specification, the molar ratio between material propylene oxide and carbon dioxide is 0.1:1-10:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the first reactor T1, reacting raw material under the action of the catalyst to obtain first mixture; condensing the gaseous light components (mainly carbon dioxide and propylene oxide) at top of first mixture by condenser E-1, re-entering the T1 cycle; entering other lower boiling liquids (methanol, ethanol and its azeotrope, ethyl methyl carbonate, etc.) to T2 pressurized rectification column for pressure separation, and returning the light components of the T2 overhead e.g. methanol, ethanol and small amount of azeotrope composition to T1 reactor reaction. The pressure in column of first reactor of T1 is 0.01-5 MPa, the temperature at the top of the column is 64-90 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the column is 130-160 degrees C; the pressure in the column of the pressurized rectification column T2 is 1-15 MPa, the temperature at the top of the column is 50-70 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the column is 130-150 degrees C; the pressure in the column of the atmospheric distillation column T3 is 0.1-0.5 MPa, the temperature at the top of the column is 105-125 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the column is 110-130 degrees C; the pressure of 1,2-propylene glycol batch rectification column T4 is 0.1-0.5 MPa, the temperature at the top of the column is 130-160 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the column is 140-180 degrees C; when the content of 1,2-propanediol in the first reactor column of T1 reaches more than 20%, then condensing liquid in the lower portion of the kettle into T2 pressurized rectification column through the E-2 condenser; and when the 1,2-propanediol content reaches more than 90%, then charging bottom of the T1 reactor into T41,2-propanediol batch rectification column. The reactor and the separator of rectification column are disposed such that the ratio of the cross-sectional area of preliminary distillation section to cross-sectional area of side line section is 1:1-3:1. Preferred Components: The number of trays in the initial distillation section of the reactor is 1-30 pieces, the number of trays in the side line section is 1-30 pieces, the number of plates in the common rectification section is 1-30 pieces and the number of plates in the common stripping section is 1-30 pieces. The reactor T1 first reactor, T2 pressurized rectification column, T3 atmospheric distillation column, T41, 2-propanediol batch rectification column is plate column, packed column or plate-filler mixing tower.

MC E10-A11B1; E10-E04B; E11-A02; E11-F04; E11-F06; E11-W; N07-D05; N07-D07; N07-F08

PN CN113036163-A; CN113036163-B
TI Anode structure of carbon dioxide battery has            water-in-salt electrolyte and carbon dioxide positive            electrode.
AU LIANG F
   YANG X
   HOU M
   XIE Z
   XU B
   MA W
   YANG B
   YAO Y
   DAI Y
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202176309Y
AB    NOVELTY - Anode structure of carbon dioxide battery has                water-in-salt electrolyte and carbon dioxide                positive electrode.
   USE - Anode structure of carbon dioxide                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The positive electrode structure has easy                explosion and the traditional aqueous electrolyte                is easy to volatilize and narrow electrochemical                window and improves the charging and discharging                performance and stable circulating performance of                the battery under the pure carbon dioxide                condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                metal-carbon dioxide battery, which comprises                negative electrode structure, positive electrode                structure and a separator between the negative                electrode structure and the positive electrode                structure, where the negative electrode structure                includes a metal negative electrode and an organic                electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water-in-salt                  electrolyte is selected from potassium acetate                  (CH3COOK), sodium acetate (CH3COONa), sodium                  hydroxide (NaOH), sodium chloride (NaCl), sodium                  perchlorate (NaClO4), and sodium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide (NaFSI). The carbon                  dioxide battery positive electrode includes                  carbon paper and catalytic layer arranged on the                  carbon paper. The positive electrode of the                  carbon dioxide battery further includes current                  collector is a conductive nickel mesh. The                  catalytic layer comprises active carbon. The                  adhesive of catalytic layer comprises                  polytetrafluoroethylene. The catalyst of                  catalytic layer is nano gold, platinum carbon,                  and ruthenium carbon. The metal negative                  electrode includes metallic sodium. The organic                  electrolyte includes an ester electrolyte or an                  ether electrolyte. The separator includes a solid                  electrolyte is NASICON structure fast ion                  conductor of Na3Zr2Si2PO12 and a beta -Al2O3 fast                  ion conductor. Preferred Conditions: The platinum                  carbon comprises 10-50% platinum. The ruthenium                  carbon comprises 5-40% ruthenium. The organic                  electrolyte comprises not greater than 6 parts                  per million water. Preferred Compositions: The                  mass ratio of catalyst, conductor, and adhesive                  is (5-10):(1-5):(1-2). The ester electrolyte                  comprises (0.1-1) mol/L NaClO4/((EC+DMC)(1:1)+1                  vol.% FEC). The ether electrolyte                  comprises(0.1-1) mol/L                  NaClO4/((TEGDME+DME)(1:1)).
