PT P
PN CN111118601-A; CN111118601-B
TI Catalyst for carbon dioxide reduction to ethylene, comprises cuprous oxide nanoparticles, where cuprous oxide nanoparticles exposes both crystal face and crystal plane.
AU WANG Z
   GAO Y
   HUANG B
   ZHANG X
   QIN X
   WANG P
   LIU Y
   ZHENG Z
   CHENG H
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2020413498
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises cuprous oxide nanoparticles. The cuprous oxide nanoparticles exposes both crystal face and crystal plane.
   USE - Catalyst used for carbon dioxide reduction to ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyt has higher ethylene selectivity and activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electrode for producing ethylene by carbon dioxide reduction, in which active layer is attached to electrode surface, and active layer contains catalyst for producing ethylene by carbon dioxide reduction; and
   (2) a method for producing ethylene by carbon dioxide reduction, which involves using electrochemical method to reduce carbon dioxide at carbon dioxide electrode to produce ethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The size of cuprous oxide nanoparticles is 1-5000 nanometer. The mass ratio of cuprous oxide nanoparticles to carbon black is 0.01-10:1. The volume ratio of isopropyl alcohol, water and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution is 1-20:1-10:1. The ultrasound is used for mixing for 1-5 hours. The input ratio of copper salt and polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1 mmole: 0.1-5.5 g. The temperature is 50-60 degrees C. The ratio of copper salt to sodium hydroxide is 1:19-21. The ratio of copper salt to ascorbic acid is 1:5-7. The concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 1.5-2.5 molar. The concentration of ascorbic acid solution is 0.5-0.7 molar. The thickness of catalyst is 10-10000 nanometer. The drip coating rate is 1-3 drops/second. The three-electrode system at (-)1.1 V vs RHE is used for preparation. Preferred Components: The catalyst further comprises carbon black. The solvent is composed of isopropyl alcohol, water and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution. Preferred Process: The cuprous oxide nanoparticles and carbon black are added to solvent, mixed uniformly and dried. The cuprous oxide nanoparticles is prepared by adding copper salt, polyvinylpyrrolidone and sodium hydroxide to water and mixing uniformly. The ascorbic acid is added and obtained after reaction. The sodium hydroxide solution is added dropwise to solution containing copper salt and polyvinylpyrrolidone. The ascorbic acid solution is added dropwise. The cuprous oxide nanoparticles and carbon black are added to solvent and mixed uniformly to obtain copper oxide/carbon (Cu2O/C) mixture. The Cu2O/C mixture is applied to electrode.

GA 2022C73523
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recovery system (200) comprises                a reaction layer that carries an amine compound and                adsorbs and desorbs carbon dioxide, a medium                circulation layer through which a heating medium                for heating the reaction layer or a cooling medium                for cooling the reaction layer flows is alternately                laminated, a medium flow pipe connecting at least                two structures and one end of the medium flow layer                of one of the structures and one end of the medium                flow layer of the other structure, and at least two                medium flow pipes including a medium flow pipe                connecting the other end of the medium flow layer                of the one structure and other end of the medium                flow layer of the other structure, where the medium                flow pipe is a pipe for circulating a cooling                medium or a heating medium, and the heating medium                is caused to flow out from the one structure that                absorbs carbon dioxide to one of the medium                circulation pipes, and the heating medium flows                into the other structure that releases carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces amount of heat required to                recover carbon dioxide recovery system, and                prevents the evaporation of amine during carbon                dioxide recovery absorption/release to prevent a                decrease in the amount of carbon dioxide                absorbed/released.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide recovery method.

AB    NOVELTY - Ship (100) comprises a cargo hold (110) provided on a hull. A carbon dioxide tank (120) is provided in the cargo hold. A hold space (130) is formed between an inner wall of the cargo hold and an outer wall of a carbon dioxide tank, and a refrigerant in the hold space so that the carbon dioxide tank maintains a temperature at which carbon dioxide is stored as a solid. A refrigerant tank (141) is used for storing the refrigerant. A vaporizer is used for vaporizing the refrigerant supplied from the refrigerant tank and supplying it to the hold space. A reliquefaction device is used for liquefying the refrigerant discharged from the hold space and supplying it to the refrigerant tank. The refrigerant stored in the refrigerant tank contains LNG, and the carbon dioxide tank stores carbon dioxide at an atmospheric pressure.

PN DE102018210030-A1; WO2019243204-A1; EP3810561-A1; US2021268460-A1; JP2021528350-W; RU2780061-C2; RU2021100925-A
TI Coating urea-containing granules with organic            polymers comprises e.g. compressing and condensing            gaseous carbon dioxide, dissolving and obtaining            polymer-containing solution, and mixing with            urea-containing granulate.
AU MAKHYNYA Y
   EL-HAWARY T
   EL HAWARY T
   MAKHINIIA E
   EL KH T
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP AG (THYS-C)
GA 2019A7241L
AB    NOVELTY - Coating urea-containing granules with organic                polymers comprises: (a) compressing and condensing                gaseous carbon dioxide and obtain liquid carbon                dioxide; (b) increasing the pressure and/or                temperature above the critical point of carbon                dioxide and obtained from supercritical carbon                dioxide; (c) dissolving an organic polymer in the                supercritical carbon dioxide and obtaining a                polymer-containing solution, and (d) mixing,                spraying and/or bringing the polymer-containing                solution into contact with a urea-containing                granulate. A simultaneous or subsequent lowering of                the temperature and/or the pressure below the                critical point of carbon dioxide takes place; and a                coated urea-containing granules and gaseous carbon                dioxide are obtained.
   USE - The plant complex is useful for coating                urea-containing particles with biodegradable                polymers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plant complex provides coated fertilizer                granules without the using solvents which are                harmful to the environment and/or to health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                system complex for coating urea-containing granules                with biodegradable polymers at least comprising a                urea synthesis system and a system for coating                urea-containing granules, comprising a supply line                for supercritical carbon dioxide from the urea                synthesis plant and a supply line for a                biodegradable polymer, a mixing device for                supercritical carbon dioxide and the biodegradable                polymer, which is connected to the feed line for                the supercritical carbon dioxide and the feed line                for the biodegradable polymer, and a coater                connected to the mixing device and a feed line for                a granulate, where the coater has a first outlet                for gaseous carbon dioxide and a second outlet for                a coated product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  compression stage comprises: carbon dioxide feed                  line connected to a first compression stage; a                  second compression stage connected to the first                  compression stage; a third compression stage                  connected to the second compression stage; a                  fourth compression stage connected to the third                  compression stage; a first connecting line to a                  urea synthesis unit connected to the fourth                  compression stage; a branch between the second                  compression stage and the third compression stage                  and a second connecting line from the branch to a                  degassing system, the second connecting line                  passing through a refrigeration system; a pump                  connected to the degassing system; and a third                  connecting line connected to the pump and the                  feed line for supercritical carbon dioxide. The                  urea synthesis system is connected to an ammonia                  synthesis unit as an ammonia-urea complex and/or                  a urea granulation system. The first outlet for                  gaseous carbon dioxide is connected to a dust                  washer, dust separator and/or cyclone. A                  passivation air supply line is arranged between                  the second compression stage and the third                  compression stage.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic polymer                  comprises biodegradable polymers. The                  biodegradable polymer polylactide (PLA),                  polyglycols, polycaprolactones, poly                  (hydroxybutyric acid), poly (hydroxyvaleric                  acid), polyalkyl terephthalates, polyanhydrides,                  poly (1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione), polyamino acids,                  polysaccharides, cellulose esters, cellulose                  hydrate, cellulose acetate, carboxymethyl                  cellulose, lignin, polyhydroxy fatty acids,                  starch, biodegradable polyesters, biodegradable                  polyamides, biodegradable polyimides,                  polyhydroxyalkanoates and polybutylene succinates                  (PBS), amylose, amylopectin and/or mixtures,                  their oligomers, derivatives and copolymers. The                  polymer-containing solution contains 20-70 wt.%                  biodegradable polymers, preferably 40-60 wt.%                  biodegradable polymers. Preferred Method: The                  gaseous carbon dioxide is returned to the process                  and converted into supercritical carbon dioxide.                  The gaseous carbon dioxide and the supercritical                  carbon dioxide are provided in an associated                  industrial plant complex, preferably in a urea                  synthesis plant, coke plant, refinery plants                  and/or in an ammonia-urea complex, preferably in                  an ammonia-urea complex. The condensation in step                  (a) is carried out with the aid of a coolant,                  preferably ammonia, from a connected ammonia                  refrigeration system. The liquid carbon dioxide                  after step (a) is subjected to a flash process                  for the removal and/or outgassing of inert and                  non-condensable gases. The gaseous carbon dioxide                  is compressed in a first stage in an LP (low                  pressure) compression stage and in a second stage                  in an HP (high pressure) compression stage. A                  cooling or heat recovery and/or cooler are                  provided. The urea-containing granules and/or the                  coated urea-containing granules contain ammonium                  salts, nitrates, phosphates, sulfur, potassium,                  calcium, preferably urea, ammonium sulfate,                  ammonium nitrate, phosphates and/or sulfur. The                  urea-containing granules have an average particle                  size of 0.5-8 mm, preferably 2-4 mm. The                  polymer-containing solution is mixed with the                  urea-containing granule in coater, preferably                  drum coater, fluidized bed coater, and/or                  fluidized bed coater with tangential                  spraying.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Capturing of carbon dioxide from gas stream and            releasing it into another gas stream used in            greenhouse, involves contacting packed bed with stream            of gases, and repeating above step.

   OKEEFE F
AE CARBON SINK INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON SINK INC (CARB-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Capturing of carbon dioxide from gas stream                (A) and releasing it into gas stream (B) involves                contacting packed bed or fluidized bed with stream                of gas (A) which comprising humidity-sensitive                sorbent material that absorbs carbon dioxide from                gas stream (A), contacting packed bed or fluidized                bed with stream of gas (B) which release the                absorbed carbon dioxide, resulting in net transfer                of carbon dioxide from gas stream (A) to gas stream                (B), and repeating above steps optionally. The                water vapor pressure in gas (A) is less than                equilibrium water vapor pressure for gas in contact                with sorbent. The water vapor pressure in gas (B)                is greater than gas (A).
   USE - Capturing of carbon dioxide from gas stream,                and releasing into another gas stream used in                greenhouse, bioreactor, promote growth of plant or                algae, cure cement or concrete, production of                protein, starch, starch derivatives, animal feed,                biofuel, bioplastic, biochar, syngas, graphene,                energy, heat, calcium carbonate, magnesium                carbonate, sodium carbonate, carbon fiber, carbon                nanotubes, fullerene, graphene, polymers, methanol,                formic acid, oil recovery, and coal bead methane                recovery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved efficiency of carbon                dioxide capture, and provides moisture swing or                humidity swing to reduce high energy consumption                and cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide                from ambient air;
   (2) apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide                from indoor air;
   (3) system for capturing carbon dioxide from                gas or air; and
   (4) removing of carbon dioxide from gas and                utilizing in secondary process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                capture of carbon dioxide.
   System (1100)
   Inlet (1102)
   Absorber (1104)
   Separator (1106)
   Outlet (1108)

PN JP2019178056-A; JP7136577-B2
TI Apparatus for manufacturing carbon dioxide,            consists of gas circulation line for circulating mixed            gas, heat exchanger, reactor, splitter, raw material            powder supply line, carbon dioxide recovery line, and            diluted medium supply line.
AU SHIMA H
   KOMATSU T
   TAKAYAMA Y
   TAKADA Y
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
   UBE-MITSUBISHI CEMENT CORP (MITV-C)
GA 201986439G
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus (10)                consists of a gas circulation line (11) for                circulating a mixed gas (G) containing carbon                dioxide and a diluted medium (E), a heat exchanger                (12,15), a reactor (13), and a splitter (14)                arranged on the gas circulation line in this order,                a raw material powder supply line (21) connected to                the gas circulation line, a carbon dioxide recovery                line (22), a diluted medium supply line (23), and a                diluted medium separation unit (29). The heat                exchanger performs heat exchange between the gas                circulation line and a high-temperature atmosphere                region (H) maintained at a temperature higher than                the gas circulation line. The raw material powder                supply line is connected between the reactor and                the heat exchanger or connected to the reactor, and                introduces a raw material powder (M) for generating                carbon dioxide into the gas circulation line. The                reactor generates carbon dioxide by a                decarboxylation reaction.
   USE - Apparatus is used for manufacturing carbon                dioxide (claimed) used as food material and                industrial material, and for growing plant and                algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus                provides carbon dioxide with reduced content of                toxic substances, by economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus                consists of a gas circulation line for circulating                a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and a diluted                medium, a heat exchanger, a reactor, and a splitter                arranged on the gas circulation line in this order,                a raw material powder supply line connected to the                gas circulation line, a carbon dioxide recovery                line, a diluted medium supply line, and a diluted                medium separation unit formed in the carbon dioxide                recovery line. The heat exchanger performs heat                exchange between the gas circulation line and a                high-temperature atmosphere region maintained at a                temperature higher than the gas circulation line.                The raw material powder supply line is connected                between the reactor and the heat exchanger or                connected to the reactor, and introduces a raw                material powder for generating carbon dioxide into                the gas circulation line. The reactor generates                carbon dioxide by a decarboxylation reaction                carried out by contacting the generated raw                material powder with the mixed gas heated by the                heat exchanger. The splitter separates the                generated raw material powder and the mixed gas.                The carbon dioxide recovery line recovers the mixed                gas from the system of the gas circulation line.                The diluted medium supply line supplies the diluted                medium supply line to the gas circulation line. The                diluted medium separation unit separates the                diluted medium from the mixed gas. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of carbon dioxide, which                involves supplying the diluted medium to the gas                circulation line through the diluted medium supply                line, heating the mixed gas using the heat                exchanger in the high-temperature atmosphere                region, carrying out decarboxylation reaction for                generating carbon dioxide by contacting the mixed                gas directly with the generated raw material                powder, separating the generated raw material                powder and the mixed gas, recovering the mixed gas                in an amount corresponding to the amount of carbon                dioxide produced during the reaction, refluxing the                remaining mixed gas to the gas circulation line,                and separating the diluted medium from the mixed                gas; and
   (2) method for designing carbon dioxide                manufacturing apparatus, which involves determining                the supply amount of generated raw material powder                to the gas circulation line based on the                temperature of the mixed gas, after heating, and                the supply amount of diluted medium to the gas                circulation line, such that the generation amount                of carbon dioxide becomes plus minus 10% with                respect to the maximum value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of                carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus                (10)
   Gas circulation line (11)
   Heat exchangers (12,15)
   Reactor (13)
   Splitter (14)
   Raw material powder supply line (21)
   Carbon dioxide recovery line (22)
   Diluted medium supply line (23)
   Diluted medium separation unit (29)
   Preheater (31)
   Cement kiln (32)
   Clinker cooler (33)
   Calcination furnace (34)
   Diluted medium (E)
   Mixed gas (G)
   High-temperature atmosphere region (H)
   Raw material powder (M)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The diluted medium                  separation unit separates the carbon dioxide and                  the diluted medium using the boiling point                  difference or the solubility difference of the                  carbon dioxide and the diluted medium. The                  high-temperature atmosphere region has a cement                  kiln (32), a clinker cooler (33), a preheater                  (31), a calcining furnace (34), and an extraction                  duct connecting the clinker cooler and the                  calcining furnace in a cement manufacturing                  apparatus. The carbon dioxide manufacturing                  apparatus further consists of a return line for                  returning the generated raw material powder                  separated by the splitter towards the cement                  kiln, the preheater, the calciner, or the                  extraction duct.

TI Small gas turbine for offshore applications, has main body provided with exhaust gas recirculation unit that increases concentration of carbon dioxide in gas, where gas enters into compact carbon dioxide capture unit.
AU 
AE STATOIL PETROLEUM AS (DENO-C)
   KOEIJER G D (KOEI-Individual)
   JAKOBSEN J (JAKO-Individual)
   PEDERSEN S (PEDE-Individual)
   FOSTAS B (FOST-Individual)
GA 201839091T
AB    NOVELTY - The turbine has a main body provided with an exhaust gas recirculation unit and a compact carbon dioxide capture unit. The exhaust gas recirculation unit increases concentration of carbon dioxide in gas that enters into the compact carbon dioxide capture unit, where exhaust gas from the small gas turbine is routed through a waste heat recovery unit before the exhaust gas is divided into two streams, one of the streams of the exhaust gas flows through a blower to a heat exchanger and other stream of the exhaust gas flows through another blower to the compact carbon dioxide capture unit.
   USE - Small gas turbine for offshore applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The turbine recycles part of the exhaust gas, so that carbon dioxide concentration in the gas entering into the compact carbon dioxide capture unit is increased, thus increasing driving force for capturing the carbon dioxide and allowing the compact carbon dioxide capture unit with minimized weight and size for off-shore application.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a small gas turbine.

when the sodium bicarbonate solution in                  the dilute mixing tank exceeds 2/3 of the total                  capacity of the dilute mixing tank, the inflation                  pressure of TI Catalyst includes natural minerals, inorganic oxides, silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieves and precious metals.
AU WANG L
   YAN J
   TIAN H
   SUN M
   LIN W
   WANG P
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 202150597U
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst includes 10-70 wt.% natural minerals, 10-60 wt.% inorganic oxides, 10-70 wt.% silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieves and 0.01-10 wt.% precious metals.
   USE - Catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst effectively improves selectivity of low-carbon olefins, and realize the effective utilization of carbon dioxide under mild conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing silicoaluminophosphate-containing molecular sieve catalyst, which involves mixing and spray-drying silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve, natural minerals, inorganic oxides and/or inorganic oxide precursors to obtain a solid product, loading the noble metal on the solid product, and then calcining the formed product; and
   (2) a method for catalytic conversion of gasoline, which involves contacting gasoline and carbon dioxide with a catalyst and optionally diluent gas for reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The noble metal is selected from gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, platinum, iridium and osmium. The silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve is selected from SAPO-11 molecular sieve, SAPO-31 molecular sieve, SAPO-34 molecular sieve and SAPO-47 molecular. The natural mineral is selected from kaolin, montmorillonite, diatomaceous earth, attapulgite, sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite, bentonite and rectorite. The inorganic oxide is selected from silicon oxide, aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, titanium oxide, boron oxide, amorphous silicon aluminum, aluminum phosphate, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. The catalyst also contains an auxiliary agent selected from IIA, IIIA, IVA, VA, lanthanide, yttrium, chromium, manganese, rhenium, iron and cadmium, preferably selected from calcium, iron, gallium, indium, bismuth, lanthanum and manganese. TI Producing calcium carbonate by adding calcium to neutral amino acid aqueous solution to eluate calcium ions, introducing carbon dioxide gas to precipitate calcium carbonate and agglomerating calcium carbonate by adding magnesium salt.
AU WAKIMOTO Y
   KOJIMA T
   YAMAGUCHI A
AE AISIN SEIKI KK (AISE-C)
   UNIV TOKYO (UYTY-C)
GA 2020490697
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing calcium carbonate involves (a) adding a solid containing calcium to an aqueous solution containing neutral amino acid to eluate calcium ions, (b) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the aqueous solution containing neutral amino acid in which the calcium ions are eluted to precipitate calcium carbonate and (c) agglomerating the calcium carbonate by adding magnesium salt to the aqueous solution containing neutral amino acid in which the calcium carbonate is dispersed.
   USE - The method is useful for producing calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method rapidly filters calcium carbonate while reusing aqueous solution in which calcium ions are eluted.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (b), the molar ratio of the magnesium ions of the magnesium salt to the calcium ions contained in the solid material is 0.02-0.25. Preferred Components: The magnesium salt used in the flocculation process comprises magnesium carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Regenerating sodium bicarbonate, comprises e.g. dissolving desulfurization by-product including sodium sulfate and sodium chloride in water, cooling first solution, adding carbon dioxide and aqueous ammonia to separation solution.
AU LEE Y M
   KWON I
AE HYUNDAI STEEL CO (HYSL-C)
GA 202049573S
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating sodium bicarbonate, comprises dissolving desulfurization by-product including sodium sulfate and sodium chloride in water to obtain a first solution; cooling the first solution; separating a separation solution and sodium sulfate precipitated from the cooled first solution; preparing a second solution containing supernatant containing ammonium chloride and sodium bicarbonate precipitated under the supernatant by adding carbon dioxide and aqueous ammonia to the separation solution; and recovering sodium bicarbonate from the second solution.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, minimizes the cost of landfill, thus it is and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The dissolution comprises a condition in which a high liquid-liquid ratio of the desulfurization by-product and water is 1:3 g-1:4 ml. The dissolution process is performed at 30-40 degrees C. The first solution is cooled to 5-20 degrees C. The method comprises recovering the supernatant after the step of recovering the sodium bicarbonate, adding calcium hydroxide to the recovered supernatant to obtain regenerated aqueous ammonia, where the regenerated aqueous ammonia is added to the separation solution to prepare a second solution.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110721716-A
TI Preparation of catalyst for catalyzing selective hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methane, by dissolving tetrabutyl titanate in ethanol solution, dropwise adding solution containing urea, calcining, and dispersing in deionized water.
AU WANG Z
   CHEN S
   SU Z
   ZHANG M
   CHEN Y
   YANG J
AE UNIV FUJIAN NORMAL FUQING BRANCH (UYFJ-C)
GA 2020110000
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is prepared by dissolving tetrabutyl titanate in ethanol solution, dropwise adding solution containing urea to obtain mixed solution, stirring for 2-4 hours, evaporating to obtain white powder, calcining at 350-500 degrees C to obtain N-titania, dispersing in deionized water, adding chloroplatinic acid solution, stirring for 2-4 hours, evaporating to give gray powder, putting in hydrogen gas, and reduction reacting at 300-400 degrees C for 2-4 hours.
   USE - The method is used for preparing catalyst for catalyzing selective hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product increases selectivity of methane and improves stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of hydrogen and nitrogen is 1:8-12.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume/weight ratio of tetrabutyl titanate and urea is 1-5 ml:40 mg. The volume ratio of tetrabutyl titanate and ethanol is 1-5:10. The urea content in urea-containing solution is 2 mg/ml. The concentration of N-titania after dissolving in deionized water is 0.5-2 mg/ml. The chloroplatinic acid solution is 0.01 mol/L chloroplatinic acid solution. Preferred Method: Dissolving is done by stirring for 20-40 minutes. Evaporation is done at 100 degrees C. Calcining is done for 1-3 hours.
